'Toronto Ageplay Group' Monthly Munch! - March 19th

Mar. 10th, 2011   8:39 PM
(cross-post)

In and around Toronto there are a lot more ageplayers, ABDLs and babyfurs than you might think!  Wouldn’t it be fun and exciting to hangout with like minded people ~ make some new friends or see familiar ones?  Come join us in a safe and friendly environment over dinner!

WHEN:    The munches take place every 3rd Saturday of each month. The next munch date is: March 19th

TIME:    Munches take place at 7:00pm until whenever.

WHERE:    The Gov’nor Pub, located at 401 Bloor Street East, just west of Sherbourne subway station.

WEBSITE:   http://www.govnorpub.com/

DIRECTIONS:    The best way to The Gov’nor Pub would be to take the subway to Sherbourne subway station on the Bloor-Danforth line, and walk from there.

If you need public transit information, check out http://www.ttc.ca, http://www.gotransit.ca, or http://www.viarail.ca.

For those who are driving, have a look at Google Maps: Click here!

If you need specific directions from a particular location, please email me: discourse-dumpling@hotmail.com

PARKING:   There is a public parking lot directly behind The Gov’nor Pub (enter off of Selby Street) and costs $6.00. Note: The parking ticket machine takes twoonies (Canadian $2.00 coin) only, so be sure to bring 3 of them along.

Additionally, there is metered street parking is available along Bloor and surrounding streets. There is a large "Green P" parking garage near the corner of Yonge St. and Charles Street East: http://www.greenp.com/tpa/parkinglocator/CarParks/Downtown%20Fringe/1/1.jsp

HOW TO FIND US:   The munch organizers looks like this ~ short, Asian and wears a spunky hat.  Our table is usually at the back, in the raised level area, near the projector screen. 

The munch organizers will generally be there a few minutes early to request a group of tables be set aside as seating is on a first-come, first-served basis. That being said, it is important to be at the restaurant as close to 7:00pm as possible to ensure that the group is seated together. Those who come later might not be seated at the group table, depending on how busy the restaurant is. (You might be able to request a table near ours as an alternative.)

IMPORTANT NOTE:    This is a *public place* so due discretion is warranted. (i.e. don't come wearing obvious diapers, bondage gear, etc. and don't make a scene. :-)

The munches are NOT a "pickup event" and are NON-SEXUAL in nature. This goes for the munch itself and anywhere the group may go afterwards. (It's not a night for "getting some".) :-)

See you there!

CONTACT INFO:   Have more questions? Feel free to email me: discourse-dumpling@hotmail.com
🐇 bouncy  |  ♫ Aladdin - One Jump Ahead
Link

Work...Furnal Equinox...Health...

Mar. 8th, 2011   10:16 PM
Work
I work a job that no one in my department wants to do because it’s physically taxing and most of the people there are old family men.  Last week I had to train one of my co-workers how to do my duties in case I ever took a day off.  That day was suppose to be this Friday but it ended up being today because my boss decided that I needed training at a sister branch in case a same situation were to occur.  The branch I worked at today – and for the rest of the week – is significantly smaller and easier.  The amount of work I do feels proportionate to how little I make, where as the complete opposite is the case at the main branch.  I feel bad for my friends who have to suffer, especially since they have years of seniority.  To be honest though, I never want to go back to the main branch =S. 

==================================================

Furnal Equinox
It’s in 3 days.  I was stressing about it last week but as the convention draws closer my shopping list gets shorter.  I’m quite happy about how much I’ve gotten done ~ so much so that I’ve already begun to pack my things.  I’ll have two storage bins, two reusable bags, and one backpack full of stuff.  Excessive I know, but luckily I’ve secured a ride thanks to Dodge and Dan Raccoon ~ you guys are awesome! 

For the cubs room I have a fun little idea with the cartoon wallcoverings and removable border stickers.  If the adhesive on the stickers are strong enough I plan on hanging the wallcovers throughout the room, this of course also depends on how long the roll is.  From there anyone can doodle on the ‘walls’ with markers much like little kids tend to do ~ fun! ^-^.  I also bought some sturdy sippy cups that are also disposable.  They came in packs of six so I got three of them; hopefully it’ll be enough.  To go along with the four board games for the competition, I found a nifty little prize after browsing through five stores!  It’s a secret though ~ but I’m bummed at how most of the things I buy to give away are the things I’d love to keep for myself XD.  That’s okay though, I bought a ‘Drunken Carrot’ and ‘Shaky Bacon’ plushie for my fursuit. 

Just about everything for this room and events has been perfectly idealized in my head.  That’s not a good thing since I’m setting such expectations.  If it flops my head will explode.  Furthermore, the con schedule is also beginning to become idealized in my head as well.  Some of this might make me sounds like a douchebag, but it’s probably easier to know now than when it happens…
- I want to fursuit as quickly as possible after arriving at the con on Friday
- I want to ditch halfway through my Friday room event so that I can hit up the Camp Feral room party and do jello shooters – then have a fun time with other friends
- I don’t want to go to the Mandarin for dinner on Saturday; I’d eat poutine with the WTF? crew instead
- I want to wear my 1 LOVE T.O. shirt, while in suit, so that I can try to get a pic on their website!  (consequences?!?! lol)

----------------------------------------------------------------------
On another note; am I somehow a well connected person?  KanaKitten wanted to know if I was interested in organizing some people to go for a supply pick-up at the B4NS warehouse – since she knows the owner – during What The Fur?  And Xaber wanted to know if I’d be interested in trying to have a room dedicated to babyfur events, along with a ‘diaper swap’ meet, at Condition: Red.

I don’t start conversations online because I don’t have anything interesting to say, but people just seem to keep talking to me through their own initiative.  It’s awesome how that works, but oddly different for me to be in a position where people expect certain things. 

==================================================

Health
I’ve been feeling under the weather since the weekend and it’s progressed to sore throat and occasional dizziness.  Everyday I’ve been nomming numerous vitamin C supplements, fruits, and water.  I hope I get better by Thursday! D:
😴 tired  |  ♫ Bruno Mars - Just The Way You Are
Link

The night before...Cons...

Mar. 3rd, 2011   10:11 PM
Work today has just been murder on my body, but the night leading up to it was way awesome! 

I went to the monthly furry dinner the other day; there was a small turn out which made it pretty laid back.  I rarely ever see Kimba, despite living fairly close by, so it was a welcoming surprise when he popped in! :D  Afterwards he invited me and another fur back to his place, stopping at an LCBO along the way.  The last time I was over he had an arcade in the living room ~ now there's five of them!  I mostly played 'Crisis Zone' and 'Puzzle Bobble' while getting drunk and baked with Laha and the other furry compatriots.  Much later into the night a couple of us watched 'Jackass 3D' before giving in to drowsiness.  I only got about two hours of sleep before heading to work feeling like shit, but for the fun time I had it was totally worth it!  I'm feeling a bit of sickness coming on though XD

=================================

One more week until Furnal Equinox!  I'm really excited for it even though it also stressing me out sometimes.  It really isn't so much about my room events - since it has been reduced to a shopping list at this point - but other people and their predicaments.  I hear about them and most of the time I know I can do something to help out even though my hands are already fairly full.  I get this feeling that I know more people than some others and so I'm expected to be the middle man ~ a position I'm not accustom to being put in.  You can't make everyone happy, but it's always worthwhile to try. 

Beyond the stress is more excitement at least ~ I'm really looking forward to attending Furry Connection North next month!  I went last year and it was a total booze and party con! :D
😫 exhausted  |  ♫ Incubus - Paper Shoes

Doing stuff...

Feb. 26th, 2011   10:08 PM
It's been a fairly productive Saturday considering I planned on doing nothing but get fat and drunk this weekend ~ which is going great btw!  I cut my own hair in the morning - possibly only the fourth time I've done it - and messed up with the clipper setting XD.  The only way to fix it was to cut my hair even shorter!  So I guess I won't have to worry about getting it done again for a while now =P.

I made tournament bracket templates (just realized I could have Googled it instead =.=*) for the board game contest event during Furnal Equinox.  I made several with varying participants since we don't know how many will actually be attending ~ prior preparation is super important to me!  I'm also in the processes of making a playlist, since I have a spiffy iPod speaker thingie, with loads of familiar tunes ~ mostly Disney and cartoon opening themes! 

In the afternoon I took advantage of the empty house and free use of the family car and went to Greenbean's place to pick up my order of diapers.  We split a case of Bambino + suffers a while ago but I just never had the time to collect heh.  I normally get Molicare cause I'm cheap, so this'll be a treat for me! XD  Afterwards I had some shopping errands to do but on a whim I asked if he wanted to tag along.  We took a ghetto tour around Value Village, Walmart, Salvation Army, and a flea market ~ lots of useless junk to look at.  I got some more removable border stickers (this time of Winnie the Pooh) and a roll of Baby Disney wallcoverings.  I'm hoping they'll jazz up the babyfur room!  I also bought Hungry Hungry Hippos, which was strangely cheaper than the other games I still need to get, and a portable fan for my fursuit head.  Bean and I ate at a Vietnamese restaurant before going our separate ways ~ it was nice having a bit of company even if just for a few hours =3. 
✨ chipper  |  ♫ Gnarls Barkley - Gone Daddy Gone

Few and far between...

Feb. 25th, 2011   12:18 AM
I was thinking of asking whether you wanted to hang out this weekend, but... I give up on making plans with you because whenever I do it rarely comes together.  Too often do I find out last minute - sometimes without notice until I follow up on it - that you won't be free.  I can understand it when the circumstances are out of your control, but don't lead me on.  I use to look forward to seeing you but more recently I set very low expectations.  It's disappointing coming from someone I wish I could get to know better.  My time doesn't feel respected and it affects my plans with other, more reliable, people.  I don't get any joy from writing this.  We had a few good times that I'm sentimental about, but I won't allow it to fluff over how I usually feel.  Please do something about this if you know who you are. 
😬 uncomfortable  |  ♫ Kanye West - Runaway
Link

I don't know.

Feb. 22nd, 2011   10:38 PM
Occasionally I write entries that I don't post publically because I might regret it later or for other reasons.  Prior to the long weekend I had been feeling overwhelmed by various emotions ~ four separate things had been making feel nervious, stressed, worried, and troubled.  I had written out what was going through my head for each of the issues but I didn't feel comfortable sharing the entry because it directly applied to very specific things and people.  Three of the things that had been on my mind were resolved over the weekend.  I would have regretted posting my thoughts and feelings for making them a 'known' issue.  I would have felt ashamed for coming to such decisions and conclusions.  Even though it is uncomforting reading what I had written, I know that at the time those feelings were relevent.  Had I posted them, would the outcomes have been different?  Sometimes being patient will make you feel happier in the long run, but knowing when to allow things to happen as they are suppose to is difficult to judge.  I still have one matter that continues to bother me ~ I think about it frequently and my emotions are sensitive to how I feel about it ever becoming resolved.  Patience or confrontation?  I don't know.
🌀 restless  |  ♫ Travie McCoy (ft. Bruno Mars) - Billionaire

Feb. 20th, 2011   12:36 PM

It's a long weekend...

Feb. 20th, 2011   12:21 PM
...and I'm feeling great! :D 

=========================================

Yesterday was the monthly littles' playdate and ageplay munch ~ I was a bit nervous with the latter event since I didn't know what the turnout would be, but it went really well! 

A dozen people were at the playdate including two new faces.  It was Valentine's themed so you could imagine all the left over discount candy there was!  Sugar rush! \o.o/  As usual there was cupcake decorating, colouring, and we watched a bunch of Animaniacs, plus two Wallace & Gromit shorts.  I always have such a fun time :3  It was way awesome that everyone there decided to go the munch afterwards ~ kudos to the three drivers for getting us there ;) 

Six additional people were at the munch bringing the total to18! D:  It was good that I knew a majority of them, but it was a bummer that the seating arrangement didn't allow for discussion between people at the far ends of the table.  That's okay though, I made a round to greet the new faces ~ and plus there were talkative people spread throughout.  I had a bit to drink and a bunch of us smoked a bowl before sitting down, so hopefully I didn't say anything stupid that night =S.  Afterwards, Nebulus and I headed home with Marvin for some Wii, weed, wine, hockey and SNL.  Max and his younger brother were there too and it was such a chill night.  It was a really chill way to end the night despite sucking badly at Wii Bowling! =P

=========================================

I think I'll just relax at home today.  In the evening I'm gonna meet up with Dodge and maybe play squash or just get something to eat =3.  I'll probably head up to B-town tomorrow and hang out with Dan Raccoon and Shiro for the day. 

=========================================

Pro tennis is going so great right now!  Relative nobody Milos Raonic impressed everyone by winning his first ATP title last week and reaching the finals of the Memphis Open ~ he plays Andy Roddick later today!  Raonic is totally putting us on the map by becoming the first and highest ranked Canadian in the world at #37.  Last year he was ranked 361; what an amazing accomplishment!

I love Roddick but I gotta root for Raonic today.  They both have massive serves and great court coverage so it should be a good match to watch.  And congrats to Robin Soderling on winning his third title in four tournaments this season ~ he beat Marin Cilic yesterday at the Open 13 in Marseille.  It's so awesome to see the players I cheer for win! XD
🌀 energetic  |  ♫ Muleboy - Ackly

Furnal Equinox Room Events!

Feb. 16th, 2011   11:22 PM
Plans are cooking!  I'll be rooming with Dan Raccoon, Shiro and Toya!

Friday Night ~ "Milk and Cookies Meet and Greet" ~ aiming for 9pm
- open to everyone!  (expect some flyers by the reg table)
- get settled into the con by getting to know the other con-goers!
- selection of cookies will depend on our benefactor

Saturday Night ~ "Board Game Competition" ~ aiming for 10:30pm
- exclusive to kits, cubs and caretakers!
- quick and fun games in a tournament style:
  • Hungry Hungry Hippos
  • Guess Who?
  • Jenga
  • Connect Four
  • One super awesome mystery game for the final round!
- snacks, sodas, and fun music!
- prizes are being discussed!
(prior to the event will be dinner at Mandarin, aiming for 7-7:30pm)

Sunday Night ~ "PJs and Movie Night" ~ aiming for 8pm
- exclusive to kits, cubs and caretakers!
- five movies available and two to be voted for watching
- PJs, blankets, plushies, and anything that makes you comfortable are encouraged!

==========================================

Speard the word ~ I'll love you more! 
I need an estimated headcount!  Expect me to pester you sometime soon!

==========================================

Does this room need a name?  AC has the Cub Hub and MFF has the C'ubHouse...Con C.r.i.b.? (cubs room is bananas! XD lol)
Also, Firebreath is hosting a babyfur panel at some time...be there!

Job goals...

Feb. 15th, 2011   7:34 PM
Time to look for a new job. 

I've been squaking about it on and off up until now but was never fully committed.  I don't think I've ever been as unhappy about my job as I did today.  It wasn't the amount of work since it fluctuates - on heavy days it just requires more backbone.  I can't stand the menialness, not having to think critically, and the lack of challenge due to constant routine.  Everything I do is just depressive labour and I know I can do better than that.  I have to do better or else I'll be disappointed at myself. 

My goal is to spend at least half an hour a day job hunting.  I know that spending hours searching in one sitting doesn't work for me because it feels daunting and disheartening.  I'd like to be in a good direction by mid-April ~ if I have some interviews lined up by then, super.

Not telling...

Feb. 9th, 2011   9:55 PM
The more I hang around my ‘furry’ and ‘ageplay’ social circles, the more difficult it’s become to express how I feel about relationships towards people not in the know.  But when I get past the fact that “Yes, my interests are an awkward thing to discuss to normal people”, my mind feels liberated.  Thank you for listening and being a friend, Jully.  Although our contexts were different, I could relate to the overall experiences.  I truly had an enlightening night.

=============================

I want to expand on this and write about relationships but I don't know how.  I have pockets of thoughts that need connections, as well as people to consider, before going further.  Because it will make me feel less clouded, I know that writing it out won't serve much significance to anyone other than myself.  A part of me hopes that someone important will act upon what I'm thinking. 
😑 blah  |  ♫ Portishead - Silence
Link

Mune's Birthday Party

Feb. 6th, 2011   11:47 PM
Deoge picked me up at 4pm and as we drove there I got to know him a little better. As we were parking the car we bumped into Mune, Howiepup and Zets ~ they'd just come back from food and buying supplies (they'd be shopping for gifts too ~ Zets got Mune some baby supplies and a sleeper!  He's a caretaker!) At their apartment, we unloaded everything, met James for a second time, and then helped Zets in the kitchen.  I chopped up garlic and hot peppers while getting to know him better ~ he's so much fun!  He made shrimp and spicy basmaty rice.   We watched Mythbusters while cuddling, eating, and talking.  It got pretty hot and heavy! Kissed Zets and we tongued quite a bit.  He bits too.  But we were mostly just being a tease. Arinmal arrived!  He drove his racing car which is no bueno cause the tires can't handle the snow! Arinmal, Zets and I went to LCBO ~ Howie and Deoge went too. In the parking lot we bumped into Pyhrra and Dusk ~ they didn't seem to want to get to know me.    Everyone but Howie and I bought alcohol ~ Zets bought a huge case of Smirnoff cooler which I bummed off of for the night.  Back at the party a lot of finger foods were being made, I had a couple of drinks, Babs, Dar, Kandi, Raver, Fulwer, Leum, and maybe a few other people showed up. Zets got into suit first.  I followed with Babs' help.  Then Mune and Howie.  We had a really fun time but I sweat like a mofo!  Babs left while we were suiting ~ she was lazy and didn't bring hers. Zets and I got pretty kinky in suit and everyone was probably watching ~ pictures were being taken too .___. We went outside on the balcony for air too and even spotted some on lookers.  When I got out of suit my divesuit was drenched and so was my underwear!  Got to know Howie better ~ his kinks include diapers. Pyrrah and Zets jerked me off while being a tease.  Pyrrah also bites ~ left a hicky on my neck. - Pyrrah also gave me a blow job Dusk later got in suit, but was mostly alone.  His suit is really cool. Cheese cake with cherry jam. Took a few puffs of the joint with Raver, Dar, and some others ~ but was really cold outside. Arinmal, Zets, and Mune had sex in the other room.  (Deoge, Howie, Mune, Raver, James, Zets, Pyrrah, Dusk, Dar, Kandi, Babs, Leum, Matt/Fulwer,
Link

'Toronto Ageplay Group' Monthly Munch! - February 19th

Feb. 6th, 2011   4:32 PM

(cross-post)

In and around Toronto there are a lot more ageplayers, ABDLs and babyfurs than you might think!  Wouldn’t it be fun and exciting to hangout with like minded people ~ make some new friends or see familiar ones?  Come join us in a safe and friendly environment over dinner!

WHEN:    The munches take place every 3rd Saturday of each month. The next munch date is: February 19th

TIME:    Munches take place at 7:00pm until whenever.

WHERE:    The Gov’nor Pub, located at 401 Bloor Street East, just west of Sherbourne subway station.

WEBSITE:   http://www.govnorpub.com/

DIRECTIONS:    The best way to The Gov’nor Pub would be to take the subway to Sherbourne subway station on the Bloor-Danforth line, and walk from there.

If you need public transit information, check out http://www.ttc.ca, http://www.gotransit.ca, or http://www.viarail.ca.

For those who are driving, have a look at Google Maps: Click here!

If you need specific directions from a particular location, please email me: discourse-dumpling@hotmail.com

PARKING:   There is a public parking lot directly behind The Gov’nor Pub (enter off of Selby Street) and costs $6.00. Note: The parking ticket machine takes twoonies (Canadian $2.00 coin) only, so be sure to bring 3 of them along.

Additionally, there is metered street parking is available along Bloor and surrounding streets. There is a large "Green P" parking garage near the corner of Yonge St. and Charles Street East: http://www.greenp.com/tpa/parkinglocator/CarParks/Downtown%20Fringe/1/1.jsp

HOW TO FIND US:   The munch organizers looks like this ~ short, Asian and wears a spunky hat.  Our table is usually at the back, in the raised level area, near the projector screen. 

The munch organizers will generally be there a few minutes early to request a group of tables be set aside as seating is on a first-come, first-served basis. That being said, it is important to be at the restaurant as close to 7:00pm as possible to ensure that the group is seated together. Those who come later might not be seated at the group table, depending on how busy the restaurant is. (You might be able to request a table near ours as an alternative.)

IMPORTANT NOTE:    This is a *public place* so due discretion is warranted. (i.e. don't come wearing obvious diapers, bondage gear, etc. and don't make a scene. :-)

The munches are NOT a "pickup event" and are NON-SEXUAL in nature. This goes for the munch itself and anywhere the group may go afterwards. (It's not a night for "getting some".) :-)

See you there!

CONTACT INFO:   Have more questions? Feel free to email me: discourse-dumpling@hotmail.com

🐇 bouncy  |  ♫ The Lion King - Hakuna Matata
Link

"So how was your weekend?"

Feb. 1st, 2011   2:45 AM
This month feels like it has gone by fast!  When I look back on the things I've done so far this year, it does seem to justify this feeling though =P  So....what did I do this weekend?  I find it kinda funny that I get asked this sometimes by people who read my blog ~ they want me to elaborate more when i donno how else to! D:  I purge all my thoughts and feelings from what I experience into writing so finding other ways to convey what's been written sometimes makes me go blank o.o"

Anywhoozle, this weekend has been pretty tame compared to most others.  I get the feeling people come to expect something crazy-awesome-hella-fun-time, but I had a physically exhausting work week and needed to mellow out.  I don't really think it helped out that I played squash on Friday night with Dodge! XD  I was so brain dead afterwards that it felt like I wasted our time together while simmering at Tim Hortons .___."  I am not capable of holding a conversation when my limbs feel like noodles ~ but it was really peaceful in a sense =3

On Saturday I loafed at home a lot.  I didn't get much sleep in the morning due to an early dentist appointment ~ it left my gums a bit achy and despite trying to nap during the day, the feeling was too distracting.  It probably would have been a good excuse to fix myself a stiff drink; Comrade Smirnoff is displeased >>" 

On Sunday, at three in the morning, I watched the Australian Open men's finals between Novak Djokovic (3) and Andy Murray (5).  The first set had some top notch tennis, but it was apparent Murray's spark just wasn't bright enough.  He choked as usual during big events, which is really disappointing since he's such an amazing player.  Djokovic creamed him in straight sets 6-4 6-2 6-3.  The next tournament to get excited for?  Probably not until March during the Masters 1000 series and then the French Open in May. 

During the day I went out and saw "Somewhere" ~ it's directed and written by Sofia Coppola, who also did "Lost in Translation", my absolute favourite movie.  The two movies share a lot of similarities, but the latter is still the better.  Sofia uses a lot of silence and still life, which can be boring but it allows you to think about what isn't going on.  For example, interpreting a characters life.  In the silence you can get a feel of what's troubling a character without needing a scene to explicitly show it.  It's way existential and it can be a huge hit or miss for a lot of viewers.  I'm pretty okay that the movie doesn't have a high rating even though I really enjoyed it ^-^. 

Also...during the evening I got to romp around in a teddy bear sleeper =3

It was waaaaaaay to adorable not to show off!
💤 sleepy  |  ♫ Lil Wayne (ft. Cory Gunz) – 6 Foot 7 Foot'
Link

Sunday with Daddy Josh

Jan. 30th, 2011   11:47 PM
On Sunday, after a bit of late txt msging suspense (which pissed me off cause I don't like last minute planning),  I met up with Josh at Bloor Station.  We went to several toy stores ~ two underground and two in Yorkville.  He bought this Squirrmles thing which was kinda fun but ultimately a waste of money.  We each took one to play with =P.  There were some really awesome animal stacking blocks that I want because they look really well made!  I also need to get something for Mune's birthday ~ a trip to Rolo gave me some ideas.  Perhaps a germs plushie haha!  (while at Rolo, I took out my paci to see if it would fit in the holder, but it didn't) Afterwards he recieved a few calls from his sales agent saying he couldn't come home till later, we went to West 7 for coffee.  We each had big bowls of latte and shared a slice of caramel cheese cake.  We talked about a lot of things but sometimes he seemed distracted like he had a lot on his mind.  I got the bill even though he wanted to. At 4:20pm we went to Varsity and watched "Somewhere" ~ he picked up the tab plus some munches (popcorn, soda, and a Kindersurprise!).  We sat at the very back and I sucked on my pacifier throughout the movie.  Occasionally I would rest my head on his shoulder.  He would sometimes ruffle my head, but he seemed into the movie, more so than me heh.  As we were leaving the theater my diaper was really really wet and I knew I still had to go!  I wonder if he noticed my discomfort when he walked behind me. We took a cab back to his place ~ again, he paid.  We stopped at a store for juice, again, he paid.  I love his suite!  It has stairs leading to the roof with a wicked view of the south end of the city.  The old CBC warehouse turn condo has really big doors making me feel little.  His suite is very well furnished since he designed it himself ~ but it's got a lot of open space and emptiness...kinda sad when you're all alone.  He gave me a quick tour and I asked if he could change me.  He didn't seem to hesitate.  He changed me into the Cushies I brought alone, while digging out some wipes of his own. We cuddled on his sofa while listening to some sleepy music ~ I cuddled two plushies he brought out too.  It was really really relaxing but in his embrace.  After a little bit we went back into his room and he dug out a TON of ageplay gear ~ onesies, shortalls, sleepers, bibs, booties, blankets, plushies, rompers, etc.  Most of it was for Jason, his old boyfriend and baby boy.  He dug out some play mats and plushie bowling toys.  After another diaper change I romped around in a teddy bear sleeper.  It was really hot after a while, but I loved it sooo much.  It's made by Privatina ~ really expensive stuff.  We took pics hehe.  After a bit more lounging, talking and relaxing, we ordered pizza from Pizza Hut.  He put a baby Mickey bib on me, but I ate very cleanly.  I wonder if he wanted me to be messy ~ it probably would have pleased him.  After eating I watched him mix some muic while dancing a bit.  And then we cuddled and talked about life some more.  He changed my diaper one as time before walking me to the bus stop.  I got home by midnight.   I feel deeply indebted to him.  He spoiled me and paid for a lot of things.  He has hid away so much amazing ageplay stuff that's it's incredibly surprising.  If I were to be his little boy, I think he'd pamper me with everything he has.  I don't know he I'd feel about that.  I don't know what I could ever possibly give back as repayment.   He asked that I don't blog about this so I'm writing it privately for myself only. 
Link

Plans: Ageplay Munch and Furnal Equinox

Jan. 27th, 2011   11:52 PM
I'm pretty behind on writing about my day-to-days, but if you follow my Twitter you'll know what I've been up to! ^-^

On Tuesday after work, Boyricky, Nebulus64 and I had dinner at The Gov'nor Pub (warning: website is fail).  It's located where the Groundhog Pub use to be,  the old ageplay munch location.  Funny enough it still has the old restaurant sign on the back door! =P  They really didn't put any effort in changing the place other than the menu.  The reason we went was to scope out a new location for the monthly munch.  The food was good and cheaper than the Blake House, the current munch location.  Plus there's less mood lighting, a bit more open space, and a nice sense of familiarity.  So the next munch - third Saturday of February - will be held at The Gov'nor Pub at 7pm!  Newcomers are welcomed with open arms ~ I know that the munch practically runs by itself because of the regulars, but it's always awesome seeing new faces! :D

I'll make a more constructed entry with all the information cross-posted on RUP, FetLife, LJ, DD, FA, etc two weeks prior to the dinner :3  Orca has passed the torch of hosting the munch to me after years and years, but I don't want to make it a big deal.  I'm getting a lot of help and support from friends within the local community and I feel that that is what it's all about.  We work together so that we can enjoy it together! :D

(After the dinner on Tuesday I hung out at Ricky's place.  We got into some comfy crinkles and watched President Obama's State of the Union.  It was hilariously fustrating watching John Boehner, the Speaker of the House of Representatives, show his lack of support =P.  Good times though ~ it was a much needed distraction from my work week!)

==============================================

I booked a room for 'Furnal Equinox' ~ it's a spiffy single king bed corner room with a sofa bed.  Quite ideal for holding babyfur hangouts me thinks.  I donno how many people the room can actually fit, but it was cheaper than the con blocks room rate! XD  I still need to plan this stuff with my roommates (they're probably reading this anyways, so lets talk about it!), but some of my ideal room activities would include:

- milk and cookies meet on Friday night (I know where to get good cookies for cheap!)
- Saturday morning cartoons (everyone would still probably be asleep)
- board game competition with prizes (name that classic tv show tune comes to mind)
- pjs and movies night
- ice cream social
- daily doors open for for snacks and relaxing

These are just some of the things I'd like to do, but may not get around to since it requires planning, commitment, and responsibilities.  Plus, it heavily depends on the con events/panels schedule and the availability of those watching over the room.  I definitely don't want to be in the room all day ~ I wanna fursuit, hangout with friends I don't see often enough, swim and enjoy the con inside and out! XD  We'll see how things play out in the following weeks and I'll keep the posts current =3. 
🐇 bouncy  |  ♫ Jamiroquai - Virtual Insanity
Link

ABDL weekend in Niagara Falls!

Jan. 27th, 2011   12:28 AM
I haven’t had the time to sit down and write anything concrete lately, but I’m glad I have a few minutes right now before I get distracted again!  Last weekend I went to Niagara Falls with some ABDL friends and had an incredibly fun time :D

The trip took off after Blondboy, Cameron and I met up at Kipling Station.  For most of the way there we passed the time with good laughs, while occasionally distracting Cameron – who was driving – with our plushies and “are we there yet?” *poke poke*  Each time we asked he would add two hours to the base time ~ I think we ended at 17 hours o__o.  Luckily it didn’t take THAT long and we rolled into town by noon.  While Cameron parked the car, Blondboy and I stayed in our room unloading the luggage.  We were super quick so that we could build a fort out of blankets, pillows and chairs.  We hid inside until Cameron came in and then ambushed him with balled up towels and socks!  It was so awesome! O3O  Not long after, while passing the time with cartoons, BaBySB1 showed up and we gave him the same welcoming ~ what followed was an epic pillow fight XD

    

Once we had settled in we decided to hit up the Fallsview Indoor Waterpark, which was connected to the hotel.  While making our way there (and throughout the park) everyone stared at us because Blondboy was in a smexy speedo! O3O  But we didn’t care ~ it was so much fun!  We went down all the tube, mat and body slides, bounced around in the wave pool, and relaxed in the hot tub ^-^.  For some of the slides two people can share a tube; because the heaviest sits in the back it gets crazy once you spin and go backwards down the slide shaped like a toilet bowl!  And on one of the body slides it dips on such a sharp angle you catch air for two seconds; it was scary but so much awesome! \o.o/  Occasionally the waterpark would do birthday or missing people announcements over the sound system ~ we abused it so badly and pulled a few hilarious pranks on each other! XD  The only bummer was that the outdoor heated pool wasn’t open; it would have been so cool to swim while it was snowing. 

When we had enough of the waterpark we headed back to our room to get dressed and into some comfy crinkles.  We poke fun at our ethnicities every so often, one thing led to another and Blondboy ended up covering me in baby powder so that I could be whiter! XD  After grabbing a quick bite to eat we headed for Casino Niagara.  I gave up within a minute of playing the slots and losing $5.  SB won $120 off the slots after only two tries and Cameron won $60 after working the blackjack tables D:  While they gambled I got to know Blondboy better, which was way awesome since I don’t see him very often despite living in the same city =P.  We made reservations to a fancy restaurant for later in the evening because our hotel gave us $40 off; to pass the time we relaxing in our room, lounging in baby clothes, colouring, cuddling and watching SpongeBob SquarePants.  When it was time, we all wore onesies underneath our grown-up clothes ~ sheep prints from SB’s collar peaked out which was pretty cute X3. 

We ate at Massimo Capra’s Rainbow Room Fallsview Restaurant.  The food was tasty and the price was expectedly above average.  I would’ve been super happy with eating pizza instead but dinning out is one of those things you just do to enjoy the “experience” I guess =\.  The service was really friendly but very slow though, so we found ways to speed it up XD.  Throughout the day all of us except Cameron picked an animal noise to randomly and shamelessly do for fun.  SB had a pterodactyl call, Blondboy had a monkey holler, and I mooed like a cow.  Cameron mostly just signed (lol).  I’m sure the people at the restaurant wanted us gone quicker when we made our silly noises ~ win!  Plus, candles and pepper shakers are fun to play with =P.  At night we went out on the tourist strip and had a fun time.  We first went to ‘Nightmares Fear Factory’, which is a pitch dark haunted house that you get through my following tiny red lights.  Along the way a whole bunch of scary things pop out! OwO  Then we played glow in the dark mini putt at ‘Galaxy Golf’ ~ we took a bunch of pictures with their silly props.  If it weren’t for the other people we would have had some glowy diaper pics too! XD

    

At around midnight, before retiring back to our room to get comfy, we bought some snacks.  We cuddled up and watched ‘Despicable Me’ ~ it amazes me that they hadn’t seen it yet so I demanded we order it out of all the other movies listed >:3  Awesome movie is awesome.  But by the end of it my diaper had sprung a leak so no one ended up sleeping on it .___.”  My bad.  Luckily the room had two queens and a bunk bed!  I shared the top bunk with Blondboy, SB on the bottom and Cameron on the queen =3.  Bed time at around 3am.

In the morning we had a slow start sharing the bathroom while packing up our stuff for check-out.  We ate breakfast at ‘Perkins Family Restaurant & Bakery’ because the hotel gave us 10% off.  It was yummy and the service was friendly, but that didn’t stop us from making weird noises and building towers of coffee creamers! XD  Soon after finishing we went to the waterpark and did EVERYTHING again ~ slides, wave pool, hot tub, pranks, and even poked around the kids area =P.  They have this gianormous bucket high up that water pours into.  The bucket sways as it gets full and eventually topples over; standing underneath and getting splashed by the water is such a rush!  After our fill of fun, we got dressed and had ice cream \o.o/  It was really refreshing since it was humid indoors and the waterpark was tropically.  We went into the casino one last time where I lost another $5 to the slots ~ I think SB and Cameron had similar bad luck. 



By late afternoon before leaving Niagara Falls, we got a quick bite to eat at McDonald’s.  Blondboy and I got Happy Meals and the included toy was a monster truck with stickers! :D  Next door was a Value Village so we browsed around and found some amazing goodies.  I bought three Baby Disney removable border stickers, two Pooh and Tigger pillow cases, and a SpongeBob SquarePants wallet! O3O  They also had two shortalls; I couldn’t fit into either but Blondboy was lucky to snag one ~ I know he’ll spiffy it up and make it look awesome!  The ride home was filled with sleepiness and nostalgia.  All we listened to were song intros from cartoons of our childhood, seeing who could guess the show first.  It was a really nice way to end a short but fun filled trip =3. 

(Blondboy took all of the pictures at the mini putt so I'll try to get them ^-^)
🐇 bouncy  |  ♫ Ronettes - Be My Baby

Link

Living for the weekend...

Jan. 16th, 2011   10:27 PM
I've been feeling pretty Lazy McGee today ~ I slept for 13 hours throughout the night and day but my body still feels pretty feeble ^^;;  I'm sure I'll bounce right back after sleeping some more and going to work tomorrow =P  Till then I've had a really awesome weekend!

On Friday, work went by relatively smoothly and with lots of things to nom.  For once it felt like I was gaining rather than losing weight since my job is physically taxing daily.  On top of everything I normally eat in a day, I had a danish, muffin, and a slice of cake because the departments I deliver to had functions going on {o'.'o}  Oh wells, I burned off a bit of weight at night playing squash with Dodge.  He picked up the sport quite quickly so I'm look forward to getting in some more games whenever we can ^-^.  Any other locals interested?  I've got the court and raquets but finding people to play can be tricky :V

On Saturday, I enjoyed being little for most of the day =3.  In the afternoon for the littles' playdate, Greenbean picked up Seven and I, and we headed for the tobogganing hill.  It was crazy!  After nearly an hour of going up and down we were joined by Hugsandkisses, Kittycakes, SadisticBroccoli, Boyricky, and BabyMary ~ definitely a great crowd for having fun! :D  We had sleds that sit three, saucers, and a magic carpet...I need to get one so that I can toboggan whenever! X3  I hate the cold of winter but at least you can dress appropriately to have a fun time in the snow! 

     

When we were all tuckered out we headed back to Seven's place for hot chocolate, cupcakes, cartoons, colouring, and a lot of relaxing ~ BabyDroo joined in a little later.  Coming out of the cold and warming up in some crinkles, a sleeper, and a plushie is the best! ^-^  I love how this months cupcake design was a snowman and kudos to Greenbean for reading us a story :D  I probably could've fell asleep right there but some of us had the monthly ageplay munch to attend =P  Boyricky, BabyDroo and I headed out for that in the evening, not before making a pit stop at Ricky's place for a drink ~ switching from sugar high to alcohol mode! \o.o/ 

Way fewer people showed up to the munch than I'd thought would; there was the three of us and Nebulus64.  Oh wells, it was laid back and we had some good laughs.  (NHL > NFL mister gay waiter =P)  A change of location might be in order for future munches, so be on the lookout for more info if you're planning to attend!  Afterwards we all headed back to Ricky's place for some drinks and to goof around online searching for videos.  Great way to end an exhausting but fun filled day!  As always, thanks for the lift home Neb :D
😎 relaxed  |  ♫ Liza Minnelli and Joel Grey (from Cabaret) - Money

Random stuff update...

Jan. 13th, 2011   9:42 PM

The new (old but never updated) zodiac constellations - Ophiuchus - is pretty wtf.  This article does a good job briefing the dillio. 

Capricorn: Jan. 20-Feb. 16.
Aquarius: Feb. 16-March 11.
Pisces: March 11-April 18.
Aries: April 18-May 13.
Taurus: May 13-June 21.
Gemini: June 21-July 20.
Cancer: July 20-Aug. 10.
Leo: Aug. 10-Sept. 16.
Virgo: Sept. 16-Oct. 30.
Libra: Oct. 30-Nov. 23.
Scorpio: Nov. 23-29.
Ophiuchus: Nov. 29-Dec. 17.
Sagittarius: Dec. 17-Jan. 20.

So I'm no longer a libra, but a Virgo...this feels funny even though there is no logic to it.

XD  I blame  for putting dollar signs in my head.  Since he knows soccer and football best, this week I'll bet on Blackpool, Patriots, and Bears.  And since my co-workers know a lot about hockey, I'll ask them for tips on who to bet on =P  If this becomes a bad habit down the road, feel free to beat my arse, anyone.

Smiling silently...

Jan. 9th, 2011   11:42 PM
"Silence doesn't have to be awkward.  There is such a thing as comfortable silence."

I've never really considered that until this weekend.  The more I think about it the less I feel the need to make conversation around close friends.  Let things take it's course and if there's a break in discussion just allow yourself to processes things you otherwise wouldn't ~ it enriches the moment.

==================================================

On Friday night I watched 'Tron: Legacy' with Nebulus64 at the tiniest and most oddly decorated theater I've ever been in, which was the Rainbow Cinemas Market Square.  I'm satisfied with the experience considering how little I paid ~ plus I'm a sucker for buttery popcorn.  The movie itself was pretty good, but not great.  It was predictable and the plot didn't really present anything new or mind blowing.  It kinda reminded me of a tamer and less thought out  'Inception'.  The visuals were of course spectacular and the acting was fun to follow.  Overall it was good entertainment!  

On Saturday I was a bit bummed my afternoon plans with Diesel and Mune fell through, but glad that I rebounded by having an enlightening night with Dodge.  We had dinner at Jack Astor's and simmered over coffee at Tim Hortons.  In a way, it didn't feel like he was ever that far gone - which I find uncomfortable since I invested a lot of thought and feeling when he really was.  But that's behind me now.  Even though we talked about many different things, I liked how catching up wasn't as important as moving forward. 

Even though the measure of one's success is subjective, I'd like to think a number of close friends - these two in particular - are successful with respect to their positions in life.  Sometimes I take for granted what I've learned from these people because I'm blinded by the need to have fun.  I'm very fortunate for the more mellow moments and comfortable silences ~ thank you.
🌀 satisfied  |  ♫ Our Lady Peace - Are You Sad?
Link

Tennis, food and awesome...

Jan. 9th, 2011   11:30 PM
The professional tennis tournament circuit is back in full swing!  Three tournaments have already gone by last week, each with really great results and all of them leading up to the first grand slam ~ the Australian Open.

Stanislas Wawrinka (21) won the Aircel Chennai Open.  Roger Federer (2) won the Qatar Exxonmobil Open ~ where the other top contenders was his rival Rafael Nadal (1).  The tournament I paid most attention to was the Brisbane International since my two favourite players, Robin Soderling (4) and Andy Roddick (8), made it into the finals.  It's hard to take sides when I love them both; Soderling won 6-3 7-5, but I'm really happy for them both! :D

==============================================

Winterlicious is in a few weeks but reservations begin January 13th!  I've already got some people I'd like to go with, but I'm always down with doing more ~ anyone wanna hit up a few restaurants?

'Pangaea' and 'Far Niente' are two that I'd like to try :3

==============================================

I love this website ~ '1000 Awesome Things'
🌿 calm  |  ♫ Mika Miko - Sex Jazz
Link

Writing for me and you ...

Jan. 9th, 2011   2:18 AM
Lately I’ve been finding it difficult to begin a blog entry.  I know what I want to say in the bulk of it but I don’t know how to set it up.  Sometimes I feel like writing casually as if I were talking, while other times it’s more formal and structured for things I feel that are important.  Sometimes I just don’t know how I feel with what I want to write about so I end up sitting in front of the computer in an awkward silence.  It can be frustrating because I’ve been told by several people not to change the way I write ~ to just continue being myself. Knowing that I have a particular audience, it’s harder to feel comfortable being as open as I was when I didn’t.  I’ve always known that there would be consequences to the way I blog, but I hardly expected that it would be of my self-consciousness.  I just figured a future employer might find it or I’d get trolled or something.  I love blogging and I don’t want to stop, but doing it is becoming increasingly complex.
🤔 contemplative  |  ♫ Cyhi Da Prynce - Ring Bellz

Random stuff update...

Jan. 5th, 2011   11:44 PM
Dude...tonights monthly furry dinner was the shiznit.  For me, these things always depend on who I sit around, so a shout out to Mr. Dad, HappyNickFox, Katy, PizzaCat, Spyatuplink, and Dodge for being aweosme :D  I really wish you could've made it Babs, but get well soon! *hugs*  (it's Alison's fault Canada lost 5-3 to Russia) ========================================== I know a descent number of furs heading to Further Confusion on the 13th to the 17th ~ kinda bummed that I can't make it.  I can only take two personal days for the entire year at work so I've really gotta use them wisely.  I really want to go to Furry Connection North again in April!  ========================================== I changed the theme of my blog to 'Winter Elephant' and I'm in the processes of taking down pictures from posts older than six months.  ========================================== I think I'd like to get back into reading books and writing ABDL short stories .___." ========================================== Things to look forward to: Jan 7             -    gonna watch Tron: Legacy with Nebulus64 Jan 15           -    littles playdate, ageplay munch and after party Jan 17           -    new episode of House MD Jan 22-23      -    Niagara Falls waterpark trip with local ABDLs Feb 3            -    Chinese New Year!  I think a commission is in order Feb 5            -    Mune's fursuit party for his birthday Keep the good times coming! ^-^

I am what I am...and I don't like green eggs and ham.

Jan. 3rd, 2011   11:47 PM
Today in the afternoon I met up with Daddy Josh; I dont think I've ever felt more glad to meet someone. 

When we were making plans the night before I wasn't entirely confident because the same opportunities fell through last year, so I didn't feel a need to set any high expectations.  I'm not bitter about it since we still occasionally talked and I got to understand his situation outside of the ABDL scene.  I also felt uncertain about not knowing what to expect from myself.  In a lot of ways, who he is reminded me of Matthew - another local daddy that I thought I had a thing going on with, but only lasted briefly.  I was really happy with what I had but I didn't want things to end similarly nor did I want to be sentimental towards someone I didn't know as well. 

But I couldn't help it.  I feel more like myself than ever when I'm with people such as them.  I love their insights on life because of their age and I'm fascinated by their bourgeois style of living.  But what really made Josh different than Matthew was his natural sense of nurture.  It was alluring and I wanted it without being impatient or needy. 

==================================================

We made plans to meet in front of the Toys R' Us at the Yonge Eglinton Centre since I wanted to spend the $30 gift card Dan Raccoon and Shiro got me (thanks again :D).  I thought I showed up early so I went into the Pharma Plus to buy baby powder (since Shoppers Drug Mart is a rip off) only to bump into him as I left ~ kinda caught me by surprise there! =P  It was really great finally putting a face on the person who's blog I really enjoy reading and it was awesome how casual we just hit it off.

For a store so big it's amazing how difficult it was to find ~ we had to ask security for help after wandering around {o'.'o}  It was totally worth the effort though because there was so many toys and things to look at inside.  Unfortunately it made deciding on what to get really difficult!  I had narrowed down my choice to either something 'Cars' or 'Transformers' related, or an adorable giraffe plushie that included a blankie.  I went with the plushie cause I'm a sucker for soft things XD.  I even had enough money to get a tiny elephant plush that plays music. 


(We named the giraffe Noodle Macchiato)

I stuck'em in my knapsack with its head popping out, I'm sure I got some looks hehe.  From there we went to BMV across the street and browsed through some books.  I love their selection of comic books, graphic novels, and mangas.  We spent the bulk of the afternoon in the cafe section at the Pickle Barrel.  He bought me a chocolate cupcake with rainbow sprinkles and a chocolate macchiato ~ so delish ^-^.  We talked about everything and I feel as though I've gotten to know him and myself a lot better.  Not often do I have such thought provoking conversations, so it was a refreshing change.  We called it a night after browsing through Indigo; I was like (O___O) when he bought $80 worth of books. 
🌀 ecstatic  |  ♫ Outasight - Here We Go Again
Link

A new day, a new year...

Jan. 3rd, 2011   12:32 PM
(I haven’t been in the writing mood lately, but I had to get this down or else I’d forget! >.o) On Friday I had a really easy day at work since there were no stationeries for me to deliver and a lot of the business-types were on holiday ^-^.  Most of the time I was sitting at the front desk joking around with Maria, a co-worker I’m closest with because we were hired at the same time.  Even though it was only a half day for me, I stuck around town and hung out at Eaton Centre.  At around 5pm I met up with Nebulus64 for a few drinks and a lot of laughs at the Amsterdam Bicycle Club.  We watched Sweden beat Canada 6-5 in a shootout at the World Juniors :C.  After getting a quick bite to eat he saw me off at the GO Bus Terminal where I then headed for Brampton.  (I love how the GO was free after 7pm compared to midnight with the TTC!) I had planned to sober up on the bus since I expected a dry night to follow ~ I don’t need alcohol to have a good time, but it being a celebration so I was satisfied with how I was feeling.  So it came as a surprise to see booze in stock when I got to Dan Raccoon and Shiro’s place =P.  While Shiro was at work, Dan and I watched ‘Titanic’ over a beer and then cuddled to a couple episodes of Family Guy.  At around 11pm we picked up Shiro and then headed downtown to see the fireworks \o.o/  Actually, they were pretty lame compared to the earlier showing at 9pm.  I guess they had hoped everyone would be either too high or drunk to enjoy it =\  Anywhoo, after weaving around the crowds and picking up some snacks, we headed for home.  We made a toast to the New Year – whiskey and coke in a sippy cup for Dan and me – before spending the rest of the night relaxing.  Didn’t get to bed until 3am! :V On Saturday, I woke up at around 9:30am and spent the morning watching Family Guy episodes with Dan while Shiro slept.  Funny now that I think about it, it was kinda like how I use to watch Saturday morning cartoons! :D  The two of use went out for a quick bite at Tim Hortons before heading back to watch more episodes.  We must’ve watched a total of ten episodes from the volume seven DVDs (o__o).  It was a bummer having to go home in the afternoon because I had a family dinner to attend.  But I’m glad I ended a year and began a new one with some great friends and good times! ^-^
🐇 bouncy  |  ♫ Drake (ft. Alicia Keys) - Fireworks
Link

2010 Year In Review

Dec. 31st, 2010   3:31 PM

How should I begin this?

The first thing that comes to mind about 2010 is the people – I met many.  I made a lot of new friends as well as strengthened existing ones.  But when I think about all of these people, I can’t help feel that the most relevant ones were the two that I lost. 

To you I reflect…
We've only known each other for a year, but our friendship felt much longer than that.  “I grew up too quickly” is how you described yourself, but for someone who refuses to grow up, I really admire you.  Within a community of very different people, you stood out as the most normal person I knew.  I guess it made me forget that the only normal people are the one’s you don’t know very well, and so I didn’t think that things would fall apart.  I just wish I could have done more. 

And to you I reflect…
Even though we were together for a short period, I’d never felt more happy.  You showed me a different kind of love and for that I must thank.  But when it ended, I knew right away that it was irreplaceable.  I needed you.  You were important to me but I didn’t feel important to you.  I don’t think I’ve felt more hurt.  And so, if you love something, let it go. 

I lost two very close friends for reasons beyond my control and understanding.  I felt helpless knowing that I made the effort to re-establish what we had but getting no reply.  I don’t want it to end this way and I don’t want to give up not knowing anything. 

===============================================

Despite the two strongly sentimental downfalls of the year, I am not pessimistic.  2010 was my most memorable and enjoyable year!
  • I commissioned a partial fursuit in March
  • I decked out my wardrobe that better reflects my personality
  • I went to my first local furry convention, followed by travelling to four others with friends
  • I graduated from university in the summer
  • I started my first permanent full time job three months ago
  • I commissioned a full fursuit in November
  • I told my parents I'm a furry
  • I attended numerous furry and ageplay events throughout the year
  • I found happiness in giving back to society
To those who've been a part of this incredible year, thank you very much. 
🤔 contemplative  |  ♫ Sondre Lerche - Goodnight
Link

What's been happening...

Dec. 28th, 2010   11:22 PM
This past Christmas weekend and the two days of no work that followed was filled with vast amounts of boredom.  Traditionally I spend time with my folks but they went down to the states ~ I'm not bitter about it.  I am cranky at my older brother though.  In general he goes out more than I do, but we happen to decide to stay home all holiday...like what the hell? =.=** 

Anywhoo, I'm a bit bummed that nothing really peaked my Boxing Day sales interest.  From NCIX, I bought a relatively inexpensive headset to replace the one I sat on (XD), as well as an 8gb MicroSDHC (for only $10!).  I've been drooling over getting a funky new watch, so instead of just settling with one I bought four from Slap Watch!  (technically I just got the white and orange watch plus a black and blue band) 

I want a new wallet but I don't know which to get.  'FriendsWithYou - Rainbow City' or 'So So Happy - Friends' ?  Both are designed by Poketo; I can get the first one locally at Magic Pony while the latter needs to be shipped (which will cost $20!)  I also want a new winter jacket and boots before it really begins to get cold (x_x")

Lately I've been thinking about how I can do more for those who don't have it so well.  For the past three months I've been donating money and it's beginning to feel too basic ~ like an easy way out.  Committing time and energy into something is far more worthy but I don't think I've gotten to that level where it's feasible just yet, but someday it will be.  I'm going to start donating my blood on a regular basis.  (cookies and juice beware!)

Countdown to New Years...

Crazy random happenstance ~ I delivered a bag of apples today.  It almost ranks as weird as the time I delivered a box of party clappers =S.  These weren't no ordinary apples though...they were freakin' huge!  Like the size of my face gianormous!  (okay, maybe just bigger than my fist, but it totally blew my mind since they were organic)

Blah ~ I thought I'd be spending Christmas Eve and Christmas with my parents, but apparently they recently made plans to go skiing in New York tomorrow till Monday >>"  So I have absolutely no plans for the entire weekend, as well as Monday and Tuesday because I don't have work.  That sucks.  Anyone got any suggestions?

Time is ticking...

Dec. 22nd, 2010   11:23 PM
Today I kept on thinking it was Thursday ~ everytime someone reminded me that it wasn't, a small piece of me died on the inside lol.  I just want this week to be over with because I've been so bored at work due to the declining load of deliveries.  I complain when there is too much to do and I complain when there is too little >>"  I'm so hard to please heh.

Christmas is in two days, but I'm not super excited because I have zero plans for my four day weekend.  Traditionally I spend time with family - which I will do - but I'd really like to go out after Saturday.  Someone gimme some plans! :V  Wanna go shopping?  Wanna watch Tron?  Or perhaps Black Swan?  (I watched 'Billy Elliot' the other night and was like ballet kicks ass!)  You know how to reach me!

Project For Awesome 2010

Dec. 20th, 2010   12:00 AM
If you watch as much YouTube as I do, I hope you've noticed a bunch of P4A videos being uploaded.  For one day, December 17, YouTube users post videos promoting a charity of their choosing; usually the more popular users hold raffles to generate money for donation.  Currently the raffle funds have raised $97,078.89 for a bunch of different charities which is just incredible!  A really huge kudos to the vlogbrothers - Hank and John Green - for organizing this and various other YouTube community events ~ they've accomplished so much and continue to do so.  It is truly inspiring.


Do the right thing.  Do more. 
😄 cheerful  |  ♫ Theophilus London - Aquamilitia

Fun weekend was fun!

Dec. 19th, 2010   8:47 PM
The furry dinner on Friday was much better than I expected.  I was a bit bummed knowing ahead of time that Raverfox, Mune, HappyNickFox, and Mr. Dad weren't going to be there.  They're always a lot of fun to hang out with but luckily Babs made up for it and we had a lot to laugh about =3. 

Ms. Seven's potluck and playdate on Saturday was definitely good times.  It was so great to see some old faces and make some new friends.  I had a messy fun time helping in the kitchen with the kid friendly menu and filling people up on sugar ~ fresh gingerbread cookies are the shiznit! o.o  I had to take off after watching "How the Grinch Stole Christmas!" (1966) and half of "How to Train Your Dragon" (2010). 

The monthly ageplay munch in the evening was really cozy.  The small turnout due to the holidays was expected but being among good friends was what it was all about.  My sugar rush tapered off so I switched to alcohol! :D  While being shuffled to another sitting area in the restaurant, we watched the Canucks rape the Maple Leafs 4-1.  Afterwards Boyricky had an afterparty at his place ~ very chill and relaxing as usual.  It was a lot of fun making another gianormous structure on wheels out of building blocks!


BaBySB1, Boyricky and me with our creation behind us!

Today, Sunday, I just slept like a mofo because I didn't get home until 3am.  I re-watched "Watchmen" (2009) for like the tenth time ~ awesome as always.  I'm gonna watch "Tron" (1982) in a little bit before calling it a night =P.

Weekend plans...

Dec. 16th, 2010   10:57 PM
Today felt like a Friday, but too bad it wasn't >.<**  It mainly had to do with the department potluck lunch and gift exchange; definitely good times ^-^.  There was soooooo much food that many of us saved some for tomorrows lunch.  I brought salad fresh from the super market ~ I stuck it in a bowl that I bought at the dollar store to make it look homemade! =P 

Although I've only been with the company for a little over two months and don't know the people extremely well, I had fun with the gift exchange.  The other day, a co-worker and I went to Rolo (my favourite gift shop in town) and bought some gifts.  The person I was giving to has a bushy caterpillar mustache, so I got him a "fancy mustache set"!  He died of laughter when he recieved it so I'm totally happy about that :D  Me and several other people recieved alcohol from the exchange ~ I've got a bottle of brandy that I'm thinking of mixing with eggnog!

I hope tomorrow will be an easy day since today I got bogged down by huge orders for delivery.  After work I'll probably shop around downtown and kill some time before going to the furry Spaghetti Factory dinner event.  I didn't go to this months usual furry dinner (every first Wednesday of the month) due to a lack of attendence among people I wanted to see, so tomorrow will make up for that =3 

Then on Saturday, Ms. Seven is hosting a littles' playdate and holiday potluck lunch!  I'm bringing mac and cheese cause it's delicious and easy to make =P  I think the turnout will be a bit small but it'll be among folks I'm use to seeing, so good times ensured ~ I always have a fun time at her events!  Later at night I'll be going to the monthly ageplay munch where hopefully a few more familiar faces will spring up, it's hard to say since it's the holidays though. 

I think I'm going to be fairly plump from eating and drinking this weekend, so Sunday will be my day to rest and recover.  Come Monday, it'll be just a few more days till Christmas!  I have the Monday and Tuesday off afterwards, but I don't plan that far ahead so who knows that'll happen.

My weekend...

Dec. 12th, 2010   10:03 PM
I had a fun and relaxing weekend despite the horrid work week that led up to it.

On Friday night I played squash with Stephanie.  I really enjoy the sport and I use to practice alone as part of my routine while working out, but I haven't hit the gym in months.  Finding a partner to play with is also difficult, especially one that's at the same level as me.  I'm still a beginner, but Steph was way more noob heh, so I didn't break a sweat and the challenge wasn't there.  Oh well, she can only and I'm only playing for the fun of it ^-^.  Hopefully we'll get to play one or two more times before she travels to Australia for a month ~ me so envious!

She also had my Threadless tees so I was super excited to finally get them!  And thanks for the Christmas present ~ I got red mittens!  I wanted them cause they were pretty trendy, but I lost the right one today so I'm really heart broken :*(  I'm sorry.  

My body felt really broken on Saturday and unfortunately I didn't get to sleep in as much as I wanted.  I had dim sum for lunch with my family and then caught a GO bus for Brampton.  Dan Raccoon and Shiro invited me up for the weekend so I was totally down with that.  I got there in the afternoon but we didn't hang around for too long because we had an early dinner reservation for Mandarin buffet.  They had lobster - but the meat was mixed with some sort of potato stuffing and then refilled into the shell - it was interesting.  I was surprised to see rainbow rolls in the sushi section because it contained more fish than rice.  The roast beef (rare) impressed me and crab legs never fail.  I lasted six rounds before feeling ready to explode XD.  It's good to feel like a fatty sometimes hehe.

Later in the night we watched "Twas the Night Before Christmas" (1974 cartoon) and "Polar Express".  Afterwards we played some "Street Fighter II - The World Warrior", "Streets of Rage", and "Super Smash Bros Brawl".  Didn't sleep till around 2:30am and got up at 9am o__O.  Shiro had to work at noon so we 

- breakfast nom
- grocery shoppping
- watched how to train your dragon and grown ups - cuddled dan

Link

Yippy!
- Backpack has arrived!  It's a wee bit smaller than I envisioned, but I'm a small guy so it's not too bad heh.
- I bought some Christmas presents for other people today.  Strangely, I tend to buy things that I'd want to have but not necessarily want ~ they're just a lot of fun to have!  Rolo made a dent in my wallet :V

'Ten Days' meme: Day Ten

Dec. 6th, 2010   11:26 PM
Having finished this meme - with the exception of day six - and reading over everything, I see myself and feel weary about being open.  I know it isn't everything there is to know about me, but I hope others are aware of that as well.  Don't judge me until you've gotten to know me in depth and in person ~ I'm sure you'd appreciate the same gesture as well :)

'Ten Days' Meme

Day One: Ten things you want to say to ten different people right now.
Day Two: Nine things about yourself.
Day Three: Eight ways to win your heart.
Day Four: Seven things that cross your mind a lot.
Day Five: Six things you wish you’d never done.
Day Six: Five people who mean a lot.
Day Seven: Four turn offs.
Day Eight: Three turn ons.
Day Nine: Two smileys that describe your life right now.
Day Ten: One confession

___________________________________________________________


Day Ten: One confession.

1)  I have a very deep desire to be someone important.  I don't necessarily mean important like someone who holds a reputable position in society, although being popular is a quality that seems appealing to me.  Instead, I've always wanted to be in someone's thoughts often because I am important to them.  What would define my importance isn't for me to say, but the idea of being just a somebody isn't enough for me.  I want to stand out, but I know that there is a lot that I don't do enough of to warrent the impression.

I don't think I would ever have wanted this so badly had I not had it before.  For a brief period I recieved this feeling of importance from somebody and it made me really happy to be acknowledged, but I realized that it was a very needy desire.  I think, because of that, it was partly my fault that things didn't progress any further and eventually dissolved. 

Even though a lot of the time I don't feel important and the idea of being a nobody frightens me, I do have self-worth and I know there are people who care about me.  I am not self-loathing and dysfunctional.
😶 blank  |  ♫ Jadakiss - Feel Me
Link

Shopping, good music, and ageplay.

Dec. 5th, 2010   10:22 PM
Having put a lot of thought into doing the 'Ten Days' meme, I haven't written anything about my day-to-day all week! 

I've been spoiling myself lately, but only because I've decided not to put away half my pay cheques into savings for this month.  My two new Tokidoki hats arrived on Friday and thankfully they fit like a glove ~ I love'em so much!  I'm still waiting for my backpack but it should be arriving soon since it's been nearly a week and a half. 

Threadless was also having a Black Friday weekend sale so I bought seven new tees!  (A Simple Plan, Space Needs Colours, Cookie Loves Milk, Marshmellow Factory, Monster in the Closet, Robots Only Come Out at Night, and Know Your Dinosaurs)  I had them shipped to a friends address since we both ordered things and it'd be cheaper on the shipping, plus they've already arrived, so I'm just waiting to pick them up!  I really want them now :V

The last thing I'm gonna get for myself is this watch ~ then I'll move on to Christmas shopping =P. 

======================================================

Wicked collaboration between two awesome Canadian musicians ~ Dallas Green and Shad K.  Too bad the video audio isn't the greatest.  


That's Dallas Green's arm! :V

======================================================

On Saturday night, Boyricky and his daddy had a fun little gathering with some local ABDLs and grown-up friends.  It was quite an interesting mix of people and behaviours.  What I mean is that everyone knew about ageplay to a degree (one step into Ricky's bedroom and you'd think you were in a toddlers room) so even though it was impossible to hide, it was just never brought up.  I was drinking booze from a sippy cup while others had wine glasses and no one said a word.  Of course we showed some respect and kept our pants on until those not comfortable with seeing diapers left, but in general, I find this type of understanding among everyone truly amazing.  How do you even get to that level?  To seemlessly blend your infantilism with your everyday friends who discuss politics, theater, and culture.  It is inspiring to know someone who doesn't need to hide who he is in front of people while sharing mutual respect and understanding. 
🤔 contemplative  |  ♫ Miike Snow - The Rabbit
Link

3)  Diapers.  If you're a regular reader of my blog or know me well, this turn on is a no brainer.  I have a diaper fetish.

Day Seven: Four turn offs.
 
1)  Poor hygiene (particularly smells) and lack of manners (mostly dinning-wise).  These things disgust me since they should be common sense and proper etiquette. 

2)  Being condescending because then you're just being an asshole.

3)  Mullets.  I'm not a fan of guys with long hair, although it really depends on how it looks with the entire body and features.  But for mullets, just no. 

4)  Lifeless individuals.  People who don't get out enough despite being fully capable of doing so when the opportunities arise.  I find these types boring to be around.
