'Ten Days' meme: Day Five

Dec. 1st, 2010   11:14 PM
I know that it isn't good to live a life with regrets because thinking about them contribute nothing positive to where I am right now, but I have several that don't necessarily stress me on the same levels. 

'Ten Days' Meme

Day One: Ten things you want to say to ten different people right now.
Day Two: Nine things about yourself.
Day Three: Eight ways to win your heart.
Day Four: Seven things that cross your mind a lot.
Day Five: Six things you wish you’d never done.
Day Six: Five people who mean a lot.
Day Seven: Four turn offs.
Day Eight: Three turn ons.
Day Nine: Two smileys that describe your life right now.
Day Ten: One confession
___________________________________________________________

Day Five: Six things you wish you’d never done.

1)  I wish I never chose University of Toronto: Scarborough Campus for medical anthropology and health studies.  Had I chosen York University/Seneca College for nursing instead, I probably would be in a more rewarding work situation right now.

2)  I wish I never let so many friendships dissolve due to my lack of effort to keep in touch.  But even then, I wish I could be a better friend to those I care about. 

3)  I wish I never had unprotected sex with someone I didn't completely trust.  Even though I am clean, the risk of contracting something really scares me.  I will never do that again.

4)  I wish I never burdened my parents - hurting my mom emotionally and my dad financially - when I was younger.  I've done a lot of stupid things and it's made me feel like the black sheep in the family.

5)  I wish I never spent so much money impulsively in high school.  I worked during the summer so that I'd have money to use, but the prospect of saving as a long term thing just never stuck too well.  This practice has led to poor financial habits today. 

6)  I wish I never stopped playing the trumpet or violin.  Even if I didn't enjoy progressing in grade for the latter instrument, I could have continued doing it for the leisure of it.  The ability to make music is a beautiful art. 
😢 sad  |  ♫ Danko Jones - Too Much Trouble
Link

'Ten Days' meme: Day Four

Nov. 30th, 2010   10:36 PM

The current things that cross my mind a lot are pretty run of the mill day-to-day stuff.  Whether it's a stressor or something I look forward to thinking about, they make me who I am.

'Ten Days' Meme

Day One: Ten things you want to say to ten different people right now.
Day Two: Nine things about yourself.
Day Three: Eight ways to win your heart.
Day Four: Seven things that cross your mind a lot.
Day Five: Six things you wish you’d never done.
Day Six: Five people who mean a lot.
Day Seven: Four turn offs.
Day Eight: Three turn ons.
Day Nine: Two smileys that describe your life right now.
Day Ten: One confession
___________________________________________________________

Day Four: Seven things that cross your mind a lot.

1)  'I hate my job', followed by, 'When is my next break?'  A lot of the time I feel as though I'm wasting my education working at this dead end job.  But nothing is stopping me from quitting other than sheer laziness to look for another job, so really, complaining gets me nowhere.

2)  'What do people think of me?'  I always wonder whether people think about me as I think about them - whether friends, family, or people I don't know.  At the end of parties or gatherings I question whether I've embarrassed myself by saying something stupid, or if I have a lasting impression just by being myself.

3)  'Am I eating healthy?'  Currently I get enough exercise everyday, but having a balanced diet is very difficult.  I know I don't eat enough fruits and vegitables.  I know that I drink way to much coffee.  I know that I keep putting restrictions with alcohol on myself but rarely keep them.  

4)  'Privacy.'  I very much enjoy my privacy at home, but I live with my family and share rooms with my older brother.  I can't do simple things that I enjoy without being judged and criticized.  For example, cuddling a plushie, going to bed in a sleeper, having to hide my fursuit, looking at furry art, etc.  It's a stress that gradually accumulates and I constantly look forward to the day I move out.

5)  'How should I spend my free time?'  When I'm not working, I'm sitting in front of the computer and it's not something I alway like to do.  I have a list of tv shows and movies I want to watch, but never get around to it.  I want to hang out and have fun with friends, but they aren't always available and deciding what to do together is cumbersome - especially when on a budget.  

6)  'The bus schedule and ensuring I catch it.'  My main mode of transportation since I rarely use my parents' car to drive is the bus and subway.  It's unpleasent waiting for a bus not running on time in freezing temperatures, but it's a daily affair.

7) 'How can I make "Do More Mondays" more engaging for others?'  It is important to me to help others through charity - if I could I would give my time and energy instead.  Do More Mondays makes me happy at the start of the week and by the end of it I look forward to doing it again.  I really want other people to feel the same way by showing their support, suggesting where I should donate to, and being aware of what is going on in the world.  I've only been doing this for seven weeks with forty-five more to go, so this is something definitely on my mind quite a lot.
😴 tired  |  ♫ Sambomaster - Futatsu no Namida
Link

'Ten Days' meme: Day Three

Nov. 29th, 2010   11:12 PM
This day's meme was a lot of fun ~ it made me think seriously but also light-heartedly.  I'd like to think that there are different ways of interpreting how one could "win my heart" rather than strictly in a romantic sense of the phrase.

'Ten Days' Meme

Day One: Ten things you want to say to ten different people right now.
Day Two: Nine things about yourself.
Day Three: Eight ways to win your heart.
Day Four: Seven things that cross your mind a lot.
Day Five: Six things you wish you’d never done.
Day Six: Five people who mean a lot.
Day Seven: Four turn offs.
Day Eight: Three turn ons.
Day Nine: Two smileys that describe your life right now.
Day Ten: One confession
___________________________________________________________

Day Three: Eight ways to win your heart.

1)  Enjoy eating and have a passion for good food.  What a majority of people I know consider ‘good’ food I normally find it mediocre.  I don’t consider myself picky, but I’ve eaten from a long list of places so there is a lot for me to compare with.  Knowing how to cook and/or bake would be a bonus.  I really enjoy helping out in the kitchen because it can be a lot of fun!

2)  Be interesting, however, this doesn’t necessarily mean share the same interests as me.  I am a boring person and it takes an interesting person to bring out the playful and talkative side of me.  Introduce me to things I’m unfamiliar with, broaden my mind, and be culturally aware.  I find it creepy when people are sole focused on one interest that we both share. 

3)  Communicate; I am no good at this.  I am very guilty of rarely starting conversations and it has hampered many friendships.  But I listen more than I talk and I greatly appreciate it when people talk to me.  Sometimes I have a hard time expressing how I feel in person, and I end up bottling it inside of me.  But I encourage it for people to ask me questions and pry out the answers.  It feels good to get it out there and not have something on the back of my mind. 

4)  Indulge the little kid in me.  Ageplay is an important aspect of who I am.  When I’m feeling little, feed my imagination, tickle my curiosities, amplify my fun, and surprise me.  But also be parental, decisive, and guiding with a sense of structure.  I know when is the right time and place to behave accordingly and it would greatly benefit others to know as well. 

5)  Live life with style.  This is more of a romanticized way to win my heart since style is subjective and constantly evolving, but I love people with a bit of class, finesse, and good taste.  Bourgeois domestic setting makes me feel pretty giddy.  It doesn’t even need to be materialistic ~ having that type of mindset really intrigues me.   

6)  Be empathetic but also offer differing opinions.  There are a lot of people who can offer a shoulder for me to cry on but there are few who can also shake me to my sense.  Sometimes I feel that emotions cloud logic and rationality; being able to keep the two sorted for when I need a wake up call is very important to me.  I think this is why I have a special spot in my heart for older people. 

7)  Smile, it’s contagious and warms my heart.  This partly has to do with laughter and sharing a similar taste in humour, but smiling is a very simple way of making another person happy and think positive.  I believe that the smile itself doesn’t need to be beautiful, just the inner beauty of making that smile and passing on a good feeling. 

8)  Have ambitions and know what you want in life.  It doesn’t matter to me if you’ve failed at achieving something, but as long as the goal was in mind and you put in the effort, my heart goes out to you.  I fully understand that progress is a slow process, but people with no direction in life who choose not to go anyway really irk me.  Life is all about progression.  
🌿 calm  |  ♫ Miss Li - Polythene Queen
Link

'Ten Days' meme: Day Two

Nov. 28th, 2010   3:20 PM
I find it challenging to list nine things about myself that hasn’t already – explicitly – been mentioned in my blog.  Plus, it's always easier and more interesting to hear what other people have to say about you rather then doing it yourself.

'Ten Days' Meme

Day One: Ten things you want to say to ten different people right now.
Day Two: Nine things about yourself.
Day Three: Eight ways to win your heart.
Day Four: Seven things that cross your mind a lot.
Day Five: Six things you wish you’d never done.
Day Six: Five people who mean a lot.
Day Seven: Four turn offs.
Day Eight: Three turn ons.
Day Nine: Two smileys that describe your life right now.
Day Ten: One confession
___________________________________________________________

Day Two: Nine things about yourself.

1)  I have two scars on my face that aren’t remarkably noticeable.  One is just above my hairline I got from falling down the stairs as a toddler.  The other is above my left eyebrow from getting hit by a tire swing as a kid. 

2)  I’ve been wearing glasses since I was five years old and I’ve always hated it.  I think that they make me look ugly and I’m embarrassed to wear them when I’m not at home.  I will rarely go out in public without my contact lenses.  There have been times where I’ve strained my eyes when sleeping over at a friend’s house, all for the sake of not having to wear my glasses. 

3)  From an early age to the end of high school a large part of my life was devoted to music.  I played the violin but didn’t enjoy it because it felt like a chore that my parents made me do.  I regret discontinuing after getting my RCM grade 9 certificate because I could have achieved higher with a better attitude. 

4)  In person I whine a lot about things I can actually tolerate quite well.  For example: cold weather, my short stature, travelling long distances, eating vegetables, waking up, being bored, etc.

5)  Material things make me happy, but I am not materialistic because I do value intellect, culture, companionship, etc.  Rather, it’s because I am very forgetful and I find it easiest to remember moments marked by material things.  I am sentimental about things given to me by people I care about. 

6)  I use money as an easy way out for a lot of things that could have been potentially more meaningful if done differently.  It is difficult to think of examples. 

7)  I have a strong need to belong, but I often feel timid while in big groups.  I scarcely initiate conversations, which has made maintaining friendships difficult.

8)  I find it easy to be devoted to one person, however, often times the feelings aren’t reciprocated or the timing isn’t right. 

9)  I envy a lot of people for various reasons, which can cloud my judgement sometimes when I’m told by other people what they envy about me.  I don’t want others to feel that way about me and I’m sure other people don’t want the same.  I need to be happier with the things I have. 
🤔 contemplative  |  ♫ Smashing Pumpkins - Tom Tom
Link

'Ten Days' meme: Day One

Nov. 27th, 2010   10:44 PM

I am the type of person who listens more than he talks ~ which usually leaves me with things unsaid to people, whether appropriate or not.  I don't encourage people to guess if any one of these is direct to them or a specific person.

'Ten Days' Meme

Day One: Ten things you want to say to ten different people right now.
Day Two: Nine things about yourself.
Day Three: Eight ways to win your heart.
Day Four: Seven things that cross your mind a lot.
Day Five: Six things you wish you’d never done.
Day Six: Five people who mean a lot.
Day Seven: Four turn offs.
Day Eight: Three turn ons.
Day Nine: Two smileys that describe your life right now.
Day Ten: One confession

___________________________________________________________

Day One: Ten things you want to say to ten different people right now.

1)  "Please stop being so critical about me.  I’d much rather you encourage my interests or just turn a blind eye and respect my privacy."

2)  "You are a waste of space and contribute nothing to society.  Stop complaining about practically everything and do something with your life."

3)  "What happened to the short time we had together?  I thoroughly enjoyed being with you – it made me very happy – and you can’t say I didn’t try.  But where was the effort on your behalf?  Be the bigger man, I’m still waiting."

4)  "We don’t talk often enough and we really should ~ my bad.  There is a lot I'd like to know about you so that I can be a better friend."  

5)  "I wish she didn’t control your life so much, but she is a big part of it and I respect the decision you made.  We don’t talk very much because you’re hard to get in touch with.  Just know that you’ll always have a friend in me."

6)  "I’m envious of you and I wish I could be more like you."

7)  "For all those times in the past, I am so deeply sorry for hurting you.  I wish I had never done any of it.  You know that I love you very much, I’m sorry that I don’t show it enough." 

8)  "You are so beautiful in the most charming of ways.  I wish more people would acknowledge that.  Don't ever stop being you!"

9)  "Make yourself more interesting.  You have got a one-track-mind and it's not cool."

10)  "I use to find you very interesting and I’d look forward to chatting with you because we rarely do.  But now you’re becoming increasingly faceless to me ~ you’re just another person I wanted to hang out with, but you let the opportunities go by for one reason or another."

🤔 contemplative  |  ♫ Homeboy Sandman - Opium
Link

Twitter > LiveJournal

Nov. 27th, 2010   5:42 PM
I haven't made an entry in a few days ~ my small thoughts and activities have been jotted on Twitter.  This past week has been good to me ~ I had this routine of going to work, coming home to watch Harry Potter, and then going to bed =P. 

On Friday, after work, Nebulus64 and I wandered through the P.A.T.H and successfully made it to Yonge and Dundas from Union ~ don't expect me to brave the cold anymore!  From there we watched the seventh Harry Potter movie.  It was really good!  Lots of violence and spiffy magical spells, plus an array of cool scene locations.  The acting is just a smidge better; Ron's acting was better than Draco's...no way! o__O.  The plot definitely takes the cake ~ it is impressively well thought out and I can't wait for part two!  Afterwards we went to Fran's, the ol' munch location we use to hit up, for some food.  It was definitely good times ^-^. 

I'm gonna take it easy this weekend.  So far today I've been playing with numbers and catching up on things online (so much talk about the new AUP on FA! o__O).  In the afternoon I watched Roger Federer (2) rape Novak Djokovic (3) in the Barclays ATP semi-finals ~ tomorrows finals against Rafael Nadal should be really good.  Later tonight I'm gonna have coffee and possibly desserts with Stephanie, an old high school friend ~ it's been a while so it'll be nice to have some good laughs together =3. 

I'm also going to do the '10 Days' meme that's been circulating all over.  It just looks like something to do since I haven't been writing much lately, but I'm not really sure how personal and open I want to be about it. 
😎 relaxed  |  ♫ N.E.R.D. - Life As A Fish
Link

Wishlist, ATP, Harry Potter, and Yeezy!

Nov. 22nd, 2010   11:28 PM
My wishlist getting shorter is directly proportional to my wallet getting slimmer.  Balls >:C

I bought these two Tokidoki hats ~ waiting for them to arrive makes me bouncy! o__O


Why yes, they do look ridiculous...ridiculously awesome and friggin hilarious! XD  If I could I'd get them all <3

I also placed a bid for this bag on eBay ~ I said I wanted it last month, so now I'm gonna get it! ^-^


What's left on my wishlist is a watch (or two), maybe some spiffy looking belt buckles, the red mittons from 'The Bay' look pretty awesome, and I kinda want a Miga toque from last years Olympics =P  If I'm not shopping for clothes it's gonna be accessories! XD

===================================================

Blah...my two favourite players on tour - Andy Roddick and Robin Soderling - lost in their openning matches at the Barclays ATP World Tour Finals.  Luckily it's round robin so they still have a chance to climb their ranks within the groups they are in.  I'll be sure to catch some of the action at work when I'm on 'break' since the food court has TSN!

===================================================

HARRY POTTER MARATHON...starting tomorrow...at home...by myself heh >.o

Nebulus64 is lending me his collection ~ if I successfully complete it by Friday we're gonna go watch the seventh installment! :D

===================================================

Kanye West's fifth studio album "My Beautiful Dark Twisted Fantasy" released today ~ and leaked only weeks ago =P.  Despite the fact that most of the tracks have already been released a long time ago thanks to his G.O.O.D. Fridays program, it is still an incredibly flawless album!  It's completely quality over quantity ~ rather than rushing into songs he lets the music ride out.  The list of featured artists on top of strong and well worded lyrics is amazing.  This album is a must year even if you don't like Kanye's personality; you've just gotta respect his music!

My AB-ness on this blog...

Nov. 18th, 2010   9:55 PM
It was brought to my attention today that my blog got pimped by this hot looking AB girl ~ Pampered Penny.  Thanks! 

I checked out the other links she's listed and practically all but mine and anothers are entirely centred around ageplay.  I have nothing against people who focus their blogs on that one aspect of their life but I doubt that I could ever do that.  I'd probably run out of things to say if I didn't do very much, plus I'd get bored of it.  There is more to me than just a particular thing I am interested in, and that should be true for everyone even if they don't show it.  So I've always found it a bit interesting when I come across those types of blogs and how they're different but the same ~ and I still enjoy reading them =P

I noticed an increase in traffic since the 15th, so perhaps some of them were coming from Penny's site hoping to see some stuff about ageplay.  Here watch this music video!


Hehe, it's so bizarre and rips away your attention from the lyrics ~ which is disappointing.  I love Homeboy Sandman!  His lyrics are sharp, he creates songs about stuff I’ve never heard anyone rap about before - some being very funny - and his samples are fresh to death.  He is just one of many talented NYC indie hip hop artists that need to be signed to a good label.  His second album 'Actual Factual Pterodactyl' is one of my favourites and can be downlaoded here if you're interested ^-^. 
🌀 creative  |  ♫ Homeboy Sandman - Gggrrraa!!

Link

Good times!

Nov. 15th, 2010   6:52 AM
I had a really fun weekend with some great friends ^-^  Thanks everyone!

On Saturday, I went to Ms Seven's littles' gathering ~ a small one since next weekend is the regular monthly playdate =3.  I managed to mooch a ride from Greenbean which helped tremendously since moving my fursuit around in its bin through public transit is a pain.  We went junk food shopping prior to going hehe.  It was nice to see familiar faces (Stefonknee, Greenbean, Kittycakes and her boyfriend) along with a new person who was pretty cool :D  I really enjoy the lack of structure at Seven's events and how things just progress in a 'whatever' fashion.  Plus, having the opportunity to wear my shortalls and diapers openly is a lot of fun!  After much talking and loafing around, we did a McDonald's run and watched 'The Incredibles'.  Unforutnately because of the time I didn't get to frolic in suit, but at least I got to show it to Seven since she helped me with the duct-tape-dummy.  I had to bounce shortly after to catch a bus to Brampton.

It's been a couple of months since I've last hung out at  and 's place ~ as always, it's really awesome of them to have me over :D  When I arrived in the evening, they gave me this really adorable Christmas ornament to hang on their tree ~ that's so sweet of them to include me in their tradition! =3


(I love how the mouse looks as though it's been sniffing Joker gas or something XD)

For dinner we pigged out at Tokyo Maki Sushi - an all you can eat Japanese restaurant.  I found it pretty funny how they randomly have a Chinese dim sum item on the menu =P.  If I were to go back, I'd mass order on tempura and sashimi since their deep fried eats were done well and the cuts of fish were great.  There were a ton of specialty rolls but some of them tasted the same; those that didn't had only a slight difference in taste.  Heh, I'm just picky ^^;;.  Afterwards we headed for home and watched 'National Lampoon's Christmas Vacation' ~ it was pretty jokes and I like movies with a warm ending.  When a portion of our food had settled, Dan and Shiro began decorating their tree while I got in suit ~ it was so much fun! \o.o/

     

  
I "helped" decorate too ~ which really just meant that I stood around and looked cute =P


"Where are all the presents?"  :V


Peace!  Dan on the left and Shiro on the right ^-^.
(I mentioned it on my FA but not here ~ I'm collecting 'peace' poses whenever I do group shots! :D)

I conked out for the night at around 4am but woke up sometime between 9-10am .____.*  Dan was next to wake so we randomly selected "Goldenfinger" to watch ~ I've got the theme song stuck in my head now!  James Bond is such a womanizer, he must've been with four or five women in that movie D:  Shiro joined us about midway through and when that was over we decided on going to KFC to try the Double Down for lunch.  It was overhyped and unsatisfying me thinks.  Plus the picture makes it look bigger! >:C  So that was a waste of calories haha, but at least I can say I've tried it now.  With less than two hours to go before I needed to catch the bus home, we watched three episodes of "The Big Bang Theory".  It's really funny and definitely another series to add to my watchlist =3.  Good times!
😴 tired  |  ♫ Damien Rice - Amie
Link

Crazy! :V

Nov. 12th, 2010   10:14 PM

Can't even see the base of the CN Tower, let alone the Air Canada Centre or Union Station and the train tracks.

  

And then I had to work crazy hard so the day kinda sucked =\

This was a little over half the stuff I had to push around today T___T. 

Oh wells, it's the weekend and I'm gonna have crazy fun!  Ms Seven is having a little's shindig at her place tomorrow afternoon.  Then afterwards I'm gonna bounce to b-town and hang out with Shiro and Dan Raccoon till Sunday ^-^.  Now I just have to ponder ways of getting my fursuit around ~ it's in a big plastic bin, but I think I've got a collapsible trolley somewhere in the basement.

Two things...

Nov. 11th, 2010   5:07 PM
IT'S HERE!



Heh, I'll stop teasing with the box  ~ real pics under the cut! =P
I don't normally like being a camera whore, but I have to pop the cherry at some point XD

     
     

Oh my gawd it was hella hot being in suit!  I had two windows open (it was like 10C outside) which helped a tiny bit.  Putting it on by myself was tricky since the zipper is in the back, but I know I'll get better at it (I has coat hanger for hooking, yar!)  Taking it off on the other hand was even more difficult =S  I definitely need a dive suit.  Anywhoozle, I love it to death! XD

Also, today is Rememberance Day (also known as Veterans Day among other things).  Please take a moment out of your day to honour those who served and/or died, whether soldier or citizen.  It doesn't matter which side you're on, who won or who lost ~ be thankful for the freedom you have. 


Wear your poppy with pride.

"Canstruction"

Nov. 9th, 2010   10:52 PM
Canstruction ~ "is using one can as a catalyst for change. One can to represent the building blocks of massive sculptures. One can to prove that every act of kindness makes a difference. Since 1992, Canstruction has contributed over 15 million of pounds of food to community food banks demonstrating that we can win the fight against hunger."

Toronto's Canstruction is in support of Daily Bread Food Bank.  Five of the seven display sites are in the buildings I work at (TD Centre ~ Front/Bay St.) so I took some pictures! :D  Seeing these really warmed my heart knowing that it's for a good cause and builds awareness.  The exhibit ends on Saturday.

 
 
(Clockwise from top left: an arc, NASCAN stadium, an igloo and eskimo, and Garfield)
The rest of the pictures I took can be viewed here.
♫ Pacific - Sunset Blvd
Link

Today couldn't have been better!

Nov. 8th, 2010   10:15 PM
During the day my workload was WAY less than the nuttiness of last week ~ I managed to finish most of it before lunch and slacked off the rest of the time ^-^. 

Not long before leaving everyone in the mailroom sat for a meeting with the HR ~ 1.5% raise for everybody!  That's pretty peanuts but whatever, more money is more money :D  Most of the chaps there haven't seen a raise in two years and I've only been there a month.  After my probation is over I'm going to fight for another raise! >:3

When I got home a big ol' box was waiting for me ~ my new fursuit has arrived! \o.o/  Unfortunately my dad was being his usual self and demanded to know what I got since he paid for the customs.  He wanted to know what I wasted $400 bucks on since that's what was labelled for 'value of duty'.  If he had known it actually cost five times as much I'd probably be dead right now, like seriously.  So I had to come out to my parents that I am a furry ~ I don't wanna get into that. 



Just teasing! =P  I'll try to have actual pictures on Thursday since doing this sort of stuff is difficult when you share rooms with your older brother ^^;;  I can't wait until then! *bouncy*

What better way to end the day than with a new episode of House MD?  O3O

Work rant...furry dinner...awesome videos!

Nov. 4th, 2010   10:55 PM
I hate my job.  That's nothing new but I just need to rant about it =\.  These past few days have been murder ~ it feels like I've been doing the work of two people.  Three days in a row I've had to work through my regular one hour unpaid lunch break and then later having a very quick bite to eat.  It's tremendously lonesome working by myself practically all day despite seeing dozens of people briefly when I deliver to them.  I've grown to hate (probably friendly) people who aren't at their cubical when I make a delivery.  At the end of the day I feel dirty all over because I've been sweating from loading and unloading boxes of stationary from dusty loading bays and service elevators.  My arms are marked with bruises and shallow cuts that I only notice after showering.  And waking up soar knowing that the cycle repeates itself is really depressing. 

I've thought about quitting, but it was impulsive and during moments of fustration.  It has only been a month since I've started and looking for new work is time consuming.  I wouldn't be so bitter about my job if I wasn't being underpaid, and I'm not just saying that because most people feel that way about their jobs.  I am literally being underpaid ~ the previous position holder started with a dollar an hour more than me.  I'm making the same amount as the other people in the mailroom who only sort and deliver letters and light courier.  No one in the mailroom wants my job. 


This was part of my workload today.  Each white/green box is a box of photocopy paper that weighs 20lb.  There are eight on each row and it's four and a half stacks high.  So that's 720lb not including the the rest of the stuff on top >>". 

================================================================

Last night was the monthly furry dinner and definitely something I needed to kick back and relax to.  After work and prior to going, I killed some time at the Eaton Centre ~ returned three t-shirts and a pair of jeans ^^;;.  I saw this Swatch that I really like!  It's the only one they have left and 30% off, but it's $95 before tax! XD  If they still have it by Boxing Day and it's cheaper, I'll definitely get it.  There's also a yellow G-Show that I want, but again, digging deep into my wallet would put pins in my conscious.  

I bumped into Babs at Indigo ~ it's always pretty looney hanging out with her.  We went into the Disney Store and poked fun at everything =P.  They have some Mickey Mouse reusable water bottles I really like!  Afterwards we went to the dinner and had a hilarious time with Raverfox, Mune, MimiBadger, Six, and Grey SnowCat ~ among many other furs, but they were the ones I sat around =3. 

I can safely eat lots of carbs and drink enough to get me tipsy without having to worry about my waistline.  I do so much walking and heavy lifting at work that people have been telling me I look a little slimmer.  I haven't gone to the gym since September =P. 

================================================================

Another video DeStorm recently posted that I think is genius!  I love this guy <3


This is such an awesome food bank program for school children in poverty.  Pure inspiration. 
😫 exhausted  |  ♫ Mother Mother - Body
Link

Oct. 31st, 2010   9:57 PM

Help whatever way you can...

Oct. 29th, 2010   10:02 PM
Repost from 's entry...

==================================================

4 local furs - now homeless

As many of you know, 4 of our local friends,    and  lost their house this morning due to a fire.  The house next door to theirs caught fire and it spread into their house very quickly.

Details of the fire itself are here:
http://swift-fox.livejournal.com/740374.html 

Our friends have lost nearly everything they own, besides some clothes and a handful of possessions. While they have received a meager cash assistance from the Red Cross, it's not nearly going to be enough to replace all of their clothing.

We all know that times are hard, but if you can spare some money to help them out, please do so at the link below. I will make sure that they get every dime of it as it comes in. 



Physical donations of clothing, small appliances, furniture, electronics, etc. can be sent to:

Andrew Koch
1109 Gladys Ave
Pittsburgh, PA 15216-3920

I will make sure to get them everything - anything that can't be used (wrong size, etc) will be donated to a local charity for others in need.

Also, this entry is public - anyone can see it. Please, PLEASE link this around in your own journals and chartrooms and help get the word out. Even a couple dollars here and there will add up quickly and help them a LOT.

==================================================

More info about it can be found here. 
😢 sad  |  ♫ At The Drive-In - One Armed Scissor
Link

Good but brief night...

Oct. 29th, 2010   12:32 AM

Buying stuff...

Oct. 27th, 2010   10:10 PM
I really dig these 'team buying' websites ~ I check'em out daily and I've already bought into a couple.  But I really hate how tempting some of the offers can be, particularly the food ones!  Like todays TeamBuy is a "Pay $5 for $10 worth of food at deKEFIR, limit of 4 per person".  I've never snacked at deKEFIR before but it's reaaaally close to where I work and most food vendors in the area are kinda pricey.  I can't decide whether to take this opportunity or not since I'm also trying to lose weight and save money so that I can buy non-edible things XD. 

$20 for four days of snacking on $10 worth of food each time?  Or use that money, for example, on four days worth with of transit fare? =P

Or I could buy it now and hold on to it until next summer since it expires in June, but that'd be testing my will power haha.  I've still got a $30 voucher for "Snakes & Lattes" and a $60 voucher for "Ginza Sushi" ~ both of which I paid half price for.  Hrmmm, I need to call out people and do stuff!

=======================================

I want this Paul Frank backpack! o.o  For a while now I've wanted a childish backpack that's big enough for an adult.  A few days ago I saw a man, with his kid, wearing it and it looked like a fairly decent size.  

I also want a new watch that's colourful and awesome looking =P.  I have my eyes on either G-Shock or Swatch, but I'm not too keen on spending so much ~ I'd go as high as $50-60.  

=======================================

I just noticed this tweet not long ago ~  Bunny!  It looks a bit chubby, but I'm still really excited about it!  \o.o/
🤔 contemplative  |  ♫ Lil Wayne (ft. Robin Thicke) - Tie My Hands

YouTube

Oct. 26th, 2010   10:50 PM
Here are some videos I've been enjoying these past few days ~ after the cut =3

An incredibly beautiful short film by Kanye West called 'Runaway' (which is also the name of a track he released last month).  His fifth album - My Beautiful Dark Twisted Fantasy - drops next month, but most of the tracks have already been released via his GOOD Friday series. 


The first time I've seen Kanye West with ballerinas was during his performance of 'Power' on SNL earlier this month.  I've never paid attention to ballet, but now I think it's pretty awesome when it's with the context I really dig =P.

==========================================

I love this teaser trailer ~ you can already tell it's well produced XD.

I've been following Wong Fu Productions for years ~ since the day I watched 'Yellow Fever'.  And gradually they've been getting better and better.  I love that.  I've never heard afterschoolspecial until I saw this trailer and after listening to their songs, sadly, 'Name' is the only one I really like =\.  Oh wells, great song with a great music video ~ I can't wait! 

==========================================

Oh my gawd!  I love you Robin Soderling! <3

I reeeaally want him to win a big tournament so badly!  Either a Masters 1000 or a Grand Slam, or better yet the World Tour Finals at the end of November.  He occasionally wins the smaller tournaments but always falls in the finals for the big ones T___T.  Come on man!  You can do it!

Link

Pre-Halloween fun!

Oct. 24th, 2010   12:34 AM
Another busy week has gone by which means fun times are in order for the weekend! 

After work on Friday I headed over to Boyricky's place and totally took a load off from being grown up.  Along with Cameron, BaBySB1, Incomplete Dude, and two of his friends (one was visiting from Quebec) we carved pumpkins!  I haven't done that since I was a little kid and it was messy good fun!  We filled a big bowl of pumpkin guts and joked about where else we could have put'em XD.  (In our diapers if you didn't catch on! haha)  I carved a kitty face and paw on mine =P

  

     
On the back of Ricky's pumpkin is his name spelt backwards so that when you shine it against a wall it shows his name!  Way cool!

  
Cameron's spider web is really intricate and I love the eyes on the classic Jack-o'-lantern BaBySB1 made.

At one point during the night we decided to order a pizza ~ I dared BaBySB1 to answer the door in what he was wearing, which was a cute shortalls.  He only agreed to do it if I answered first and then calling him out; I was in a footed sleeper XD.  I am impressed that the delivery man didn't even flinch!  He was an older gent and we were close to Church Street...so that could explain a lot haha.  I'd totally do it again just for the laughs =P.


This bad boy has two rooms and sits on wheels! :V  Ricky, BaBySB1 and I are block building champs! 

On Saturday I went to the Toronto Littles' Play Date ~ this month was held at Ms Seven's place since we're still looking for a new venue.  It was a really cozy and relaxing atmosphere shared among great friends; Stefonknee, Greenbean, BabyDroo, JeiSiN, Kittycakes and her boyfriend.  Along with the usual colouring books, cupcake decorating, and copious plushie cuddling, we watched 'Wallace & Gromit: The Cures of the Were-Rabbit' and a collection of Looney Tunes shorts!  It's so awesome to have fun with simple things and to share those experiences with people that love doing the same things <3. 


Zomg!  My belly is gonna be doubly large since I've eaten soooo much junk food these last two days! D: 
🐇 bouncy  |  ♫ afterschoolspecial - Name
Link

Work...Dinner...DeStorm...

Oct. 20th, 2010   12:12 AM
While at work I Tweeted..."Working this job will eventually make me a god at Teteis! \o.o/"...and this is why...

     

When I'm not delivering mail, I'm hauling heavy skids of stationary.  Building a semetrical and prioritized load is a fine art! XD  *le sigh* Man do I ever I hate my job >>" lol

==========================================================

Today I had dinner with two university friends ~ Jully, Lucy, and one of her friends.  Having not seen either of them in a long time I thought the night would be more enjoyable, but it was pretty meh.  I didn't feel much connection because our interests have always been pretty polar and reminiscing was thin because of the accompanying friend.  I guess the main thing that made me feel out of place was how whitewashed I was and how Asian they were.  Even though they know how to speak English, I guess they felt more comfortable with Cantonese ~ which really isn't my dominant language.  I understood most of what they were saying but responding was fairly narrow.  Having that sort of expectation to say something or remain awkwardly quiet isn't something I want to go through ever again =S. 

I find it kinda funny how the roles are reversed since I'm normally the Asian token in my circle of white friends =P. 

We ate at Joons (4852 Yonge St.) and shared dak galbi, which is a stir fry of boneless chicken, cabbage, sweet potato, rice cakes, instant noodles, mozzarella cheese, and chili paste sauce.  A very different combination foods - I rarely eat Korean food - and probably something I won't be having again in a long while. 

==========================================================

I love DeStorm ~ he is such a talented musician ranging in a number of genres.  Plus his wardrobe is really up my alley!  Check out his other videos if you're interested, they're really good. 

💤 sleepy  |  ♫ DJ Dakk - Halloween Sneak-peek
Link

Spirit Day

Oct. 18th, 2010   9:40 PM
Originally posted by at Spirit Day
 


It’s been decided. On October 20th, 2010, we will wear purple in honor of the 6 gay boys who committed suicide in recent weeks/months due to homophobic abuse in their homes at at their schools. Purple represents Spirit on the LGBTQ flag and that’s exactly what we’d like all of you to have with you: spirit. Please know that times will get better and that you will meet people who will love you and respect you for who you are, no matter your sexuality. Please wear purple on October 20th. Tell your friends, family, co-workers, neighbors and schools.

RIP Tyler Clementi, Seth Walsh (top)
RIP Justin Aaberg, Raymond Chase (middle)
RIP Asher Brown and Billy Lucas. (bottom)

REBLOG to spread a message of love, unity and peace.


🌀 sympathetic  |  ♫ Grace Jones - I've Seen That Face
Link

Diaper changes...

Oct. 17th, 2010   2:51 PM
(Continuing from last post...) Greenbean changed my diaper and I changed his ~ it was quite hot.  He's very good at nurturing <3 Ricky's daddy changed me ~ I was slow to getting hard and didn't show much horniness. Ricky and Cameron were feeling me up in the crotch prior to leaving.  Again, I was soft hard.  They double diapered me too.
Link

My ideal Saturday...

Oct. 17th, 2010   2:10 PM
I slept in, wore crinkles all day, got drunk, smoked up, hung out with some great friends, and had a really fun time! =P

In the afternoon, Greenbean and I went out to watch 'Toy Story 3' because he hadn't seen it yet (surprising right, who hasn't seen it?!)  I was happy to go with him since he wanted to see it with another ageplayer ^-^.  We drove to the Interchange - it wasn't playing anywhere else - only to find out that the movie projector wasn't working.  What a huge let down ~ he was so excited for it too >.<**  We'll make plans to see it another time before it rolls out the big screen.  So instead, we went next door to Putting Edge and played a round of mini golf.  The glow-in-the-dark made it kinda trippy since we smoked a bowl before playing XD.  It was a lot of fun and surprisingly I didn't do as bad as I thought I would!  I took 68 strokes while Greenbean took 60 and the par was 53.

    

Afterwards we went next door to Boston Pizza for a small bite to eat.  Lucky for us we had our mini golf score card out on the table and our waitress said we could redeem it for a free order of Cactus Cut Potatoes \o.o/  Along with that we ordered nachos and two pints.  We watched the Texas Rangers clobber the New York Yankees 7-2.  With some time to kill before heading for the munch - showing up late has become my norm - I went back to his house for another drink and to smoke another bowl =P. 

At the munch it was nice to see Seven, Tomin, and Stefonknee from the Littles' Play Date attending ~ new faces are a welcoming change!  Along with the regulars - Boyricky, Orca and his mommy, Dan Raccoon, Shiro, and Anthony - there was a total of eleven people.  It's too bad we all didn't come at the same time since our seating had been broken off into two groups, but oh well, I bounced between the tables.  Good times ^-^.  By the end of dinner a couple of people had already left and those that remained eventually made their way to Ricky's place.  It was really sweet of his daddy to change me, but he said I had to wear a shorter shirt so that I couldn't hide the baby prints on my diaper! D:  I think I may have stretched one of their shirts a little haha XP.  I had a few sippy cup beers, lots of good laughs, and some relentless tickling >:3  Before calling it a night at around 1am, Ricky, Cameron, and I had fun playing with building blocks! 

    
🐇 bouncy  |  ♫ Quantic - Common Knowledge
Link

Work hard, play hard? :V

Oct. 7th, 2010   9:08 PM

I started my new job the other day as a mailroom/stationary clerk at the TD Centre (Front and Bay St.)  There are too many things I need to know that three days of training cannot cover ~ the dude who I'm replacing leaves after tomorrow with all the knowledge I'm suppose to have D:  There are a number of things that I've already grown to dislike -  I'm getting paid less than what the previous position holder started with - but I won't rant about it.  Instead I'm going to give this job a little bit of time before assessing how much or little I value it; that only seems fair =\

Since I haven't worked a job in two years, I'm only now realizing how difficult it is to feel little when being grown up takes up so much time and energy :(  Back then I wasn't as open or active with my interests and my focus was more on school.  This job just leaves me so exhausted and feet aching that my mood isn't on doing anything else but turning off my brain and sleeping.  That's not how I want to be ~ I wanna be full of spunk and life D:  i'm young dammit! :V

I really need fun things to look forward to.  Like last nights monthly furry dinner was a good way to mellow out with friends (Thanks so much for the birthday present    You're too generous!)  But it's only once a month and kinda costly.  I need something substantial and cheap ~ like people and coffee o.o"  Where the heck are all the people I want to hang out with? =\  Man, I need to do something over the long weekend or else I'll break down at work next week!

If I ever want to just chill on the 55th floor, I get to stare out at Toronto ~ I love my city!
        

==================================

The Asian Swing is underway!  Robin Soderling and Andy Roddick are both into the quarter-finals in the tournaments they're playing ~ I'll be rooting for them as always! ;)

Link

Achievement Unlocked: Job Quitter

Oct. 4th, 2010   10:53 PM
So tired =.=**  I got home a little while ago from a 1-9pm shift at SDM ~ last Friday I had an 8am-4pm shift; waking up that early was hell!  Both days consisted of "store set-up", which meant assembling the store from scratch since it's brand new.  I lost count of how many steel shelves I had to assemble ~ not stock, assemble piece by piece and they were friggin' heavy!  I feel like I deserve an award from IKEA or something =P  

   
(Teehee ~ diapers go here and incontinent products go here, in the basement storage room, on the shelves I built)

Oh wells, I quit the job at the end of the day XD.  They owe me around 18 hours of pay.  And I should get an award for my shortest job wored D:

I mentioned it on my Twitter but not here ~ after my interview on Thursday I got hired on the spot.  I find it rather creepy that SDM replied back with this.  My Twitter links to this blog and this blog has info about me that make people go o__O.  Sooooooo I'll watch what I Tweet more carefully next time ~ there are eyes everywhere D:  Anywhoo, I'll be working as a mailroom clerk at the TD Centre downtown, hopefully starting sometime this week.  I love the hustle and bustle of being downtown - it's right up my alley - but this excitement will probably die after commuting for a month =P.

That's about all that has happened since I last wrote an entry =\  September has been kinda-good and mostly-sucky.  Some people have impressed me less and I'm a bit disappointed, but I won't give them any more of my time waiting for things to change.  If they want it, they know where to reach me.  I don't have much to look forward to in October (not even Halloween D:  I'm feeling so anti-social >>"), but hopefully that'll change later on.  Until then, Pokemon will suck up more of my life o_o  I have already put in 35 hours; won 8 badges, preparing to catch Ho-Oh, and my line up consists of Shiny Gyarados, Typhlosion, Ampharos (all lvl 38) and Espeon (lvl 39).  Krabby and Hoothoot are my HM slaves =P.  Wooper is in my PokeWalker ~ I named him smiley for obvious reasons XD. 
😴 tired  |  ♫ Foxy Brown (ft. Ron Isley) - The Letter
Link

My job...Pokemon Trainer! XD

Sep. 30th, 2010   10:14 AM
Been keeping busy...one way or another! Out of sheer boredom, I impulsively bought Pokemon HeartGold the other day ~ I've already put in ten hours of gameplay >>".  I'm at Azalea Town, got my second badge, and my line up consists of Butterfree, Flaaffy, Togepi, Wooper (all lvl 15), and Quilava (lvl 18).  I have become one of "those" people haha! =P Outside of that I've got another interview today ~ third one this week!  Unlike the last two - Prestige Connect and Primerica - I'm actually interested in working with Grenville as their mailroom clerk.  So I'm more nervous for this one but happy as well.  Furthermore, I get to start working with Shoppers Drug Mart earlier than expected.  They gave me a shift tomorrow morning along with my current five day shift next week!

Good times and looking forward...

Sep. 27th, 2010   2:20 PM
Yesterday was a lot of fun, even though it was on such short notice.  I hung out with Ruff and his papa bear Twicks ~ there has rarely been a dull moment when I'm with them =3  I got to be my cubby self for a change; it's been a while heh =P. 

We went to Yorkdale Mall and had lunch at Rainforest Cafe ~ it was so awesome!  There were a bunch of huge fish tanks, tall trees (one of them had a creepy talking face o__O), moving gorillas and noisy elephants, a scary tropical thunder storm with flashy lights, and even waterfalls!  Zomg!  Sensory overload *dies of glee* XD.  Everything there was so grand; even the menu and dining area was crazy huge.  And talk about a ton of people for a late lunch!  Ooooh I have to go back again sometime and maybe pick up something in the gift store too ^-^

I really love their bar stools! O3O  They don't look comfortable at all, but I'd totally want some if I lived in a house with a high table! :D


Afterwards we walked around the mall ~ can you believe they only have one toy store and like a million clothing stores?  What the hell! >.<**  Eventually doing a full circle, we headed for the movie theater and played in the arcades.  I won 7-3 in air hockey against Twicks! \o.o/  But only because most of the goals he scored on me didn't register haha.  Awesome glitch is awesome! >:3  When it was about time, we went in and watched "Alpha & Omega" ~ it was sweet but nothing spectacular.  I thought the movie would really negative between the two castes and eventually make up in the end, but they were super friendly to one another =P.  It was fun and lighthearted, but the 3D really wasn't necessary. 

Later in the evening when I was home, I got to watch TaviMunk draw on Livestream (pic) ~ we were chatting in the afternoon and he said he had a little something for me O3O

*squeak!*  Gift art!  That's so sweet of him! <3  Thank you very much lil munk; I can't help but smile each time I look at it =3  The outfit I'm drawn in is something I actually own XD.  I've put up a picture earlier in the month, but here's a side-by-side hehe.
    
============================================================

I get to meet Daddy Josh on Wednesday ~ I'm looking forward to it especially since the opportunity slipped yesterday ^-^.  I love his blog (I'm in the most recent entry XD).  Chatting with him as been fun and interesting as well!  We're gonna go to 'Snakes & Lattes Board Game Cafe', I'm so excited :D  And luck would have it, todays Living Social is a deal for where we're going ~ limited to one per person/per table though, but I got dibs on it!  haha

============================================================

I have a full weeks worth of "work" with Shoppers Drug Mart at the start of October.  I'm suppose to be a merchandiser until they train me for whatever other position they want me to do.  I'm fine with that, plus not having to wear a uniform is great since the stores grand opening isn't until the end of October =P.  I guess I'll just have to relax extra hard this week but I don't have any concrete plans made yet! XD
🐇 bouncy  |  ♫ Kevin Drew - TBTF
Link

Boring weekend is boring...for now!

Sep. 26th, 2010   11:35 AM
On Thursday I went to another job fair at Centennial College ~ the campus feels like a high school it's kinda different.  There were a number of known employers and training schools there so I had a pretty good time.  I've already heard back from two companies for an interview on Monday and Wednesday.  For the most part I'm not interested in working for them, but like before, I'll probably go just for the interview experience. 

I hung out with a good friendin the afternoon.  We had lunch at a quaint little burger joint down the street; the lady was so friendly!  That must be the secret ingredient to yummy food!  I wanna go back just so she can brighten up my days lol.  Later we went to Markville Mall, which used to be my favourite mall as a kid, but now it's just meh.  They've still got Toys R Us, but a lot of the fun stores I remember are gone and the pet shop is lame.  Afterwards we went to STC for some more mall crawling and java!  Butter pecan lattes on ice is kinda weird; it'd probably taste fantastic warm...with rum in it XD.  I've fallen in love with new store 'Hot Topic' ~ their website has way more stuff, but I donno if they ship to Canada >.<  The retro cartoon stuff is so awesome!  I want everything there so they can expect some business from me soon =P.

I haven't really done anything else up unitl today.  I feel like a fatty because i haven't gone to the gym in over a week ~ I went jogging Monday and Friday, but that was hell lol.  Today I'm gonna have lunch at the Rainforest Cafe in Yorkdale with Twicks and Ruff.  Then in the evening, hopefully hang out with Daddy Josh.  It should be a good day =3.

========================================================

A few days ago YouTube featured this interview with Elmo ~ it's soooo incredibly cute!  It reminds me of the video when Rocketboom interviewed Sesame Street. 



I want to live on Sesame Street now! D:
✨ chipper  |  ♫ The Hoosiers - Goodbye Mr. A
Link

“If you love something, let it go..."

Sep. 24th, 2010   12:22 AM
Sometimes when I’m unhappy, upset, or uncertain with a situation in my life, I watch ‘Lost in Translation’ and feel all right again.  I’ve seen it more times than I can count and it’s one of my most favourite movies.  There is a line that came across more noticeably when I watched it today.  Bob Harris: The more you know who you are, and what you want, the less you let things upset you. Charlotte: I just don’t know what I’m supposed to be.  ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- To have been able to say “I have a daddy” used to fill me up with pride.  But now it makes me feel uncertain.  I don’t know where our relationship stands and each time I think about it my heart aches.  It has been a month since I last saw you.  I didn’t want to contact you – hoping that you would instead – because it made me feel needy, which is something I don’t like admitting because it’s true.  But I did it anyways.  My email and phone call a few weeks ago only prolonged how I feel today.  I need you daddy, but do you need me?  You are important to me but right now I don’t feel important to you.  A few weeks ago I could be sincerely understanding with how busy you may have been, but right now you are testing my patience.  I don’t want you to lose.  I love you in a different way than I do of others, but I’m becoming more and more critical of my time alone.  I know I have a strong need to belong and I know I find it easy to be devoted to one person.  But I’m feeling really hurt right now.  I don’t know what to make of what we have and I don't know if things will ever be the same either.  Should I let it go?
😞 depressed  |  ♫ John Legend & The Roots - Hard Times
Link

Days gone by...and fresh beats!

Sep. 22nd, 2010   5:44 PM
It's Wednesday again.  Three entries ago it was a Wednesday.  Occasionally, it feels like I'm measuring my days based on the number of entries I've written rather than what I've actually done.  Seemingly, time goes by quicker this way despite there being no logic in that feeling =\. 

Anywhoozle, on Monday I had a job orientation with Shoppers Drug Mart which mostly consisted of signing a bunch of agreements =S.  They're really anal about procedures to a point where I'm actually a little scared I might do something out of line unknowingly resulting in my termination haha.  I'm waiting for them to call me back with a start date ~ no one has been assigned a position yet so how this will play out should be interesting.  Also, new season of House MD started!  Woohoo! \o.o/

On Tuesday I had a job interview with Direct Sales Force in the morning.  After looking into the company and position, I walked into the interview knowing I didn't want the job lol.  I only went through with it for the interview experience since I don't have much.  I think I did okay despite being a bit nervous =3.  If they offer me the job I'm gonna respectfully decline the opportunity ~ it might be hard to keep a straight face though XD.  Later in the afternoon I hung out with Greenbean and we took advantage of the great weather by strolling around Edwards Garden.  It was really chill just getting to know him better and tossing around a football.  There was also this enormous tree perfect for climbing, so we tried, but I struggled getting past the first branch lol.  (The tree was slippery and it left scrapes on my arms!  Don't judge me! XD)  I can't remember the last time I went to a park to do those kinds of things ~ it was fun! O3O  New season of Glee started as well!  Bonus! \o.o/

Tomorrow there's another job fair I want to go to because a number of big companies will be there, but it's at Centennial College which is kinda out of my way =S.  I hope the weather holds up! 

=============================================

I'm looking forward to the release of Chris Faust's (formerly known as Print) latest project 'FAUST'.  His previous album, 'Comic Books Unlimited' (free to download) was solid and I still listen to it frequently.  A few days ago he put up this short sample...



...sick. 
🥱 bored  |  ♫ Nneka - Heartbeat
Link

Last Saturday

Sep. 20th, 2010   5:24 PM
I probably could have posted this on Sunday, but I was being lazy =P.  Out of the three things I could have done on Saturday, I decided to hang out with other ageplayers ~ from what I remember, it was a blast! XD

The Toronto Littles' Play Date (req reg) is in the processes of finding a new place to hold the events, so this month was a bowling event.  It was also the birthday for one of the little girls that came; so there was party hats, balloons, streamers, noise makers, and cake! \o.o/  A total of seven people showed up, including Lenny, Greenbean, and Seven.  We booked a five pin lane for an hour and managed to finish one game with only a few minutes to spare.  I came in second with 134 points (beaten by a single point!) ~ I think that's relatively low haha.  Oh wells, it was a lot of fun and that's all that really mattered! :D  Afterwards we moved to a private area in the back with comfy chairs, tables, and a tv, where we ate a yummy chocolate cake and watched cartoons.  It was also a really good way to just relax and joke about things you wouldn't want kids and families to hear about =P.  Before going our seperate ways, we played at the arcades where I lost 3 to 7 in air hockey >.<

With a couple of hours to kill before the TAG munch, Lenny, Greenbean, and I went to the Goodwill across the street.  There was a perfectly good looking leather wheelchair for $10 ~ don't new ones go for hundreds? o__O  There's usually a few gems among the heaping piles of junk at thrift stores, but I don't have the patience to dig deep enough, though I probably should haha.  Later we went back to Greenbean's house for some drinks and to smoke up ~ this is when my memory gets a bit fuzzy lol.  We mostly just sat around talking and listening to vinyls.  Lenny and I intentionally planned on showing up late for dinner, but that ended up being a whole hours worth XD.  I do feel bad about that since Nebulus64 and BammBamm were the only ones there when we arrived.  Shortly after though, people began to arrive ~ three of the Brampton babyfurs, two from Ottawa, Philippe (from B4NS) and his friend, and Pardis nearing the end of the night.  I don't remember most of the munch outside of...the chicken pie and cheesecake were delish, but I might have just had the munchies lol.  Whatever was talked about seemed to go by really quickly so hopefully I didn't say anything stupid heh =S.  It was really generous of Philippe to covered all our drinks, which kinda makes me regret having only water XD. 

When dinner was over, we all decided to gather at the hotel the out-of-towners were staying in.  Prior to going, myself and a few others went to buy drinks but the liquor stores were closed.  No worries, I have forsaken drinking unless it is on someone else's account not ~ to sound like a cheap ass, but for the benefit of my own health =P.  At the hotel I was kinda expecting Philippe to hussle us a little harder since he had brought a lot of merchandise down XD.  I donno if I should feel bad or not since I walked away with four free Abenas and an Abri-san pad haha.  Oh wells, it was good times.  I think most of us wrapped up the night sometime around midnight.  Thanks to Nebulus for the ride home and the pit stop at Timmies =3.  And a big thanks to Dan Raccoon for including me in an art commission O3O.  (I don't follow Kelvin's stuff, so I hadn't noticed till it was pointed out to me XD).  
😄 cheerful  |  ♫ Franz Ferinand - Your Diary
Link

This is good...

Sep. 18th, 2010   12:06 AM
What's the good word?  I got a job orientation with Shoppers Drug Mart on Monday and an interview with Direct Sales Force on Tuesday!  I'm not going to stop looking though since I want something better and it's easier to turn down an offer than not get one.  I'm thinking of putting up my resume on sites like LinkedIn, Workopolis, Monster, etc. 

==============================================

My birthday is in ten days!  I WANT...*ahem*...I mean, here is my shameless wish list!  hehe =P

  • my mouse is dying and giving me grief whenever I play games, so I need a new one
  • I snapped my headset and "fixed" it with duct tape...but it's still pretty crappy
  • external hard drive cause I've only got 14gb of free space left!
  • a fun looking USB flash drive for my key ring ~ the designer ones are nice but I like this one
  • Lego watch ~ either the analog or digital one, I'm a sucker for colourful things!
  • tees from Threadless, but I really ought to be shopping for warmer clothes XD
  • I can never have enough hats
  • a childish looking backpack, but big enough for an adult
  • furry art of my character Clouwy since I don't have much of any

Money is always golden!  That way I can buy whatever =3  

==============================================

Local-ish (London, Ont) rapper, Shad K dropped a new music video today for "We, Myself & I" off his album 'TSOL'.  Amazing artist, solid album, great track, but the video is too flashy and strains my eyes XP.  I wanted to share something of his without killing other peoples' eyes, so here's the video to a song I really like ~ "'Yaa I Get It".




Also, earlier this month P.SO and 8thw1 came out with their mixtape 'Suicide by Jellyfish' (free to download).  They sample and remix beats from Paul McCartney, Chester French, Coldplay, Bjork, Flying Lotus, MadLib, and Billie Holiday.  It's groovy and fun to listen to ~ I'd expect nothing less from members of the AOK Collective.  Not from the mixtape but something I wanted to put up, check out this music video of DJ Spinna's "Get On Down" featuring three members of the collective!

🐇 bouncy  |  ♫ Feist - New Touch
Link

Over the hump of the week!

Sep. 15th, 2010   11:22 PM
How goes the job hunt?  Making progress...kinda!  I went to job fair the other day; oodles of immigrants!  Probably because their ads were in ethnic newspapers ~ found mine in the Asian classified =P.  There were a couple of intriguing prospects, but a majority of them were sales which I'm not too fond of.  A geezer from Paragon (a security company) said he could have hired me on the spot had my security license not expired...tits!  Like hell if I'm gonna go through the whole bullshit training/licensing process again >:(  I ended up giving out three resumes and taking home a bunch of info sheets.  The only super exciting thing about the job fair was the $1 hot dog vendor right next to it!  XD  Other than that it was pretty lame.

I also got a call from Shoppers Drug Mart asking for references!  Last week I applied to a recently renovated store not far from where I live.  There is a good chance I'll get some kind of position since they're doing mass hiring.  I wonder, do I get Optimum Points when I buy their uniform? o___o  Heh, anyways, I don't really want to work there but at the moment it's better than doing nothing.  Hopefully I'll still have some motivation to look for a better job even if I get hired. 

===========================================

This morning my dad grilled me about my resume ~ despite going through it together weeks ago and me revising it to his liking =S.  I was hoping to have a relaxed morning with just a dabble of job searching since I had already booked the day off for some fun.  So that was kinda blah...but my resume is, like, extra super spiffy now? 

In the afternoon I hung out with a good friend (note to self: think of a pseudonym).  We played tennis ~ it was fun because we were terrible at it haha.  My love and fanboy-ism for the sport doesn't translate to awesome skillz on the courts unfortuantely...but if life were like that! :O  Playing next to us were two old timers wearing jeans and whipping groundstrokes like nothing; they totally outclassed us haha =P.  Later we had Indian buffet at 'Tandoori House' (random: how come Indian non-buffet restaurants are uncommon in Toronto?).  The food was average but inexpensive because of TeamSave ~ $20 for $40 worth of food.  What did we do with the left over credit?  We ordered drinks!  Salty lassi tastes like yogurt and tears lol XD. 

He invited me over to his new home when we finished ~ cozy and squeaky!  I like :D  (Duuuude!  You live so close to 'Planet X' - comic book shop - I use to go to the one in Richmond HIll religiously! O3O)  After loafing for a bit we went to Starbucks for java.  Pumpkin spice latte was delish and I had a really relaxing time; a good kind of quiet time to just mellow out.  I get a different vibe when I'm around a different friend ~ I really like that =3.  

===========================================

Congrats to Rafael Nadal (1) for beating Novak Djokovic (3) and winning the US Open on Monday.  At only 24, he is the youngest to complete a Career Golden Slam ~ to have won all four Grand Slam tournaments and an Olympic gold medal.  Umm...holy shit? haha o___O  I predict that in five years he'll have matched, if not surpassed, Roger Federer's current stats D:

===========================================

This is such a cute site ~ "I used to believe" 
Sometimes I wish I still had a child-like sense of wonder and misunderstanding =P

===========================================

Happy Birthday Bunny!  I miss you lots but your Christmas present from the year before misses you even more! XD  Quit being such a granny or I'ma call you Fanny for real!  Have a blast on Saturday ~ we'll definitely make plans to see each other...eventually =P (meaning stay in town for the weekend!).  Love you long time ;)

===========================================

In the last little while I have watched: A-Team (2010), Ghostbusters (1984), Mr. Nobody (2009), Ed Wood (1994), Batman (1989), This is Spinal Tap (1984), Up in the Air (2009), and talhotblond (2009). 

And I have read the graphic novels: Escape from "Special" (2007), and Why I Killed Peter (2008).  I'm nearly finished The Eternal Smile: Three Stories (2009), afterwards I plan to start Shortcomings (2009). 

Guys got any recommendations?
😴 tired  |  ♫ Torley on Piano - Avenew
Link

Next weekend awaits!

Sep. 13th, 2010   3:29 PM
I'm having a good day so far ^-^.  I'm most productive in the morning and then it goes downhill right after lunch haha.  So I woke up early and starting poking my nose into the different sectors of government employment/internship/recent grad programs.  It'll be my dream to work for the government and retire early lol.  I was lacking on references to go with my resume since the previous jobs I worked at didn't give me any ~ the security company I worked with said I abandoned them when I took a leave of absense for school, the printing company went bankrupt, and the retirement home is under new management =S.  So a big "thank you" to the friends I got in touch with (or will be soon) who will help back-up my awesomeness with the occasional lie =P  I owe an old co-worker lunch when I land a job haha. 

Anywhoo, I'm already thinking about next weekend - gotta have things to look forward to ;)  But I can't decide which to go with!

On Saturday there's the Toronto Littles' Play Date, which I believe is going to be a bowling event in the afternoon.  Following that, there's the monthly ageplay munch ~ though honestly I never really know who shows up anymore so I'm kinda iffy about going. 

Or I could go to Bunny's hootenanny ~ her birthday is this Wednesday, but dinner and clubbing is on Saturday.  I'd like to go since I haven't seen her in ages, but there are a bunch of people attending that I don't know and clubbing really isn't my thing.   

Or I could go to Michigan XD.  Hypr and Mako offered me a ride down for the "Dave & Busters and Talk Like a Pirate Day Furmeet".  It sounds like a lot of fun and it'll be awesome to see the friends I made at FCN again, but it's the most expensive thing I could do for the weekend XP. 

If anything, I won't loaf at home next weekend ~ I'll force myself out rain or shine! >:3

Do more...

Sep. 12th, 2010   11:10 PM
Being introverted is stupid ~ time to bounce out of this funk. 

Since the start of last weekend I've hardly done anything of positive worth for myself.  I've been over thinking certain issues that I could have better distracted myself from instead of drowning them with alcohol.  I'm ashamed of myself, the momentary feelings of numbness were hardly worth wasting my body over.  I need to get back into exercising routinely.  I have to get serious about finding a job.  With the people I'm surrounded by, I need to just do rather than think ~ I enjoy life more as a go-getter.  This is going to be a week of change.  If it isn't and I fall back on my ass, everyone has the privilege to kick it.

Kiss and blog #20

Sep. 2nd, 2010   11:00 PM

Happiness is...

Sep. 2nd, 2010   4:55 PM


...material things!  XD


I have a new pair of shortalls ~ I love'em! =3             
It's Pooh riding a balloon to some pots of honey =3                                                                  		I also bought these awesome looking key covers
I really like having things that are fun and "little boy-ish" ~ like bright colours or a spiffy watch!

Last night I went to the monthly furry dinner and had a great time mainly thanks to Mr. Dad and HappyNickFox.  We poked fun at everything ~ like how stupid her sister is XP.  Thanks for the ride home as well!  Prior to going I was a bit unexcited about furry events in general; it really had to do with the circle of people within the 'local' community.  I find that some of the people I hang out with really irritate me while the people I want to be around don't make themselves visible ~ which is actually quite appealing to me.  It's really just a matter of getting my foot in the door among the different local groups cause right now I'm feeling pretty superficial and don't want to be seen around certain people >>". 

Tonight I'm gonna hang out with K-Dawg at his new place ~ I haven't seen him in a while so it should be a crinkly good times! ^-^

Random stuff update...

Sep. 1st, 2010   4:25 PM
I  jazzed up my profile bio here on LJ ~ I think it looks more organized and niffy now! =P

==========================================

I watched 'Arashi no Yoru Ni' (One Stormy Night) the other day ~ it's so good!  I love how cartoony the characters are; the wolf is so animated while the goat is just adorable and simple looking.  In contrast, the background for every frame is literally like looking at a painting.  It's amazing.  The story is about friendship; it's beautiful and full of heart but the ending was just good and not great.  I would have loved it if it ended after the avalanche scene ^^;;

==========================================

I'm kinda surprised I haven't mentioned Nardwuar anywhere in my blog yet ~ I love Nardwuar the Human Serviette!  People usually don't take him seriously due to the way he dresses and his unorthodox approach, but he honestly makes some of the best interviews with musicians ever!  Nardwuar really does his homework and often the case he knows more about the people he interviews than they do themselves!  Genius! 


Other memorable interviews include; Pharrell Williams, Drake, Snoop Dogg, Lykee Li, and Lady Gaga,

==========================================

This interactive music video for 'Arcade Fire - We Used To Wait' is incredible!  When it loads it'll open a bunch of other windows, just leave them be ~ they'll automatically close at the end.  And if it doesn't work for you, this is what you're missing =\

==========================================

[Edit @ 4:25pm ~ I forgot to throw this in here...]

Zomg!  'Snakes and Lattes'  just opened the other day!  I so want to go someday just to chill out XD.  It's pretty much a board game cafe, how fun is that?!  For years Asian bubble tea restaurants have had playing cards available for hours of fun, so I wonder why its taken so long board games to catch on?  With more than 1500 games to choose from though, it's quite impressive =3

Link

Old profile bio

Aug. 30th, 2010   7:10 PM

Thank you for taking an interest in reading this part of my blog. I am dumpling, a Canadian born Chinese and in general an all-around curious person. My curiosity provides me with many interests but a sense of shyness and a lack of initiative cast me as a listener than a talker. I am always looking to make new friends so please don't hesitate to give me the push I need.

I am an AB/DL as well as a furry/babyfur - neither of these are lifestyles for me, but rather they are just one part of my life. If any of this eludes you, you can Google it, ask me about it, or ignore it and all appropriately tagged entries.

I am in my final year of university working on a B.Sc degree; a Specialist program in Medical Anthropology and a Major program in Health Studies. I have no idea what I’ll do for a living after I graduate but I’m always open to suggestions.

I am pretty forgetful so the purpose of this blog is to help record my thoughts, feelings, and experiences – as well as to help cope with life’s absurdities. Currently my blog is open to everyone and I do not use filters, but I try to tag all my entries accordingly. If you are sensitive to certain topics please judge entries based on the tags. Lastly, a majority of the names used in this blog are pseudonymous or usernames. If you wish to have your name removed from an entry or the entirety of this blog, please let me know so I can accommodate changes.

[Last updated: November 8, 2009]

Link

Blog, music, and tennis

Aug. 30th, 2010   10:32 AM
I'm really digging this guys blog ~ 'Year of Giving'.  It's inspirational and full of interesting stories.  It's about a man named Reed who has commited himself for a year to giving $10 each day to one person, receiving nothing in return but an experience to share. 

=============================================

Despite his big ego, Kayne West is a really hard worker and makes some killer songs.  He announced on Twitter last week that he'd drop one new track every week until Christmas ~ last week was a remix of 'Power' with Jay-Z and Swizz Beatz.  This week it's 'Monster' featuring Jay-Z, Rick Ross, Bon Iver, and Nicki Minaj.  I love it :D


=============================================

Today marks the start of the two week US Open ~ Grand Slam tennis tournament.  The draws were released on the weekend and it's looking pretty good for Roddick (9), who I'll be cheering for.  He should easily make it into the third round where he might face Gael Monfils (19) which could be a tough match.  If he prevails, he might have a fourth round match with Davydenko (6), who has a 5-1 losing record to Roddick.  I'll also be rooting for Soderling (5), who also has a good draw until the quarter finals where he could meet Federer (2); coincidentally they clashed in the same round last year at the tournament.  Unfortunately Federer has a 12-1 winning history against Soderling and they've played against each other in four of the last six grand slams =S.  Regardless though, there should be some good games to look forward to!
🌀 impressed  |  ♫ Stars - Dead Hearts

Aug. 27th, 2010   11:00 PM

Things I can do this weekend...

Aug. 26th, 2010   11:04 PM
Dylan's ABDL Birthday Gathering
Potluck, booze, crinkles, and good friends...I'm game!  It feels like it's been a while since I've done anything with the local ageplay crowd, but that's probably untrue.  It'll be nice to kick back tomorrow night after enduring a boring week.

Canadian National Exhibition
This is one of those places I might go each year just to say I've been - I'd be indifferent if I didn't go.  If I had to choose, I'd rather go to Wonderland ~ which I would like!  The "$5 after 5pm" deal is quite tempting.  Plus, Classified with Keys N Krates are performing on Friday night, which is doubly tempting since I'm a fan of the Nova Scotia rapper =P  I think there were plans being made by a bunch of furs to go on Sunday? 

FanExpo Canada
I had plans to go on Sunday with some highschool friends, but it seems to have fallen through.  The convention has become another one of those events I'd go to just to say I've been - I think it'd be a waste of money.  The only real attraction is the dealers area just because it's so massive and there are a lot of things to see, but I don't end up buying anything.  Some tables give out free swag, usually promotional comics, but you've pretty much paid for them via admission ticket. 

Scotiabank Buskerfest
Free entertainment from an array of talented street performers...wicked!  I think I'll be checking it outon Saturday with  ~ lets make this happen dude =3.

Blah...

Aug. 24th, 2010   11:45 AM
Where is the line drawn between being lonely and being needy?  It seems like a lot of the time I am the one who calls people out to have a good time.  It's usually not the opposite especially among friends I enjoy being around.  I don't like being needy while at the same time I hate being lonely.  I'm sure there are a number of valid reasons, but I think about it more than I should.
