How I pass the time...

Aug. 22nd, 2010   12:28 AM
I haven't watched any anime in a long while, until recently I had a geeky conversation with my friend Jeno.  Now I'm seven episodes into the series 'Ghost Hound' and waiting for 'Arashi no Yoru ni' to finish downloading =3.  The series has a slow and kinda weird beginning but the story is getting interesting.  I like how it's got a bit of Japanese horror to it and the soundtrack is quite different from what I'm normally use to.  I find the characters are hard to like since they're fairly linear, but hopefully they'll unfold as I get deeper into the series ^^;;

To further curb my boredom, I've started watching 'Batman: The Brave and the Bold' via YouTube; i'm seven episodes into the first season.  This IS NOT the Batman I grew up with.  This series is so toned down and full of lulz, cliches, and horrible one liners that it's so bad, it's good! o__O  I have facepalmed myself more times then I can count XD.  The only cool thing I like so far is that each episode features another superhero in the DC Universe fighting alongside Batman.  The one thing I dislike is that there isn't any other supporting characters or story outside of the action ~ what's Batman without Alfred or Bruce Wayne? 

============================================

On the topic of progress, I am on page 123 of 656 in 'The Amazing Adventures of Kavalier & Clay' by Michael Chabon.  Sadly I've only been reading it during breakfast so it's been slow =\. 

============================================

Tomorrow is the finals for the men's Cincinnati Masters and women's Rogers Cup.  I haven't exactly been following either all week, but the draws on the men's side have been quite interesting.  I really wish I could have watched some of the matches leading up the the finals, there were a lot of great meetings particularly for Roddick and Baghdatis (quite possibly the jolliest player on tour).  I really hope Federer wins against Fish tomorrow because he hasn't been winning enough tournament this year lol.  I'll probably watch the match if I've got nothing to do, but I'd really like to go out since I've been staying at home nearly all week .___."

============================================

I missed my second ageplay munch in a row last night due to a family thing =\.  Oh wells, I'm not really bummed.  It was my great grandma's 105th birthday dinner ~ although technically her birthday is next week, but she's as healthy as an ox.  Plus, I probably wouldn't have wanted to commute downtown in the pouring rain >>".
😶 blank  |  ♫ Mayumi Kojima - Poltergeist
Link

Being awesome...together!

Aug. 20th, 2010   1:20 PM
Bored but living on a budget?  Welcome to the club!  \o.o/  Hit up the websites below for amazing deals - but the catch is that it requires teamwork =P.  Each day a new deal will be posted but they can only be activated if enough people sign on to them; so if something catches your interest buy now, spread the word, hope it goes through, and then have fun!  Now if only I got paid to promote these sites that'd be a sweet deal lol, oh well, share the love ^-^

Groupon     TeamBuy     $tealtheDeal     WagJag     TeamSave

Don't forget to change the city if they don't do it automatically!

==============================================================

I'm a couple weeks late to the party but I recently started playing 'Alien Swarm' ~ it's a free top-down shooter game by Valve that you can download on Steam.  The cool part about it is that it's kinda like 'Left 4 Dead' such that it has four player co-op online.  Teamwork is pretty important when your Tech is unlocking and sealing doors while a mob of aliens are rushing you.  And of course having a Medic is vital.  I've never gotten pass the first level in offline practice mode, but only because I haven' been playing it too often ^^;;.  I don't like how it's fairly easy to run out of ammo and pick-ups are very sparce.  If you aren't a fan of the view, you can switch it to first-person by following these instructions ~ but I find the mouse sensitivity really slow and I don't know how to adjust it in this mode =\. 

==============================================================

Lastly, the traffic on my blog has jumped up recently for whatever reason o__O.  I never really know who reads the things I write particularly when the number of friends I have on LJ is what it is and I don't get comments often.  But when I do talk to people whether online or in real life and they tell me they're a reader, it always surprises me.  Even though I mainly write for my own enjoyment (and later recollection), thank you all for the support ~ you guys are awesome! =3
♫ Shred Kelly - Goodbye July

Playing is my forte!

Aug. 17th, 2010   7:08 PM
Wheeee!  I had an incredibly good time yesterday at another “Toronto Littles’ Play Date” :D  There have been only a number of times where I’ve genuinely felt like a little boy, and this was one of them!  Having my daddy with me really made a difference and of course Seven hosts the most awesome fun events! Prior to the event, I hung out at daddy’s place when he got off work.  We relaxed on the balcony, talking, nomming on popcorn, and I had a sippy cup of fizzy lemonade.  With comfort food in mind, we decided to have nuggies for dinner ~ something I haven’t eaten in ages!  Daddy changed me into my shortalls and helped pack the diaper bag before going; I brought along my blankie, sippy cup, pacifier, a teddy bear, and some water guns because it was suppose to be a beach party! >:3  We definitely got some looks while out and about, probably due to the colourful way I dressed and the odd duo of questionable relation.  I hope it didn’t make daddy feel uncomfortable but in those moments it really didn’t faze me ~ I was totally in my own little head space.  Wicked! :P We arrived at the play date some thirty minutes late, and while mostly everyone was settling into their own activities, Seven was still setting up.  Surprisingly, there were two little boys present whom I’d met a year or two ago at the local ageplay munch (back when it was at Fran’s).  They sorta dropped off the radar for a while and to see them again all the sudden was really cool!  Plus, after months of trying to get him to come to an event, Winter Tw Wolf was there :D  I also got to meet a new little girl, Naina, and her daddy ~ they were really fun! Daddy read me ‘Green Eggs and Ham’ – doing a great job at expressing the characters hehe – before I got busy decorating cupcakes with everyone.  This months sample design was either a flower or a ladybug made with cookies, candy and icing.  Some of us also made abstract looking thingies; it got pretty messy but it was a lot of fun!  The best part was eating them XD.  Afterwards I went off and doodled on the ground with a bunch of really big pieces of chalk!  Someone had already drawn a gianormous dragon so I added a mouse and spaceship to the scene :P  Naina also added lots of flowers and we drew her daddy holding a camera cause that’s exactly what he was doing hehe.  Eventually everyone else joined in and added a little something ^-^. It’s a bummer how time flies when you’re having fun! D:  Before wrapping up the event, all the little’s cuddled by the sofas and daddy read us a story about bunnies going on a picnic.  It was such a relaxing way to end the night <3.  After cleaning up a bit, I went back to daddy’s place to change into my big boy clothes ~ although some how the diaper stayed haha.  Then he drove me home ^-^. 
🐇 bouncy  |  ♫ Shad K - Yaa I Get It (Oddisee Remix)

Kiss and blog #19

Aug. 16th, 2010   11:00 AM

(continuing from last post)

We had some great sex the other night.  Daddy licked my ass and stuck his tongue deep in me ~ it felt amazing!  But it was a bit of a turn off when he'd kiss me and it'd smell like poop.  I have the same problem after being fingered (which he did with I think three ~ again, way hot!) and he's touching my body.  It's just unsanitary and kinda gross.  

I didn't want him to use his dildo because it was big and hurt me last time, so instead he used his own dick.  He pumped me long, hard, and deep.  He had great stamina and I totally loved it.  Unfortunately I couldn't come for him even after getting a blowjob and jerking off.  There's just something rather disappointing about that :(

We showered and then got thickly padded before calling it a night. 



(Not to undermine anyone else I hang out with, it goes without saying that I appreciate all the good times.  I live on fun vibes and it keeps me distracted from being bored.  Thank you as well :D)
 

Link

A breath of fresh air

Aug. 16th, 2010   10:53 AM
Last night I had a relaxing time with daddy; we talked a lot over coffee =3.  It was exactly what I needed - other than having a Tim Hortons craving - because we touched base on a few things I'd been stewing over.  I don't lead a particularly boring life but I have a hard time making myself believe otherwise when there are days that I don't do anything at all.  Such are the ones following days when I've had fun ~ for example, earlier this week since coming home from the convention and Saturday.  What's troubling to me is that when I do feel as though I'm marinating in boredom, I become introspective and I think about my regrets, values, friendships, desires, weaknesses, fears and so forth.  It's depressing, unmotivating, and it makes me feel fustrated. 

In light of how I put myself in these uncomforting slumps, I'm glad that there are people in my life I can turn to for advice.  It just makes it extra special when it's my daddy.  Whether his role is active or not when I'm being grownup and talking to him (I'd like to think that it always is), I feel at ease and secure about opening up.  I rarely do because more often than not I listen more than I talk and as a result I have poor communication skills.  I've always enjoyed being in the company of people much older than me and I tease him about it all the time, but it's true ~ he's got "sage" advice.  The things old people say hold greater weight because of their experiences, perspectives, and insights.  It's completely awesome!  (No offense to the younger gents, but I have a hard time acknowledging daddies who are are under forty >>"  Sorry.)

Thank you for the pep talk.  You are more than just a friend and you reinforced your role as my daddy whether you were trying to or not ~ I love you for that :)
💪 determined  |  ♫ Metric - Sick Muse

Aug. 13th, 2010   3:52 PM
It's Friday the 13th!  Anyone get struck with bad luck? D:

==============================================

Gonna go watch 'Scott Pilgrim vs The World' with Pete later tonight!  I've read four of the six volumes and it's really entertaining ~ the visuals and style of the movie based on the trailers looks like its done justice.  Plus, looking forward to some crinkly/cuddly good fun afterwards ^///^.

==============================================

Happy birthday Jully! :D  Grrr!  Why you gotta be older than me by three one month? >:(  I'll be sure to take you out for food or drinks sometime ~ don't stop being awesome! =P

==============================================

The Rogers Cup is in town since the beginning of the week and I haven't been paying much attention to it =(  I'm a bit bummed that Soderling lost the other day, but Nalbandian's playing has been on fire lately so it's well deserved.  And it sucks that Roddick pulled out of the tournament ~ he drops to 11th in the ATP ranking (he's been in the top ten for year too!)

==============================================

Blah local furry drama.  Dan Skunk's Ontario Furries Forum is shut down and now there are three (of not more) forums people are venturing off to.  Take your pick =\.  TorFurs.  Canadian Furries.  Ontario Furs.  I think I'm just going to put myself further into lurk-mode ~ forums aren't really my thing anymore. 

I'll get to the completing the con reports whenever =P

Horsey good time

Aug. 12th, 2010   8:48 PM
I met a horse today ~ his name is Sterling!  How often do you get to say that?! O3O



Thanks to a good friend of mine, I got to hang out and meet his horse this morning.  It was an incredible experience even though I was just a spectator (heh no "I'm on a horse" jokes XP).  I can't get over how huge and powerful they are D:  While at the same time they're so gentle and intelligent.  What a majestic and calming animal to be around =3.  (Although, Sterling is a bit lazy and likes to snack a lot haha, but who wouldn't want to live that kinda life? XD)  I suppose the only bummer was all the poop and flies at the farm!  But that's unavoidable =P

   

Good times ~ it certainly helped curve my post-con depression ^^;;.  Thanks again man, seeing you beaming with content made my day :D

Unfinished Con Report: Anthrocon 2010

Aug. 5th, 2010   8:36 PM
So I'm going to attempt to sum my Anthrocon in a quickly written report since I've been holding it off for way too long and I want to get it done with before my next con, 'Condition: Zero Hour'...which I'm leaving for in a couple of hours! :D

Wednesday
- headed up to Brampton to crash for the night with Dan Raccoon and Shiro since we'd be flying down together
- mostly just distracted Shiro while he worked on con badges ~ it was way awesome of him to have made me one! <3  Thanks!
- Dan got home really late from work!  Instead of sleeping he packed his luggage while Shiro and I played Guitar Hero =P

Thursday
- we headed out the door by 4:45am, it rained a shit storm while making our way to the bus stop
- it didn't help that I had rolled my ankle recently, so I got uber drenched
- arrived at the airport before 6am and cleared security within an hour
- blah, sat in the waiting area for two hours before boarding ~ at least the skies cleared up
- recieved a text from PadderCat, he was flying out too ~ the race was on! >:3
- first time I've ever been in such a small plane, it had maybe 25 passangers?
- Yay!  Window seat! \o.o/  Got to see a lot of clouds...and more clouds...and even more clouds!
- dude sitting next to Dan sparked a pretty interesting discussion about furries, he seemed genuinely curious ^-^
- saw a lot of hills and trees while landing in Pittsburgh ~ first impression is pretty spiffy!
- off the plane by 11am and took a wicked shuttle thingie to the baggage claim area hehe
- stopped for food and pocket change to get on the bus
- 'Welcome to Anthrocon' was written on the digital sign board at the airport! XD
- at the bus stop you could already point out those going to Anthrocon just by their attire lol
- while on the bus after it makes a bend around a hill, the cityscape (most notibally the gianormous yellow bridges and the monsterous football stadium) just pans out and it's incredible beautiful!
- just short of 1pm we arrived at the Omni Hotel, there's already a ton of people including some suiters!
- loafed around Dan and Shiro's suite waiting for Luca and Pawpy ~ they both fell asleep while I watched TV
- Luca and Pawpy arrive by 2:30pm and we're off to register at the main hotel (Westin)
- bumped into a ton of people while making my way to the registration line ~ Toracub, Xaxoqual, Soba, Jeno, PadderCat, Siddy, Javi Roo, Dantee, Babs, Dash, Hypr, Howiepup (poor Deoge broke his collar bone!  Ouch!), Danruk, Odin, Matthiasrat, Tempest, D Bandit, Baby Tiger and probably a few others I've forgotten ^^;;
- Sweet!  Picked up a badge I pre-ordered from PadderCat ~ it's bigger than I expected! O___O  I love it!
- wandered around the con space and chatted with people after registering ~ I got a spiffy 'Sponsor' ribbon hehe
- after playing phone tag for a loooong while, finally got to meet Happyfeet and Pinko!  *happy hugs!*
- lulz, Happyfeet had issues with registering because they put him down as a minor XD
- got a call from BushyCat, my rommie, so I headed up to the room to get my room key ~ Odin tagged along ^-^
- afterwards, Odin and I went to Happyfeet, Pinko, and their three roomies room to get drunk! :D
- but first we had to get the liquor =S, bumped into FireStormSix and Kit Kiama along the way!
- blah!  Climbed nine flights of stairs carrying a case of water and alcohol back to Happyfeet's room
- mmmm taking shots of vanilla vodka
- I brought Happyfeet a Canadian souvenier ~ maple syrup!  So we mixed it with the vodka! XD  Tasted like butterscotch <3
- lulz, and then we mixed peanut butter, grape jelly, with the mapple syrup and vanilla vodka X3  It tasted like ass!
- Happyfeet recorded it all ~ you sooo have to send me the vids dude! 
- next Happyfeet and Pinko went down to the lobby to buy some soda...to mix...which we did but this time at Odin's room XP
- afterwards we decided to head out to buy (shamefully) more liquor
- just outside I bumped into Dantee with a couple of other hookah-furs!  We had a bit of yummy watermelon sheesha <3
- blah, walked as far as the Doubletree hotel; practically everything was closed or didn't sell booze
- went to a nearby cheesesteak restaurant to get some noms
- back at the Westin, I bumped into Dan Raccoon and Shiro ~ plus got the room number for the Cub Hub ^-^
- ate my sammich and drank the remaining booze at Happyfeet and Pinko's room with Odin
- Odin and I decided to head up to the Cub Hub sometime after 9pm, but got stopped in the elevator by his Rock Band friends
- so we went to their room for drinks ~ I had something with whisky in it and tasted like peppermints...I was way tipsy at that point =P
- went to the Cub Hub not long after and nommed on cookies and rum pound cake with a bunch of other cubs XD
- sometime before 10pm I left with Dan Raccoon, Shiro, and Pawpy to get my luggage out of their room and into my own
- while making the trip between hotels I bumped into Xaxoqual, Blueberry Baby, and Crassadon
- sometime before 11pm I remember heading to "Who's Lion is it Anyways" just to catch up with Happyfeet, Pinko, and Dantee
- luckily I met up with themso we went drinking up in Dantee's room ~ got to meet his roomies, fun bunch :D
- I had something with Absinthe in it, and two of something with Midori in it lol
 by about 11:40pm, Dantee got in his fursuit and we all headed down to the Zoo ~ talked with Mako, Jeno, Xaxoqual, Shngy, and Bigfox Pye
- ended up going up to the Cub Hub at around 12:30am with Xaxoqual for half an hour before retiring to bed ~ there were a couple of suiters at the hub :D

Friday
- Agh!  Recieved two wake up calls before 6am because my roomies - BushyCat and Jinxtigr - needed to set up for the Dealer's Den.  Worst way to wake up with a hang over!
- plus I slept on an air mattress next to the vents which were blasting cold air =.=**
- blah, got recruited to help BushyCat move her stuff down to the Dealer's Den sometime before 8am
- wandered the con space afterwards and bumped into Crassadon ~ on the way to a sammich shop we bumped into Hypr and Odin X3
- after breakfast, I went with Crassadon to register
- there was a long line up for the Dealer's Den already ~ Toya Pup was waiting near the front and spotted me, so I cut in line (o.o)
- apparently Toya knew me through Blue Ferret (O___O)
- while waiting for the Den to open, I played Five Card Poker and Big Two with Yeyon, Stormy Wolf Pup, Toya, Crassadon, and Bigfox Pye
- as it was about to open, Paws and Kalika also cut in line XD  I gotta feel bad for the people behind us haha
- I followed Toya to Marci's table ~ would have been fourth to take an order had I wanted one; she was only accepting ten at the time
- lulz, Toya was beaming with joy whilte getting his commission ordered; we headed to Mary Mouse and Michele Light after where he got some more commissions =P
- surprisingly, the Dealer's Den seemed a lot tamer than I imagined it to be
- Toya and I proceeded to the Artist Alley, where he got another commission from Shiro ~ around his table I met up with Pawpy, Crassadon, and Dan Raccoon
- among probably many others while in the Dealer's Den, I bumped into Odin, Unicow, Happyfeet, Pinko, Cavalier (BT in suit), Tempest, Lore Fox, PadderCat, Frostscar, and Xaxoqual
- there is nothing I want more than a Tani Dereal mood badges set, but unfortunately she wasn't available to doing them T___T Bwaaa!
- while wandering the Den, I got to meet Wondering Wolf and his girlfriend Len (w00t!  A furrypino!)
- apaprently Wondering Wolf knew of me through Blue Ferret ~ dude you talk a lot! XD  It's cool though =P
- later I went back to Marci's table with Toya to pick up his commission ~ lulz for joking at how she's like a photocopier that charges up the ass!
- at some point Toya, Wondering Wolf, Len, and I bumped into PadderCat, Kalika, and Sliken, so we headed up to their room for a bit
- we eventually deciding to go out and eat pizza before going our seperate ways
- I went back to my room and took a nap till about 4:30pm
- went down to the Gaming Room and spotted Luca, so we played Rock Band before heading to the Dealer's Den
- I had remembered about picking up my Tavi tag, so I went to Nexxi and Zuumie's table with Dan Raccoon and Happyfeet
- boo, they haven't arrived yet, but it was awesome meeting the two ^-^
- went to the Art Gallery with Happyfeet ~ we talked a lot more than looking at stuff
- there was talk about having sushi for dinner with Toya and Fen Black, so I rounded up a bunch of people ~ Happyfeet, Pinko, Xaxoqual, Dan Raccoon, Shiro, and Unicow
- unfortunately we all bummed outside the Dealer's Den when it closed and waited for Shiro to get paid for his sales in the Artist Alley, that took a long while!
- while making our way out the hotel, I bumped into Kit Kiama and Jeno (two people I'd run into quite frequently throughout the con XD)
- we finally made it to the restaurant by 7:30pm ~ sunk two boats full of sushi XP
- hung out in Toya's room with Fen afterwards ~ we were later joined by Cerilian, Paws, and Lore Fox
- I eventually went to the Cub Hub for the 'Pants Off Party' ~ quite literally, take off your pants (and wear a diaper) when you walk in! :D
- among others that were already there or showed up later included: Firefox, Fox Cub, Pardis, Pasha, Dan Raccoon, Shiro, Stormy, Natasha Softpaw, Pandr, Cloveroo, Lore Fox, Neon Bunny, Toracub, Cheeta, Stage Lion, Myth, Darkrage, Baby Tiger, Radix, Kynewolf, Kelby, Teddy MD, Patches, Unicow, and Landis
- there was a fun game a bunch of us played involving a device that gave answers but you had to make questions for other people to answer
- if no one answered before it buzzed you'd have to pour a cup of water down your diaper XD  It was awesome!
- I got my crinkles changed twice by...I won't say who...but it was way sweet of you! <3
- aside from meeting and talking with a bunch of people, I got stupid drunk after eight or so cocktails in me =S
- I stayed till about 2:30am and helped clean up a bit before stumbling to bed

****Times up ~ I failed =\.  I have to head out soon, so I'll finish this later ^^;;
😴 tired  |  ♫ Mickey Factz - Turn It Up

Follow up from last post...

Aug. 4th, 2010   11:58 PM
The nothern lights were a lie!  >:(  My dad and I drove way past Aurora (about 50km north) and star gazed till 2am.  We saw a lots of stars, a few comets, a couple of planes mistaking them for shooting stars, and a red banana moon.  =.=  Where's the lights?!  Boo!  While out there I was following Twitter and searching to see if other people saw them but most didn't or lived nowhere near me XP.  

=================================================================

Went to the free Metric concert tonight prior to going to the monthly furry dinner.  It was awesome but boy was it crowded!  They closed a small section of Front St; from Bay to York.  The concert was sponsored by Samsung to promote one of their new phones, but when the spokesmen got on stage to talk no one showed him any love.  Everyone shouted for Metric! XD

Below are some pictures I took, but you can find some really good ones online like here.

 
     
 
🐇 bouncy  |  ♫ Metric - Black Sheep
Link

Local stuff update...

Aug. 3rd, 2010   7:55 PM
Tonight the northern lights/Aurora boriealis are suppose to be visible in parts of Canada!  It's a bit cloudy right now, but I might drive out of the city when it's dark to check'em out!  I've never seen them in real life before so it'd be quite amazing to catch a glimpse ^-^. 

=================================================================

Drama on the local Ontario Furries Forum has led quite a few core members and friends of mine to remove themselves; either due to ban or leaving by choice.  Personally I just ignore it and take no side with the issue.  I mainly go on the forum for the events listings anyways =\.  But here's the official Furnal Equinox statement to clarify what's going on between them and the forum.  Quite interesting ~ I hope this doesn't affect next years con!

=================================================================

Hmmm, what to do tomorrow...the monthly furry dinner or free Metric concert?  I really like Metric and I'm totally for supporting a local band...they're both really close to one another in terms of time and location...hmmm....

Random stuff update...

Aug. 2nd, 2010   11:18 PM
Brilliant!


If you donno TED than you are missing out on some incredible stuff!  This is a clip from last years TEDx event in Toronto  with Gavin Sheppard speaking about creative education.  This year's TEDxToronto is on September 30th, 2010 ^-^. 

===================================================================

I have backed-up all of my blog entries and removed all the pictures up until this year.  It was at 300+ and it is now at 100+, but I still think it's a bit excessive XD.  Oh wells.  I'm also thinking of proof reading and revising some of the tags to all my old entries, but that could take a while ^^;;

===================================================================

I'm really digging these two blogs that I've recently been following...

Children of the 90s ~ "Dedicated to the reverence and perseverance of all things 90s".  This blog covers everything from toys, movies, catch phrases, fads, celebrates, and so forth.  It's a real blast of nostalgia!  \o.o/

ABDL Daddy ~ "Adult Babies from a Dad's Perspective".  I love Daddy Josh's insights and experiences; it's very much the ideal daddy that I'd like to have ^^;;  (Read some of this daddy D:  It's your homework hehe)

===================================================================

I have started a new book that daddy lent me!  'The Amazing Adventures of Kavalier & Clay' by Michael Chabon ~ it's a highly praised piece of fiction!  It's about two Jewish boys living through World War II in America while working in the comic industry.  I'm only on page 24 of 656 so I've got a long while to go heh, but so far I'm really enjoying the way it's writen ~ very fun to follow! :D

===================================================================

My left leg is giving me problems again =(  I went jogging two days ago for the first time since rolling my ankle ~ but then again I've been doing a ton of walking with it anyways.  And then later that day I went to the gym and swam for a bit lol, so it was pretty overkill but it felt great until the day after.  Everytime I lift the leg a certain way I get a really sharp, shooting pain in the knee joint area T__T.  I went jogging again today - I love torturing myself - so I'm either making it better or worse =S.  I should go see a doctor about this if it persists because I really don't think the possibility of 'permanent damage' has sunk into my head yet.  It's like, I know I'm young and sometimes I do feel invincible {o'.'o} but acknowledging reality is kind of a bummer that I don't want to do lol.  Blah, I don't like feeling unhealthy .___.
😶 blank  |  ♫ Sea Wolf - You're a Wolf

Aug. 2nd, 2010   8:30 PM
(continuing from last post) I gave daddy a blowjob :D  He was really thick and he pumped my mouth kinda deep so it was kinda choky and I got a bit teary, but I thought it was pretty hot hehe.  Daddy also made a hole in my Bambino and tried to butt fuck me, but he had a bit of issue with that >>".  It didn't want to go in my hole after many attempts and it didn't stay in too long.  I blame the diaper.  He used his long dildo toy on me and went really fast...it hurt a lot and I probably could have cried.  I should have told daddy to go slow so it was my fault ~ smex is all about communication and I'm not very good at that.  I jerked off for him and he jerked off for me; he dribbled in my diaper hehe.  He peed down my diaper and I did the same to him! Lazy Sunday full of cuddles...

Aug. 2nd, 2010   8:20 PM
I had a cuddly and crinkly time with my daddy yesterday from an otherwise boring long weekend XP.  I arrived at his place a little before noon and it took an hour and a half of cuddling to decide what else we should do for the rest of the day hehe.  We walked to The Blake House and enjoyed the great weather out on the patio ^-^.  I couldn't make it to last months munch and I've already made plans for the night of this months, so it was nice to have finally tried out the new location.  The food was delish and the service was very friendly!  They had a doggy bowl full of water in the front and occasionally people walking their dogs would come up.  So many cute fuzzies to goggle over O3O.

Unfortunately our plan making only went so far and we ended up back at his place and cuddled some more XD.  It was really comforting and I blame the itis for falling asleep hehe.  After the nap we listened to some lovely (but kinda repetitive) Diana Krall, a Canadian jazz singer and watched a bunch of funny shorts from the National Film Board of Canada.  The animated films by Oscar nominated Cordell Barker are a bit strange but hilarious in their own unique way!  Go watch 'The Cat Came Back' (1988), 'Strange Invaders' (2002), and 'Runaway' (2009) now! 

For dinner we decided to play it by ear and see what was good to eat in the Beaches neighbourhood.  We walked along Queen St E. and passed a bunch of cafes, Thai, Japanese, and grill restaurants.  There was this really awesome candy store (The Nutty Chocolatier) we went into that had a bit of everything!  Daddy bought some sort of chocolate, nut, and fudge bar thingie that tasted sooo good but gets stuck in your teeth hehe :D  Candy is totally a food group!  Out of all the restaurants we past, we had dinner at a shawarma place ~ it was totally a good idea!  Today's Shawarma is the place and it's relatively new.  The person behind the counter was really friendly!  He gave us each a sample of falafel, which I've never had before but was really yummy.  And just as we were finishing our delicious dinner he gave us some chocolate cake because it was his birthday!  That's so awesome of him <3  We wrapped up the night with some more relaxing cuddles back at daddy's place before putting me in a thick diaper and driving me home =3.  Good food and fun made for a great day! 
😄 cheerful  |  ♫ Diana Krall - Cry Me A River
Link

Diaper Boy Camp!

Jul. 30th, 2010   2:46 PM

I usually find starting an entry the hardest especially when I've procrastinate over something several days old.  So it's taken me a while to write about my crinkly weekend at Boyricky and his daddy's farm, but I think I'm in the right frame of mind to do this now!


There are some pictures in this entry and just to give you an idea here's my butt...

I love this pic because of the colours and my shoes lol. 

So on Friday afternoon Cameron came and picked me up, along with Dylan riding shotgun and Lenny in a car seat ~ bless his tiny little build XD.  I think a couple of other local ABDLs had considered going but couldn't due to timing and getting a ride, so that was a bit of a bummer.  Oh wells, I'm sure other opportunities will come ^-^.  The drive took a little over four hours and along the way we picked up some liquor ~ no restrooms needed since most of us were padded!  When we arrived Ricky showed us around the big plot of land and where we'd be staying.  For the most part we were either sleeping in the bunkhouse (it has an awesome second level accessible by ladder O3O) or hanging out in 'The Erection' (a cozy building that has a kitchen, family room, and sunroom). 

After changing into something more comfortable and settling in with a sippy cup beer, we got to meet Bobby, an ABDL local to the farming area ~ he was really cool!  While there was still light out, Ricky, Lenny and I went to collect firewood elsewhere on the farm.  I didn't put any bug repellent on and received some major itchy bites! T__T  The wood we collected was used to start a campfire where we cooked up dinner ~ hotdogs!  But the best part was roasting marshmallows and making yummy s'mores later at night :D  These marshmallows were way big and made for a sticky but fun mess hehe.  It was a relaxing night by the fire just boozing away with some great friends and I really enjoyed the fresh air while sleeping in the bunker.  It's too bad I didn't sleep all through the night though; besides the bugs (that sounded like vuvuzelas with wings!) someone was snoring crazy loud (I won't say who to spare the embarrassment ^^;;)



The next day I started my morning with pancakes and bacon for breakfast ~ it was delish!  Kudos to Cameron and Dylan for making them ^-^.  Ricky's daddy made me wear a bib just in case I made a mess D:  I really like how the dinning table seemed big because I was sitting on kiddie chairs, plus the plates and cutlery were cute!  After getting ready for the day, we all headed out to the farmers market and the opportunity store.  The farmers market was quaint and welcoming; there were booths for produce, baked goods, preservatives, pottery, etc.  It also had a living labyrinth - a maze constructed from a tall field - that was tricky to get out of! o.o  The opportunity store, which was a second hand freebie shop, had a bunch of fun stuff ~ Ricky scored two adorable Elmo plushies!  Afterwards a couple of us went into town to buy more booze and munchies while checking out some of the nifty shops.  We also got to meet Bobby's partner who'd both later join us at the farm for the rest of the day!  I found it funny how there were only white people everywhere XD. 

For lunch I got to sizzle up burgers for everyone ~ little boys shouldn't be playing with the stove D:  One or two of them came out pretty fugly cause they got squished (o__O) but it still tasted yummy!  In the afternoon Ricky, Lenny, and I went for a dip at a nearby river in just our diapers.  That was way fun!  I love that gushing feeling when the diaper is about to become oversaturated, it's quite a rush hehe.  And then white fluff began floating out my rear ~ my diaper had popped!  It was embarrassing but totally hilarious {o'.'o}  We snapped a pic of it just for jokes XD.  In the middle of boozing and having a great time, two families came down to our spot on the river.  They were able to see what we were wearing and drinking out of, but that kind of embarrassment I can live with since I'd never see them again.  The first family stayed and enjoyed the water.  They didn't say anything and gave us a few looks; the father stood between us and his kids kinda solemnly lol.  The second family just left when they saw us =P. 



Back at the farm we all got much needed changes from daddy before going off and playing some more.  I love how Ricky's daddy gets happier the more 'little boy' you behave ~ it's so easy and fun to do! ^-^  While a bunch of us were relaxing in the sun room, Ricky and I built a huge tower (on wheels!) out of Duplo and Mega Bloks.  It was so cool!  And then we destroyed it haha XD.  For dinner we had pizza entirely made from scratch; Bobby and his partner did all of the work and were really skilled at it.  I got to help out a tiny bit but mainly because I just wanted to mush and squish the dough O3O.  So if it ever tasted like it wasn't suppose to, then it'd be my bad heh.  I thought it was really delish though!  After doing the dishes we got the projector set up and watched an episode of 'Dinosaurs' followed by 'The Simpsons Movie' =3  The rest of the night was mostly spent outside mellowing out by the campfire.  I had a cozy sleeper on and Merci, my polar bear plush, to snuggle with <3  A couple of us also spent a bit of time chilling in 'The Lounge' - another building with a bar and glowy alien decorations everywhere - before retiring to bed.

     
I love that fist bumping pic =P.  And the tower has wheels! D:  Madness! 

On the last day at the farm, we had a late but yummy breakfast of pancakes, bacon, and potatoes.  I got to help chop up the potatoes ~ little boys should be playing with knives! O__O  Most of the day was spent indoors because there were scattered showers =(.  But that's okay, boozing and hanging out was way relaxing.  A bunch of us came up with get-rich-quick schemes that was jokes XD.  Because of the late meal we skipped lunch and were ready to get back on the road to Toronto by the afternoon.  Prior to taking off, Ricky and his daddy gave us each 'Diaper Boy Camp' certificates with cute prints on them!  We had to sign them using our non-writing hand to make them look childish and even more authentic-like!  (I left mine in Cameron's car ><*  Hopefully I'll remember to get it back)  It was sad leaving since I had such a wonderful time and met some charming new friends, but hopefully I'll get to do it again sometime ^-^.  I slept through most of the drive since I was a bit tipsy heh, and we stopped at a Harvey's for dinner ~ which killed my stomach when I got home =S  I didn't have any cell phone reception all weekend, so my bad to those who tried to reach me =P.  Good times though and a big thanks to Ricky and his daddy for having me over!


I approve this camp!
😄 cheerful  |  ♫ Billy Danze (ft. Busta Rhymes) - Undescribable
Link

Wow man...

Jul. 29th, 2010   12:11 AM
Just came home not long ago from watching 'Inception' with my bro.  Un-freakin-believable!  It blew my mind and left me thinking the whole drive home.  It definitely deserves an Academy Award for the level of thought put into it and the acting was very good.  Rarely does a movie leave me so amazed!  Bravo. 

1 LOVE T.O....Pop-Up Shop - Part 3...Tomorrow at Revival Bar...5-10pm!
I'm going to try to make it so that I can buy one of their t-shirts, but if not I can always get my brother to help me since he doesn't work too far from the bar.  If you don't know what '1 LOVE T.O.' is, it's a blog run by a couple of locals that predominately focuses on art, music, social culture, new media, etc.  I really dig the website and I really love their tees!

Get my fur on...

Jul. 26th, 2010   11:22 PM
Grooving back into the furriness after a month of not hanging out with furiends ~ minus Pride Parade and the monthly dinner XP.  Helping me pounce back into things was Paws(thegame), a Montreal fur I met at 'What The Fur'.  He had some vacation time to kill so he came down to Toronto for a few days ^-^.  It was too generous of him to have treated me to lunch even though we went for cheap and tasty Chinese food XD ~ thanks again man!  I love how most of our talking revolved around comparing our two cities, foods, ethnic backgrounds, and whatever, just for laughs ~ it was definitely good times.  He brought his new doggie, Whisky, along and oh my gawd is he so much fun.  Whisky is only two years old so he's still got a lot of training to do, but he is incredibly friendly and way gentle.  We took him for a walk around Shops at Don Mills and Edwards Garden before going our separate ways.  I've never walked a dog before but jeez they can be a handful as well as being total chick magnets!  I want a dog when I moved out LOL!  :D

I think I've got a week to decide whether to attend 'Camp Feral' in September before reg ends.  But in the mean time I'm totally looking forward to 'Condition: Zero Hour' in August! ^-^

I'll be making an entry about my crazy fun and crinkly weekend in the days to follow.  And probably before Condition I'll have an Anthrocon con report to share =P.  But those entirely depend on what will be keeping me busy this week since last minute plans have a way of finding me XD. 

Lastly, what's with all the new layouts?!? D:  Hotmail...Google Images... FurAffinity...I don't like .___.   Blah, I've got a 17856S and 6567J...I think I'll nuke everything ^^;;
♫ Junia-T (prod. Rich Kidd) - Hypnotic

Crinkle weekend

Jul. 23rd, 2010   12:12 AM
Ahhh glorious!  It's the weekend again!  Man, I sure haven't been writing many entries this month but it's not like I haven't been having a great time.  I thought it'd be pretty mellow since every month prior there has been a con to look forward to.  But truthfully, after Anthrocon I'm fine with there being no con to attend this month ~ I'm rather 'furried out'.  I don't think there is an off button to being a furry, but being physically distant from furry friends this month has been pretty enjoyable =P.  Its given me a chance to have fun as a little kid - instead of a cub - among other ageplayers.  I mean, this month has totally been about diapers, playing and hanging out with other ABDL's! ^-^ 

Tomorrow I'm heading up, along with a couple of other ABDL's, to Boyricky and his daddy's farm for the weekend.  It'll be similar to going to the cottage but with way more open space to roam around in just a diaper hehe.  I really hope the weather isn't poop and I'm anticipating some sort of rules we'll have to follow, which should be interesting XD.  This will be a lot of fun! \o.o/

Some good listening...

Jul. 20th, 2010   11:28 PM
This video was made by a Broken Social Scene (a Canadian indie rock band) fan as a response to the G20 Summit in Toronto last month.  The rest speaks for itself.  (Warning: may cause seizure, eyes strain, and/or headaches...but I think it's worth it =P)



I'm a big fan of conscious hip hop and I've really been digging 'Revolutions per Minute' by Reflection Eternal (a duo of Talib Kweli and DJ Hi-Tek).  Practically whatever Kweli spits is golden, but he really steps up when surrounded by talented featured guests like Estella, Mos Def, Jay Electronica, J. Cole and others.  Plus, Hi-Tek's choice of samples is mixed flawlessly the entire album!  I highly recommend those interested to check it out! 




Lastly, Toronto producer and rapper Rich Kidd is set to release Vol. 4 of 'We On Some Rich Kidd Sh*t' next week ~ it should be great!  The first three mixtapes can be downloaded here.  Check out this track he produced for k-os (another local rapper), Saukrates, and Nelly Furtado!


🌀 energetic  |  ♫ Reflection Eternal (ft. Estelle) - Midnight Hour

Kiss and blog #16

Jul. 20th, 2010   8:21 PM
(continuing from last post) On Friday, Pete and I downed two bottles of champagne, so we were a bit tipsy heh.  He had a ton of condoms but no lube, so even though I let him have my ass, he couldn't get it in without it hurting, so we didn't continue.  He stuck a butt plug up my ass and let me keep it, so that was way generous of him! <3  He's really into the diapers and sex thing ~ I let him dry hump my crinkle butt and he came on my back! He has furry tendencies!  A koala fan ~ maybe convert him haha. On Monday daddy and I did a bit of kissing and feeling each other up ~ he had his foot on my crotch which got me a bit hard.  Daddy diapered me and dry humped me, which got me hard.  I wanted to come for daddy, so I took the top takes of my diaper off and fapped.  It took a while, but with some encouragement I came for him.  I'm happy I did!

Days gone by...

Jul. 20th, 2010   4:44 PM
Hiya!  Although I haven't written in the last couple of days, if you read my Twitter you know I've been out and about ^-^.  But like I mentioned when I began using Twitter, it ain't gonna replace me from blogging on here cause I love doing it! =3  So what have I been up to...

Friday night I hung out with Pete, whom I met earlier in the month at an ABDL party.  We chilled in his sparsely furnished apartment ~ I don't think I've ever seen anyone with a smaller tv =P.  I supplied the crinkles and he supplied the champagne; fair trade!  It was nice getting to know new friends by just loafing around and talking rather than doing stuff, especially since it feels like I don't take the time to do that often.  But I'm always a bit jealous at people younger than me who've already moved out haha ~ good on them though! XP.  We ate some really burnt pizza and watched parts of 'The Sixth Sense' before calling it a night.  I left my hat at his place and now i can't wear any clothes that go with it or else I'd feel incomplete .____."  (lol...don't judge me! XD) (Oh snap, I just remembered, I'm gonna have to convert him to furriness O3O.  He exhibits great interest in it and holds a passion for koalas heh.  But I've had my fill of furriness this month, so many more on that next month XP)

Saturday was way boring; I went to Ottawa with my family.  We try to go once or twice a year to visit family and friends, but also to pay our respects to deceased relatives ~ Its been exactly a year since my grandpa died =(  Waking up early was a pain, but sitting in the car for four and a half hours was worse.  It would've been less boring if I'd gotten to drive =.=**  Luckily, thanks to MSN on my cellphone I passed the time by connecting with people I less frequently chat with =3.  What kills me is that we stayed in Ottawa for only three hours!  But at least it was really great seeing my grandma again ~ she's always so quick wit and sarcastically funny =P.  While heading home we stopped at Kingston for two hours to see some family friends and eat dinner.  When we were back in Toronto at around 9pm, my brother and I hit up the gym and pool to unwind =3.   

Sunday for no particular reason I stayed at home all day and played Team Fortress 2 and Second Life.  I'd be having connection problems with TF2 for the last two weeks and as a result of that I'm no longer in the top ten ranking for the server I play on XP.  Oh wells, I'm more keen on unlocking the new Engineer gear ~ they look like something that'd raise my game especially now that I know how to make multiple builds! O3O  As for Second Life, it's always fun (when I'm bored) hanging out and chatting with creative people.  I watched and mildly distracted Camron build stuff on Happyfeet's sim ~ it's gianormous! D:

Monday was pretty awesome!  In the morning I went to the gym, swam for a bit, and then hit the sauna.  Ate lunch, killed time on the interwebz, and watched 'The Book of Eli'.  I still can't take Mila Kunis's acting seriously, but Denzel Washington was bad ass.  The story was just okay since you know how it'll end.  There were a few 'wtf' moments that really made the movie though!  I had a few hours to waste before going to the Toronto Littles' Play Date so I msg'd a few people to see if they wanted to do a quick dinner someplace downtown.  My daddy got back to me first so I ate at his place ~ I figure we'd eat out, but when I got to his place he was already cooking!  Eating out on his balcony was really nice and raspberry sherbert for dessert was delish! ^///^  We kinda lost track of time after getting padded and cuddling for a bit, I ended up being two hours late for the TLPD haha.  It was worth it =P. 

This months playdate was a movie event, so I walked in on the middle of 'The Princess Bride'.  It's a really silly movie and I enjoyed it with a sippy cup of juice while cuddling a plushie!  In the movie there are creatures called 'Rodents of Unusual Size', so everyone got to decorate mean looking rat cupcakes XD.  It was a bummer when the movie ended because mostly everyone began calling it a night ~ I didn't get to play with any of the toys! T__T  At least I got to meet a little boy and his daddy; both were new to the event and seemed really cool.  The little boy had this awesome toy gun that shoot darts and made sounds ~ I sooo want one! O3O  After helping with cleaning up, Stefonknee gave me a lift to Yonge and Dundas which was way awesome of her!  Good day, good night, I say ^-^. 
🐇 bouncy  |  ♫ Rammstein - Ohne Dich
Link

Jul. 16th, 2010   8:10 PM
(continuing from last post) More butt smex back at daddy's place again ~ this time he put a whole in my diaper and fucked me through that XD.  We made out prior and he was really hard in his undies hehe.  I couldn't cum for him afterwards when jerking myself off :( We showered together and he padded me in a thich Abena with stuffer ~ I made it leak the next morning haha.   Great food and smex are a great combination!

Food and fun pics!

Jul. 16th, 2010   2:28 PM
It's the weekend again!  \o.o/  Sometimes when I look back on this blog it feels as though I'm measuring my days based on the entries I write.  So three entries ago I wrote about the weekend, which really doesn't feel all that long ago ~ there is no logic to this feeling =P

I kicked off the good times by having an awesome night yesterday ^-^.  My daddy and I went to North 44 for their Summerlicious dinner ~ it was pretty classy!  I wanted to hit up at least one fancy-pants restaurant and Canoe was on the top of my list - since it's considered one of the best places in Toronto - but they were all sold out!  Oh wells, I still ate like a rich young professional and had a really incredible experience XD. 

(Yay!  Crappy cell phone pics!)

I had...
      
- Day Boat scallops with smoked bacon, creamed leeks and chili oil
- 6 oz USDA Steak with asparagus and crisp herbed tempura onion rings
- Banana strudel with peanut butter ice cream

Daddy had...
       
- Steak tartare with fried capers and fresh country toast
- Tandoori spiced salmon with potato – pea ragout and okra
- Dark chocolate mousse with caramel and crisp rice

Taking pictures of your food is a really Asian thing to do lol.  There were two other tables with Asians doing the same thing where as no one else did! D:  I really liked the appetizers.  My steak was so juicy and perfectly cooked, but the onion rings were nothing special.  Dessert was perfect ~ and where the hell was there crisp rice? lol  Didn't taste it at all.  We also ordered four different glasses of wine ~ daddy was really sophisticated-like and knew what to pair what with so he did the picking.  I rarely drink wine with my meals but it totally makes a difference in accentuating the flavours and/or cutting the richness of the food.  Foodgasm, enough said haha.  I'm really glad their food and service was so up there because I've read reviews that they aren't always consistent XP.  We closed the night back at daddy's place with some comforting cuddles <3. 

Hey look!  Pictures from last weekends trip to the zoo...



You can easily tell apart which are from my cell phone and which are from daddy's camera =P. 

        
Touching a stingray O3O.  Poking a horseshoe crab.  Making a car out of sand!

         
Depressing barbary ape ~ poor guy seemed like such a specimen.  Frowny rino =(

      
Africa!

     
Big arse sleeping polar bear! D:  Merci ~ the plushie daddy got for me <3


I really like this pic heh =P
♫ Regina Spector - Rejazz

I'm on a horse...I'm on a boat...Swan dive!

Jul. 14th, 2010   3:56 PM
"Home is where the heart is"...but if you're me than it's where the air conditioning is! O3O  I've been indoors these last few days cause it's been crazy humid and gross out.  Plus the weather people keep saying it'll thunder shower but it never does, so I don't bother making plans =.=**  I'm glad to say I went to the gym and swam on Monday and Tuesday ~ it's awesome when you don't have to share equipment or lanes heh.  I can do ten laps before I need to stop for a break, so I'm hoping I can bump up that number by the end of the month!  The only downer is that my body gets really achy afterwards and I still feel like a fatty cause my nutrition is poop XP. 

Things to look forward to...going to North 44 for Summerlicious dinner tomorrow with my daddy.  It's a pretty spiffy restaurant so I'ma have to be grown-up-like! >:3  Probably gonna hang out with Pete on Friday and have a crinkly-movie-fun time O3O.  Going to Ottawa with my family on Saturday; I'm not looking forward to the driving =.=** (is bummed I can't make it to the ABDL Munch or the Furry Wonderland meet up!)(Munches @ The Blake House now too!)  Then next Monday is the Toronto Littles' Play Date again! \o.o/ 

=======================================================================

OMFG!  Old Spice is genious!  I am hooked on the videos they've been uploading on YouTube ~ it's comedy gold!  Now I wish I were black and buff O3O.



 

Go to the channel now and watch the videos of the other - less toned - black dude; he's answering questions in the most hilarious ways! 




=======================================================================

Final thoughts...

This site is sooo useful! 

'Dark Night of the Soul' by Danger Mouse and Sparklehorse featuring a ton of awesome singers is a really fun album to listen to!

(continuing from last post) When we were alone we'd kiss and touch each others crinkles at the zoo. He changed my diaper in the stall that was relatively clean.  The cool wipes were refreshing heh. In the shady area I undid my shortalls and daddy put his hand on my crotch.  I was hard cause I enjoy the public naughtiness =P During the car ride home we were feeling each others crotch ~ he was quite hard.  Distraction while driving? haha As soon as we went through the doors we made out ~ lots of kissing and tonguing.  He pissed down the back of my diaper once and again in the front.  He fingered and butt fucked me!  I was a bit hard while he did this, thanks to the diapers though.  I jerked myself off while playing with his wet diaper against my face.  I wonder how he feels knowing that I don't get off on butt sex, but more to do with diapers... I jerked off again (he was on top of me, his dick was by the leg gathers) before he changed me for the last time.  He sent me home in a Abena with stuffer.  Being in a shortall all day was really fun!  Got splashed by the stingrays which looked like I wet my pants :P I went to bed in a onesie, diapers, paci, and hugging Merci!
Link

Love life!

Jul. 11th, 2010   5:05 PM
I'm enjoying my life right now and it feels like I'm living for the weekends even though everyday is a day off!  I just love how this summer has been almost constantly fun ~ it'll be such a drag when it ends.  Anywhoo, I’ll put up pictures when I get them =P. Friday was all about yummy food and fun times hanging out in downtown with Stephanie.  We made reservations to do lunch at Bier Markt Esplanade for 1pm but met up a bit earlier then planned.  To pass the time we loafed around Novotel hotel which was just across the street; the second floor was completely empty and had comfortable sofa chairs XD.  For lunch I had the wild mushroom soup, bier braised beef baguette, and the Belgian chocolate cappuccino cake off of their Summerlicious menu.  Of course we purposely ordered different items so that we could try as much as possible.  The restaurant has over a hundred brands of beer (you can see a small part of their cellar and it's loaded with kegs!) and we got to taste a sliver of it.  We went with the Belgium tour which had six half-pint samples, but substituted the Stella Artois for a German lager recommended by our waiter.  Oh my gawd, Früli could pass off as Fruitopia since it's got a strawberry flavour! :D The meal itself took about two hours, but it was really chillaxed and left us way full.  Quite literally we were ready to take a nap, so back to the hotel for a bit of shut eye! (lol)  About twenty minutes of down time really did us some good =P.  Afterwards we walked up Yonge street – stopping at Urban Outfitter to check out their fun toys – and caught the 4:10pm showing of 'Despicable Me' at the AMC on Dundas.  Everyone should watch this movie ~ it's hilarious and heart warming!  It's on par with 'Toy Story 3'!  When the movie ended we had some time to kill before our dinner reservations at Café Moroc, so we browsed around Eaton Centre, BMV Books, and HMV.  For dinner I had the maftoul, chicken Marrakesh, and the Moroccan treats; again off of the Summerlicious menu.  The food was great and definitely different from the cuisines I normally eat.  I really like the trendy but casual atmosphere of the restaurant, but I’m not too fond of how dimly lit it was heh.  Nearing the end of our meal there were belly dancers performing in the adjacent room but we were only able to catch glimpses ^^;;. After dinner we walked along Queens Quay and the waterfront, stopping to browse inside the spiffy Westin Harbour Castle and Queen’s Quay Terminal.  The weather was so nice since it had cooled down from the morning rain, making the walk really relaxing :D  As part of the ‘Beats, Breaks & Culture Festival’, there was a free concert at the Sirius Stage by Harbourfront.  The performers consisted of two DJ’s, a guitarist, and a drummer.  They had some catchy beats that you could really rock your head to ~ later I found out it was Kid Koala and The Slew performing.  Kid Koala is a Canadian turntablist with a great ear for samples and a jazz genius; he’s quite underrated.  We called it a night after browsing through the night market in Ann Tindal Park, I bought some crappy but cute earphones heh =P Saturday was all about spending time with my daddy even though it was a last minute thing – planned it in the morning and met up at noon.  We went to the Toronto Zoo and I had so much fun being in a ‘little state’ of mind! \o.o/ I was home alone when he drove over so I invited him in for a tour ~ and then I immediately put him on diaper duty XD.  We headed out after he helped me into my shortalls and packed the diaper bag.  I haven’t been to the zoo in more than a decade so every step of the way was really exciting :D  We went to Stingray Bay first and I got to touch a horseshoe crab and a bunch of stingrays ~ they were kinda slimy-like hehe.  One of the stingrays splashed water on my shortalls making it look like I wet myself {o'.'o}  Next we went to the Kids Zone, where it seemed like the main attractions were the kids themselves XD.  Most of the animals in the section were fake while the set up was more hands on ~ I got to play in a big sandbox for a little while =3.  We spent quite a bit of time making our way around the Eurasia section only because it was so big and required a ton of walking!  My favourite animals were the snow leopards, wallaby, barbary sheep, and the depressing barbary ape (it was surrounded by its own poop and looked terrified o__O).  We began to notice that most of the animals were either malting, looking the other way (we saw a lot of butts!), or sleeping in the shade usually someplace out of sight. After a quick lunch at the Caribou Café, we began looking for a family washroom for a much needed change ~ no luck with that >.<*  We ended up going to the African section where they had washrooms with less traffic.  From that section we got to see the giraffes, hippos, elephants, white rinos, and a sleeping lion (booo!).  I should pack a vuvuzela next time to wake and get the animals attention! >:3  Besides the animals, I really enjoyed the simpler things at the zoo.  In between all the regions and pavilions are long paths with grassy areas for sitting.  Daddy and I found a shady spot to cool down and we relaxed for a good while ^-^.  The only bummer was that we didn’t have a blanket to lie on so now I’ve got itchy bug bits all over my arms XD.  The last section we went to was the Americas and the new Tundra Trek ~ we skipped Canadian Domain and Indo-Malaya.  My favourites were the arctic fox (or was it wolf? lol) and the polar bears!  Even though the bears were sleeping, they were right up against the glass so it was cool to see how massive and fuzzy they were!  Plus the fox was really active and wandered around everywhere =P Before leaving – we were way tired from all the walking and baking in the sun – we checked out the gift shop.  It must have been fated or something because there was a stand full of placemats, each with individual names on them, and one atop of the other had ‘Dad’ and my real life name on them!  So daddy bought the two haha X3.  He also bought me a polar bear plushie that I picked out ~ it’s one of his favourite animals <3.  I named him Merci, which is French for ‘thank you’.  I was really yawny during the car ride, so we cuddled into a nap when we got back to his place.  I can’t recall how long we were out, but when I woke up daddy gave me a bubble bath!  I haven’t had one in the longest time ~ it was so much fun! :D  My fingers were all wrinkly by the time I was done playing hehe.  Afterwards I ‘helped’ with dinner, although it mostly involved sitting and watching.  We made a yummy pasta with chicken, green beans and red pepper in a cream sauce.  Daddy made me wear a Cookie Monster bib but I was a good boy and didn’t make a mess =3.  Later into the night we snuggled to some Ella Fitzgerald – her voice is just golden – before taking off for home.  I couldn’t have had a better day!
😄 cheerful  |  ♫ Ella Fitzgerald - Summertime
Link

Update and stuff...

Jul. 8th, 2010   11:26 PM
Just got back not long ago from swimming for the first time in years!  Oh my gawd does it ever feel so weird to be in a pool ~ the resistence feels like it's choking you when the water is up to the neck.  I thought this would be easier on my ankle than jogging but boy was I mistaken.  One bad kick in the wrong direction and I feel a shooting pain, so for now I'm kicking from my knees.  At least I'm getting some great exercise cause after fifteen minutes of laps I was starving!  I'm gonna try to keep up this routine of working out in the gym, then hitting up the pool, followed by the hot tub, and lastly the sauna.  I feel awesome =P

(Agh!  Learning how to breath and swim all over again is tough.  Water up the nose is no fun, but what's worse is the bubbles in my ears!)

======================================================================

Oh snap!  I threw money at Telus and juiced up my phone plan!  In this first week of July I've already spent ten bucks in texting, so it only made sense to get a fifteen dollar package that gives me unlimited texting ^^;;.  It also includes unlimited Twitter and MSN, so I'll be staying connected on my BlackBerry for most hours of the day!  Even though I have a Twitter now, I don't intend to use it as a replacement for this blog, it'll just supplement it.  I've seen people post on LJ their daily Tweets, which seems sorta cool even though I don't read them haha XD.

======================================================================

Yesterday was the monthly furry dinner at the Old Spaghetti Factory.  It was great!  Met some new faces and made some cool friends ~ not to mention seeing the regulars too.  I spared my liver since I normally drink a couple of pints ~ I'm saving it for tomorrows lunch!  Going to Bier Markt with Stephanie for the Summerlicious dealio.  Personally I'm not too keen on the place since I could go there any day rather than wasting it on the -licious event, but I let Steph pick the places so I won't complain.  I've just heard that their food is overpriced and nothing special, but their selection of alcohol is suppose to be amazing! :3  Plus we're going to watch 'Despicable Me', so I'm happy regardless!

======================================================================

I'm going to Condition: Zero Hour!  I pre-reg'd a couple months back because I wanted the cool looking multi-pass but wasn't entirely sure if I had the funds to make it when the time came.  Wells, I'm gonna transfer some money around and 'pretend' to look for a job a little harder so that things work out heh.  I'll be room and ridesharing with Dan Raccoon and Shiro, and I've got all my fingers crossed hoping that Karis can make it!  Either way though, I have a feeling this con will be FCN all over again!  Yay for boozing! \o.o/
🐇 bouncy  |  ♫ Foals - Spanish Sahara

Jul. 6th, 2010   10:49 PM

Gay, jolly, and merry...

Jul. 5th, 2010   11:14 AM

Hooray ~ another fun day to add to this weekend! ^-^

On Sunday I watched the Pride Parade with Ruff and Twicks.  I always have a fun time hanging out with them even though we only see each once every couple of months.  Originally I was going to watch the parade with Familliar but never got word back, which makes me feel bad since I had the opportunity to meet up with him Saturday evening >.<**  Plus, I was hoping to get together with Paws(thegame) since a couple of Quebec furs were coming to town for the long weekend, but his plans fell through.  Oh wells, I'm glad I had plenty of options even if on short notice =P.

I brought blowing bubbles and party horns to play with, which were put to good use while waiting for the parade to start :D  We planted ourselves in the shade by Ryerson campus on Gerrard St. E. but because of that our view wasn't the greatest.  No real loss for me since this is my fourth year watching and it's generally all the same ~ plus getting away from the sun reduced the heat exponentially (the news says it felt like 37C!!).  It was really generous of Twicks to give me his purdy beaded necklace since it cost a bit...but dude, the colours run off either due to sweat or suntan lotion!  Bad day to wear white clothes haha.  And apparently, I can use Blackberry Messenger for free?  He and Ruff have convinced me that I can since they're both on it, but I'm a bit sceptical.  We'll see...I just hate how this months phone bill will be over a hundred bucks T__T.  I really need a better texting plan ~ damn you Telus!

Anywhoos, so the parade was kind of ‘meh’ for me since I didn’t see much of anything except for the big floats and platform trucks.  When it ended we walked up an extremely crowded Church St, browsing at the various displays and spectacles, as well as getting a bite to eat before going our seperate ways.  Not long after I joined a grouop of ABDL's whom I'd met on Friday, including two visiting from Detroit ^-^.  We went to Zippers for drinks and dancing; it was a lot of fun since I wanted to go dancing at Anthrocon, but didn't get the chance to.  Lulz at the bouncer for checking my bag before going in ~ I brought along two diapers 'just in case' even though I didn't wear anything all day.  I didn't mind the embarrassement so much, it was kinda giddy hehe ^///^.  Later into the night we all went to my daddy Matthew's place to relax and nom on delivery pizza.  He's got a really spiffy collection of archaeology/travel/history/design books which we kicked back to.  I also got to read the first issue of 'Diaper Daze' ~ it's quite hawt XD.  Good times all around! =3

========================================================================

Later today I'm gonna go see 'The Last Airbender' with Lenny.  I can't say I'm surprised to hear no good reviews about it, so I'm not going to expect much ~ I'm just a huge fan of the cartoon series! <3  Afterwards we're going to have fun at the "Toronto Littles’ Play Date".  I'm gonna deck out in my new shortalls and I hear there'll be paint involved! XD

I'm fun size!

Jul. 3rd, 2010   11:18 PM
What a weekend it's been so far!  I feel fluffy on the inside =P

Friday afternoon I went to STC.  For personal reasons it depresses me to go there, but its got a lot of stores I like >.<**  I bought a new duffle bag, two pairs of flip flops, swimming trunks and goggles.  I figure I'd swim laps since my ankle hasn't recovered enough to go jogging and using the bike doesn't burn enough calories for me.  I haven't swam in like eight years so I'm going to die after a few minutes haha!  I wanted to get some new hats as well - something a bit more childish - but they're so damn expensive.  There were two that I liked; one had the 1-Up Mushroom from Mario, and the other, Teenage Mutant Ninja Turtles (I had a TMNT hat as a kid!).  Owning both would be sweet, but I can settle for just one ^^;;  Lids is having a 'buy one get one half off' sale ~ anyone looking to get a new hat so we can jump on this deal? D: 

At night I went to Matthew's ABDL party and had a blast (I love what he's done with the balcony O3O).  There were maybe twenty of us and surprisingly I didn't know a third of the people ~ two were visiting from Florida while the rest were local-ish.  It's so nice to meet new people since I've already gotten acquainted with the regulars (you guys are still awesome though, heh).  Oh man, I had quite a lot to drink that night but what really killed me was the pot!  On average I probably only smoke up once a month so I'm a total light weight and I get knocked out pretty easily XD.  On top of that I had an epic nose bleed so I was lying in bed nearing the end of the party.  Despite having great company with me in the room, I completely passed out and don't remember a thing =P.  Lulz to those who Sharpied 'goodbyes' on my diaper! <3 

Suffices to say I crashed at Matthew's place for the night.  I spent most of Saturday relaxing with him ^-^.  We didn't make pancakes this time, instead, I got to chop up fruits and mix it with some yogurt and granola! O3O  (What were you thinking?  Letting me play with knives ~ tsk tsk)  After a bit of discussing, we decided to explore and have fun with a 'daddy and little boy' relationship =3.  In recent months I've had a fascination with what that type of 'roleplay' would be like since having a caretaker has always felt like something 'right' for me.  But finding that special someone hasn't been something I've put much effort into doing since many people I connect with aren't available for that role.  So as of now I have a daddy and we're going to play around with what does and doesn't work with this relationship!  It's something very new to me and it does make me nervous, but I'm optimistic and I very much enjoy being around Matthew.  (Just as a side thought, I'm glad he isn't a furry because those networks of families/siblings really give me a mind fuck.  Like, it's cool if that's the way you enjoy it, but for some people it webs out like crazy and invites drama.  I prefer keeping it just one to one =P) 

Random ~ oh my gawd, while we were chilling he showed me this book entirely of adult baby/sissy photography called 'The Babies' and it's creepy as hell!  D:
🐇 bouncy  |  ♫ The Dead Weather - 60 Feet Tall

This and that...Things to look forward to...

Jun. 30th, 2010   10:10 AM
I'm kinda bummed that I missed going to watch 'Toy Story 3' with a couple of local ageplayers Monday night.  I got the invite early, but I would have had to rush downtown from the airport with my luggage ~ quite the hassle.  It would've been a nice way to clear my head after a blah trip home ^^;;.  Oh wells, at least my new shortalls arrived when I got home! O3O  I love'em.  Plus, I'm feeling a bit better more jolly having gone to the gym last night.  The last time I worked out was two weeks ago and since then I've been eating and drinking rather unhealthily.  My ankle isn't fully recovered, but enough for me to get around with tolerable pain =P.  Anywhoo, looking forward is the way to go...

July 1 - Canada Day ~ I has no plans yet...someone suggest things to do! D:
July 2 - ABDL Party
July 5 - Toronto Littles’ Play Date
July 7 - Spaghetti Factory Dinner
July 9 - Summerlicious ~ going to Beir Markt for lunch and Cafe Moroco for dinner
July 17 - ABDL Munch ~ I'll probably have to miss it because I may be going to Ottawa
July 23-25 - ABDL Farm Camping
Aug 6-8 - Condition: Zero Hour ~ still deciding whether or not to go; already pre-reg but no hotel or ride plans made

Meh, not as many things planned as I'd imagine there be =\.  I want to hit up one or two other Summerlicious restaurants but finding people to go with me - plus getting reservations - might be tricky.  I want to go to Wonderland at least once!  There are also some fun movies I'd also like to watch ~ The Last Airbender, Despicable Me, and Inception.  If anyone is interested in doing anything, gimme a shout =3.

What'd I miss in Toronto?  Oh yeah...

Kiss and blog #12

Jun. 23rd, 2010   8:39 PM

(continuing from last post)

Snuggling with Familliar was so hot.  We diapered each other up =3.  We kissed a lot (another gay guy really into kissing haha), nibbled here and there, and rubbed all over.  It’s interesting because he wanted to pleasure me first, where as it’s usually the reverse.  I has hard 85-90% of the time ~ must be because I think he’s really cute heh.  We got into a 69 position and I pissed in my diaper while smooshing his face…hot!  He gave me a blowjob, which as usually felt good and bad, before we got walked in on lol.  We scurried for pillows ~ can’t recall if our shirts were off D:  Familliar sat between my legs the rest of the night.  Later I told Galen I was embarrassed about being walked in on, which was why I was really quiet that night ~ he said we were really cute in diapers ^///^.
