Con Report: What The Fur? 2010

Jun. 23rd, 2010   6:41 PM
The looooong overdue con report.  I left out 'Monday' because I ran out of time XD.  I need to start packing and head out for Brampton tonight ~ and then Anthrocon tomorrow morning! 

I didn't take any pics at this con and I'm having a bit of difficulty finding them ~ so use your imagination! O3O

Thursday

In order to get to Montreal, Shiro, Dan Raccoon and I made plans to take the VIA Rail there since we’d also be rooming together.  I’ve only been on a railway train maybe twice in my life but I can’t say I recall anything about them.  I thought this would be a fun way of traveling…not so much now XP.  I take back my initial excitement since it was a boring and butt aching ride!  What’s more we weren’t seated together XD.  At least Shiro was able to sneak a seat next to me a few stops prior to reaching ours and the return trip was a bit better.  While waiting at Union Station before departure, Richard K Niner spotted me.  I didn’t know he was going to the con  let along be riding in the same train, so it was nice hanging out with him since we didn’t get to do much of that in the later days =3.

When we arrived at around 8pm we were joined by one of Shiro’s old friends, Bobicus.  Pretty laid back and open-minded fellow though I wasn’t entirely sure if he was ‘in the know’ about furries at first, but Shiro assured me that he was later in the day ^-^.  I think he was pretty new to it and still coming up with a fursona.  Cool beans, I like meeting new people!  We had a quick dinner at McDonald’s before walking to the hotel.  I suppose I was a bit cranky from the train ride because I whined a lot getting there (it was drizzling too! ><**).  Now that I think about it, it wasn’t even all that far >>”  Heh, my bad.  After check-in and unloading luggage into our rooms, we scoped out the con space and hung out for a bit with those already there.  It was nice seeing some of the con staff I had already met at Furnal Equinox, especially before they got really busy.  Dan, Shiro and I spent the rest of the night back in our room relaxing and catching most of ‘South Park: Bigger Longer & Uncut’. 

At midnight, Shiro and I hung out in the lobby with Feli, Twitchy and her mate; we waited for a couple of other Toronto furs to arrive ~ Shane, Blindsight, and Pakesh.  When they did and got settled in, we were joined by MAnlantrio and two others.  Then we all headed to Dunn’s for some late night noms ^-^.  My first taste of Montreal smoked meat in a very long time was really satisfying!  Fun conversation among great company and yummy food made for a good way to kick off the con. 

Friday

I began my morning early by hitting up the hotel gym at around 7:30am.  The only bummer was its small size and lack of equipment ~ people were already there so I had to wait and share things ><.  A little over an hour later I got ready for the day and then went out for breakfast with Dan at Tim Hortons.  We explored the west side of Rue Sainte Catherine, stopping to chat with Pak, Shane, Blindsight, and Arctic while they had breakfast at a restaurant with a front patio.  We got up to Avenue Greene before turning around; on the way back we checked out the Pepsi Forum (home of the Habs before becoming a movie theatre), the mall beside it, and a comic book shop.    The city and weather was simply beautiful so I couldn’t have asked for a better way to enjoy the morning.  Plus, we bumped into Firebreath and Galen ~ always a delight to see old and new friends! =3

Back at the hotel Dan and I wandered around the con space and got recruited by Twitchy XD.  We helped set up the chairs for two panel rooms before retreating back to our room.  We had hoped to catch Shiro so that we could make plans for lunch, but he had already gone out.  While waiting we watched ‘Word World’ and ‘Marsupilami’; both are kids shows ^-^.  Oh my gawd ‘Word World’ is so full of creativity and imagination ~ I love it!  And ‘Marsupilami’ brings me back to my childhood – even though it’s French – because when I travelled to Paris with my family they bought me a Marsu plushie.  (In the middle of typing this I just had to rush to my garage and dig it out!  It’s incredibly well made and the tail is just over three feet long!  I am so bringing this to everywhere now LOL!)

After the two shows and no luck on Shiro’s return, Dan and I hit the streets again this time wearing our tails and badges ~ it was awesome =3.  This time we explored the east side of Rue Sainte Catherine at the peak of lunch hour; stares and subtle comments ensured heh.  Our walk was much shorter but still quite pleasant; I really enjoyed seeing the collision of old and new architecture in the city.  Back at the hotel we registered for the con and again went back to our room and watched TV – this time ‘Jeopardy’ – while waiting for Shiro.  Not long after he and Bobicus came through the door, full from lunch ~ boourns! XP  So Dan and I got a quick bite to eat Quiznos before hanging out at the Dealer’s Den/Artists Alley.  A big part of my con was spent in the Artists Alley because sitting next to Shiro was Mokuu (Mmmm what’cha say…XD) and Familliar (Tré Awesome the cyberpunk clown =P).  They’re so much fun to joke around with!  Wandering the Dealer’s Den, I bought a squid plushie from ‘Critterama’ (owned and operated by Kimba and Laha! ^-^)  and stuck it on my fursuit head ~ it seemed vaguely con theme related, pirates.  I also commissioned Kalika (always loved her use of colours and background) for a pirate themed badge ~ I wanted to look like a cabin boy XD. 

Later in the afternoon I went to the ‘Fursuit Care’ panel with Dan, hosted by Sense and Paws(the game)(in suit).  More or less everyone in the audience was a Francofur and Sense communicated mostly in French while Paws translated in English ~ most of the jokes completely eluded me (lol) but it was interesting to sit through.  Afterwards I went up and met the two hosts – kinda hoping to see if they could help fix the droopy ears on my suit, but it’s no biggie.  Under the suit, Paws is Asian XD.  Represent!  In the evening Dan and I went to the ‘Opening Ceremony’.  They’re mostly all the same at every con, but this one had a ‘Chocolate Sofcial’ afterwards hosted by Niall MacConaill!  He use to do them at C-ACE (an Ottawa con that’s no longer running) so it was awesome to get the chance to sample a huge spread of chocolates!  There were ones with fruits and cheese, maple syrup, green tea, truffles, and even one that tasted like a Thai restaurant! X3

When that was over and I regrouped with Dan, Shiro and Bobicus; we all went to 3 Amigos for Mexican food ~ it was way filling but very yummy.  On our way back to the hotel I noticed how busy the night life in downtown Montreal is, quite similar to Toronto (they’ve even got a tall CIBC building like us D:).  Shiro and I thought about fursuiting for a bit, but we ended up loafing in front of the tube watching ‘Son of the Mask’.  I got some shut eye for parts of it :P  Afterwards, Shiro wanted some java so Dan and I went with him to a nearby Second Cup, but I didn’t make it past the lobby heh.  Paws was there and I wanted to get to know him better so we chatted for a while before two other furs I knew from Toronto joined in ~ again, always a delight meeting old and new peeps =3.  I don’t recall at what time, but Paws and I eventually wandered around the hotel and ended up in the basement panel room.  Three other furs were there hoping that enough people would come to watch the movie ‘Bolt’, but instead we just ended up just talking ~ fun regardless.  By about 1am we wandered the hotel again and ended up in the con suite; Raereth, Dan, and Shiro were there.  Stayed and chatted some more before retiring for the night. 

Saturday

I woke up brutally early to hit the gym again, this time from 7 to 8am.  While we got ready for the day, the roomies and I watched Saturday morning cartoons =3.  We had a quick breakfast at McDonald’s and then hung around the con space.  In between the two, the streets on a Saturday morning were so calm it almost felt like a Sunday!  I eventually went back to the room and watched the last set of the women’s French Open finals ~ Francesca Schiavone (17) won it with a lot of pride to show for!  (It’s too bad she lost in the first round of Wimbledon a few days ago >>”)  When that was over I went back down to the Artists Alley, stopping to talk with Tundru (another Ontario furry) along the way. 


I took part in the ‘Fursuit Parade’ in the afternoon – I think there were twenty of us total.  I wasn’t really fond of the hotels narrow corridors or having to walk down stairs, but they’re very minor things.  Overall it was fun to be in suit since I didn’t do much of that at the con ^^;;  After going around the con space, all the suiters gathered outside for a photo shoot; though the ground was a bit damp, luckily it didn’t rain.  We got a couple of good toots from some passing vehicles :D  Back at the hotel, I rested up a bit in the headless lounge and got to meet a couple of suiters =3.  Later I picked up my Kalika badge (I love it! O3O) not before checking out the Art Gallery (Heather Burton had an awesome water painting up for bid; was tempted to get it).  I spent the rest of the afternoon chilling in the room with Dan ~ we watched the Blue Jays beat the Yankees in 14 innings. 

By the time the Dealer’s Den/Artists Alley closed up in the evening, plans had been made to have dinner at an all you can eat sushi restaurant with a bunch of furs.  There was: Dan, Shiro, Bobicus, Familliar, Kimba, Laha, Poop (love the name, his art is awesome, and he’s Asian! XD), Talmak, and another fur who’s from Netherland (sorry, can’t recall your name .___.”)  Their method of ordering food is rather inefficient and paying the bill was a bit disarray, but other than that I was a total feast!  I can’t imagine how much I must have eaten!  Plus, a couple of us split a bottle of sake so the small buzz was a nice bonus for the night.  When the dinner wrapped up Familliar made plans to go bowling, so I met with him, Poop, OshBear, Trigali, and Delmir at ‘Sharx Pool Bar & Bowling’ (it’s a really hip place O3O).  Unfortunately all the lanes were taken and the wait time was ridiculous so we all went back to the hotel, blah >.<*

Familliar and I wandered around the hotel for a bit, eventually getting enlisted by MAnlantrio to help bring down Rock Band equipment from the con suite =P.  We decided to go dancing afterwards; there were maybe a dozen people and at least three suiters.  It was a sweaty good time! :D  I enjoy dancing even though I know I can’t do it for the life of me XD.  Oh wells, it was really fun.  Eventually we retreated back to my room to cool down and relax ~ cuddles from him were really comfy ^-^.  I traded him crinkles for art O3O.  I was expecting it eventually cause I didn’t lock the door, but in the middle of cuddling we got walked in on by Dan, Shiro, and Galen.  It was pretty jokes so I can laugh about it now that it’s in the past hehe =P.  We all talked and got to know one another better until about 2am; Galen called it a night while Dan and Shiro retired to bed.  Familliar and I wandered the con space, played and watched Rock Band till closing, and then crawled to bed sometime after 3am ^-^. 

(I missed the ‘Furnal Equinox Ice Cream Social’ and the ‘Masquerade’ that day because they were during dinner hours >.<*  What a bummer.)

Sunday

Sunday is for sleeping in and skipping the gym, naturally =P.  I woke up with the rest of the roomies at around 10am, but Shiro and Familliar needed to head down to the Artists Alley to do their thing.  Dan and I went on a food run to Tim Hortons and Subway before joining them.  While making my way back to the room I said goodbye to Paws and, surprisingly, hellos to Dan Skunk, Stew, and another fur from Ontario.  They made a one day trip to the con ~ kudos to them.  I relaxed and allowed my food to settle – while watching Nadal beat Soderling to reclaim the number one ranking – before getting suited for the ‘Fursuit Games’.  When I got down there, they were still setting up, so I hung out in the headless lounge with the other eight participants =3.  The games consisted of musical chairs, mannequin clipping, ball catching, and charades.  Even though the team I was in lost, I had a lot of fun and it was a great assortment of play.  Bravo to Stripes and BelicBear for coordinating everything! ^-^


After unwinding and cooling off in the room, I hung out with the usual group in the Artists Alley.  I picked up my finished badge by Familliar ~ it’s really awesome! O3O  The Dealer’s Den closed earlier in the afternoon since it was the last day of the con.  After packing up I joined Familliar to the Art Gallery so that he could collect his pay.  I got to meet Kana while waiting; she’s quite charming =3.  When that was done with, Familliar and I skipped the ‘Closing Ceremony’ to cuddle some more back in my room ^-^.  By dinner hour, we joined Dan, Shiro, Bobicus, Firebreath, Galen, Mokuu, Raven, and Richard K Niner for deli food at Dunn’s.  Good food and great friends, what more could I ask for to end the convention? =3  Most of us went our separate ways afterwards.  Back at the hotel, Dan packed up his things because he needed to head home early for work ~ Shiro and I saw him off at the bus stop.  What remained of the evening was spent watching ‘The Cleveland Show’ and parts of ‘Meet the Fockers’ back in our room followed by the ‘Dead Dog Party’.  I was feeling kinda out of it that night so I wasn’t very social, but it was fun to see a couple of suiters having a ball on the dance floor =3.  Most of the con staffers were there as well; they definitely deserve the moment to relax.

Monday

Began the day by going to the gym for a quick work out.  When I got back to the room Bobicus was there and Shiro was awake, so we hung out while packing our things.  We then headed out for breakfast at a yummy begal shop ~ it was quite a long walk, but worth it! XD  Afterwards we proceeded to the train station where we saw some other local furs in the line up for boarding (Shane, Blindsight, Pak, and another fellow).  The train ride home wasn't as boring as coming to Montreal =P.  We sat near the other furs and Shane gave me a jar of Play-Doh to keep myself busy hehe! ^-^  When we were back in Toronto, Shiro and I had dinner at the Old Spaghetti Factory before going our separate ways.  I was pretty pooped by the time I got home, but it was a really great trip and a fun con I hope to attend again next year!
😴 tired  |  ♫ B.o.B - Don't Let Me Fall
Link

Today...flood...cars...Guu...

Jun. 23rd, 2010   12:16 AM
Tired and don't feel like writing much...

- watched 'Toy Story 3' this afternoon with Stephanie ~ it was incredible.  Everyone should watch it to bring out the kid in them!  OMFG Totoro is in it! D:
- ate at Kelsey's afterwards, 2 for 1 starters (nachos and calamari) makes me feel like a fatty .___."
- while driving home, doing 130km/hr on hwy 404, a sneaky cop pulled over the car in front of me ~ so lucky XD

Happy Father's Day

Jun. 20th, 2010   11:45 PM
I wanted to write about my dad; how I grew up disliking him but maturing and better realizing why I love him as well.  But the words don't come out the way I think it.  What matters though is that I know it.  I know it is important to me and me alone.  Thank you for everything dad - words cannot describe how grateful I am to have you even though we don't always see eye-to-eye.  We've come a long way from the days I caused you grief, but I know better now because of you =3.   I know not everyone has a father, but it's always important to take a moment to appreciate the things a loved one has done for you.  Thank Hallmark for making this day, even though it should be done everyday.  
🤔 contemplative  |  ♫ Masta Ace - Ain't No Game
Link

Hobbling around the weekend...

Jun. 20th, 2010   5:24 PM
This weekend has been pretty relaxing!  I've been spending most of the afternoons loafing comfortably, sipping wine and reading (book is due in two days and I'm on page 330 of 386).  If I could have it my way, I'd love it so that every afternoon this summer would be like that ~ then have mornings for exercise and nights for going out.  It'd be such a chill and mellow way to live until winter rolls around XD.

Last night was the monthly ageplay munch ~ I was worried that there would be a low turnout like last month.  I met up with Anthony and BaBySB1 and we sat inside the restaurant unbeknowst there was outdoor seating in the back - we just figured more people would trickle in eventually.  Nearing the end of our dinner we were joined by Pardis, always a delight to see another Asian albeit rare heh.  Not long after that Nebulus64 and I spotted one another but he'd been outside in the back all along with a bunch of other munchers XD.  So we all joined in with everyone outside for the rest of the night.  There was about ten people in total: two I don't think I've ever met and sadly can't recall their names, but they seemed charming, Orca and his wifey, and Dan Raccoon ^-^.  It was really great seeing those who don't have the opportunity to come out as often! 

Afterwards, Anthony, BaBySB1, Nebulus64 and I went to Dylan's place to hang out.  Along the way we bought overpriced junk food (ice cream! O3O) ~ it was a delicious way to beat the heat :P  After settling in and getting comfortable, we cracked open a brand new box of Monopoloy and played for a while.  My second time playing in under twenty-four hours and I still did pretty poorly lol.  I only had three properties and was going broke by the time midnight rolled around; that's when Neb and I called it a night.  Good times and I'm already looking forward to the next ABDL gatherings in July ^-^. 

(I'm also looking forward to Summerlicious!  I'm planning to hit up one or two restaurants with Stephanie, but it'd also be awesome to call out some other friends ~ anyone interested?  I know many of you aren't the fine dining/sophisticated foodie types, but it's a really fun experience =3)
😄 cheerful  |  ♫ Meaghan Smith - I Know
Link

Decision making fail...

Jun. 19th, 2010   1:51 PM
Playing tennis the other day with Stephanie wasn't a good decision, but I'll justify it because we had some great rallies and it was a lot of fun =P.  What it did to my foot though, oh my gawd! >.<  The swelling hasn't gone down and the discolouration has spread to three of my toes.  I'm still walking with a limp, going up and down slopes is a pain, and shifting weight to my right leg gets tiring T__T.  Here's hoping to getting well soon because I already miss going to the gym.  Besides playing tennis, I also got together with some of my old grade school friends ~ I've written about them before; we try to get together a few times a year for game nights.  I had dinner with Andy at some Korean restaurant that does shabu shabu (which is Japanese .___.") but it was rather expensive so we had noodles instead ~ they were different from what I've ever had before and not very good lol.  I can take the blame for that since we were originally going to eat at a HK style restaurant next door, but I didn't want to wait in line XP.  Later we took a stroll through a nearby park.  One of the things I miss about Richmond Hill is all the parks and open space; it's so calm and relaxing.  After a bit of phone tag to figure out where we'd be gaming for the night, we headed for Mark's house ~ the most ideal of places since he's got a projector and an assortment of video games ^-^.  Unfortunately we later found out he only had two Wii controllers and I wasn't informed to bring mine XD.  So we watched 'Planet Earth' (the BBC documentary thingy) while waiting for Kelvin to arrive.  When he did, we played Monopoloy until two in the morning.  For the longest time I had the fewest property and I made the mistake of selling one of the four railroads ><**  Andy won by a landslide ~ good times though =3.
😶 blank  |  ♫ Drake (ft. Jay-Z) - Light Up
Link

All is good till you get hurt .___."

Jun. 17th, 2010   11:44 PM
There are only so many ways to make a grown man cry.  Rolling an ankle is probably one of them, at least for me it was T__T.  I was helping my dad move boxes from the third floor to ground level and didn't even get through one of them ~ good thing it didn't happen while making my way downstairs or else I'd probably gone flying ^^;;  Frozen veggies on disgustingly swollen foot is bliss. 

If by some miracle I'm in little to no pain by tomorrow than I might just take up a religion.  I was going to go jogging tonight, but now I won't be able to workout at all for the next few days.  What's more, I was going to play tennis with Stephanie - been looking forward to it - but I'll probably have to call it off.  Being injured and feeling like a crippled really sucks :(

=============================================================

I'm on page 228 of 386 with the book I'm reading ~ 'Open: An Autobiography' by Andre Agassi.  I'm quite happy with how quickly I'm going through it since I'm a slow reader.  Plus, I didn't think autobiographies would be so enjoyable :P  I want to read James Blake's autobio when I'm done with this one; he use to be one of my favourite tennis players XD.

=============================================================

Looks like I'm going to Anthrocon :D  I was rather worried since my rideshare didn't work out and there being a possible airline strike fumbling my second option of getting to Pittsburgh.  But if Dan Raccoon isn't worried than I won't be worried ^-^.  He's a pilot and the man getting me plane tickets ~ you're awesome!

I'm a bit nervous about going to the con though; the size of it has always intimidated me.  What's more, I'm not rooming with people I'm really tight with.  I just hope I have a repeat of FCN, where I can make some fast friends and have people to hang around with .___."  So insecure right now lol, but I know I'll have a fun time.  If anything, I talked Happyfeet into attending so I'll get to meet him in person for the first time, having known him for a number of years.  Dantee will be there so hookah friends will be made XD.  A handful of locals will be there if I'm able to find them.  And of course, can't forget about the Cub Hub and all the babyfurs ~ I wonder what that'll be like :3

(What The Fur? con report coming...eventually.  I'm terrible at this, I know ^^;;)

=============================================================

I finally bought a pair of shortalls XD.  I have found a way to buy them smart.  There are two or three sellers on eBay that cater to the ageplay community whom I follow.  Often the case when I find something I like of theirs I get out bidded >>".  It's hard finding things my size so it's pretty annoying not winning the things I want.  Anyways, a majority of the shortalls they sell are previously own but refurbished ~ like adding a snap crotch and embroidering a design.  So I figure I'd do as they do :P  But when you search for shortalls, you need to look under women's clothes (don't forget the sizing is different too).  I bought this, which incidentally is cheaper but practically the same as this (I don't care much about the onesie).  The only problem is now I want to buy more stuff using this route cause gawd knows I ain't going to pay for something like this! XD

=============================================================

Speaking of buying stuff, I have put down a deposit (non-refundable D:) for my future fursuit of Clouwy.  After much consideration, I've decided to go with Made Fur You ~ I'm hoping to before Halloween at the very earliest, latest at the end of the year :3  I've commissioned PadderCat for a badge to go along with the suit, it'll probably be done by Anthrocon even though I won't be wearing it till way later ^^;;  But in the mean time, I commissioned TaviMunk for a tag badge of my mousie fursona ~ I've wanted one for the longest time X3. 

=============================================================

Lastly, a long over due thank you to Hippotaur ~ you are too generous!  He gave me this cute little mouse plushie!  It came in the mail a day after WTF?  (was going to add the 'thank you' at the end of the con report but obviously that hasn't happened yet ^^;;) 

It's a Webkinz, meaning I've been sucked into this new age Neopets website for about thirty minutes a day doing all the daily tasks lol.  It's fun if you're an OCD gamer XD. 
😶 blank  |  ♫ The Black Keys - Unknown Brother
Link

Living better...

Jun. 15th, 2010   8:15 PM
I'm feeling pretty good today considering I was in a bored slump for the last little while.  The days when I have nothing to write about are generally the ones that really bore me ~ and I hate being bored.  It makes me think about my friend Stephanie and how she describes her blog as "...not be(ing) the most realistic depiction of my life...idealized 'fanciful' moments highlighted..."  I can see a bit of myself using that description when times are going good; but when I write about the things on my mind, not so much.  Lately I've been thinking about why I share the things I do.  I enjoy writing about my experiences since I have bad memory, but why a blog and not a private diary?  Sometimes I have this impending feeling that consequences will arise for writing about the real me.

Anyways...I have decided to enrich my mind and make my life a little less boring by reading books.  It probably sounds insignificant, but to be fair I'm not the brightest crayon in the box.  I'm the type of person who doesn't read books if they aren't required ~ the closest thing to a book I've read all year - other than textbooks - are comic books.  So this will be a slightly different way for me to pass the time instead of loafing in front of the computer; I'm sure I'll enjoy it.  The other day I went to the library and borrowed 'Blink: The Power of Thinking Without Thinking' by Malcolm Gladwell, 'The Omen' (the 2006 remake isn't very good), and 'Sleeper' (hilarious Woody Allen flick).  I haven't started the book yet, but I swear I will.  Today I went to another library (neighbours are doing construction so it's been making me restless) and borrowed 'Open: An Autobiography' by Andre Aggasi, 'Imagine: John Lennon' (a 1988 biography movie), and 'Domino' (an action flick).  I only have the book on a seven day loan that's not renewable so I'm trying to get through it first ~ on page 47 of 386.  I feel good about reading, its rewarding in a way similar to exercising. 

To further better my life, I have also decided to remove (as much as possible) meat from my diet only during lunch and to consume at least two fruits a day (usually apples, oranges, and bananas).  I really want to have a flatter belly and I'm already working out as much as I can, but my nutrition is what's holding me back.  I can do this! 
🙂 good  |  ♫ Yael Naim - Too Long
Link

Blah...

Jun. 13th, 2010   10:42 PM
...haven't been blogging all that much this month ~ can't say I've been in the writing mood, but it's mostly just an on and off thing.  'What The Fur?' con report will be coming soon!  I started it like four days ago but than procrastinated a lot after that; hopefully I'll still remember stuff when I pick it back up XD.  I kinda regret not going to yesterdays Islington Furmeet 6 ~ the last one! D:  I didn't plan on going before hand because I wasn't all that interested in hanging out with that sorta crowd, but I probably would've end up having more fun regardless.  I spent most of my Sunday catching up on season 6 of House MD, went bowling in the evening with Jully and a few of her friends, then hit the gym for a short workout =\.

Be Careful!! Guuing is addictive.

Jun. 12th, 2010   2:36 AM
Oh my gawd…I just got home not long ago from one of the best dinners I’ve had all year!  *foodgasm*  It is so incredibly hard to describe happy I am! XD  I went to Guu Izakaya with Stephanie after playing some really great tennis in the evening.  Based on what we heard prior to going, a long line up was expected ~ instantly the first sign of a really great restaurant!  We starved ourselves outside waiting from 8:45pm to 10:25pm for a seat.  Probably the longest I’ve ever waited but it was sooooo worth it. 

The moment you step inside you are greeted loudly by the staff in Japanese, but practically everyone in the restaurant joins in!  The atmosphere is chaotically energetic, I kid you not, words can not describe.  You get so sucked into a jolly, feel-good mood that you’re whole dinning experience is elevated to a new level.  Again, waiting for a seat was worth it!  We sat at the bar and got a decent view of the kitchen just in front of us ~ I recommend this area over the tables and patio.  I kinda did my homework before going so I had an idea what was good to eat.  I love how it’s a Japanese restaurant that DOESN’T do sushi.  They serve bar food or the equivalent of tapas.  Plus it’s pretty authentic considering the whole staff is Japanese and they speak really little English ~ another sign of a great restaurant! 

We shared a big mug of Sapporo beer – which looked to be about a litre.  We then started with Takowasabi, Kabocha Korokke, Tontoro, and Aburi Salmon.  For a second round we ordered Avocado and Tuna Tartare (off the special menu), Kalubi, Takoyaki, and a Bamboo cocktail ~ after the meal we felt that the ordering was just perfect XD.  It was all so insanely good!  What’s more, with tax and tip it only came to $35 a head! ^-^  Win!  This was a total steal for the kind of environment, food, and excellent service.  I would gladly come back even if it means having to wait in line again :D  On the bottom of the receipt it says 'Be Careful!!  Guuing is addictive' O3O

(I’ll post pictures when I get them and I’m not going to describe what each dish was ~ look them up on their website.)

Also thanks so much for the grad gift Stephanie, it was really sweet of you! ^-^
😎 relaxed  |  ♫ Murs and 9th Wonder - Murray's Law
Link

It's raining but your son is shining...

Jun. 9th, 2010   3:20 PM
Despite having to wake up really early to get my butt downtown in the rainy weather, I am super happy!  This morning was my convocation!  Woohoo!  \o.o/  University of Toronto: Class of 2010.  Honours Bachelor of Science ~ Specialist in Medical Anthropology and Major in Health Studies.  I saw Suki, Lena, and Arvin there as well, so a big congrats to them!  We did it! ^-^  Now what?  I continue having fun with my summer! :D



         
The inside of Convocation Hall at University College / Find me for a cookie =P / What I see from my angle.  ﻿
﻿


Me with Prof. John M. Kennedy; he's done pretty important research for psychology and the blind.

Also, Happy Birthday Mom!  Thanks for coming out this morning and always giving me your support ~ I'm sure it was one of the best gifts you've received!  Keep living a healthy life and I hope there will be more moments for you to cherish =3.

Link

Con Report: Anime North 2010

Jun. 2nd, 2010   2:00 PM
Last week I wasn’t feeling all like myself ~ I had a lot on my mind about the things I take interest in.  I didn’t feel like doing the things I had been looking forward to – like going to conventions and talking to people – because they seemed less appealing and lacked excitement.  I was just pretty blah about everything and found it hard to keep sober at night.  Thank gawd it was a short week and I’m really glad I made myself go to Anime North ~ it helped remind me why I love the things I do and to move forward in life.  Thanks to everyone there I had a really great time!  ^-^

(I take no credit for the pics ~ they go to Smash and whoever else I ripped from the internet ^^;;)

Friday

The one thing I dread about going to the Toronto Congress Centre is the commute.  I have to take three buses and ride one stop on the subway, which can take up to two hours >>”.  To save myself a bit of grief for Saturday morning I brought along my fursuit so that I could leave it at Vinnie and Andy’s suite.  I arrived a little before 7pm and called up Vinnie because he had plans for a room party.  Unfortunately hotel security was way strict and access to the elevators were restricted to people with a key card.  I managed to sneak up and dropped off my luggage, also meeting Andy’s mom in the processes (quite possibly the coolest mom ever!).  Later I went to register for the con with Vinnie keeping me company ~ it took an hour and it was still hot outside >.o”  Incidentally D’Otter, Stryker Coyote, Sparrow, and Cornwall were standing behind us XD. 

    
Giant chocobo!  *grope*                                                                        Giant metapod!

While waiting in line I saw a lot of awesome cosplayers.  There was a ton of Bleach, Naruto, Pokémon, Hetalia, and video game characters, as well as Na’vi’s, Kick-Ass, and even a Pedobear.  I love the diversity! <3  Prior to the con I had considered cosplaying as Peter Kirkland (aka Sealand from Hetalia), mainly cause I love how he’s such a kid trying to be taken seriously ~ plus sailor suits are just really adorable ^///^.  But the timing for shipping wouldn’t have worked out.  Oh wells, I don’t think I’d do well with a wig anyways =P.  After registration Vinnie and I fursuited around for a bit, but not before getting a quick bite to eat at Tim Hortons – we bumped into two other furries there but I can’t recall their names, my bad.  We spent most of the night hanging around the outdoor rave with Kitty, HappyFoxNick, Alkane, Smash Meerkat, Riffti, Cornwall, D’Otter, and probably a couple others.  Sweaty good fun ^^;;  I headed for home at around midnight after downing a small bottle of sake ~ it helped make the commute go by quicker XD. 

    
Kitty and Smash (wearing Vinnie's head)                                      Nereza and Riffti

Saturday

I got to the con at around 10:30am; there was already a ton of cosplayers on the bus ^-^.  I called up Vinnie and we got a bite to eat before heading to the Dealer’s Room ~ sitting next to Bowser and an annoying Mario while nomming XD.  We then headed to the congress centre, bumping into Danny Dingo but going separate ways since he needed to register.  I don’t like how the entrance and exit to the dealer’s area have been swapped this year, it just means more unnecessary walking >>”.  There wasn’t anything being sold that screamed at me to be bought, but I kinda expected that.  I don’t love anime enough to buy the merchandise =P.  It would’ve been nice not to have left empty handed, but oh wells.  I know some people who’ve spent hundreds at this con ~ just ‘wow’ XD.

         
Bowser and bitch Mario.                My favourite character punching my least favourite character from Soul Eater!  Win!

While making our way back to the hotel to relax, Vinnie and I bumped into a bunch of furs.  There was Cornwall, D’Otter, and Ronnie (w00t! Another Asian fur \o.o/) just outside the congress centre.  Yani while approaching the parking lot (I feel so bad not recognizing you, I didn’t know what you looked like let alone know that you’d recognize me ~ my bad =P).  And then Stew, Silent Hill, and Danny Dingo at the intersection.  We are everywhere! OwO  Most of us headed to the ‘Ears & Tails…WHAT?!?!’ panel in the afternoon where we joined a lot more local furs ~ like Cheeto, Aelyrin, Alkane, Smash, Mordecai, and not surprisingly a bunch of others I can’t recall.  We took up two rows and totally joked throughout the supposed ‘furry’ panel.  The host was talking about totems and profiling species characteristics…it was a load of crap pretty much.  

         
Tail nomming...                                                                 ...tail molesting...                                ...tail jerking!

When the panel finished the whole lot of us headed for the congress centre ~ everyone with a fursuit got dressed up.  We eventually bumped into Nereza in her awesome Wolf Link suit and decided to head over to a shady area and relax.  It seemed as though every step we took there were people stopping us for pictures XD.  It was fun despite moving at a snails pace =P.  Our group got even bigger while loafing around in the secluded area; we were joined by Maxykun, Marrow, Zerrif, Rozz, Blake, and a couple others.  Hanging out, nomming on cookies, and just doing whatever made for good times ^-^.  As the sun began to set, a few of us either left for home or went to do their own thing.  There were maybe ten of us that went to Maxykun’s suite later in the night to play video games. 

   
Someone in a quad suit, me, Cheeto, Mac Cat, Vinnie, Cornwall, Nereza, and Ronnie.   And a doodle by Babs =P

I gave Vinnie a hard time about his driving that night, so I got to drive his Yaris for the rest of the con!  Oh gawd was it ever fun! XD  He lives out in the boonies!  What can I say?!  He doesn’t have that aggression needed to drive in the city!  But seriously, that takes a lot of trust so thanks man! :D  I hope I didn’t scar you with my driving heh.  After the gaming, Vinnie, Andy, and I drove around looking for a Shoppers Drug Mart and got a bite to eat at Timmies.  We watched parts of ‘Ninja Death 2 and 3’ (hilariously dubbed!) before calling it a night.  Thanks as well for the crash space! ^-^

Sunday

The last day of the con means juggling luggage after checkout and squeezing in some fun before say goodbye.  I hate those days =P.  After cleaning the room up a bit, we got a bite to eat before contemplating whether to fursuit or not ~ Silent Hill joined up with us.  There was a bit of an incident at the congress centre involving a fire truck and two ambulances, so suiting around the building didn’t seem like a good idea.  We went to the Sheraton instead – where most of the gaming for the con had been held – and suited for an hour.  The rest of the day was spent driving around (cramming people into a tiny car), loafing under the sun, checking out the Dealer’s Room (I didn’t reg for the day so I sneaked in D:) and finding things to amuse ourselves.  It was a mellow way to end my weekend ^-^  Though I probably shouldn’t have bothered registering on Saturday since I could have ‘ghosted’ this con (not reg and hang outside the con space) and still had a fun time.  Next year I’ll remember to be even more of a cheap ass XP.﻿ 

*squeak!*  Heading to my next con tomorrow; What The Fur?  I'll be rooming with Dan Raccoon and Shiro ~ it should be a lot of fun! ^-^  
🐇 bouncy  |  ♫ Meaghan Smith - 5 More Minutes
Link

May. 28, 2010

May. 28th, 2010   12:48 PM

I haven't been feeling all like myself lately.  Being introspective most of this week has led me to question the things I'm involved in.  Its made the things I've been looking forward to - like Anime North, What The Fur?, and Anthrocon - less appealing and lacking the excitement it once had.  I'll still go to them and I'll probably end up having a fun time, but right now I'm so blah they're just looking like numbers for my next credit card statement.  Worst of all, when I add it all up and factor in the things I want, my summer might be a whole lot shorter!

  I normally don't have a problem with spending the bucks to have a good time (and shopping is certainly one source of my happiness) but it's also the bane of

I find that

I've got a lot on my mind causing me to question the things I'm involved in.  Stuff I've been looking forward to don't really have that excitement which use to appeal to me.  Things like Anime North, What The Fur?, and Anthrocon.  I'll still go to them and probably have a fun time, but right now they're just looking like numbers for my credit card statement. 


Like, 'Anime North' is this weekend and I'm still debating when to go.  Just tonight for Vinnie and Andy's room party, or Saturday as well?  I doubt I'll spend money in the Dealer's Room and I can pass on the panels, so really, is there a point in spending money for registration? =S  I'm leaving for 'What The Fur?' is less then a week - and I know I'll have a good time for a number of reasons - but adding up the costs has been more than what I wanted to spend.  Anthrocon is also coming up and I've always felt nervous about going to a con so large.  These a budding situation with the rideshare, which makes me want to just find an alternative route there - it might cost more, but it'd save me the headache.  Part of me wants to not go, but I'd feel bad pulling out of the roomshare I'm in since it's with good people.

"Whatever lifts your luggage"

May. 23rd, 2010   11:02 PM
Wow!  Yesterday there was at least four birthdays that I knew of, so a big HAPPY BIRTHDAY to all of them! \o.o/

One of those was Conker's birthday and to celebrate there was an ABDL potluck at Kevydl's place ^-^.  About a dozen people were there; many familiar faces with the exception of three I had never met ~ it's too bad I didn't really get to talk with them much >.o  I feel like a fatty for eating and boozing so much this month, but I have been jogging every other day so I suppose it balances out.  One of the mixed drinks I had contained bubblegum vodka and it was freakin' delicious! <3  It definitely helped me make good use of my diapers heh ~ thanks for changing me K-Dawg {o'.'o}  Other than having a really relaxing night in a comfy sleeper among fun friends, I can't honestly say I remember a whole lot =P.  I was pretty tipsy when I left and only blue line buses were operating, so I stayed over at Matthew's place =3. 

I got to take home some awesome swag!  From the 'chicken feed' (grab what you want from a pile of goodies XD) I got two pajama pants with Sesame Street and Mighty Mouse patterns and a pair of Mickey Mouse slippers. ^-^  I feel inadequate knowing that a majority of my ageplay clothes were given to me; like the party last year and my birthday present D:  Oh yeah, and whoever outbidded me SEVEN freakin' times in the last twenty seconds for the shortalls I really wanted...I don't like you =.=**  *cries in the corner* 



I spent most of today loafing around padded at Matthew's place.  Originally I was gonna go to the Ontario Furries Meet #2: Toronto Zoo event but I slept in XD.  Oh wells, I still had an awesome day!  We made apple and oats pancakes ~ I didn't say 'helped' this time because I actually did shit LOL.  I did everything but cut the apples and fry the batter, that's grown up work O3O.  Later we watched 'Sin City' on blu-ray and cuddle in for a nap ^-^.  I really got to know him better and I appreciate his insight on the stuff we talked about.  I enjoy being in the company of older people because listening to their life experiences is very interesting.  Plus his style of living is so bourgeois (because he's furnishing his home) it's both charming and fascinating.  I love it!  =P 

(During this span I missed six calls.  Sorry about that ~ I don't have call display so I donno who to get back to .___.")

Fursuit shopping...

May. 22nd, 2010   12:22 AM
...is serious business! D: 

As I've mentioned two entries ago, I created a new bunny character named Clouwy!  I do make a distinction between my fursona and a (personal) character; the former being a reflection of who I am and the latter being an identity with desirable characteristics.  I'm not the type that will give a character a background story.  Clouwy is just something I want and will use for fursuit performances, so think spunky and full of affection ^-^.  I love bunnies and I wanted a design that reflected summer since it's my favourite season ~ yellow to represent sunshine, blue for the sky, and of course clouds.  Also, this character is gender ambiguous because I just think it's cuter that way =3. 

In the last few days I've been asking around for quotes from fursuit makers ~ besides sending out emails I put up an ad on Fursuit Auctions here on LJ.  So far I've gotten eleven quotes and waiting on four.  There are also a number of builders I haven't emailed yet or they just aren't available for commission.  (Just browse through the Fursuit Tourney to get an idea of how many amazing builders are out there!)  Surprisingly, the prices are very broad from a wide range of talented artists - right now it's between $600 to $2000 USD - and I've expected as much from those in the upper end.  I'm totally fine with thowing down that kind of cash.  The other factors I'm keeping in mind is completion date, duck-tape-dummy requirement, shipping, and how the cloud markings will be done (whether sewn or airbrushed).  If airbrushed, it'll eventually fade out after a couple of washes and sewn in is pretty labour intensive, which costs more. 

I'll probably have picked a builder by the end of next week since I've narrowed it to two right now!  \o.o/
🐇 bouncy  |  ♫ Nas & Damian Marley - As We Enter

"You can't tell, but I am wincing at you..." XD

May. 20th, 2010   2:28 PM
Yesterday I headed up to Brampton to spend the day with Shiro, Dan Raccoon, and Karis!  Thanks for the good times you guys! ^-^

*squeak!*  I got to meet Karis!  \o.o/  *does a little dance*  He's kind of a big deal in the babyfur/diaperfur/ABDL circle since back in the days (like, nearly a decade ago) his website was among the very few geared towards our interests.  Although sites like FurAffinity have probably shrunk his traffic, he's still producing amazing comics like 'Diaperman', 'World of Wetcraft', and 'Incontinent Student Bodies' O3O.  I think I have a man crush now that I've met him XD.  He is so playful and funny and cuddly and hairy o__O  *dies*

Heh, all jokes a side though, I had a really crinkly fun time! :D  I arrived in the afternoon and 'helped' Shiro tidy up the place a bit (one could say I did next to nothing ^^;;).  Not long after Dan and Karis came through the door; lots of hugs and hellos of course.  We went to Crabby Joe's for a late lunch ~ the back of my neck got really tanned cause we ate outside, but it was worth it cause the weather was sooo nice.  The food was really yummy too!  Roast beef sammich with whisky BBQ sauce *drool*  and cheesecake for dessert! XD  When we got back, Dan and Shiro decked out in their new ABU cloth diapers ~ we had seen them at Ricky's place a few weeks ago and they are way awesome!  They are insanely thick and the plastic exterior is smooth and shiny; everyone should wear them! >:3  I loved how Karis had on big kid underware with Transformers designs on them =P.  Lots of tickles, cuddles, and scritches later we all played Mario Party 6 where I miraculously won by two stars denying Shiro a very close steal at victory hehe.  After the game I got freshly padded by Karis; I wasn't allowed to change sooner even though it was really soggy and cold (ick .___.")  Yay!  Excessive use of powder and returning to a house full of family smelling like a baby ~ hehe, you know I loved it ;)  We called it a night after going to Dairy Queen for some ice cream ^-^.  Everyday of my summer needs to be this awesome! :V

But lets be realistic, I have doubts this mall will be any success.  Markham already has a shit ton of Asian malls and plazes (some half empty!), the cities infrastructure is often congested, and most Asians are migrating further north ~ like past Richmond Hill now.  Go us! =.=**

I still love you Robert Munsch!  Your addictions would explain why 'A Promise is a Promise' was such a messed up story! OAO

I watched 'Momma's Man' the other night ~ damn torrent took me nearly two weeks to download!  It was interesting in an awkwardly different, yet subtly entertaining way.  It's hard to explain, but it was definitely right up my alley ~ if you liked 'Lost in Translation' (which I loooove <3) than you might enjoy this movie =3.

Holy crap that was quick!  The case of Molicare I ordered yesterday (at 11:30am) arrived today (9:45am-ish).  You are awesome 'Quality Life Services Inc.' <3  Plus, it was pretty cheap ~ $75.80 CAD for a case of 56, which is $1.35 a piece \o.o/

Shop and play \o.o/

May. 18th, 2010   2:02 PM
Last night was the second "Toronto Littles' Play Date" - movie night event - and it was a lot of fun! ^-^ 

Prior to going, I spent the afternoon shopping around town =P.  Went to Mountain Equipment Co-Op and bought a new pair of workout gloves since I lost mine last week; without them I get nasty catalysts from lifting weights.  Their brand of sunglasses are inexpensive and look really trendy so I'll probably pick up a pair the next time I go ~ I've still got a month left until I can no longer return my Oakleys XD.  I also went to Snug As A Bug and bought a new footed sleeper!  I love the baby animal pattern <3.  The size medium is a bit snug around the shoulder/armpits and feet, but I'll break into them. 

At the play date there was maybe thirteen to fifteen people there ~ I had already met more than half, but knowing them only by name from last months event ^^;;  There's one thing I've noticed with this event (particularly on movie nights), it's that socializing outside of 'play talk' or 'role talk' is less frequent.  Getting to know some of these people outside of their kink has been almost non-existent.  Many of them seem to know one another from other events as well, so that doesn't really help either heh.  I suppose I'm just too use to going to events where talking takes the forefront rather than playing.  Oh wells, I did get to meet another (and probably the only other) adult baby there ~ I let him borrow one of my new pacifiers for the night =D.  We did some colouring, decorated penguin cupcakes (last time it was Cheshire Cat), and watched 'Mary Poppins'!  I also got to cuddle with Nana Grace and my blankie, which was bliss ~ except when her hyper active puppy DJ slobbered all over me, that just ticked XD.  All in all, I had a really enjoyable night and can't wait till next month ^-^.  

    
My new footed sleeper =D.  The black pads on the feet are way sticky but I prefer ones where I can slip and slide .___."  The two new Nuk 5 pacifiers I recently got from PacifiersRus ~ they came with two cheapy diapers heh.  Also, the sippy cup I've been using for a while; I have a green one but it's at Ricky's place and used for drinking beer XD.  I ordered a case of Molicare which should arrive in two days, and I'm deciding whether to bid on a Mickey Mouse shortall (only one day left!) or a Winnie the Pooh shortall (five days left).  Which do you think I should get?  I don't care much about the shirt since I like my own tees heh ^^;;.
🐇 bouncy  |  ♫ Travie McCoy (ft. Bruno Mars) - Billionaire
Link

Days gone by...

May. 16th, 2010   2:41 AM
Ever have a string of days where it's been all fun, but on a day with no activities it becomes really boring?  That funk hit me hard on Friday .____.

On Tuesday I met up with K-Dawg at night for a few hours ~ fuelled up on cuddles after having my feelings twisted into a knot from the day before ^^;;  He made turkey lasagna; it was healthy so it didn't taste good (just kidding).  We padded up and watched two recent episodes of Glee <3 

On Wednesday I met up with Jully and treated her to dinner because she's been feeling like "a single mother" for the last week XD.  We went to Destiny on Silver Star Boulevard ~ one of them Taiwanese bubble tea restaurants for youngsters.  The interior is completely different from the last time I went, it now looks similar to the one up in Richmond Hill.  I prefer the old design =\.  Tthe food, drinks, and price tag were unreasonably expected, but catching up with her was worth it ~ she always makes me smile =3.  Lets hope we can stick to our bi-weekly badminton plans!

On Thursday I met up with Stephanie ~ the ol' blog says I last saw her in September! o__O  It's interesting to me how she's been asking "what's new?" when instead I feel like it should be "what's different?".  I suppose it's because in the last eight months I've been far more active in the ABDL/furry scene; which makes up a majority of my social life.  So it isn't anything new to me, but to be unable to share in detail the things I've been up was a bit awkward.  Pays to be different?  I donno.  She reads this blog (hey you! *waves*) and has an idea about what I'm into, but it's hardly a conversation piece XD.  Anyways, we went to Starbucks for cheap frappuccinos and sorta caught up.  Aside from the rainy weather, I had a really enjoyable time ^^.

Friday was so boring I, admittedly, got drunk at night just to end the day in a good mood.  It totally helped but I'm not happy about having to resort to drinking =S.  Prior to that I began reading Scott Pilgrim (in the middle of the second volume right now), watched the movies 'Trainspotting' and 'Alice in Wonderland', and killed my thighs by jogging outside (taking the stairs causes mad pain now T__T). 

On Saturday I went to the ABDL munch and furry bowling events.  En route to the munch I bumped into Mimi on the subway ~ it's crazy how tiny the odds of that ever happening are!  I literally sat right next to him and didn't even know it until he pointed me out XD.  It was great seeing him back on his feet and hunting for a place of his own ~ housewarming party soon! \o.o/  When I got to the pub there was no one there.  I expected a really low turnout because of the short notice for the new location, as well as from speaking with some of the regulars prior.  I ended up waitng outside with Dylan (not that it was bad, he's a delight to talk with :D) for about forty-five minutes before spotting BaBySB1 and Anthony arriving by car.  I headed for the bowling event after that so I don't know who else might have showed up ^^;;.  There was about fifteen other furs with only two or three I've never met.  I didn't do any bowling because of my thighs, but watching them play and just hanging out was a nice way to pass the time =3. 

I need sleep now ~ right eyes been feeling strained and in some degree of pain >.o  I should probably be concerned but I keep telling myself I'll just sleep it off XD.
😑 blah  |  ♫ Drake - Over

It's okay to show weakness...

May. 10th, 2010   11:53 PM
...because that's when you find out how strong you are. 

I keep wishing that there was more I could do for you and it pains me to see you going through this.  But it was also good seeing you today ~ it helped remind me how capable and self-aware you are.  I know you'll be okay and you know you'll be okay.  Stay strong while you work your way through this; your friends have got your back if you need a distraction or someone to talk to. 

Plus, half-price frappuccinos are made of win =3.

============================================

I got home from a killer workout not long ago ~ it really helped clear out the sucky feelings from earlier today.  I normally go on Tuesdays but I may change that since there was far fewer people.  Other than not having to fight over the equipment, it also means I won't have to feel inferior to all the macho people curling 60lb dumbbells XD.  The only crappy thing I realized after coming home was that I left my workout gloves on the bench, and since my aunt no longer lives in the condo I can't call security to help me hold on to them.  Tits! 

Oh yeah, it also sucked balls coming home to find out the pair of ageplay overalls I wanted to buy online had been sold =.=**  Damn you whoever got them!  It's so hard finding things in my size since most custom AB clothes are way large =\. 

============================================

I've got my week lined up nicely, I hope.  Gonna hangout with some friends I haven't seen in a long while; tomorrow possibly with K-Dawg, Wednesday possibly with Jully, and Thursday possibly with Stephanie ^-^.
😴 tired  |  ♫ Two Hungry Bros. (ft. 8thw1) - You're Always Here
Link

Update on the this and that...

May. 9th, 2010   5:38 PM
Happy Mother's Day!  It's not easy being you mom, but I love and appreciate everything you've given me.  Thank you so much.  I hope someday I can provide for you <3.

=========================================

Eww, Roger Federer (1) blew another tournament, the Estorli Open, the other day against Albert Montanes (34).  Personally, I find that he's only worth watching five times out of the year ~ the four grand slams and the year-end championships.  All the other tournaments, even the Masters 1000, he's been playing pretty blah.  He's still a very well respected player, but I think I'm ready to watch someone dethrone him...which could be a while since he's still got a ton of ATP points to back his ranking =\. 

=========================================

Holy awesome-sauce! D:  Mickey Mouse talks!  I want to meet him now! <3 
The next step to fursuiting ~ a better moving jaw, blinking eyes, and a voice changer =P


﻿

=========================================

Last night's ABDL potluck at Ricky's place was a lot of fun ^-^.  There was about eleven of us and it was really great seeing Blondboy, Dylan, and Marvin again since its been a while.  I sooo want to get some new AB clothes after seeing what everyone else was wearing! XD  There was also a lot of yummy food to nom on and alcohol to get tipsy over =P.  Oddly enough, I had at least three tallboys and they didn't go right threw me so I didn't need my diaper changed all night o__O.  Other than the usual fare of tickles, cuddles, interesting discussions, and YouTube-ing (watch this!), we also watched Saturday Night Live hosted by Betty White!  Oh my gawd was she ever hilarious.  Jay-Z was the musical guest and I thought his first mix was pretty good, but he definitely didn't get much fan support >>".  After taking a look at Ricky's new car seat (way cool!) I called it a night; got home by 2am.  Thanks a lot for the ride Cameron! :D

Oh yeah ~ sippy cups don't prevent spills...when you shake them! >:3  Bad Ricky!  hehe

=========================================

Didn't have teh internets for a couple of hours the other day, probably due to the storm, so I read the comic 'Wanted' by Mark Millar and art by J.G. Jones.  Definitely waaay better than the movie ~ they follow very loosely and lots of stuff are taken out.  I like how the comic has a relatively original plot - if supervillians ruled the world - but I didn't like how supervillians could be killed so easily with bullets.  Oh wells, it's very Millar ~ lots of violence, swearing, and sex used purposefully for its shock value.  If that's what you're after then cool beans =3. 

Anime North is fast approaching!  I think I need to get my geek on and watch/read lots of anime/manga O3O.  And then when FanExpo comes along I'll geek it out with more comic books!

=========================================

Agh!  Texting is so expensive T__T.  Telus is raping me $30 for 82 sent and 111 received messages.  I have a feeling June will be similar unless I change plans.  Just one more year and the contract expires >___<.  Please call instead of texting me peeps, just introduce yourself since I don't have call display.  Or if it isn't urgent or important feel free to email/offline IM me =3. 
😶 blank  |  ♫ Plants and Animals - Faerie Dance
Link

Games, movies, weekend!

May. 7th, 2010   11:50 PM
The Humble Indie Bundle ~ just three days left!  Pay what you can for two charities and receive five awesome games!  The number of contributors and how much they've raised so far is really incredible.

So far I've only tried Aquaria and Penubra.  I find the first one visually appealing but a bit boring.  I've only played thirty minutes but I know it gets better.  The second game has pretty good graphics but it's also rather creepy.  I'm a lightweight when it comes to horror games so I'll probably play just a bit of it >>".

================================================

I watched a couple of movies to pass the boring moments of my week:

Kick-Ass ~ I'm gonna have to go against most of the reviews and say I liked the comic book more than the movie.  I thought some of the acting was a bit cheesy and I didn't like the changes they made.  If the movie wasn't based off of anything than maybe I would have liked it, but the rawness of the comic won it for me. 

The Crazies ~ Pretty awesome.  I would complain about the stereotypical plot, but it's a remake and it was done very well.  Horror movies use to scare the bejeezus out of me, but I've gotten use to knowing what to expect.  So now I pretty much just sit back and complain about how stupid certain characters are or just laugh it off =\.

How to Train Your Dragon ~ Loved everything but the end monologue XD.  I don't like how the main character referred to the dragons as pets >>"  It devalues their awesomeness and badassery.  Other than that I really enjoyed everything about it; probably would've paid to see it in theaters too!

================================================

Yay!  \o.o/  ABDL potluck tomorrow at Ricky's place ~ it'll be a lot of fun!  I'm bringing salad and I'll attempt to "cook" mac and cheese.  I kinda feel like an alcoholic since it seems like I drink everytime I hang out with friends.  I don't rely on that to have fun, really.  I'm more worried about my waistline than anything else to be honest >>". 

Mother's Day is on Sunday ~ I bought some flowers for the garden in the backyard cause she said she wanted some.  My brother and I will probably take her out for lunch; haven't decided where yet, but hopefully we won't need a reservation ^^;;. 
😎 relaxed  |  ♫ The White Stripes - Conquest

Oh furries...

May. 6th, 2010   2:58 AM
Interesting proposition ~ I was asked if I wanted to be a staff member for Furnal Equinox at yesterdays monthly dinner by Blindsight and Shane.  Not to boast but I knew they would eventually; having been in the same car with Arminal for so many hours, he said he'd put in a good word to them...or something like that.  I don't like responsibilities cause if shit hits the fan it goes all over your face.  But I also don't like not being a team player and giving back to the community is a win-win.  *shrugs*  I'll see how things unfold; I just don't wanna be one of them staffers who hold the rank but don't do a fair share of labour. 

The 'Guest of Honours' have also been picked ~ FireStormSix and ZEN!  They're both incredibly amazing! \o.o/ 

Lulz ~ the Ontario Furries Forum now has a Babyfur section primarily due to a recent thread that caused some debate (I wouldn't even call it drama...).  Basically a topic was posted about the monthly ageplay munch that I regularly go to.  Some people were quick to jump the gun on the issue of pedophilia and that didn't make me very happy.  I have some really great friends that go to that munch so of course I defended my opinions.  Surffices to say that topic got removed (I saved a copy of it =P) and Dan Skunk offered me the moderator position for the Babyfur thread.  Like I said, I don't like responsibilities...and I don't want to be a team player on Dan's forum since it's riddled with issues =S.  So to Bennerman, the current mod, - whoever you are - good luck to yeah; I'm doubtful I'll make any posts on that thread XD.

Lastly ~ furries talk a lot about other furs when they aren't around, and I don't mean in the good kind of way.  It's not cool, but it's becoming increasingly unaviodable.  I don't like getting caught up with it, but I'm guilty of participating sometimes.  What does one do to remain neutral while not interjecting awkward silences in discussion?  =S 
💤 sleepy  |  ♫ Drake (ft. Lykke Li) - Little Bit
Link

Great start to a fun month!

May. 3rd, 2010   11:14 PM
This weekend has been really fun!  I hope it's a good sign for how the rest of the month plays out! ^-^  Slick posted a few pictures on the OF forum of the Fur-B-Que from Saturday ~ lookie...

     
The first pic is Frostscar, Rozz, myself, and Danny Dingo as Mac Cat standing outside Pulp Comics; there's a DeSoto sedan behind us.  And the second pic is at an ice cream parlor that had air conditioning that smelt of deliciousness!  (I <3 my 'pillow fight' t-shirt XD)  Hopefully Kitty will put up the pictures she took - there were some videos with a couple of us each taking dabs of hot sauce that was killer >.o

On Sunday I went to Brampton to hang out with  and , as well as stay over for the night.  I always have a fun and relaxing time cubbing out with them, though typically I end up being the only one who's always crinkle-bottomed, romping around in a sleeper, and drinking from a sippy cup =P.  Other than the video gaming, loafing around (I'm very good at that), cuddling, tickling, and hilarity of John Pinette, we had dinner at a Chinese buffet and sang karaoke at a pub.  This was my first time doing karaokee and I now have a certificate which states I lost my 'cherry-o-kee' XD.  I should probably mention it took two pints of liquid courage to accomplish.  Me and singing open mic probably won't happen again or at least not for a long while ^^;;  We didn't crawl into bed till around 4am and I didn't get home till about 5pm ~ good times =3.  (Aha, can't forget, fart jokes with Shiro + Second Life = Epic lulz!)

Anyways, all fun-ness aside, how do you get over that feeling of wishing that there was more you could do for the friends that really matter?  Whether they're having a personal or work-related issue, twice this past week I've felt that there wasn't anything I could do to help and it's a bit unsettling not to mention a real bummer.  I can give people their space or listen to their problems, but at times it's hardly enough to lift any burden off their shoulders.  I donnos...these are strong individuals so I do trust that things will be okay for them =3. 
🎵 mellow  |  ♫ Massive Attack (ft. Hope Sandoval) - Paradise Circus

Fur-B-Que...

May. 2nd, 2010   12:28 AM
...was awesome!  Thanks for the good times Thadius-Effingham, Beckaroo, and everyone else who was there ^-^.  My eyes don't want to stay awake right now (pit fire smoke + contact lenses = no no) so I'll just spit out a few words and hope I remember what they mean whenever I look back on this XD.  - car ride to Niagara Falls with Stew and Furious...rainy but in good company - dog name Jade...so lovable - fursuiting around an empty part of downtown...free comic books \o.o/ - steak you can cut with a plastic knife...sooo sooooo delish D: - Blair's After Death Hot Sauce...made me cry .___. - nomming on munchies other people brought...I feel like a fatty >.o - Nerf battle at the park...missed it completely - fire pit and s'mores...like fishing in fire = fun! - meeting and talking to a bunch of people I've never met...priceless Possibly Brampton bound tomorrow to spend the night with some fellow cubs ~ should be super relaxing <3

Update on the this and that...

Apr. 28th, 2010   12:23 AM
Phew, my legs feel like noodles D:  I got back from the gym not long ago and had a great workout.  It mostly had to do with neglecting to go for a whole week while eating a bunch of unhealthy stuff.  I burned 740 calories in 68 minutes (5.68 miles) on the treadmill; which is my second highest count! ^-^  Seeing as I have more free time on my hands I'm going to try sticking to my old routine of working out at least three times a week.  Must look good for summer!  Grrrr, determination >.< 

====================================================

Baskin Robbins is having their 31¢ Scoop Night again this Wednesday!  Nothing like enjoying some yummy ice cream for a good cause =3.

====================================================

New music recently came out from my two favourite genres!  Sweet!  \o.o/  

'B.o.B Presents: The Adventures of Bobby Ray' comes out today (though it got leaked several days ago :S) and it's a pretty solid album.  A lot of the beats are really fresh and go well with his style, especially since his lyrics are really fun and moving.  The one thing that keeps bothering me though is his voice, it's distinct but the tone and delivery are very uniform in most of his tracks.  Switch it up a bit and play around with your range when you're rapping, I say.  I really love the diverse number of artists featured throughout the album ~ Eminem, Lupe Fiasco, Bruno Mars, T.I, Playboy Tre, Janelle Monae, Ricco Barrino, Hayley Williams of Paramore, and Rivers Cuomo of Weezer.  Outstanding.

'La La Land' by Plants and Animals came out last week ~ they were performing in Toronto a few nights ago and my bro got to go >.o  I have yet to find a download for this album, but I've heard a couple of tracks from YouTube and I like it!  If you haven't heard of them and you're into indie pop-rock, definitely check out their last album 'Parc Avenue' ~ it's good listening!  Plus, they're from Montreal, so of course I gotta show some Canadian love :D

====================================================

Oh snap!  Going to Dave & Buster's tomorrow ~ the second time in less than a week! XD  Last Friday I went with Dodge, his wife, and HappyFoxNick and had an awesome time.  There was a big and adorable Dinkleman plushie (the bear backpack Boog wears in Open Season) that I wanted but didn't have enough tickets to trade in for, so I got the tinier version =P.  This time it's my goal to get the big one! >:3  Grrr, even more determination! >.<  I'll be going with MimiBadger, Cameron, Boyrick (and possibly his daddy), and possibly Kimba.

Hopefully I can take advantage of whatever specials they have going on since it was a bit pricey last time ~ I've already spent about $300 in the past week and the latest VISA statement is ripping me a new one for $950 ^^;;

====================================================

I watched 'The Constant Gardener' yesterday and it was honest to gawd brilliant.  It's about how the world is raping Africa ~ more precisely, through unethical drug trials led by big pharmaceutical companies.  This kind of stuff is going on right this moment.  Powerful people are trading the lives of innocent people for money.  This movie does a great job shedding some light on the issue; it's got murder-mystery, drama, a bit of suspense, and a documentary-like style.  I highly recommend this - don't watch the trailer though, it does the movie no justice.

Apr. 23, 2010

Apr. 23rd, 2010   3:38 PM

It really surprises me that we first met just half a year ago ~ it seems like much longer.  Getting to know you has been a real delight; you're quite different from most people I've met and I find that really interesting.  For example, you're very accomplished - married, got your own house and car, working, enjoying life - and humble at the same time.  It's a very inspirational quality that I very much like about you =3.  Thanks for being a continuous source of fun, social support, getting rides off of you (XD), and all the good times in between!  I hope the many years ahead of you are filled with awesomeness!

Awesome shops is awesome!

Apr. 22nd, 2010   9:15 PM
Instead of studying this afternoon I went downtown to do some shopping...it's not procrastinating!  It's serious business!  XD

I love shops that sell kooky, fun looking, and practical items that aren't commonly found in big malls (with the exception of Asian malls).  To some extent I'm talking about places like Urban Outfitter and Blue Banana Market, but they're relatively big and hard to miss on the street.  The real gems are the small indie stores that either pack their shelves head to toe with items or they take on a minimalist/stylish theme.  I went to three little shops today that rocked my world and made me feel like a kid again...

Rolo - It's a one man store owned by Rowley Ocampo; he's hilarious, cool, down to Earth, and quite fabulous.  Ditto with the store ~ everywhere you look there's interesting and imaginative stuff.

Propaganda - There are more items for girls than guys since they have a selection of clothes and bags, but the unisex items are really hot.  I love the big plushies they have for sale...cookies, donuts, toast, germs, organs and even bacon! 

Magic Pony - This place is a bit more artsy, though they do also sell toys and fun looking things.  There's a room with a gallery in it that's pretty awesome!

It sort of goes without saying that even though indie stores sell really unique stuff, their prices are a more expensive as well.  However, when they have sales going on it's a pretty killer deal!  Give or take it's a fair trade off I say.  Where else are you going to find an umbrella with a samurai sword handle that can be worn on your back, or a condiment dispenser that looks like a cartoon gun? 

I bought myself a new watch ~ I've been meaning to get a new one that's either classy or eye catchy.  The way I've been stalking my wardrobe these past few months you could probably guess which I went for. 

    

Alessi's Calumet 1000 (in lime green) by Italian designer Stefano Pirovano...doesn't that just sound really swanky? XD lulz  Big whoop, I just love the way it looks!  Got it at Rolo for $55 ~ it was 50% off! O3O
🌀 satisfied  |  ♫ Metric - Handshakes
Link

Edjumacation smells funny...

Apr. 21st, 2010   10:43 PM
I love how good weather always brings out smexy cars.  In the last couple of days I've seen a Maserati GranTurismo, Audi R8 and Bentley Continental.  *drools*

=====================================

I've been studying lots for my exam next Monday ~ want some edjumacation?  Here's an easy one...

'Schema' are the mental frameworks that organize or synthesize information about a person, place, or thing.  Basically they are what we think about things.  Our schematas are automatically triggered when we hear certain words or see certain things.  Read this little senario and proceed to after the cut :D

"Two young, nice-looking students are seen having drinks with a professor.  They both receive an A+ in their course."

- you're probably assuming the professor is a male and the students are females (why weren't you thinking the reverse or all the same gender?)
- you're probably assuming 'drinks' refer to the alcoholic kind (if alcoholic than probably at a bar, but why not coffee and a cafe?)
- you're probably assuming there is sex or some sort of inappropriate relationships involved
- you're probably assuming the senario is only going one way (what if the students received A's and were celebrating with the prof?) 

Schematas are very useful in our day-to-day, but because they're done without much thought we make a bunch of assumptions that may or may not be valid!  Hooray brain power! \o.o/﻿

=====================================

Made a new entry that'll be staying on the top of my blog with some things worth knowing.  Thanks to the journal statistics I've been noticing more and more people reading my stuff.  I don't know who most of them are - and first impressions are fairly important - so I figure I'd give them a heads up =3

"When I get older I will be stronger/They'll call me freedom, just like a wavin' flag"

Apr. 20th, 2010   1:30 PM
Last night was the first 'Toronto Littles' Play Date' - 'Unbirthday Party' event - and all things considered it was fun in a different sort of context from what I'm use to.  I like different ~ trying new things is a good way to spice up your life =3.  Before going, I hung out at Boyricky's place with Lenny at around 6:30pm.  Some liquid courage in a sippy cup helped mellow me out, not to mention make me piss like a foutain; luckily I was well padded heh (had on a cloth diaper over a Molicare).  They helped me name two (cute and cheap) puppy plushies I brought along, K'Naan and Sprocket!  Unfortunately Ricky had to take care of some grow-up responsibilities and couldn't come to the party, so Lenny and I headed out at around 8pm.  We got there later than expected  because of my poor sense of direction ^^;.  We also lost a bit of time from having a conversation with a friendly chap who wanted to know where he could cut loose for the night and pick up chicks ~ he was married and looked to be in his mid-thirties .___.  We got there sometime before 9pm, which was a bit of a bummer since the event was from 7-10pm.  There was around fifteen people; predominantly little girls - two came with their daddy's - I think there were two other caregivers, and one trans (MtF) that I probably offended with a bad Cinderella joke (sorry!) D:  It was pretty obvious we were the only two diaper boys XP.  The scale of ageplay regression was more 'littles' (ages 3 and up) than 'babies'.  I still enjoyed myself none the less, mainly because I like the idea of being a toddler more than a baby and I've always had a curious interest in sissification.  After settling in and quickly being introduced, two of the grown ups put on a puppet show!  It was really fun and helped eased me into the right mindset ^-^.  Afterwards I changed into my sleeper (mostly everyone there had a cute outfit on) and toddled around with K'Naan and my sippy cup =3.  We played 'Pin the Candle on the Alice in Wonderland Cake', decorated cupcakes, sang to a Happy Unbirthday song, and there was a story time ~ one of the daddy's read 'Purple, Green, and Yellow' by Robert Munsch.  Nearing the end of the event we each received a loot bag and got to pick out toys from a big spread ~ then we all helped clean up ^-^.  I wish we arrived earlier so not to miss all the fun! >.o  Oh wells, maybe next month depending on who I go with ~ Ricky and his daddy should come to make all the little boys feel safe and supportive :D  I wrapped up the night at around 12:30am with Lenny and Ricky ~ a bit more beer, some pretty harsh weed, and a whole lot of children's music (Raffi is awesome <3).  We spiffied up one of the pictures on Ricky's wall; I wonder if his daddy will notice O3O.  Agh!  Back to hardcore study mode until I go have fun again on the weekend and then forget everything!
😄 cheerful  |  ♫ Raffi - Bananaphone
Link

Kiss and blog #9

Apr. 18th, 2010   5:48 PM
(continuing from last post) Oh gawd, why do I keep making out with +40 year olds who are balding, wear glasses, and are scruffy in the face?  Sure some of them are cute, but do I really need to make a display of it?  I could feel people look at me when Matthew was all over me in Ricky’s living room.  I don’t know whether he was way drunk or genuinely into me, but I’ve always felt he was a pretty cool guy.  We full blown made out on Ricky’s bed ~ he was kissing and tonguing me ALL over.  Kinda weird but also pretty erotic.  He was also a nibbler and a finger sucker like my time at FCN with Pasha.  I wasn’t hard when he was feeling me, but that doesn’t mean I don’t think he’d make for a good rump.  Had I stayed over at his place for the night I probably would’ve gotten fucked…in a diaper…and I may have enjoyed it.  There will be next times I’m sure.  Facts: -    Lenny has slept with Ricky and Cameron -    Lenny has a thing for Cameron -    Dan is rooming with BaBySB1 -    Anthony has a thing for BaBySB1
