Hungover after sippy cup beer...I gotta study now? Agh! =.=**

Apr. 18th, 2010   3:02 PM
Despite not having an ABDL munch this month (the pub is in the processes of relocating) I had a really fun night yesterday with a couple of the regulars.  Dan Raccoon and Shiro called me out in the afternoon to arrange some dinner plans ~ sort of a play it by ear thing since none of us knew of any good eats.  I met them and Xaxoqual at around 6pm and then we wandered down Yonge St. looking for a place to eat.  I think we easily passed a dozen Japanese restaurants in the span of three blocks =S.  We settled for Fran’s on College St; incidentally Fran’s (on Victoria St.) was the previous munch location =P.  I had the beer and burger combo and it was pretty delish!  After dinner we walked a full circle almost back to where we started.  I received an invite to Boyricky’s place earlier that night so we all went up and hung out with Matthew, Lenny, and Cameron.  Not long after settling in Kimba and Mimi joined the party! ^-^

It’s interesting to me how I use to write about these ABDL/babyfur gatherings in my blog, describing them as liberating experiences.  They still are in some sense, but it’s no longer something I’ll become overly excited for, or need to look forward to in order to unwind.  I don’t want to say its become routine, but the opportunities are much more abundant that they’ve become some thing of a part of me.  It’s just something I enjoy doing and I’m perfectly fine admitting =3.  Okay, that was a silly spiel…

So I had a really awesome night!  Got padded in some comfy Cushies.  Got to show off my spiffy new Gelato shoes.  Got tickled to death by practically everyone >.o  Got drunk off beer…in a sippy cup!  Got a lot of laughs from watching YouTube.  Good times all around ~ thanks for all the fun Ricky!  At around 2:45am Lenny and I headed for home; got some Timmies before going our separate ways.  I didn’t get home until sometime after 4am because of the infrequent blue line buses ~ probably should’ve spent the night over at Matthew’s place T__T.  From what was discussed, it looks like I’ll be enjoying some more ageplay fun tomorrow night.  Ricky, Lenny, myself and hopefully Marvin will be going to the TLDP ‘Unbirthday Party’ ~ my early impressions are hinting that it’ll be…different. 
🐇 bouncy  |  ♫ Nine Inch Nails - Complication

Kiss and blog #8

Apr. 18th, 2010   11:46 AM
(continuing from FCN con report) Hanging out with Pasha, Lights, and Sirrah provided some new experiences.  Getting my picture taken while cuddling them in crinkles was different ~ I'm not sure if it was kinky or frightening or whatever.  But I'd really love to see those pics some time hehe.  During one of the changes I got a hard one probably because of a bit of humiliation, which I'm really beginning to dig.  I also got to wear a thick leather collar and spanked with a paddle.  I guess I can tolerate both, but they weren't really turn ons per say.  I'm not a big fan of pain, but it was cute when Pasha said he spanked me so that I knew not to do something I hadn't yet done heh.  I kind of liked it when I had to carry a stack of diapers back to my room ~ in the elevator there was a suiter and a fur.  They seemed to mnd their own business, but they were cool about it.  Pasha made out with me and he's quite keen on nibbling my neck and ears.  It hurt a bit, but I'm not really sure whether I liked it or not.  The moments were pretty hot none the less.  Lastly, I finally got to experience someone peeing in my diaper while I was wearing it!  I freakin' loved it!  Pasha pissed down the back of my diaper and it felt warm and tickley and smelly, but I was so horny.  I want more of that. Random: - Sirrah sucking on my thumb was pretty cute - I feel bad for bumming so many Bambino and Abena off Pasha - Buying the old Attends from Lights was a rip off =\ - I really liked wandering around the con in my diaper (Pasha kept my boxers).  Having the crinkles peek out was wicked.
Link

Con Report: Furry Connection North 2010

Apr. 17th, 2010   4:02 PM
The longer I sit on this the more I eventually forget what I did at the con.  Sucks to be such a procrastinator especially since I have an ass load of studying to catch up on .___.  Oh wells ~ here we go! 

(It’s definitely worth mentioning that I met a LOT of people and there is no way I’d remember them all.  So apologies in advance and thanks to everyone that made my con experience so awesome ^-^)

Thursday
The combination of alcohol from the night before and the excitement upon waking didn’t give way to much sleep.  I had to get up early to meet at the pick up location, which was the Doubletree where FE was held ~ always an unpleasant commute to get to but convenient for the driver.  From there I met with Danny Dingo and shortly after 9am Diesel came to pick us up.  After a bit of highway delay (GPS is our friend <3) we picked up Hypr in Guelph – where we fuelled up on free coffee – and then continued on to Novi by way of Sarnia.  We were making pretty good time until we got stopped at the border for a ‘random’ secondary inspection.  They searched the truck while we filled out a form and were asked questions in the office.  It was a bit nerve-racking but also quite interesting to watch how jittery the others were =P.  While waiting we bumped into Sammy Stripes and Kitty ~ a furry coincidence?  At that point I was kinda hoping the rest of the Canadian furs would get stopped at the border since we lost fifty minutes >>”. 

I haven’t been to the states in probably seven years, so crossing over to Michigan was suddenly very apparent ~ there were a ton of American cars, American flags, and medical/religious billboards.  We arrived at the hotel by about 3:30pm and the lobby was already full of furs and suiters (zomg!  Skroy is so adorable!).  I’m not sure how many new people I met but it was quite the warm welcoming.  While waiting for our room I bumped into BushyCat and her mate Moonshine Fox.  Surprisingly she remembered me (having met at FE) since I’ve heard that she tends to forget people.  I’m her #1 Azn! \o.o/  After settling in Diesel, Hypr and I wandered around the nearby Best Buy, Meijers, and Trader Joe’s ~ I bought some Svedka and orange juice ^-^.  At around 5:30pm we met with a large group of furs in the lobby and then flocked to Chili’s for the pre-con dinner.  So many people, too little brain space to remember them all .___.  But I suppose I can give a shout out to Rei Meerkat, Jinnithan, Ratchet, and Mace since they sat closest to me.  It was a lot of fun and the food wasn’t too bad. 

    

After dinner I left with Dodge back to the hotel to get our pre-reg stuff.  The badges weren’t as cool as the FE buttons and I’m not really sure why the mascot is a moose; it seems a bit too Canadian o__O.  Afterwards I helped him suit up in his room ~ it takes a bit of work getting into but it looks really awesome!  We wandered the con space meeting a bunch of people and then danced a bit before retiring back to our rooms for a break.  I love the set up for the dance, its got everything from a fog machine to lasers! D:  Back in my room I joined Diesel for some drinks with Odin, Azure and Razor; it’s always fun meeting new babyfurs ^-^.  The rest of the night was spent wandering the con space, hanging out in the Fursuit Lounge (got to meet SyberFox =3), and eating pizza while watching Adult Swim with Dodge, Sammy, Kitty, and Diesel.  I hit the hay a little tipsy by 2am and can’t honestly say I had a good sleep =S.  But whatever, if that day was any indication how much fun the rest of the con would be than kick ass! 

Friday
Woke up sometime before 7:30am and loafed around the lobby in front of the TV news with some other furs.  It was also one of the few times I was able to browse the web since everywhere else didn’t have free WiFi.  A short while later Hypr and I ate at the hotel breakfast buffet – a bit overpriced and the selection was small, but good eats none the less.  Later I checked in early to the room I’d be staying in for the next three days with Danny and Diesel; the only thing that really bugged me was the king size bed rather than a double >.<**  After getting settled, I worked out in the fitness centre for about an hour.  Their treadmills are so sweet ~ the display is touchscreen and it doubles as a TV!  At around 10:30am I met up with Rozz – who was already in full suit – to give her my old key card, we then headed to the Opening Ceremony.  Nothing out of the ordinary you’d expect to see at an Opening Ceremony; keeping it short and sweet was good at least. 

We headed to McDonald’s afterwards for some fruit smoothies.  I don’t think they have that here in Canada and it’s surprising that they use real fruits.  It’s hardly a healthy drink though, sadly =(.  While walking there we passed three guys who asked the usual ‘What’s with the animal suit?  What’s a furry?’ kind of questions ~ I was freezing my nips off while we answered them T__T.  We had to go through the same ordeal while ordering, but at least they seemed friendlier about it.  Back at the hotel we hung around the lobby with some other furs until about lunch time.  The shout outs I made on the babyfur LJ community for a Friday lunch meet-up was fail! \o.o/  Hooray!  Actually, I didn’t have a clue what anyone looked like so I could’ve walked passed them without even knowing.  And plus I would eventually meet peeps like Toracub, Blithe, and Swift Fox later during the con.  I ended up having lunch with D’Bandit and Dragoth in the Sponsor’s Lounge ~ always a pleasure meeting new people ^-^. 

In the afternoon I ran into Pardus – like one of the other dozen or less Asians at the con – and then I never really saw him again until Sunday =S  (sneaky like ninja).  After picking up my Mary Mouse badge and going to the GOH panel, I got suckered into helping with a food run with Blind Chris for the Dealer’s Den ~ at least I got tipped =.=**  Checked out Uncle Kage’s panel on ‘Presenting Furry Fandom to the Public’ where he gave some interesting tips while recapping funny experiences; he was drinking of course.  I didn’t stay very long for that since I wanted to check out the ‘Lego Building Contest’ at 3:30pm.  There was a room with three of four big round tables covered with Lego pieces! D:  Wicked fun!  It’s too bad I didn’t have anyone to play with =P.  For the contest there were three themes that you could build for: ‘80s Movies’, ‘Pirates vs. Ninjas’, and ‘The Future of Tomorrow Today’.  I ended up building a small display of pirates fighting a mecha ninja over some booty ~ laugh all you want, it got me third place and a free con tee! XD 

Headed to the Sponsor’s Lounge since meals were being served and the kegs were open.  Can’t say I’m too fond of the ‘Motor City Nut Brown Ale’ but the alternative ‘Michigan Lager’ wasn’t too bad ~ whatever gets me drunk, I say!  While nomming I met a bunch of furs including Dantee, who might possibly be one highlight of my con.  Dude, you’re awesome!  We headed up to his room where I met Norby, Matt, and Kailani, and we all smoked pink lemonade flavoured shisha.  I’ve only done hookah once before this and it’s always seemed like such a chill way to hang out ~ smoking isn’t cool kids!  We were a bit worried the fire alarm would go off since the room was getting hazy, but all was good and I had a fun time ^-^.  Later in the evening Dantee suited up for the ‘Fursuit Photoshoot’ but got trumped by the long line-up, so we went to the ‘Fursuit Performance’ panel.  Not what I expected.  Maybe it’s because we only stayed for the first half an hour – out of the two hour – but the stuff they discussed was really depressing.   

   

The rest of the evening was spent mostly either drinking in the lounge or smoking hookah outside ~ possibly the unhealthiest way to be social.  But it’s always such a riot when you’ve got music blasting, furs in a circle, and suiters dancing around.  I met Ark the Shadow, Xander, Sara, La Rune, Morin, and so many others in the processes =P.  I also got to say hi to FirestormSix, RoboWolf, Deoge, Howiepup as well as hug Arinmal in his new glowy fursuit; he’s so much fun to be around <3.  At around 10:30pm, Dantee suited up again and we went to the dance for a while – a couple of b-boys were really carving up the floor.  Later into the night we went and watched ‘Who’s Lion is it Anyways?’.  Lots of audience participation made for a really great watching.  Unfortunately I didn’t stay for the whole thing (since it was like three hours long) cause I needed food and liquor in me .___.”  Hung out with Diesel and met Pasha at the Sponsor’s Lounge, and was later taken back to Pasha’s room and met Naka, Lights, and Sirrah.  \o.o/  Yay (again) for meeting new babyfurs/diaperfurs!  I had a really relaxing time getting padded and cuddling in front of the TV <3.  Though I probably could have stayed the night, space was a premium, so I crawled back to my room at around 3:30am. 

Saturday
Began my morning pretty early again – had a decent work out in the fitness centre from around 7:45 to 9:30am.  After getting ready for the day I kicked it off by being one of the three judges for the ‘Fursuit Charades’.  There were at least a dozen suiters and guessing what they were performing was a lot of fun!  Afterwards I hung out with a couple of furs in the lobby before heading up to the lounge for lunch.  Took a spot next to Pakesh for the ‘Fursuit Parade’ at 1pm ~ he made for good commentary while I was recording XD.  There were definitely a lot of amazing suits which made me a bit jealous not having my partial, while at the same time it made me quite interested in getting a full suit of a new character.  (Who wants me to throw money at them?! D:)  The real treat at the end of the parade was the DeLorean (the car from ‘Back to the Future’) parked outside; quite fitting with the 80s theme and it made for an awesome photoshoots.  As the large group outside began to disperse, I headed back in with Dodge and Rozz – they were my access pass into the Fursuit Lounge where we hung out for a bit.  I met a lot of suiters including Tzup ~ another Asian!  Apparently I completely missed him at IF5 >.<**  Oh wells, I’m a noob. 

    

Having rested for a bit, Dodge and I scoped out the Dealer’s Den ~ got myself a new badge by BushyCat ^-^.  I didn’t buy anything else at the con despite seeing a couple of tempting items; I suppose my wallet really appreciated that.  Next we went and watched the ‘Furry Variety Show & Bucktown Live’.  I’m going to have the ‘YES Dance’ skit engraved in my brain for a long while o.O  It’s hilariously entertaining.  Unfortunately I can’t really say the same for the other acts, sorry =\.  And if you haven’t heard of Bucktown Tiger you should look into him; he’s pretty talented! ^-^  Following the show, we headed out for Tim Hortons with Rozz but not before browsing around the Artist Alley.  I love how people were digging my ‘This Game Sucks’ tee ~ awesome shirt is awesome D:  And holy crap they have candy bar donuts down here!  I don’t think I’ve ever seen them in Canada before .____.  They’re deliciously fattening. 



Back at the hotel I hung around the Sponsor’s Lounge getting my drink on and meeting Amma and Irish Fox.  After forgoing a trip to White Castle with Hypr and eating lounge food, I headed outside to smoke some hookah with Dantee, Diesel, Corvin, Addik, and a couple others.  Smoke coming out from a suiters mouth, eyes, and sometimes even ears is really awesome to see!  Plus double apple is just yummy.  Went in at 8pm to watch ‘Uncle Kage’s Story Hour’ with Dantee ~ pretty damn funny though it’s unfortunate that 2 the Ranting Gryphon couldn’t make it to the con.  When that was over I headed up to Pasha’s room to hang out for a bit and get some crinkles on.  For the life of me I can’t remember the names of two furs that invited us up to their room for some drinks ~ sorry! >.<  But your homebrew beer and wine was really delish!  Later into the night I joined a huge circle of furs in the back terrace to smoke two hookahs; one belonging to Rob and the others Dantee. 

    

Incidentally it was also Dantee’s birthday and he accidentally put vodka/Mt. Dew into the hookah instead of water ~ best mistake ever!  Getting drunk off smokes o.O  I had a really great time despite not remembering most of the people there.  What ever happened to that book being passed around with our names written on it?  Or getting a hookah group on FA started?  Damn furries =P.  It was really awesome of Ashley to pass out a ton of glowsticks as well; it really set the mood and provided some much needed light.  And lastly, Rei Meerkat shotgunning with Lil Chi Wolf in suit ~ that’s pretty smokin’.  It kinda sucked balls when everyone upped and left when the hookah cashed at around 11:30pm.  At least show some class by sticking around until everything is cleaned and packed away =\.  Damn furries >:(  Afterwards Dantee and I met up with Diesel and we did a drive-thru run at Taco Bells and McDonald’s.  Other than roaming around the con space and dancing with a bunch of furs, the rest of the night spent chilling in BushyCat’s room.  It was quite reminiscent of FE.  We stayed up and talked, watched TV, and of course I distracted her while she worked on commissions ~ the only difference this time was her mate snoring up a storm XD.  Didn’t crawl back to my room until around 5:30am .___.

Sunday
Sunday was really laid back but the time just sorta flew by.  Woke up at around 10am and missed the ‘Fit Furries’ panel that Razor and Odin hosted; I wanted to check that out but oh wells.  Ate breakfast at a nearby diner with BushyCat, Dantee, Ark the Shadow, and Danny Dingo ~ I wasn’t very good =\.  Bummed around the Dealer’s Den for a while and then hung out in Pasha’s room with Sirrah, Kiva, and Dash until the ‘Fursuit Games’ started.  There were quite a number of suiters there, but the ones that weren’t participating were the ones I enjoyed watching the most – like JD Puppy, Tzup, and Vin.  I didn’t stay for the whole thing though, by about 3pm I went out with Pardus, Blithe, and Lights for a quick bite to eat.  The rest of the afternoon seemed to go by pretty quickly as some people began heading for home.  I sat around with Pak, Shane and Blindsight in the lobby and saw them off with Rozz and Dodge.  If I wasn’t smoking outside with Dantee or drinking in the Sponsor’s Lounge I was hanging out in Pasha’s room to pass the time. 

    

After the ‘Closing Ceremony’ I joined Dantee, Silverpaw, Sgt. Shepherd, Diesel, Ark the Shadow, La Rune, and Morin for a final puff of the hookah since we were down to the last couple of coals.  I got really hyped when Silverpaw told me where he got his Heelys ~ I had to have them! >.<  Unfortunately all the stores in the mall were closed when Diesel, Dantee and I got there at around 8pm.  Oh wells, the pair I’ve ordered online have already arrived so I’m quite happy =P.  We ate subs for dinner before heading back to the con.  I have no idea how I got it but for the rest of the night I had a really bad headache, which sucked a lot since there were a bunch of room parties going on with copious amounts of alcohol involved T__T.  Didn’t really get my fills worth but I hung out with various furs before calling it a night at around 2am. 

Monday
Nothing really worth writing for Monday.  Said my goodbyes to some really awesome people that I’ll hopefully meet again and then hit the road before noon.  It was a relatively mind numbing drive home with a couple of stops here and there.  (Final shout out to Jeno whom I kept bumping into at the con but never really got to know XD  I just had to throw you in here somewhere =P) 

In sum, total booze and party con ~ I loved it!  Plus I’ve probably inhaled and smoked enough to shave five years off of my life.  \o.o/  Yay for early deathbed!  But really, I had an insanely fun time and met a ton of amazing people (many I probably won’t remember, sadly).  Props to the staff, security, and all the volunteers; without them I’m sure this con would be full of fail.  I only regret not taking more pictures…must be my resentfulness of not having my partial with me >:3  I’ll definitely make an effort to come back next year and I totally look forward to the next American cons I attend ^-^.  PafCon…YES! 



Total damage to my wallet: approximately $360.  Not bad I say =3.
﻿
😌 content  |  ♫ B.o.B - Out of Time
Link

Yay! \o.o/ Mail!

Apr. 15th, 2010   5:42 PM
1)  Beauty is a government cheque!  My income tax refund came in the mail today ^-^  Sure it's less than half of last years, but considering I haven't worked in a while, it's all good =3.  Save it or spend it ~ hmmmm.

2)  At last!  My partial arrived this morning!  I was beginning to get really worried.  *does a little dance*

3)  Yum, gelato...on my feet!  My new pair of Heelys came in the mail today ~ quite the speedy delivery since I ordered them just two days ago.
   
There were a lot of different styles but I loved the Gelato's the most.  OMG the colours! O3O  Don't judge me! XD  I'm a kid at heart, if you didn't already know that than you don't really know me ^^;;  These will take some getting use to ~ I've already fallen hard on my ass once T__T.  But I'm determined!  WHEEEE! *bruises his delicate male ego*

FCN report...coming soon...

Apr. 12th, 2010   10:20 PM
The good:
Got home from Furry Connection North a few hours ago ~ I had an insanely good time!  I'll share my thoughts when I get around to writing them down ^-^

The bad:
I'm unhappy that I didn't get my partial back before the con since it would've doubled my fun easily.  But now I'm a bit worried about it because I still haven't gotten it .____.

The want:
Two furs at the con (Skroy and Silverpaw) were wearing roller shoes ~ Heelys.  I've only ever seen them for kids and never in adult sizes.  My life will not be complete without a pair and sadly my attempt at buying them on Sunday was foiled by early closing hours.
😴 tired  |  ♫ Ingrid Michaelson - Keep Breathing
Link

Furless =(

Apr. 7th, 2010   11:48 PM
Looks like I won't be fursuiting at FCN ~ this sucks big time! T___T  I sent my partial back to MS-Manuscript two weeks ago to get some changes done, mainly the ears because they flopped forwards which I didn't really like.  He sent it back to me March 31st on a three day delivery so I was really hoping to get it today =(.  It's really disheartening since I've already raised that level of expectation from my first con experience in suit ~ to take a step backwards is just blah.  I won't even have my tail! ><**  Or get to participate in events like the fursuit charades, parade, games, photoshot, or dancing in suit.  It sucks as well because I bought some cheap and crappy 80s looking clothes at Value Village the other day which I won't get to wear .___.  I suppose the only benefit to not suiting is having way less luggage, but regardless I'm not a happy camper ~ donno if I'll be able to sleep it off either.  You know what'll probably end up happen?  I would bet my right nut that the package will arrive tomorrow a couple of hours after I've left the house =.=** Anyways, tonight was the monthly furry dinner made extra important because it was Morgan's memorial dinner.  There were probably 35-40 furs there with maybe half whom I'd never met.  Celebrating Morgan's life and listening to speeches that recalled experiences with him was very heartfelt.  I really wish I could have known him better, he had an impact on many people's lives.  Going to bed now, gotta wake up early -.-*  Peace.
😞 depressed  |  ♫ Torley on Piano - Cold Motion
Link

A trip to ponder...

Apr. 7th, 2010   2:30 PM
Tomorrow morning I'll be on my way to Novi, Michigan, to attend Furry Connection North ~ it'll be my first time travelling with friends.  To an extent it feels like a long time coming; I know a lot of people past and present who've travelled by themselves, with family, friends, school and/or work related.  Occasionally I feel jealous with what those people get to experience, whether they are riding out on their parents' expense or using own hard earned funds.  I've always enjoyed the idea of travelling but doing it requires time, money, planning, and a sense of self.  I'm 22 years old and only now am I going somewhere.  But this place I'm going to - the reason I'm travelling - isn't something people outside of the fandom can relate back to ~ it isn't tradition so to speak.  And in that regard, this trip doesn't feel like that big of a deal anymore.  I doubt I would have felt the same way had my trip to Japan worked out.  Don't get me wrong though, I'm excited to be going and expecting a really great time, but it's a different kind of travelling that feels really foreign to me. 
😑 blah  |  ♫ Styles P (ft. Pharoahe Monch) - The Life
Link

Easter Day and stuff...

Apr. 4th, 2010   10:05 PM
Happy Easter to those who celebrate the occasion ~ can't say I do.  I remember when I was a little kid my parents did an egg hunt...and I got pwn'd by my brother cause he found most of them before me >.<  Oh wells, me and chocolate aren't friends =\. 

Check out these cool Easter Eggs! O3O

============================================

I watched the Sony Ericsson Open finals today ~ Roddick was victorious!  Overall it was a good match, but nothing really spectacular.  I'm happy he won none the less.  This marks the end of hard court tournaments, now it's on to grass and clay!

============================================

Congratulations to Jully who got baptized today ^-^.  Her sudden serious focus on religion was a bit unexpected, but I support her all the way! 

============================================

I watched Defendor today after looking up Arrowroot's fanart of the main character.  Woody Harelson's acting is brilliant ~ his character has a very child-like sense of morals and understanding and he captures it so well!  Sandra Oh is also in it and she plays a pretty good counter-character.  The other star actors are Elias Koteas and Kat Dennings.  The movie itself is fun to follow and fairly well thought out.  I recommend watching this movie mainly for the characters though; it's got a lot of heart. 

============================================

Jeez!  Only four more days until I leave for FCN ~ exciting! :D
😊 happy  |  ♫ Ratatat and Despot - Freestyle
Link

*piggyback*..."Purple nurple for throttle"

Apr. 3rd, 2010   11:11 PM
The High Park gathering was pretty awesome thanks to a decent turnout and some relaxing fun ^-^.  Regrettably, I wasn't able to really get to know some of Arrowroot's friends mostly because they hung around in their own group, but they were really cool.  I also get to meet some new faces, like Justin and Diesel, as well as some old, Mimi and Furious.  We did quite a lot of walking, which was a bit tiring but also refreshing since the last time I'd gone on any of the trails was when I was a kid.  I remember going to the little zoo and riding on the trackless train back in the days; we went past both today.  I brought along a frisbee and some badminton racquets, though sadly we were all pretty terrible at the latter XD.  After snacking on some ice cream at Baskin Robbins we called it a day ~ much appreciated for the lift home Diesel =3.  I hope this becomes a regular thing cause it was an enjoyable (and inexpensive) way to spend a couple of hours in Toronto!


Pic taken by Arrowroot.  I don't know why but I really like how our shadows look on the planks =P.
💤 sleepy  |  ♫ Theophilus London - Grey x Sage (Sabali Remix)
Link

Yay! Roddick is into the finals!

Apr. 2nd, 2010   5:27 PM
Oh man, I watched the semi-finals of the Sony Ericsson Open between Rafael Nadal (4) and Andy Roddick (8) and it was incredible!  For the first set and a half Rafa was playing his usual high quality tennis while Roddick seemed a bit sluggish around the court.  He couldn't keep up while at the baseline and whenever he went up to the net Rafa would blow a winner right past him just out of reach.  Playing too defensive just isn't his style and relying on his Roddick serve can only get him so far ~ even though both his first and second serves are very reliable.  What's more he kept using his backhand, which paled in comparison to to Rafa's forehand mainly because he's a right handed player.  Rafa's forehand down the line were do deadly where as last year he used it less in favour of doing crosscourt shots; dude is a brilliant player no doubt and he's still got a lot of game in him!  In the second set Roddick was back in great form after winning twice on Rafa's serve.  The third set followed similarily with a lot of great serve and volley from Roddick ~ after taking the lead from one of Rafa's service games, all he had to do was maintain his serves.  I am so happy he won!  It would be wicked if Robin Soderling (7) also wins his semi-final match against Tomas Berdych (20), and if he does I don't really care who wins the finals.  It'll just be an incredible match to watch! ^-^

I feel great ~ crazy what a little sports on tv can do heh =P.  I went jogging outside prior to the match and I'll probably hit the gym later tonight.  Then tomorrow High Park gathering ~ which could get a bit hectic since Harry's Spring Run-Off falls on the same day.  I hope everyone has a great long weekend! =3
🐇 bouncy  |  ♫ Our Lady Peace - 4am
Link

I am so dead...

Apr. 1st, 2010   4:09 AM
HOLY F**K AM I SCREWED!  I really did it this time T___T 

I was doing 140km/hr last night on hwy 404 after playing badminton with Jully.  I wanted to get home before midnight so that I could shower ~ my parents are anal about using the shower late at night since it's noisey and wakes those trying to sleep.  I was turning into the exit ramp to get onto the 401 west and out of no where I smacked into a car that was in my blindspot!  I swear to gawd I didn't see it and only heard the guy honking at the last second!  I've collided with another car before when I was in highschool, but that was way different cause I was only going like 20km/hr.  This was freakin' serious and I was scared shitless when I pulled over.  When i got out of the car this tall white guy was yelling his lungs out at me and immediately I could tell the left front side of his car was really busted up ~ he was driving a blue VW Passat.  Surprisingly the RAV4 I was driving only had a minor indent on the right side of the back bumper and a couple of nasty scratches.  I didn't really say much since it was totally my fault, but after he calmed down a bit he called the cops!  I know it's the right thing to do and I would've done the same, but I was practically begging him not to call.  I even offered we go to the bank so that I could pay him off, but seriously I wouldn't have had enough anyways.  LONGEST AND MOST MISERABLE TWO HOURS IN MY LIFE!  I'm not going to go into the details; it was just simply the worst experience of my life and I know it's going to get way worse.  I only got home about half an hour ago so I have no idea how I'm going to tell my parents.  I am so dead...I can probably forget about going to any conventions, dinners, munches, gatherings, or anything that involves money.  Agh!  I should've got off my ass and got a job earlier!!

Awww come on, tell me you saw that coming...It's April Fools' Day! =P  I'm not the most brilliant driver on the road - Jully made clear of that twice yesterday XD - but I'm not a moron like my brother.  It's all good, I'm just playing with you all ^-^.  
Link

"Oh sheesh y'all..."

Mar. 31st, 2010   7:41 PM
So the Condition: Zero Hour early bird registration ends today, but the whole signing up processes is a bit bollocks.  It's a two step processes where you gotta wait for them to email you back rather than a straight sign-up/paypal dealio.  The silly thing is that the password they gave me isn't logging me in ^^;;.  I've been debating whether or not to go since I'm no good at long term planning and it's way out in August ~ but the main draw being the awesome looking multipass badges and of course all that other fun con stuff.  I need to find a way to get there and room with some peeps to make the trip more affordable, but I figure I can always bail if I decide not to go, it's only fifty bucks =P. 

Duuuude, Elliott's Spring Gathering looks like a world of fun!  Go-karting, a water park, an arcade, some meals provide, plus the usual fun con stuff for three days!  And it's all practically free! D:  Holy shit I want to go!  I'm totally down with paying for a plane ticket and hotel room, but I wouldn't want to go alone =(  Anyone interested?!?  T___T

================================================

I <3 Roddick.  That man is on fire!  I watched his quarter-final match this afternoon against Almagro and he owned.  The other QF match later tonight should be a really good one, it's between Nadal and Tsonga.  On the other side of the draw is Berdych against Verdasco and Youzhny against Soderling.  I would love to see a Roddick vs. Soderling finals ~ two of my favourite players!

================================================

Gonna have coffee with Jully tonight and then polish off the night with some badminton ^-^.  It should be a lot of fun!
🐇 bouncy  |  ♫ The Mars Volta - Cassandra Gemini
Link

"There are words in a language and there are just life experiences."

Mar. 30th, 2010   8:02 PM
Not much has been going on with me lately so there's been nothing to write about =\.  I'm a really boring person and these past few days have been mind numbing >>".  I find that I need to be attracted to fun or curious people in order to make the most out of myself.  It's a bummer when those types of people aren't available and I'm sort of left with people that interest me less.  I guess I'm a need person ._____.

I'm looking forward to ending this month; somehow it feels as though once I turn the page on my calendar to April things will be much better.  The warm and sunny weather is definitely making it seem that way.  Plus there are a number of things to look forward to, the biggest being Furry Connection North.  I've recently begun downloading the second season of 'Departures' and after watching the first episode I can't help but want to get out of the house and do stuff.  I would love to just go into the city and see Novi, or even Detroit, during the con rather than being cooped up in the hotel.  But somethings are out of my control ~ c'est la vie.

My night

Mar. 26th, 2010   10:46 PM
Smoked Winter's bong once and felt really really warped all night.
Gave me Fallout installers.
Ian's chocolate covered coffee beans.
Gavine's Guiness ~ hard to drink!
Drank only two of my Holsten Maibock.
Pop corn.
Diaper wetting.
Watch cartoons and didn't talk much cause was high.
Bus/Sub home safely. 
Link

Random update or 'if I had Twitter...'

Mar. 26th, 2010   4:10 PM
Jeez, I donno where my head was at this morning =S.  I had a dentist appointment at 1:30pm and an hour prior to that I have to take meds for my heart.  For whatever reason I took'em at 11:30am ~ I donno what I was thinking heh, hopefully I don't get sick cause that'll be a barrel of fun =.=**  Anyways, it's a good thing they reminded me about my appointment yesterday because I completely forgot about it!  Great timing too since I chipped my incisor at the start of the week =P.  As much as I don't like going to them, I really love my dentist.  He gave me a white filling for free - which is normally around $100 - and called it a graduation present T___T.  He's sort of a family friend (went to school with my uncle who's also a dentist) and I've been going to him since I was little, so the perks are nice =3.

==================================================

Heading over to Winter Tw Wolf's place tonight ~ St. Patrick's day all over again?  Nah, it should be a tamer night, but I will be bringing the booze and video games again ^-^.  It'll be a nice way to end a sick and boring week and the start of a relaxing weekend. 

==================================================

Submitted my taxes the other day with UFile.ca  Whether or not I did them correctly is questionable, but at this point I don't really give a damn XP.  It was kinda confusing!  In any case, it said I should get a little over two bills, so that should be spiffy =3.

==================================================

I'm using a new LJ Theme ~ it's called 'Aurora'; before I was using 'Elephant Summer'.  Probably a little late, since winter was last season, but I thought a change of theme would be nice to look at =).  I don't think other LJ users can see themes when they read off of their friends pages, unless they actually go into the entry or the journal itself.  In any case, it surprises me how many people even read the things I write here until they give a shout out.  So that's pretty cool.

==================================================

My grad photos are online and ready bought and packaged D:  They look okay and all, but I've never been a fan of self portraits ~ I got that whole squinty eye thing going on -.-"

==================================================

TAT I need start going to the gym regularly again ~ my stamina has gone completely poop!  I went yesterday afternoon and only managed to do 1.89 miles in 25 minutes (248 calories) on the treadmill.  Compared to last Friday, before I got sick, I did 5.90 miles in 65 minutes (819 calories).

==================================================

I'm getting back into reading comic books o.o  Or more specifically, ones by Mark Millar heh.  I read Kick-Ass #1-7 and The Ultimates 1-3, currently reading Marvel 1985, and soon I'll start Wanted. 

For Kick-Ass, if the movie follows with the comic than it'll be pretty good and gory ~ I'd watch it despite knowing the plot twists and stuff.  The Ultimates, which is essentially the Avengers in the Ultimate Marvel universe/imprint, is fun to read but a bit over the top.  One after another someone is plotting to take over or destroy the world; it gets a bit predictable.  In The Ultimates 3, Jeph Loeb's writting kills the series...it literally blows chunks =X. 
😊 happy  |  ♫ Eagles - Hotel California
Link

Gut got anger after a gay ol' night

Mar. 23rd, 2010   7:56 PM
As I've mentioned last week, I went to a couple of drag shows for the first time with Anthony on Sunday night.  We hit up Woody's first, and briefly got to chat with Miss Conception (aka Kevydl) and Danididit.  I've only seen her dolled up in pictures, but I gotta say she looked really good in person!  Speaking of skewed conceptions, am I the only one incredibly amazed at how large that place is ~ it looks tiny on the outside but damn can it fit a ton of people inside.  After a little while we went to George's Play, which seemed to attract more younger folks.  Maybe it was just the acts that we watched but the drag there didn't seem to be as classy and instead more modern.  Later went to Zelda's, which didn't have any shows going on just by looking through the window, so we went back to Woody's.  Drank a little, watched some great performances, and got to know Anthony a little better ~ good times.  All in all, it was an entertaining experience that I'll probably only do on occasions since my gayness doesn't peak that flamboyantly XD. 

Before heading home that night I bought some pizza at Pizza Pizza (the one on Chuch and Wellesley) and that stuff really messed me up.  I doubt it could've been anything else, so if you don't want to catch food poisoning becareful with what you eat there ^^;;.  Possibly the worst 36 hours of my life >>"  Fever, chills, nausea, cramping, vomiting, diarrhea...a barrel of fun! \o.o/  I also chipped my top left incisor!  It happened after puking my guts out, but it's surprising since I wouldn't think the acid would do such damage TAT.  I don't think it's anything serious since it's a small loss, but I'll go see my dentist when I find the time.  Anyways, I won't go into any nasty details but I'm only now finding the energy to even sit in front of my computer.  It sucks how I've lost two days out of my life ~ I could have not been doing my taxes or homework in that time XP.  
😑 blah  |  ♫ Torley on Piano - Mellow Meadows
Link

Mar. 21st, 2010   6:25 PM

Link

Munch of good times!

Mar. 21st, 2010   11:02 AM
(It’s interesting that I’ve noticed how little I write about my ABDL/babyfur happenstances in comparison to my furry experiences.  The former is equally an important part of who I am and how I enjoy my life as is the latter, but I suspect that I raise a certain level of sensitivity in expressing each aspect due to being unsure of who reads what I write.  Far more often than not, I don’t include whether or not I am diapered during the occasional outings I have with friends ~ often the case I am, but find no relevance in mentioning it in my entries.  For example, I was padded when I was hanging out with Winter Tw Wolf, as well as Feathery/Hippotaur, and for practically all of Furnal Equinox.  Had I brought them up earlier, it would probably be an immediate turn off for some readers who are only interested in certain topics of my life.  I am a very open person online - and I write this blog mainly for myself - but a part of me feels like I’m not holding true to that due to being considerate for my audience.  For the most part, the only time I explicitly write about my ABDL/babyfur involvements is when I am among people enjoying the same activities ~ the lack of this writing, to me, seems cheapening with how engaged I truly am to it. 

Anyways, that was just a bit of rambling before going into my Saturday entry that relates to said topic.  But first…)

I spent the first half of the day with my parents; eating lunch, going to Yorkdale Mall, and helping with the groceries.  I saw a couple of things I probably could have bought while shopping, but just in general that mall is way too expensive for my taste.  There were some Oakley shades at Sunglass Hut that caught my attention ~ expensive as hell (+$200) but oh so smexy.  I wonder how long I can resist the thought of buying a; I can afford them, but it would probably mean skipping on a new camera or maybe going to a convention XD.

In the evening I went to the ageplay munch, which also happened to be the last time it would be held at the Groundhog Pub since they’re moving to a location closer to Wellesley station.  A bit of a shame since I really liked the Pub ~ great sitting environment and yummy food.  I guess we’ll need to find a new place for April and then possibly try the relocated restaurant when they open in May.  In total nine people showed up for the munch, which I think is a very ideal size when it comes to socializing from one end of table to the other.  Aside from the regulars – Dan, Shiro, Ricky, Matthew, and Marvin – were three from Ottawa (or was it Montreal? =S) that I’ve probably met in the past but forgotten.  It was definitely a really enjoyable dinner with great discussions among good friends ~ if anything, some of us were louder than usual in public about our diapers and ageplay fun.  But it didn’t really matter much since business wasn’t exactly booming and it’d be, maybe, two months until we’d see the restaurant staff again.  On that note, it’s a bit sad that no one up until today knew the name of our waitresses - the same one that served us month after month and usually knew what beer I’d order. 

At one point during the dinner a lady that no one had ever met – going by the name Seven – approached the group to invite us to the TLPD (Toronto Littles’ Play Date) Event (must be logged in to see).  Of course she was in the ‘know’ with what we were interested in; and looking at her FetLife page she’s into quite a lot of things!  She was very friendly and approachable, but some of us felt her Play Date Event came off a bit strong and straightforward on certain rules and etiquette.  Personally, I prefer ageplay gatherings that have a balance between fun activities and social engagement, or ones that are strictly of the latter.  Her event seems orientated around being your ‘little’ self only and having fun with others of that mindset.  That’s fine and all, but I only like it in moderations since I also enjoy thinking and talking about grown-up stuff while amongst other ABDLs.  I don’t want to raise any negative expectation without even going to her event yet, so I’ll probably go check it out with someone I know.  If I have a good time than win win!

After the munch it was decided that we’d all hang out at Marvin’s place, since his partner Max was out of town.  Prior to that, however, a few of us made a pit stop at Ricky’s place to get our diapers changed XD.  It was very generous of him to give me an ABU Cushies, which I’ll try on at some point, since they’re fairly expensive for their awesome looking design.  When we all made it to Marvin’s place we watched the Toronto Maple Leafs beat the Montreal Canadians in a shootout ~ kudos to them!  A lot of good laughs, two bottles of wine, and one joint later, those of us who were still there decided to go hang out at Matthew’s place next.  It was like bar hopping, but instead of a bar it was peoples’ homes and instead of lining up for the washroom you just went were you sat (LOL).  While over, we watched the Detroit Red Wings beat the Vancouver Canucks ~ the winning goal was incredible since it went in with only 0.4 seconds left of the game.  I also had more wine, which got me a bit tipsy and numb around the mouth XD.  I think my tolerance is going up since I’ve been drinking more frequently this past month; that isn’t really a good thing >>”.

Before calling it a night to catch the last train home, Matthew changed me into an Abena X-Plus with a stuffer and some baby lotion ~ very comfy! ^-^  It was also really sweet of him and Marvin to offer me a place to crash for the night, which I would have taken in a heartbeat had I not been living with family.  I doubt my parents would have appreciated not knowing why I didn’t come home for the night unexpectedly despite probably being able to sly by with legitimate reasons…*ahem* excuses.  I managed to catch the last northbound train, but my stupid brain made me get off at Eglinton station thinking that the Leslie bus would take me much closer and a lot quicker home than the York Mills bus.  Normally this would be true except for the fact that there’s never any Leslie buses running at two in the morning!  So I ditz’d around the station for twenty minutes before realizing it had been empty for a long time.  Again I had to take the super infrequent blue line buses and got home a little after 3am.  Thank gawd my bro is gone for the weekend cause I just collapsed in bed with my crinkles and sleeper.  I suspect today will be another late night out TAT.
🙂 okay  |  ♫ Lauryn Hill - I Find it Hard to Say (Rebel)
Link

Rest in peace Morgan...

Mar. 19th, 2010   2:06 PM


It's saddening to hear that this morning Michael "Morgan" Bard passed away after being taken off of life support decided upon by his parents the day before.  He died without pain as he was in a coma due to a brain aneurysm. 

I did not know him well; we knew each other by name but I seldom spoke with him at the various local furry gatherings.  But I considered him a friend and he was an important person to many people I know as well as the community.  It pains me to see them hurt knowing that he's no longer with us.  He was a gentle, trustworthy, respectable, enduring, and inspiring individual who will be deeply missed.  My sincere condolences and strength to his friends and family.

A video tribute - created by Dodge - to thank and recognize Morgan...
😞 depressed
Link

Live out loud

Mar. 18th, 2010   12:21 PM
Signs of a great night...holding down my liquor longer than an Irish XD. 

I had a lot of fun yesterday while at Winter Tw Wolf's place.  I got to meet his two roomates and awesome landlord ~ dude brews some pretty good beer in his basement!  We mostly just sat around the TV (further confirming my theory that 1 in 3 furry households have a smexy flat panel tv ><**) and chilled to some cartoons and video games, drank copious amounts of alcohol, passed the bong around, and had a lot of good laughs.  Oh and we cooked burgers outside at midnight to satisfy a completely random cravings ~ tasty XD. 

The only thing that sucked balls was getting home ~ the neighbourhood they're in is kinda sketch and I was worried about getting lost on the way to the subway station.  The walk felt like forever and it was unrewarding cause I literally missed the last train by seconds! >.o  The damn blue line buses are so infrequent and I didn't get home until 3:30am!  Ah well, it was worth it =3. 

==============================================

Threadless $10 sale is on!  I bought eight tees and can't wait to get them ~ it was so hard to pick and choose since I wanted twice as many ^^;;.  What would've costed me $144 dropped down to $80...win! \o.o/  The shipping is kind of weird, for the base price it would take 1-3 weeks, but tack on an extra buck and it'd take 5-10 days, throw in two bucks extra and it'd only take 2-5 days o__O. 

I got the Giraffe Slide, This Game Sucks, Nuts!, The Cheshire, Endless Forms Most Battleful, That Night We Talked About Our Dreams, Training, and Pillow Fight.

==============================================

In tennis news at the BNP Paribas Open...holy shit Marcos Baghdatis (33) beat Roger Federer (1) in the third round...and then losing to Tommy Robredo (27) in the fourth round *sad face*.  Starting today for the mens side are the quarterfinal matches (I haven't been following with the womens but they're already underway with the semifinals) and the match ups look pretty intense.  I don't know who to root for!  Robin Soderling (6) has been playing really impressively, but he'll be in tough competition against players like Andy Roddick (7), Andy Murray (4), and Rafael Nadal (3).  It should be some good watching ^-^.

==============================================

I'm having dinner with Jully tonight ~ haven't seen her in months so catching up should be pretty relaxing. 
😊 happy
Link

Cheers!

Mar. 17th, 2010   5:28 PM
Happy St. Patrick's Day!  I don't exactly know what this holiday is celebrating, but I've got my green on and at night I'll have my drink on with Winter Tw Wolf ^-^.  He's Irish too ~ maybe if I poke him I'll get lucky D:  Anywhoozle, drink responsibly for those who do, and have a good one ;)
😄 cheerful  |  ♫ Daniel Joseph - Orions Belt

Update and stuff...

Mar. 16th, 2010   11:48 PM
Sweet!  Threadless is celebrating their 10th anniversary this Thursday by lowering the prices of their tees to $10!  I've already got my eyes on a couple of them that are priced at amost double the amount ~ personally I'd never spend more than $12 for a silk screen tee =S.  I really like the indie/artsy clothing sites such as Shirt Fight and Shirt Woot! because they've got such creative designs XD.  I need to shop for more clothes as the weather keeps getting better and better!  Next on my list; jeans, sunglasses and hats <3

========================================================

It's a pretty good day in hip hop.  XXL Magazine "2010 Freshman Class" is out in print!  Honestly, I think last years line-up was better ~ there were more groundbreaking artists that left an impact in hip hop.  But as it stands, I think J. Cole and Fashawn are at the top of the class.

In preparation for his second album, 'Con TV', Consequence released his mixtape yesterday entitled 'Movies on Demand'.  It's free, download it and give it a listen, it's pretty good.  It features (or how he labels it 'co-starring') a bunch of really great artists: Asher Roth, Clinton Sparks, Common, Talib Kweli, Kanye West, P. Diddy, Kid Cudi, The Lox, John Legend, Maino, Pharoahe Monche, Q-Tip, Rick Ross, Spree Wilson, and Syles P.  If his album is as good as this mixtape than I'm looking forward to hearing it!

Lastly, 2 Hungry Bros released their latest mixtape, 'Back for Seconds' to promote their upcoming album 'No Room For Dessert'.  Again, it's free, download it and give it a listen if you're interested.  Quite refreshing and innovative ~ and as always, they've got a really awesome groove going on.  The mixtape features artists such as Homeboy Sandman, P. SO, Fresh Daily, Silent Knight, Substantial, Senor Kaos, 8thw1, and many more.  This is fun, wholesome, quality music right here - don't miss it!

========================================================

The ATP World Tour Masters 1000 BNP Paribas Open - a tennis tournament held in Indian Wells, California - is underway.  I watched the match between Andy Murray (4) and Michael Russell (68) this afternoon and it was pretty bad.  Murray was playing poorly and very inconsistently.  Hands down he was going to win, but he could have finished the match like thirty minutes sooner had he just concentrated more.  Russell put on a pretty good come back in the second set at 3-5, but other than that, it wasn't a good match to watch ~ I kinda feel cheated of my time >>".

It sucks that the sports channels here only air two hours of game time in the afternoon, especially since this is a pretty big tournament.  Certainly there are nine masters tournaments that each reward 1000 ATP points - compared the the four grand slam tournaments that rewarnd 2000 ATP points - but tournaments like these are can really propel a player up the rankings and limelight.  Basically, they're quite important and very much worth watching ~ stupid sports channels =.=**

========================================================

Oh shit!  I need to do my taxes and I have no idea how it's done! >.o  My parents are cutting me off this year, because normally I just leave it in the hands of their accountant.  I gotta figure out how this grown-up stuff is done; luckily it shouldn't be too difficult since I don't have an income XD lol. 

========================================================

Yay!  Drag shows! \o.o/  If things don't fall through this weekend I'll be going to one or two of them with Anthony =P.  It should be a gay ol' time, at least from the looks of it while browsing through the events listings on Fab Magazine. I don't really know what to expect, but I could use some liquor in me. 

Plus, the ageplay munch is this Saturday!  Yay!  More things to look forward to! :D

========================================================

Blah, okay tired of typing now.  I went outside and did my 6km jog in the afternoon, and not long ago came home from working on my upper body at the gym.  Sleep...goodnites.
😴 tired  |  ♫ Consequence (ft. Asher Roth) - Childness Games

Goosh Goosh...blah...

Mar. 12th, 2010   11:38 PM
My body was in a world of pain this morning! TAT  Yesterday for the first time in a week I worked out.  The weather in the afternoon was deceiving because it was crazy sunny but windy and chilly at the same time ~ it didn't make for an ideal jog.  But I'm quite happy to say I done did it!  If my math skillz are correct, I jogged 6km (or 3.72 miles) in a little over forty-five minutes; probably not the greatest but it's about what I average on the treadmill ^-^.  My route, which was rather hilly, went in a big square along York Mills, Lesile, Lawrence, and Don Mills.  Then at night I went to the gym and worked on my upper body ~ it always leaves me with a great feeling, so I guess the pain from the morning after was worth it .___."

==============================================================

Last month I didn't get much in the way of art commissions since I won an art-slave auction, got a partial fursuit, and spent a TON the month prior.  This month I've already spent quite a lot on at Furnal Equinox and next month will be the same with Furry Connection North...but I had to get a Mary Mouse badge! T__T Don't judge me! XD  I got the 6" cutout badge matching the theme of the con, Rocking' the 80s!  I don't know much about the 80s so I asked her to do whatever she wanted ~ I love her work so I trust it'll turn out awesome! ^-^  I'd reeeeally also like to get something from Dingbat, the other Guest of Honour, since her art is equally as amazing ~ but preferably some of her pottery commissions! O3O

==============================================================

I spent most of the today with Lance Feathery and his daddy Kevin Quarton - both whom I met at FE - mall crawling due to the crappy weather.  It was really awesome of them to come down from Barrie, especially since Kevin is going back to San Francisco on Sunday.  I only wish there were more things to do =.=**  We started the day with Second Cup, where I was introduced to 'Goosh Goosh' (NSFW!) ~ I didn't get around to watching the video until going to the Apple Store in Fairview Mall.  It has become a word engrained into my vocabulary due to its constant usage throughout the day and applicability in various situations XD.  Kevin treated me to food court lunch, which I'm not use to accepting since it's usually the other way around, so thanks very much!  The food at Caribbean Queen isn't bad and it's the first time I've had fried plantains =3. 

Afterwards we went to Scarborough Town Centre and spent a copious amount of time in the pet, toys, games, and book store.  There was this incredibly awesome blue bunny plushie that stood maybe more than half my height (perfect cuddling size) at Wal-Mart for under $20!  Do want! D:  Agh, the one thing I don't like about Easter is all the teeth rottingly cute plushies that I can't bring home >.o  Anywhoozle, we paid Dodge a visit while he was still on the clock at work ~ it was great seeing him again even for a short while.  We were all 'goosh goosh' and he probably didn't know what we were going off about XP.  Look it up...I'd love to know how people react!  I must've watched it a dozen times LOL.  We got ice cream at Laura Secord before heading off to Markville Mall.  We only went into Toys R Us and Best Buy before having dinner at East Side Mario's.  Agh!  I leave my seat to take a leak and Kevin grabbed my bill, that's such an Asian thing to do .____."  I feel so backwards and grateful ~ I really hope someday I can return the favour!  Thanks so much, you two were a LOT of fun to hang around even in the gloomy weather =3. 

==============================================================

*sigh*  Not long ago while typing this entry out I got a call from my parents.  My aunt Liana, uncle Viktor, and their one year old son got t-boned out on one of the major intersections near where we live (our families live less than ten minutes apart from one another).  They're in the hospital getting x-rayed right now, but luckily from the sounds of it no one was hurt - just a lot of bruises and stiff necks.  It's unfortunate and I really hope nothing serious comes out from this =(.
😊 happy  |  ♫ Chris Cornell - You Know My Name
Link

Con Report: Furnal Equinox 2010

Mar. 10th, 2010   8:14 PM
A friend of mine once told me that he first considered himself a part of the furry fandom after attending his first convention five years ago.  The processes of going to one, being there, meeting friends from online to real life, and having a very positive experience overall left a lasting sense that he was a part of something he really enjoyed.  I can relate to this feeling even though I’ve felt like a part of the fandom before attending my first con – but going to one further reinforces why I love the things I am interested in.

So before continue on from my entry about Friday, it’s definitely worth mentioning that I met a TON of people and I did a LOT of things – there is no way I remember everything.  I may remember what people look like but many of their names elude me, so apologies in advance!  Everyone and everything made my first con experience that much more incredible, so thank you as well! 

Saturday
I got about three hours of sleep before leaving the house by 6am since it takes over an hour to get to the con by transit; it was a brutal morning.  When I got there I assisted with setting up the Dealers’ Den as well as helped BushyCat get her table going.  It was nice chatting with some of the other dealers – like Xenotropos and Lady Serpent – even if it were for the briefest of moments.  I met up with Vinnie not long after that and unfortunately Andy couldn’t make it due to food poisoning ~ we suspect it was the burger he wolfed down at Perkins the other night.  Poor guy, it was his first con and he missed an entire day of it =(.  While waiting for the Opening Ceremonies to begin I wandered around a bit, ran into a bunch of people including those I was rooming with, and picked up a badge I pre-ordered from Familiar ^-^. 

After the Opening Ceremonies I moved my luggage out of Dodge and his roommates suite and into my own ~ sorry for the intrusion and thanks again =3.  I got in suit and took it out for the first time ever; it was exhilarating and so much fun!  You get those curious looks from people while making your way to the con space and then the playful interactions when finally there, it’s really awesome.  I went to the Rodents panel (hosted by MatthiasRat) with Ruff and Shiro; it was okay but not something that peaked my interest.  There were a couple of panels I wanted to check out (Intro to Fursuiting, Furries A to Z, and Head Building 101) but they either conflicted with something else or I got distracted and forgot about them.  Oh wells, maybe next time or something =P.  After wandering around some more I kicked back a bit in the Headless Lounge with other suiters.  The bong-looking head drier is so cool! O3O  I also found out that making lunch plans on the go is really difficult!  Vinnie and I were planning to eat at Harvey’s but as we were leaving the con space we kept running into people and inviting them to come with us.  Each time we did, they would need a couple minutes here and there to get ready or collect something from their rooms.  Hunger is the best spice I guess o.o” 



In the afternoon Dan Raccoon and I went back to our room and relaxed a bit ~ I was completely bushed.  We were later joined by Shiro and Seifer who came up to get in suit for the Furry Games, so I did the same.  Although the planning for it was a bit last minute, I thought they were pretty well coordinated and definitely a lot of fun (cheating was half the fun =P) ~ kudos to you Arinmal!  The team I was in won silver but I got a bronze instead due to confusion and mix up at the end XD.  Oh wells, it was still really awesome that everyone got to take home a spiffy prize that could be shown off.  Following that event I went for dinner with Hypr, Lance Feathery, and Kevin Quarton at Ginkos, a Japanese restaurant within the hotel.  We definitely got our moneys worth for the teppanyaki, the chef put on a firey show and the food was excellent ~ Lance was having a foodgasm by the end of our meal XD. 



At night I went to the Condition room party and had some slurm, I think it was a green apple/vodka mix =P.  There were a lot of people I didn’t know as well as a couple I did know like Rozz, Dodge, Ashley, Kitty, and Sammy Stripes.  I am soooo going to London con, Condition: Zero Hour, just for the acrylic conbadges ~ they look freakin’ awesome!  Afterwards I went and checked out the What The Fur room party and had some poutine ^-^.  Again, I didn’t really know anyone besides Feli and Firebreath whom I met the day before, but whatever, it was still fun and yummy to hang out at.  I went down to the dance in suit a little later and had a sweaty good time with Rozz, Dodge, Ashley, Lance, HappyFoxNick, and probably a good number of others I can’t recall.  I wrapped up the night sometime after midnight and went back to my suite with BushyCat, who was in a cat kigurumi =D.  From then until 2am we were up with Shiro, Seifer, and Xaxoqual just joking around and poking fun at each other.  I got pounced for pinching BushyCat with glowsticks TAT. 

Sunday
I’m not exactly sure why I got up so early, but I was down in the con space by about 7am ~_~”.  It was a really relaxing day none the less; I mostly hung out in the Video Games room with Dan Raccoon, Ruff, Twicks, Vinnie, Andy (who was feeling much better and was in a Mario outfit XD), Ralph Lupus, and a couple others.  I got pwn’d in Left 4 Dead, Street Fighter 4, and Guitar Hero 5.  After lunch I helped out with Ki-Wind for most of the Guitar Hero tournament before rushing to get in suit for the Fursuit Parade.  Holy crap were there a lot more suiters than many of us would have expected for such a small con!  I think they counted 40 in the parade, which probably didn’t include a couple who were busy doing other things.  The parade and pictures afterwards were definitely something I really enjoyed mainly because of the ambiance with having nearly everyone involved in someway or another.

                           
Pic taken by Andy, stolen from Vinnie's FA.            Pic taken by Arinmal, stolen from FireStormSix's FA.

I wandered around and hung out with various groups of people before retiring back to my room.  Apparently the descriptions for the adult movies on TV are pretty hilarious o__O  But you could probably use the hotels WiFi to download them instead of paying =P.  Anywhoozle, I rested up a little and then joined Shiro, Seifer, Dan, Orca, Firebreath, and Soba for dinner at Mandarin.  I haven’t had buffet in a long while so I definitely got my fills worth and surprisingly some of their items were really good!  I’m kinda picky when it comes to white-washed Asian foods heh, but I had a really good time ^-^.  I am a bit bummed that I missed the Closing Ceremonies because of dinner though.  I missed the auctioning of the last conbook – signed by the con staff and important peoples – that sold for nearly $200, all going to a good cause might I add!  And I missed HappyFoxNick wearing one of Kitty’s fursuits of a dog – the one Bluey was suppose to pick up. 

After I had let my food settle in, I went to the Dead Dog Dance and had some more sweaty good fun with Dan, Crassadon, and a few others during Pawz’s set.  The turn out was way smaller since most people had to return to their normal day-to-day the morning after.  I was the only one in suit for most of it, but was later joined by BushyCat in her tuxedo cat suit.  I called it a night at around 1:30am and watched the crowd fizzle out with Kinny and MAnlantrio.  The Russian furs were having a drinking party in the room next to mine but I opted not to go even though I knew some of those who were going ^-^;;  When I went up to shower and sleep, my roomies informed me that BushyCat was looking for me so my night hadn’t ended just yet .___.”  She invited me over and I kept her company until about 3:30am while she packed her things ~ I swear she must have had enough stuff for six people!  She offered me some sleep space since I was so tuckered and was already rooming with four others, so I took up her offer.  Relax folks, there were two beds in her suite!  Plus, she was pretty relentless with slinging elastic bands at me T__T. 

Monday
I only got a couple hours of sleep before heading down to the con space at 7:30am to help clean up.  It was a pretty slow morning to say the least.  Everything was in full swing by noon though and there were lots of hugs and goodbyes to go around in the processes.  The day went similar to Friday but in reverse ~ Aurora Kitsune, Kane, and I brought mostly everything to the loading bay and waiting for the drivers to pick up.  By mid-afternoon everything and everyone was gone except for Crassadon, Taridium, and myself.  While waiting for his flight, Tari was telling me about how he’s been messing with BushyCat for the last four years.  Each con he’s met her at, she would never remembers who he was.  So, each time Tari has been giving her different identities and information XD.  I’ll probably do the same because I’m almost certain she won’t remember me even in a months time when I see her at Furry Connection North. 

At around 5pm, Arinmal came by to pick up Crassadon and Kimba’s sound equipment, which would have been the second last load.  But plans had changed and Blindsight decided not to keep the wooden walls used for the Headless Lounge in order to save time and labour.  A bit of a shame since the materials cost a couple hundred bucks and Arinmal could’ve used them even if they wouldn’t be needed for next year.  The issue was the timing since if it had been the last load he wouldn’t have gotten home till 2am! D:  So we left that behind and headed for Crass’s place followed by Kimba’s.  Before dropping me off home, I treated Arinmal to dinner at Tako Sushi ~ now that I think about it I’ve taken a LOT of people there! XD  It was a really good meal but his company was definitely the icing on the cake.  He is such an awesome person and he made helping out with the con that much more enjoyable for me!  He totally deserves a lot of credit for all his work throughout the con.  Thanks a lot!

In sum, my first fur con was an unforgettable experience.  I really liked how everyone who helped did it for the con and not for themselves; the teamwork was so tight.  The con staff really deserves an applaud ~ Blindsight and Pakesh De were constantly on their feet, Twitchy and Shane led the volunteers very well, Arrowroot handled everything art related immaculately, Silvax and Ki-Wind manned the registration table (which couldn’t have been fun at all), and just a ton of other people that deserve recognition.  The security team, led by Arctic, did an amazing job handling certain situations that I know of but won’t mention ~ issues got resolved quickly and efficiently.  I loved how the con space was small enough that you could run into friends multiple times in a day or meet new people much easily.  Next year will definitely require more space since an astonishing 330 people registered this year!  Lastly, I found it incredible how there was no (or I wasn’t aware of any) judgement or discrimination towards babyfurs.  I counted at least twelve crinkly furs, so our presence was there! XD  There was definitely a lot of love going around at the con and hugs were very much contagious <3. 

There were only a couple of things I enjoyed less at the con, which were mainly due to my own blunder.  I didn’t take much time going from dealer to dealer ~ I would have loved going through what was for sale and possibly getting some commissions.  I’m not really the type of person to buy prints (even though I bought some cute cubby ones from BushyCat), but I do enjoy supporting smaller artists.  I wish I got something from Heather Bruton!  There was just too much things to do and people to see ~ which is quite the opposite with AnimeNorth and FanExpo where I mostly hangout in the dealers area XD.  I never stepped foot in the Zoo, whether to socialize or play tabletop games.  It would have been fun to learn a new game, but most of them seemed super specific and intimidating for noobs.  I don’t like furry time…period.  But we’re furries so one can’t expect much of that to change unfortunately =\.  And lastly, I’m kicking myself in the face for misplacing my sponsor tee while packing my luggage on Monday ~ hopefully I can buy a size medium if they have one left T_T.   



Oh yeah, I was cleaning out my wallet and looking through all my receipts…holy crap did I spend a lot on food! XD  In total, Friday through Sunday came to approximately $320, which includes hotel, registration, food, art, transportation, etc.  I blame the three Asian restaurants I dined at ~ we wouldn’t give you a bargain for the world XP.

TL;DR ~ The con was terrible and everyone farted very loudly.  I'm lying so you should read everything after the cut!  >:3  I never provide summaries. 
😄 cheerful  |  ♫ The Dead Weather - New Pony
Link

XD  Con report is still in progress...my procrastination hasn't begun just yet ^^;;  Rather, I just wok up not long ago heh.

FE report...tomorrow...

Mar. 8th, 2010   10:56 PM
Furnal Equinox was an amazing convention.  The efforts put forth by everyone who made it possible - whether their contributions were big or small - is highly commendable.  I am incredibly tired since I didn't leave the hotel until sometime after five and didn't get home until sometime before ten.  A much thoroughly written recollection will be posted tomorrow depending on how much I actually remember.

Pre-con labour and leisure...

Mar. 6th, 2010   1:45 AM
Furnal Equinox, my first furcon, hasn't even started yet and it's pooped me out T___T.  At least I met a ton of people in the process and had quite a bit of fun! 

I spent a lot of time in traffic with Arinmal going to and from the hotel ~ it was wicked getting to know him since he's such an interesting and fairly accomplished fellow.  We did a LOT of loading and unloading TV's, speakers, bulletin boards, boxes of various supplies, and other random equipments, all around the GTA.  Between stops I got to hang out in the staff suite where I met members of the committee from FE and WTF ~ such as MAnlantrio, Feli, Strongpaw, Firebreath, and Arctic.  I feel quite privileged knowing most of this con's staff - having met them from pervious furmeets and dinners - especially since I've only been active in the local scene for a handful of months.  It makes walking into a room full of them that much more chill when you can joke around without anyone getting butthurt. 

I'm rather bummed out that I couldn't make it on time to hang out and watch a movie with the B-town furs ~ I was halfway across town loading up Kimba's stereos and plushies when 6pm rolled around XD.  On the flip side, I stayed at the con for most of the evening and hung out with some new and old friends.  I got to meet BushyCat - one of the cons guest of honours - and had her sign the back cover of my conbook since her signature wasn't on the print of her artwork.  She so easy going and fun to be around; plus she gets mushy over Asians \o.o/  Win!  Later on BushyCat, Peromyscus, Vinnie, Andy, Crassadon, and I went to the nearby Perkins for dinner ~ good times.  There were still quite a handful of people in the con space setting up equipment and just hanging out, including two suiters, when I left for home.  It kinda sucks that it takes so long to get to and from by public transit =S  I probably could have stayed the night at BushyCat's suite fairly easily since she offered a few times, but there were still a few things I needed to bring to the con from home. 

Blah, so tired now, but at least I had a great feel of how the con will be like, as well as scope out the inner workings.  There is a LOT of work involved, I kid you not, so huge props to everyone involved ~ and even bigger kuddos if it's a major success!  Regardless though, they have my full support and I'm aiming to get to the hotel bright and early tomorrow to help out some more ^^.  It's going to be a great weekend!
💤 sleepy  |  ♫ Sia - Buttons
Link

O hai thar!

Mar. 4th, 2010   11:48 PM


As you might have guessed, my partial fursuit came in the mail today! ^-^  It is very furry...almost untame-ishly so XD.  I'll have to see to it that it gets a little trimming sometime.  But other than that, it's pretty damn spiffy =3.  The visibility is good but it gets really hot after a short while.  I don't blame the ventilation since the mouth and eyes are porous, but it might just be the thickness of the material itself.  I'd hate to wonder how foam framed suiters would feel ~ mine is mesh with minimal foam o.o"

   
 

I've noticed that it has its angles...I guess my shorty-ness comes in handy this time =.=**  It'll make its debut this weekend at Furnal Equinox!  I'm both excited and nervous D:  To some extent I don't really know what to expect, but a part of me thinks the experience will feel like the anime/sci-fi conventions I've gone to - just smaller.  It terms of going about the con, I'm not really sure who I'll be hanging out with since there seems to be dichotomy of groups ~ for example the babyfur/cubs or the local furs I've met at various furmeets.  I guess that it's good to have prior preperations in place so that there will be things to look forward to - such as dinner at Ginko on Saturday with Hypr and a few others.  Speaking of Saturday, I'm missing an ABDL party at Dan & Kevy's (T___T) ~ I need to get drunk that night to make up for it >.o

Anywhoozle, I'm sure I'll have my thoughts and feelings about what to expect sorted out by tomorrow.  On Friday I'll be helping with loading things in vehicles and unloading them at the con ~ even though I do weight training for jazzy looking arms I gotta put them to good use sometimes! XD  I don't look forward to waking up way early >>".  In the evening, if the volunteer work wraps up on time, I'll be hanging out with the B-town furs to watch 'Alice in Wonderland'.  We're going to the theater by Square One in Mississauga; I haven't been to that mall in at least six or seven years o__O.  It should be a lot of fun, but I'll probably be getting home late which is a bummer since I'll be leaving the house early on Saturday.

Super secret commission! O3O

Mar. 3rd, 2010   3:22 AM
Okay it isn't really a secret if you know where you look, but I have been pretty secretive about it since I haven't told anyone! 

I commissioned a partial fursuit!  Well...more partial than the usual partials since the maker wasn't comfortable with his abilities to do feetpaws.  Oh wells, I've got the head, handpaws and tail =3


Lemme just make it clear that this picture really doesn't do the head justice because it's on a mannequin with a smaller head; making some parts curved inwards.  That's why there's also a really awesome video you have to watch! O3O


(Close up starts at 2:15.)

I won't lie...I feel uncomfortable knowing that I paid a fraction of what other fursuit makers charge for a head.  For example, at Azure Coyote Studios heads start at $500 - which seems to be the rough norm - while at Kilcodo Costumes heads range from $300-450.  The head I got - from Muddle Designs' - only cost what the materials did because the head was built as a portfolio piece and to gain some rep.  That being said...I paid $65 for both the head and paws! XD  Hellz, the shipping cost more than the suit because I'm having it FedEx'd on a two-day delivery so that, hopefully, it'll arrive before Furnal Equinox.

And yes, of course there are some differences with how a professionally made head looks compares to an amature one - perhaps it's the polish, design, material or general style - but honestly the huge price gap compared to the small quality gap is wack!  I can't complain one bit =X.  The only thing I might touch up with my head is the length of the fur - I might get someone to help trim it shorter just a little ^-^.

Mad thanks to the maker ~   (his FA) and his art/fursuit commissioning business Muddle Designs' (the FA). ~ I am way happy and can't wait until it arrives! :D
🐇 bouncy  |  ♫ The Stroke - Take It or Leave It
