Stealing my brain juices! OAO

Mar. 2nd, 2010   11:20 PM
Today I participated in a psychology experiment.  They hooked me up to an electroencephalogram and made me gamble! O3O

 

Okay...it was actually waaaay less exciting and interesting than that >>".  The experiment was on "Problem Gambling Symptoms, Decision Making Soundness and Physiological Reactivity".  I had to answer three to four hundred question - most of them seemed off topic - as well as play two computer games that were soooo lame.  It took two hours but at least I got compensated with 3% credits towards the current course I'm taking XP.


*smilie face*  Win $55!  \o.o/  Yay!  The green bar at the top is your 'Cash Pile' and the red bar is how much you 'Borrowed'.  In the end I didn't break even ~ I owed a couple hundred! o.o"

Procrastinating on ways to procrastinate...

Feb. 28th, 2010   9:53 PM

It's been an uber relaxing weekend since finishing my midterm on Friday and I'm way excited about the coming weekend ~ six more days until Furnal Equinox! ^-^

The midterm was a bit trickier than I led myself to believe coming into it, but at least now I know the profs style.  It just sorta sucks that each exam is worth half my grade so it's do-or-die =.="  I don't need to pass with flying colours - I've always been an average student anyways - I just need the bloody credit so I can graduate!  Anyways, I won't need to stress about it until the end of April =P.  Plus it was quite a nice surprise coming out of the exam and bumping into a friend I haven't seen in at least three years ~ it's reassuring to know others who've stayed back a year.  Afterwards I hung out with Dodge and we mall crawled around Pacific Mall/Market Village.  I haven't been in either malls for at least a year mainly just because there isn't anything I need to buy from there, but there's always so much stuff to look at o__o  (and I don't just mean the merchandise, some of the people are pretty eccentric). 

For the rest of the weekend I pretty much stayed at home, played a lot of TF2 and relaxed ~ possibly had the best sleep in a long while.  I watched the men's hockey finals a couple of hours ago and CANADA BEAT USA FOR GOLD!! \o.o/  It wasn't the greatest match, but it had enough exciting moments to suffice.  We were in the lead for most of the game until the third period ~ it was 2-1 and in the last twenty-five seconds the US scored a goal taking it to overtime!  The main issue with our team is that we lose a lot of steam and play more defensive as the match progresses; evident in the last three games.  In overtime Sidney Crosby scored the winning goal to give us the win - I'm pretty sure it was a fluke since Ryan Miller has consistently stopped the type of goals that he let in.  Oh wells, it's quite incredible that Crosby is my age and he's won a Stanley Cup, an Olympic gold medal, and rakes in a couple million a year XD.  Bravo to all the athletes, its been an amazing games to watch ^-^.

Aight, time to hit the gym!  Peace! =3

😄 cheerful  |  ♫ Electric Light Orchestra - Mr. Blue Sky

Planning...go!

Feb. 24th, 2010   2:16 AM
Agh...I was on a really good natural high, like half an hour ago, and then my brother killed it with his constant bitching >>"  It sucks balls...I was feeling so awesome after coming home from the gym!  ><**  I ripped the treadmill a new one ~ one hour, 700 calories, 5.07 miles!  The only thing I don't like is that everytime I extend the cardio portion of my workout it leaves me pretty drained going into my upper body exercises.  Plus it's so time consuming - especially if I have a late dinner - I usually don't get home until around 11pm =S.  I might need to switch up my routine but I'd definitely like to keep my schedule at three times a week.

Other than what I did at night, most of my day was spent study.  I watched two lectures in the morning and then shaved off a chapter in the afternoon at the ol' coffee shop.  I'm all caught up with the readings for the mid-term but I've still got about four or five hours of lectures to watch ~ it shouldn't be too difficult.  And then I'll spend the rest of the time studying for Friday :D  Lets hope my plan doesn't fail; my favourite pastime is procrastinating after all heh.  I need an incentive!  o.o  Anyone wanna do something Friday night or anytime Saturday?  XD

Last on the things I want to write about is conventions!  Time to pop that furcon cherry! XD  So, I'm definitely going to Furnal Equinox which happens in less than two weeks ~ gonna be rooming with Shiro, Xaxoqual, and Dan_Raccoon to my understanding.  I just wish I had some clue as to what I'll be doing volunteer-wise, cause if set up is happening before 10am than I'm gonna need to leave my luggage somewhere (since check-in is at 3pm).  Plus, if I know what I'll be helping out with I'll have a better idea with how I can plan my activities during the con ~ the Condition and WTF room parties sound really fun and a few of the panels seem interesting.  

I'm also going to Furry Connection North which happens April 9-11th in Michigan!  I pre-registered not long ago.  Worked out a rideshare where Diesel will be coming down from Sudbury to pick up Danny Dingo, Hypr, and myself ~ I'll be rooming with the first two furs ^^.  I won't lie...I'm a little nervous about jumping into a car where the driver is two or three years younger than me (please don't take any offense if you're reading this ^^;)  It's something I'm very unaccustomed to...but then again, I feel awkward about girls driving me around (oh gawd now I'm sexist! D:<)  I'm sure I'll get over my nervousness, we've been getting to know one another these past few days and he's pretty cool =3.

The last con I have on paper that I'll be attending is Anthrocon, which happens June 24-27 in Pennsylvania!  This is the big one ~ I'm pretty stoked! XD  I've already pre-registered and I've worked out a roomshare with Danruk and three other furs who I'm not really sure are ^^;.  The rideshare is in the pipeworks due to some unforeseen events.  What'll probably end up happening is that Hypr will rent a car and I'll be driving it o.o"  We haven't sorted out all the implications but if it comes down to that it'll be my first time driving a bunch of furballs across the border ~ exciting and scary! D:

Anywhoozle, there are of course other cons I'd also like to attend; Condition: Zero Hour, What The Fur?, Feral!, FA: United 3, and Midwest Furfest, but making plans for them can wait until after FE.  If fact, I find it pretty damn funny that I'm saying I'll be going to x, y, z con and yet I haven't told my parents about any of this!  XD  One swift 'no' and it's all over! o__O

What's been happening...

Feb. 22nd, 2010   12:12 AM
One more week to go before the month ends! D:  At least I haven't been wasting much of my time doing nothing.  Its been an especially good week since I've had the room to myself for most of it, my brother was skiing in Montreal for five days ^-^.  I went to the local coffee shop twice to study and shaved off at least a hundred pages from my textbook ~ one more chapter until I'm caught up for the midterm!  I also went to the gym three times to workout.  I'm quite happy that I've been progressively increasing my stamina since the start of the month ~ I'm averaging at least forty-five minutes on the treadmill; with the settings I use that translates to around 540 calories and 3.89 miles :D 

I had a fun time at the ABDL munch Saturday night ^-^.  The turn out was really small though; Anthony, Matthew, a new fellow, and the B-town furs.  I hope the new guy comes again next month because I'm positive there'll be more people!  Kimba called me out the night before so I was sorta expecting to see him at the munch, but he didn't show till pretty late XD.  Anywhoo, afterwards us furs headed over to his place to hangout ~ I've been invited over a couple of times but was never able to go, so it was nice to finally be there!  Oh my gosh it was like walking into the most awesome zoo ever! XD  I don't know how else to describe a household of seven to eight furs, where three or four of them are cubs :P  To bad I didn't get introduced to most of them let alone remember the ones I did get to meet - except for mimiBadger since we've already met.  It was great getting to know Kimba a little better; I swear he's got the coolest accent and personality ever! o.o  He tried to pass off as a 19 year old while going through the subway ticket booth ~ lulz!  He also showed me his massive plushie collection, which literally took up half his room...it scares me that he's got more in storage XD.  I would definitely love to hang out with him and his housemates again sometime, it's bonus that I live fifteen minutes away by bus :D

Monday I need to go to school in the afternoon to get my grad photo taken.  It sucks how it'll only take ten minutes but will cost me $25 for a sitting fee, not including transit fare, travel time, and the crappy weather >>".  Oh wells, it needs to be done heh.  Afterwards I'll be hanging out with Dodge so that's something to look forward to :D  Studying?  Sure...I'll find time for that.  Midterm exam is this Friday, blah =.=*

T____T

Feb. 19th, 2010   5:28 AM
I wish I could say right now that I've woken early to begin a productive day, but really I haven't gotten so much of an hours sleep and I feel l crude =.=**  I don't understand why I can't fall asleep.  I doubt it could be the two cups of coffee I had yesterday while study in the afternoon since I had a really great workout and wore myself out at night.  I even went to bed earlier than usual =(.  There are several things on my mind, but most of which aren't as pressing as I make myself believe they are.  So saying that I'm stressed feels more like an excuse than a reason since most of the time my days are mind-numbingly boring.  *sigh*  I just want to sleep so badly right now.

Glee!

Feb. 18th, 2010   8:20 PM
Just finished watching the first season of Glee...sad how it only took me a few days =.=**  I'm not sure if I hated the song they ended it with or that they just ended it with a song to begin with ~ it's really corny even though they've ended each episode with a song for a while.  There wasn't any feeling of a cliff hanger (even though we all know what 'general' plot of the second season will be about) but rather a lot of resolutions.  I guess I just prefer shows where the drama leads up to something epic only to have wait to find out what it is next season ~ sorta like House and Fringe.  In fact, I don't really care much about the romantic/preggy drama in Glee at all.  Most of the time it just seemed to easy, like they pulled cast member names out of a hat and slapped them together only to dissolve it by the end of an episode.  I really loved Sue Sylvester though ~ she is one hilarious bitch!  XD  So I'll look forward to season two just for her =P.

Show tunes...

Feb. 16th, 2010   11:28 PM
...I'm loving it.  I blame K-dawg for showing me a clip of the movie 'Connie and Carla' last week ~ I ended up downloading it and watching it the other day.  It's about drag queens and musical theater; the plot and acting were lame but the music was really good.  This sudden interest has got me watching the tv series 'Glee', I've streamed a couple of episodes already...I feel so dirty for enjoying what I've watched so far! XD 

The next step is the watch it live! :D  I'll probably pass on going to an actual musical cause they're expensive and I don't have the attention span, but I've always been curious about going to a drag show.  I know of two local ABDL's that are drag queens who do shows, but I'd much rather go watch them with someone in the know rather than by myself .____."

Anywhoo, I have this one song stuck in my head ~ 'Maybe This Time' from the musical 'Cabaret'.  I first heard it from 'Connie and Carla' and then I looked up the version from the film adaptation of 'Cabaret', and then I found out 'Glee' also does a version of it XD.  Videos from each under the cut, I hope they get stuck in your head as well! >:D

Kiss and blog #7

Feb. 14th, 2010   4:58 PM
(continuing from last post)

The thing I wasn't happy about doing last night was making out with Diaper_Police_TO in front of a bunch of people >>".  I was pretty tipsy but I could have handled things better.  But to my defence, he was also all over me with the lips, tongue and groping ~ not that I totally minded if it were in private.  I had to tell him to behave somewhat while we were in the presence of others.  Not long before leaving we went to the private changing room and he changed me while totally feeling me up.  Again, I was pretty drunk so I know I was flobbery everywhere heh, it's a good thing he's tall and strong.  At one point he got my cock out from the side of my diaper and started sucking on it, even asking if I needed to pee (in his mouth).  I pumped his face but it was difficult to get completely hard while drunk.  The one thing I didn't really like was the kissing and major tonguing ~ it feels pretty weird especially with his facial hair XD.

Also, while at Marvin and Max's place, it was my first time sleeping with two other guys.  Not the most pleasent experience in my opinion because I couldn't get any solid sleep.  I was either feeling and kissing one of them, or they were snoring really loud.  Plus, Max gets really sweaty which is kinda gross.  he also likes to bite which is meh in my books ~ but he's a really fun and outgoing person.  I really enjoy cuddling with Marvin, he's so loveable and hairy ~ the type of guy I'd like to suck off heh.  I kinda wish we could have gone further than just diaper masturbation.  I'd have really liked to jerk them off without having to play favourites XP.  It was pretty fun sharing a single pacifier between the three of us and snuggling them in my arms ~ it just really sucked when my arm fell asleep while under Marvin's head, and I didn't want to wake him ><.

(For my own reference:  The people at the party ~ Dan/Kevy, Matt/Brad, Jerry/Drew, Marvin, Neb, weird looking Ryan, Murray, Dylan, Diaper_Police_TO, Ricky, and Matthew.)

What's in this drawer? O3O

Feb. 14th, 2010   4:40 PM
Last night was Matthew's ABDL party at his recently furnished (complete with a $100 diaper pail!) apartment ~ it was so much fun! ^-^  The space wasn't very big but it managed to hold fifteen of us, all of whom I've met from previous munches/parties.  It was really great seeing those that don't come out as often due to travel distance :D  This time I came prepared too!  I brought along my onesie, sleeper, a couple of beers and some Molicare; all of which were put to good use.  As per usual, other than the good laughs and tasty nibbles (wasabi peas! O3O) was the drinking and smoking up =P.  Yay for moderation!  I didn't get baked as bad as last month, but I was definitely way tipsy by the end of the party.  There were one or two things I'm not happy about doing, so I'll definitely keep that in mind and try to save face for next time >>".  At one point during the night I was reminded (again) how little I know about celebrities and musicians from the past few decades XD.  We were labelling what was considered gay and straight songs (our token straight guy could attest to it) ~ I'm blaming my lack of gay-ness and age for being so uncultured heh. Mostly everyone began leaving a little before 2am.  I had already missed the last train and the shuttle buses are pretty crappy, so I stayed over at Marvin and Max's place with Nebulus.  Cozy little place they have ^-^.  It seems as though everyone has a smexy flat screen tv ~ I'm jealous XP.  It's really heartbreaking though when there isn't any cable; *cough**Matthew and Dodge**cough*.  Anywhoo, we hit the hay by around 3am and I don't think I got many moments of shut eye just because it was so accommodating being between two guys ^///^.  In the morning I woke to my onesie and socks covered in red fuzzies from their blanket; there was even some on my diaper o.o"  Kick started the day by sharing a joint and then headed home sometime around 11am.  As always, thanks for the driving me Neb ~ you're awesome! :D  A week from now is the monthly munch and the following month is another gathering, if I recall correctly.  Lather, rinse, and repeat? XD
🐇 bouncy  |  ♫ Sondre Lerche - Pioneer

Let the games begin...

Feb. 12th, 2010   10:14 PM
Hopefully everyone is aware that today is the start of the 2010 Winter Olypmics held in Vancouver, Canada.  I'm more of a summer person so there isn't a lot of sports I enjoy during the winter.  There are a few sports I will watch more than others - such as hockey and figure skating - but for the most part I'll be sticking with the highlights and morning paper.  I'll definitely be rooting for Canada of course, but it'd also be pretty wicked if China won some shines ^^.   Silver would be awesome!  Gold would be incredible!  Snow would be a miracle!  The beginning of the Olympics kicks off a long weekend as well, in Ontario at least.  Monday is Family Day, while Sunday is Valentine's Day and Chinese New Year! ^-^  What a festivas time of year ~ I hope everyone enjoys it one way or another :D [Edit: Feb 13, 2010, 00:08] Just about finished watching the last hour of the opening ceremony.  Talk about cauldron fail!  How embarrassing ><*  A lot of people are Twittering about so-and-so fail =P.  I can't believe the CTV announcer would bring it up ~ he should've kept it hush hush to save outselves the stupidity.  Blah.  And it was really sad that Nodar Kumaritashvili died before the games, but also incredible that millions of people watched and stood in silence in rememberance for him.

XD  Need I say more?  Heh, it was my first time and it really wasn't as bad as I thought it would be ~ it really didn't require much though prior to doing it.  I've wanted to suck K-dawg's dick (his in particular) for a while but the opportunities never really seemed good enough in the past.  Like, if there's baby powder or oil or he's got a wet diaper I wouldn't suck him ~ I'm picky like that!  But last night while jerking him off while my head on his chest I just went in and sucked it, I think he enjoyed it heh, but he didn't pump in my mouth which would have been hot.  I think my next big thing to try is sucking and swallowing cum.   Another awesome thing was that I blew my load (after some effort), which rarely happens.  At first we were in a spooning position and we were just grinding, but my right calf muscle cramped and I had to stand up ~ it totally ruined my hard-on which took a while to bone up.  (My leg is still in a bit of pain too which is really odd since its been all night)  Later we were watching a diaper video and I mentioned one of the positions I really liked, so he did it for me.  He put his diapered crotch right up on my face and grinded and I jerked myself off to coming.  Freakin' awesome!   Throughout the night I was in a Molicare and later changed into a Kendall, while K-dawg was in a Kendall later doubled with a Depends cause his butt blew a HUGE hole (lol).  I find that he can be such a huge tease in a good way...most of the time.  Like when I want to just cuddle up or lay on top of him he'll tickle me.  When he seriously wants to get me hard I tend to return the favour and tease about everything on him ~ I tell it like it is sometimes and it's really hilarious to me.

Good good...

Feb. 9th, 2010   3:12 PM
My day is proving to be a pretty good one ^-^.   I woke earlier than usual and got a haircut at a place that does it really cheap ~ only $7 compared to the place I went the last couple of times that charged more than double >.<*  Afterwards I went to the gym and had a really great workout, felt so good just to move my body having not gone in a week because I was sick.   I'm feeling a lot better now but I've still got a few days to go before feeling 100%.  I should be a-ok by the weekend and then I'll restart the cycle all over again by getting tipsy and freezing my butt outside XD.  Right now I'm at the local Second Cup doing some reading for school ~ and typing this on my iTouch while bummimg their free wi-fi.  In a couple of hours I'll be meeting up with K-dawg for dinner, crinkled and cuddles ^-^.

Music, movies, Formspring, LULZ, and Cushies...

Feb. 8th, 2010   12:28 PM
B.o.B's latest mixtape - May 25th - came out last week and it's awesome ~ definitely a rising artist to look forward to (when his actual album drops in May).  In the past few years he's been very diverse in his music, from strong songs to radio tunes, to alternative hip hop.  This mixtape features J. Cole, Playboy Tre, Bruno Mars, and fellow XXL Magazine freshmen Charles Hamilton and Asher Roth. 

Definitely one of my favourite tracks: B.o.B (ft. Asher Roth)(prod. Kanye West) - Fuck the Money


Another album I'm looking forward to is LP4 by Ratatat, which comes out next weekend.  They're an indie electronics instrumental duo - not something for everyone - who've had their hands in the hip hop circle for a while.  Their latest feature was in KiD CuDi's album with two tracks, 'Alive' and 'Pursuit of Happiness'.  Two of their six albums were entirely remixes of hip hop songs (such as 'Raekwon - Smith Bros.' or 'Jay-Z & Notorious B.I.G - Allure'). 

============================================

The last little while I've been watching and rewatching a lot of movies.  Just off the top of my head: The Cove, Click, Star Trek, The Princess and the Frog, Daytime Drinking, Talladega Nights, Anchorman, Babel, Groundhog Day and possibly a few others. 

The Cove is a documentary about dolphin killing in Japan ~ quite a powerful and informative film.  Daytime Drinking is a Korean movie about a heart-broke man getting himself in a depressing travel experience and drowning out his sorrows with alcohol ~ excellent movie, but it made me want to drink >>".  I think I want to watch more Disney movies after seeing The Princess and the Frog o.o

============================================

Formspring.me seems to be the it thing that a lot of people on FA are jumping at ~ yummydumpling.  (T___T all the cool dumpling names were already taken lol)  Feel free to ask me whatever anonymously or as yourself ^-^.

============================================

Double LULZ!
           
For Furientals (art by Gel)                           For myself (art by Dar Wolverine)(scan fudged the colours in the eye)

I also receieved Bae Bunny's Valentines present in the mail this morning ~ so seet of him ^-^.  It was a card with a bit of poetry in it as well as two pictures of my fursona; one in the form of a badge while the other drawn in the card itself.  And it all smeels like cologne! XD  I'll try to get a scan of those when I get the chance =3.

       

============================================

The new Cushies diapers from ABU look way adorable o.o  I've never warn any of their products before, but I've heard pretty good reviews about them ~ they're out of my price range though.  Maybe when they get enough sales and lower their prices I'll take a stab at them =P.  Riley Kilo does a great review of them while looking cute in them ^///^, plus her blog is really awesome.

Not like it's important...

Feb. 4th, 2010   11:08 PM
The things I write that don't get posted make me feel uneasy about why I bother with a blog to begin with.  There are things on my mind that I would like to clear out, but a growing sense of self-consciousness creeps over me.  I don't want to be judged equally as much as others don't want to be talked about concerning certain topics, regardless of how indifferent they may be.  The liberty to write my thoughts without friends being aware - while complete strangers are free to criticize - is something I'm beginning to miss.  I feel so vile for having nothing else to say that could amount to anything of value or importance.  Anyways...in continuing the trend of making an entry only when shit happens...I'm sick with the cold.  Last week my brother gave it to me and I was getting better, until after last nights furry dinner.  I probably shouldn't have had any alcohol ~ giving off that false sense of warmth, especially when the weather recently began snowing ~ because the morning after my throat was shot.  I haven't gone to the gym in a week which makes me feel extra cruddy.  I hope I can get better before next week because I have a ton of school work to catch up on. 
😬 uncomfortable  |  ♫ B.o.B - Don't Feel So Good

Like it like that...

Feb. 1st, 2010   1:52 AM
Following up on a post I made a few weeks ago about art commissions, I received a couple pieces this weekend which I'm very happy about ^^. 

My new reference sheet by really puts on paper what I envision.  I am very happy with it!  Normally I like big, bright, beaming anime eyes rather than beady, squinty ones, but I'm really liking how she did them ~ the style remind me of Mouse Guard.

Maitre Joker finished my Chinese New Year picture and it is definitely another I am way ecstatic about!  It came out exactly as I wanted it to and I couldn't be happier =3.  I'll definitely frame it when it arrives in the mail, but I'm holding off on submitting it to my FA until the 14th.

Not long ago I got an email from Michele Light with a preview of my con badge...

This easily puts a smile on my face ~ I love it! :D  The only small add-in I asked before she mails it out was to include some whiskers.

There are still a couple of artworks I am waiting for but these three are enough to keep me going until the then.  Plus I also won this auction so now I have an artist I can put to work at anytime throughout this year heh.  I plan to get art done from her for other people such as birthday presents and random gifts ~ I'm very uncreative like that >>". 

I don't think it is wise for me to get any more art this month considering my spending.  If I do end up getting art for myself, I give any locals permission to kick me in the nuts.  (Oooooh I already see some artists tempting me with their awesome art T_T)  I have, however, been looking into several fursuit makers to find one I like as well as one who can possibly finish a partial before AC.  Money for that is not an issue though, heh.  What's more, in the pat week or two I've been gradually convincing myself to tell my parents about my aberrant interests.  Maybe it's just a matter of time now before I psych myself into doing it :S

What's been happening...

Jan. 22nd, 2010   2:04 AM
Phew, just finished looking through a weeks worth of submissions on FA ~ lots of great stuff!  Particularly some wallpapers (that tempt me to get one for myself) and two bunny pics that easily put a smile on my face ^-^.  Twinklestah made a great point a few days ago so I've been trying to follow through ~ whatever I +fav will include a comment...which can get tricky sometimes XP.

================================

I watched 'Oldboy' and 'The Imaginarium of Doctor Parnassus' a few days ago.  Dare I say Oldboy is a brilliant revenge movie?  Perhaps a bit implausible but holy crap is it ever well thought out!  The twist, revelation, and ambiguity at the end is just incredible ~ not to mention the gore and violence.  I highly recommend watching this one, especially if you've seen 'Ichi the Killer' :D  Doc Parnassus was good but not great mainly due to the crappy cam quality.  It's a movie you really have to see first hand or at least high quality since the visuals are artsy and imaginative.  One issue I had with watching the bootleg was that I couldn't really tell apart Heath Ledger, Johnny Depp, Jude Law, and Colin Farrell ~ they all looked rather similar which is a shame since they're all pretty hawt ^///^.

================================

I'm nearly finished re-watching the first season of 'departures.'  It's been rather uncomforting at times since some episodes really make me wish I had gone traveling ~ if I had it would be day 12 right now and I'd be in Kyoto >>".  I honestly don't want this to be something I'll regret later in my life (considering I already have one huge regret that I think about way to often...) so I'm going to try and roughly plan out my con circuit in the next few days.  Probably super early, but it'll definitely give me something to look forward to rather the dread on things past.  Plus hotel reservations for AC begin next month!

================================

I love Robin Soderling!  But it's ironic that they put out this video after he lost in the first round of the Australian Open grand slam tournament to an unseeded player.




I am so heartbroken for James Blake.  I use to root for Blake a year or two ago since he was a total underdog, but since then he's dropped to 45th rank.  He lost to Del Potro (ranked 4th) in the second round, but put up an epic 6-4 6-7(3) 5-7 6-3 10-8 battle.  I really wish I could have watched this match!  I haven't really been following any of this, which is odd because I love grand slams.  I guess I'll let the enthusiasm build until the quarter-finals =P.

================================

Most of this week I've been catching up on lectures/readings for the Intro to Psychology course I'm taking.  A brief study mentioned in the 'Intelligence and Thinking' chapter I found quite interesting.  There is a theory called stereotype threat - which is a type of confirmation bias - that occurs when a person is exposed to a stereotype (over a period of time) that can have an impact on their performance in the context of said stereotype.  Identities and labels carry stereotypes; such as 'Asian', 'Black', 'woman', 'professor'.

For example, Margaret Shih conducted a study among Asian-Canadian women.  After dividing them into three groups she gave them a math test.  But prior to the test group one was asked to fill in a survey about their ethnicity and heritage, while group two was asked to fill in a gender-related survey.  Group three served as a control group and went straight to the math test.  Math scores were highest among participants in group one, and lowest among participants in group two.  It's as though math skills become enhanced when associated with positive stereotypes, and vice versa with negative stereotypes (men do better in math/science than women). 

Interesting, no?  Can you imagine the messages being conveyed on a cultural level to people of a minority group, such as African-Americans?

================================

On Tuesday I ordered a case of Molicare Super Plus from Quality Life Services Inc. and they arrived Thursday morning ^-^.  Quick, discrete, and much cheaper than B4NS.  I've never worn Molicare but I've known about them for years and years ~ probably safe to say they were the precursor to today's top quality diapers like Abena, Bambino, and the like.  Man have I been missing out!  These freakin' awesome! XD  And I'm not just saying that because I've been pretty deprived of plastic-back diapers for a few months now, heh.

The only crap I've had to deal with are my parents >>".  My dad has been taking a curious interest in that big box I received in the mail...to which I abruptly said was new clothes.  So now the whole family thinks I bought new clothes which they'll probably expect to see in the next few days lol.  Not exactly sure how I'll work my way through this one, but hopefully they'll forget or never bring it up...much like my mouse tail. 

Oh gawd ~.~**  I was stitching up my tail last week before the furry bowling event because some seams had popped.  I left it on my bed and pretty much forgot about it until my dad came into my room.  He had an awkward and confused tone when he asked about it...to which afterwards it was never brought up again.  At least I hope it'll stay that way XD.  I suppose if I keep this up I'll have to break the ice sooner than later ^^;;
😴 tired  |  ♫ Birdman & Lil Wayne - I'm Ridin'
Link

Social obstacle...

Jan. 21st, 2010   9:39 PM
For a while now I've been feeling a gradual disconnect with the friends I've made in high school and university.  It wasn't something that bothered me very much in the past because I've disassociated myself from many of them ~ for example ceasing to use Facebook and removing nearly 150 contacts from my MSN.  But my relations with the small handful that remain feel as though they are slipping away; two of whom I've often wrote about - Stephanie and Jully - seem to be most evident.  I realize that people change and find new cliques, myself included, but watching it happen is disheartening.  For example, in the past months the vast majority of my social life has been invested in either the ABDL or furry circle while much of Jully's seem to either be Japanese or religion related.  What interests people pursue are obviously out of my control, but my inability to maintain a steady communication with these people is inexcusable and pathetic.  What's more, it feels as though I've confronted myself with this problem many times before and yet I continue to see it even on my other MSN ('dumpling') account.  I feel fortunate that people begin conversations with me because I rarely ever do so myself, but at the same time, with those I've become most distant with, I feel as though they are the ones who rarely seek out a conversation with me, so than why should I?  

My purpose for using MSN ~ to stay connected with 'friends' ~ feels superficial.  Certainly there are a handful of friends I genuinely care about and could easily entrust meaningful dialogues with, albeit rarely, but the vast majority of my contacts are faceless to me.  It is self-defeating when I allow myself to believe that when other people dded me, it is they who have something to say ~ because I rarely ever add people despite being curious about many.  What often happens in these cases is that we no longer talk after the initial days of being added and introducing one another.  Every now and then I'll receive a message from someone who only does so to roleplay, which makes me feel like a tool but it reinforces what little meaning I have for using MSN to begin with. 

I don't want the bulk of my social life to stem from one or two interest groups, but I don't know what to do about my lack of effort and commitment in maintaining relations.  Maybe a part of the obstacle is the medium itself.  If I stopped using MSN, would I find ways to be more connected with only those I truly cherish or would I become further distanced?  And at the same time, the decision to no longer use MSN feels like losing an appendage since I've become so dependent on it for 'staying connected' with people; even those who I consider faceless. 
🤔 contemplative  |  ♫ Ugly Casanova - Cat Faces
Link

Commission explosion!

Jan. 19th, 2010   12:08 AM
I need to make a list of commissions I'm waiting on or else I'll forget...it's happened before o.o"

Purdy watercolour badge by  (Chromey on FA) ~ it almost feels I'm stealing from her since it includes lamination and shipping .___."  For a while now I've wanted a badge that wasn't babyfur, looks like I'll be getting one =3

Michele Light con badge ~ considering she doesn't often take badge commissions and one of her auctions for a full body pic ended at $675...I'm gonna say I'm pretty happy with this one!  One commission from her is enough for me though, even if the opportunity ever comes up again. 

Oh FeverDog, every time I look at your queue I'm always still there, even when you take on more commissions.  You don't work in any specific order, but it grinds my gears when I've been waiting nearly two weeks and I see someone just added on the list two days ago have their commission already done.  *sigh*  I'm not in any rush since it's a re-do to an idea I've already got drawn from another artist, but I love the way you draw fur and hair so I'm really looking forward to getting mine .____."

I splurged a bit on this one because it was in Euros, but there was something about Maitre Joker's colouring that made me fall in love with.  So I commissioned a Chinese New Year (which is on Feb 14th this year) themed pic, which I think will turn out awesome.

I don't know why, but I've always wanted a traditional drawing (particularly a painting) of me sleeping in a bamboo steam basket XD.  It just seemed really jokes playing on words with my fursona name and my ethnicity =P. 

A new year calls for a new reference sheet, me thinks!  Maybe I'll start a tradition of that if I can afford too ^^;;  Heh, but really there are just a few things I'd like changed from my current ref.  (Tiggress on FA) has been super awesome walking me through each step of her progress and accommodating any changes.  Here's how its been going...


I like big, bright, beaming eyes, so that's one of the last changes I've asked for before she begins inking it.  I love how it's coming together <3  She's such a delightful artist, so definitely look into her if you're interested ~ she's going to FC as well ^-^.

A digital badge by Dar Wolverine ~ this honestly slipped my mind had I not checked my notes on FA o_O.  I haven't even given him any refs yet either...crud!  I'd better get on that >.o**

After looking through my inboxes and PayPal history, I think that is all I have so far o.o"  Although, I think there might be one commission where I pay after it is done but I can't remember from who...so maybe it'll come as a surprise or something ^^;;.

First ABDL munch of the year...

Jan. 17th, 2010   3:42 PM
...was awesome!  I got so baked it's miraculous I made it home in one piece XD. The night started off really gradual though.  I was the first to show up at the pub, which for some reason I always feel awkward about, but was later joined by someone I had apparently already met ~ Lenny tells me I met him two months ago and talked at length about stereos and headphones.  I don't recall at all and I don't think he recognized me either (lol) =S.  Oh well, we certainly know each other now!  I'll call him Anthony for the entirety of this blog.  He's a really cool guy and being twice my age his experiences have been interesting to learn about ^^.  No one showed up for almost forty minutes after him!  And to think furry time was bad >>" heh. BaBySB1 was next to arrive followed by Dylan, then much later boyricky and Lenny, Marvin and Max, and lastly Cameron ~ all of whom are practically regulars.  We stayed at the pub for quite a long time after eating; mostly drinking, watching the hockey game, and talking.  I had a deluxe pizza, a Rickard's white, and two 7-Up's ~ that put my European Attends Slip (+ stuffer) to good use ^-^.  I'm kinda glad I didn't go to the furry dinner instead (which was a tempting thought during the forty minute wait) since there probably would have been twice as many people that I wouldn't have known on a personal level.  I prefer small groups where I know everyone's name and a little bit about them.  Meeting new people is fun, but the formality of introducing yourself can be tiresome.  Just knowing you'll have a good time among good company is perfect for unwinding ~ it definitely felt right with that crowd.  And besides, who else can I talk about diapers and drag shows to but with them? XD I think by around 9pm we left the pub and were all invited to Ricky's place; his daddy was out having dinner so we had a bit of a party =3.  The only bummer about the munches is that I never know whether anything happens afterwards, or else I would come better prepared with something to change into like my onesie or sleeper.  I did eventually change into a Bambinos, but I'm not really a big fan of mooching for diapers especially since many of us needed changes heh ^^;;  I'm sure we left a small dent in Ricky's stash ~ it was really generous of him to be so accommodating.  Most of us were pant-less while a few had on cute outfits.  Throughout the night there was a contagious amount of tickling, groping, patting, and cuddling! <3  Oh man, I probably should have watched my limit too.  I had three bottles of beer (in a Diego sippy cup :D) and smoked up twice with Lenny, Marvin, and Max.  I'm a complete light weight compared to them o.o"  I was pretty zoned out after midnight.  It was a nice fuzzy funny feeling, but for the life of me I could barely understand what people were saying let alone say more than a few words (from what I remember, I was having the most random conversation with Anthony about hair removal creams...yeah =.=**).  I'm sure it was obvious that I was blazed ~ hopefully I didn't act too retarded >>"  It was really nice of Ricky to offer me a place to stay for the night, but my parents probably wouldn't have appreciated the short notice.  I took off at around 1am and got home an hour later with a case of the munchies, feeling way dehydrated, and too drowsy to do anything but collapse in bed.  All signs of a great night! :D 
🌀 ditzy  |  ♫ Mos Def - Supermagic
Link

Bowlerama radda radda

Jan. 16th, 2010   4:27 PM
Furry bowling last night was a lot of fun and astonishingly I didn't do as terrible as I thought I would ^-^.  At least twenty furs showed up and we took over, I think, five or six lanes.  There were a couple of new faces - two I think were non-furs - but I unfortunately didn't get much of an opportunity to meet some of them, mainly because they were the shy/quiet type .___. 

After the cut are pictures of my scores for the four games I played.  I didn't take any pictures of anything else because I was using my cell phone camera and everything was glowing in the dark since the lights were out.  Hopefully Shane and Furious will put up some videos and pictures on the forums =3

My first game with Ruff and his daddy Twicks.  Second place ~ not bad! :D

I couldn't get a shot of the scores for the second game between the three of us, but we all ended up with the same standing heh.  That didn't make Ruff a happy pup :P

The third game between the three of us was really close.  I could have came in second or maybe even first had I not guttered all three shots in the last round! XD  That was a completely fail ~ how embarrassing heh ^^;;.


My four game was with Peromyscus (V because he was in his Vendetta costume), Stew, Cornwall, and Ekevoo (one of the new furs I got to meet).  As you can see, Cornwall killed everyone ~ which is incredible since he was in suit too! D:  I did really poorly until the sixth round heh.



Besides bowling it was really great just hanging out, between games, with everyone.  My tail got groped a lot o__O LOL.  I really love Howiepup and Deoge's characters' ~ their fursuits are incredibly adorable and they're so much fun to play with!  Everytime I think about fursuits it makes me want one all the more XD.  Anywhoozle, I called it a night at around 10:30pm and took the train home with  .

Gonna head out to the ABDL munch in a hour, but I'm not really sure I'll do afterwards if there aren't any plans made during then.  I might just walk over to the Golden Griddle and see what's up with the furry dinner depending on the time =3. 
😄 cheerful  |  ♫ Telekinesis! - Rust
Link

Update...

Jan. 15th, 2010   10:38 AM
I beat Might & Magic: Clash of Heroes a few days ago! ^-^  The final boss, where you play as Nadia against Lord Bloodcrown, took at least a total of thirty tries on multiple sittings.  That mofo-ing boss is HARD! ><**  The trick is to use either Gremlin units only or that plus Phoenix units, mainly because the former is quick to charge and the latter can help you revive if it is charged.  For artifacts, either the Gauntlet or the Battle Wand will serve the most use I find. 

I put, give or take, forty-eight hours into this game and it's still got quite a lot of replay value since it has a 'Quick Battle' option rather than playing through the campaign again.  I definitely recommend anyone interested to get this game (buy or R4 card or PC ROM) ~ it's awesome!

============================================

I spend WAY to much time on YouTube ~.~**  But I suppose it's my substitute for television since I don't watch much of that.  Which is a bit funny because some of my friends are the opposite, so while they can refer to a ton of mainstream shows, I can refer to a bunch of popular YouTube channels ~ sometimes they get it but most of the time I just get weird looks XD.  Seriously though, there are a lot of great YouTubers out there: Shay Carl, Philip DeFranco, Natalie, Nigahiga, Michael Buckley, VlogBrothers, ROCKETBOOM, Barats and Bereta, Mr. ChiCity, Blunty, Chinese Guy, Vals Art Diary,  and quite literally many many more that I follow (and this doesn't even include channels that have their own websites like College Humor, failblog, NooBTooB, etc).  One of the YouTubers I really like, Ray William Johnson, recently created a second channel for vlogging, BreakingNYC, and it's really down to earth.  I love how he's relatable too since there are some interests we share ~ like graphic novels and Outasight X3  And he's not a sell out like a lot of popular YouTubers!  D:

============================================

Furry bowling is tonight! :D  When it was first brought up, I didn't plan on going because it was far, kinda late at night, and the turnout wasn't looking to lively.  I suppose it was a good thing I held off on giving a definite reply because the list of furs and suiters going has grown ^^.  But really, it was Ruff and Twinks that teetered my decision ~ I haven't seem them in a while and they're always fun to be with! XD  I haven't bowled in probably a decade (unless you include Wii Sport =P) and I don't recall doing five-pin, so hopefully I don't lose too badly.  Regardless though it should be a blast!  It's too bad I can't make it to the furry dinner the day after since it coincides with the monthly ABDL munch, but oh well, I'm still looking forward to the latter ^^.

Oh my gawd I won!  *squeal*  X3  I received a PM from Bae_Bunny last night that I was one of the eight that got picked ~ wicked!  It's way generous and sweet of him ^^.
🐇 bouncy  |  ♫ Incubus - Admiration
Link

Blue aliens with white freckles..."I SEE YOU" O___O

Jan. 13th, 2010   12:42 AM
At last...I have seen Avatar!  I don't care about the controversy over the absence of homosexuals and racist plot, I thought the movie was incredible!  Thank you very much  for watching it with me ~ it was next to impossible finding someone who hadn't seen it, so I do feel bad that this was his second time watching it in less than a week ^^;;  You're awesome!

We were really lucky too because we went to the IMAX theatre up in Woodbridge for the 3pm showing, but all the screenings were sold out.  It was sorta my bad since I didn't think there would be a ton of people given the time and day, heh.  Anyways, it was all sold out so we were just standing in line to purchase the regular showing when a girl came up and asked everyone if they wanted to buy her tickets for the IMAX 3D screening.  Apparently she bought the last two tickets but was in a group of five people.  So Dodge jumped at her offer despite there being two people behind us, whom were also closer to the girl.  WIN!

The only bummer was that there weren't any good seats left ~ we ended up sitting in the second row.  At least we could really feel the speakers ~ it gave me shivers and helped double my heart rate at some moments =P.  I've seen IMAX and 3D movies before, but combining the two for such an epic movie was breathtaking.  I really did feel transported into the environment, but not necessarily into the action sequences - those were just stunning.  The characters looked so real and I found myself rooting for them despite the cornball plot, which didn't phase me one bit surprisingly.  Like, I went into the movie knowing it had a Pocahontas/Ferngully storyline, but I was never completely reminded of that while watching it.  Maybe I just forgot about the plot since the visuals were so insane or maybe the progression of the plot made it's ending just feel appropriate.  Had it ended differently I would probably have been thrown completely off.  I'll admit a few tears somehow found their way out of me during the heated final battle when a bunch of characters died; but I'll just say it was a fluke since I laughed through The Notebook >>".  I'm definitely going to watch this movie again (paying for it?  probably not) and I encourage those who haven't to go see it in theatres! 

Afterwards we snailed through traffic to STC and had dinner at Swiss Chalet with his wifey ~ always a delight to hang around with ^^.  Later we had some desserts at Second Cup before heading home.  Good times!  And goodnight =P
😌 content  |  ♫ Mickey Factz - Ashes
Link

"Off to Japan for a month! See yea..."

Jan. 11th, 2010   7:55 PM
...is what I probably would be writing about had my plans not changed.  Instead, in a few hours I have to go pick up my brother from the airport =.=**  How depressing.  And it doesn't help that I downloaded the first season of departures. the other day XD.  I've mentioned the show a few times and it's simply incredible.  It's about two (hawt! ^///^) Canadians travelling around the world, often venturing outside the touristy areas, with a amazing cinematography.  Everytime I watch an episode the travel bug just gets right in me.  Doubly depressing! ><** 

This is probably an illegal upload, but here's the second episode from the first season!  The first episode was a 10-day trip all across Canada.  Watch it and love it <3


Anyways...besides losing four hundred bucks from the non-refundable parts of my plane ticket, insurance, and hostel bookings, I still have money to do stuff with.  I'm thinking conventions and maybe a partial, but the latter would involve breaking the ice with my folks ~.~" 

A new episode (finally!) of House is on in a few minutes so that's all for now =3.

Fur-stuff and music...

Jan. 9th, 2010   1:24 AM
Fur Affinity Ranking ~ quite an interesting site, especially the system of ranking.  But it shouldn't be taken too seriously and be sure to read the 'how does this work' section first.  Some of the 'up & coming' and 'ranking' artists are really quite talented, so the site is definitely useful for expanding my appreciation for different styles of art ^-^.

Toronto Furries Forum ~ spawned after some zesty drama on the Ontario Furries Forum.  (You won't be able to see much of anything from either forums without becoming a memeber.)  Although I am not an advocate for drama, it's interesting to see how it unfolds in the Town Hall sub-category (which I only recently ventured into, much like most of the threads on OFF).  TorFur is still in its infancy but I encourage Ontario furs going there over the other forum, it doesn't matter if you're not from Toronto!

============================================

I've mentioned my love for 'behind the scenes' and 'production and recording' type videos before, and this one is definitely worth checking out!


Mos Def working on 'The Ecstatic' - an album he released last summer and probably his best since his going solo.  No doubt he's a talented and influential emcee, and one of the few 'conscious rappers' around, but some of the tracks on his previous albums had really uninteresting beats.  'The Ecstatic' is so full of soul and real hip hop that it's practically deserving of Mos Def's style.  He was featured in two tracks on the Blakroc album I previously wrote about.  Definitely look into him if you haven't already (or seen him in movies =P).

There are plenty of great tracks on the album - one of my favourites is Auditorium, featuring Slick Rick and produced by Madlib - but here is just one; this video came out three days ago. 


In the late 90s Mos Def and Talib Kweli made up the hip-hop group 'Black Star' and this track is totally reminiscent of their awesomeness.
♫ Mos Def (ft. Slick Rick) - Auditorium
Link

Spice of life...

Jan. 7th, 2010   4:24 AM
Since things have gone back to 'normal' at the start of the week, there hasn't been anything interesting about my day-to-day worth writing about.  So far my days have been filled with boredom because I'm only taking one online course (PSYA02 - Intro Psychology: Part 2) this semester that's an hour long for three days a week.  It's a first year course and I'm only taking it for the half-credit I need to graduate, but the prof is excellent as well as the format - one midterm and final each worth 50%.  I'm just not use to watching the lectures since I'm accustom to sitting through two or three hour in-class lectures at a time; so I might just hold off until the end of the week and watch them all at once.  Plus if I don't do that it makes reading the assigned readings feel disorderly since lecture material gets broken up into parts. 

Outside of school I've pretty much been doing nothing.  I know there are things that I should be doing, but lately I've been pretty unmotivated.  I blame the post-holiday blues >>".  Probably the biggest thing on my 'to do' list is to get a job ~ easier said than done, of course.  Bumming around at home all day is boring and having my parents nag about it is tiresome.  But the whole processes of landing a job is going to be long, discouraging, and painstaking ~ it's like I've set myself up for defeat without even trying, go me! =S  Oh wells, when my mood picks up I'm sure I'll get the ball off the ground, or work my charm and score some opportunities through connections).  Oddly enough, money hasn't been much of an encouragement for me to look for a job.  I'm hardly well off but it's not something I desperately need; it's just nice to have if I decide to work for it.  Certainly there are lots of things I want and stuff I blow my money on ~ but I know it'll never come to a point where I'll be dirt broke.  I just complain a lot about being poor, but it's not often I'm too serious about it >>".

Anywhoozle, enough about that.  Anecdotes!  My brother is in Vancouver skiing and having fun for a week (that fortunate jerk face ~.~**).  Having the room to myself is pleasantly different.  I've been putting my sleeper and cloth diaper to good use and staying up until gawd knows when (like right now) has allowed me to chat with people I rarely have a chance to <3.  So even though during the day I've been bored, at least my nights have been fun and relaxing ^^.  I also recently watched Babel, Dorian Gray, and Fantastic Mr. Fox ~ all of which I really enjoyed but wished I could have seen in theatres .___."

In furry news, last night was the monthly Furry Dinner ~ it was a lot of fun and I'm glad it gave me something to do or else the rest of my boring week would leave me in tears.  There was probably twenty people in total; most I've met from previous dinners and meets, some new faces, and a few I found quite curious.  I don't remember their names, but there was a couple who came as 'guests'  - curious attendees without a fursona - and they were really delightful.  I hope they come out again =3.  There was also an underage furry accompanied by his dad (who is questionably a furry?).  At first I wasn't sure if it was a roleplay or real life relationship since I didn't notice any resemblances, but it turned out to be the latter.  Way cool!  They were really relaxed and open about furries and the prospect of being more active; I love their attitude.  I could never see my dad and I hanging out in social events for things that I'm interested in, so it totally amazes me to see it with other people ~ that's highly respectable, me thinks.  I also got to meet an Asian fellow (this makes the second I've met in Toronto :D) who is still new to the furry circle - quiet but understandably so.  Everyone starts somewhere so it's awesome that he jumped right into it ^^.  After the dinner Dodge and his wifey, Peromyscus, and myself went to a nearby Tim Hortons to socialize some more, but we didn't stay out too late because it was a weekday.  I got a ride home from Pero's dad, who owns a cab ~ lucky that we don't live very far from each other =P.  It was cool how his dad would join in with our conversation about furry stuff because he knew enough about it; I don't think I'll ever cease to be amazed at these types of family relationships. 

I wonder if I would be closer to my parents if I told them about my furry and ABDL interests >>".  I don't know how I would feel if it was kept at a don't ask, don't tell relationship if I did come out to them, primarily because these interests are important to me and they should be aware of that.  I don't like having to lie to them whenever they ask who I'll be hanging out or having dinner with ~ always telling them university friends or friends-of-friends.  It becomes really impersonal, especially when my parents are quite familiar with my brothers' friends.  If they were to ask my brother who he would be hanging out with, he would reply back with names and they would immediately know - on a more personal level - who that person is, what they look like, how they know each other, what they study or do for a living, maybe their family structure, etc.  I can't relate to that and it just hangs with me whenever it's brought up.  I don't want to be the shady character in the family ~.~**  I suppose all this is because I keep picturing a negative reaction if I tell them I'm meeting people from the internet, let alone people with so-and-so interest.   

There are stuff I want to ramble on about, but it's wicked late so I'll save it for another entry.  Peace.
😴 tired  |  ♫ Josh Rouse - Sad Eyes

Diaper changes...

Jan. 2nd, 2010   4:12 PM
(continuing from last post) Yes, I "secretly" enjoyed my diaper change by Seifer.  Or in other words I had a hard-on as Shiro teased me a bit from behind the curtains of his bed.  I loved it.  I guess I'm into humiliation since the other two times he changed me I was only slightly stiff for one of them.  Humiliation isn't something I've had my opportunity to experience or explore for myself, but it definitely gets me giddy ^///^ For most of the time I was over at their place, I was alone with Seifer ~ he seemed to convey an interest in putting his dick in me, but never said it explicitly.  I wanted him to, so I did try to further his dicision rather bluntly by asking if he wanted to be naughty.  But he didn't =(.  I wonder why.  He's a really cuddly guy and I'd love to have his dick in me at least once ~ it would make diaper changes so hawt!
Link

A new year...a fun start!

Jan. 2nd, 2010   4:08 PM
On New Year's Eve I stayed over at Seifer and Shiro's place (Dan_Raccoon was out of town for the holidays).  I had a lot of fun cubbing out ~ thanks for having me over! ^-^

Unfortunately, getting there wasn't so much fun since it took an hour longer than usual - something about making a stop at the airport and taking a detour due to street closures.  Oh wells, I made it by the late afternoon and not long after settling in Shiro had to leave for work; Seifer and I accompanied him on the bus since we needed to do shopping at Wal-Mart.  Most of the stuff we got were for dinner ~ ground beef comes in tubes o__O  wow, that's new to me!  When we got back, I was put to work again and made meatloaf using Seifer's super-special-awesome recipe X3.  As odd as this sounds it was really fun playing getting my hands all messy with the ingredients, to bad the beef was really cold!  I like 'helping' in the kitchen and having someone watch over me, especially when it involves squishing and moulding stuff ~ like pizza! <3

We cuddled to an episode of House MD and some video games while waiting for Shiro to come home.  There were some really pretty fireworks at 9pm on one of the nearby streets that had been closed off; we got to see them from the window ^^.  I get so easily amused when I'm cubby, and It doesn't help when I get showered with compliments and tickles either XD.  It's too complex to describe in words, but I love those feelings! =3

The meatloaf took longer than expected to cook - even though I kept saying beef can be eaten practically raw and that it continues to cook after it's out of the oven.  But they didn't believe me! ><  *pouty*  It tasted awesome though, so I'll take all the credit for that heh (kidding).  Anywhoozle, time seemed to go by really quickly that night.  At midnight there was another fireworks display at the same spot as the last one, but it was mostly just Roman candles so that was a bit lame.  It's strange that the good stuff was shown earlier than the actual time of the event, but we stood on the balcony and froze outside to watch regardless =P. 

To be honest, everything in that particular moment felt really different to me.  Because every single New Year's Eve I've had with friends and family would involved either watching fireworks from around the world, a countdown with the local tv news, drinking, cheering, kissing, hopes for a good year, or any combination of that kind.  It's just something I've associate with how a 'traditional' New Year's celebration should be.  They didn't have cable tv and the basic channels didn't include any local news.  There wasn't any champaign or grapey beverage.  And time just sort of went on, without a countdown, like any other day but with some fireworks.  The sense of celebration was pretty nil and I felt out of place when they were calling friends and family afterwards ~ I couldn't because my cell phone battery died =(.  But by no means am I criticizing them, they can do whatever they like, the moment was a bit of a bummer for me is all ^^;;

Four hours of video gaming, YouTubing, and roughhousing flew by before Shiro and I retired for the night.  Seifer stayed up to continue gaming, but not before changing me into an Abri-Form.  I had already been padded since leaving my house in cloth-like disposables and my cloth diaper.  Having been deprived of plastic disposables for weeks now (T__T), it was a really comforting change ~ a bit of a humiliating one as well, but I 'secretly' enjoyed it XP. I got about five hours of sleep before my inner alarm clock kick started my morning.  Seifer was still gaming (that's hardcore o__o"), but after a little while we traded places and he went off to bed.  I played Might & Magic: Clash of Heroes on my DS for four hours.  Currently my saved file is at 45 hours, but this includes maybe five to eight hours of idle time (game is paused and DS is closed).  Even with that difference I think it's still pretty extreme for me to get sucked into a game I've been playing for less than a week!  I think I'm close to finishing though since I'm on the last character ~ I wonder how will I spend my free time afterwards? D: 

Eventually Shiro woke up and we chatted for a while.  I was awesome looking through some of his art I had never seen before ^-^.  When he had to leave for work, Seifer woke up and we chatted for a bit as well =3.  I headed home a little after 4pm.  All in all it was definitely a fun and relaxing way to start the new year!

========================================================

Yay!  New art to being in the New Year! \o.o/

Sasha Dobie was having a sale on baby badges so I got three for twenty bucks =3  The other two can be found here and here ~ given to  and .  They look so adorable! O3O
😊 happy  |  ♫ KRS-One - Womenology
Link

Looking back at '09...

Dec. 31st, 2009   11:40 AM
It is fair to say that 2009 is my most memorable year because nearly all of my experiences were recorded in this blog.  I wrote a 'Looking back...' entry last year, and so for preparation this year I went over all of my entries.  I am honestly at a loss for words and I feel deeply touched inside.  I did so much this year.  To go back and recollect everything was very moving for me since I have terrible memory - I would have forgotten a wealth of experiences had I not written about them.  To try and sum up my year in just one entry is so incredibly difficult because it feels as though it would devalue everything I've done.

First and foremost, I would like to thank everyone for being a part of my life.  I am not a social butterfly and I only have a handful of friends ~ I value each of them for everything they've done, no matter how big or small.  It seems with every passing year my social network shifts somewhat.  I have long since forgotten a lot of people I was once acquainted with from high school and university, save for a small handful.  In March I discontinued using Facebook because most of the people on my friends list were hardly people I would consider friends - it was mainly a facade.  In 2008 my social circle mainly embodied online gaming friends and ABDL friends.  This year I've lost contact with the former but filled that void with furry friends.  It is unfortunate that I don't keep in contact with people and rely on them to make conversation with me, and for those who do, I am truly thankful for. 

I have notice, while reading my old entries, how my interests have developed but not necessarily matured.  For example, at the start of the year I considered myself 20% AB and 80% DL but now it leans more towards fifty-fifty.  I have found that the level of enjoyment I receive from regressing has become more important to the things I want.  In effect, throughout the year I have been accumulating things that reflect this desire; such as my blanket, onesie, sleeper, and several plushies.  I know that this side of me has certainly made me happier about everything so I am glad to be embracing it.  Another interest that I am equally happy about is being more active as a furry ~ in 2008 I became more active as an ABDL by going to the local munches and gatherings.  Throughout the latter half of this year my furry interests felt more fleshed out with the aid of FurAffinity.  Conceptualizing my fursona into something that can be shown and appreciated, and not only that, but going out and meeting other furs - it is deepening and rewarding.  Although I spent a lot of money on commissions and outings in just a few short months, looking through my gallery and recalling fun experiences continues to be enriching. 

I use to think that it wasn't normal to have more online friends than in-person ones and that there was something wrong with me.  But friends are real life regardless of the medium they are found in.  I am glad there are people who encourage me to be more active whether online or offline, I have made many enjoyable memories and tried things I otherwise wouldn't have from both these two interests.  It is without a doubt I will pursue more of these experiences in the years ahead.

I know I write at length about my ABDL and furry interests, but they are just parts of my life.  Throughout the year there have been many other things that have affected my life, for example school.  I had high aspirations to finish in December and graduate in May.  My oversight of academic regulations hindered that goal of finishing due to being half a credit short for graduation - next year I will be doing my final semester.  Included with that disappointment were my plans to travel for a month in Japan.  I was incredibly enthusiastic while making arrangements for the trip but now it has become a waste of time and money.  Even though I no longer plan on traveling to Japan when I graduate, I hope to use some of that money I have set aside to attend some conventions.  This year I had an amazing time at Anime North and Fan Expo, I will certainly make an effort to go again next year.  I will also be going to Furnal Equinox, and hope to attend What The Fur, Condition: Zero Hour, and Anthrocon - time permitting.

In between the times spent on school and engaging in fandoms and communities, I invested countless hours on things completely unrelated.  I have read a ton of comic books and watched a lot of movies, tv shows, cartoons, and anime series.  It's amazing how much of that would have cost me money had I not downloaded them.  Furthermore, computer games and YouTube have taxed even more hours out of my year; although the numbers are on record, they are too staggering for me to admit.  On a healthier note, this year has consisted of working out at the gym a lot more, as well as playing squash and tennis with friends.  Although eating and hanging out with friends have increasingly been difficult among certain circles of friends, I understand that others social circle also shift and work is crucially important.  Even still, I am very happy with the many opportunities this year has allowed for and I hope things continue to be as prosperous.  For my family this year, these fortunes and hard times were most strongly felt with the birth of my aunt and uncles' first son, and the passing of my grandfather. 

There is a lot to look forward to next year: my graduation, future conventions, the start of tennis season, 2010 Vancouver Olympics, etc.  I have come to realize that my life is hardly predictable; last year I would never have imagined I'd be where I am today.  I had a very good year and it's hard to say whether next year will be as good or better, but I most certainly will hope for it.  Some things are beyond my control and happen without reason while others I can expect repercussions for doing; I have come to accept that and I'll take the good with the bad.  From the bottom of my heart, thank you again to everyone for being a part of my life, especially if you've followed with my blog.  Who I am is reflective in the entries of this bog and it continues to be a great pleasure sharing it with people. 

Have a safe and happy New Years.  I hope the year has been good to you and that 2010 will be even better.
🌙 nostalgic  |  ♫ Torley on Piano - Unlimited Adventure
Link

Tripping on hip hop

Dec. 28th, 2009   11:16 PM
Awesome-sauce! <3



Chris Faust (previously known as Print) is awesome!  I've mentioned about his album (Comic Books: Unlimited) months ago ~ I still listen to it practically daily.  For the last little while he's been putting out webisodes of production and recording sessions for his latest album (FAUST); very cool stuff.  I can't wait until the album drops! 

Outasight has been featured on a couple of the Comic Books tracks ~ one of my favourites being 'Super Friends' because it features a ton of underground NYC rappers.  He recently got signed with Asylum/Warner Music.  Two weeks ago his latest mixtape came out, presented by LRG Clothing, free for download called 'Further'.  It's a pretty solid album - refreshing and different.  Give it a listen if you're interested ^^.

Kiss and blog #5

Dec. 28th, 2009   12:25 AM
(continuing from last post) We did a lot more cuddling than usual ~ probably because I went to his place earlier than usual.  It was really comforting feeling and touching each other; I haven't had that in a while.  It didn't take very long before I was jerking him off with a tape from each side of his diaper undone.  He was pretty messy after coming on his chest ^///^.  After a short rest we dry humped one another, whether on his leg or on his crotch, I wasn't hard unfortunately.  I got hard momentarily when I was in a doggy position; I eventually jerked myself off and came.  It was a lot of fun resting my head on his crinkly butt or wet bulging front.  The smell and warm makes me want to suck him off ~ or at least try it once.  I kinda wish he would butt fuck me though, but he didn't want to so I don't want to push it.

Quick update...

Dec. 28th, 2009   12:16 AM
My boxing day consisted of zero shopping.  My parents went mall crawling.  They say the line up already began from the exit ramp off the highway to get into Yorkdale Mall ~ that's nuts!  I went and played jitz with my brother at night, I was really rusty since its been months since I last played.  We managed to win a couple of free games so that was fun =P.  Afterwards we went to the gym.  I did 2.92 miles (400 calories) in 37 minutes on the treadmill, followed by my usual weights exercises; so that felt great!  My gut still feels bigger than normal ~ I wanna get rid of this holiday weight! XD

========================================================

I just started playing Might & Magic: Clash of Heroes for the Nintendo DS today and I'm really loving it.  (Didn't buy it - no monies.  Downloaded it and put it on my R4 card >>")  It's an RPG pick-three game (so think Final Fantasy storyline but with Bejeweled battle style) that's challenging but really fun!  I've put in 3:23 hours and my elf character is level seven right now ~ there are four other characters that you'll eventually get to, each with their own story and different special moves.  I'm going to try and beat this game...unlike Scribblenaut or Professor Layton and the Curious Village, both I hyped about but lost interest in ^^;;.  I tried downloading Mario & Luigi: Bowswer's Inside Story but the file doesn't seem to work ~ like I can open the game on my DS but I can't create any new files.  Might be a firmware issue =/.

========================================================

Tonight I went to K-Dawg's place; can't say I remember the last time I went, and I'm too lazy to check ^^;.  We did the usual ~ padding each other up in Kendall's, lots of cuddles (getting assaulted with tickles o_o"), movies, and pizza!  We pretended to watch most of 'Julie & Julia', but as usual got distracted with other things =P.  We did manage to sit through Twilight, but only cause I hadn't seen it and wanted to XD.  Now I must watch New Moon!!!  (>< Don't judge me! LOL)  We ordered delivery from Pizza Pizza and it was wicked delish ~ if I told you the topics and sauces I'd have to kill you >:3  It was a really fun and relaxing night, something I really needed since I haven't worn plastic back diapers in weeks.  Plus one can never get enough cuddles <3.  (Hrm, I know pacifiers are bad for your teeth, but I get an odd 'out-of-place' feeling around my lips and teeth after the one I used tonight o__O)

========================================================

I just noticed that if you set your 'mood' status at the end of an entry and it is the same name as one of your 'userpic' it will change to that.  So if I set my userpic to default and my mood to content, when I post an entry the userpic will be the one I named content.  Blah.  I like to set my userpic depending on the context of my entries, so the glitch is a tiny bit annoying.  
🐇 bouncy  |  ♫ The Roots - You Got Me
Link

Food me =3

Dec. 25th, 2009   11:58 PM
I've been eating quite a lot lately o_o"  I really ought to hit the gym sometime but its been so hard to feel motivated when the weather has been so dull.  Blah, not a big of this lethargy.  So, I got a haircut yesterday and it was a total rip off >>".  Nearly twenty bucks for maybe ten minutes of labour!  That'll probably be the last time I ever go to a Magicuts ~ so much for being a 'cheap' barber shop; I'll try a Supercuts the next time XD.  It would have been nicer if I had the car, but my parents went skiing for the day.  Oh wells.  In the afternoon I met up with Jully for afternoon tea...Asian style!  For most people afternoon tea would probably consist of some kind of baked good with some kind of caffeinated drink, but for Chinese people it's practically a meal in on itself.  If you go to most noodle and congee restaurants they'll have a special menu catered for the mid-afternoon only ~ foods usually consisting of both Wester and Easter, like sandwiches, fried egg, spaghetti, wontons, curry rice, etc.  All of the items usually include milk or lemon tea, coffee, Horlicks, or Ovatine.  What's more, the prices are freakin' cheap ~ usually less than five bucks.  You could probably skip lunch and just have afternoon tea, and then have a late or light dinner to save yourself some money! X3  Oh those damn Asians (lol).  But yeah, I'm not use to eating so heavy in the afternoon so that pretty much ruined my appetite for dinner ^^;;.  Oh wells, I mainly just wanted to see Jully so that I could give my Christmas present to her; it was a fleece blanket <3.  (Since you're sick, I hope it comes in handy ~ get well soon =3)  Other than re-watching The Dark Knight, I didn't do much of anything the rest of the night.  Today I slept in, which is funny when I think about it because as a kid I'd always wake up ridiculously early on Christmas so that I could open presents.  After settling into the morning, the family and I got to the presents ~ we took pictures and I had to be in my reindeer kigurumi cause my grandma was ape shit about it X3.  My brother and I got our parents pajamas.  The prices for them were kinda messed up since my dad got pajama pants from Roots, but it cost more than my mom's full pajama set from the Bay >>".  My gramps got a scarf and my grandma got a collection of spiffy jams ~ she's a packrat so she'll probably keep the jars and goggle over them everytime she uses them heh.  My bro got me new headphones (the Skullcandy iCon) and I got money from everyone else ^^;;.  Afterwards, we all went out and had dim sum with our old neighbours.  In the afternoon I played TF2 and watched Paranormal Activity.  I can't honestly say I found the movie all that horrifying.  I really hated the characters, especially Micah...the worst boyfriend ever.  I was practically rooting for the demon to come and eat his head off ~ gawd was he ever stupid and annoying!  The only parts I liked where the 'haunting' scenes while they were sleeping, but they were more interesting than scary.  Apparently there were three endings; the one I watched was pretty lame (SPOILER!  the police shoot the girl).  Should you go see it?  Only if it's free and you have an hour and a half to kill.  You aren't missing much.  Kudos to the director though for making a low budget flick and pulling in millions! :D  Anyways, my Christmas dinner consisted of a big boiling pot of water and lots of uncooked food o_o.  The Asians call it hot pot =P.  Can you believe people go to restaurants that only serve this style of eating? D:  What kind of person would pay for a meal only to have to cook their own food?  Heh, I'm only kidding ~ hot pot is friggin' awesome!  Plus, it's a hell of a lot cheaper if you do it at home ^-^.  My family only does it for special occasions, so it was a nice change from what we usually eat.  We had beef, pork, fish, shrimp, scallops, sausages, fish balls, meat balls, a ton of different veggies, udon, eggs, and the list goes on.  Hot pot is served with soy sauce to dip your foods in, but you can kick it up with garlic, onion, and various herbs.  And in the end, you get a ridiculously tasty broth ~ but it contains a lot of 'hot' properties ('hot and cold' tie in with traditional Chinese medicine; the two should be kept balanced) so you shouldn't drink more than a few spoonfuls unless you want your face to break out the day after ^^;;
💤 sleepy  |  ♫ Outasight - 3 Cheers Forever

Happy Holidays!

Dec. 25th, 2009   11:06 AM
Whether you celebrate Christmas, Hanukkah, Kwanzaa, something else or nothing at all, have a safe and happy holidays everybody!  I hope everyone is able to cherish this time of year with friends, family, and loved ones.  As much as I love material gifts, it shouldn't be the pinnacle of your holidays =P.  So give someone a hug and make them smile ~ it feels good deep down and it's contagious ^-^.
