Link

Yesterdays happenstances...

Dec. 23rd, 2009   2:27 PM
My Tuesday was a lot of fun ~ hung out with my high school friends Mark, Andy, and Kelvin (+ his girlfriend) =3.  I can't exactly remember (and too lazy to look up) when the last time we got together, but it generally happens once or twice a year.  It's interesting to watch how some of their traits have changed and remained the same over the many years I've known them ~ Mark especially, since we've known each other since early grade school.  Our original plan was to meet up at STC, but I've been going there quite often so we switched to Fairview Mall ~ kinda regret that now since it would have cut travel time for when we did stuff afterwards >>".  We pretty much just ate, had coffee, and caught up with what was going on; the food court was surprisingly conversation friendly.  I bought my older brother Mario Kart Wii for his Christmas present, which cost twenty bucks cash since I still had a lot of credit from selling my PSP a few months ago.  Personally I don't think he's worth that much money, but we've been on good terms lately and I'd feel bad if he got me something and I couldn't reciprocate.  He wanted the New Super Mario Bros. Wii but the store was sold out ~ and really without the co-op or new items, it's mainly just an expensive Super Mario Bros. 3 clone.  Other than maybe some clothes, there wasn't much I wanted to buy.  After a bit of deliberation we decided to head over to Kelvin's place to just bum around.  The drive was a bit traffic ridden since we were heading east (again, would have taken less time had we gone to STC ~ my bad XD), but it was nice to see parts of town I rarely go to.  The condo he lived in had an incredible lobby area.  It kinda looked like a business office since it was a bit open square with a fountain and Christmas tree in the center with elevators to the sides.  Anyways, we loafed around on the sofa and chatted some more ~ some of it was boring I'll have to admit, because it held little relevance to me ^^;;.  At around 5:30pm Kelvin and his girlfriend has his own things to do, so the rest of us headed to Richmond Hill.  It was a bit nostalgic seeing the area where I use to live and how much it has undergone development; big box stores were everywhere.  For the rest of the night we played video games at Mark's place, not before I had dinner at Andy's house.  I don't recall if I've mentioned it before, but it is so awesome to play games on a giant projector screen!  Mark's entertainment room is so decked out that I'm sure he's got a money tree hidden away somewhere ><**.  We played a lot of Super Smash Bros. Brawl, which I didn't do too bad in probably because I had a Gamecube controller =P.  Mario Strikers Charged was an okay game, the controls are fairly easy to pick up, but the game itself I found a bit bland.  And we also played the New Super Mario Bros, which unfortunately we only had two Wii Remotes for.  Either way, the co-op is wicked fun but challenging at the same time.  Its got great replay value for the co-op, but once you beat the game on single player it gets a bit vanilla without friends. By the end of the night, we played until 2am X3.  Andy game gave me a lift home ~ thanks, I really appreciate it ^^.  Yet another night where I've gone to bed without showering (ew >>") cause I've come home so late, but it was worth it heh, I had a really awesome night :D
♫ Smashing Pumpkins - The End Is The Beginning Is The End
Link

I'm a reindeer...

Dec. 22nd, 2009   12:38 AM
...for Christmas =P.  Like I mentioned before, I was a penguin for Halloween, so to get in to the spirit of the holiday (and failing to find a mouse kigu), I bought this instead ~ a Christmas Reindeer Kigurumi (or Japanese pajamas)!
 

It arrived on Monday ~ to bad it didn't arrive on Friday so that I could have worn it to Saturdays party >.<  Ah well, it's still really awesome and super comfortable!    I wonder if I can take out the stitches on the hat so I can wear this year round without looking out of place XP.

Link

FE Christmas Party and Furry Hijinx #2

Dec. 20th, 2009   11:58 PM
I had an awesome and exhausting weekend at Saturdays Furnal Equinox sponsored Christmas Party followed by Sundays Furry Hijinx #2  ^-^  Recapping now cause I've been lazy all day!  Hopefully I'll have some pictures to show once they start circulating, since I didn't have a camera!

Saturday
The party was wicked fun even though there was a ton of furs I didn't get to meet.  I think it's probably because I already knew a handful of them so I had people to stick around with and didn't really feel the need to come out of the circle.  Not exactly the best way to meet new people, but it wasn't like anyone was holding me back but myself ^^;;  There's always next time.  Among some of the new furs I got to meet was Zerrif ~ an Asian! O__O  Woohoo!  I feel like less of a minority X3.  We did a lot of back and forth stereotyping which was hilarious ~ none of it you can honestly take too seriously though; they're jokes after all.  There was also this one fellow who I thought I recognized from even before I began identifying myself as a furry.  (He confirmed that wonder the day after.  We met once three or four years ago at the Gay Pride Parade through a friend we both knew.)  Although I didn't get to meet a lot of people at the party, at least I got to put some faces to names I've seen on the OntFur forum =P.

There was probably around forty to fifty people at the party, some of whom brought food for the potluck.  There was a lot of good stuff to eat; so much that some items weren't even brought out...like the salads I brought >>".  Oh wells, I'm sure it'll feed the hosts of the party for a while since there were a lot of leftovers.  , your pumpkin pie was delish! :D  And oh my freakin' gawd thank you so much for the Christmas presents ~ like that wasn't enough he even included a coffee card! O__O  Duuuude, you are too generous and I feel so bad for receiving and not giving; I will do what I can to reciprocate!  What's more, your new tail looks way awesome! =3.

Unfortunately Santafur couldn't make it, but Rudolf (Danny Dingo in a rental suit) with disproportionately tiny eyes was able to take his place :D  Since it was a con sponsored event the organizers were able to get small gifts for everyone ~ I got one after sitting on Rudolf's lap XD.  I'm not exactly sure what it is, but my gift was a duck-thing with springs for its neck and legs so it bobbles.  Err, it'll probably just let it take up space in a box under my bed >>".

I think I stayed until around 11:45pm-ish.  By then a lot of people had already taken off while the rest were either knocking back rounds or cleaning up.  I'm pretty sure I heard LizzardBoy say he drank something like twenty bottles ~ I probably shouldn't have encouraged him have a beer with me ^^;;  He looked only a little tipsy, but still it seemed a bit unfashionable to drink so much; even for a Russian X3.  Oh well.  After a long night of hanging out with friends - hugs, cuddles, tickles - some Cribbage, movies (+ Jeff Dunham Christmas special), and a few drinks, I headed out with Peromyscus.  Didn't get home until 1:30am-ish.  I'm actually quite surprised by how many furs live relatively close to me; around four or five.  I ought to get to know them better =3.

Sunday
Thankfully the meet up time for Furry Hijinx #2 changed from 11am to 1pm, or else I'd have had difficulty waking up.  But then again, everyone was going by furry time so getting together took a while XD.  I met up with Smash, Skye, and Dodge, followed by Danny Dingo, Nyxwolf, Babs (in suit as Cheeto), and Maurice.  From Union station we walked to the hotel where Vinnie and Andy were staying ~ along the way and while in the lobby we got a lot of looks from people.  It was fun to watch people ask for photos to be taken with Cheeto, especially when it puts a smile on little kids. 

We all headed to Nathan Phillips Square afterward - Vinnie and Danny were in suit as well - and hanged out by the skating rink for a while.  That was a lot of fun, getting on ice and just having a good time.  It's so amazing to be able to watching people light up with a smile when they see any of the fursuiters.  Makes me a bit jealous XD  I so want a partial now <3.  Hells, money isn't so much the issue, but rather the household I'm in.  Perhaps I ought to spill the beans to my family o.o"

Anyways, we spent quite a bit of time at the rink before heading west on Queen St.  Coincidentally while we were hanging around Much Music, my friend Stephanie pointed me out o__O.  She and her friends just finished watching a Raptors game (we beat the Hornets 98-92 :D) so to cross paths like that was kind of a small world experience >>".  I wonder what went on in her mind when I told her I was hanging out with my friends...and then pointed to a group of eight people; one with a horse tail and gloves, and three in fursuits ^^;;  (Hey, if you're reading this, hit me up so we can get together sometime ~ I have a present with your name on it X3.)

So that was somewhat different - to say the least - but I'm not stressing it.  Whatever =P.  We eventually made our way to the Chapters next to the Scotiabank Theatre.  Rested for a while at the Starbucks and planned out where else we should head to and what to eat for dinner.  It was suggested that we should head to Dundas Square, which would have been a hell of a hike, but everyone was cool with it...sorta.  There are things that I should probably leave out of this entry to avoid repercussions, but I'm gonna say it anyways because I'm really unhappy with how it went down.  At about midway to where we were heading Babs pretty much told off Maurice ~ or rather, suggested he should go home bluntly >>".  That really wasn't cool, especially the way she conveyed her message, as though her feelings were shared by everyone else in the group.  Whether or not everyone saw eye-to-eye with him (because even I'll admit I thought he was a bit different) should be kept to themselves ~ if there is an issue, it should be dealt with one-on-one.  The fact that she skipped that processes, didn't give him a chance to straighten up, and made him feel unwanted by everyone is just uncalled for.  I don't want to be put in the position of 'the bad guy' just because I followed everyone else in the other direction afterwards, but I feel bad about it.

(Maurice, if you're reading this and despite your name is a pseudonym, I'm sorry for what happened ~ please don't let her actions reflect mine >>".)

After that the plan pretty much changed to backtracking and heading down to the CN Tower, not before stopping at a buttons shop and eating at Pizza Pizza...which sparked another unnecessary moment in the day.  While we were eating on the second floor at Pizza Pizza, an argument broke out between some customers and some staff on the ground floor.  There was banging and shouting, so naturally a bunch of us went to look over the ledge.  And then Skye went downstairs and pretty much simmered the situation by taking the angry customers outside.  I thought he did a really good thing and deserved kudos, but Babs wasn't too happy about it ~ I really don't know what her problem was >>".  She plainly said he wasn't welcome back to any future furdays if he were to behave like that ~ again, speaking on all of our behalves =.=**  I'm not to fond of her opinions and level of bossiness, or outspokenness.  It's one thing to put down someone repeatedly by telling them to "use their brain", but to convey some level of authority and mask it as something mutually agreed upon when it isn't just pisses me off.  It's rather deceiving too because I love her suit and her character Cheeto, but the way she does things beyond those aspects aren't things I can agree on all the time.

So the plans got scrapped and there was a general vibe that everyone was going to call it a night since tension was still high strung after what had happened.  It was enormously considerate of Vinnie to tell us what was really going down ~ the fursuiters (and Andy) were still planning to go to the CN Tower without us.  What's more, Vinnie welcomed Skye to come to any future furdays regardless.  I can understand the position he was put in, having to take sides and trying to appease everyone, so for him to tell us behind there backs was really respectable thing to do.  No hard feelings were felt, I'm sure.  And again, if whatever I write makes its way to someone who disagrees with it, then that's fine.  If there are consequences for saying how I feel about what happened, then lay it on me.  I just think some things could have been handled better and some people deserve more credit.

Smash, Skye, Nyxwolf, Dodge, and myself went off and did our own thing afterwards ~ things Babs wouldn't be cool with, lets just say.  And it was freakin' fun!  The things you can do on an escalator or a giant sign attached to a spring - wow o__O - I would never have imagined (lol).  We went into the Eaton Centre, followed by PATH (the underground walkway), which led us to the financial district, so we made our way into St. Lawrence Market where we relaxed at a Second Cup. We eventually called it a night at around 10pm, headed to Union station, and eventually went our separate ways.  Big props to those who had a camera or camcorder ~ I can't wait to see how everything turned out ^^. 

(After the weekend, the tip of my tail has gotten really dirty D:  Nuuuu! *scrub scrub scrub*)
😴 tired  |  ♫ Ape School - Deathstomp
Link

School is out. Holiday is in.

Dec. 19th, 2009   1:04 AM
I had a pretty good day to cap the end of a stressful week ^^.  I had my SOCB49 exam at 9am ~ that was a real joy to wake up for >>".  The exam wasn't too difficult or easy; it was fair and I'm pretty sure I did okay.  Then again, I would still pass the course even if I bombed the exam ^^;; 

Afterwards I went to STC to do some Christmas shopping.  I'm awful when it comes to thinking of things to get for people.  Often the case i just opt for something really expensive and go all out for a handful of friends.  This year I'm strap for cash so I settled on cheap but practical things ~ I'd spell out what I bought but I donno who reads this blog so no sense ruining the surprise.  I just hope I can make some plans to meet with friends I haven't seen in a while.  (Yeah, I got something with your name on it Bunny!  You want it?  Get your booty out of Ancaster for a day ><**.  Thanks again for the Christmas card ^^.)

After surviving the long line-ups and painfully repetitive music - followed by chilly outdoor weather - I got home at around 3pm.  Caught up with my horrible addiction to TF2 ~ probably played for three hours >>".   (PickyZim on FA) commissioned a pic for me as a Christmas present {o'.'o}  That was really sweet of him ~ thank you very much!  ^^  Since I got my present early, it only seemed fair for me to give him his early as well =P.  The pic was done by WingSpots.  Freakin' adorable if you ask me X3.  I've got three others done, one in the works, and hopefully three others that she'll pick up on depending on her availability.  I'm not gonna say who they will be going out to ~ it isn't Christmas yet and people might give me the stink eye if they weren't included >>".  All-in-all they were cheap and they'll make a bunch of people happy, sounds like a win win to me! :D

I wrapped up my night by working out at the gym; I haven't gone in about a week and a half.  I planned on going last Tuesday, but I found some  excuse not to ~ kind of regretted it since I was in a crappy mood and in general feeling unhealthy.  I know my diet hasn't been the healthiest this past week (a lot of carbs and chocolate .___.") so sweating out some of that guilt felt great XD. 

My day would have been ten times more awesome if my parcel had come ~ which I'm still waiting on T__T.  Seeing as it probably won't come tomorrow morning, I'll just spill the beans.  I was a penguin for Halloween, so I figured I'd be a reindeer for Christmas! XD  I bought this reindeer kigurumi early last week (while I was still crazy obsessed with searching for a mouse kigu) and was hoping to wear it to tomorrows Christmas gathering.  So much for the six day delivery that the seller advertises ~ but considering it is coming from Japan, I already knew it wouldn't be that fast.  I just had high hopes (lol).  Now I need an excuse to wear this somewhere if it arrives before Christmas >>".  Someone plan a sleepover or gathering or something! XD

As well as the ones I commissioned for people, I got one for myself!  The inner ears and hands are suppose to be pink, but I can look past this since it's a cheap and adorable commission ^///^.
😴 tired  |  ♫ Lupe Fiasco - A Bathing Harry
Link

Update

Dec. 15th, 2009   8:08 AM
Its been a stressful and anti-social couple of days ~ not something I'm enjoying, but it'll probably stretch until the end of the week. Lately I've been staying up late and waking up early in order to get things done; so despite going to bed earlier than usual last night I'm already awake now and I'll probably crash by mid-afternoon >>". I'm not super happy with how my report turned out, but I've already handed it in. I'm going to rehash it sometime after the weekend so that it'll be less embarrassing when I it to the participants and post it up here. I've also got a SOCB49 final exam Friday morning which I haven't done an ounce of studying for - I'll probably start late-afternoon and pick up the pace the following few days. I'm still on the brink about whether to travel or not ~ I've talked it out with my parents and there are a lot of pros/cons about going, rescheduling, or canceling. Just another something that's been cluttering my mind lately. Luckily there have been a few things that have helped pick up my mood and things that I'm excited to look forward to. Thank you very much to those who txt'd, offline IM, or noted me on my cell, MSN, or FA. I haven't been using any of those much lately, but there's always a way to get a hold of me and I'm glad people poke me even when I'm not around. I'm thankful for having an efficient brother as well ~ he got done all of the Christmas shopping for members of our family, which we usually share gifts for. So although it put a dent in my wallet, at least I didn't have to think or go out and buy presents. Hopefully sometime next week I can find somethings for my friends, but that's cutting it really close. I'm looking forward to a parcel that should be arriving soon ~ it's a secret until it comes, but I'm really excited about! Also, I'm having lunch with Jully ~ who I haven't seen in over a month. It'll be nice to catch up with her and this time around we're hanging out in my area so I don't have to bum the transit like usual. I think I really need this to unwind before jumping back into study mode; she always makes me smile ^^. Lastly, I'm looking forward to the furry Christmas gathering on Saturday; what better way to brush off a stressful week? Furry Hijinx: Part II looks interesting, but it'll depend on the weather if I go or not.
🎵 mellow  |  ♫ Immortal Technique - Dance With the Devil

One year of blogging...

Dec. 12th, 2009   11:22 PM
Yay! Happy first birthday or anniversary...or whatever! ^^ It's been an entire year since I started blogging on LJ! [Created on 2008-12-12 03:07:54 (#17471267)]. It surprises me that I have 181 comments received and 236 comments posted considering I blabber about mundane things most of the time ~ they're nice to have but it's not something I strive for. I am, however, quite thankful for the friends I've made thus far! Getting to know you guys whether online or in real life has been an honour. I don't have a hell of a lot on here because I don't add or accept random people ~ so you guys are awesome! =3 And yes, I purposely made an entry earlier today so that this one marks my 300th! (even though I having a couple set to private and filtered XP) Just skimming through my blog really quickly (because I'd like to write a detailed 'year end' entry), here are the entries that stood out for me...I probably missed quite a few since I was just reading the subject headers... Hairs on my chinny chin chin *stroke stroke*

Dec. 12th, 2009   10:18 PM
Hiya... (Probably a bit odd to mention this, but it feels nice to start this entry with a greeting >>". Probably due to the fact that I haven't been on MSN, FA, or LJ very much in the last few days. Talking to people is important...no duh!) Anyways, lately I've been busy writing the report for my ANTC60 research study. Unlike my usual procrastinating self, this time around I can honestly say that I've been working very hard ~ I'm quite proud of that since I've never been so engaged to an assignment before. BUT the shear volume of data I have to go through along with how much I want to give voice to my participants has been an unexpectedly difficult task. I blame you guys; it's all your fault and I hate you all! (joking ^^;;) Suffice to say, I'm not the brightest Crayon in the box so I asked for an extension until Monday ~ which I am eternally grateful for getting. Thanks prof. M! You are the best! So far I have six pages (double spaced in MS Word) for the 'Findings and Analysis' section - which primarily is the bulk of where my research is going - but I'm expecting around eight pages in total. I haven't started the four sections prior to it (yeah I'm still way behind >>") but those mainly concern how the research was conducted since it's a fieldwork and methodologies course. My progress after the cut ~ pseudonyms have been used and personal information that can be linked to an individual have been altered for the purpose of confidentiality. Things can be changed if I see fit.

The word ‘furry’ is an adjective that can be defined as consisting of or resembling fur; it can also refer to an ideal state of mind for a particular group of people. For the latter, it is easier to describe what a furry is rather than to define it because, as my research suggests, responses within the group – also known as the furry fandom – can sometimes differ considerably. One aspect that many self-identified furries share is an interest in animals and anthropomorphism, the attribution of human characteristics to nonhuman entities. Although animals are the most common anthropomorphic characters, other hybrid or extinct species, mythological creatures, fictional monsters or aliens, and so forth. Even though children may have an interest in characters such as Mickey Mouse and Pokémon, they are not considered to be furries. This is because many cartoons are anthropomorphized for their appeal and so being a furry only applies to people who carry that interest when most others would have given it up. There are a diverse number of ways to express these interests; they generally involve the appreciation and enjoyment of art and literature, music, roleplaying, attending conventions and local gatherings, participating in online social networks, and costuming – also known as fursuiting. For many furries, the most common form of expression beyond the activities already mentioned is to have a fursona.

Fursona is another word that is best described rather than defined because it is not always applicable to all furries. A fursona can be described as the identity of a furry, idealized into an anthropomorphic character, which may embody real life traits or desirable qualities. How furries interact through their fursonas largely shape how they are perceived by one another. As an example, my fursona is a grey mouse named Dumpling. He is a representation of who I am as a furry and how I am known to other furries. I have attributed personal meanings that characterize and reflect why I identify myself as a mouse. For instance, mice are quiet animals that have proportionately large ears; in real life social settings I listen more than I talk. They are very small animals; compared to most people I usually feel small in stature and significance. We both share curious dispositions that can instigate enquiries. Although Dumpling largely reflects my real life personality, he also possesses qualities I desire such as contentment, youthfulness, and belonging.

Not all fursonas have symbolic meanings attached to character traits or personalities that reflect their owners – distinct physical, mental, and fictional aspects can make up the identity of a furry. Nirojia, for example, based his fursona on a fictional creature; he describes ‘it’ as ‘my’ correspondingly:

“My fursona is a feral houndour, non-anthro in body shape but still anthropomorphised to have human intelligence. A houndour is a dark/fire-type Pokémon dog. It’s not all that big, between knee and waist height on an adult. It has mostly black fur but the chest and muzzle are orange…And my eyes are blue, they aren’t in real life but I like the colour…Physically there’s not really any relation between us. Mentally we’re practically identical. I don’t try to be someone else as my fursona, it’s just a different name. The only real mental difference is online there’s not really any inhibitions so I can just do whatever, in that sense Nirojia is more me than myself.”

Although her fursona is not a Pokémon, StephKat identifies with them as a furry:

“I was very into Pokémon, and it’s still a huge preference in what anthros I enjoy looking at. Pokémon are in many ways similar to me – possessing human-level intelligence and emotions but finding it difficult to express without a perceptive and caring go-between who understand them; good at heart but highly vulnerable to the influence of their trainers, their good nature easily exploited and corrupted.

Besides being based on fictional aspects, a fursona can be dissimilar to the gender, age, and personality of their owners. There is greater freedom in the furry fandom over gender rules – the sex of a furry and the gender of their fursona can be different if that is how they personally identify themselves as. Within the fandom are self-identified transgender furries that can include overlapping categories of cross-dressers, transvestites, drag queens and kings, transsexuals, androgynes’ and intersexuals. This is less transparent when names do not immediately emanate masculine or feminine, and when appearances do not transpire from fursona to real life. For example, Ahau identifies his fursona as a hermaphrodite despite being born a male in real life. His behaviours are feminine and the shape of his iguana character is female with the addition of the male reproductive organ. People respond to him as a female when he interacts through his fursona, this is reflective of his desires to be female in real life. Furries that identify with their fursonas to a different age have their own categories within the fandom – babyfurs are the most commonly known group. There are different types of roles in this community, each focusing on different aspects of infantilism and ageplay, ranging from baby characters to caretakers. For Pup_Fluff, his fursona takes the form of a Border Collie pup who is playful, energetic, cute, incontinent, and dependant on others for support. By becoming his fursona a sense of comfort, security, and blissfulness can be attained. He relates these desires to having a rough childhood in real life; reasons may differ for other babyfurs.

Even though having a fursona is among the most common form of expressing a furries identity, the examples put forth so far only represent their respected owners. A fursona can be many things because individuals attach various meanings to their identity; there is no clear-cut explanation or definition of what it should be. From this diversity are a number of other ways a furry can express their interests in anthropomorphism. When asked whether it was possible to be more or less of a furry, respondents largely disagreed with how the question was asked. Many believed that becoming a furry was not a conscious choice but instead something that they felt they were. In other words, an individual either is or isn’t a furry based on whether they are or aren’t interest in anthropomorphism. Those who disagreed with the question related to how they could be more or less involved in furry activities and the fandom. Art and literature are among the predominant ways of engaging in furry activities. Art provides a way of showing how a furry conceptualizes their identity, while literature – or furry fiction – encompasses all genres in “what if” scenarios. It is easier to provide a visual reference of a fursona than it is to describe it in writing; for example my fursona (see Appendix). Many furries purchase commissions and produce stories as a way of engaging in the furry activities.

Art and literature have often being cited as the initial point of interest leading up to becoming a furry; these included references to Jim Henson’s Muppets, Disney movies, Warner Bros. and Hanna-Barbera cartoons, Japanese anime, Redwall, and Watership Down, among other things. To illustrate this connection, Tensai is a thirty year old artist and has identified himself as a furry for half those many years. As a child he had a fascination for werewolves but noticed early on that they were commonly depicted as creatures of malevolence in cartoons and stories. Wanting to see them in a different perspective, he began drawing them and other anthropomorphic characters in amusing situations. Although unaware of what furries were in his early teens, he had already created a fursona primarily to establish an identity on the Internet where he shared his drawings. A few years later he was labelled a furry by a friend who encountered his art, which subsequently led him to the fandom after some enquiry. The advent of the Internet is a common gateway for many furries in how they discover what their interests are categorized and characterized by. It is possible to be a furry without ever hearing the word or knowing about the fandom; but a furry of isolation is not always a desirable thing. For example, Finster does not consider himself to be an artist but he occasionally enjoys doodling cartoon animals. When he was in highschool he came across the furry fandom while browsing online for images to use as a reference. He appreciated the talent in the fandom and wanted to be a part of it. At that time of discovery he was also coming out to his parents about being a homosexual. How the two coincided, he states, “The acceptance of the fandom helped me come to terms with who I was. Being a part of it helped ease me into feeling not alone because of its openness and tolerance of different kinds of people.”

Although discovering the fandom has primarily been done online either accidentally or purposely, the feelings expressed with being a part of it can vary. The furry fandom is a term used to refer to a subculture composed of furries; it can create a sense of community with significant social meanings. It has occasionally been characterized as a safe setting for self-discovery and to become more comfortable with one’s own and others identity. This is because the fandom draws a broad spectrum of lifestyles where visibility is inherent among those least expected. For example, I have seen businessmen, professors, technicians, doctors and executives actively participating with people who identify themselves as Goths, nerds, otaku, stoners, and so forth. Some of these individuals are transgender, babyfur and homosexual, while others take an interest in sadomasochism, pregnancy, and voraephilia, among other things. Even though not everyone draws the same enjoyments in those aspects of the fandom, “there is a spot in the fandom for everyone no matter the type of interest – someone will share it”, to quote Ace_Wing. Ace_Wing considered himself a part of the fandom after his first convention in 2005; an accumulation of factors helped him feel as though he belonged. These include the initial encouragements he received to attend a convention, meeting friends he had only known through the Internet up until that point, seeing what people were capable of sharing such as artistry and fursuit performances, along with having an overall positive experience. Becoming a part of the fandom can be a gradual process with its own challenges, as was the case for Glory. Largely due to having an anti-social childhood while growing up around farms, he was very self-conscious with what being a furry meant. His initial exposures to the fandom through the Internet were shocking – he did not know how to handle the wide variety of interests that existed because he had never come across them before. As a result he removed himself from the fandom for a period. At present, he is much more comfortable with who he is despite the difficulties it can create in his marriage to a non-furry. He feels that his participation in the fandom is only limited to the things he cannot offer or afford to do; which can be confusing for his partner when adjustments in their relationship are required.

On an unrelated note, I'm having a lot of fun stroking the hairs on my chin! :D Just cause I'm Asian doesn't mean it takes me months to grow a few centimeters of facial hair! ><** Lastly, what the fuck... This looked kinda cool until Nicolas Cage showed his face. Don't get me wrong, I'm a Cage fan but this movie really doesn't suit his style of acting. Nothing about him fits the role. And Jay Baruchel doesn't look like a good character for an apprentice ~ he looks to dimwitted and old. The plot seems really cheesy too. At least the music in the trailer was good, ditto with the filming and some of the special effects =P.

We went our separate ways at around 6:30pm-ish. I went to a Second Cup to do homework before going home at 9:30pm-ish >>" (leave it to the token Asian to bring homework to a furday lol) This picture is made of awesome! XD

What sharp teeth you have...

Dec. 3rd, 2009   11:40 AM
(I'm not sure how I feel about writing this. There's only a small handful of people I know on a mutual level who read my blog, and another handful who I am less aware of that also read it. For example, I mentioned something on this blog and to a couple of friends, which eventually was brought up in a discussion from someone I wouldn't have thought would know. Granted it wasn't personal or something of negative consequences, but information that pertained to me none the less. Then again, there is the off chance that I may have forgotten where else or to whom else I may have told. And so I feel that there is an elevated level of concern about what I write. I may need to consider using more filters or keeping my entries friends only ~ until then, I'm fine with what consequences might eventually proceed.) "If you listen, you will learn a lot." This seemed to be the thing that stuck with me most after last nights furry dinner; which has also led me to change my impression about some people. People can have their own opinions and share them just fine, but when it comes to talking behind people's backs, that sense of concern comes creeping back again. No one likes being the center of discussion when they aren't around, but when you've got a reputation for doing stupid shit then you've pretty much earned it. While at the same time, some of these things come as a surprise to me because I just don't know the people being talked about very well or as well as I thought I did. I know that I am naive and rightfully so since I've only really been active in the local setting for several months. I'm not one to judge whether what people talk about is morally right or wrong, justified or uncalled for, speculative or definite. I'm just not sure how I feel about it. Every now and then I say that I feel awkward to being in certain situations, which really isn't the most descriptive adjective. Awkward was being a listener among other people's shared experiences which typically put down other people. If I had to convey it any other way, that awkwardness was a combined sense of guilt and pleasure. I would laugh along side those who did the talking, but I couldn't relate to them because I've never had their types of run-ins. Because of this I would feel some guilt for being a part of something that I didn't need to be involved in ~ which also made me feel sorry for the people who were talked about behind their backs. They've never done wrong with me and yet I've become grossly interested in what other people have to say. Had I not listened I wouldn't have to think differently about some people; and it bugs me because I don't necessarily want to think differently. The more you know, the less easier it becomes to be friends with everyone despite the nice face you put on in front of people. Do people talk behind other people's back because they feel that their opinions are shared, or to create awareness, or just to get it off their mind? Should I feel some sense of privilege for being allowed to listen in on discussions made by well informed and experienced people? Am I expected to learn from them and avoid those who've been talked about? And if I don't, am I perpetuating the reasons why those people get talked about? I want to distance myself from these types of things, but I have my own experiences and opinions as well ~ whether I've shared them purposely or accidentally, it still situates me in a position of involvement. I find it hard to judge myself. Only when it actually happens do I get a sense of how bad it is and then afterwards I just feel awkward. At least it has also helped reinforced certain friendships ~ I have a stronger sense of who can be trusted and I'm quite thankful I can talk to them about it on the same level of thought. And...I guess its also made me think about how inadequate and skin-deep my research study is =\. Anywhoozle, I don't want to end this entry on a down note so it's probably worth mentioning that I did have a great time last night. I got to know more of the familiar faces better, as well as meet some new ones ~ thanks for buying me a pint of beer LizzardBoy, I'll certainly return the favour someday ^^.

Cubbing it out...

Nov. 30th, 2009   9:24 PM
My Sunday was spent having a lot of fun over at Dan_Raccoon, Shiro, and Seifer's place ~ thanks for having me over! ^-^ I arrived in B-town a little before 2pm; Seifer was at the terminal not waiting for me. He said he was there for someone else - Xaxoqual, a fur I hadn't met before - but I kept him company anyways. I didn't really expect it would mean waiting in the frigid weather for an hour but whatever, I took the time to get to know him better :3. When Xaxo arrived it was quite surprising to learn he lives really close to me o__O ~ we ought to get together sometime! The day mostly consisted of video gaming, cuddling, tickling, roughhousing (Ruff is such a troublemaker =P), and nomming on yummy finger foods ~ everything a growing cub needs X3. I 'helped' Seifer with some of the cooking, which of course meant that I just took up space and watched him do all the work ^^;. Later into the evening Sol (who I met really briefly at last months ABDL munch) joined up with us while Xaxo, Seifer, and Dan retired for the night. We went out and picked-up some pizzas to fuel our sleepless night and while walking to and from the Pizza Pizza we got to watch the freight trains go by up close ~ it was pretty cool standing just meters apart from a gianormous moving train. Since I was staying over for the night, I got to get all snuggly in my padding, onesie, and sleeper! Good times is a sleepover with a bunch of other crinkly furs <3. The night seemed to go by relatively quickly probably because Dan and Seifer were up by around 6-7am o__O. I wasn't very tired physically, but because I'd been wearing my contacts for hours on end I had trouble keeping my eyes open ~ I eventually submitted to taking a short nap on Seifer's bed D: When I got up it was almost time for me to get ready to head home since I had an eye doctors appointment at 11am. Sol left as well since we were heading in the same direction. We just missed our 9am bus so we had to wait another forty minutes for the next ~ it was friggin' cold out >>". But again, no big deal since I got the time to know him bit better ~ quite an interesting person ^^. He lives with Kimba and the other East York (baby)furs, so we'll definitely hang out sometime :3. After my eye appointment I ate lunch, showered, and passed out for a couple of hours XD. My new cloth diapers came in the mail today as well ~ they fit comfortably well and look really cute! I don't plan on using them very often though since doing laundry is a hassle ^^;;

Life is golden...

Nov. 29th, 2009   12:16 AM
Noooooes! Robin Soderling isn't in the finals of the Barclays ATP World Tennis Finals! =( He lost to Juan Martin del Potro a few hours ago ~ 6-7(1), 6-3, 7-6(3). I didn't get to watch the match (or any of the tournament matches for that matter) but I've been following with the highlights and results. I'm really impressed with how Soderling has been playing considering at the start of 2008 he was ranked 52 and now he is 9. Technically he shouldn't even be playing in the ATP World Tour Finals since only the top 8 players are allowed in, but Andy Roddick withdrew three tournaments due to injuries. In any case, Soderling has been playing solid beating both Rafael Nadal (2) and Novak Djokovic (3) in this round robin tournament. Del Potro (5) will play Nikolay Davydenko (7) in the finals tomorrow. It should be a good match since Davydenko really stepped up today in the semi-finals against Roger Federer (1), breaking his twelve match losing streak. =================================== I <3 Torley. I've mentioned him one or two times in past entries and I check out his site occasional, but one of his recent videos really elevated my love for him! Damn! I had no idea he liked RZA let alone hip hop music. Freakin' awesome! His Thanksgiving video post is really inspirational as well =3. =================================== I don't want to go into detail about my furry research study mainly because I have to uphold confidentiality to those who participate, but so far things have been going well. I've always enjoyed picking at peoples brains and knowing why it is things are for them, but casually asking questions can be intrusive. So the interviews that I've done so far have been very fulfilling ~ especially when it's hardly a formal process. Learning about other people really broadens my perspective as well as adds value to meanings I might have taken for granted ~ somethings may not be as important to be, but they are to others and it's been really interesting and enjoyable to have that information shared to me. Thank you to those who have been or will be a part of my study, I greatly appreciate your responses! =================================== TAIL! (my hair was kinda fugly so I wore a cap >>") It arrived in the mail yesterday ^-^. I asked for it to be anywhere between three feet to three and a half feet ~ kinda regret not asking for it to be a bit shorter now, only cause the tip is a dirt magnet XD. Dammit, why couldn't I be just a little taller!?! Heh. Ah wells, I really like it and it's very well made (and machine washable :D) ~ I'll consider wearing it on Dec 6th. If you're interested in getting some a tail for yourself, look into commissioning Muddle_Designs! =================================== My excitement about listening to the new Blakroc album has twisted into a pretzel ~.~ *le sigh*. It's good but not great, which is disappointing considering it features some of the biggest names in hip-hop. For example, the very first track (Coochie - feat. Ludacris & Ol' Dirty Bastard) has great lyrics but it just doesn't flow right with the instramentals ~ plus, something about it just doesn't sound like it's a good track to open an album with. 'Tell Me Things - feat. RZA' lacks any lyrical depth, as though he was just improvising on the mic. Q-Tip does not sound good when he slows down his flow and goes all monotone >>" (on the track 'Hope You're Happy - feat. Billy Danze, Nikki Wray & Q-Tip'). But don't get me wrong, there are still some really solid songs on the album ~ my favourites being; 'On The Vista - feat. Mos Def', Stay Off The F***in' Flowers - feat. Raekwon', and the two tracks NOE is on ^^.

I hope the food isn't going to be too expensive ~ haven't been to a Jack Astor's in ages >>". Dec 5th - Got an invite from Dan & Kevy - they're having a potluck party! :3 Gawd knows what I'll bring since I can't cook for the life of me. I suppose I could bring either a salad or a dessert, or frozen food like meatballs or some kinda dumplings (I think people would prefer Italian over Asian XD). Whatever, diaper fun-ness ensured! Dec 6th - Furries Day Out #3. It comes earlier than usual next month! Hypr wants to have lunch at Toko, I might pass on that and catch up with the group afterwards since that restaurant kinda meh. Not to sure where we'll be bouncing around to (hopefully not Chinatown on Kensington Market ^^;;) but I'm definitely hoping there'll be new people to join the growing group! Dec 9th or 11th - ANTC60 research study is due. There was talk of an extension, so hopefully it'll be the 11th. I'm kinda nervous since it's really not that far from now T__T. Dec 18th - SOCB49 final exam. Blaaaah, it`s at 9am! ~.~ Kill me. Dec 19th - Hmmm, decisions decisions. The monthly ABDL munch or the Christmas party sponsored by Furnal Equinox? Pros and cons! The party is in a potluck style so that involves bringing food. I'll get to wear my new tail (pending it arrives before the event). I'll probably meet a bunch of furs that I've only gotten short opportunities to talk with. The munch has no RVSP so there could be a small turnout...or a really big one. There could even be a post-munch gathering someplace! >>" To many unknowns for the munch ~ it is more than likely I'll be going to the party =3. Dec 25th - Christmas! Woohoo! I love the ambiance of this holiday, but I really dislike the cold weather and any kind of Christmas music. Plus, giving and receiving presents is freakin' awesome! Although, I'll probably be a little stingy this year since I'm pretty broke .__."

*rolls a D6 to whore up some health*

Nov. 26th, 2009   2:33 PM

Blah...so far my week has felt so cluttered and full of 'stuff doing' - I don't really expect it to wind down much either. So far my research assignment is coming along okay; I've done two interviews (expecting three to five more later into the week), scheduled for two Skype interviews, and got back six questionnaires (while waiting on fifteen). Not particularly bad considering I only made cross-posts about it three days ago. I have a feeling it'll die down within the week if I don't bump the posts I've created on forums, but then again I don't necessarily want to be overwhelmed. I just feel more worn out than usual since I've had to think more ~ oh gawd, using my brain is so hard! >>" Oh wells, the sad part is I haven't gotten around to reading most of the responses (both in fieldnotes and questionnaires) so I've still got a lot of work ahead of me. Anywhoozle, other than the rain and gloomy weather, my Wednesday was pretty good. In the afternoon I bumped into Darcy while printing out some documents in the computer lab. I always had the impression she already finished school because of our age gap, but apparently not. Good luck with the full course load this and next semester, it really sucks that you're unable to keep your full-time job...gimme gimme gimme! XD I so want your job working for the city! D: It sounds so important-like, lol. We didn't get to talk nearly as much as I'd like to since I had to head to class. Nothing out of the ordinary happened in class - just the same old things to stress about in one form or another. After school I headed over to 's place to hangout ~ apparently it takes almost an hour and forty-five minutes to get there from school (I assumed it would only take an hour ~.~). Not long after I arrived RalphLupus showed up as well ^^. We headed over to Harvey's for a quick dinner ~ mmm delicious onion rings. Some time afterwards we played 'A Touch of Evil, The Supernatural Game' (a tabletop game). It was my first time playing so naturally the shit load of different decks and rules were hard to follow, but eventually I got the flow of it ~ it's fairly easy to pick up. I think the game took a little less than two hours, which isn't bad since we only had one major Villian on basic rules - advance rules would've taken longer to finish. Hells, I'd prefer this game over Risk XD. Oh, Happy Thanksgiving to all the folks south of the border! ^-^ I honestly can't recollect what I did on Thanksgiving (Oct 12th for Canadians) but I know it didn't involve any turkey.

🥱 bored  |  ♫ Post War Years - Soul Owl
Link

Looking for research participants

Nov. 23rd, 2009   2:55 PM

My research study has concluded ~ thank you to all who participated or showed interest in participating. (cross-post)

I am conducting a research study on ‘what it means to be a furry’ for a class project. I realize that there already exists a small amount of academic and non-academic research that consists of demographic and quantitative data (see Alex Osaki, UC Davis, David Rust, Kyle Evans, and Kathy Gerbasi). But I am interested in qualitative data, which relies less on numbers and more on subjective explanations and experiences. Because the fandom means different things to different people, I am interested in knowing what those meanings are, where they are made, and how they are reproduced.

I am looking for voluntary participants who are self-identified furries and over the age of 18. If you are interested, a consent form will be provided outlining your rights and how they will be maintained – it must be signed or emailed back to me. You can participate in one of two ways; 1) fill out a questionnaire, or 2) arrange an in-person (if you live around Toronto) or Skype interview with me.

To get started, please let me know (by email only: discourse-dumpling@hotmail.com) which approach you’d like to participate by as well as preliminary information about yourself:

- website from which you are reading this

- your furry name (or username)

- age, biological sex, self-identified gender, and location

- ethnicity

- spirituality/religion

- occupation

- education level

- relationship status

- fursuiter (full, partial, or none)

I will be happy to answer any questions you have. Also please spread the word about this study, it would be very much appreciated =3

♫ Rachmaninnoff - Piano Concerto No. 2 in C minor
Link

Munch of good times!

Nov. 22nd, 2009   10:39 AM
It was that time of month again, last night was the ABDL munch and it was wicked fun ^-^. Hellz I donno what to write without repeating myself from last months munch entry XD. I just always have a great time and it's definitely the one thing I look forward to every month. The turnout might have been one of the highest all year, there was well over fifteen people with a separate table to accommodate late comers. Most of the regulars where there (like Nebulus64, Diprguy_toronto, Dylan, Blondboy, Ricky and his daddy, Dan_raccoon, Seifer, BaBySB1, Lenny and probably a few others) as well as five or six other furs I'd never met and were first timers at the much. It was pretty funny as well because when I got to the pub, BaBySB1 and Nebu were already sitting at our regular spot, but the table next to them was occupied by a dude who we weren't sure if was one of us. We'd never seen him before so we assumed not. People trickled in and there must have already been seven or eight people with us before Ricky recognized the dude! Man do I feel bad about that ^^;; Anywhoozle, after the munch there was a gathering at Ricky and his daddy's place ~ the turnout for that was well over twenty people, I'm sure. I changed into my onesie and the Attends+Bambinos I already had on held well throughout the night. As per usual there were several others in really cute adult baby clothes and thick crinkly diapers ~ that's always the best way to party it out! Diaper_Police_TO brought a ridiculous board game that was next to impossible to play. I don't remember what it's called but you're suppose to answer questions or do silly things depending on what the cards read out ~ most of the questions no 8 year old could answer. A couple of us also got to play with Ricky's toys; mainly a train set and Diago Mega Bloks. That was a ton of fun especially since I've never gotten a chance to play with kids toys since growing out of them =(. There was a lot of assembly and destruction, but only cause Blondboy is a troublemaker XD. Omg and there was a ton of tickling ~ I swear it must have been contagious! I'm really ticklish so I had a lot going against me haha. There was a bunch of other fun stuff but it's mostly a blur to me now ~ I called it a night sometime around midnight and got home by 1am. Good times =3.
🐇 bouncy  |  ♫ Sublime - Santeria

Fur stuff...

Nov. 21st, 2009   10:57 AM
I will be looking for research participants soon! They must be self-identified furries, over the age of 18, and a consent form needs to be signed (or e-mailed stating they understand what the study is about). There are two ways to partake: 1) take the questionnaire, or 2) face-to-face, Skype, or instant messaging interview. Face-to-face or Skype will be more strongly sought after, but I won't bank on getting more than the other two modes of participating. I'll write up a more detailed breakdown after I've refined my questionnaire, since I had recently had the opportunity to test them in an interview. Just to give a broad sense of what my research assignment is about, here's a part of the Intro I had to hand in as part of the proposal:

Topic and Research Questions (Introduction):

This study intends to investigate the meanings behind being a furry. The word furry can refer to people who are interested in and/or identify themselves as an anthropomorphic animal often expressed in art, literature, roleplay, fursuiting, conventions, and online communities. In light of how the media has often negatively stereotyped furries as overly sexual costumers, most furries will agree that this is untrue along with other misconceptions such as it being a fetish and involving bestiality. It is important to recognize that the furry fandom – a subculture comprised of furries – means different things to different people; I am interested in knowing what these meanings are, where they are made, and how they are reproduced. More specific questions may include: how and when did you find the furry fandom? What animal(s) or creature(s) do you identify with? In what ways do you participate in the community? How important is being a furry in your social life? Answering these questions will provide a deeper understanding of what it means to be a furry contrary to popular perception. My motivation behind this study is brought forth by my own involvements in the fandom. I am a furry and I am interest in knowing more from others like myself.

==================================================== One thing I don't like about watching so many people on FA (and honestly, 340ish accounts excluding groups and double accounts really isn't that much compared to some other people) is the number of submissions and journals that accumulate if I don't visit the site for three or four days. I can't find the time to look over 606 new messages even if it's just a passing glimpse =\, heck some of the journals are pretty irrelevant to me anyways. Despite all this, I don't like nuking all the submissions in fear of missing something that sparks my interest. Blah. ==================================================== Eep! Dodge made a video of the Furries Day Out #2. Good gawd do I have squinty eyes...it's weird seeing myself ~.~** On his YouTube account he's also got a video of the Islington Furmeet #5.

*bother**bother**bother* :D

Nov. 18th, 2009   11:56 PM
Strange. The other night I felt an odd sense of detachment to websites I stopped going to in which I use to frequently visit ~ primarily the ABDL ones. I have never stopped being an ABDL and yet I've transitioned a lot of my online activities into the furry sphere. I wonder why o__O. Anyways, on that note, I have updated my RUPadded profile and sent out a bunch of friend requests to people I recognize; apparently the last picture I uploaded there is 576 days old. Why that site? I don't know, it seems smaller and easier to fit into than DiaperSpace (which I'll leave un-updated). I doubt I'll frequent any forums or make an attempt at writing stories any time soon ~ I'd rather just be a part of something and leave effort-making as minimal as possible. And onto todays happenstances. School was pretty baloney, but afterwards was much more fun. I went over to K-Dawg's place and we made pizza ^-^. The dough we bought was the Play-Doh kind so I got to "help out" by poking and squishing it ~ plus it was whole wheat which made it even stickier to handle. Yay for messiness! \o.o/ He didn't have any tomato sauce so we used a basil pesto, which surprisingly went well with pepperonis, mushrooms, olives, and lots of cheese. After "helping" with the dishes we got padded into Kendalls and cuddled to 'Over the Hedge', which for the most part we managed to watch all the way through. I've seen it before but it's still an awesome movie ~ I <3 Hammy! Zomg, I was really close to leaking on his bed! D: I'm such a leaky faucet; someday it'll get me into real trouble, I just now it >>". I got changed into a Depends before we headed out for ice cream ~ felt like I was wearing tissue paper .__." Sadly the place he wanted to go to had already closed so we went to Tim Hortons instead. There was a photo session going on in the corner with a couple dressed in wedding attire and a bunch of donuts stacked like a cake - not something you get to see every day =P. We wrapped up the night sometime after 10pm and he ZipCar'd me home ~ much appreciated for the ride and relaxing night! ^-^

Stuff of the day...

Nov. 16th, 2009   11:53 PM
- Ate lunch with K-Dawg since he's working around Shops at Don Mills, which is like a four minute drive from my house. All the restaurants were really pricey so we ate street meat XD. I find it so completely random that there is a hot dog stand in an upper-middle class shopping district ~ it was possibly the best street meat I've ever had. - I didn't want to resort to it but I transferred a grand from my TFSA to my daily chequing account *sigh*. My credit card balance is at three-hundred bucks and there's still a week and a half until the statement comes. So much for saving >>". - Dropped SOCB05 =.=* damn that course. Enrolled in PSYA02 (Intro to Psychology: Part 2), an online bird course that'll set me back six-hundred bucks .__." - Still contemplating whether or not to travel in January. I've told my mom about my situation and she's given me something to think about. From the looks of it right now, I'm leaning on not going =( But I'd like to pass out some resumes to see what other options I have in the mean time. - Finished the third season of The Venture Bros today ~ comedy gold! Episode ten (The Lepidopterists) is easily my favourite; I <3 Henchman 21 and 24. - Downloaded The Umbrella Academy: Dallas. I read the first series but got impatient with waiting for the second series so I eventually forgot about it. Well, half a year since its completion and I remembered! Better late then never, meh =\. I'll probably read it tomorrow while I pretend to do homework. - Which brings me to my last point. I've really got to get my ass started on my anthro research project .___. It's due in three weeks; I should allocate two of those into gathering data and one into doing the final write up. "I'll start tomorrow", says the procrastinator >>".

Link

*musicgasm*

Nov. 16th, 2009   10:28 AM
Oh my gawd...this made my jaw freakin' drop! Blakroc ~ easily gonna be a solid record! Comes out next Friday! The concept has so much soul and meaning; anyone who says hip hop is dead just stupid. Blues rock duo The Black Keys are respectable in their own genre of music (even though I've never heard of them...and some of their songs sound similar to The White Stripes) and I really hope they receive more acclaim for taking on this project. The album features a ton of big name artists ~ Raekwon, RZA, NOE, Jim Jones, Mos Def, Pharoahe Monch, Ludacris, Billy Danze, Q-Tip, and Nicole Wray. Zomg, I'm so excited! I highly encourage any music lover (even if you disklike rap) to watch some of the recording sessions either on YouTube or on the Blakroc site. Not only is the filming superb, but who honestly doesn't like watching behind the scenes action? =P Well, maybe I'm bias, but I really love the visible progression ~ the way things fall into place (after some video editing no doubt) is incredible. Plus I find it pretty funny that there's a bit of culture clash. Like the white dudes seem way more stone faced and working on the grind, while the black dudes are drinking, smoking, cursing, and having a good time. The status difference is really apparent to me (the black musicians have more fame and wealth), but the level of artistry, passion, and creativity is so fucking rich on both sides it's amazing. The way something like this comes together is so rare it really must be admired. Anyways, below is a session from the first webisode. I think each week gets better and better; especially week 9 when Pharoahe Monch is recording. Compared to some of the other rappers on the record, he isn't as famous, so you really get a sense of the pressure and difficulty pitted in writing lyrics.
🐇 bouncy  |  ♫ Murs And 9th Wonder - Love & Appreciate

Furries Day Out #2

Nov. 15th, 2009   10:04 PM
A good day but a tiring one at that ~.~" This time around there was eight of us to begin: Hypr, Ruff, JD, LT, Dodge, Danruk, TJ, and myself. It's nice to know that the crowd is getting larger each month, hope the trend continues :3. Ruff brought along his wolf hat, gloves, and tail which got a ton of looks and compliments throughout the day ~ especially since today was the Santa Clause Parade and a ton of families were downtown. One kid called him a squirrel while another called him a polar bear! XD. Ruff, Dodge, and I also sported our badges (wore my Marci) <3. We didn't really do anything that differed significantly from last month, which I suppose was a bit of a drag since it's the same-old-same. We ate lunch at Sky Dragon Chinese Restaurant in Chinatown ~ most of their unfamiliarity with how dim sum works made me lol. Being the token Asian, I also had to translate what some of the dishes were XD. Ah well, it's good to expand the culinary palette so kudos to those who enjoyed the meal. After wandering Kensington Market for a little bit, we met up with Bietrowolf (who, no offense, looked pretty blazed .__.) and then headed to Blue Banana, Sonic Boom, Honest Ed's, and The Beguiling. And then we headed to Steam Whistle Brewery and had freebie beer, where afterwards we met up with Ralph Lupus (D: his hair cut made me go zomg!). That's pretty much my outing experience in a nutshell. I went home for a dinner with family and relatives not long afterwards while everyone else got off at Dundas station and I presume went looking for a place to eat desserts. Don't get me wrong, I had A LOT of fun, but I'm just way tired so this entry is really dry =P. Oh, and today I learned that...pidgins go *pop!* o__O

FML

Nov. 12th, 2009   11:46 AM
I feel like a huge idiot and I don't think I can ever live it down.  I got a letter in the mail today from my school; it said that one of the courses I'm taking is an exclusion/equivalent to another course that I've already completed, thus even if I pass the course it won't count towards my GPA or credit count.  It's my fault for not reading the course calendar more carefully when I was enrolling.  I've talked with the registrations people and the academic advisers, there is no way around this.  I am short half a fucking credit to meet the degree requirement.  Mind you, this is just for the degree, I've already completed all of my program requirements, so the course I'm taking is just an elective for the credit. 

I've always known that my convocation would be in June, but I planned on finishing school by the end of this semester.  Now, if I want to graduate in June I have to take a course next semester for that half credit.  I am so fucking tired of school and to have to go through this shit right now...it feels like it will never end.  I'm going to drop the course because I don't need it, but I've wasted my time and money for nothing.  I don't know how to tell my parents either.  I've lead them and myself to believe that I'd be done in December.  I don't even know what to do about my trip in January.  I've been looking forward to it for so long, but now I don't deserve it.  I can't reward myself for something I haven't accomplished and it wouldn't feel right to travel while taking a course ~ and not because I'd miss a month of school, that I don't care much about.  Gawd...I feel so broken up inside.  
😞 depressed  |  ♫ Sondre Lerche - I Guess It's Gonna Rain Today
Link

Remembrance Day

Nov. 11th, 2009   8:44 PM
In Flanders Fields

In Flanders fields the poppies blow
Between the crosses, row on row,
That mark our place; and in the sky
The larks, still bravely singing, fly
Scarce heard amid the guns below.

We are the Dead. Short days ago
We lived, felt dawn, saw sunset glow,
Loved, and were loved, and now we lie
In Flanders fields.

Take up our quarrel with the foe:
To you from failing hands we throw
The torch; be yours to hold it high.
If ye break faith with us who die
We shall not sleep, though poppies grow
In Flanders fields.

— Lt.-Col. John McCrae (1872 - 1918)

I haven't read this poem since I was in grade school. 
May we never forget the sacrifices of those who have served and lost their lives in times of war. 
Thank you for the future.
😶 blank  |  ♫ Brother Ali - Picket Fence

=.=** School...and 63 more days...

Nov. 10th, 2009   12:38 AM

Blah...finished a sociology paper worth 35% of my grade.  I suppose I should be happy considering I won't have to stay up all night trying to finish it ~ amazingly, since I've procrastinated so much this weekend.  The paper was on gender inequality in reproductive labour and it couldn't be more than three pages.  Quite possibly the shortest essay I've written for such a huge chunk of my grade >>"  Which sucked because it meant that every sentence and citation had to be relevant to the topic - no room to fill the gaps with bullshit, which I'm so accustom to doing (much like everyone else I know *ahem*).  Anyways, despite all this, I won't get as much shut eye as I'd like to tonight.  I've got to read four or five articles for my anthropology class.  There's a small assignment due Wednesday where my group and I have to present a methodology.  Normally I'm okay with group projects, but this time around I've just been so unfocused and leaving things to last minute, I'm really hating myself for it.  No one likes being the deadbeat in the group so I've got to pull my weight tonight. 

At least I have something to look forward to ~ next Sunday the 15th is another Furries Day Out.  With the exception of the brewery tour, I doubt there'll be anything I'm not familiar with, which really isn't a bad thing considering it'll be more fun to hang out with furiends.  Plus there'll probably be four or five furs I haven't met yet, so all the more reason to go ^-^.  I ain't going back to Toko though if everyone decides on it...no thank you >>". 

Most of this month will consist of school assignments and some sweet social events.  But right now all I can think about is going to Japan (T_T)  Man...I just gotta get through these next two months and I'm in the clear!

Yay for me for pretending to study!  Yes, I studying on a Saturday night >>"  how uncool *sigh*

I distracted myself by watching Victor_Leo do a couple of sketches on LiveStream and chatting with other spectators.  He was doing lottos and I won a free doodle :3  I asked to be hugging him <3 


I will put up a link when he posts the pic on FA  ^-^   Check him out the next time he streams, they're a lot of fun when a good crowd gets together. 

[Edit: Pic has been uploaded ~ link!]
🙏 thankful  |  ♫ Justin Timberlake - SexyBack
Link

What's been happening...

Nov. 7th, 2009   10:48 AM
I find it irksome when people instant message me only to roleplay - what's more, when they contribute a very disproportionate amount =\.  Like this one dude who got my MSN from FA ~ makes no other conversation whatsoever other than to roleplay.  The first three or four times he wanted to play I said no because I was busy or not in the right mood.  I felt bad for saying no so often, especially having gotten to know nothing about him, so eventually we got a short roleplaying going and I swear it was like chatting with a brick wall .___."  I'd wait a little after sending something but got nothing until almost wrapping things up.  

Please don't be like that if you are going to roleplay with me ~ it isn't much fun if you were in my position.

=====================================================================

Possible strike at school on Monday...oh my.  CUPE 3902, Unit 3 is a union that represents probably a third of the teaching staff on campus and they want more monies o__O.  On Monday they'll be able to commence a legal strike and as far as I know one of my sociology classes will be affected.  Granted that class is boring as ever, but if it affects when I finish school then I ain't gonna be happy ~ reminiscent of the three month strike at York University...I feel so bad for my friends who go there >>".

=====================================================================

Parents are home from their three week vacation in and around China ~ time sure flew by.  Had to pick them up from the airport last night - never much fun because of bad traffic, somewhat confusing exit  ramps, and no parking by the pick-up area.  At least now I'll have food to eat that actually has flavour .___."  Getting tired of my grandparents' cooking...or take out food...or instant noodles.  It just kinda sucks that I didn't use the time when they were away to order stuff online *cough**cough*B4NS**cough*  I have no idea why I didn't and I'm still banging my head against the desk D:<  Oh wells, there will be next times.  It's nice to have more people in the house despite less privacy ~ plus they had a lot of fun and interesting experiences to share ^^. 

=====================================================================

I had lunch and went window shopping at STC with Jully yesterday ~ it was nice hanging out with her since we hadn't seen each other in a few weeks.  We ate at Boston Pizza (same restaurant, different location, oh how much business they get from us XD) cause I was craving pizza since the night before ~ I had planned to hang  out with K-Dawg for pizza and cuddles, but something came up :<.  Jully was looking to get a dress for her convocation in a few weeks ~ I'm gonna need to get her a gift soon!  I was a bit surprised to learn that she's getting more serious about her religion, Christianity.  It kinda makes things feel awkward since I'm not a fan of that subject but I support her decisions.  Plus it means she'll have to give up yaoi - which she's really into - and me not teasing her about homoerotic stuff >>".  All the best to her!

===================================================================== 

I got back my research proposal a few days ago and I suppose I should be happy with my mark (83%), considering how rushed and inadequately formulated I think it was.  The prof gave quite a few helpful suggestions and noted areas that could be refined a bit more, so I'll need incorporate those changes sometime.  Two things in particular that she raises are; "determining to what extent you think furries constitute a community (or communities) and in what ways" and the issue of ethics in exposing any links between individuals and their fursonas - "is there some expectation of privacy in the form of adopting a fursona to begin with".  

Anywhoozle, that's all for now ~ peace out.  
😑 blah  |  ♫ Lupe Fiasco - Switch Science Project

Nov. 5th, 2009   8:28 PM

"Remember, remember the 5th of November."
