Toronto November Furry Dinner...

Nov. 5th, 2009   1:34 PM
...was yesterday (got too tired last night to write this), and admittedly I didn't plan on going despite being aware that it was happening ~ but things just fall in the right places sometimes =P  It was pretty fun!

The day before had invited me to come over to his place after school just to hang out.  I didn't really feel up to it at first because I'd been stressing over a paper I had to finish, plus his boyfriend was going to be there and I didn't really want to get in their way.  Told him not to hold much expectations ~ if I came than surprise surprise, if not than whatever. 

When my class ended yesterday I was still feeling kinda borderline but eventually decided to go since I didn't exactly have anything planned out for the rest of the day other than relaxing.  When I got there I got to meet his boyfriend Ryalseth - he's a mallard duck, cool beans :D - who was a bit modest, but a nice guy overall ~ plus they look good together ^^.  We played a few games of Cribbage before he had to head back home.  Danruk hadn't really expected me to show up because I gave him an indecisive answer so he had planned on going to the Furry Dinner as a fall back, which was how I ended up going =P.   

The dinner was held at the Old Spaghetti Factory; but after years of service there, next months dinner will be elsewhere.  Fourteen furs turned up for the dinner - some of whom I had met at the Islington Furmeet but completely forgotten their names.  Despite sitting at the end of the table (which I try to avoid because conversations tend to become nil there) I got to meet Shane, Blindsight, Dodge, and Kat.  The other furs I know by name who attended are Danruk, Dan Skunk, Stew, Morgan, and Bungee Skunk ~ the rest I never got to meet or talk with.  I had a good time so chances are I'll probably make an effort to go to next months dinner ^-^. 
😴 tired  |  ♫ Jay-Z ft. Drake - Off That
Link

Rest in peace Claude Lévi-Strauss

Nov. 3rd, 2009   9:38 PM
Considered the father of modern anthropology, it was announced today that Claude Lévi-Strauss died at age 100 last Friday.  It's sad how I didn't know he was alive up until hearing about this ~ it's because his development of structural anthropology is so grounded in the discipline that when I learned about him in school, I had considered him a figure of the past.  Man, 100 years is a hell of a long time ~ what an accomplishment!  Thanks for contributing such influential thinking (and some countless pages of reading throughout my undergraduate); rest in peace. 
😢 sad  |  ♫ Lukestar - The Shade You Hide
Link

My Halloween weekend ^w^

Nov. 2nd, 2009   12:08 AM
This past Halloween weekend has been a really good one all thanks to , Shiro, and Seifer ~ you guys are awesome and thanks again for having me over!  ^-^  

Despite living in and around Toronto all my life, I have never used the GO Transit before >>".  So on Saturday I missed my bus and had to wait thirty minutes because I wasn't sure where the terminal was XP.  Ah wells, at least I'm more familiar now and the bus route I took was really frequent...as well as expensive ~ three times the price of a TTC token D: 

Anywhoozle, Shiro was waiting for me when I arrived a bit prior to 2pm and it was a really short walk back to his place.  Got to check out some of his completed pics and works in progress; very spiffy as always.  And I got lots of hugs from Seifer upon seeing him <3.  Dan got off from work not long after and we all watched a couple episodes of Family Guy on DVD ~ uncensored stuff is the greatest! ^^  Then Seifer pwn'd me in Brawl (T_T).  A little later Dan and I watched a couple episodes of The Muppets Show and Popples (mid-80s cartoon), we talked at length about fast food (which got me craving for some XD), and he showed me his gallery of badges :D  (some of which he hasn't posted online ~ you totally should sometime cause they're way awesome!). 

After a quick dinner at Wendy's, we got dressed in our costumes and headed for the local pub (Seifer in his fursuit, Shiro as a werewolf, and me as a penguin =P).  Lots of praise and snapshots went out to Seifer and rightfully so cause it was the best costume there, in my opinion.  Too bad almost everyone thought he was a tiger or a lion rather than the jaguar that he is o_O".  In one of his paws there is a vibrating massage thingy which was a big hit with the ladies XD ~ friggin' awesome!  He took first prize for best male costume despite coming close to being out best by Pocahontas (or should I say a grossly dressed up old guy >>").  Last year he lost to a dude dressed as a hick humping a sheep...kinda loco if you ask me.  Ah well, the bar thing was so-so for me since it was way crowded, hot, and loud from the karaoke ~ things I don't really enjoy =\.  But I didn't want be a downer cause it was Halloween, plus it was my fault for not ordering more than one drink XD.  (Not saying I encourage drinking to have a fun time o__O Nononono)  I think we headed home at around 1am.

Even though for most of the day I had been padded and received lots of tickles and scritches, I felt most blissful cubbing out at night in a thick Tena and Bambino, plastic pants, onesie, and sleeper {o'.'o}.  There's no other feeling like it especially when shared with friends ~ Shiro, Dan and I got comfy on the couch and watched Ratatouille :3.  I'd never seen it before and it was really awesome!  (Rémy: "Change is nature. The part we can influence. And it starts when we decide."  I <3 that lil squeaker)  Afterwards I got tucked in for the night, which was really sweet of Shiro!  ^-^

The next morning I mostly just layed on the couch pretending to sleep.  I do my best thinking in the morning so it was nice to have that private moment.  Dan had to go to work so I said my goodbyes to him ~ such a hard working coon!  A little while later I packed up some of my stuff and woke up Shiro.  Besides getting tickled, we watched some flash animations and talked a lot ~ it was nice getting to know him much better :3.  We woke Seifer a little after 2pm so that I could say goodbye and then it was off to catch my bus.  I got back home at around 4pm-ish ^^. 

Thanks for the fun time and I hope to see you guys again soon! :D
😌 content  |  ♫ Queens of the Stone Age - Medication
Link

I'm a penguin...

Oct. 30th, 2009   4:41 PM
...for Halloween =P.  Still a little mousie on the inside heh.


After getting my passport renewed I checked out three Wal-Marts, a Zellers (I didn't think they still existed but I drove past a brand new one), and two thrifty type stores.  All that was left were XL costumes, really cliche ones, and good ones that were missing stuff cause they'd been opened ~ I was tempted to go with either a cow or a beer keg XD.  I gave up and went to go buy milk at No Frills and amazingly enough there was a Halloween store right next door that I'd never known about (probably only a seasonal thing since the mall is really sketch).  They had quite a lot of great costumes - some even identical to the ones this site sells (which I was going to order from).  There was a doggy costume, but it cost $90.  My penguin costume cost $50 ~ where as if I bought it online it would have been $90 >>".  So that's awesome and I'm happy about it...but the killjoy was after I got gas the car battery died -.-*  I had to call my gramps to give me a boost who was, luckily, only a few blocks away.  Ah well, first time for everything, and at least now I know how to properly hook up jumper cables. 
✨ chipper  |  ♫ Saigon - L.O.V.E.

End of the night entry...

Oct. 29th, 2009   11:44 PM
Tired but feeling good ~ got back from the gym a little while ago.  I've only been going once a week lately which kinda sucks because exercising really helps lift my mood.  The rest of the week I feel rather blah, so I really ought to work out more often.  Just some final thoughts before I call it a night...

- I did my coffee maintenance runs today and I'm getting much quicker at it (hopefully not cause I left something out ^^;;).  I don't get paid but I helped myself to a French Vanilla and a Mochaccino.  I've noticed that strong coffee messes with my appetite (since the machines actually grind and brew the beans on the spot and mochaccinos are one third espresso).  I think I'd better stick with tea >>".

- o__O I've only bought one commission this month (the $10 bust).  There has been quite a lot of temptations to get more, but this month has been really expensive (mainly because of the new contacts) so I'm happy that I didn't give in.  Maybe...probably...December will be a commission-ful month.  

- On Halloween I'll be hanging out with and sleeping over at
dan_raccoon
, Shiro, and Seifer's place ~ very much looking forward to that.  I was looking into getting a costume the other day (cause we'll be hitting up costume contest at a bar) and saw this one ~ Cat in the Hat!  Awesome-sauce!  I didn't mind so much about the price, but express delivery would cost $20...no way am I playing that especially since they're local.  But they aren't local enough that I'd feel comfortable driving way the hell south-east to do a pick-up (I'm not to confident with my sense of direction when it comes to driving >>" blah).  Gonna try to look for another costume tomorrow.

- Le sigh, more monies leaving my wallet when I renew my passport tomorrow. 

- LOL, yesterdays College Humor - Hardly Working video.  Just cause I watched a couple of Woody Allen flicks recently XD.

Kiss and blog #1

Oct. 29th, 2009   2:04 AM
When I wrote in my last entry that and I cuddled for a while, it was quite the understatement.  We cuddled, kissed a lot, got naked, and for the first time in my life I received anal, got my dick sucked, and showered with another man.  It was new and exciting to me because in recent months I've really been enjoying and exploring my sexual preferences.  In the past I've been fingered, I've been jerked off, and I've jerked off other people ~ I now have more on that list ^^.  Anal felt pretty good even though at first it hurt a bit because I was kinda tight ~ once the momentum got going it was alright.  I find that I'm very particular with the way I like things, which can sometimes offset the mood for others.  For example, I don't get hard from regular porn/sex.  When my butt was getting fucked I wasn't hard - which to me feels like I'm giving someone a slap in the face.  I enjoy making other people happy - whether by masturbation or sex - but it don't like how it doesn't turn me on very often...it feels selfish or hypocritical or something >>".  I got hard when he sucked my dick, but I couldn't come because it didn't consistently feel good ~ sometimes it tickled and sometimes it hurt.  I get pretty turned on when diapers are involved, but that isn't something for everyone so that gets left out for the most part.  I guess in sum I feel like a phony bisexual since I don't do blowjobs, I can't come when getting a blowjob, I'll receive anal but won't get a hard on, yet I love the affection from being in bed with someone and I like satisfying people.  Le sigh.

Just as an odd side note: I got rather gassy afterwards.  He came inside of me and with every fart it felt like a wet one >>" (yeah..ew...).
Link

Kiss and blog? Nah...

Oct. 29th, 2009   12:54 AM
Hmmm...I've never put much thought into filtering my entries since what I write - for the most part - is in the truest sense of who I am and how I want to be perceived.  I don't force anyone to read what I write let alone care very much about the number of page views or comments given.  I use tags as fittingly as I can not just to categorize my entries but to establish some sensibility to what you might expect to read; whether it is something you'd like to or not.  But when it comes down to it, there are certain things I want to write about that will come at the price of my own self-respect.  I'd feel more reassured if I filtered the things I did behind closed doors (and I don't really mean the ABDL/Babyfur stuff).  By doing so the people who would read those entries would be doing it because they wanted to and not just because it was there to begin with.  I donno...my thoughts right now are just puzzling to me because I love blogging and I want to maintain the ideals of this blog by not using fliters, but I don't want to lose face, so to speak.  I believe in trying new things at least once when it's within a certain level of comfort, but until then I think I'll write and keep them as private records for myself. 

Anyways!  I got a haircut today (Wednesday) and boy did it feel like a rip off.  My dad normally does it for me (shuddup! >< it's cheaper that way!) since it's way easy ~ a 1-2-3-4 with the shaver thingy.  But seeing as he isn't around I went to Super Cuts and it cost $16.50 (plus a $1.50 tip) for fifteen minutes of labour.  o__O Good gawd man, I know the place is at the bottom of the ladder in terms of hair salons, but that's still pretty pricey in my books!  I was really expecting $8-10 =.=**  Blah, I'm such a cheap ass when it comes to the essential things I need in life and yet I rarely complain when it's for leisure.  My anthro class in the afternoon went pretty well ~ I absolutely love my prof for making me think about relavent things I'd normally only dabble over.  These types of teachers honestly deserve high praise for being so passionate and engaged to their work, and to be able to share that knowledge.

Afterwards I went to danruk 's place where I got to meet his roomie Jimi ~ I think musicians and artists are some of the coolest people ever!  He beat me twice in Cribbage X_x but they were close games.  I'm gonna have to consider getting familiar with the rules and point system just so I can teach other people how to play ~ it's a lot of fun!  I helped name his kangaroo plushie Raspberry, as in the tongue blowing sound =P.  When Jimi went out, we cuddled for a while :3  Later we walked to Young Thailand Restaurant for dinner.  The prices were a tiny bit above average and the proportions were kinda small ~ it was quite tasty none the less.  Next time we're getting pizza! XD  While eating he shared his story of how he came to identify himself with his fursona; long story but an amazing one at that.  He had this really awesome statement about 'being your character vs. becoming it' which was rather profound ~ I remember what he said, but to spell it out would be to much of a task to ask of me >>".  When we got back to his place, we played another close game of Cribbage ~ I won that time making our record 2-2 for now.  Headed home not long after that ^^.
😴 tired  |  ♫ Mickey Factz - The Inspiration
Link

Blah school...looking forward to the end of it!

Oct. 27th, 2009   11:38 PM
Le sigh...I handed in my anthropology research proposal on Monday and I still feel embarrassed for submitting something that wasn't to the best of my abilities.  I know I could have done so much better on it had I been more focused on Sunday ~ another way of saying I procrastinated >>".  I literally worked until the last minutes before it was due and although it was only missing one annotation, certain sections could have been way more thought out.  I'm just not impressed with myself for putting a part of my life on paper - something that I'm whole heartedly into - and knowing that had I not messed up, I could have done a better job.  It's stupid to me because I know that anything in anthropology requires time to think through the concepts and methodology, especially in research.  Ideas for these kinds of things don't come to me when I'm under pressure; I guess I was to sure of myself this time.

Well, this might sound like a deadbeat talking, but I really don't care about the marks I get for any of my classes ~ so long as I'm passing then I'm fine with whatever.  The only reason I'm taking these classes are for the one and a half credits I need to graduate.  Marks have never been a big deal to me since I've gotten use to being an average kind of student.  And besides, I doubt I'll land a job that requires looking at my transcript...I hope >>". 

Anywhoozle, today was a really long day ~ eight hours at school plus two hours of commuting.  I had a sociology test worth 20% of my grade in the morning, I think I did okay.  I got back an assignment in the afternoon which I didn't do so great partly because I lost some marks for handing it in a day late ~ luckily its only worth 15%...luckily >>".  But my midterm grade for that course was above the class average so that's nice and peachy. 

Tomorrow I'm gonna try to wake early so that I can get a haircut.  I've never been to a Super Cut, they sound really amature-ish, but I doubt you can screw up when most of your hair is getting shaved.  After class I'm gonna go hangout at 's place; not really sure what to expect but it should be nice and comforting.  Sometime Thursday or Friday I need to get my passport renewed (long line-up ensured) and do my coffee machine maintenance.  Maybe find some time to meet up with friends I haven't seen in a long while ~ starting to miss them *sad face*.  Still working out the details for my Halloween ~ looks to be a sleepover at Shiro and dan_raccoon's place while hanging out with Seifer before hand :3.  I'd very much like to cub out a little so I hope things work out! 
😑 blah  |  ♫ Print (of Good Day, Good Night) - Last Man Standing
Link

Islington Furmeet #5

Oct. 25th, 2009   10:56 AM
The Islington Furmeet yesterday was a lot of fun ~ thanks to all the new and familiar furs I met for making it awesome!  ^-^  I honestly cannot remember the names of a lot of furs just because there were so many there and I only got to strike up a short conversation with some of them; so I won't tease my brain by attempting to recollect everything ^^;;  No hard feelings to those who've slipped my mind!  But honourable mentions go out to...

- Me (lol) cause I showed some pride in representing my babyfur self by wearing my Shiro, Marci, and Spiffy badges!  Either babyfurs are accepted and no big deal or others were just being polite, I do not know.  But whatever =P lots of people said my badges were cute {o'.'o}.
- Kin Nakajima; we've chatted on MSN for a couple of months so it was nice to finally meet him!  Thanks for trying to find me amongst the crowd even though I ended up bumping into you by the dealers table =P.  Other babyfurs I got to meet were mimiBadger and another whose name starts with 'S' but I can't remember the rest >>".
- Lots of cuddles (<3) from Shiro, Seifer, and Danruk while watching Silent Hill, Army of Darkness, and Van Helsing
- Got to meet Peromyscus ~ besides his fursuit he also wore a hilarious Big Dollar Daddy costume, comedy gold!
- Danruk taught me how to play Cribbage (a card game) and I beat him in my first game! :D  Very fun but there are lots of different combination of cards you can make to get points, so remembering them all and being observant is something I'll need to work on.  Hopefully next time I'll get to play with everyone else ~ Jacob, Ruff, Lupus, among others.
- All the fursuiters (Babs, Kitty, Howiepup, Cornwall and so many more), host and staff of the furmeet because they're awesome!
😌 content  |  ♫ Sharam ft. Kid Cudi - She Came Along
Link

It's an entry! D:

Oct. 24th, 2009   10:14 AM
"Awoooooooo!"  *bouncy*  =3  I watched 'Where the Wild Things Are' last night (Friday).  It was really heartfelt because it gets at what it's like to be a kid ~ complex.  There were moments of silliness, lots of imagination, raw honesty, darkness, and uncertainty - words have trouble describing it all.  I'm really happy I saw it and I wouldn't mind seeing it again.  But on the other hand it also shook me up a little because of my ABDL/Babyfur side.  I yearn to be immature and child-like but in a very selective way (innocent, care-free, playful, la-dee-da); to see the wild, angry, and sad aspects of childhood were different and powerful.  I guess I just find it even more incredible how kids experience things so differently because they're always in a processes learning ~ I take for granted that the things I have control over.

(I just had to get that off my chest while I'm still feeling good from the movie.  Now onto the rest of this entry.)

===============================================================

I haven't made an entry in a few days and although it feels a bit weird not to (since its become somewhat of a routine) I fear that if I continue this slump for too long it'll become permanent ~ its happen in the past for other things.  The last few days have been unstimulating but when you add up the little things its been a week I can't complain about.  

On Wednesday in anthro class we watched a very fitting film for the course - some might find it boring but I really enjoyed it.  'Kitchen Stories' (or Salmer fra kjøkkenet) is about researchers studying kitchen habits during the post war period.  The researchers essentially observe their hosts' activities from a tall chair in the corner of a kitchen; supposedly unobtrusive to their hosts behaviours.  The movie focuses on a researcher and his host (an old grumpy geezer)' the progression in their relationship is funny and fundamentally truthful, while the ending is sad and beautiful.  This is a very awesome movie and I highly recommend it for any social researchers out there.  Just be aware that it's Swedish so you'll probably need subtitles.   

Thursday morning was an expensive way to start the day - my VISA statement came in the mail (a little over $100) and I had to write a cheque to some furnace cleaning guy who comes annual (-$84 :<).  My parents will pay me back for the cleaner, but it still sucks that I'm getting broke.  In the afternoon I had to help my dad service his coffee machines at two sites ~ he's vacationing in China for three weeks so each week I have a couple hours of 'work'.  I've gotten familiar with how to do maintenance on the machines, but it's so boring and unrewarding.  It kind of boggles me why my dad decided to get into this business since it pays crumbs (not even peanuts >>").  

On Friday I had lunch with K-Dawg since he found a temporary job really close to where I live.  We ate at Tako Sushi which I'll probably avoid during the lunch time since the service wasn't as good as the many times I've been there for dinner.  In the afternoon I had an eye doctors appointment downtown, mostly just to measure my curvature for the contacts I'm getting ($200 out of my wallet T_T).  The doc said she could tell that I've been wearing contacts for a few years now because there are signs of oxygen deprivation ~ not a good thing in the long run, but I'm not a big fan of glasses.  At 6:30pm I met up with K-Dawg again and we watched 'Where the Wild Things Are' at Varsity ~ the movie didn't start until a quarter-till so I was really antsy and bothersome to him =3.  Afterwards we went to Gourmet Burger Co. but I can't really tell you how good their burgers are because I ordered mine with all the complimentary toppings XD ~ lettuce, tomatoes, sautéed mushrooms, balsamic marinated caramelized onions, red onions, roasted red peppers, beets, avocado, jalapeños, pickles, and relish!  Their sweet potato fries and beer battered onion rings are delish!  Later we went back to his place and watched some YouTube (fuk, can't get that Jesus or duck song out of my head!), a few episodes of Wonder Showzen, and a bit of cuddles. 

Today is the Islongton Furmeet!  It's from 12pm-2am but I'll probably get their by 5pm and stay till midnight at the very latest.  I'm pretty excited to meet some new people, but I'm a bit iffy about wearing my badges since they're babyfur o.o"  It's a good guess that there'll be over fifty people there and I only know of four babyfurs that'll be there ~ hrmmmmmm.

Tomorrow I'm going to have to work my ass off because my anthropology research proposal is due the day after (originally due last Wedn but it got extended ~ and I've made such good use of my time *sarcasm*).  I've been trading e-mails with the prof and she's been super supportive by suggesting some readings I'd never thought to look for.  I'll write more on this when I get around to it.  On Monday, I'll have to study like crazy for a sociology test I have the day after ~ it's worth 20% of my grade and the last test I did wasn't so great.  There's a study group session going on, but I doubt I'll go since I'll either be finishing my proposal or I'll be unprepared and unable to contribute anything.  Anywhoozle, I'd better get cracking on my assignment before I begin to enjoy the rest of my day.

The final word is...commissions!

:D  punchy punchy!  Other than the gianormous jelly bean nose, I really like how this pic came out.  I'm actually quite interested in getting another bust done by since they're only$10 and the quality is superb ^^, the next one would be less serious looking though =P.


Zomg!  It's the Marci badge that I've been waiting nearly a month for!  I got it in the mail yesterday morning and she e-mailed me a scan in the afternoon ~ kinda odd how that works o.O"  I should have asked for bigger ears or to have the tail upright when I had the chance (since it kinda looks like a cat if you didn't know I was a mouse), but I still really love this <3  Hmmm, wear this at the furmeet?  =3
🐇 bouncy  |  ♫ The Mars Volta - Cotopaxi
Link

Furry Meme

Oct. 19th, 2009   11:08 PM

I may consider using some of these questions if my anthro research assignment gets the green light ~ the idea is there, but some of the wording can be tidied up.  Anywhoozle, this meme was a nice way to put some of my personal involvements into perspective and how it has affected me.  Give it a go if you're interested ~ many others in the last week or two already have!

=========================================

Are you a furry?
I identify myself as a furry and I do furry related things, but I don’t consider it my lifestyle ~ it is one aspect of my life.

If you have a spouse or S/O - Is he or she a furry too?
I am single but if I was with somebody I would be fine if he or she was or wasn’t a furry. 

How long have you been in the community?
Since early 2006.

How did you find furry?
I found furries – more specifically babyfurs – from links on ABDL sites many years ago.  A few years after that I took an interest in becoming one through Second Life.  

What's your reason for being a furry?  What interested you to get into the community?
A majority of the things I’m interested in comes from satisfying my curiosity – how I got into the community is no different.  Reasons for why I remain a furry is because of the friends I’ve made, the wicked art, and because in general it just makes me feel good. 

What's ‘furry’ to you?
I guess the first things that come to mind are ‘sex’, ‘drama’, ‘identity’, and ‘art’.  But in terms of the textbook definition it would be an interest in anthropomorphic animals that serve as representations for an individual or an idea.

If someone found out you’re a furry and asked you about it, how would you respond?
I would tell them enough to know what is a furry and why I am a furry.  If that person wanted to know more due to curiosity and not to be judgemental then I’m fine with them furthering the discussion.  I know who I am and I’m quite comfortable sharing aspects of my life if someone asks, but I would never bring it up otherwise. 

What are your favourite aspects of our community?
The diversity and creativity are my favourite aspects of the community.  I enjoy the fact that there are people from all walks of life brought together (but not restricted) to our shared interest of being a furry.  The diversity helps me maintain an open-mind on life while giving me new things to learn and experience.  The creativity (especially in the form of art) keeps things fresh and tangible since a picture is worth a thousand words.   

Do you use any furry terms? (i.e. yiff, paws, murr?)
I sometimes start off with a *squeak* when I write comments or shouts on FA, but other than that I only use furry terms when I roleplay.

What do you wish furry was NOT associated with?
Stupidity…there is a lot of it out there ranging from immoral decisions to immature comments some people are capable of. 

How strongly do you feel about anyone bashing you yourself for your interest based on the media's aspect of what furry is?
It hasn’t happened to me yet, but it wouldn’t concern me very much since I know the media’s portrayal – what little coverage we get – doesn’t represent me.  I would make an effort to stand up for what I believe in, but it wouldn’t be something of a mission. 

How strongly do you feel about someone bashing the community as a whole?
There’s rarely ever good reason to bash a community as a whole, because misconceptions don’t represent every individual in the community.  I would feel pretty strongly against it but I would leave it to others to defend the community ~ there will always be haters and I will give none of my time for them.

We all know furries have a lot of sexual aspects in this fandom, what’s your opinion on it?
I think that the copious amount of sexual content is due to a predominantly male fandom.  Males seem to be more sex-driven no matter what the kink is.  I’m not complaining; it just means more variety of things for me to get turned on by. 

PERSONAL
What is/are your fursona(s)?
I’m Dumpling, a grey (babyfur) mouse of no specific species.  My fursona and I, more or less, share the same personality.  The main reasons I identify myself with a mouse is because:

1) they are small animals – in real life feel small as a person (both small in stature regard)

2) they have big ears – I’m more of a listener than a talker

3) they are very curious – I’m inquisitive

Do you have any fetishes that pertain to the fandom?
I have a diaper fetish that pertains but isn’t limited to the fandom.  Related to diapers, I have a desire to be infantile (which doesn’t turn me on) because it provides me great comfort and joy.  Light bondage and humiliation turn me on, but I don’t consider it a fetish of mine. 

If you could magically morph into your fursona would you?
I would if more than half the world were also living their fursonas ~ I don’t want to draw attention to myself by being the only one.  Or, I would morph into my fursona if it were reversible at will. 

Do you believe you have a spirit animal?
Nope. 

What other fanbases/groups are you a part of?
I’m a part of the ABDL community and I am a fan of anime, comic books, online games, and hip hop. 

Anthro/Feral, Therian or Kemonomimi (Not a furry, just a human with animal ears and a tail)?
Anthro.

Are you a fursuiter? If yes, how many do you own? If No, do you ever plan on being a suiter?
I’m not a fursuiter but I do plan on being one when I move out and I’m financially stable ~ it won’t be a priority for a long while. 

How many of your friends are furry?
About 50% of my friends are furries. 

How many pets do you own, if any?
I don’t own any pets.

Aside from furry what other interests do you have?
I’m interested in most racquet sports, cooking, traveling, social media, blogging, indie music, discourse analysis, a whole ton of stuff. 

Are you confident enough in yourself to say that you're a fur, no matter what the media says?
I am very confident.

MEET-UPS
Do you attend any cons? If no, would you attend any cons? If yes, which cons have you been to?
So far I have only attended anime and comic book conventions.  I hope to attend some furcons next year when a couple of new ones will be held around my area (Furnal Equinox, What The Fur, and Bytown Furry Con).

Do you go to local meetups (bowling, public outings...etc)?
I go to a monthly ABDL munch where there are a couple of babyfurs and I’ve been to furry outings where we explore the city for a day.   

Have you ever attended a furry party? If no, would you attend one?
Not yet, but after this coming Saturday’s Islington Furmeet I will have.

ONLINE
Have you ever met up with a fur you talked to online?
Yes I have and I hope to meet up with more sometime in the future.

What furry websites do you attend?
Mostly FA, sometimes ontariofurries.ca

What non furry websites do you frequent?
LiveJournal, YouTube, TED, FMyLife, and a couple of webcomics. 

Has the fandom done anything for you that you're thankful for?  Has it taught you anything or brought you anything you treasure greatly?
I am thankful for everything the fandom has done for me so far.  Besides keeping me not bored of life on a daily basis, it has helped broaden my social life ~ fun people and interesting conversations are very important to me.  I have a much greater appreciation for art and Internet humour as well.


🥱 bored  |  ♫ Cage The Elephant - Ain't No Rest For The Wicked
Link

Munch night :3

Oct. 18th, 2009   12:08 PM
(The munch was yesterday night but I was getting sleepy halfway through this entry)

It felt so good to go to a munch after having missed the last two ~ I had a really fun time!  ^^

Surprisingly I came on time and was the first to arrive.  Minutes feel a lot longer when you're sitting by yourself in a row of tables >>".  Luckily Matthew popped in not long ago (he recently moved so he lives really close to the pub) and we had a good chat going :3  Sometimes I really love the age differences from the people I meet because I get to hear a lot of cool life experiences ~ like hearing about his trip to Thailand many years ago, which sparked after I told him I was traveling to Japan.  A little while later Dylan arrived, followed by
dan_raccoon
, 
orca_and_mommy
, Shiro, and Seifer.  We were also joined by a new person who I've never met, I'll call him Lenny in this blog, and the famous boyricky :D  

I thought that there was a lot of great conversations ~ something thats really been lacking in my day-to-day since I only go to school for two days and the rest of the week I ferment at home.  The munch really took a lot of stress off my chest so it was comforting to be myself more :3.  Plus, after eating noodles for a countless number of days (because I can't cook for the life of me), it was really satisfying to have pizza and beer!  I got to sit in between two artists D:  Lenny showed a bit of his portfolio he did to get into art school with a scholarship - yes, it was that good ^-^ - and Shiro showed off his collection of works in progress ~ he's got a really wicked three page comic in the works!  Zomg!  And Shiro began sketching a picture for me!  It was way generous of him {o'.'o}.

After the munch everyone was invited to Ricky's place for a little gathering ~ way fun ^^.  He cautioned us though, that we shouldn't whip off our pants as per usual because he also had friends over ~ I wasn't too sure to what extent they knew about AB/DL's but they were really friendly.  Before heading over we all took a pit stop at Matthew's new place to pick up some wine; he's gotten brand new furnishing so everything is coming together really nicely.  At Ricky's place, as always, I was really astonished at his room ~ it's freakin' awesome and perfect for any (grown-up) little boy.  He made a new video (that's posted on his blog) and it is hilarious!  The little dancing alien totally takes the cake XD.  A bunch of other ABDL's whom I've met before trickled in as the night progressed ~ can't give them all pseudonyms or else I'd have a long list to remember @_@. 

There are plans in the works for an ABDL gathering up at Ricky and his daddy's farm sometime during the winter.  I'm not a big fan of cold weather, but it sounds like a lot of fun and I hope it works out :D.  And hopefully something can get planned out for a furry (Harry Potter Marathon) sleepover at Dan and Shiro's place sometime this month or the next ^^.  But until then, Islington Furmeet is next on my calendar...next two studying these next two days for a midterm on Tuesday >>".

The awesome sketch by Shiro!  Thanks again dude :D

Nuuuuu!  Stop looking!  T_T  *scurries away crinkling after transforming*  (At least it wasn't a pic involving any special cookies XD)

Update on my boring life .___.

Oct. 17th, 2009   12:28 AM
Where do my days go I wonder...

Thursday was really slow and drawn out because I didn't get much sleep the night before.  I had to drive my parents to the airport for their departure to Shanghai ~ they're vacationing around China for three weeks.  Driving while your heart is pumping twice as slow is a bad idea - I nearly switched lanes into a van while on the highway >>".  Afterwards I went to get my passport photo taken at a Wal-Mart; it cost $17...ridiculous.  And in the afternoon I went and got my prescription taken by an eye doctor ~ they haven't gotten worse (surprisingly since I stay at the monitor for hours on end) but I'm being tested for glaucoma.  I didn't do much of anything other than taking a nap and playing TF2 when I got home.   

Today I woke early at around the time my brother leaves the house.  I fell right back asleep after getting padded and putting on my sleeper ~ one of the most relaxing feeling I've had in a while.  I got up again at noon and after having lunch I did a lot of nothing on the computer.  My anthro prof sent me an e-mail a few days ago with a really interesting link, 'The Virtual Campfire: An Ethnography of Online Social Networking', so I read a bit of that and it gave me something to think about for my own research proposal.  My prof is still figuring out the ethical implications of doing student fieldwork on online communities, so really my ideas depend entirely on her okay.  I replied back with a long e-mail stating why I wanted to research furries, that I was one and had access to possible informants, and some issues I might have besides the ethics.  She's a cool prof so I trust it didn't come off as something utterly bizarre, but I could be wrong ~ in any case I won't ever have to see her again after December. 

I've been looking into booking hostels for my trip starting with the ones in Tokyo.  I'm split between two hostels.  According to hostelworld.com, K's House is the highest rated, 90%, with 144 reviews given.  On the other hand, Khaosan Ninja has a 85% rating with 533 reviews given.  At K's House it costs $33 to sleep in an 8 bed mixed dorm (essentially four bunk beds, unsure whether they have curtains).  At Khaosan it costs $33 to sleep in a cabin-bed in a room with fifteen others ~ I think the cabins are really awesome since there's a lockable slide door, wifi, and inside each are outlets, a light, and hangers.  They are both located around the same area.  My brother has stayed at K's House and he recommends it.  I don't know which to pick ~ cool cabins and high number of reviews, or Tokyo's top pick hostel that my bro recommends.  I suppose I could trouble myself with booking both since I'm staying in Tokyo from the 12th-21st of January and 7th-11th of February. 

Not long ago I watched Antichrist ~ quite an uncomfortable movie to sit through.  The filming and editing are beautiful, and the acting is quite fitting with the characters.  But there's a lot of graphic violence and sex.  I didn't really understand the plot either; despite there only being two characters throughout the entire movie ~ the things the woman does is disorderly.  I wouldn't watch it again to try and figure out the meanings of the movie ~ this guy does an interesting job describing it, but don't watch his review if you don't like spoilers. 

I really want to watch Where the Wild Things Are!  But my bro is using the car all this week end so I can't really call out anyone without having to take public transit >>".  I have no recollection of the book while growing up ~ whether it was read to me or if I've read it myself.  I'm really surprised that the book only has ten sentences in it o__O  It seems a lot longer especially since this motion-picture book is almost seven minutes long.  Anyone else think Max's wolf pajamas looks awesome?  Man, I really want to watch it soon ~ it looks so much fun! 
😑 blah  |  ♫ Jane's Addiction - Superhero
Link

School sucks...=.=*

Oct. 14th, 2009   4:28 PM
I had to hand in my research topic, research question, and annotated bibliography yesterday for the sociological methodologies class I'm taking.  It was rather straight forward and I happen to like annotated bibliographies (since it makes writing other stages of the assignment after it much easier) but I couldn't do the topic I originally wanted to.  To put it broadly, I was interested in knowing where meaning making takes place for participants in the furry fandom ~ whether through art, conventions, online discussion, roleplaying, from some sort of influence, a combination of...etc.  Unfortunately, annotated bibliographies are almost always done on scholarly writings and there is next to nothing in academic literature about furries or other relevant key words such as anthropomorphism and zoomorphism.  *train of thought explodes* 

My universities scholars portal (a search engine for academic literature) came up with only one article I could use.  Normally this would be better than nothing since I could then look through that articles reference list for more usable stuff, but unfortunately they were almost all non-academic stuff ~ such as personal websites, magazine articles, interview transcripts, tv shows, etc.  (The article I'm referring to is 'Furries from A to Z (Anthropomorphism to Zoomorphism)' by Gerbasi et al.  The main author, Dr. Kathy Gerbasi, is quite interesting and I await to hear back from her :3)  I doubt that I'm not looking in the right areas, but rather there just isn't any scholarly writing on this subculture.  Instead there is a wealth of writing by non-academics (which goes without saying are the ones that shape and contextualize the fandom) on thing message boards, personal websites, blogs, etc.  For example 'State of the Fandom' and 'The Furry Sociological Survey' are two articles that are quite credible and extensive in value and research...but I can't use them because they aren't published or peer-reviewed.  I really pisses me off! >.<**

This is doubly sucky because I'm running into the exact same problem for my feildwork in cultural anthropology class.  The only difference is that for this class I don't just write about the methodologies and hand in a proposal, I actually have to go out and gather data.  This wouldn't be such a pain in my ass if I could actually have things my way...naturally.  The prof wants us to gather data in the flesh, at physical locations, and keep within the ethical protocols of the discipline.  My problem is, I want to gather data through the Internet (since furries are way more accessible that way) by way of questionnaire and interview ~ both of which are very limiting when done on the Internet since you can't be reflexive.  Furthermore, the issue of ethics is taken dead serious.  I will require signed consent from the people I interview ~ I can make a consent form easily, but getting a signature over the Internet I have no idea how that'll be done (and this is even if I am allowed to gather data over the Internet).  There are a lot more issues that make this topic of research way more trouble than it's worth despite how much I want to do it ~ that's what really sucks.  Anyone can start a topic on a forum asking things I want to know about, but I can't hand in any of that information because of the rules and institution =.=**.  I don't know what to do about this...it would suck balls to do research on any other topic since I can't think of anything I'm more interested in (btw, the research topic I switched to for the sociology course concerns the value of social capital from social networking sites such as Facebook). 
😑 blah  |  ♫ Lil Wayne - Tie My Hands
Link

A furry encounter...

Oct. 12th, 2009   12:36 AM
Sunday I met up with six other local furs through the OntarioFurs group on FA ~ it had its ups and downs.  As per usual when it comes to meeting people from online, I was the token Asian and shortest person in the group >>".  I didn't know anyone except hypr, whom I had chatted with only a couple of times ~ everyone seemed to know at least one or two other people, or other local furries from past meet-ups.  I didn't have much of a problem with this since you have to start somewhere, so it was nice to finally get in the know-how even with the awkward moments.  My impression of these furs (despite questioning how wise it is to be writing about it; for better or worse) are as follows:

Hypr - Even though I didn't know what to expect from those I haven't chatted with before, he wasn't what I expected .__."  I guess I just expected a more confident person because he set up this meet-up ~ something about organizers and not being indecisive can be a flawed way of thinking on my part, so my bad.  He walked really fast and a head of the group by a lot quite often...I found it difficult deciding how to approach this ~ be a nice guy and keep him company while walking at his pace or hang back with the others?  He didn't really have anything interesting to say other than plans for the next meet, which he spoke of quite often.  I don't mean to sound like a complete jerkface, but I'm kinda glad we couldn't meet up one-on-one last month because it would have been way awkward and uncomforting. 

Lightning Tiger - Dude, it would be way awesome if you talked more because the very few times that you did, it was fun!

FreshStart - Woah, he knows his anime and then some! o.o  It's nice to throw my geekiness at someone who can do the same, so he was easily one of the people I talked to the most throughout the day.  I thought he was fun as well from the occasional teases he and Ruff had. 

Ruff - A fellow babyfur with quite a number of characteristics I'd imagine a babyfur to have ~ playful, cuddly, sociable and active, among other things.  He talked a lot, which isn't a bad thing in my opinion since I'm the opposite.  He bought a pair of really cute white paws and a ridiculously childish Tigger tuque that had ear flaps with fluff balls on the end ~ to have worn them all day was quite Jonesy of him.  He's adorable and I hope to meet him again.  (how come so many babyfurs are in B-Town? *sad face*)

Danruk - I think it is safe to consider him the most experienced furry in our group ~ possibly having to do with the fact that he was the oldest.  He was calm, cool, and collected...it would have been nice if he bossed up more often since I would have been happy to go wherever he decided.  It was amusing to watch his little antics with Ruff ~ they seemed to be quite close.  I wonder what he thinks about when hanging around people nearly half his age ~ forgive the curiosity, it just interests me. 

JD-W - Appearances can be so deceiving and he is a prime example.  At first glance he is a goth ~ all that black clothes, safety pins/spikes/chains/bullets everywhere, and punk-death-heavy-metal-etc logos.  But past the appearances he's such a sweetheart wrapped in awesomeness ~ I counted three times when he gave spare change to the needy.  He is super sociable when around the right people, and it intrigues me when he isn't.  He bought the most stuff in our group...which was a bit scary since it seemed a bit reckless. 

(I have my own flaws and possible fine points which I'm sure they may have passed judgment on and that's fine.  I know the do's and dont's and I accept whatever consequences my opinions might have if they are taken bitterly.  My impressions aren't static so who knows, maybe the next time I see these furs I'll think differently :3.)

The day consisted of a lot of walking, TTC-ing, and shop browsing.  Worthy mentions go to:  Chinatown, Anime Xtreme, some fake DVD shop we got suckered going into, King's Noodle House (so-so food), Honest Ed's, Sonic Boom, The Beguiling (awesome comic book shop worth revisiting), CN Tower, Steam Whistle Brewing (free sample of beer!), Kensington Market, and Toko Sushi (conveyor belt sushi, food was nothing special).  I hate to say it but the worst part of the day was when Ruff and Danruk left for home...awkward silent moments ensued.  I don't blame any one person since I'm at fault as well, but dinner had to be the most uncomfortable sitting ever =\.  I guess I'm just blah'd by how the day ended making it hard to write about the many moments of fun.  Live and learn I suppose ~ I'll consider going to any future meet-ups depending on who else goes as well, but I'm more than certain to attend the Islington Furmeet 5.

New commissions and travel!

Oct. 11th, 2009   12:20 AM
Its been a tiny little while since I've put up new pictures that I've commissioned =P  According to my PayPal history its been twelve days since I've last paid an artist o.o  Maybe next time I'll be able to last half a month...and than three-quarters...and than an entire month!  Pff or not .__."


Psychobat finished my $10 full pic + two icons package and they look really spiffy! ^^  I kinda feel like a crook because the first icon she finished was the orange one.  But due to some computer mishap it got saved as 100x100 instead of a 500x500 (difference being you can scale down the latter, but not blow up the former) so she gave it to me for free.  I didn't mind if it was small since icons are small anyways :<  So that was generous of her.  I gave her a list of prompt words for her to choose, so the blue icon = anxious/worried, orange = blank/bored, and purple = annoyed in a cute/cuddle style.  ^^;; I like the first two the most.  I really like the full colour pic (despite the gray hands...I can ask her to change it no problem, but whatever =P).  The look I was going for was to be picked up and she did an excellent job!


This pic by Kannakin makes me melt D:  It's so simple and clean ~ I can't help but smile when I look at it =3  It also makes me consider getting more flat coloured pictures (rather than shelling an extra $5-10 for shading) since solid colours make things look more...artificial?  In a good way.  I donno, some things look really good in solid colouring =\, that's just my taste. 

I've got another commission in the works (I don't really know how many I have going on tbh...haven't update my excel sheet in weeks o_O) by .  I'm getting a $10 bust, which I think is a good deal for the quality and beauty of her drawings ~ the colours on that third bust are insane!  I asked to be drawn something along the lines of this ~ with artistic liberty on the design of the power fists, clothes, and angle/pose.  I donno why but I really love power fists/gauntlets as weapons, they just scream cool! D:  My Second Life avatar has power fists; they're scripted for combat ^^.  

=========================================================

I'M GOING TO JAPAN!  (if you didn't already know :D)  AND I'VE BOUGHT MY TICKET!  W00T!! \o.o/

$1005.93 for the ticket and another $36.72 + $90.55 for the cancellation/medical travel insurance T_T.

Leaving on January 11th and coming home on February 11th :3.  From the 12th to the 20th I plan to stay in Tokyo.  Then I'll head to Kyoto and stay there from the 21st to 31st.  Still debating on this, but I'll most likely stay in Hiroshima from the 1st till the 3rd.  After that it's on to Fukuoka from the 4th till the 6th.  On the 7th I'll take a six hour train ride back to Tokyo and stay there until my departure date ^^.  I haven't booked a single hostel yet, so I really ought to get on that soon >>". 

Oh and lastly, Google Map: Street View is the shit!  Sure it's been around for a while in the states, but only recently has it made its way to Toronto and other Canadian cities ^^.  It's really incredible how technologically advanced we've gotten...you can 'walk' the streets of Tokyo as I've done for...*ahem*...hours.

Cars and condos...

Oct. 10th, 2009   8:28 PM
The other day there was a silver Maserati GT parked across from my house ~ so smexy.  And today I saw a red Hyundai Genesis Coupe while out for lunch ~ my first time seeing one D:  It's unfortunate that people underrate it even though it's comparable to a mid-luxury Lexus, BMW, or Benz.  I'm not a car nut but I've seen a lot of smexy vehicles while working condo security.  When I worked at The Villas (18 Yorkville Ave. condo), way more than half of the cars in the garage were Audi's, BMW's, Benz's, and Porsche's - there was only a handful of Honda, Toyota, Mazda's, and the like.  Naturally, I took crappy cellphone pictures of them XP...after the cut.  

 

Lamborghini Gallardo ~ $180,000 to $210,000.
Ferrari 355 Spider ~ if this is a 1999 model, back then it would have cost $165,000.


Maserati Coupe ~ use to cost $125,000 before being replaced by the GranTurismo.
Range Rover ~ I'm guessing $50,000 and up?  


Bentley Continental ~ at least $175,000.
Gawd I miss working at The Villas.  I worked the graveshift and I pretty much spent the whole time on the amenities floor or the roof (such an incredible view of the city) >>"...yeah, don't go depending your life on condo security...they don't get paid enough to put it one way.  I also worked at The Merchandise Building (on Dalhousie St, it use to be a Sears warehouse) which had the most awesome roof I've ever seen.  Lookie after the cut D:



I feel like some sorta real estate agent >>"  But damn I loved this roof!  It's way huge; one end has an indoor pool while the other has this outer area with a party room (last two pics) in the middle, and at one corner of the roof is a suite o.O  Although the roof is really awesome, the suites (like most of the condos I've worked at) are hella tiny.  The Merchandise Building is really unique because it still has that industrial feel on the inside, but the suites are really narrow and the floors are raised/submerged (which I guess is cool if you like having two steps of stairs >>").  Just to give you an idea at how cramp some suites can be, in the picture below the front door doesn't open all the way =.=  It smacks the counter which is part of the kitchen and the living room is on the other side of the counter.

"Your face..." o__O

Oct. 10th, 2009   1:39 AM
*yawn*  Feeling rather tired but unable to sleep comfortably because I haven't showered >>".  Got home not long ago from K-Dawg's place ~ the usual dinner and cuddles :3.  We made pizza...more rather he went shopping for the ingredients and made the dough before I arrived.  Damn you >.<  I wanted to play with the dough since it's all gooey and fun =P.  Ah well, I grated the cheese...my only contribution, besides my stomach XD.  While it was in the oven I kicked his butt in Mario Kart and Smash Bros. on the Nofriendo Nintendo 64, but later he pwnd me in Crash Team Racing for the PS2 ~ it was my first time playing so that's my excuse.  Mmmm home-made pizza is so delicious even when the dough is wheaty and healthy-ish.  Besides the cheese and sauce, we had pepperonis, mushrooms, and olives ^^.  That satisfied my pizza craving maybe for a day or two hehe. 

After eating we cuddled up on his bed to watch Annie Hall because I had mentioned an interest in Woody Allen movies.  Recently I've seen Take the Money and Run, Bananas, and Zelig ~ all of which are incredibly funny films I recommend.  I brought my new sleeper and plastic pants along so I got to wear them after being padded.  K-Dawg had his yellow sleeper on as well so we were extra cozy :3.  We really shouldn't watch movies that interest us whenever we cuddle because we almost never pay attention >>".  No need to go into the details with what got me distracted XD.  After the movie and then some, we ate grasshopper pie o.o"  I had no idea what sorta pie that was until today...and it's friggin' delish!  Bleh, then we watched the second episode of Glee...which I think I've already seen .___.  Other than the sucky weather and coming home past shower time, my night has been a really good one ~ thanks K-Dawg ^^.

Foolish fur...?

Oct. 7th, 2009   1:08 AM

So for the 'Fieldwork in Social and Cultural Anthropology' (ANTC60) course I'm taking, the major assignment for the class is an ethnographic research proposal followed by the actual project itself ~ complete with fieldwork and the proper write up I'm assuming, since she hasn't given us the guidelines yet.  We're also required to hand in response papers to the weekly readings we are assigned.  I've taken about three other couses with this prof so I'm pretty familiar with what to expect.  I haven't pitched her my idea for the research propsal in person just yet, but I plan to since it's better to get consultation than jump into something blindly.  I did bring it up in this weeks response paper though...probably a foolish idea, but whatever ~ it's my final year of school so it's not like I'm ever gonna see my prof again, and I'd actually like to do something genuinely interested in.  Here's what I got so far for my response paper...after the cut.

[This week's topic is on 'Ethics, Relationships, and Subject Positioning' and the two articles I refer to are:
Shaming of the Anthropologist: Ethical Dilemmas during and in the Aftermath of the Fieldwork Process by Rachel Burr
Anthropologist in the Field: Scientist, Friend, and Voyeur by Friedman Hansen]

            I think ethics is an issue I’ve never put complete consideration into throughout many of the cultural anthropology courses I’ve taken because when it isn’t raised I take the research I read about at face value.  But when I do hear about ethics in the social sciences, my most immediate thoughts are Humphrey, Milgram, and Zimbardo – three extreme examples before code of ethics were drafted.  I’ve always been aware of anthropologists using pseudonyms and contextualize situations differently in writing, but in the case of Rachel Burr’s experience it gave me something more to think about.  The ethical dilemma she wrote about concerned taking the role of an anthropologist or of a social activist while doing research among potentially HIV-positive kids.  Her article made me think about how fundamental ethics is in anthropology – even though some of our research topics are very specific and we utilize an array of methods, ethics is almost always the backbone of the academic. 

            In terms of how this week’s topic relates to my ethnographic project, it has helped flesh out the basic do’s and don’ts.  Last week while thinking about my proposal, I was interested in Internet communities.  But to be more specific now, the communities (or people who are part of a fandom) I am most interested in researching are deviant ones; such as the furry fandom.  Given that the furry fandom is aberrant, a lot of the aspects involved are personal by nature but much more open in an online community.  I am interested in knowing where does meaning making take place for these people (through online discussion?  Roleplay?  Art?  An influence or personal experience?  At conventions? etc)  I’ve thought about this subculture even before taking this course, but despite having resources and access to the participants, Hansen’s article helped bear in mind the ethical issues and sensitivities involved in such a study.  Furthermore, given the limitations of this course it probably isn’t a good idea to pursue this topic despite my enthusiasm. 



If she rejects the idea for my propsal than I'll do something smaller and much less related...like anime fandom - considering there's an anime club I could always 'observe' and 'participate' amongst.  In any case, I can most likely recycle these ideas for the 'Logic and Social Inquiry' (SOCB05) course I'm taking.  The major assignment is somewhat similar, minus the 'ethnography' and 'doing stuff' part (lol).  Pretty much the proposal and final write up for that assignment revolves entirely around methodology and justifying its use for the topic you are researching ^^.
😴 tired  |  ♫ Torley on Piano - Cold Motion

Professor Layton and the Curious Village on the DS

Oct. 3rd, 2009   10:44 PM
I began playing Professor Layton for the DS about an hour ago and a few of the puzzles I've come across are pretty tricky!  Right now I'm only at the Manor Border with 12 puzzles solved, but here are two to tease your noodles... 

No. 005 - Digital Digits
Imagine a digital clock.  How many times will the clock display three or more of the same number in a row (ex. 03:33) over the course of one day?  (Note: this digital clock displays time on a 12-hour scale, not on military time)

No. 013 - Sinking Ship
Fifteen people are trapped aboard a ship that's going to sink in exactly 20 minutes.  Their only chance for survival is the five-person life raft stowed on their vessel.  To make matters worse, the waters around the ship are teeming with man-eating sharks, so swimming to safety is out of the question.

A round-trip to the nearest island and back to the boat takes nine minutes on the raft.  How many people will live to see dry land?



Answers!
No. 005 - Digital Digits
01:11    02:22    03:33    04:44    05:55    10:00    11:10    11:11    11:12    11:13    11:14    11:15    11:16    11:17    11:18    11:19    12:22
Double all of this to account for AM and PM.  Thus, a series of three or more of the same digit will appear 34 times over the course of one day.

No. 013 - Sinking Ship
In 20 minutes the raft can make two round-trips to the boat.  However, on the return trip back to the boat 1 person must steer the raft.  Furthermore, after two round trips only 18 minutes have passed, meaning the ship hasn't sunk yet so the raft can in fact pick up three loads of passengers.  In total, 13 people will live to see dry land while two will be eaten by sharks D:

Mid-Autumn Festival

Oct. 3rd, 2009   10:12 PM
Today, for most east Asian cultures, is the Mid-Autumn Festival (or Moon Festival).  Despite celebrating this day with family for years and years I don't really have a clue as to what the history is or what is getting celebrated.  But to be honest I'm not going to look into it because it isn't that big of a deal to me.  All I can tell you is that on this day Chinese people eat overly priced and extraordinarily fattening sweets called mooncakes. (they're about the size of your hand and on average contain 790 calories and 45g of fat!)  Weeks prior, family and friends give mooncakes as gifts - often receiving way to many and in effect re-wrap and give them out to other people.  Also, it's generally not a good idea to go to your regular dim sum restaurant (for larger establishments at least) since they'll be persistent as hell to sell you mooncakes >>".  Without fail, Asian grocery stores will overstock so the day after they're sold at cut-throat prices. 

Oh yeah, Ontario Place also does the Chinese Lantern Festival at the time of year ~ its been canceled this year, though, for whatever reason =\.  I went two years ago with Fanny and it was pretty spiffy...

(Here's just one picture ~ panda madness!  They look pretty awesome at night as well since everything lights up!)

🥱 bored  |  ♫ Jay-Z ft. Alicia Keys - Empire State of Mind
Link

Dinner and a movie...

Oct. 2nd, 2009   7:04 PM

Went out with Jully last night for a belated birthday dinner and a movie :3  (only getting around to write about it now because I'm a major slacker >.o).  I've been kinda craving pizza for the last little while, but found out last minute that I could take the car out.  So rather than going to the usual Boston Pizza near her house we went to Jaipur Find Indian Cuisine ~ heh, quite the curve from pizza, but I also wanted something different while dining with Jully.  I can always hold off on the craving since it's much easier to get at than Indian food.  The last time I went to Jaipur's was in early summer for lunch and the food is exactly as I remember it.  Which is a bit disappointing in regards to the buffet selection being exactly the same as the lunch spread but with the price up >>".  Other than that, I still enjoyed myself very much and I'm sure Jully found something she liked as well since it was her first time at an Indian restaurant.  (*drool* The butter chicken and gulab jamun are enough for a foodgasm!)  It was so good I had trouble sleeping from such a full belly .__." 

Afterwards we went First Markham Place to see what was on ~ I didn't really think we'd watch anything since it wasn't my idea to begin with, but we ended up catching the last screening of Surrogates.  I don't think many people are aware that the movie is based on a five-issue comic book series published a few years ago.  Unfortunately the writer and artist (Robert Venditti and Brett Weldele) aren't famous so I suppose credits to their idea aren't well preserved =\.  However, their limited series was published by Tom Shelf Productions ~ which also published one of my favourite graphic novels, 'Blankets' by Craig Thompson ^^. 

Anywhoo, the comic book is very good.  The level of thought, depth, and complexity are quite astonishing and I think the series could have easily taken off to double or triple the number of issues.  Essentially the setting is in the future where remote control androids replace daily living ~ like a next gen Second Life.  In effect, there is no crime, no disease, no racism, no sexism, no unattractiveness, etc because all people do is stay home and live their lives through a hawt robot.  The comic book does a great job at showing the downfalls of this paradise ~ the absence human expression and feeling.  The movie on the other hand did what Hollywood does best - it's flashy, there's a bit of action, the plot is more twisted, the heart-felt parts are glossed over, and the ending kinda sucked.  What's more, the movie was only 88 minutes; despite changing the plot here and there, it really only scratched the surface of what it could have been.  The movie stars Bruce Willis and in general it felt like a Die Hard movie >>" - whether good or bad, that's for you to decide.  There were things I liked in the movie that weren't in the comic book, but those are mainly just aesthetics.  I really wish they stuck with the intricate but straightforward plot from the comic book, as well as incorporating the gritty atmosphere, emotional depth, and sober ending.

I don't recommend watching this movie in theaters.  There's just nothing special about it even with the cool concept.  Rent it or download it, you'll be less disappointed.  Had I not read the comic book prior to watching the movie I'd probably have enjoyed it a little more. 

🥱 bored  |  ♫ Muse - Uprising
Link

Update o__O

Oct. 1st, 2009   2:28 PM


It's October!  Bwahahahaha! ^w^  I don't like Bleach but this episode, for whatever reason, immediately came to mind when I think about October.  *shivers*  What a horrible episode...

It seems so sudden that the weather here has gotten much shittier.  It gets dark out by about 7pm and the weather is never above 16C during the day .___.  I can tolerate the cold but I still hate it, and long nights are generally when I'm least productive >>".  What's more, I'm already feeling a cold coming on ~ I blame the person on the bus whom I sat next to -.-*  I've been stuffing my face with fruits and liquids in hopes of getting better.  Blah, just to conclude all this whining, I haven't played squash or worked out since sometime early last week! D:  Nuuuuuu!  I think it's because I have to dress more appropriately for the weather making it so that I have to cover up all that hard work >>"  Either that or I've just been way lazy.  

==================================================================

Anywhoozle, zawisza finished my Halloween icon commission a few days ago!  She's (he?)  re-open five more slots if anyone is interested ~ only $3.

Wicked me thinks.  I asked to be drawn as Professor Franken Stein from the anime series Soul Eater ~ he's one of my favourite characters next to Black Star ^^.  I'll be using this icon as my furry default for the rest of the month month :3 

I've also got three things in the works by Psychobat :D.  A full coloured pic that'll look something like this, and two icons ~ all for only $10 ^^.  Also, a chibi pic by Kannakin - whose art is quite simple but fun - for the price of a small donation (is $3 too meager?  I always have a hard time knowing how much is adaquate for pay-what-you-can/donation commissions >.o)

==================================================================

I've been playing Scribblenauts on the DS for the last two days ~ quite challenging for uncreative folks .___.  I'm almost always relying on wings, fire, and boulder (lol).  Some fun words to use have been nuke, blackhole, and clone ~ they don't serve much relevence though XD.  Man, if only this ACTUALLY worked ~ to bad it just gives you a dud =P. 

After I get bored of Scribblenauts I think I'm going to try some of the games from the Professor Layton series since I've heard great reviews ^^. 

==================================================================

Blah, progress is a slow process ~ my excuse for being lazy.  I intended to have bought my plane ticket and reserved rooms for a couple of hostels by the end of September, but that hasn't happened yet.  I wanted to get it out of the way since most hostels start taking reservations three months in advance and the popular places fill up quickly ~ plus it's easier to over book and cancel.  I'll try to get that done by the end of this week...or sometime .___. 

I'm thinking something along the lines of ten days in Tokyo, nine or ten days in Kyoto, possibly two days in Hiroshima (either that or stay in Kyoto and commute two hours whenever I want to go), and five days in Fukuoka.  Staying at the major cities while using a JR Pass will allow me to visit the nieghbouring towns quite easily: Tokyo (Mt. Fuji, Nikko, Yokohama, Kamakura), Kyoto (Nara, Osaka, Kobe), and Fukuoka (Nagasaki, Beppu, Aso-san).  JR Pass is only available to foreign visitors and allows you to board the bullet train to get between major cities and any other locomotive under the JR branch (which is quite extensive).  A 21 day pass costs $689 which sounds like a lot but it is actually cheaper than buying individual tickets to get to the major cities.  So I was thinking stay in Tokyo for six days, and then activate the JR Pass to visit the surrounding cities for the next four days, and then move on to Kyoto ~ it's a bit constraining since in terms of the number of days since I need the Pass to get back to the airport for departure (which isn't in Tokyo) to save some bucks.  

Despite having five grand to spend, I'm doing the math at $4500 just for safe measure.  Minus the JR Pass I'll have about $3810 for twenty-seven days, which is about $140 (or 11,671 Yen) a day for food, hostel, shopping, and some transportation and admissions fee.  Yeah, I think I'll be alright! XD (and then three months down the line I somehow get stranded in Morioka...*trip planning FAIL!*)

Sep. 28, 2009

Sep. 28th, 2009   11:06 AM
Happy birthday to me!  ^^;;  Today I turn 22, but no matter how old I am I will always be a kid at heart :3.  It's good that I don't feel that much older since I'm usually the youngest (and shortest) among my peers.  Rather I feel excited and scared with every passing year ~ I've accepted the accumulating responsibilities but whether I'm prepared for the next stages of adulthood is something to shiver at.  Anywhoo, I guess it's also good to know that somethings won't change...like how I honestly cannot remember what I did on my birthday last year.  No doubt it's because I'm really forgetful, but probably also because the year has been so full of good times, moments of stress, new friends, and memorable experiences.  Thank you to everyone who's made it all possible!  ^^

Sep. 28, 2009

Sep. 28th, 2009   12:08 AM
Happy birthday !  ^^

Dude, are you stalking me?  XD  You know I'm not serious, but sometimes the coincidences are both amazing and eerie ~ we both share the same birthday, we both study anthropology, and we are both AB/DL/babyfurs.  I'm sure there are other crazy random happenstances that we both share and it continues to surprise me that you knew of me while under my old alias.  In any case, I feel fortunate to have gotten to know you and to chat with you so regularily.  You are an interesting person with infinite potential and I wish you the very best no matter what life throws at you! 

And honestly dude, you surprised the shit out of me when you said you tried to commission Remmy for a birthday present to me.  I would never have expected such generosity given that we've only known one another for a few months :3.  It's unfortunate that she never got back to you, but the thought alone makes me feel very special.  What's more, you're sending me plastic pants as a subsitute!  I am at a loss for words and luckily I am a firm believer in reciprocity ~ so I got you a full body pic and a badge by Princess_Rei ^^.  I hope the two of you can work out the details in fleshing out your fursona since I did convert you into a furry (oh noes!).  I look forward to it's completion!

Groggy but good...

Sep. 27th, 2009   5:28 PM

I was a bit hungover from last nights ABDL party at Kevydl & Danididit’s house so I stayed in bed for most of the afternoon ~ feeling a lot better now \o.o/.  Good times.  There must’ve been twenty people, many of which I’m now familiar with, and about three people that I’d never seen before (the number of good looking guys is increasing {o’.’o} hope I get to see them again).  As always there were a few weed connoisseurs which meant I got blazed quite early on ~ didn’t even have a beer in my hand and I was already smoking Ice and Juicy Fruit XD.  It’s unfortunate the weather was nippy and raining heavily, even though their deck is covered I couldn’t hang out on it for very long.  Time to invest in a sleeper cause a onesie just doesn’t do it anymore for this kind of weather!  Blah…the one bad thing about last night was that I got shit faced from four tallboy cans and a big glass of screwdriver which I didn’t mix but probably should’ve since this person went heavy with the vodka =.=*.  Suffices to say I had my head over the toilet bowl for a little while =( Totally not cool.  I stayed the night ~ the only time I looked at my watch, which surprised me, was at 3am o__O.  Dan and Kevy were really accommodating which is so awesome of them!  Even though their place is small, they managed to sleep eight people ~ cuddles and then some ensured for many of us XD.  The next morning Nebulus64 gave me a lift home which I’m always grateful for ^^.  Without you I’d probably have less fun at the munches and gatherings, so thanks a lot man :3.  Tim Hortons + shower/brush teeth + bed = nirvana.

High on life o__O

Sep. 26th, 2009   4:58 AM

Rawr…can’t sleep >>”  Kinda on a caffeine and sugar high right now from my (Friday) night with Stephanie.  Good times ^^.

We had dinner at Dickens Café up in Richmond Hill – they serve Hong Kong style Western food =P (which is pretty much a steakhouse + cheap HK food + soup, dinner rolls, and a hot drink + Western price tag).  I had steak and Steph had curry fish ~ both were really delish!  Afterwards we went to Michael-Angelos Market Place and got three slices of cake – marble cheesecake, mango cheesecake, and Napoleon cake – but we didn’t eat it until later since we were still full.  Rather, we walked off our dinner at First Markham Place (window shopping at all the cute toy stores XD) and Wal-Mart (toys section and $5 DVD bin!). 

(I suppose my usual complaint of Richmond Hill is that there is very little to do aside from eat, wander stores aimlessly, maybe watch a movie, and eat some more >.<  Ah well, sometimes I feel the same about Toronto even though I love the city .__.”)


(crappy cell phone pic of cheap and tasty cake)

Later we went to Tim Hortons to indulge in cake ~ Steph got a half steep tea/half coffee, double-double combination which surprisingly wasn’t bad.  Even though we didn’t eat it all, the cake was really yummy ^^ (friggin’  Napoleon is impossible to dig into with a plastic spoon!).  We called it a night after that and I got home a little after midnight.  All-in-all it was a very typical (in terms of the things we do), chillaxing night, with lots to talk and joke around about ~ gawd we’ve got this one friend who’s the butt of every joke…it’s so wrong but undeniable!  Here’s hoping we go to Niagara Falls next weekend and congrats on landing a full-time job!  :D (tsk, already enthusiastic about getting your own car hehe)

D: And thank you so much for the birthday present!  The other day she said got me an uber cute present ~ part of me was totally beaming for a bunny plushie XD.  But instead I got something way more awesome and hilarious!



Straight out of the WTF department, it’s freaking dumpling plushies! XD  Leave it to the Asians to put a happy face on anything and making it super cute.  I love it!  She also got me fifteen comic books (five for $1 = random assortment \o.o/) and a tennis ball key chain from the US Open :3 

Continuing with this happy high, I got my commission picture from JadeFox in the mail yesterday!  It's the size of a normal 8.5x11 piece of paper but a little thicker, and it came with a cardboard backing so it wouldn't get bent.  I'm thinking of getting a cheap frame for it :D  Guess what else I got?  Marci finished the sketch for my badge and emailed it to me this morning! \o.o/  I love it and I can't wait to see it in colour!  I'll post it when I get it since I've been keeping the description to myself hehe >:3

Blah, an emo entry...

Sep. 26th, 2009   1:38 AM

The weather was pretty nice today so I took a stroll through Edwards Garden and Wilket Creek Park in the afternoon.  Its been a while since I’ve leisurely walked around by myself at my own pace, and I suppose it was also good early preparation for my trip since I’ll be walking a lot.  Surprisingly there was a fair number of people walking and biking as well so the experience wasn’t as solitary as I imagined it to be. This kinda prompted some self reflection, as I so often do when I’m in my own little world. 

I have no problem with solitude since I value my privacy a lot, but for as long as I can remember I’ve always had a fear of loneliness.  The persistent thought of growing old and dying a lonely person is really unsettling to me and sometimes I feel as though it’s a likely fate.  I think about this often enough that sometimes it seems as though I’m just kidding myself for having a good time among family and friends on a particular day.  My loneliness, I believe, is a fear of not being connected to anyone or anything on a dialogical level.  It isn’t about the situation since I could be the only person in a room and not feel alone, while in a room full of people I might have one of the loneliest experiences ever.  Rather than being about the absence or presence of other people, I think loneliness is about the absence of self. If I don’t apply myself to being more connected than there is no sense of self, but changing is easier said than done.  If I sit around waiting for others to do it for me I feel as though a counterproductive push-and-pull battle wages from within.  Contributing and being of service to other people sometimes feels so awkward, and I’m just left with wondering what aspect of being connected brings me happiness. 

My self-concept is so blurry right now I’m starting to think in circles >>”.  Excuse the emo-ness, it’s just one of those days.

😑 blah  |  ♫ Deftones - One Weak
