Anthro and traveling...

Sep. 23rd, 2009   11:12 AM
I wish I could experience this...but without the institution and having to write papers on said experiences upon completion =(.  The gist of the article is something very familiar to anthropological fieldworkers ~ the feelings and experiences of separation, incorporation, and reincorporation/culture shock from working with a group of 'native' people for an extended period.  It fascinates me a lot to live among people socially and culturally different from what I'm use to, but at the same time the etiquettes, language barrier, isolation, etc is quite frightening. 

I suppose this interest is mainly grounded to the fact that I'm so bored of what I'm already doing, and at the same time it makes me really want to travel come the new year.  The only issue I've begun to brew over is that my wallet is really beginning to shrink ~ this months VISA came to $300, half of which were from two course readers =\.  I've got enough in the bank to last a few months on things I normally need to spend money on (transportation, phone bill, health-related, some leisure, etc) and I've still got five grand tucked away in my Tax Free Savings Account which I never want to resort to.  But I'm really pondering whether I should go on that trip to Japan using another five grand I have saved up ~ because when I come home I'm going to a broke bachelor in dyer need of a job.  It might be worth my while in the long run to have some money to sit on by not traveling and look for a job right after I finish school in December.  It feels like a kick to the gut to get hyped over a plan but not be able to go through with it, and it's funny because I have no interest in traveling for a shorter period or spending less than what I've got saved up >>".

Sluggish day...

Sep. 22nd, 2009   11:32 PM
Blah, Tuesdays...long day of school...horrid humidity outside...short workout at night...*sigh*  How do people work a nine-to-five with so much gusto >>"  I'm such a whiner haha!

I got mail this morning!  The two outrageously adorable badges I commissioned from Spiffy arrived ~ the colours were way more vibrant than the scans.  I'll try it with my scanner but no promises since it's laminated.  They're the size of my palm and don't have a clip, so I'll have to get some.  In any case, she's opening up for commissions tomorrow so I'll probably get two more pics from her :3  (yeah nvm, the prices she has now are double what I first paid for.  I don't mean to sound like a hater, but she's got something like a Marci going on ~ high prices for a really cute pic but with low detail)

Kalida also finished my commission ~ which is impressively quick since I sent her my description only two days ago.  Lookie ^^

O__O I'm a player!  Okay maybe not.  For this pic I didn't go with the usual babyfur, but instead just a diaperfur.  As I mentioned in an entry earlier this month I had three ideas I could have gone with that I thought would go well with her style.  I decided to go with a 'George look', he's from one of my favourite anime series, Paradise Kiss.  The character in the series almost always wears a spiffy suit with a fedora, and he's kinda got this thing for roses {o'.'o}.  But he's also a bit of a jerk and holds strong opinions making him a bit of a lonely romantic.  (shuddup!  XD  he's gorgeous and I love him no matter what!  *sigh* why'd it only have to be 12 episodes...)  Anyways, I think she did a wicked job even without the whiskers and one coloured tail ^^;;

Anyone seen the new iPod Nano (5G) commercial? 

I can't get the damn song they used out of my head!  The song is Bourgeois Shangri-La, by Miss Li (ft. Amanda Jenssen) from the album Dancing The Whole Way Home.  If you love the song you'll love the whole album!  Like honestly I don't know what's not to love ~ her music is so full of life!  (mix of pop, jazz, blues, and cabaret ^^)

Well that's all for tonight.  Looking forward to the end of tomorrow so I can start enjoying my birthday early hehe ^^.  Gonna have dinner with Stephanie on Friday, but we haven't really made any concrete plans for the rest of the day.  Saturday is Kevydl & Danididit's ABDL gathering at their place ~ that ought to make up for missing the munch XD (note to self: find out how to get there >>").  Sunday and Monday are up in the air, so maybe I'll try to meet up with Jully or take a stroll through Edwards Garden if the weather is nice.  Hrmm, wonder if Hypr is free this Sunday =\.
😴 tired  |  ♫ Miss Li - Polythene Queen
Link

Blahness...

Sep. 21st, 2009   11:10 PM
Blah, pretty boring weekend and Monday, but can't quite say I'm looking forward to my only two days of school XD.  Something about having to get up early in the morning, the rainy weather, and trying to stay awake through lecture ~ it was difficult enough getting through 70+ pages of reading today. 

Anywhoozle, I got a message from hypr Saturday morning - something came up so he had to cancel our meet up on Sunday .___."  My grandpa's birthday dinner wasn't bad though and I did manage to forget about the munch long enough to enjoy it =P.  We had the traditional Chinese ten course meal which was very tasty.  More often than not the first course is a meat platter - it can be a spread of different cuts or just a roast piglet.  Ours was the piglet and what was different about it from the other times I've had it was it included the head and feet o__O.  It was kinda creepy actually because the head was split in half (so that it was butterflied) so that it could stand upright ~ you could see the actual teeth.  My brother took pictures but I won't post them because it's a bit disturbing.  And as cruel as it sounds, it was a damn good piglet .__.

On Sunday I passed the time with TF2.  My Steam account says I've invested 22.1 hours in the past two weeks into the game.  It might sound like a lot but last week it was much worse XD  My Steam rating (whatever that is) dropped from 10 to 6.9 in the past week, oh wells.  It's fun if I'm bored, but by no means am I addicted to it. 

Today I did my readings for tomorrows lectures ~ hella boring since the opening chapters were mainly introductory.  I did find a tiny bit of this article interesting though; it's for Sociology: Family in Transition (SOCB49) to which this weeks topic is 'Canadian Family in Transition'.  The gist of the article is that in the past two decades more working people have invested time in their work than they have with their families/spouses.  Of course they also take into account level of education, age, gender, single parent, etc.  What's more, people are spending more of their free time alone ~ which the author breaks down depending on the activity, such as reading or watching tv.  I found it a bit interesting that there is no mentioning of the internet ~ yes, people with families who work for a living do spend copious amounts of time on the web.  Ah well, the article was really quantitative based so it kinda turned me off.

I also watched the two hour season premier of House MD!  It was pretty good although it felt like they packed a ton of stuff in a short amount of time.  The real question at the end is, has House really changed?  He's been addict and a jerk for five seasons...has rehab really changed him? o__O  The only thing that kinda sucks is now I can't work out Mon, Wedn, Fri since this show messes up my routine.  And Fringe (the only other tv series I follow) is on every Thursday!  Blah, some how I'll have to figure out a way to work around this since my dad uses the car on Thur so I can't work out in the morning or afternoon. 

Oh yeah, Fall is creeping up quickly it seems >>"  There are a couple of small trees that I can see from my room window and it seems like just over night they turned really red D:  It would be really awesome if I took a picture of them each day for a time lapse dillio...but I'm a lazy kid so that probably won't happen ^^;;.  I would really love to take a stroll through Edwards Garden someday, but it'd be really scenic and pretty. 

And just as a random closing thoughts...the hell is up with the currency conversion from the Canadian dollar to the Japanese Yen?!  For the longest time I was led to believe 1 CAND = 100 JPY.  But instead it's sitting at around 1 CAND = 85 JPY!  Stupid guide books and websites for being not up to date >>"  Bah, kinda tinkers with my budget since five grand Canadian translates to ¥426,604...which is almost seventy-five thousand yen less than I was originally led to believe T_T.  Hrmm, to go or not to go...could always settle with Cambodia and live like a king for a few weeks! (lol)
😑 blah  |  ♫ Sambomaster - Tsuki ni Saku Hana no You ni Naru no
Link

Relaxing...

Sep. 19th, 2009   12:58 AM
I can get use to this 'five days of no school' schedule...oh the relaxation...and joblessness...and piles of reading to do *sigh*.

Yesterday was pretty fun ~ in the afternoon Jully and I played badminton.  Last year (or two?) we use to play every now and then, but we stopped after she lost her ID card.  Her apartment facilities are pretty strict on having the card, signing up, and what not (you need a key fob just to exit the floor >>" why not just make the doors lock one way?) unlike my aunts condo XD  We rallied for an hour ~ I didn't suck that bad, but it was pretty obvious she was better than me =P.  She kept hitting the birdie at me so I had to get out of the way in order to make a return >>".  And I'm not too sure how to make a return such that it's angled towards the floor rather than way up high or far back.  So my game could use a little work ~ might have to see if she wants to play on a regular basis ^^.  Afterwards we loafed in the library for about an hour brewing over our guide books of Japan, it was quite difficult not to fall asleep since the sofas were so comfy! XD

We had an uber early dinner at a ramon restaurant not far from her house, but I had the curry on rice since I was pretty sick of eating noodles.  I thought their curry was too salty while Jully thought it was too sweet o__O".  I picked up the bill despite knowing the reaction I'd get ^^;;.  Later we strolled around T&T (an Asian grocery store that recently got bought out by Loblaws) ~ it made me think of how much fun grocery stores are!  Maybe it's just cause I enjoy food, but there are so many interesting things to see and touch ~ yeah I'm the kinda person who pokes at pizza dough or cheese or buns or tofu .__.  I'm sure I'd get bored of it if I went too often though heh, but it's a fun way to pass the time I suppose.  We did the same in Shoppers Drug Mart.  Then we went to Boston Pizza for desserts :D  I had a pint of Rickard's red and we shared a chocolate explosion cake ~ chocolate usually gives me a tummy ache, but Jully's a chocoholic...I think it's genetically programmed into most girls.  It was really tasty none the less, so it was worth the pain.  Bah, she was sly and picked up the tab ><**

I felt like a fatty that night, so I went to the gym this morning (lol) ~ plus for the last while I've been going Mon, Wedn, and Fridays...wouldn't want to kill the routine.  I think I managed to burn off half the calories from that chocolate cake XD.  The rest of the day I watched old reruns of CSI (Vegas), began re-watching Densha Otoko (a Japanese drama series ~ there's a lot of great scenes of Tokyo!), and took a really nice nap while the sun baked my room (crinkle bottomed and in a onesie, might I add ^^).  I suppose I'll "research" my trip to Japan tomorrow if I don't get distracted XD  But I'll definitely have some stuff planned out by the end of the month since most hostels accept booking three months in advance.  On Sunday I'm meeting up with hypr for the first time ~ we've only actually chatted online for a few days.  Oh wells, it's not like I've never done that before (lol) although someday I'm sure it'll get me raped or killed >>".  He seems like a friendly dude ^^. 

And what would ending an entry be without putting up any art!  Lookie, scrapbook style commissions!

I've mentioned this commission in an earlier entry ~ it is by twinklestah  (her FA).  I guess the thing that prompt me to do the age stages were a couple of age regression pics I've seen in the last few weeks.  I suppose the only difference was I commissioned an artist who doesn't do AR or babyfur pictures in particular.  Oh wells, I've said it before but I love diversity ~ maybe someday I'll go back and get something from someone I've already commissioned, but for now I like the 'new' and 'different'...and 'on sale' XD.  Look into twinklestah if you're interested, her water colour pictures are really cool ^^.


It's a dumpling reference sheet by Tamatha K.G.  For $15 I'd say this is some really spiffy work! D:  The background paper was definitely an awesome pick from her ^^.  Muhahaha, no more having to go into explicit detail for my future commissions >:3

Zomg, it's almost 1am!  I'm going to bed >>"  Peace out.
💤 sleepy  |  ♫ Sondre Lerche - Good Luck
Link

A furry controversy...

Sep. 17th, 2009   11:58 AM
Interesting.  So right now the big buzz in the furry fandom is about Chewfox and her partner Tom Cat going on The Tyra Banks Show for a segment called "Is your sex life normal?"  Not surprisingly it generated a ton of hate in the fandom ~ Chewfox's FA account was banned for a while which I thought was stupid since she didn't break any of the website rules in the TOS.  Some of the responses in related journals are really extreme and violent ("punches you in the vagina then washes my paw and then kicks you in in the vagina")(the likelihood of them going to a con and being abused is pretty high from what I gather) which is so unnecessary.  And then there are those that make fun of Chewfox's appearance by calling her fat?  Come on...grow up people.  It's one thing to disagree and voice your opinion, but personal attacks garner zero respect.  No one is perfect and self-awareness seems to be a very big problem amongst many people in the fandom.

Just to make it clear I am indifferent to what happened on the show despite not agreeing with everything that was said, but I do have a problem with some of the reactions that followed.  I'd like to think many furries are open-minded simply because what they are involved in is not normal and the fandom encompasses many fetishes and genres of interest, but it is embarrassing to see that some people are so quick to judge and bash another individual just because there is a disagreement.  Personally, when I hear the word 'furry' a couple of words immediately come to mind - sex (or yiff) and drama top the list.  You cannot deny that sex isn't a part of the fandom ~ just like saying bondage is a part of the gay community.  But neither represent each community as a whole.  Not all furries have sex in their fursuits and not all gays are into bondage.  Furthermore, there is nothing wrong with fursuit sex or bondage.  The fact of the matter is you cannot let any one person represent a whole since everyone represents themselves and no two furries have the exact same intentions.  The couple spoke on the subject of sex on their behalf and not as a representative of the fandom - Tyra Banks is the one hinting that all furries have abnormal sex lives.  If you're going to hate someone, give it to Tyra Banks for exploiting attention-starved people (if it weren't Chewfox and her partner it would just have easily been someone else).  They made a spectacle out of themselves and it might have been foolish, but it was their prerogative. Get over it.
😑 blah  |  ♫ Kaiser Cheifs - Love Is Not a Competition (But I'm Winning)
Link

o__O It's the mid-Sept already!?!?

Sep. 16th, 2009   11:44 PM
And thus completes my week of school...\o_o/ Yay!

Today I only had ANTC60 (Fieldwork in Social and Cultural Anthropology) ~ the course I couldn't get into for the last two years because only 25 students can take it at a time =.=  Which is kinda blah since it's a required course for my program (although it can be substituted for a stats course, but like hell I'm taking another math course).  At least I know the prof is awesome ~ Prof. Lena Mortensen.  I've taken a couple of courses with her (ANTC64, C66, D15, and I think a B level course) and she's got a lot of spunk, super approachable, and a fair grader.  It's wicked that two of my classes - SOCB05 and ANTC60 - have somewhat similar assignments ~ both involve creating a qualitative research design, both have proposals due as well as the final write up...I could easily use the same topic!  Plus, I've already got a couple in mind :D  Win!

After class I met up with Jully and Suki at Tim Hortons ~ it was quite nice to see some familiar faces since I'm not to fond of my 'fifth year' title >>".  It's not that there's any kind of failure attach to it because I know several people who've taken five or six years to graduate...I suppose I just never thought it'd happen to me since I could have finished on time if I really wasn't so lazy.  Anyways, Suki wanted to borrow my osteology textbook cause she's taking ANTC47 (Fuk!  The classes are on Mondays and Dewar is teaching it!  I sooooo want to visit her, she's awesome).  I brought her the older version despite having the newer one as well, so later in the evening we met up again and everything was hunkydory ~ I rode my bike to see her...I should really get out more the bike cause it's pretty relaxing.  Loafed around with Jully for about an hour, we mainly goggled over one of the Tokyo guide books I borrowed from the library.  .___. Jully was totally beaming...I know she wants to go so desperately, and I really want her to come with me.  But she's brewing her own plans involving the Japanese Exchange and Teaching Programme (JET) and I know she'll make the most of it if she puts her heart into it.  I'm slowly accepting the fact that I'll more than likely be traveling alone, which I have my pros and cons for...but in general it's saddening to think about during the planning stages.  I'm sure when I get out there I'll be dandy. 

I'll be meeting up with Jully again tomorrow afternoon ~ we're playing badminton ^^.  Afterwards we'll goggle over more Japanese guide books ~ she has two while I have three.  I'm sure I'll get plenty of pointers from her considering she's stayed in Tokyo for 3-4 days...I'll probably get an extended wishlist from her as well, hehe.  Sometime before Saterday I have to look for a birthday present for my gramps ~ he's turning 80!  (very prosperous number for the Chinese ^^)

Oh and that $130 I made yesterday poofed!  This semester I only have TWO required readings to buy (the rest I can download online) and in total they cost me $149.26...ballz! ><  The SOCB05 book has about 400 pages but it's not very big and it cost $99.65 T_T  I have until the end of the month to return it so I'm madly looking for a used copy online.  The SOCB49 reader is non-refundable and it cost $42.50 ~ I suppose that's a fair price although it is kinda thin compared to other readers.  I also got myself another commission XD  It was inevitable!  I can't go a week without getting one LOL!  *jitters and scratches like a crack addict*  

Lookie...

I suppose this commission is long overdue since I've wanted a reference sheet...like...when I got my first commission.  Although it's primarily the commissioners responsibility to make sure their character is drawn to their desires, a few of my commissions have had one or two minor tweaks that don't match the fursona I had in mind, which I've let slide under the table because I'm relatively flexible.  Not any more! >:3  All future commissions will follow this reference!  (in the past I had to use at least two or three pictures as references >>"  It gets annoying after a while).  This is only a sketch and I've asked for a few minor changes (like the hair and pads on the hands/feet) but for the most part I like it ^^  (the sorta hammer pants waist and thighs are just the artists' style so it's no biggie XD).  Oh, and of course all credits go to the artist ~ Tamatha K.G.  She's really friendly to trade messages with and her commissions are pretty cheap ~ this ref. cost $15, whereas other artists who draw less cartoony can charge as much as $60 or more.  What's more, she'll be doing a scrapbook style with this ref (seems to be quite popular nowadays) meaning after inking and colouring she'll cut out the characters and glue them on pretty paper!  I've been told that my character is going on some paper with childish prints ^^  I can't wait to see it!  W00t...one more piece of art to expect in the mail!  XD
🐇 bouncy  |  ♫ Bedouin Soundclash - Jeb Rand

Sep. 15, 2009

Sep. 15th, 2009   11:42 PM
Happy birthday Bunny!  ^^  (Phew, another save from BirthdayAlarm.com to help me remember when is what) (Donno if she still reads this blog, might have weirded out out with my alt lifestyle from the our last chit-chat heh) I remember we met through Stephanie cause you two lived in the same residence in first and second year.  We talked a lot on MSN and hung out a few times during the summer in those two years ~ I really enjoyed it.  I remember we went to the CNE and the Chinese Lantern Festival at Ontario Place.  And the time you wanted to get plastered so we went drinking - I picked up the bill which sorta made you mad heh, and helped you home.  I remember commuting all the way up to your campus for your birthday in first year, and the time we surprised Stephanie with your visit for her birthday.  Omg, I remember the time you broke my sunglasses which hung off the collar of my shirt when you gave me a hug!  You were so embarrassed and I was laughing my ass off!  Damn I miss all those good times...what happened in third and fourth year?  We just stopped talking and even now we rarely exchange a message.  I guess that's always been a flaw of mine, never taking the initiative to be the one to start the conversation ~ I'm sorry.  Well, regardless of how our paths have forked I'm really glad there was a time they convened.  Best wishes on this special day and everyday following ~ there's rarely a dull moment with you so I know you'll make the most of your life ^^. 
🌿 calm
Link

Classes and monies...

Sep. 15th, 2009   9:46 PM
Ah, my first day of classes!  I wish I could say it was like the first day of grade school back in the days...but I don't really remember that far back ^^;;.  It felt more like nothing in particular but not so much something of a routine.  I donno what I'm trying to get at...I'm just tired of school but I'm tired of doing nothing >>". Anywhoozle...today my classes consisted of SOCB49 (Family in Transition) and SOCB05 (Logic of Social Inquiry) ~ both of which are sociology classes, none of which are requirements for my program, but I need to take something for that one credit.  I can't say I'm to pleased with who's teaching SOCB49 ~ Prof. Ping-Chun Hsiung.  I took SOCB41 (Qualitative Methods in Social Research) a few years with her and it was pretty awful.  She can't explain things very clearly or thoroughly, which goes hand in hand with being unable to answer questions >>".  Her lecture structure and organization is pretty gibberish, while her actual lecturing is unenthusiastic and fatiguing.  It's a real shame because her research on feminism and family structure in Asia is quite interesting, and she wouldn't have gotten to where she is without knowing what she was doing.  Either way, it's going to be a brutal class to sit through and if you can avoid taking a class by her I highly recommend doing so .___."  On the other hand, I enjoyed sitting through SOCB05 with Prof. Denis Wall.  I've never seen or taken a course with him, but his first impressions were great!  I don't know what it is about him that makes him so down to earth ~ maybe it's cause he talks understandably fast while maintaining a mutual connection to the students.  He's kinda funny too =P  Since the course is about social research he used the example of the 'Dead Grandmother Syndrome'.  There is a coefficient correlation between C, D and F grade students and grandmother deaths, particularly during times of exams and/or assignments.  However, there is next to no correlation between A and B grade students and grandmother deaths during the same times.  There is no correlation when nothing is due in a course.  Sure it's immoral to use your grandma's death as an excuse for an extension or deferral, but how about some other plausible reasons?  Most grandmother's worry the most in families and high stress can lead to other health complications ^^  Ta-da! Oh, and I made $135 today ^^.  I sold some textbooks ~ which I've never done before because they're kinda like trophies of "yeah I read all that, aren't I smart!" (shuddup!  it makes me feel good!  XD).  I sold textbooks I'd never skim through again just cause they don't interest me - so that's my excuse...plus I need the green.  Two of which were on prehistory (North American, and the other was World), and one was my stats textbook from last semester.  It sucks cause I went to the school book buying booth first ~ which is a known fact that you'll get the worst deal but least amount of hassle.  The woman was going to give me $40 for the stats and $20 for one of the prehistory...I sold the prehistory cause it was quite old.  I then walked into the bookstore and saw the book I just sold selling for $90 =.=**.  So for the next little while I just stood around and sold off my last two textbooks to people reaching for the ones on shelve.  Sold the stats book for $70 (retail price at $165) and the second prehistory book for $45 (retail price at $120).  Sooo yeah...I made very little from what I originally paid T_T.  I think I might look into selling some of my textbooks on evolution if I get tight on cash again, especially since I haven't bought any of the required text for this semester.
😑 blah  |  ♫ Queens of the Stone Age - Running Joke

Her scanner faded the colours a little, but who cares!  I'm melting in my seat over here from the cuteness! US Open 2009

Sep. 14th, 2009   11:18 PM
So the US Open (a grand slam tennis tournament) concluded today with the men's finals, which I watched four of the five sets.  It was quite disappointing.  Juan Martin del Potro (6) defeated defending champion for five straight years Roger Federer (1) 3-6, 7-6, 4-6, 7-6, 6-2.  In the first set Del Potro played horribly - bringing into question how he was able to dominate Rafael Nadal (2) in the semi's - probably because his nervous worked against him as it was his first finals in a majors.  He began to pick up in the second set and then played much more consistantly for the rest of the match.  So that was only 1/5 of the disappointment.  The rest all goes to Federer who served unbelievably disgusting.  His first serve percentage was less than 50% for nearly three sets ~ in tennis you get two attempts on a serve for a point, so more than half the time his first attempt didn't make it over the net or went out.  I was quite disappointed that he didn't adjust his serve all throughout even though his second serves were fairly solid.  There were definitely some really great plays...but a majority were from Del Potro and his insanely powerful forehand.  Ah well, Federer only lost a new Benz and a lot of prize money.  He's still number one while Del Potro is ascends to fifth in the world (bumping Andy Roddick to sixth :<).  Other than that, the big buzz in the tournament was Serena Williams spazzing out at a lines woman for calling a foot fault at a really crucial moment ~ to call a foot fault is practically a joke since professionals practically NEVER make such a basic mistake.  Go YouTube it =P  If you find a good quality video you might be able to hear what Serena says - something about jamming a tennis ball down the woman's throat and something about killing her >>".  She got penalized for unsportsmanlike conduct, which gave her opponent Kim Clijsters the crucial point for her win.  Kim Clijsters eventually went on to win the women's title ~ quite amazing considering she's unseeded due to going into retirement two and a half years ago and only recently coming back onto the circuit.  Serena did however win the women's doubles title with her older sister Venus Williams, so kuddos to them. 
😴 tired  |  ♫ KT Tunstall - Suddenly I See

Oh my gawd this is so friggin' awesome!  I wish I woke up earlier just so I could have seen this soon - since he emailed it to me at 6am.  I am really happy with how this came out!  The way he draws and colours clothing just blows my mind.  And the fuzziness on the fur, which is distinctly his style, makes it stand out that much more.  He really didn't have to include the boarder with the four elements so that was an awesome add-in.  Also, it really didn't take him that long especially given his situation ~ I sorta guessed it would take two months.  Thanks again JadeFox (his FA), I am thoroughly impressed ^^.

Travel, crinkle, and yawn.

Sep. 12th, 2009   2:52 AM
Blah, having trouble falling asleep .  This always seems to be the case when I come home too late to shower without having to wake up everybody.  Oh wells, the day (Friday) has been good to me.  I spent most of the morning and afternoon (like the last couple of days) researching for my trip to Japan ~ although its mostly been focused on Tokyo since I intend to stay there the longest. 

I've mostly been reading up on a ton of blogs and forums mostly because the info is so much more down to Earth than things found in guide books and general travel sites.  Plus, they have pictures that convey much of what cannot be typed ~ which also heightens my excitement!  If you are ever interested, here are some of the sites I've been visiting:

A Japanese Cultural Experience - This blog provides some nifty information on etiquettes, language, and general destinations to look into. 

A Travelogue by Steve Boland - I really like this site because it's written intelligently and from an urbanist perspective.  Unfortunately it only takes a glimpse of Japan since he only traveled for a week.  I'm tempted to take the same route as he did :3

Tokyo Snap Photos - This is a blog by a Japanese fellow with entries roughly every other day.  Each entry contains pictures so that's pretty niffy ~ especially when I want to scope out what sort of clothes I should wear in January. 

Travel Blog: Tokyo - This site is a no brainer to go to for some really concrete info.  Unfortunately there's just a ton of stuff to read and it can get quite overwhelming especially if you are only looking for specific information.

Japan Forum - It's in English and the 'Going to Japan' sub-forum is well stocked with info.  I haven't gotten through most of the threads though.

Hostel World - I've been using this site to track room availability and reviews.  I can't really do much other than browse since I haven't bought a plane ticket yet so I don't know when exactly I'll be leaving and arriving.  Plus I still need to renew my passport >>".  I really want to stay at Backpackers Hostel K's House since it's got the best reviews, multiple branches in the major cities, and my brother has stayed there.  The only problem is the Tokyo hostel only takes reservations 3 months in advance (so the January room availability isn't up yet) and booking requires plane ticket information.

Anywhoos, I think I've got a rough itinerary for a 7-10 day stay in Tokyo ~ of course it's subject to change (probably add a few days) and I haven't looked into the other major cities, which I'll probably only stay for 3-5 days. 

Besides researching and playing TF2 in the morning and afternoon, I went over to K-Dawg's place in the evening for dinner and cuddles :3  Its been a while since I've hung out with him despite being around him at Dylan's party last month.  For the most part I miss not seeing him, but that's due to my own lack of initiative for not calling him out when I want to ~ or rather wanting him to take that initiative rather than me having to.  Either way, the night surely made up for what I missed!  We had pizza for dinner ~ fresh from the grocery store freezer to the oven.  Despite loving pizza, I burnt the roof of my mouth last night while eating pizza =.=**  so eating pizza again was difficult but delicious.  We watched the first two episodes of Glee, but I can't say I'm interested in that type of shows.  We then got padded into Kendall's ~ but of course I was double diapered with the Attends I'd already been wearing along with plastic pants, for fear that I might leak {o'.'o}.  Aside from not watching episodes of Pinky and the Brain that he put on, the cuddling was definitely something I enjoyed ~ I'll spare the details since I'm certain I don't need to write about it to remember it, heh.  I stayed for about five hours before catching the subway at 11:30pm-ish, and getting home at 12:30pm-ish. 

Hoi, I think I can let my eyes rest and eventually fall asleep now.  Peace out.
💤 sleepy  |  ♫ Eminem - Deja Vu
Link

Pimping...

Sep. 12th, 2009   1:14 AM

9/11

Sep. 11th, 2009   4:36 PM
It has been eight years since that tragic day.  I don't think I'll ever forget how glued to the television I was ~ watching the footage of the planes hitting the towers was like something out of a movie; shockingly unbelievable.  Even now, watching clips of what happened (especially the jumpers) still give me goosebumps. May we never forget the many lives lost and acts of selfless bravery.

D:

Sep. 9th, 2009   11:48 PM
Balls...probably won't be able to make it to this months ABDL munch :(  It's my grandpa's birthday and there's gonna be a family dinner ~ was hoping they could change it to Sunday, but most likely ain't going to happen.  T_T  This'll be the second time in a row I miss it - first was cause of the stats exam.  Blah, was looking forward to going too.  C'est la vie.

On a much brighter note, I got a sketch from JadeFox for my commission ^-^  Lookie...

I'd say he nailed what I had in mind with 95% accurate :3  I replied asking for whiskers, a bit more hair, the ears in red, and one or two other things ~ I am very happy none the less, especially since he hasn't drawn in months.  The outfit I'm decked out in is Toph's clothes from the cartoon series Avatar: The Last Airbender.  Gawd, I just love the way he draws clothes! {o'.'o}

Oh, and my slot for a commission by Kalida is coming up!  I haven't told her what I wanted her to draw for me yet, though.  There are three ideas floating in my head that I know she'd do an amazing job at (three is a lot since I usually only have one idea for one particular artist ~ she's very good *nod**nod*).  The first is me in a kigurumi (Japanese full body animal pajamas) of some animal I have yet to decide on.  The second is me in a childish school uniform in a 'looked down upon' pose or maybe a sad puppy dog look =P.  And the third, me sorta looking like George (from Paradise Kiss, one of my most favourite animes) reaching out to give a rose.  I'm leaning towards the third idea right now just cause George is so gorgeous {o'.'o} and I like the romantic spin with the rose giving.  I'm totally open to suggestions ^^.

Things I'd like to do if I go to Japan...

Sep. 8th, 2009   10:55 PM
Things I'd like to do if I go to Japan...

- Watch a sumo match.  Every January for 15 days there is a sumo tournament held in Tokyo.  Nose bleed seats a quite affordable to my understanding.  I don't know anything about the sport, but I don't give a damn...it's friggin sumo! 

- Watch a baseball game.  I'm not a fan of baseball ~ it's almost as boring as golf, imo.  But that doesn't bother me since the Japanese go apeshit for baseball!  I want to experience that intensity, time and wallet permitting though. 

- I want to stay at a ryokan.  A ryokan is a traditional Japanese Inn ~ it's got the futons, sliding paper doors, wooden mat floors, and generally two traditional meals.  Since I'll most likely be staying at a hostel where privacy is pretty nil (it's cheap so I don't mind, Western style hotels are expensive!), I'd really like to stay at a ryokan for one or two days just to feel the Japanese-ness (lol). 

- I want to go to a onsen, also known as a hot spring.  I'd prefer outdoor, but I'll settle with indoor if it comes down to it (since most of the really good ones are outside of the major cities).  It's relaxing and good for the body, so why not do it atleast once? ^^ 

- I want to eat fugu.  Fugu, or pufferfish, simply because of all the hype and because I'd like to know what it tastes like.  I'm not to nervous about the lethal poison part since I'm pretty sure the death statistics are really low...I think.  I just hope it isn't too expensive. 

- I want to eat natto.  Natto, or fermented soybeans, just looks so interesting.  I've heard about how it tastes and smells (both sorta nasty and difficult to eat) and I want to be the judge of that.  Looks like something that came out of my noise...but brown and chunky >>"...yummy.

- I want to eat kobe beef.  Renowned for its flavour, tenderness, and fatty marble texture...makes my mouth water just thinking about it.  I'm sure it'll cost half of my arm, but I'd like love to have a steak of that! 

- I want to eat sushi/sashimi from a restaurant just outside Tsukiji Fish Market.  Tsukiji is the biggest wholesale fish and seafood market in the world ~ you'll see the most exotic looking fish in all kinds of shapes and sizes...but you have to get there uber early!  Depending on my flight into Tokyo, it could possibly be the first place I go to (also depends on how much luggage I have to carry) since jetlag will definitely be a factor and I'll probably be pretty hungry XD. 

Most of this stuff I can do in Tokyo (since that's where I've been looking into the most recently, plus I plan on staying there the longest throughout my trip).  And luckily most of this stuff is very realistically do-able...though I would like to see a real live Godzilla...dammit! ><** 
🐇 bouncy  |  ♫ Franz Ferdinand - This Fire
Link

Early thoughts on traveling...

Sep. 6th, 2009   10:46 PM
Blah, the last few days seem to have gone by quickly...or rather, my days seem so much shorter when playing computer games (lol).  It was quite a nice change of pace to go out and enjoy the day with my parents today ^^.  (Just if you were curious though, I've drained 36.8 hours into The Orange Box since the beginning of the month)

Started the day by going to Caplansky's Deli for lunch; my dad had heard that they serve really good smoked meat sandwiches.  The service sorta sucked though because it was only the second they since their re-opening into a new location ~ they didn't have turkey or liver (which is pretty much 1/3 of their menu), their credit card network wasn't set up yet, and they had way to many young waiters who didn't know what they were doing.  I had a smoked beef on rye which wasn't bad ~ comparable to Montreal's famous sandwich.  They had four different types of mustard at the table which was pretty awesome.  But in terms of price, there are other cheaper good eats out there in my opinion. 

Afterwards we went to Mountain Equipment Co-op - there's only one in Toronto (King and Spadina) and it's really gianormous.  My parents wanted to look for shoes and clothes for their trip to China later this year.  I loafed around the book section and went through some travel guides of Japan.  Despite intending to do a rough planning of my own trip last week, today was actually the first time I really looked into things.  It got me pretty excited to put it lightly =P  But there's a huge difference between being enthusiastic and realistic, so I really need to dig into researching and planning things accordingly.  So far I've asked Stephanie and Jully if they were interested in coming with me.  Steph is a travel bug but she's realistic as well so I know that for her to commit to something four months in advance is kinda iffy, since in that time she could have a job at stake or something else.  As for Jully, she's Japan crazy so she'd say yes any day...if she weren't strap for cash.  That's totally understandable since I know her situation ~ but it's in four months and who knows how much money she can pool in that time.  I told them both to think about it for a week or two since it is a pretty big decision.  It would be really awesome if they both say yes - not cause I don't wanna travel like a loner >>" I don't mind if it comes down to that - since they're two of my closest friends and if I were to ever experience something as epic, I'd really want them to be the ones by my side.  I've talked to Monica about my plans and she shares similar enthusiasm to travel, but the only bummer is that her parents are of the cliche Indian kind ~ uber protective.  I would love to travel with her since she's such an amazing person, but what can I do about it =(  Like I said though, if it comes down to it then I'll just go by myself even though personally I'd feel much more comfortable going with someone (anyone?) I know.

Anywhoos, I didn't buy any of the travel guides since they average around $30 - I'd rather do as much research online before buying a guide book since it's far more specific with how I'd go about in my planning.  Having not bought anything, my parents and I then went down to Queen's Quay/Harbourfront Centre to check out the Hot & Spicy Food Festival ~ it wasn't that big of a deal.  But then again, it's summer in Toronto where practically every weekend there's stuff happening at either Harbourfront, Dundas Square, Mel Lastman Square, or other places (all of which usually just put up a stage for performance with surrounding attractions).  I guess I'm to use to it and it doesn't impress me much anymore ~ the friggin' Shops at Don Mills sets up a stage and attractions once or twice a month, and that's only a three minute drive from my house, no sense going all the way downtown anymore for that kinda stuff heh.  Anywhoo, there was lots of stuff for the kids and a couple of tents set up with sauce samples.  I can handle most hot sauces so I found some enjoyment in the 'festival', although most sauces tended to specialize in a particular flavour.  For example, some were either sweet (from a fruit or honey) or sour or just plain hot.  The ones I sampled didn't have a really good blend of flavour and heat.

Next we went to Yorkdale mall ~ I haven't been there in months, but then again I tend to only go to malls when I need to buy something rather than window shop.  Again, I loafed around at Indigo book store looking through travel books.  The main thing I'd really like to know is what to do with my luggage while staying at a major city for a couple of days.  Cause I don't plan on staying at a hotel where you can leave stuff in your room no problem ~ I'm bloody cheap so hostles are the way to play it.  But I'm not sure if you can leave things at the hostle even if you get your own private room.  And I doubt they'll have lockers that can fit a suitcase.  I don't mind packing light enough to only need a hiking backpack (I'm thinking European backpacking style here, heh) since I will be washing my clothes as I'll be gone for weeks.  Plus, I'm not fashion savvy ~ I'm a guy: two pairs of pants, two or three sweaters, and enough t-shirts, socks and underwear for five will do me fine.  I'm gonna have to ask my brother how he did it since he traveled to Asia last summer for a month ~ stayed in Japan for nine or eleven days.  I'm also not too too finicky with my meals even though I love Japanese cuisine and do intend to indulge ever so often.  But when I don't, I don't mind living off of convenient stores ~ 7-Eleven's are abundent in Japan and they have a huge selection of prepared foods (unlike the shit they serve here).  It sorta sounds like I'm traveling like a hobo (lol) even though I've got five grand to cover my costs.  Oh well.  I just really want to avoid traveling like a tourist for this trip.  I will go to some tourist sightseeing locations no doubt, but I really just want to travel at my own pace.  I want to walk down streets and neighbourhoods that take me no where in particular - I just want to do it because I can.  I want to sit in a coffee shop and just admire the people.  I want to look at a school from up close and step in a park where the locals would go.  Sure historic temples, department stores, and museums are interesting...but they lack some quality of fun...they're just there to round out the trip, the way I see it.  Maybe my opinions will change as I research and plan for this thing, but currently I just want to Japan and be me rather then some ecstatic traveler hoping to experience as much as they can before moving on. 

(Well that was a pretty long spiel.  You can certainly expect more of that as the week progresses! *evil laugh*)

Finished the day with an incredibly delicious dinner at Saigon Star up in Richmond Hill.  It's a really good Vietness restaurant, but it's a bit more pricey (hellz worth it though).  Practically every table of three people or more had an order of curry crab - its their specialty and if you love crab and you're willing to splurge, I highly recommend it.  I'm crazy for curry, and this place takes the cake ~ it was rich and semi-chunky from the mix of curry, spices/herbs, onion, and coconut oil (not to be confused with coconut milk since that would have made it much creamier).  You can order small baguettes or roti to scope up all the delicious curry since you'll have a lot left over after eating the crab.  The dish is a friggin' foodgasm waiting to happen, no lie!  There's this Chinese restaurant (I think it closed down) that also made really delicious crab ~ it's served in a rum and cream sauce, again bread is essential to soak up all the good stuff.  >>"  Oh gawd, all this typing about food is getting me hungry again.  Oh, and despite all the gaming I've done this week, it hasn't gotten to a point where I've become a fatty XP  I still managed to practice squash and work out three times this week (honestly though, I've been going during the day rather then at night because I've been so bloody bored). 

Okay that's all for tonight ~ it was nice to type mucho grande again having been distracted by computer games the last few days.  Definitely helped clear and organize some of my thoughts :3  I wonder if I'd be able to blog while in Japan .__. if not the, the withdraw might be painful haha!
😌 content  |  ♫ Smashing Pumpkins - X.Y.U.
Link

Quickie post...

Sep. 3rd, 2009   11:52 PM
Another quickie entry tonight ~ started playing Team Fortress 2 two hours ago and haven't stopped until now!  It's pretty awesome despite sucking a lot.  Most of the classes are easy to jump right into while others (engineer and spy) take a bit of learning for me.  So far I've mostly been playing medic and heavy ^^.

The US Open (grand slam tennis tournament) started earlier this week.  I haven't really been paying much attention to it even though it's one of my favourite tournaments to watch.  A lot of the seeded women lost in the first and second round ~ Dementieva (4) surprisingly lost to 17 year old Oudin (70) and Jankovic (5) lost to Shvedova (55) in a pretty horrid tiebreaker.  I got to watch a bit of women's doubles today ~ Venus nailed Serena in the back while serving...I was like O__O holy shit that's gotta be painful! 

Finally got around to listening to 'Self Explanatory', the latest album by Classified.  Solid album.  I totally love the concept of the album, which is choose your own adventure.  So pretty much each track has a bit of storytelling to it and at the end it'll ask whether you want to do a) or b), thus skipping to tracks x) or y).  Obviously you don't have to, but it's all in good fun.  This guy is so true to his roots as well (he's from Nova Scotia), he mentions it a couple of times but the track "Oh...Canada" is definitely something to be proud to listen to.  Honestly, even if you don't like rap, I encourage anyone to give this artist a listen.  He's golden. 

Okay, back to gaming >>"  So far I've played 13.1 hours of Half-Life 2, but I think I'm gonna focus on getting better at TF2 for now XP.

🐇 bouncy  |  ♫ Classified - Trouble

Fan Expo, Buskerfest and ABDL Birthday Party

Aug. 30th, 2009   8:38 PM

Good gawd I did a lot yesterday o__O”.  I am genuinely surprised at myself considering I was running on two hours of sleep.  If I could do it all again I wouldn’t hesitate at the prospect ~ it was just an incredibly fun day! ^^.  I don't usually cut my entries, but I'm gonna be considerate this time since it's long and contains pictures.  
 
 

So, the day before I was trying to catch up on comic books since I didn’t want to go to the Fan Expo any less geeky.  I finished the June batch of the Dark Reign crossover, but only half of the July batch ~ I just read the titles that I liked the most and actually progressed the plot (since many of the mini-series just revolve around a single character and what they were up to during the event).  >>”  As the crossover continues, each month the batches are getting bigger and bigger cause they’re added more and more mini-series (Mr. Negative, Lethal Legion, Sinister Spider-Man, etc) – and some of them aren’t even that good.  Damn Marvel’s evil scheme to make money! XD  Anyways, I ended up not going to bed until 5am ~ wasn’t that bummed since I got through most of what I wanted.   

Woke up at 7am to get ready for the Fan Expo and met up with Mark and Andy at Union Station by 9:30am.  We probably should have arranged to meet earlier because there was already a TON of people at the convention center!  Like you really don’t see the clusterfuk of people until you’ve gotten your ticket and made your way to the very bottom floor since the south building has three floors (this is all outside the dealers/exhibitors area too!).  So the three of us stood in line for a good forty minutes, with easily 500 people in front of us, before actually getting into the expo – but once the lines started moving it was pretty quick.  My first thoughts upon getting in were “holy shit o__O”.  The sheer size of the expo was unbelievable!  The dealers area was easily four times as large as Anime North’s, while the artist alley was about the same size.  (Check out the floor plan.  The line up area to get in is between Hall E Entrance and Hall G ~ so if you can imagine two thousand plus people fitting in there in proportion to everything left of the entrance…fuking gianormous!)   


(This is just the second floor!)

We started from the south – the gaming area.  They had a lot of different consoles hooked up with everything from fps to rock band to fighting to racing games.  I watched more than played since a lot of the games I had no idea what the controls were.  They had a couple of sixty inch plasmas ~ so sexy for gaming! D:  After that we made our way into the comic book area – stopping at the DC table to pick up a TON of free comic books, pins, posters, stickers, and a manga.  Thank gawd a retailer was giving out free plastic bags before entering the expo cause my backpack was already pretty full.  After the freeloading I wanted to check out a Storm Hawks panel at 11am ~ Andy went wandering the retail area since the cartoon wasn’t really his thing.  Scott McNeil and Colin Murdock (the voice actors of Stork and Junko) along with three other writers/directors sat at the panel and despite there being a small audience, it was a really hardcore crowd.  There was actually a group of eight people cosplaying the cast of Storm Hawks ~ they looked really awesome and I wish I took a picture of them >.o  I also scored a free Nerd Corps Entertainment (the production company) t-shirt for asking a question!  It was kinda weird because they were suppose to do a premier screening of Storm Hawks Origins, but instead they showed ‘Cyclonia Rising: Part 1’ (episode 51).  It seemed as though people in the audience had never watched that episode before just from the general vibe and reactions…which is really weird because the episode aired on April 3rd and I’ve even downloaded it.  So I donno what was up with that o__O”.   


(Storm Hawks panel.  Scott McNeil (white hat) and Colin Murdock (far right).)

Andy joined up with Mark and I for another panel I wanted to watch – Marvel Digital Media: Motion Comics.  Lucky enough it was in the same room so we were able to get front row seats as people were leaving and entering.  Sitting at the panel was Joe Quesada (editor in chief of Marvel), Mike Pasciullo (VP of Merchandising & Communications) and Arune Singh (writer).  The turnout was pretty big, but some of the questions asked were kinda lame.  A majority were business and marketing related…which sits uncomfortably for me since I don’t support the industry (I’ve downloaded hundreds of gigs of comic books >>”).  Oh well, I was mainly there for the motion comics – they showed the first episodes of Spider-Woman and Astonishing X-Men, both in different styles.  It was really wicked because the Spider-Woman episode isn’t based off of a comic book, rather the motion comic was made before the published comic (which comes out in October).  The style was similar to the Watchmen motion comic, which I really liked since whole bodies within the frame moved in a rather gritty way.  I didn’t really like the style used in the Astonishing X-Men motion comic since the textures were stretched and skewed giving the effect of facial expression and limb movement (it all seemed way too digitally processed and eerily smooth).   


(Marvel motion comic panel.  Joe Quesada, Mike Pasciullo, and Arune Singh.)

After the panel and going out for lunch, we made our way through the rest of the dealers area for a couple of hours.  It felt sorta like Anime North all over again – there were a lot of stuff to look at, but nothing really jumped out dying to be bought.  There were a few things I’d have liked to gotten for people ($1 yaoi mangas for Jully XD) but I ended up not getting anything (Mark got a Wii accessory and Andy got a figurine thingy).  I didn’t really feel that bummed about it since I still walked out with a ton of free stuff :3.  At 4pm I went to another panel, Marvel: Cup of Joe, for some great Q&A with Joe Quesada.  He really didn’t leak all that much good stuff (always replying the juicy questions with “wait and see” or “possibly coming soon…”) but I do look forward to some of the new titles being released (particularly the “What If…?  Jean Grey didn’t die”).  It was really interesting hearing other people’s questions – the scope and diversity in terms of what people were thinking was really awesome (made me feel less of a geek XD).   


(The line out just outside the south building entrance during lunch time.  This stretched up Lower Simcoe St.)


(The Light Cycle from Tron Legacy.  Joe Quesada signing autographs)


(Nazi price tag if you ask me.  A Green Lantern cosplayer.)


(Power Girl and Two-Face cosplayers.  For the life of me I can't remember where I've seen this anime character ~ really awesome costume though ^^.  I didn't take many pictures while inside the dealers area just cause it was way too crowded.)

Later into the day we left the con and went walking up Spadina Ave. and into Chinatown looking for a place to sit and relax a little (I was a bit whiney cause I was beyond tired >>” my bad).  We went to Tea Shop 168 ~ mmm, I haven’t had bubble tea in a long time.  The sugar rush definitely got me going again, heh.  We then went into Kensington Market and browsed through Blue Banana Market – that store is just way to cool not to go into.  There were a few things I saw that I wanted to get for Dylan’s birthday (got tired of calling him “that guy from the munch” lol, so this is his pseudonym) ~ a banana shaped lollipop and a rubber high-heel shoe door stopper because he’s a drag queen.  I didn’t get either cause lets face it, it’d be pretty damn weird to buy in front of my friends.  After that we walked to College St. and took the street car to the subway station before going our separate ways.  Thanks for the really good time Mark and Andy ~ my geekness made you two look cool! (lol) 


(All the free stuff I got!  The expo program, a manga, lots of pins, a pen, two Green Lantern rings, a couple of posters, and a ton of comic books.  The pile of comics to the left (After Watchmen...what's next?) are pretty cool because they're a selection of really really good series, but summarized just so you get a taste of it ~ so if you liked Watchmen chances are you'll enjoy these as well.)

 

It was about 7:30pm-ish by the time I got down to Union Station.  Went to the LCBO and got four tallboy cans, I figure one an hour would be sufficient despite being able to drink more (thank gawd I didn’t buy more for reasons below!).  Excluding the $27 for the expo ticket, I only spent $3.50 for lunch and $9 for the beer ~ not bad I say ^^;.  To get to Dylan’s place I had to cut through the Buskerfest on Front St.  There was soooo many people and some of the performers were really awesome.  There was a clown on a giant tricycle thingy that shot out fire and bubbles!  There were also a lot of food stands and independent arts and craft retailers, but I didn’t browse through any since everything was really expensive. 


(Lots and lots of people!  There's actually a performer juggling fire in the right picture.)


(Tricycle performance with clowns, bubbles, and fire :D.)

Got to Dylan’s place a little after 8pm and met up with Nebulus64 just as I got buzzed through the front door.  We were the first to arrive but people trickled in not long after (BaBy_SB, K-Dawg, danididit, TorontoGUY and a bunch of other people I’m going to have to make up pseudonyms for ~ I do remember their real names D:<  No lie!).  I think there was about fifteen people or more when the party got off the ground.  I know I say this all the bloody time after one of these events, but whatever…to be yourself and unloosen a little around like minded people is incredibly liberating and fun!    I just can’t express that enough since ABDL play has always been something in my life I’ve grown accustom to enjoying in private.  To do it with others where the ambiance feels so right…it’s indescribable.  Plus, you can’t go wrong when the party involves diapers, onesies, hawt looking guys {o’.’o}, cute guys wrestling, paper airplanes, foreplay, ass slapping, racial humour, sitting on the ground in a circle, hats, body hair, weed and mushrooms, pizza, cake, and a bunch of other stuff! (Yesh…I just summed up the night with only words that could come together and make sense to me and those at the party lol.  A lot happened and trying to organize my thoughts could give me a hemorrhage.) 

Good gawd man, I ate shrooms three times last night o__O.  That was a fun thing to try =P.  I didn’t have a lot (maybe like a tablespoon) but it certainly got me laughing at just about everything.  I was still in control of myself (just had to keep reminding myself while taking deep breaths) and I’m sure I didn’t say anything stupid (maybe acted a little weird), but I did repeated myself a lot ~ ‘you got my back?’ lol!  XD  And the weed…oh gawd, aside from taking a puff of the joint more times than I remember, the best had to be with the pipe.  I took a couple of hits while the bud was still fresh and man was it ever smooth.  Seriously, thank gawd I didn’t come with more beer ~ my sense of balance and reaction time was just butchered XP.   

If and when I ever move out I’d really like to live on the east side of downtown.  A lot of the people at the party live practically a block away from one another.  There was quite a lot of talk about moving ~ so the prospect of having a fellow ABDL as a roommate in the future really isn’t something unfeasible.  That would be so awesome, but certainly something I won’t need to think about for a good few years down the line since I’m so financially insecure >>”.   

Lastly, I so appreciate the people looking out for me.  Had K-Dawg not changed me at the end of the night it’d have probably taken me twenty minutes of straight up difficulty XD.  And I can’t thank Nebulus64 enough for driving me home all the time ~ I’m really grateful ^^.  I got home a little after 2am, took out my contacts, and just passed out in bed with all my clothes and diaper on.  =.=* I’m actually quite amazed I woke up before 9am with only a bit of a headache.  All I did today was bum around and recuperate. 

 

😌 content  |  ♫ Tool - The Pot

Even more art! XD

Aug. 28th, 2009   11:00 PM

Art update ^^

Aug. 27th, 2009   1:28 AM
Wickedness!  Scored me a free ACEO pic by talutie earlier today!  I am quite tempted to commission her for two more since she's made a pretty good offer ~ one for $8, while two for $12 plus shipping.  I donno though since my wallet is a bit light.  I'll sleep on it.  Here's the pic she drew for me, I just asked for something musical and playful ^^. 


Also, PadderCat is uStreaming right now and he's working on my pic! *squeak!*  XD  He started at 11pm and finished the sketch, after making a small change, at 12:30am.  Check it out :3

He's working on the lineart right now then afterwards it'll be coloured and shaded!  I can't wait ~ and I wish I could keep watching him but I'm uber tired D:

Man, the only thing that would totally complete this night would be getting my badge from Seasenshi.  She sent me a note the other night say she'd scan and mail it today.  Oh well, at least I know it's done and I trust it'll look awesome ^^.

Aug. 26, 2009

Aug. 26th, 2009   8:26 PM
Happy birthday Andy! ^^  (Thank gawd for the creators of BirthdayAlarm.com ~ I have no idea when I signed up or which of my friends it's tracking, but I'm so glad I got something looking out for my forgetfulness XP) Where do the years go?  We've known each other for a decade and it amazes me how much we've changed.  You're always thinking or holding an opinion on something, which is hilarious because you've got such a poker face ~ I've always thought that made you a really interesting person.  Plus, you were such a ladies man!  I wonder if that's still true about you today, heh.  Oh man, I just remembered some of the gifts I've gotten you throughout the years - the one that tops the cake was a mug for Christmas.  A few days later you told me the tag was still on it...from my grandma!  Shit was that ever embarrasing, but so friggin' funny now that I think about it.  Gawd I'm  such an awful person! XD  Anyways, keep that head up as you fight the good fight called life ~ you're capable of a lot of things and I know it'll be really deserving when you've earned it.  I'm glad I have you as a friend, and here's hoping to many years to come! :3

Catching up on comic books!

Aug. 26th, 2009   7:22 PM
Haven't had the time to read any comic books in a few weeks, so I thought kill a few hours and do so today.  Right now I'm four issues from finishing the May batch of Dark Reign crossover.  I've already downloaded June and July ~ in total that's 53 issues.  I really really really want to finish them all before Saturday because I want to keep current when I go to the Fan Expo.  I'm sure I can make it, if not then I'll just read the titles I'm most interested in.  Speaking of which, as the crossover progresses, a couple more titles have gotten their own limited series and they're very good!  While others, not so much ~ the four part "All-New Savage She-Hulk" is kinda lame, Punisher is edging towards lame after introducing resurrection (a huge cliché in comics), and Wolverine Origins surprisingly breaks away from logic and story context (hopefully it's just this arc).  Here are some titles I'm really digging:

Secret Warriors - Nick Fury: Agent of Nothing
Really is a really solid series ~ possibly the most exciting of all the titles in the crossover because it nicely blends new and old characters. (Brian Bendis, the writer, is incredible!)  The fourth issue (May released) really builds up a great hype for the next issue ~ a three sided war between Howling Commandos (lead by Nick Fury), Hydra (lead by Baron Strucker), and H.A.M.M.E.R (lead by Norman Osborn).  It's gonna be awesome!  What's more, the Secret Warriors have recruited a replacement for Slingshot (cause her arms got chopped off XD) and he's a reality warper!  Here's a few panels out of the May release when Quake and Druid try to recruit Eden Fesi ~ you'll know why I'm showing it, heh.


Dark Reign: Elektra
This mini-series is coming along nicely despite the weak first few pages of the first issue (lets just say it leavings you saying "really now? Pfff").  The series is pretty much about Elektra trying to figure out what her impostor fuked around in while she was abducted by the Skrull during the Secret Invasion crossover.  A whole roster of assassins want her dead, H.A.M.M.E.R is after her for information (because she was the very first abductee who was tortured and cloned), and best of all her old rival Bullseye (in Hawkeye's costume) won't let anyone kill Elektra but himself!  I really love the Bullseye/Elektra chemistry, not because they have a long history, but just cause it's so hawt and bloody (lol).  Speaking of which...

Dark Reign: Hawkeye
I'm only on the second issue of the five part series, and I'm loving Bullseye's (in Hawkeye's costume ~ part of the Dark Avengers dillio) psycho-ness.  He totally fits the Hawkeye character as well ~ they're both incredible marksmen and they're both big talkers.  The only difference is Bullseye is just friggin' insane!  I lost count of how many innocent people he's killed in the last two issues! XD  I hope the third issue (June release) will be good since the second one ended with what seems to be the schizo card ~ regardless though, there are bound to be some awesome twists. 

Deadpool
More Bullseye! (in Hawkeye's costume)  I've always loved the Deadpool series just cause it's so hilarious, but now that issue 10 (May release) has him going up against Bullseye...more to love! 

New Avengers
The search for the new Sorcerer Supreme is over (as concluded in issue 53, May release).  Unfortunately I have no clue who this character is ~ wiki'd him and he doesn't seem to have that big of a role in the Marvel universe.  I guess all that will change!  Every issue prior has had a excellent pacing, interesting plot development and characterization (especially since the team consists of SO many different flavours).  Gonna have to say it again...Brian Bendis is the man! 

Anywhoos, I'm looking forward to Saturday.  Going to the Fan Expo with Mark and Andy ~ haven't gotten word from Kelvin yet.  Every year the four of us try to get together once or twice just to catch up and play some video games or whatever.  I remember writing about the last time we meet up way the hell back in December and it's unfortunate that we don't get together more often let alone chat online (especially with Mark since I've known him since grade three o__O).  Oh wells, I doubt I'll be spending much at the con ~ hellz I download all my comic books, don't think I'll be supporting the industry any time soon!  Afterwards I'll be going to a fellow muncher's birthday party which isn't too far from the convention centre.  I'll have to be discrete with what I bring since I'll have to keep it in my backpack all day ~ plus I'll need to hit the LCBO on the way there.  >>" Hope I can get a ride home cause I rarely ever go way downtown and I'm kinda prone to getting lost (lol).  
🌿 calm  |  ♫ Marilyn Manson - The Fall of Adam
Link

Morning bike ride...

Aug. 24th, 2009   12:36 PM
Blah, my legs feel like cooked noodles (x__X) but everything else feels great!  I just got home not long ago from biking with my dad.  We started at Edwards Garden (which is less than 5 minutes from where I live), and then went south down Wilket Creek Path and Sunnybrook Park, then went east into Taylor Creek Park before stopping at Victoria Park Ave.  All throughout there was a great mix of hills, flats, bridges, creeks, and a bunch of other smelly/naturey goodness.  At some points the creek had flooded due to the storm from several days ago, which caused parts of the path to flood ~ I took in to much speed at one of them I totally got sprayed, which suchs cause I had just washed the white clothes I was wearing ><**.  In total we did 32km in about an hour and a half.  I don't know if that's above or below average, but it felt rather slow to me despite having to play catch-up with my dad on a number of occasions (he's a bike fanatic and despite not being in the best of shape, he can out pace me any day ^^;;). 
😌 content  |  ♫ Blink 182 - Not Now
Link

Chinatown, Kensington, and Baldwin...and tennis...

Aug. 24th, 2009   12:46 AM
I am too lazy to write at length about my day (Aug 23) in and around Chinatown with my parents for the whatever festival they had going on.  I don't not want to write about it since this last month I've been to the 'Taste of the Danforth' festival, as well as some other festival at the 'Shops at Don Mills' but skipped on writing entries about them.  So instead, here are some poor quality cellphone camera pictures (I toggled with the contrast and brightness to make them less crappy) along with a bit of caption ~ after the cut.



So they closed off the southbound side of Spadina Ave. and at one section there was this small stage.  They had live music, a fashion runway, and martial arts demos ~ none of which were very entertaining to be honest.


Despite brightening these pictures, threatening clouds were present all throughout the afternoon.  Luckily it didn't rain since it probably would've made Chinatown smell even worse than it already does >>".  I'm not hating, Toronto Chinatown is literally just a very very dirty place ~ don't wear flip flops and avoid the water on the ground!


There were a couple of food tents lined up.  These two sold bbq meat on a stick ~ the tent to the left is cheaper and tastier, but the tent to the right had squid tentacles. 


This is a coconut drink - practically every time I go to Chinatown I get one of these because they're so damn refreshing.  Coconut juice is actually very good for you, but the meat itself not so much.  They sell them $2 for one, or $5 for three ~ they use a meat cleaver on the spot to make the hole at the top.  


Decapitated Chinese dragon heads and a drum used for the dragon dance performance.


If you've ever walked down Spadina Ave, you'll notice a TON of incredibly cheap clothes, shoes, luggage bags, and a bunch of other stuff.  I just thought this sign was funny =P.


After walking along Chinatown, we went to Kensington Market.  There's this store called Blue Banana Market - it's huge and they sell a ton of really awesome artsy, creative, niffty, stuff.  This is actually a plastic bowl, the hat is a lid ~ thought it was hilarious!  I'm totally gonna have to check this store out again sometime, its got something for everyone but the prices are pretty steep. 


After Kensington Market we went to Baldwin St (donno if it's considered a part of Chinatown or wherever).  They closed off the street and had a couple of tents set up - the Art Gallery of Ontario had two tents with arts and craft activities for kids. 


You can't see it, but near the end of the street there was a huge chess and Scrabble board set up with people playing them.


Totting some awesome tunes. 

There were a lot of things to see beyond the pictures I took.  And there was quite a few good eats along the way that I won't go into just cause I'm really drained right now.  In any case, it was a good day ^^.  Going to bed now, peace out.


(Edit: I forgot, today were the finals at the Rogers Cup and Cincinnati Open.  Roger Federer (1) beats Novak Djokovic (4) an easy 6-1, 7-5.  Elena Dementieva (4) beat Maria Sharapova a smooth 6-4, 6-3.  Sharapova will climb the ladder up to the thirtieth ranking ~ not bad for a gal whose been off the tour for nine months due to shoulder surgery ^^.)

😴 tired  |  ♫ Torley on Piano - The Games We Played
