Commissions and a WTF bid...

Aug. 23rd, 2009   11:47 PM
I made a spreadsheet to keep track of the commissions I've got going for me because I actually forgot about a few of them until I dug through my inbox.  And yesh, I've been keeping my spending on the down-low ~ my will to resist is so weak >>" I know (lol).  Five of the nine commissions will actually be something physical and delivered to me ^^, always nice to have something beyond the computer pixels I say.  Here's a run down on the artists: PadderCat, Jade Fox, Marci, Lincub, Kalamu, Kalida, Seasenshi, Vombatiformes, and ClownCarOnFire.  Hmmm, it's hard to say which one I'm looking forward to the most, maybe Vombat since it's a plushie of my fursona XD.  Earlier tonight Dingbat was uStreaming and I thought it was really awesome since every other time I've watched artists work, they're drawing.  Instead, Dingbat was making ceramic cup and pots on a spinning wheel...I am now tempted to get something from her >>".  But I'm pretty sure I have enough strength to resist that temptation, heh.

And to my defense, I am not a big spender! XD  Just look at this ridonculous bid!  Basically Michele Light is opening one spot for commissions, having not done so since late 2006.  Rather than taking a first come first serve, she's set up a bid for that spot...starting at $250...and right now it's at $425 with 11 bids?!?!  Holy shit...is one commission off of her honestly worth $425...let alone $250?  Well obviously someone thinks so >>".  Gawd, if I had that kind of money to spend...well let's not get into that.  But seriously...freakin' nuts for an artist whose been criticized (near the bottom of that link) for being too repetitious with the faces of many characters being remarkably similar to one another ~ sorta like Marci.  But hey, I'm totally not hating on her ~ I think her art is cool...I'm just impressed and surprised how high value she and other people think her art is >>".
🔍 curious  |  ♫ Linkin Park - A Place for my Head
Link

o__o watch District 9....now!

Aug. 23rd, 2009   12:43 AM
Got home not long ago from watching District 9 with my parents ~ it's just cheaper that way ><** shuuudup!  Just wow...this movie is beyond awesome.  It's friggin' groundbreaking for its time because of this perfect combination of action, drama, thrill, effects, everything you want in a sci-fi movie (groundbreaking like Blair Witch Project for its time ~ the calibur of horror/thriller was so different and fresh).  Gawd, it's brilliant...you must watch it preferably as virgin as you can ~ as in read as little reviews before watching it.  I don't even know how to approach a more indepth entry about this movie...everything was just so good.  The aliens...wow!  I love how they were a subclass to people of second and third class already ~ it just makes it feel so much more grounded and deeply human.  The documentary style totally completed this feel, not to mention the remarkable CGI.  All the aliens are CGI, and yet the detail and character put into them are so believable.  The level of violence - and gore - were really cool (it's like watching scenes from Transformers all over again).  Huge props to Neill Blomkamp who directed and co-wrote the movie ~ he's only 29!  I really hope there'll be a sequl because the ending is totally asking for one.  >:D Go watch it now! 
🐇 bouncy  |  ♫ Bloc Party - The Pioneers
Link

Semifinals day in tennis...

Aug. 22nd, 2009   6:48 PM
This should be a very good weekend if love tennis.  Playing today are the semi-finals for the men's Cincinnati Open and women's Rogers Cup.  In the men's tournament, Roger Federer (1) defeated Andy Murray (2) in two sets 6-2, 7-6(8), and Rafael Nadal (2) will play against Novak Djokovic (4) later in the evening.  In the women's tournament, Elena Dementieva (4) defeated a very disappointing Serena Williams (2) also in two sets 7-6(2), 6-1m and in the evening Maria Sharapova (49) is up against Alisa Kleybanova (36).  Good gawd did Serena Williams play horribly today ~ I was quite tempted to tone off had their been nothing else to watch on tv.  This is the problem with her, she doesn't play consistently in the smaller WTA tournaments but she plays phenomenal in the Grand Slams.  This is also why she doesn't have the number one spot (which is her current beef with Dinara Safina since Serena has won the last three Grand Slams, while Safina has won many of the smaller tournaments).  I love Serena, I really do, but she just played so shitty today.  In the first set, Dementieva had an early service break over Williams, at 2-1.  Williams broke back and kept on serve until gaining the lead at 5-3.  Between the break point, there were some amazing plays by both players despite Williams looking very casual ~ unlike her usual fierce self.  Dementieva's serve is one of her weaknesses since she lacks variety - most of the time they to the forehand for an easy return.  However, throughout the set she had very good ball control and pacing - she returned wide and quite disappointingly Williams made zero effort to go after the ball.  What's even more impressive is that Dementieva caught up for a 6-5 lead.  The only sucky thing was that Dementieva could have closed the set much quicker had she not missed a very crucial point - Williams made a defensive lobe that went way high, Dementieva attempted to return it like a serve but it barely cleared the net.  In the tiebreak, Dementieva sweeped it with a 7-2 win ~ both players had double faults. In the second set, Wiliams sense of urgency - to go after the ball and to quickly get in position for the next return - was still absent.  She made sooooooo many silly mistakes and unforced errors it was disappointing to watch.  Her mental focus really wasn't there despite the crowd giving her much cheer, and Dementieva really took advantage of that weakness with some deep returns.  Williams should have been more aggressive by going up to the net more since every time that she did in this match, she won the point (she plays doubles tennis so her volleying skills are very good).  She wasn't able to win a point until 3-1.  At 5-1 Williams finally began to show her aggressive and powerful style of playing by moving quicker and making a fuking huge smash.  Unfortunately her efforts were too late and she lost having missed by large margins and making a lot of unnecessary mistakes.  Bah, oh well, Dementieva is a really great player with lots of athleticism - the only thing I see going against her is the fact that she's 27.  At that age you aren't close to your end, but you'll certainly have a bit of a tougher time against the younger, rising stars.  What's more, Dementieva could have been so much more a lot earlier had she not had her mom (who isn't a tennis player) as a coach (no offense).  Only until 2004 did she get an actual tennis coach was when she began playing really great (reaching the 2004 finals in the French Open and US Open) ~ she turned pro in 1998, so that goes to show that she's been on the tour for over a decade.  Oh well, she's still a very accomplished player (Olympic gold medalist at Beijing and silver at Sydney) with a few years left in her game ^^.
😑 blah  |  ♫ Backstreet Boys - Incomplete
Link

'-ology' Meme

Aug. 22nd, 2009   5:19 PM
This was pretty fun to do ~ I encourage others to give it a go if they're interested.  Gimme a shout if you do cause I'd be interested in seeing what you wrote ^^.

Taken from   kaffre

FOODOLOGY

1. What is your salad dressing of choice?
Ranch or balsamic vinaigrette.

2. What is your favorite sit-down restaurant?
Century Palace Chinese Restaurant ~ I go there for dim sum at least twice a month.

3. What food could you eat every day for two weeks and not get sick of?
There are actually a few, but pizza, dumplings or curry top the list. :D

TECHNOLOGY

1. How many televisions are in your house?
Four.

2. How long would it take you to look up who invented the Rubber Bands
I just did, and it took less than 5 seconds! XD

3. Have any idea how many Gigahertz your computer has?
Yup, two gigahertz.

BIOLOGY

1. Are you right-handed or left-handed?
When I was learning to write, I was left-handed. My parents forced me to be right-handed though. :(

2. Have you ever had anything removed from your body?
Other than teeth, nope.

3. What is the last heavy item you lifted?
Fifty pound dumbbell the other night.

4. Have you ever been knocked unconscious?
Yes, I got hit in the head with a tire swing at a summer day camp when I was little ~ got stitches above my left eyebrow.

RANDOMOLOGY

1. If it were possible, would you want to know the day you were going to die?
Yes, so I can plan ahead. Not to prevent it, but to say goodbye and then party hard! XD

2. If you could change your name, what would you change it to?
I would, but I haven't settled with what it would be.

3. Would you drink an entire bottle of hot sauce for $1000?
Yes, I like hot food and I don't find some hot sauces that hot.

DUMBOLOGY

1. Last time you had a run-in with the cops?
Earlier this year a cop gave me a parking ticket while I was heading towards my car.

2. Last person you hugged?
My dad.

FAVORITOLOGY

1. Season?
Spring ~ it isn't too hot nor cold, just perfect for my taste.

2. Holiday?
Christmas because it gets me into a really jolly mood. Plus shopping is pretty fun. ^^

3. Day of the week?
Friday or Saturday.

4. Month?
June.

CURRENTOLOGY

1. Miss someone?
Yes, a friend I haven't seen in a while. Whenever I'm busy, she's free, and vice versa.

2. What are you listening to?
AOK Collective - Incredible.

RANDOMOLOGY

1. First place you went this morning?
Bathroom.

2. Do you smile often?
Not as often as I should.

OTHER-OLOGY

1. Do you always answer your phone?
I miss quite a few calls because my Blackberry isn't very loud, so not always.

2. If you could change your eye color what would it be?
Gray or purple.

3. Have you ever had a pet fish?
Pfff yes! I've had at least twenty goldfishes ~ I'm so Chinese >>".

4. Favorite Christmas song(s)?
I don't have one.

5. What's on your wish list for your birthday?
There are a lot of pricey things I want, but a simple bunny plushie tops the list. *cough**cough*Sept 28th**cough*

6. Does the future make you more nervous or excited?
Both. I'm graduating soon and possibly travelling afterwards! Then after that I have no idea what I'll do with my life. ^^;;

7. Do you have any saved texts?
Yes, I am quite sentimental.

8. Ever been in a car wreck?
Nope, but I have drove into the side of a car ~ it wasn't anything major though.

9. Do you have an accent?
Not that I know of. I'm Canadian but I don't speak like a lumberjack nor do I say 'eh' after ever sentence.

10. What is the last song to make you cry?
Can't say I remember.

11. Plans tonight?
Making plans for future plans! XD Other than that, I don't know since things to do come sporadically for me.

12. Have you ever felt like you hit rock bottom?
Not yet I haven't. Hope I never will.

13. Current worry?
Friends and finance.

14. Current hate right now?
Not knowing what to do with my life. :(

15. Met someone who changed your life?
In the last year there have been quite a few people actually ~ thank you to those people. ^^

16. What song represents you?
Nujabes - A Day by Atmosphere Supreme. Just because it's boring but mellowing at the same time. :P

17. Would you go back in time if you were given the chance?
Yes and I'd take along a few textbooks so I could change history. :D

18. Have you ever dated someone longer than a year?
Nope, only a few months at a time.

19. Do you have any tattoos/piercings?
Nope, although I have thought about getting a tattoo on my right shoulder.

20. Ever had someone sing to you?
Yup. ^^

21. Do you like to cuddle?
Very much I do. :3

22. What kind of music did you listen to in elementary school?
90s music ~ pop groups and alternative rock.

23. Are most of the friends in your life new or old?
I have some old and some new - it's pretty evenly split.
🥱 bored  |  ♫ Asian Kung-Fu Generation - The Far Far Side
Link

Schools out for summer!

Aug. 21st, 2009   11:13 PM
W00t!  Summer school is over and I had a good day to kick it off ^^.  The HLTB01 exam was hardly difficult so I'm pretty confident with how that went.  It only sucked to have to wake up so early to get to school by 9am ~ it was a two hour exam but I finished it in one.  Got home by 11am, ate lunch and then began cleaning my room.  At the end of every semester I tend to clean out my binders and reorganize my bookshelf, and eventually I just end up cleaning everything else while throwing out a ton of stuff.  I keep most of the books that I've bought for classes cause I've always had this ideal image of owning something I can actually show off in the future.  I guess I just feel that it's somewhat of a reflection of all the things I've learned since I've quite literally read each and every book ~ there's approximately 50, but size and thickness vary greatly.  I might have to sell a few next semester though because my wallet is getting thinner.   When my dad came home I helped him with the groceries.  The other day he wanted to know if I'd be interested in going to the CNE today since it's opening day and admissions are only $1.75 to mark its 175th anniversary.  Regular admission is around $15 so the prospect of going was kinda tempting.  But the weather was pretty hot day and in general there really isn't much to do at the CNE since a lot of the attractions don't appeal to me ~ so I turned him down.  Rather, I suggested we go watch a movie since it'd been ages we've done that together.  We watched Inglourious Basterds which I thought was really fun watching.  The plot isn't to complicated, but it develops rather cleverly between a mash-up of two other plots.  The cast and the characters are very well assembled ~ Christoph Waltz's acting was brilliant.  My only complaint was the length (two and a half hours) since some scenes could have been shortened.  If you love Tarantino flicks, this one is probably for you since it's got a lot of his hallmarks.  There are still a couple of movies I'd like to watch, which hopefully I'll be able to during this break in school, but District 9 is definitely at the top of my list.  Later at night I had a really great workout.  I played an hour of squash and then went to the fitness room for half an hour to work on my upper body.  The room was completely empty which was a nice change since usually there are at least two or three other people.  My aunt's condo has a swimming pool which I'd like to take a dip in someday ~ I haven't swam in years and I don't even think I own trunks that fit me anymore, heh.  Swimming is practically a full body workout since you use almost all your muscles, plus it'd be nice to be in a pool when the weather outside is blazing.   Anywhoo, the main thing I'd like to get done this break (since school starts up again on Sept 10th) is to call out some friends and see if they'd be interested in travelling come New Year.  Right now I have my sights set on Japan.  Ideally I'd like to go to one area for a few weeks and get the full experience rather than travel like a tourist and only get a day or two of guided tour before having to move on to another area.  The thing with Japan though is that it's a really big place ~ even though I don't mind going to only a handful of prefectures, the weather can be wildly diverse depending on where you go.  So packing may be a pain.  I suppose it also depends on who can come with me, but at this point I'm just too enthusiastic and getting way ahead of myself, heh.  I really want to get some serious planning done though since I've never travelled without my parents before and this sense of experiencing something new for myself is really important to me.  
😴 tired  |  ♫ Red Hot Chili Peppers - Venice Queen

HELLZ YEAH!!! XD

Aug. 20th, 2009   6:32 PM
WOOHOO!!!  I passed STAB22 and did better than I expected!  I got a call from Suki not long ago and she said the marks were up ~ I checked this morning just cause I also had to make sure all my fees were paid, and they weren't up.  I wasn't going to check again until next week.  Holy crap, I am so happy!  My final grade is better than my midterm mark ~ which was worth 40% (quizzes from tutorial were worth 10% while the final exam was worth 50%).  So I know I did good on the final exam despite how bloody difficult it was.  I think part of the reason might also be because the prof bell curved the class average ~ but I have no way of confirming that.  But who gives a damn, I'M FREE FROM HARDCORE MATH FOREVER!!! XD *does the happy dance*

One more...babble...

Aug. 20th, 2009   3:24 PM
You know what sucks about having an exam on the very last day of the exam schedule?  Other then the fact that your summer gets cut that much shorter (despite being a summer semester), and I just have absolutely no motivation to study.  For the most part this always seems to be the case with having one last exam ~ it just sucks double that I have to wait days on end just to get it over with.  For the last two days I've been studying just fine for my HLTB01 exam tomorrow morning, but today I am just so antsy and I keep distracting myself.  I know I have to do well for this exam since the last two haven't been sitting very well for me, yet I'm not too too worried since some of the course material is quite easy.  I really just want to get on with my summer since I haven't stepped foot outside my house for maybe a week @_@. 

I was gonna write an entry about social media and the retarded short-term study on Twitter carried out by the market research firm Pear Analytics ~ since social media fascinates me and reading some news articles concerning the Twitter topic made me realize something about myself.  But, it's pretty much old news by now so I'm not gonna bother with it.  Basically Pear Analytics did an unscientific, and non-representative study on Twitter to which they put a couple thousand tweets into poorly defined categories.  They claim that 40% of all tweets are pointless babble.  There are plenty of news and review articles about it, but here's one if you're interested.  Blunty, an Australian YouTube personnel, makes a great point about what 'pointless babble' is which has got me thinking.  Man, I really suck at making small talk >>"  I'm still trying to figure out why it is I generally have any to say when face-to-face with people - other than not sharing similar interests, a general disregard for certain topics, and all-around just being a boring person.  Being a 'listener' type of person sucks most of the time, and  I completely agree that small﻿ talk is essential for social interaction.  So I'm gonna need to work on that =\. 

Anyways, back to pretending to study.  
🥱 bored  |  ♫ The White Stripes - Blue Orchid

Squashed...XP

Aug. 17th, 2009   11:32 PM
Bah, despite all the practicing I've only gotten marginally better at squash.  My brother and I played tonight and I lost five games straight.  Each game is best to 11 points, so for the first four games I only won 1, 2, 4, and 6 points.  On our last game we played best to 7 and I had a 3 point lead as handicap.  I only won 2 additional points from that game before losing >>".  Man, as much as I love this intensely fun sport, I'm just totally worn out now and probably won't take a swing at the ball until the weekend XP.

Oh noes, Monfils! D:

Aug. 17th, 2009   8:34 PM
I don't usually watch the first to third round matches of tennis tournaments that get aired on tv mainly because it's usually expected the higher ranked players will advance and very rarely will there be a rising player to debut with a surprising upset against the competition.  But just on a whim today while doing laundry at 5pm, I turned on the tube to see if any women's matches were on at the Rogers Cup here in Toronto.  I caught the second set of the first round match between Agnieszka Radwanska (14) and Carla Suarez Navarro; Radwanska won 6-3, 6-3.  Meh.  The match was rather boring in my opinion.  In general, I'm not as enthusiastic about women's tennis mainly because I just don't keep current enough to know what's going on.  Sure I know and love the top dogs (like the Williams, Safina, Sharapova, Jankovic, Dementieva, etc), but I won't really go out of my way to watch a women's match unless it's between two great players, or nearing the end of a tourney. 

Luckily, right after the match finished they began airing the men's first round matches of the Western & Southern Financial Group Masters (aka Cincinnati Open, or Cincinnati Masters 1000).  Bah, I hate these _____ Open or _____ Masters tournament sponsored as ________.  It's just like the Canadian Open (or Canadian Masters)...sponsored as Rogers Cup - as in the Rogers from Rogers Communication Inc =.=**  It's just so corporate and rolls off the tongue all wrong.  Oh well, some ones gotta pay the bulk of everything.  They started airing the second set match between Ivo Karlović and Gaël Monfils (13).  There's rarely a dull match when Gaël is on the court, what's more Karlović is a very respectable player (I remember writing about during the Wimbledon quarter-finals against Roger Federer).  Maybe it's just because Karlović wasn't playing against the great Federer, but he seemed to be in really great form today!  He's the tallest player on the circuit and he utilizes a serve-and-volley technique very well because of that ~ basically he serves on a wide angle so the returner has to move away from the court, he then goes up to the net and volleys wide in the opposite direction (for a giant, his low volleys are very impressive).  His serving percentage is really amazing as well (both first and second serve).  On the other hand, Monfils is one of the most athletic players on the circuit ~ he can cover a lot of ground really really quickly and he's not afraid to dive for the ball (prime example right here).  This is why he's such a crowd pleaser and greatly intensifying the game (plus he's only 22 and fuking incredible!).  For the most part he's a defensive player, mostly returning from the baseline, but he's fairly well-rounded as well since he's pretty good up at the net. 

The second set was really intense.  Monfils lost the first set 6-4 and took the second set to a tiebreaker.  At 5-4 in the tiebreak, Karlović made his second serve but after a short rally, Monfils lobed it to the baseline for the point - it was amazing...to even lobe a ball over a giant is impressive enough.  On Monfils then took the next two points on his serve, one of which was a very beautiful net return.  Karlović lost the next point on his serve taking the game to a third set.  What's funny is that Karlović never lost his cool despite being only two points away from closing the match ~ which must've been friggin' hard to swallow.  But I was really happy for Monfils since I wanted him to win.  But more rather, they're both amazing players and it sucks that only one can get through to the next round. 

In the third set, Monfils played it smarter by going up to the net more often - by dominating space on the court you put pressure on your opponent to move back (like any technique though it has it's pros and cons ~ the returner needs great reflexes because once the ball goes through your net coverage you've pretty much lost the point).  Karlović was no dummy either though, unlike some serve-and-volley players, he moved diagonally rather than laterally when attacking the ball at the net - this way you cut off the ball while ensuring enough time to plan your swing.  The third set went into a tiebreak (which was only appropriate since they're both such amazing players) where it was the serve that really determined the match.  Karlović served first with an ace.  Monfils served next, losing a point to a net return but winning a point with an unreturnable serve.  Karlović's next two serves were aces making the tiebreak 4-1!  On Monfils next two serves he won a point, but lost a point from an authoritative overhead volley return.  Karlović closed the game with two more aces on his serve!  o__O 21 aces by him in this one match!  Man was that ever a good match ~ Monfils fought so hard and played so well, but it just wasn't enough for the much more experienced Karlović.  I really ought to check out the draw sheets before tournaments start so I know which matches are worth watching even if they're first to third round matches =\. 
🌿 calm  |  ♫ Quantic - Infinite Regression
Link

Fmylife - simply hilarious.  It's like reading twitter updates, but on crack!  Basically people post a couple lines of funny, fail, and unfortunate moments.

Music worth listening:
For the last couple of days I've been really loving an album called Angles by dan le sac vs. Scroobius Pip.  I've known about them since their video 'Thou Shall Always Kill' was posted on YouTube two years back, but I never really looked into them until recently.  For the most part, this album is solid.  If you're interested in some really amazing lyrics (the kind where he takes on different personas) over repetitive beats check them out.  Tracks I love the most: Tommy C, Letter from Man to God, and Waiting for the Beat to Kick In…

New art on its way:

freudian_lisp
 or Kalamu on FA.

Rogers Cup Finals: Andy Murray (2) vs. Del Potro (6)

Aug. 16th, 2009   6:04 PM
Murry won with a final score of 6-7(4), 7-6 (3), 6-1 Well that was a bit of a let down =P  It was a good match with some high quality playing, but it lacked edge-off-your-seat intensity and memorable plays.  The gist is that they both played really consistently - so much so that they both hit a sort of brick wall.  Murray didn't mix up his shots as well as I'd have hoped since one making Del Potro run is a key to winning against him.  So despite playing very well, he didn't really play smart.  On the other hand, Del Potro was playing exceptionally well.  For the first set and most of the second set, Del Potro clearly played just better enough when the points really mattered.  It's to bad he ran out of juice in the third set - if he had the stamina, it would've been anyone's game.  One thing with Del Potro though is that if he improves his net game signficantly, he'll be a very serious threat.  This could been seen in the first set during the 4-5 set as well as the 5-6 rally up at net to tiebreak the set.  Throughout the entire game Del Potro looked and acted so fatigue ~ moving sluggish when not playing, taking his sweet ass time and abusing the time limit rule, and leaning on his racket for support between points.  But the incredible thing was for the first two sets everytime he put on that tired look, he'd come back and blast the big points!  It's like a bloody contradiction if you're trying to read the guy or predict his performance.  The man can definitely rise to the occasion when it matters the most, so you can't count him out until you really see his playing slip.  The second set started with back-to-back service breaks before going back on serve at 2-2.  Again, at 4-4 Del Potro makes an amazing net return that goes narrowly crosscourt ~ it's almost like a threat to Murray that drop shots aren't as effective as he'd hope they be (since a drop shot forces the returner to run up to the net).  Very well played second set tiebreak by Murray ~ winning a one point lead not on his serve making it 3-2 and then again at 5-3.  So at the end of the second set, two and a quarter hours have gone by and it literally feels as though its become a stalemate.  Del Potro should have won the second set, imo, had he not made a few unnecessary errors.   In the third set, Del Potro just ran outta gusto - more physically then mentally since it was very apparent that he was trying and not tanking.  One main reason for his fatigue was because he had a very long evening match against Andy Roddick, where as Murray had a morning match against Tsonga yesterday, which only lasted an hour and twenty minutes.  So the amount of recover definitely has something to do with it.  Plus, the road to the finals ~ Del Potro had a tougher time since he had to play against Nadal (then 2) and Roddick (5), while Murray played Davydenko (8) and Tsonga (7).  No offence to any of those players, but ranking does translate to playing ability, so Del Potro definitely had it worse.  Oh wells, I'm very happy for Murray and his first victory in the number two spot.  What's more, he's the first British player to win the Rogers Cup (formerly called the Canadian Open ~ which is the third oldest big tourney around (1881)).  I've got a good feeling he'll eventually reach number one in the world (if even for a short period of time) since he's quite similar to other top contenders ~ Federer, Nadal, Djokovic, and even Roddick (in recent months) are percentage players.  Meaning these players don't take risks very often and their shots are quite calculated.  So for example, if you have the choice to go for a containing cross-court (70% chance of success) or a clean winner down the line (30% chance of success), they'd go with the play that has the highest percentage.  Del Potro on the other hand is a gambler type, which has worked for him every now and then since he's quite aggressive. 
😌 content  |  ♫ Dan Le Sac VS Scroobius Pip - Waiting For The Beat To Kick In
Link

Rogers Cup finals!

Aug. 16th, 2009   11:35 AM
Damn I've been missing a lot of great tennis lately!  Sure I read the scores and occasionally watch a highlight or two if it's posted, but I haven't caught a match on tv in a while.  I'm definitely gonna try to catch the Rogers Cup finals today at 1:30pm!  The last two days I've been channel surfing for the matches because they weren't being aired on the local sports network, but maybe it was just bad timing cause I couldn't find any coverage.  Oh well.  So lets see...the Rogers Cup has been really intense so far.  Every year the tournament is played in Montreal and Toronto ~ the men's and women's circuit rotate between the two cities.  This year the men, which started last week, are playing in Montreal while the women, which started yesterday, are playing here.  History was made last Wednesday when for the first time the top 8 players in the world reached the quarter-finals in a single tournament ~ from first to eighth: Roger Federer, Rafael Nadal, Andy Murray, Novak Djokovic, Andy Roddick, Juan Martin Del Potro, Jo-Wilfried Tsonga, and Nikolay Davydenko.  Man, I wish I watched the quarter-final matches.  Federer lost to Tsonga in losing two sets of three.   I am thoroughly impressed with Tsonga's solid playing in the third set ~ he was down 1-5 but plowed through for a 7-6(3)...incredible.  At the same time though, I feel so bad for Federer since he let such a lead slide!  Murray took out Davydenko 6-2, 6-4.  I'm a bit half-hearted with Davydenko since he ranked fifth at the end of 2008 - it's not that he isn't playing up to par, but I think everyone else is just playing that much better.  He lacks variety in his game since his serve is weaker than most players, and he sticks to the baseline and groundstroke returns more than anything ~ oh well, Murray isn't dubbed the best returner on the circuit for nothing.  Roddick beat Djokovic, the defending champion, in two sets (fuk yeah!).  I use to be a James Blake fan when he and Roddick were the only Americans in the top ten.  I always rooted for Blake to do better than Roddick, but that never happened since his rank dropped to 21 in the last year.  Now I'm just cheering for Roddick since his playing has improved SO much at the start of the year ~ its been five years and he's still the fastest server in the game!  Del Potro blew past Nadal in two sets ~ another quarter-final shocker!  Del Potro has been playing really consistent this year, while Nadal has had some struggles.  Nadal lost in a surprising upset against Robin Soderling at the French Opens in the fourth round.  Then he had to withdraw from Queen's Club Chapionship and Wimbledon due to tendinitis in both knees ~ he had to withdraw from last years Tennis Masters Cup due to tendinitis as well.  I feel so bad for him since he's proven to be more than an incredible player, but having his health old him back.  He's only 23 so he's still a serious threat to look out for in the future! The semi-finals were just as amazing!  Murray defeated Tsonga in an incredible two sets game (6-4, 7-6(8)), what's more, the weather was like 40C with 70% humidity!  Gawd, I'd pass out if I played any sports in that kinda weather.  With this win Murray now has 8850 ATP points, which means he takes the number two ranking since Nadal only has 8665!  Only 22 years old, the Scot has really been climbing the ladder in the last two years ~ he ranked 12th at the end of 2007, climbed to 4th after last years US Open, then to 3rd this May, and now he's 2nd!  Another serious threat to look out for in the future since he's such a well rounded player on court, mentally, and physically.  Last thing to add about this, Federer and Nadal have held the first and second ranking for the last five years!  Djokovic has had one or two matches to claim the number two spot, but was unsuccessful - it's so awesome to finally see a bit of change.  :( Roddick lost to Del Potro in the semi-finals (4-6, 6-2, 7-5).  I'm a bit disappointed since Roddick double faulted the game away in the third set, after such an amazing game.  What's more, the two played against each other in the Legg Mason Tennis Classic finals (which was the tournament right before the Rogers Cup) and Roddick lost, also in three sets.  I don't remember if I've written about Del Potro in the past (to lazy to check, but I have a feeling I have), but anyways this kid is incredible!  He's only 20 years old!  (o__O notice this trend of youngsters all under 23 - Nadal, Murray, Del Potro, Djokovic - claiming the ranks?)  He's powerfully offensive, an incredible mover on clay and hard court...but sucks on grass, great mental game (since he's gotten himself out of many tight situations), and his second serve is usually as powerful as his first - which is really uncommon.  So the Rogers Cup finals is between Andy Murray and Juan Martin Del Potro.  I think I'll put my money down on Murray since he's won three out of the four times the two have played against each other, plus he's older had has a bit more experience.  In any case this is gonna be a crazy match!
🐇 bouncy  |  ♫ Sloan - In The Movies
Link

Ouch...

Aug. 15th, 2009   12:28 AM
A little piece of me died tonight while taking a three hour stats exam worth half my grade...gawd I'm so miserable right now =.=**  The level of difficulty was beyond any of the in-class and textbook problems.  Honestly, just doing the homework isn't enough.  You practically have to dissect and understand formulas as well as how they work with others formulas.  There was only 40 multiple choice questions, but every other question could easily take half a page of work just to solve ~ and that's if you understood what the fuk it's asking and how to go about in solving it.  And even if you do come up with an answer, it's fustrating as ever to see it not listed as one of the choices!  Seriously this had to be the hardest exam of my life - never have I had to guess so many answers...omg was it agonizing having to guess with a 1 in 5 odds =.=**.  Up until taking this course I haven't done hardcore math in almost five years - and even back then I sucked at it (hurrah for going against the stereotype...dammit ><**).  If I don't pass this course I'm going to hide under my bed and cry T_T.
😞 depressed

Winning? Meh...

Aug. 14th, 2009   12:48 AM
It almost feels like I'm playing some sort of reverse game of Chicken with some of my friends...and I'm winning >>''  Like, for the last month or two my social life has been rather low key.  Certainly there have been plenty of opportunities for me to call out people when I've been bored out of my mind, but I didn't.  Personally, I've been rather tired of having to be the one to get things off the ground.  Often the case it just makes me feel really needy to call people out to do things that I want to do (even if those things are what other people also want to do).  Other times it makes me question what people think of me in the period of this absence.  For example, I can't remember the last time I played tennis with Stephanie ~ in effect, I've been playing a hell of a lot more squash by myself.  Another example would be hanging out and cuddling with K-dawg.  I don't exactly remember how long it's been, but every time in the past it's been me who calls him out.  I feel somewhat less good about myself that it hasn't been the reverse.   So anyways, I feel as though I'm winning because both of whom I just mentioned called me out today.  And yet, I'm not really sure how I feel about finally being in this position ~ since it's not a matter of being right or wrong that I've been detached from people as they have been with me...but just the feeling of knowing that someone caved in is almost laughable in a sad way.  Like a breath of fresh air with an unsettling after bite.  K-dawg just wants to hang out (which I suspect would lead into cuddling), but devoid of cake.  I guess I could do without cake since I've been watching what I eat lately.  At least he didn't say pizza, heh.  We probably won't get together until after the 21st.  Stephanie wanted to go bowling tomorrow because she has a coupon, but studying for my Saturday exam takes precedence ~ so we might do something on Sunday instead.  Unfortunately neither were interested in checking out the Church Street Fetish Fair, so I guess my curiosity will have to slide this year as I probably won't go.

Aug. 13, 2009

Aug. 13th, 2009   12:07 AM
Happy Birthday Jully! ^^ You're 22 years old, but in my head you'll always be three months older than me ~ yeah, you know what I mean.  One time when we were comparing birth dates, I had a complete brain fart and told her we were three months apart despite being only one.  She still teases me about that =P.  I remember how we met in first year ~ or more accurately I remember because you've reminded me enough times for it never to be forgotten.  You were behind me while sitting on the bus and you tapped my shoulder because you recognized me from a class we both shared.  I totally ignored you!  XD  I had my music blaring and I probably just didn't feel your tapping.  You later confronted me in class and that's how it all started.  You've changed quite a lot in such a short time, whether you know it or not.  You inspire me to work harder since I've learned never to underestimate you.  Sometimes I think you're so predictable, but every now and then you surprise me and it impresses me.  I can't help but smile everytime I look at you, so I know that you're important to me.  Keep living life by your rules, I'll always support you! =)

[enter witty title here]

Aug. 12th, 2009   2:08 AM
Hmmm, how to sum up my Tuesday for an intro into this entry....hmmmm.  It was good for the most part but with a =\ expression at the end of it. 

Txt'd Jully some time before lunch asking whether she wanted to study together in the afternoon - booked it down for 3pm at school.  I was pretty much distracting myself most of the morning so I left home right after lunch and got to school at around 1:30pm.  Tried to study some stats, but nothing really stuck >>".  I think I mainly just didn't know where to begin since I remembered some of the course material from before the midterm, but not all of it.  And jumping right into the second half of the course material without fully cementing the first half didn't seem like a good idea.  So I started from the beginning which was rather unstimulating.  I studied much better when Jully came ~ must've been her ambiance :3  It seems this summer she's been wearing more brightly coloured clothing, very pretty me thinks ^^.  Heh, plus she was wearing a necklace that I got for her birthday (or was it Christmas?) last year, but personally I liked the other one she had on the last time we met up =P. 

(Btw, my HLTB02 exam on Monday was...okay.  The prof said there'd be 80-100 multiple choice questions but there ended up only being 66.  Some of the questions were quite unpolished imo, but for the most part they were fair.  I don't really know how I think I did.  I don't feel I did as good as I could have, but I didn't second guess myself so that should be a good sign...or a really bad one =.=**).

While we were studying at Tim Hortons and I went and lined up to get something to eat, while doing so an old elementary school friend recognized me.  Quite surprising since I probably wouldn't have recognized him even if I was paying attention - not because he looks any different (which he doesn't) but because I haven't talked to him in a few years (Incidentally, he was one of the 125 people I blocked and deleted from my main MSN contact list.  I did a sweep last week of all the people I haven't chatted with in years =\  Maybe I did it a little to soon heh).  XD Muahaha, he said I looked as though I'd been working out ~ so glad I wore a tighter than my usual t-shirt today!  I think he may have recognized me by my hair as well since I didn't wear a hat like I usually do, but instead I gelled it like I use to back in highschool.  (W00t, hair and tight shirt, +10 attractiveness LOL)  So I've known this person since grade seven (possibly longer) and the one thing I remember most about him was going to his house with our group of friends.  During the lunch breaks of grade seven or eight a bunch of us would walk across the street to his house and play mahjong!  It's pretty friggin' hilarious now that I think about it because we were what?  12-14 years old?  And playing mahjong?  o__O  (which isn't exactly a kids recreational game)  It kinda ticks me off as well since at that age kids think they know soooo much and that they're capable of anything...but in the position I'm in now, they really don't know as much as they think they do.  It's interesting to think about how thoughts/understandings of stuff have changed with age.  Anywhoo, my friend is in a similar position as I am - staying back and finishing by December - so that's a bit reassuring.  It'd be nice to catch up with him someday, but it's not something I'd put effort into arranging simply because it's not that important to me. 

After studying, I took Jully out for dinner.  Its been a while since we've last gone out to eat and for the most part I usually pick up the bill ~ somewhat by force of habit.  A lot of the time when I eat out with girls I pick up the bill.  I really hate to say the reason why, but I feel it has something to do with inferiority and self-presentation.  There are a couple of people I don't do this with because of a sorta understanding with where to draw the line, but for the most part I just do it and it makes me happy.  Also, I took her out because her birthday is this Thursday (still haven't found a gift for her) and she's got an exam the day before and after which sucks doubly.  Chinese people believe it's better to celebrate an occasion earlier rather than after the actual day.  But Jully's been stressing over these exams that she could celebrate before, and hasn't made plans for celebrating after due to being so busy :(

We settle on eating at Taste of Korea, which is on the second floor of Pacific Mall, but prior to doing so we walked around Splendid China Tower (which is a recently built rival mall right across the street).  Damn Asians...how do they make a living with late opening hours and early closing times?  XD  What's more roughly 30% of the shops sold medicinal and 20% were spas/beauty shops ~ how do you make business in that kind of competition?!  Jeez, I was kinda embarrassed to be Chinese while walking in that mall ~ it was such a joke and a waste of space.  And another thing (LOL), who names a restaurant Taste of Korea when the menus is divided into Japanese, Chinese, Thai, Taiwanese, and Korean food?  Weird o__O.  At least the food wasn't bad.  We shared a 35 piece sushi boat, which came with the usual Korean appetizers, and an order of lychee iced tea each.  Heh, a piece of potato fell off the plate while putting the appetizer on our table, so the waiter gave us a bowl full of them @_@  But it was probably cause we were among the last table before closing =P.  With tax and tip it came to $40, not bad for an overindulging meal ^^.  Here's a poor quality picture taken with my cellphone...


The night ended as per usual, I suppose.  Like I always describe myself, I'm not much of a talker, so I didn't have much to say by the end of the night.  I guess I've just gotten use to the awkward feeling that creeps up ever now and then ~ sorry to put it like that Jully =\  What can I say, I'm pretty boring =\.

I got home a little before 11pm, and immediately hopped on FA (which has become the most distracting site recently XD) since I've been looking forward to a few things.  Punkaroo was doing a free picture give away the day before and lucky enough instead of originally picking only one winner, she drew pics for the first ten people to reply ~ I was number eight, wicked!

Mine is the middle one on the bottom row ^^.  No complaints from me about detail, pose, or whatever ~ it's free and totally awesome!  :D  So apparently we're all balancing a jelly bean on our nose...which came from her vagina...yeah, I have no clue how that idea came about, but that's how she said it =P.  Here are the links to the sketch and coloured finish. 

I was also looking forward to seeing a special commission I bought the other day, but the artist hasn't gotten around to colouring it yet.  I'm beginning to feel a bit half-hearted with the artist for a few reasons that will go unsaid for now, but it might just be something of a personal opinion that I'm not content with.  Anyways, I'm patient and this commission isn't only for myself, but I'm not going to ruin the surprise =P. 

Hoi, going to bed now ~ I have to make the most of everyday before my uber difficult stats exam! ><**
😶 blank  |  ♫ Alexisonfire - Old Crows

Early birthday, late studying...

Aug. 10th, 2009   1:58 AM
Eep!  I lost a couple of hours for studying tonight!  I got home a few hours ago from my great grandma's (from my mom's side) 104th birthday dinner ~ although technically her actual birthday isn't until next week.  Every year we throw a big Chinese dinner party to commemorate the moment.  This year we did it at a restaurant in Mississauga (on the north east corner of Winston Churchill/Dundas) and there was three tables with ten people sitting at each.  I've written about big Chinese dinners in past entries, and this isn't any different.  Each table cost around $400 and consisted of ten courses no including dessert.  Check out the menu:



The coolest looking dish was the 'Winter Melon Soup with Assorted Seafood & Meat' and the 'Vegitables arranged in Fan-shape'



Overall the dinner was very good but very time consuming.  I left the house at 5:45pm and got to the restaurant just shy of 6:30pm, and didn't get home until a little past 11pm.  I got a serious case of itis right now, but I have to make up for lost time and pull an all nighter since there are still some stuff I've yet to study >>".


(104 years old and still very healthy!  Seriously!  My dad has more chronic health problems and he's half her age!  I sure hope some of her good genes got passed down to me .___.)

Aside from the food and time spent, I think the only other thing that irked me about the dinner was just the normal family chatter.  Like, when I compare relatives from my dad's side of the family to my mom's, there's a stark difference.  My mom's side has always been more...fortunate?  I'm not sure what the right word is, but they have less problems and oddities than my dad's side ~ which I find uncomforting sometimes since my life seems to fall more closely with my dad's than my mom's.  Whereas my older brother seems to follow down the road of my mom and her side of the family.  I guess the main thing that gets to me is the level of education and occupational status.  A lot of the relatives on my mom's side are smart and loaded =.=*.  My mom's younger brother is a pharmacist.  My uncle (one of my mom's cousin) is a dentist who owns two clinics.  My grandpa's older brother (meaning, my mom's uncle) is a retired lab chemist who did some important work on water ~ his wife recently retired after years of working with the government as some multicultural representative something-something position.  One of my distant uncles was a fighter pilot in Taiwan.  

Soooo, yeah.  There was quite a lot of talk about school and future occupations around the dinner table ~ most of which were directed to my brother who's working on a Masters degree in Biochemistry.  Some of the discussion also derived from my younger cousins since two of them are nearing the end of highschool while another is entering university (in New York, might I add  -.-** richness...)  =\  Suffice to say I feel that i get compared a lot since I'm studying a "wtf" subject that's taking longer than the usual four year undergraduate time frame...which will probably land me a "wtf" career.  Bah. 
😴 tired  |  ♫ white noise
Link

La La La

Aug. 9th, 2009   12:24 AM
Stupid DDoS attack, I kept timing out whenever I tried to load LJ but it didn't seem to have affected other people that I follow ~ how unlucky =\.  Well, I suppose the advantage would be I had one less distraction throughout the last day and a half from studying...which is going dreadfully slow.  Okay enough of the boring stuff...

Female French musicians are so hawt! D:  Sure whenever they sing in French I have absolutely no idea what they're saying, but music is a language everyone can speak in some shape or form.  I was watching Astolpho draw on LiveStream this afternoon and he was listening to Yael Naim which instantly reminded me of Emilie Simon ~ who I loved for the longest time.  The awesome thing about both of them is that they can also sing in English, which really shines when they do cover versions.  For example, Naim's cover of Britney Spears' Toxic has so much class, she slows it down and sings it with some mellow electronic/synthesized tunes.  (Btw, Naim got big from her song New Soul which was used in a MacBook Air commercial)  While Naim is pop-folk, Simon is avant-garde/electronic ~ but they both have some sorta charm that's similar.  Simon has done "I wanna be your dog" by The Stooges and "Come as you are" by Nirvana, among others.  Check out the two videos after the cut ~ it was hard to pick just one song from each so if it's something you dig you should definitely look into them ^^.  (Another noteworthy female French artist I've yet to look into is Lola Lafon :3)






 

Sweet!  I'm next in line to get a gift art for donating to SandyPants.  I'm totally looking forward, her stuff easily puts a smile on my face :D  Ditto with ChibiYoshi ~ I really want something done by him when he's open for commissions.  His use of colour is so vibrant despite being flat and unshaded.  And his ABC Challenge has been nothing but hilarious - it's a shame it'll be over soon T_T.  Keep an eye out on two Kiriban (milestone) contests as well ^^.  asdfjkl03 is nearing 25, 000 while Punkaroo is coming up to 6000 ~ it's all win-win when free art is involved XD.  Plus, there's still StrawberryNeko's Fortune Cookie raffle that's going on until the 17th! 

(Wow, this entry has been nothing but links so far o__O)

W00t!  FanExpo from August 28-30!  If anything gets planned out between Nebulus64 and I, we might go geek it up ^^.  I'll definitely have to give dan_raccoon a poke since we brought up the possibility of going back when we went to Anime North ~ more people the merrier!  I'm rather curious about the Church Street Fetish Fair happening on the 16th, but it's not really something I'd want to goto alone.  Might ask around for any takers, possibly even get intrigued by some questions ^^;;.  The last weekend of the month might be a good one since on the 29th it's a fellow ABDL muncher's birthday and there's gonna be a party.  I'm so looking forward to the end of my exams >.<  Oh, and speaking of birthdays, I still need to get Jully a present since her's is next Thursday.  Gawd I'm so bad at deciding what to get people...maybe I can just order her some yaoi or something .__."

Anywhoo, I'm gonna go recharge with some caffeine before burning the midnight oil studying ~ I played squash tonight and it really took a lot out of me because of the humidity =.=**.  Peace out. 
🌀 drained  |  ♫ Jay-Z - DOA (Death Of Autotune)

.____."

Aug. 6th, 2009   11:13 PM
Man I'm exhausted!  Went and played squash for nearly two hours tonight.  The side of my right thumb is ready to blister and my right arm feels more macho than my left o__O.  Oh well, all in good fun and great exercise ~ I'm averaging at least twice a week so far :3.  Although, I've been playing so much I haven't actually had time to go work out or lift weights >>''. Other then that, the week has been going as expected...I study a little in the morning and by the afternoon I've distracted myself with stuff other than school work =\.  I'm tempted to loaf around at coffee shops for the next couple of days just to get myself away from my desktop, but then that requires spending money and having my teeth get stained.  I got a couple hours of studying done with Jully yesterday at school, but still, I know I can do better.  My first exam is on Monday and I've still got a lot to cover =(  That's all for now.

Oh noes! Another commission! >:D

Aug. 4th, 2009   11:51 PM
Pfff, I lied.  I do have an idea for a commission.  It's been lingering in my head for a little while so lucky for me I remembered it this morning ~ even luckier I found an artist I think will do an awesome job at capturing my idea ^^.  Lincub ( ) made a journal entry this morning about taking commissions and after going through his gallery I know I'm definitely not going to be disappointed.  $25 for two characters, full colour, shading, and background is a really good deal ^^ plus it'll be a nice change from the other pics I have where I'm always by myself.  So the idea I've had revolves around these two screenshots I took from the anime series Zuko Natsume Yuujinchou (I've blogged about the series a couple months ago).  I want something scenic but also warm and comforting.  What I originally had in mind was to have a cross between the two screenshots ~ scenic background, but close enough to the two male characters to make out some detail.  Lincub instead suggested doing both in one go.  Something like this:

Totally awesome idea!  I just hope there's enough background though to tie it all together ^^;;.  So the two characters are gonna be me clinging onto a grown-up bunny ~ that couldn't have been hard to guess since all my pics are with bunnies :3.  The bunny character isn't anyone in particular since I don't really know anyone that well...but for the most part he'll look like Happyfeet Hax, one of my Second Life friends.  Shhh!!  He doesn't know I took his look for something he's not involved in >>''  I love him for a bunch of reasons, but I know most of those feelings aren't mutual =\  Oh wells, we know one another well enough by now...sorta.  So anyways, I'm looking forward to this commissions completion (already paid and in the sketching process) since I really like the way Lincub makes headfur all soft looking (considering the pic will mostly be the back of two heads), and watery backgrounds.

I also spent $4 for two of StrawberryNeko's ( ) fortune cookie raffles, and donated $10 to SandyPants cause she's awesome XD.   Like I said in my previous entry, if I win something from StrawberryNeko I'll probably give it to someone cause I'm out of ideas, heh.  And the SandyPants donation, I'm indifferent if I get a gift art ~ it would be awesome, but I'm all for just supporting her crack addiction.  

Btw, happy belated birthday to StrawberryNeko!  She turned 16 the other day ^^.  Good time to be alive imo ~ young enough to escape certain responsibilities, but old enough to rebel and enjoy some freedom.  Best wishes on living life to the fullest :3.

Long weekend wrap-up

Aug. 4th, 2009   2:06 AM
Despite the funeral, I had a fairly relaxing long weekend (Monday was a Civic Holiday in most parts of Canada).  My brother went camping and won't be back until Tuesday night, and my parents didn't come home from Ottawa until Sunday night ~ so it was a pretty padded weekend!  Good timing too since the garbage strike is over =P.

For the most part I stayed home since I didn't have a car to get around, and public transportation in this heat, no thank you.  I finished watching season two of Storm Hawks.  The last episode could used more epic fight scenes rather than the same old same, but at least the ending ensured a third season which begins in the fall.  I'm looking forward, but I doubt I'll be able to download it until most of the season has been aired.  I also watched a movie called The Steam Experiment (also known as The Chaos Experiment) staring Val Kilmer and Eric Roberts.  The trailer looked wicked but I wasn't to thrilled midway through.  The ending is blah...it's sorta open-ended and left me a bit confused.  The acting wasn't bad though, particularly the six people trapped in the steam room.  In any case, I don't recommend it.  Also to add to the list of movies I'd like to watch in theaters ~ Moon staring Sam Rockwell and the voice of Kevin Spacey.  As well as Julie & Julia ~ staring Meryl Streep and Amy Adams ^^.

Read a lot of comic books over the weekend as well ^^.  I am half way through the March batch of the Dark Reign crossover - things are getting quite interesting.  Although, there are two things I'm not to fond of.  The first is the series 'Agents of Atlas'.  Maybe it's because they're a team of 'new' superheros (introduced via retroactive continuity of the 1950s) and they just sorta appear out of nowhere...like, wtf? >>''  The second thing is how many friggin' Avenger titles does Marvel need?!  New Avengers...Mighty Avengers...Young Avengers...Dark Avengers...just plain old Avengers...zomg! o__O  Like, they all make sense in this crossover, but when it's all said and done they'll probably axe a couple of titles.  Can't really say I'm a big fan of The Mighty Avengers, particularly the story arc "Earth's Mightiest" issues #21-23.  The gist is Chthon is using chaos magic to warp the world and the story concludes with a new team of Avengers, lead by Hank Pym, saving the day.  First of all, are you seriously?  Global destruction?  Friggin' Secret Invasions crossover concluded not long ago (in terms of the Marvel universe chronology) with an alien invasion trying to rule the world over.  Kinda soon for more global catastrophe don't you think?  Second issue I have, Hank Pym...blah, I'm not a big fan of his character.  He's got to much of an ego, plus he's a wife beater =P.  Third, not quite into the feel of this story arc ~ it's totally got an old-school Avengers charm, but I'm more into the hyper-contemporary tales.  Lastly, two Avengers teams (Mighty and New) seems unnecessary since the Superhuman Registration Act isn't as big of a deal now that Tony Stark is out.  Phew...that was a bit long winded.  Oh well, there's a lot to love about the other titles in this crossover (like Black Panther, War Machine, and Deadpool) so I'm still enjoy it all. 

Oh yeah!  My wallet got heavier this weekend!  XD  The thing with Chinese customs is that at social and family gatherings (like weddings, birthday parties, funerals, Lunar New Year, etc) red envelopes are given out by the host (or typically given to the unmarried by the married).  Red envelopes (or pockets) symbolize good luck and are suppose to ward of evil spirits - they also contain monies.  In addition, at Chinese funerals a white envelope is usually given out to everyone by the host which contains a piece of candy and a coin ~ you should eat and spend the two as soon as possible.  Meh, I've grown up with these customs so they're in no way kooky to me...I won't complain until I get married and it becomes my turn to deliver (lol).  So I got a red pocket from my grandma, and a couple after the funeral from relatives I've never met.  My dad also gave me two things that belonged to his dad ~ a leather money clip that's handmade and engraved with my grandpa's name (both in English and Chinese), as well as a Citizen watch that's got quite a few wear and tears.  I have no clue what model the watch is but it looks old (and smells old) and it doesn't run (don't know if it just needs a new battery or if it's just toast) ~ regardless I'm treasuring the two.

Now then, this brings me to the question of what I should do with the monies I've been given XD.  My first thoughts were "more commissions!" (LOL)  But I donno...there aren't many other artists I know of, other than the ones I'm waiting for, that I want something done by.  There is one though - sandypants - but she takes donations and gives art as thank yous, so I don't consider that a commission.  Her style is like none other I've yet to come across.  It's unique, fairly simplistic, hella comical, and down right cute!  Plus her animations are friggin' hilarious!  This one is her latest and it features everyone who donated upon her request prior to making the video!  Gawd I wish I donated!  She started working on the animation nine days ago...and nine days ago was when I said I'd lay off the mulla spending T_T.  Agh!  There's another artist I'm interested in getting something from, but the price is a bit scary.  Dingbat is offering mug commissions but they range from $30-40 I think.  I like the fact that a mug is practical and I'd actually use it everyday and love it at the same time ~ where as art is just sorta to show off or for icons and what not.  Sure it's a special mug, but that's a hell of a price tag imo...a mug is a mug and there's no other way to use it...you can get'em for a dollar =\.  Lastly, StrawberryNeko is putting a spin on a raffle idea I suggested to her a few days ago ~ Fortune Cookies!  Pretty much pay to buy a cookie (raffle) with a number on it.  Winner is chosen by random selection.  I don't mind buying one or two cookies to support her, but I'm not really sure I have any ideas for what I'd want her to draw for me if I ever won.  Like, I choose an artist based on whatever idea I have that's most fitting for their style.  I've already got something I want from her...so I'm kinda out of ideas =P.  Though, I wouldn't mind getting her to draw something for a friend in my place since making other people happy would be more rewarding (and again, that's only if I win...and if I buy a $2 cookie).


Aside from furry art, there are other things I want but not necessarily need.  I want a new squash raquet even though the one I'm using now is perfectly fine (with the exception that it's cheap and most likely for beginners).  I don't know if it would be a waste though since I don't have a regular partner.  Lately I've been practicing at my aunts condo by myself - which I don't mind - but without a partner I doubt think it'd matter as much how good of a raquet I have.  But then again, I can play this sport during any season so in the long run it would be nice to have.  Ideally, I just want one that's lighter with a wider face =\.  I also want a new pair of sunglasses...donno if I should consider it a 'need' since I don't go out as much anymore despite being something good for my health.  I've always gotten the cheap ones you can find at drug stores, but they have their ways of getting banged up.  I want a more expensive kind, but nothing designer =\.  I'll have to look into what sort of selection they got for sunglasses since I'm not too familiar.  There are other 'wants' that I desire, but they're mostly just unpolished thoughts =S.  I could just let the monies sit in the bank I suppose =\. 

Anywhoo, weekend is over and I've got exams to focus on!  Minimal fun for the rest of the week ~ I've gotta crack the whip and catch up on a lot of articles! 

(Btw, check out Regina Specktor's latest album 'Far' if you're into eclectic storytelling, a bit of pop/rock instrumentals, anti-folk-ness, and simply awesome vocals.  Her tunes are perfect for the summer :3)
💤 sleepy  |  ♫ Regina Spektor - Blue Lips

Die reinste freude ist die schadenfreude...

Aug. 1st, 2009   11:58 PM
I can't believe it's already August.  Living each day feels like it goes by at a turtles speed, but when I number them I'm putting X's on my calender like it's nothing.  It's good that I have a reference of what I've been doing because honestly things are just happening and I'm not remembering most of it ~ could be due to living an unexciting life I suppose.  And yet despite being incredibly forgetful I remember what I was doing exactly one year ago.  Last August the first at 11am I broke up with someone I had been in a relationship with for a couple of months since it had been built upon lies ~ mind you, I was the victim.  I guess I'm a pretty sentimental person since I still have a few things that remind me of our time together, which I really should get rid of, but it's genuinely my fault for being so easily devoted to one person.  The one thing I do miss though is the companionship.  Never once did I feel as though I were alone or not in a special someone's thoughts - which to this day I still think is irreplaceable and replicable.  I suppose one of the reasons for that was because my ears were glued to my cellphone practically every night while working the graveshift for condo security.  Looking back at old phone bills, my airtime last June was 2391 minutes and 4487 minutes in July. I wouldn't call them good times and a waste of time comes off a little too harsh.  I suppose they're just bad memories I still have trouble shaking off even after a year.  Heh, what a bitch =\. 
😶 blank  |  ♫ Green Day - Last Day on Earth
Link

Closing a chapter...

Aug. 1st, 2009   1:28 PM

My grandpa's funeral was yesterday afternoon in Ottawa and it was really emotional to say the least.  My brother, grandparents (mom's side), and I drive up there Thursday night and stayed at a hotel.  My parents, aunt Liana and uncle Viktor had gone up a day earlier since there were visitations for eight hours ~ they said a lot of friends came to pay their respects.  The funeral service was on Friday and started at around 1pm-ish, but I had to be there at 11am since I was close family and had to help greet people upon entering. 

Seeing my grandpa in the casket was...I don't really know how to describe it.  It was painfully different for lack of a better description.  He lost a lot of weight from his illness and it'd been eleven days since he passed away ~ the funeral home did a good job at preserving him as best they could.  He was surrounded by about forty flower arrangements each with a long piece of ribbon with names of the giver written in Chinese, and in front of the casket were two enlarged photos that we had chosen as a family.  As service time drew closer many friends and relatives gathered.  Excluding friends, everyone was related to everyone whether by an aunt, uncle, cousin, or direct kin.  There were so many people I'd either only seen when I was to young to remember, once in my life, or never at all that the moments were beautiful and sad.  It's sad that death brings people together, but it was beautiful to see all the love within family and for my grandpa.  (One thing to keep in mind is that Chinese families two generations back were huge.  My grandpa is one of ten sons and ten daughters ~ this is also the case with my grandma on my mom's side.  What’s more back then polygyny wasn’t a big deal.  The family tree is so gianormous that remember who is who at gatherings is really difficult - extensive introductions are sorta necessary.)

I got to talk to one of my uncles from Montreal who literally went into seclusion for a decade after a really messy divorce.  When I was little my parents would go up to Montreal during the winter to go skiing, and we'd crash at his place for a couple of nights.  So it was really hard to watch him fall apart during the tough times in his life despite family and friends trying to reach out to him.  I hope he's managed with his depression, he looked fairly well with the exception of a head full of gray hair but for the most part communication with him outside of my grandpa's funeral have been impossible.  (I've noticed that my dad's side of the family has more drama or 'issues' than my mom's side, where for the most part it's been a bit of a success story)

The service began after exchanging a ton of handshakes, warm condolences, bows in front of the casket, and dialect of a Chinese tongue I'm not fluent with.  Easily seventy people attended.  One of my uncles seemed to know a lot about funeral procedures and guided everything.  My dad gave a heartfelt speech which was very uplifting.  Not long after that everyone was escorted out and only close family remained with the deceased ~ we were allowed one more viewing before they closed the casket.  To see someone for the last time knowing on one will ever see them again was a lingering thought for me.  Again, saying it was emotion would really only scratch the surface of it all.  How everything fell into place was completely new to me since I've never been in this sort of situation...I would never wish anyone to have to feel anything like I did. 

For whatever reason there was a bit of a wait between getting from the funeral home to the cemetery, possibly having something to do with the formalities.  My dad had to walk in front of the casket while myself, my brother, and six other pallbearers had to load the casket into the hearse.  Afterwards, two limos took close family to the cemetery site while everyone else followed ~ I was a bit surprised that we didn't have a police escort and that we took the highway.  My grandpa was to be buried with his first with ~ every year I go to Ottawa with family to pay respects to my dad's first mother so I'm quite familiar with where we were heading.  The limo we took wasn't the type where people sit in a circular formation facing one another, rather it was like sitting in a minivan with rows of seats for three.  There was an unsettling silence all throughout the ride. 

At the cemetery, my brother and I were at the front of the casket pallbearing – it was pretty damn heavy even with all the extra muscle.  There wasn’t any kind of a speech aside from one of my uncles guiding everyone with how to follow traditional Chinese practices.  Everyone was given a flower to place on the casket; most gave a silent prayer and bowed while doing so.  Afterwards close family removed a black arm band or white hair ribbon and placed it on the casket as well.  We all bowed and then they began lowering it into the grave.  We didn’t stay to watch them fill the grave, so the limos took us back to the funeral home.   

Despite the emotional mourning, it was also a day to celebrate my grandpa’s life – that we did at a restaurant where the reception took place.  There were six tables of ten people at each.  The food was nothing special and it was sort of awkward eating a dinner-like meal at 3:30pm, but the ambience was good.  Although I’ll probably never get to meet a majority of my relatives again, it was really nice meeting and sharing stories with them.  My brother, grandparents, and I left Ottawa at around 5pm and got back in Toronto at around 9:15pm-ish.  Overall, the whole experience was really new to me and somewhat difficult fathom ~ you don’t want it to happen, but it’s inevitable.  I guess I just need to give it some time and move on.   

Rest in peace grandpa.  There’s a lot I’ll miss about you ~ like how you address my brother and I “young man” in the most Chinese accent ever ~ and I really wished you could be at my graduation, but that’s partly my fault for staying back two semesters. 

♫ Django Reinhardt - Vendredi

Jul. 28, 2009

Jul. 28th, 2009   8:21 PM
Happy birthday bro!  One more year and it'll be a quarter century.  You know that I blog from all the times you've seen me typing while passing glimpses of my enormous monitor - but I doubt you know the address.  Whatever the case may be, you enjoy life to the fullest so there's no need in me telling you otherwise.  Despite rarely seeing eye-to-eye with one another, I've come to accept the fact that you'll always be a pain in my rear as I am to yours.  What are brothers for?  You'll never fully understand me since I prefer my privacy, but you're always there...living in the same room as me >>''  Thanks for always being the shining example of what I'm not, you'll definitely go far in life since you work as hard as you play.  Live it out bro.

Not a bad day...

Jul. 27th, 2009   11:16 PM
Thumbs up:  It's been nearly a week since I've gotten padded, so I it was a nice change of pace going to school comfy ^^.  I had to change out by lunch cause the coffee just went right through me {o'.'o}.

Thumbs up:  I satisfied one of my food cravings ~ had sushi for lunch (^^;; food court at that, but I'm not complaining)

Thumbs down:  But I spent $147 on a suit for my grandpa's funeral.  I only really needed the blazer, but it was cheaper to get it as a set >>''  The pants are way long, but I have my own black pants that are a bit tight around the waist.  So it's either I get the new pants tailored and making it impossible to return, or I suffer for a couple of hours on Friday and then return the suit for my monies back =\  Plus, I'll need to come up with a witty reason for the return heh...

Thumbs up/down:  I wanted to play some tennis at night with Stephanie, but she already had plans :<  So instead I went to my aunts condo and played squash by myself.  As lonesome as that might come across, I gave myself quite the workout and it was a lot of fun.  I didn't have to worry about getting pwn'd (XP) and it allowed me to practice a couple of things I've struggled with.  I noticed that I hit much lower when I practice while playing against someone I return it way higher =\  I'd like to work on getting reversed heh.

Thumbs up/down:  I satisfied another one of my food cravings that I've been hankering to eat ~ cake!  Came home at around 10:20pm and celebrated with family, my brothers birthday (he turns 24 tomorrow).  To bad it was chocolate cake .__." most kinds of chocolate make my stomach uneasy.  So that slice of cake will probably kick me in the morning.

Thumbs down:  Not long ago I made a reservation for a motel in Ottawa for Friday.  My brother and I (and possibly my grandparents on my mother's side) are heading to Ottawa Thursday night and coming home Friday night.  The room cost $90.  This is like one of those things where I wouldn't mind paying for even though my parents would insist on covering.  It's like I'm old enough to make ends meet for myself even though it isn't necessary, per say.  So next months VISA statement is gonna be a killer >>''

😴 tired  |  ♫ Son of Nun - Change is Constant
