Dark Reign comic book crossover

Jul. 26th, 2009   11:22 PM
The last couple of days I've been reading articles and watching videos on Comic-Con; the epicly big one in San Diego that ended today.  Wish I were there ><**  Every summer there are tons of anime/comic book/video game conventions in North America that look so awesome, but in no way would I ever get to go!  I'll definitely look into going to the Fan Expo in August, wallet and time permitting.  Anywhoo, I got pretty pumped and its been a couple of months since I've picked up a comic book. (It's not that I've lost interest or anything, but other things have been keeping me occupied.)  So with nothing better to do, I spent most of the day reading comic books ~ I watched Equilibrium as well (for a second time), which wasn't bad, but I'm getting a bit tired of movies heh. 

I finally got to read Mouse Guard: Winter 1152, issue #6.  It's the last issue of the mini-series and I loved it despite the sad turn of events.  The whole series has always been really imaginative and the old English/Medievaly dialect is awesome.  I highly recommend it to anyone who enjoys fantasy, adventure, cute and cuddly animals living in badass times, and wicked illustrations/writing. 

I also began reading the Dark Reign Marvel crossover - it follows after the Secret Invasion crossover which I wrote about months ago.  I downloaded most of the crossover and I'm two issues away from finishing the December 2008 batch (I've got all of them up until May 2009).  I am not disappointed ^^.

Avengers Initiative, issue #20 -   An interesting introduction into the start of the crossover.  I like how some of the stolen identities from the last crossover have played out into this one ~ particularily their consequences (like Luke Cage and Jessica Jones's missing child).  However, the messy ending from the last crossover still lingers heavily >>''

Invincible Iron Man, vol. 5, #8 - *cue Darth Vader music*  Tony Stark is out and Norman Osborn is in!  This is the whole point of the Dark Reign crossover ~ Norman Osborn (formerly super-villian Green Goblin) practically rules the Marvel world now!  Awesome.

Ms. Marvel, issue #34 - Spider-Man has a tie in with this story arc and his presence is hilarious as always.  Fucking wise-cracker...I love it! XD

Secret Invasion - Dark Reign, issue #1 - So this issue is pretty much the introduction of The Cabal ~ a secret society of super villains and antiheroes very much the opposite of The Illuminati.  But rather, Norman Osborn is calling the shots and I have a feeling he's gonna eventually cave from a build up of all his shady actions.  So The Cabal consists of Namor (King of Atlantis), Doctor Doom (ruler of Latveria), Emma Frost (aka the White Queen - leader of the X-Men and figurehead of the decimated mutant population), Loki (God of Mischief), the Hood (Kingpin of the criminal underworld), and of course Osborn (head of H.A.M.M.E.R and Avengers/Thunderbolts).  What evil biddings await, I wonder?!? D:  Doctor Doom is so badass in this issue, and artist Alex Maleeve really butched Namor's face and Emma's smexy-ness.

Secret Invasion - War of Kings, issue #1 - Action packed issue, and just as the title implies there's war!  War between various intergalactic races, in fact!  As a consequence of the Skrull (alien invaders from the last crossover), the Inhumans (a hybrid race) literally pick up their city on the moon and jet it off into space.  They make a dent in the Kree empire (another race) eventually making Ronan a bitch =\.  The next issue ensures the Shi'ar Imperium (another race) will join the war!  I <3 Black Bolt (king of the Inhumans) - he's one of those strong but quite dudes...well technically he doesn't speak at all becase a mere whisper could crumble a mountain >>''.  In any case, it was totally badass in this issue!
🌿 calm  |  ♫ Nine Inch Nails - Getting Smaller
Link

Commission-ness! D:

Jul. 26th, 2009   12:28 AM
Apart from the JadeFox and PadderCat commissions, there are two more to add to the list D:  *jitters like a crack addict*  These are my last ones for a while....I swear!  I sent a request to Marci for a badge the other day after asking a few questions ~ I mainly wanted to know if she could get it done before the end of September so that it could be a birthday present for myself {o'.'o}.  As much as I like Marci's art, I think this will be my one and only commission from her for two reasons.  The first is the fact that she's pretty expensive ~ one badge is $40 (I can imagine she has a mattress made of wads of dollar bills from all the commissions she receives @_@).  My JadeFox commission is for a full page and it only cost $50, where as a one page Marci commission would cost $100.  The second reason is that I find her style repetitious ~ which in part is why she's so well known.  If you've seen enough of her badges online you could probably point them out at a con thirty meters away.  Her style is cute, but I find it too overly done for my taste.  Anywhoo, I'll keep the description of what I asked for a secret until I get the rough sketch =P.

The other recent commission I requested is by StawberryNeko ~ she did the freebie chibi for me.  A couple of hours ago she posted a journal contemplating doing badge commissions.  So when she finalized her decision I jumped at the opportunity!  (First in line suckers! :D)  What I really like about her as an artist is that she is really really quick and at the same time the quality of art does not get depreciated.  Plus, she's really affordable!  XD  I made my request for a waist badge (5x3 inches) a little more than an hour ago (with shipping it only cost $15) and she's already finished a rough sketch and working on the lineart.  Like most artists she works in stages, but unlike others she gives you the opportunity to tweak details along the way.  This is from a note she sent me after I gave her my description...

Once I'm done the sketch, I'll show you and you can change anything about it.
Once it's good, I'll do lineart. Then you can change little things about it, like, make this bigger, add a little bit of this etc.
Then I do base colour, at that point, you can only change the colours.
Then I shadow, which you can't change. But, you can have a coloured border about your charcter, the pic itself, a background, or a combo or all three if you like, just tell me.

Here's what the sketch looks like! ^^

I've probably mentioned it before, but in all of my commissions I've asked to be with a bunny plushie (I let the artist pick the colour and design).  Unlike my Shiro badge though, I'm gonna start sticking with my Second Life avatar look for my fursona ~ main difference is just the hair, really.  I'll post the other stages of this commission as I get them ^^.

(Edit:  Spot a mistake?  XD  I didn't even notice until she mentioned it while working on the shadowing, but my name on the blocks is missing the letter 'I'  D:  Eep, I should have paid more attention heh.  Oh wells, these things happen ~ it's looking great regardless! ^^

From start to finish it took roughly three hours ~ it was really fun chatting with her and making small tweaks like the tail, fur shading, and tacky cheese background.  I am very happy with how it turned out and I can look past the little blip with the alphabet blocks :3  Get yours today!  Plus, support some home grown Canadian talent hehe.

Okay, it's past 3am...I'm going to bed.  And no more placing new commissions for a while!  *incessant scratching like a crack addict*)

Try your luck for free art by Punkaroo!  Just make a comment with links to your references!  No idea how or when she'll be selecting who she'll draw, so best to make a comment sooner than later heh.

(Just as a side, I know I said I'd lay off on the crack...I mean commissions...but in the future I'd really like to get something done by ChibiYoshi.  His art for the ABC Challenge is friggin' hilarious!)


🐇 bouncy  |  ♫ Green Day - Horseshoes and Handgrenades
Link

Movies...life...

Jul. 25th, 2009   1:59 PM
Due to hermiting at home for a majority of the week (or two) to save money, I've noticed that my social life has dipped >>'' while my boredom has skyrocketed.  For the most part I spend my free time at home either browsing the web, watching movies and YouTube, loafing around Second Life, mulling over story ideas, or working out.  Blah.

In no particular order, these are the movies I've watched.

Bart Got a Room - Love, highschool, prom, and social cliques all mixed together into a nerdy comedy.  I enjoyed it since the characters weren't complete stereotypes ~ the father is just hilarious and way cool.  The main character (Danny) played by Steven Kaplan, kinda looks like a young Novak Djokovic throughout the movie o__O.  The ending was a bit lame, but I still recommend this flick if you're looking for a good indie comedy or tired of watching Michael Cera.

Cloverfield - Simply wicked.  I wish I watched this when it was in theaters.  I really felt like I was in the movie (even though that's pretty much the point of filming with a shaky camera/first person perspective).  The actors did an amazing job at acting completely freaked out and trying to survive.  The graphics were incredible.  I recommend this flick if you want a Godzilla experience that can get scary at times - but if you hate queasy-cam than avoid it like the plague. 

Borat/Bruno - I watched Borat for a second time before watching Bruno.  I liked Borat but I didn't like Bruno.  Bruno had a lot more sex that I found really gross, and some of the scenes didn't feel real ~ like they were set up or phony somehow =\.  There wasn't a boring moment throughout the movie but it grabbed my attention in unsettling ways at times.  Also, I didn't like how Bruno followed the same series of events as Borat - dude leaves country taking devoted assistant along, they get into an argument down the road and split up, they meet back up and make up in the end.  Even the way they broke up was sorta similar; in Borat they chase one another in a hotel naked and in Bruno they're found bondaged together in a hotel suite.  On the plus side, Bruno was nice to look at =P and some of his outfits were pretty hawt in a funky sorta way.  Watch it online so you can skip parts you might not enjoy.

The Hangover - Hilarious and surprisingly well thought out.  'What happens in Vegas, stays in Vegas' pretty much describes the entire movie, but it certainly wasn't generic in presentation.  Every situation was funny and connected to the next series of events very well.  The ending was appropriate (nothing like 'Dude, Where's My Car?' sorta crap).  I recommend watching it, but I wouldn't pay to go see it at a theater.

I watched American Psycho, No Country for Old Men, and The Hitchhicker's Guide to the Galaxy for a second time.  American Psycho is awesome.  Christian Bale really nailed the role (plus he was all muscly and smexy) and I loved how it ended ~ makes you sorta think, but not too deeply.  There were a bit to many names which got a little confusing since they intentionally misrecognized people throughout the movie.  Also, the movie didn't really seem to go anywhere - like the ending brings you back to where it starts, but not really.  Oh well, taking a glimpse into a psychopaths life is awesome ~ it's worth watching!  I rewatched No Country for Old Men mainly for Javier Bardem's performance.  He plays Anton Chigurh, this super badass hitman devoid of emotion.  I skipped a lot of the talking scenes and went straight to the gun fights =P.  I love how there was very little music throughout the movie (Cloverfield didn't have any except for a really awesome song during the ending credits), it really helped intensify the ambiances.  Watch it ^^.  I watched HGttG for a second time because I didn't remember much of it ~ I ended up skipping a couple of scenes.  I didn't really enjoy it as much as I thought I did...or would.  The main character, Arthur Dent played by Martin Freeman, really got on my nerve because he's such a wussy.  Plus, it was very British so some of the humour didn't rub off to well on me =\  Oh well, not a movie for everyone I suppose. 

I also watched Watchmen for the third (or was it fourth?) time.  I wanted to download the director's cut since it has something like twenty extra minutes, but the files were around seven gigabytes so I didn't bother.  In any case, I love the movie and I've accepted the subtle differences from the graphic novel (despite still being a fan of the GN).  I wouldn't be surprised if I watch it more than ten times before the year is over XD.

Last night I watched The Hurt Locker with Stephanie.  I really enjoyed it even though the plot didn't feel like I went anywhere ~ sorta 'day and the life of' so and so.  The ending was appropriate but could have been better somehow.  Essentially we learn that the main character likes his job a lot more than being around family at home ~ which is sorta messed up and cool at the same time.  I don't know anything about the military besides what I've seen in movies, so it was a really wicked change of pace to watch a flick about bomb squads rather than regular foot soldiers.  In general the movie was really intense (like, fist clenching intense) and at times really sad (in the goosebumpy sorta way).  It kinda gave me a new perspective for the soldiers fighting the war in Iraq ~ it humanized it for both sides.  I definitely recommend watching this in theaters ^^. 

There are still a couple of movies I haven't gotten around to watching; (500) Days of Summer, The Steam Experiment, Year One, pull a Harry Potter marathon, and maybe a couple of good horror flicks (haven't watched a really good one in a while).  I really ought to check my bandwidth usage because these last couple of weeks I've been going pretty overboard with the downloads/streams. 

Anyways, the nose dive of my so-called social life felt most apparent when Steph and I went for coffee after the movie.  I hadn't seen her for three weeks since she was vacationing in and around Russia...so you'd sorta expect there be something to talk.  But I don't know.  Despite sharing similar interests there wasn't much of anything that made it feel like it connected.  In the same regard, I felt out of place while having coffee with Jully on campus after my Thursday class.  I'm impressed that she's taking direction of her future and working towards somewhere she wants to be, while at the same time this lingering feeling of hopelessness shadows over me =\.  It could be contagious ~ many of my friends who graduated don't really know what to make of their life.  It's stupid that I'm not motivated by what's happening around me so that I don't end up like those people, but then again I've always lacked a lot of initiative in life.  I live to much for today by doing the things I enjoy without any plans for tomorrow which requires a more serious attitude. 

😑 blah  |  ♫ The Mars Volta - Halo Of Nembutals
Link

Kill for freaky sex?

Jul. 22nd, 2009   4:43 PM
Furries plot to kill parents for penis biting sex...um...okay...>>''  I wonder if they mean vore, but either way it's not really up my alley.  I guess it's a good thing they didn't blow the whole furry thing out of proportion by making huge generalizations (as well as only mentioning 'furries' once).  For some reason the first thing that came to mind after reading the article was 'Pain Olympics: Hatchet vs. Genitals'.  I have no idea why =\  But yea...just popped into my head.  I'm pretty certain the video is fake but in any case I wouldn't recommend those who haven't seen it to watch it - it's very disturbing...almost as bad as 2 Girls 1 Cup.

Anyways, I've been listening to a lot of Charles Hamilton - another New York rapper/producer.  His 'Sonic the Hamilton' mixtape with DJ Skee is pretty fun since he samples a lot of music from Sonic the Hedgehog.  In general though, I'm not really sold on his rhymes...they're below average compared to other up and coming artists and some of his choruses are better than the overall tracks @_@.  But he's still got a lot going for him ~ he's only 21 and he's already got more than a dozen mixtapes out (even if they're inconsistent).  His debut studio album 'This Perfect Life' comes out August 25 ^^.

Just as a side, I'm going to start working on the mommy affairs story for the ABDL short story contest.  Three entries have already been submitted but I'm not going to read them till I'm done with mine.

Rest in peace grandpa...

Jul. 20th, 2009   6:04 PM
I've had a bit of time digesting the news and for the most part I'm just speechless.  It was chilling at first when my dad called a little after four in the afternoon, and then unsettling having to talk about it when my mom and brother came home.  It happened sooner than anyone expected, but I'm really glad my grandpa got to see his daughter and her family - my aunt Liana, uncle Viktor, and their three month old son.  For over a decade and a half my aunt and grandpa have been in disagreement about old family issues and neither of them have seen each other until last weekend.  I hope none of them hold a grudge because that's got to be one of the worst ways to go.  My mom went up to Ottawa with my aunt and uncle, so she was able to see my grandpa one last time.  The same can be said for my grandma whose been out of the country for the last two weeks.  She booked a flight one day earlier than originally planned and came home on Sunday evening.  It would have been really heart-breaking if she hadn't changed flights.  My brother and I wanted to make a trip up this coming weekend, but it's to late now.  The last time we saw our grandpa was in May.  My brother is probably more affected by this than I am since I didn't know him as well.  When my brother was an undergraduate he had a co-op placement in Ottawa, so he's lived with our grandparents for more than a year.  Right now, my dad is still in Ottawa dealing with the paper work and arrangements.  I honestly can't imagine how he must be feeling right now.  Maybe a bit of relief after having seen his father in pain for the past two weeks and then pass away in peace?  You know what the worst thing is?  This isn't his first time having to deal with loss from an immediate family.   My grandpa's first wife died in a car accident two years before I was born.  My grandpa was a real wreck after that and my dad had to handle all the paper work for the funeral, financial possessions, and the grocery store that they owned.  It's really unfortunate.  After these last few days my dad isn't the person I use to think he was.  I have more reasons to admire him and be appreciative of all the devotion he has given to his family.   At around 3:55pm my grandpa, grandma, and dad were waiting for an ambulance.  They had just finished the consultation for my grandpa's lung cancer at the downtown hospital.  The doctors said they could probably start radiation therapy sometime later that day, but a conflict in scheduling postponed their session for the day after.  While waiting for the abulance that would take my grandpa back to the local hospital where they were treating his stomach ulcers, he stopped breathing.  There was no percieved pain and the doctors did attempt to resuscitate.  Rest in peace grandpa, I really wish I knew you better and I hope you're in a better place now.  You will be deeply missed. 
Link

Blah...

Jul. 20th, 2009   2:37 PM
Finished my HLTB02 (Issues in Child Health and Development) assignment at 1pm today with two hours to spare...probably should have went to lecture though since it's two hours long and I would've just finished right on time @_@  Anyways, I'm glad it's done considering it's the last assignment for this semester.  It came to eight pages on the risk/resilience factors for the social determinants of health in the lifespan of a child with Asperger's Syndrome.  Subcategorized the paper into the various phases of development starting from birth to adulthood, and made use of eleven ten sources (which to me seems pretty little).  It's worth thirty percent of my grade so I'm crossing my fingers =P

It sucks though because the weather is so perfect right now but I'm completely exhausted ~ I only got about four hours of sleep.  I could totally settle with sleeping outside right now if there were any sort of shade in my backyard >>''  Yeah know what sucks even more though?  I've got an exam on next months munch!  Like what the hell, I come out from an awesome munch night last Saturday and I can't even look forward to having another one next month?!  Bah!  Stupid stats exam from 7-10pm =.=**  It's going to be friggin' hard too!  My other exams are on the 10th and 21st, so there's plenty of spacing between each exam to get enough studying done.  Last day of classes is at the end of the month, so maybe I can squeeze in some fun things to do before crunch time.  

In more 'blah' related news, JadeFox is sinking in what sounds like a really dreadful situation - sucks for me because I commissioned him for a pic that I've been looking forward to getting when he starts.  So like, after he finishes WolfSpawn's commission I'm next in line, but who knows when that'll be :<  Hrmm, wait or get my monies back?  I really want to get a commission done by a big name artist and have the pic in my hand before my birthday in late September (two months, I'm guessing, is probably how long it'll take).  If I get my monies back I wouldn't mind putting it into getting something done by Marci (the badge to the rites of passage for babyfurs and all that jazz...).  But the thing is I already have my heart set on the commission I wanted JadeFox to do since it's something only he could make look awesome (no offense, it's just his style for that particular idea =\).  Marci makes things look cute >>'' I want something badass LOL.  I'll have to sleep on it I suppose.  I think I've still got a while to wait for my PadderCat commission, but I'm not to concern with that since I haven't paid for anything yet.

Anyways...next year might be really kickass!  Taken from a reply on one of Orca's entries by d_bandit is a list of furcons coming to Canada!
Furnal Equinox March 2010 Toronto
What The Fur June 2010 Montreal
Bytown Furry Convention July 31-August 1st, 2010 Ottawa
Condition Furry August 7-8, 2010 London

Munch night...crunch time!

Jul. 19th, 2009   10:16 AM
Munch nights seem to be getting better and better each month!  Well, technically last months was on a completely different level because of the badge and swag but yesterdays was still incredibly fun! 

I intended to get to the pub a little later then usual and settle with just having dessert since there was going to be a bbq at Kevydl and Danididit's place afterwards.  Instead, I took a nap and slept through my alarm - so I got there much later then planned.  I wore a Tena Super with a Bambino booster and plastic pants and packed a Tena Super with two Tena Day-Light Absorbent Pad, an Attends Slip, a onesie, and five tallboys into my backpack ~ chasing down the bus was hellz difficult @_@.  I arrived at the pub at around 7pm with a total of nine people - two from Michigan, two really cute looking guys from in and around Toronto {o'.'o}, one creepy old timer from Kitchener, and Nebulus64, Diprguy_toronto, and Kevydl.  Wish you guys could've made it, Dan_Raccoon, Shiro, and Orca ^^;  Hope to see you guys next month! 

The one thing I've always been buggered about arriving late is that you end up sitting at the end of the table, away from the discussions.  Sometimes I think western restaurants should use round tables like Chinese restaurants that way everyone can see, hear and talk to whoever.  But then again it's a bit impractical since you never know what the turnout will be, western food is usually by individual serving rather then sharing whatevers at the center of the table, and round tables = talking very loud (if you've ever been to a Chinese restaurant it's friggin' deafening, especially if the ceiling is low).  Oh well, just a random thought.  Guess it might have been a good thing I didn't arrive early and sat at the other end of the table with the old timer.  I was told afterwards that his wife was out of town and he came down looking to take care of a little boy o__O"  Not cool dude, the munches aren't a pickup buffet.  Anywhoo, didn't go for dessert (still craving cake D:) as planned since I was told the bbq was called off ~ it sorta threw me off because I thought that meant no gathering afterwards either and that I brought my backpack for nothing =\  But rather, I found out afterwards the gathering was still on ^^  I had the houseburger and a 7-up.  The pizza looked so tempting (even though I had pizza the other day) but I wanted fries as well ~ bad decision, their fries weren't to good =S  With tip, it came to thirteen bucks and some change. 

Can't say I remember what time we left, but afterwards I hitched a ride with Nebulus and one of the cute guys to Kevy and Dan's place.  It sucked that it started raining huge droplets as we were parking the car - their place was about a block away.  There was a bunch of familiar faces that trickled in not long after getting settled - like K-Dawg, BaBy_SB 1, TorontoGUY, BabyMatt.  In total there was at least 18 people, I remember most of their names but they'll go unsaid here since I donno their aliases.  Kicked off the night with a big cup of Appleton and sprite and a bit of weed on the patio.  Grrr!  D:<  There was a highchair on the patio I couldn't fit into!  I could sit in it just fine, but the tray couldn't lock into place because my diaper was to bulgy >>".  So it was a bit of a tease seeing other guys get locked in the chair =P  I've said it in the past, but I'll say it again ~ it is incredibly liberating to dress, socialize, and be yourself with like minded people.  I think at least six of us were in onesies and/or shortalls while most of the rest were in shirts and diapers.  XD My first time seeing Nebulus in a onesie ~ awesomeness!  One of the local ABDLs leaking unknowingly while a bunch of us watched a puddle form ~ priceless!  And another local who's uber ticklish ~ omg, so much fun to play with!  Man, knocking back four tallboys and smoking up way more then I've ever ~ kick ass night ensured.  There was just way to much fun and discussion I couldn't blog it all if I wanted to - some of which is a bit of a blur as well.  Good times never the less!

I got home at around 2:30am (thanks for the ride Nebulus, I really appreciate it!) took out my contacts and passed out on my bed.  Seriously, thank gawd I have the house to myself this weekend (I don't really count my grandparents since they usually hermit in their room most of the day) ~ I could totally make sleeping in a diaper and onesie a habit if I could.  It's a real relief because I haven't gotten diapered this much in so long (I must've used like eight diapers and boosters all weekend) partly because of the garbage strike and partly because of going green.  My family has been switching to reusable bags for groceries so the communal collection of plastic bags is dwindling >>''  You try throwing out used diapers without plastic bags, it's hella difficult!  Anywhoo, I slept off the dizziness for the most part but I'm really dehydrated.  I've got 24 hours to finish an assignment so no more distractions till it's done!  Crunch time! >:D

Oh yea, check out the new albums by Billy Talent, Twista, and The Dead Weather if you're into that type of music.  They're all really solid albums!
🌀 groggy  |  ♫ The Dead Weather - 60 Feet Tall

It's the weekend already? o__O

Jul. 17th, 2009   5:44 PM
The week seemed to go by rather quickly, not really a good thing since my summer courses end in two weeks which ensure final exams.  I did my course enrollment for Fall 2009 yesterday morning.  I'm only taking three courses - one of which I really need and the other two are sorta electives.  On Tuesdays I've got classes on and off from 10am till 5pm; SOCB05 (Sociology: Logic and Social Inquiry) and SOCB49 (Sociology: Family in Transition).  On Wednesdays I've got ANTC60 (Fieldwork in Social and Cultural Anthropology) which I've had difficulty getting into in the past only 40 people can take it at a time.  I could probably easily pick up a Minor in Sociology (on top of my Specialist in Medical Anthropology and Major in Health Studies) if I take a full course load and stay during the Winter semester...but I'm tired of school and the money well is drying up.  Speaking of school and monies, I've been running into my friend Alex at school more and more lately.  He works as a part-time cleaner for campus student union, but last month Belize doing archaeological fieldwork with a prof from another school.  He's doing the same anthro program as me, but a year younger - next semester he's taking a bunch of Spanish and anthro courses cause he wants to continue doing research at Latin American sites.  I am really impressed with his ambitions, it's really awesome watching people climb the metaphorical ladder.  Also, congrats to Nebulus64 for landing two jobs and having the luxury of picking which to pursue...nowadays I'd imagine it be hard just to land one job! Anywhoo, it's been a busy Friday and the rest of the weekend will probably be busy one way or another as well.  I drove my mom to work and dropped off my brother at Yorkdale Mall in the morning - he's going camping so I've got my bedroom to myself ^^.  Afterwards I went to the bank to get an issue with my convenience card sorted out - tis' the second time this year I had to get a new card.  Then I went to the G4S head office to return my uniform since I haven't worked with them in a year.  Got back my much needed last paycheck...but friggin' deductions at $114.36 so I only banked $666.25 :<  Waiting on my uniform deposit in the mail ~ it should come to over $100.  Next I drove to Scarborough to restock my diaper supply at the store I usually go to.  I'm getting a little less impressived everytime I shop there.  They only had one package of Attends Slip in medium and no Tranquility ATN.  Their selection of pastic back diapers is dwindling leaving only a really crappy no name brand and stuff not in my size .___.  I did a sinful thing and got a package of cloth cover Tena Super as well.  I've tried them before and they're really absorbent, but the cloth waist bands tear easily and the velcro tapes don't hold very well over long periods.  Plus, Tena are way more expensive than Attends T_T.  The Attends costed $28 while the Tena costed $37 ~ both had 28 diapers.  The store had Tena Classic, twelve for $9.50, but I've tried them in the past and they're not very absorbent.  Oh well, the only perk about the store has always been the freebie samples which I generously take xD  I scored three Tena Super, one Tena Ultra, one Tena Day-Plus Absorbent Pad, one Tena Night-Super Absorbent Pad, and six Tena Day-Light Absorbent Pad (XD)  A bit overboard, no?  Heh. After that I went for lunch.  I've been craving pizza (and sushi and cake .___.) for a while so I went to By the Slice and got a delux and meat lovers :3  It was very satisfying.  Last stop before heading home was the LCBO ~ picked up an assortment of tall boy cans.  From what I gather, there's going to be a bbq gathering at Kevydl and Danididit's place after the ABDL munch (note to self - eat lite! D:).  I don't wanna have to mooch off anyones liquor like last time =P  I took a little nap when I got home and then went to the gym at 2:30pm.  Picked up my mom from work not long after finishing, and here I am now :3  I've got a paper due Monday which I've yet to start...I really don't want this to drag on until Sunday night or worse, an all nighter =.=*  So...I'll start that tomorrow (lol). 
😴 tired  |  ♫ Twista ft. Busta Rhymes - Billionaire
Link

Taking my mind off...

Jul. 15th, 2009   11:12 PM
><** Dammit!  Every single time I hop onto FA I'm an hour late from a free smexy picture!  StrawberryNeko has been making journal posts with commission info - the first commenter gets a freebie pic of their fursona done to her liking D:  What's really impressive about her is that she's really quick with her drawings while keeping a consistent soft and smooth style that doesn't look rushed.  She's got a YouTube channel that I've subscribed to for a number of months, but recently she made a new channel - check out her timelapse videos ^^.  Yosh!  I shall make it my goal to leave an FA window open and spam refresh it every so often! 

===================================================================================

Recently I made sculptie shoes in Second Life ^^.  For a while now I've wanted kids velcro shoes with cartoon prints on them for my avatar, but a lot of the stuff being sold were cruddy.  The only problem I have with my new kicks is the attachments on the feet - my animation override makes my avatar move when idle causing parts of my feet to come out of the shoes very awkwardly (kinda hard to explain, it's just gross). 

Hellz yea that's Doraemon prints!  When I was a kid (and my Chinese vocabulary was half decent) Doraemon was my crack!  I loved that cartoon!  The first pic is just a step by step processes of rearranging the sculpties to my liking and then texturing them.  The prints of Doraemon in the bottom two pics that I put on the side of the shoes aren't from the actual cartoon series (since Doraemon is way old).  I took'em off of this website - 55 pictures of Doraemon in cosplay (LOL).  Oh, and I put in a sculptie sock, which is the white thing coming out the top =P.  To show off my new kicks, I went and bought a new pair of shorts (L$50, hella cheap at Loki's shop) since the jeans that I've been wearing since forever cover up and bleed over the shoes. 
🌿 calm  |  ♫ Theophilus London - Ultraviolent

Family update

Jul. 14th, 2009   10:44 PM
I've been feeling not like myself lately and for the most part it has to do with my grandpa's health.  It's this sort of helplessness while waiting to find out what will happen next that's been very unsettling for me - I can't imagine how much more difficult it must feel for my dad.  Writing about it, I hope, will help easy my conscious a little since it's something that's been on my mind and become a bit of a distraction to my every day life.  During the weekend my mom took a Greyhound up to Ottawa to visit my gramps and to keep my dad company during the drive back home on Sunday.  They came home in the afternoon and we all went out for my grandma's 80th birthday dinner with extended family - I think that's the only thing that's lifted my spirit all week.  Her actual birthday isn't until the 22nd, but this weekend was the only time everyone was available.  The next morning my dad took a Greyhound back to Ottawa leaving the car this time for us to use; he'll still be able to get around since my gramps has a car.  He called this morning and again an hour ago saying he had to take my grampa to the hospital again.  He passed out twice in the morning - the second time it happened he hit his chin causing it to bleed.  The bleeding didn't stop despite being a small cut so my dad called his doctor and they told him to call an ambulance; they took him to emergency.  Not only was he having respiratory problems because of the tumours in his lungs but his appetite has been nill for most of last week.  We found out why after the hospital ran some tests - they found stomach ulcers that were causing internal bleeding and low blood pressure.  For whatever reason he's been taking three types of anti-inflammatory medicines, which in the long run can damage the stomach lining.  So right now he's staying in intensive care, hooked up to a transfusion machine for 1.2 litres of blood that'll take twenty-four hours - it's really shocking since a normal human only has five litres of blood.  I'd probably getting the chills if I saw him right now since he's got like four different tubes going in and out of him...poor guy.  What's more, my dad was asked by the physicians that if my grampa's heart ever stopped, should they try to revive him.  That's one seriously heavy question - he told them he didn't know mainly because he didn't know how treatable the lung tumours were even if he makes it through the stomach ulcer treatment.  We won't know much about the tumours until next Monday when he has his consultation but for the most part it doesn't look good.  My grandma, who's in Detroit for a family reunion with her kids for the week, said she'd rather have him to pass away peacefully if his heart stopped working rather than continue suffering. I donno how these things work and who's decision takes precedence, but either way I don't know where I stand on the issue.  I just want to visit him as soon as possible but that doesn't seem possible for me until after next Monday since I've got a couple of assignments due.  Um...congratulations to my parents as well.  Today is their twenty-fifth anniversary, an incredible milestone!  It's really unfortunate they can't be together, but there's nothing that can really be done about it.  I think my mom is sorta use to it since my dad has worked in Calgary and San Francisco for a number of years.  They're travelling to Shanghai later in the year as a gift for themselves; my brother and I are paying for one of the four day excursions they're doing.
😞 depressed
Link

Hip hop and story idea...

Jul. 10th, 2009   2:14 AM
I'm feeling pretty good right now ^^  Got back from the gym not long ago and had a great workout.  I did 5.8km on the treadmill in forty-five minutes and burned 466 calories ~ I usually stop after twenty-five minutes but instead I took a minute break and continued at it.  It's great to be able to push myself despite knowing I'll feel the pain in the morning >.<  It certainly helps to get my blood pumping after a really boring week - also while reading about everyone else's fun time at AC (T_T).  It's to bad I finished watching the first season of Storm Hawks (waiting for the second season to finish downloading) or else I would have had something to do for the coming weekend.  Oh well, guess it'll be good not to have any distractions to excuse myself from working on a story I've been brewing up.

But before that, I wanna share some amazing artists I've been listening to since the start of the month.  Last month I had a couple of Blalock's Indie Rock Playlist on my iTouch (pretty much some dudes list of various artists complied into a playlist and put up for download) this month it's back to hip hop ~ the NYC underground artists more specifically.  I remember a couple of months ago I wrote about Print's (of Good Day, Good Night) Super Friends track where it features a ton of NYC's up and coming rappers and they all take on a super hero persona ~ I still love the track and l've listened to it more times than I can count!  Download Print's album (Comic Books: Unlimited) if you're into hip hop and Superman, you won't be disappointed.  So lately I've been downloading and listening to some of the featured artists on the track I just mentioned ~ it's pretty awesome since (I think) all of them are unsigned so they pretty much jump on a couple of tracks from one anothers albums.  A couple of them also work as a group called A.O.K! Collective which is made up of emcees, dj's, producers, singers, and graphic artists.  One of the artists, Mickey Factz, is featured in XXL Magazines Class of 2009 series.  Unfortunately for him, he got a B- in the Midterm Grades.  But still, most of the ten artists mentioned in the Class of 09' have great potential ~ like come on, who hasn't heard of KiD CuDi or Asher Roth yet?!  Some of the other artists from the list I like so far are Ace Hood and Blu.  Anyways back to the NYC indie rappers, the one thing I really like most about them is that they rarely (if ever) rap about guns, women, bling, money, sex, drugs, killing, drinking, or the hood.  I'm really not a big fan of gangster rap and I think that alone is what people nowadays immediately come to imagine when they hear hip hop.  But really, there are so many awesome artists that take on different perspectives through personas, or rap politically, or about life (outside the streets) or even about food! (MF Doom made an entire album about food while taking samples from Adult Swim)  Speaking of samples, My Crew's All Thinner (download it if you're into really groovy hip hop) is a mixtape by the production duo (2 Hungry Bros) of AOK Collective, they samples beats I recognize since a couple of big name artists have used them (but I couldn't tell you who made those samples since they're fairly dated).  For example: track 3 uses the same sample as the one from Touch the Sky by Kanye West ft. Lupe Fiasco, track 6 uses the same sample as Jay-Z's Roc Boys (And the Winner is), track 13 uses the same sample as Lil' Wayne's Dr. Carter, and so forth.  o__O Man this paragraph is getting long, I haven't even gotten to P.CASSO or Theophilus London yet!  There's just to much good listening =P 


The video is from Animatrix, Kid's Story.  The beat is really chill!


Theo is an electro/hip hop artist ~ I'm not into techno or dance music because I really dislike repetitive music, but his combination works well for me.  Certainly makes me wanna get up and groove to the beats!  Download his latest album if you're interested, This Charming Mixtape.


I thought this video was too friggin' funny not to share!  It's Fresh Daily, P.CASSO, and Print at the New York Comic Con 2009.  P.CASSO's mixtape was just okay in my opinion, though it had some great collaborations - check it out if you're interested, Earth Tones.

 

Now onto the story idea!  So last month I made a post about a short story contest being held by the ABDL story forum I use to post my stuff on.  Interestingly, no one has submitted an entry yet ~ maybe they're all procrastinating or coming up with epic stories o__O''  Anywhoo, I've made posts in the past about story ideas, but I've rarely had the time or initiative to flesh them out with awesomeness.  There was one idea about a mommy affair that I've begun to re-imagine, added to that someone's unfortunate circumstance which I'm incorporating ~ if you know who you are after reading my story idea, I mean no disrespect and I really hope things work out for the better!  If you don't like spoilers (because I'm more than certain I'll fnish this story!) I don't advise you to continue reading. 

For my new story I'm going to recycle the unnamed main character from a short story I wrote a few years ago.  The setting will be three years from where he was last mentioned.  He'll be an unemployed, temperamental, severally stressed dude who's in a relationship with a professor (profession possible to change) and now-and-then mommy.  They live in the same condo as in the earlier story.  So the story begins with the man having a conversation over the phone with his mother.  She's divorcing her husband, an abrupt action that's greatly distressed the family - she's also taking half of her retired husbands pension and half the value of the house.  The man's father has to vacate in a week and as a result will be moving in with him until things are resolved.  The man isn't happy because he's grown to love his mother more so than he does for his father (who placed strong values on discipline while growing up).  Pretty much, he and his dad have never seen eye to eye or gotten along very well, while on the other hand, he and his mother were really tight.  So the man feels betrayed, remorseful for his dad (having always been a momma's boy until finding out she's a bitch), and guilt (he feels as though they stayed together for his sake and after he moved out the tension began to build).  So added to the fact that he's already stressed out and temperamental, he's running on a short fuse!

Afterwards, the man hangs up angrily (haven't thought of a reason for the divorce) he recieves some affection from his wife/girlfriend (?)(haven't decided what kind of relationship they're in) - ABDL play ensures as it is the man's primary destressor.  So after that the story skips ahead to the week of the man's father's welcoming into thier home.  Before his arrival the couple dismantles their crib, changing table, dresser, and empties the closet; effectively getting rid of their nursery to make room.  In that process, it's heart breaking for the man to hide all the things he needs to relieve his stress into the basement storage.  During the stay, the man doesn't connect well with his father - the two are socially awkward around one another because growing up they had opposite characteristics.  The man's girlfriend/wife does most of the icebreaking between the two and helps the man cope however way she can.  Basically the only thing the man can give his dad is companionship and a place to stay ~ aside from that he really doesn't want to be around his dad since his privacy (and ABDL play) are non-existant.  His father doesn't help much either since he's like a brick wall and fighting his own battle mentally while trying not to let his emotions show (because that would distort the way his son has always seen him as).

So a couple of weeks go by and the girlfriend/wife has a week long conference (or feild research?) to attend at another city.  This pretty much puts the man in a position of extreme discomfort - being around his father, no social/mutual support from his girlfriend/wife, and absolutely no ABDL play.  Throughout the week the man is going bonkers - cause he doesn't have anywhere to be since job searching can pretty much be done online.  As the week nears its end, the man can't stand it anymore and seeks some form of stress relief.  He goes to Miss Sanderbrook's nursery (mentioned in the preceeding story) for a couple of days.  He has the time of his life in the most literal of senses (I don't know how much detail or emphasis I'll put in this part of the story).  During his stay, Miss Sanderbrook's makes a phone call to the man's girlfriend/wife.  It turns out they're acquainted....rivals in fact!  In the past, the gf/wife was a mommy who ran a nursery that held a much larger clientele than Miss Sanderbrook.  So as a form of revenge (and of course, some how Sanderbrook knows the man and gf/wife are together ~ haven't thought of a way to explain that one yet) Sanderbrook goes into detail (maybe even lying) about the things she's doing with her man. 

When the week is up, the man goes back home pretending nothing has happened.  His gf/wife comes home furious and explains how she knows what went down at Miss Sanderbrook's nursery.  Unable to reply back because it's all true (or for the most part), the gf/wife leaves the man ~ some sort of affair/emotional affair/infidelity (I'll have to look into which concept is most appropriate).  How I'll end the story...I have yet to come up with.  Probably somehow connect the man's situation with his fathers ~ two divorced dudes finally seeing eye to eye with one another.  If you've read this far, lemme know what you think if anything comes to mind ^^.  Despite my weekend already starting today, it's way late and I want to get up early.  Peace out. 
😴 tired  |  ♫ Nikkobya Bisou – Le Machete’

Jul. 8th, 2009   12:12 AM

Family

Jul. 7th, 2009   11:50 PM
The next few weeks are gonna be pretty heavy for me ~ a lot of stuff is going on with my family.  My dad called not long ago from Ottawa, he said the biopsy my grandpa underwent was fine and the lab would have a detailed report by Friday.  The doctors are fairly positive it's lung cancer.  There's a tumour in his left and right lung - the left one is growing much quicker and its been pressing up against the airway causing him to pass out.  Hopefully after Friday we'll know if there's an effective treatment.  Unfortunately my grandma is away for the week in Detroit visiting her son from Hong Kong (they've been planning the visit for a very long time), so my dad is taking care of him and the house.  He's planning to come back this weekend but head up again after.  I really want to visit my gramps as soon as possible =(  Among cancer deaths in Canada, lung cancer is the second highest.  I know a girl who had a father die of prostate cancer - her family and mine were really close; I practically grew up with her.  It was more than a decade ago and it was the first time someone I knew died.  I feel very fortunate that this doesn't happen often for me, but when it does (and especially now that I'm older and have a better understanding of how precious life is) the thought of it gives me goosebumps.  These feelings of worry and fright are on a whole different level than most other things that cause them.  Being sympathetic barely scratches the surface of how much I feel for my grandpa, grandma, and dad.  I wish these feelings don't continue towards the far end when the week is up.  Other than that depressing bit of information, my grandma's (mom's side) 80th birthday...um, sometime next next week...but her big dinner party with friends and relatives is this Sunday.  Other than the fact that 80 is a huge milestone, it's doubly a big deal because in the Chinese dialect the word for 'eight' sounds similar to the word which means 'prosper' or 'wealth' or 'fortune'.  So, hope she gets showered with lots of luck and happiness when that day comes by ^^ Next on the calendar, my parents will be celebrating their 25th anniversary next Tuesday!  My brother and I have no real clue what to get them ~ a silver picture frame seems a bit cliche, but that'll be a last resort.  We were thinking of maybe paying for some type of tour excursion for their trip to Shanghai, but that isn't until much later into the year =\  Oh wells, still got time to decide on something. Lastly, my older brother is turning 24 on the 28th.  I usually just let him pick his own present and then pick up the bill - doubt this year will be any different.  It could probably be cheaper to strike a deal with him ~ be more social and friendly around him....but he's a jerk so blah. 
😢 sad  |  ♫ Company of Thieves - In Passing

Reading week wrap-up...

Jul. 6th, 2009   12:27 AM
Well despite having an incredibly boring week (compared to all the previous weeks of going out with friends), it was fun in its own quirky ways.  I literally did not step foot outside my house for most of the week because the weather was constantly on-and-off rain or intimidatingly grey clouds.  A plus side to living like a hermit was I didn't spend a penny!  But then again, Jade Fox put up a post on FurAffinity that he'd be open for commissions for a short while - so I sent him an email hopeful that I can get on the list!  >>'' If my request goes through the commission I want will cost $50 ~ so, save a little, spend a little =P.  My one character full page commission will be the one I described in an earlier entry ~ the Toph outfit and stance, with chopsticks and a dumpling. 

Another bonus to staying home and growing up my facial hair was that I had a reason to play Second Life ~ I was hella bored!  I watched eight episodes of Storm Hawks so that sorta influenced me to build something that I'll probably never get done.  I'm attempting to build the Condor Battlecruiser ~ but so far I've only been working on the asthetics on the bridge.  In all fairness to my slow progress, though, I only actually started working on it during the weekend =P  



The Condor Battlecruiser


The steering mechanism


My steering mechanism ~ still in progress


Pipes!


My pipes =P  Easy to make, but painstakingly time consuming. 

I'll texture everything at the very end, so all the white circles will eventually have a gauge face of some kind. 

I also had the awesome opportunity to make a new friend ~ whom I converted into a diaper fur >:D.  I met Kandi Vendetta on Tuesday and we sorta chatted/roleplayed our way into exploring her DL side by Thursday and eventually getting her in diapers by Sunday (lol).  Actually, she got me in diapers too since I rarely wore my Musuko diapers with my current avatar.  We both bought one of SilverWulf Dryke's Pawzie's (L$350) which is fairly well designed and scripted.  It has a hide feature which isn't uncommon in most diapers, but is something that's lacking with the Musuko diaper (which costs L$1000).  I think I see a bit of myself in Kandi in that she didn't explore the furry fandom until beginning Second Life ~ I knew about the fandom, but never really interacted and hung out with other furs until I started playing three years ago.  Plus, she's never heard of AB/DL/Babyfur until this week, so kuddos to me for expanding the 'community' (lol).  Unfortunately, she doesn't use an IM program and neither of us really play SL much during weekdays ~ hopefully we can keep in contact through email or something...despite saying the same thing with a couple of other online friends from Perfect World, whose contacts have dissolved ^^;;

While on that topic,   made a post on Wednesday in the Babyfur community here on LJ - quite fascinating.  BitterGrey is the man behind this amazing website.  Despite knowing about it for years and years, I've never actually browsed around to read some of his articles!  Most of the articles are part of the Survey Project pretty much summarizing the findings while contributing some interesting discussions.  I love the fact that it's referenced.  His writing "sounds" academic, but some parts aren't written as clearly as they could have been.  I really don't know much about his background and it's gotten me quite curious ~ I sent him an email just telling him my interest in his work and if ever he needed a second opinion or help revising, I'd be more than happy to help.  Wouldn't mind applying some of my anthro knowlege aswell, but meh, he seems to fly solo. 

Lastly, I want to wish the best of health to my grandpa (father's side) who passed out and was hospitalized last week.  An x-ray showed some sort of tumour in the upper area of his lung.  He's going for a biopsy tomorrow and my dad is heading up to Ottawa in the morning to be with him.  I really hope it's benign, but then again, he was a chronic smoker for the longest time before quitting nearly a decade ago ~ late onset maybe?  I donno, I hope he'll be fine, that's all that really matters. 
😶 blank  |  ♫ Animal Collective - My Girl
Link

Wimbledon Finals!

Jul. 5th, 2009   1:56 PM
Absolutely incredible tennis!  Such high level play will honestly be hard top in the next grand slam, Davis cup, masters, any sorta tournament.  There's just something about Wimbledon besides the rich history of the tournament that captures a lot of amazing moments in tennis.  So glad I had the opportunity to watch some wicked matches. 

Didn't get to watch the Williams sisters in action yesterday for the ladies' singles finals, unfortunately.  Serena Williams (2) defeated older sister Venus Williams (3) 7-6 and 6-2.  Serena really put on the beating in the second set, which was a bit surprising since Venus clobbered current number one Dinara Safina in the semi-finals.  One thing I really dislike is that Safina is STILL first among the womens ranking.  The ranking system favours players who are most consistent throughout the tournament circuit - Safina has won the Rome and Madrid Masters and reached the finals of the Australian and French Open this year.  Serena has won three grand slams in the current calender (US Open 2008, this years Australia Open and Wimbledon).  Isn't that just ridiculous?  You don't need to win grand slam titles to be world number one despite because of the rule change this year - grand slams are no longer worth 2000 points, rather they're worth the same as Masters tournaments, 1000 points.  Bah, the Williams are on a whole different level than Safina, they deserve more glory ><**  Anyways, I also didn't get to watch the ladies' doubles finals, which was held not long after the ladies' singles.  The Williams (4) defeated third seeds Samantha Stosur and Rennae Stubbs of Australia 7-6 and 6-4.  Quite incredible considering the Aussies had the advantage playing against two tired sisters (unless they considered their finals a warm-up o__O).  Congrats to the two of them ^^.

Caught the first and third set of the gentleman's doubles finals on tv.  The first seeded Bryan brothers (Bob and Mike) faced second seeded Daniel Nestor (he's Canadian, woohoo! \o.o/) and Nenad Zimonjic.  I don't watch much doubles since they rarely show it on tv, so it was really awesome seeing how it's done - the strategy and coordination is way is amazing.  The first set went to a tiebreak (9-7) while the first three sets none of the pair broke each others serve.  Zimonjic was definitely power heavy of the pair although I didn't really like how he crouches when he isn't serving - when he gets up the direction he'll move in is more predictable.  The Bryan's were incredible at the net, but they didn't quite utilize the doubles alley as well as Nester did, especially when volleying.  Defending champs Nestor and Zimonjic won with a final score of 7-6, 6-7, 7-6, 6-3.  Good show!

That just leaves the gentleman's singles finals, which finished not long ago ~ sat in front of the television for four eyes o__O.  Friggin' epic.  Aside from the major upsetting defeat of Andy Murray (3) by Andy Roddick (6) in the semi-finals, this had to be one of Roddick's best career match ever.  This tournament really put Roddick back as a serious competitor, where as last year it was questionable whether he'd still be able to hold his own against the top ten after a string of bad runs.  Despite that and slowly dropping in rank, he's been in the top ten for the last seven years, he's got the best serve in the game, and he's improved everything in the last year (he lost a lot of weight and his volleying is way better) ~ so you really can't count the man out just yet cause he's still got a couple of years left in his career.  Can you imagine how he must have felt the day after defeating Murray (home town favourite and the best returner in the game) reading the local papers?  Serious confidence booster!  On the other hand, Roddick was up against Roger Federer (2) ~ they've played twenty times in the past, but Federer won eighteen of them (that's could squash anyones confidence =\).  Federer is undeniably the greatest tennis player of his cohort, winning Wimbledon would make it his fifteenth grand slam title ~ the highest anyone has ever gotten (Pete Sampras has fourteen). 

The two players held points on their serve for most of the match (very impressive, in fact, that Federer couldn't break Roddick).  First set, Roddick had his first chance on his serve at 5-5.  He was down 0-30 but made two unreturnable serves (unreturnable is different from ace, where a return is (un)intentionally made but in no way has a chance of making it back to the servers' side), one of which was a body serve (where you aim for the bounce at the other player forcing them to move or defend).  Federer got his first break chance, but a baseline return that was called out gave Roddick the point after a challenge that didn't go his way.  After two deuces, Federer had his second break chance, but the Roddick serve denied that.  Federer received his third break chance having won a point for advantage, but lost it again after making a return to the baseline which was called good.  Roddick challenged it, which confirmed it was actually out.  Deuce number three, followed by advantage Federer, and than another challenge by Federer after made a return to the baseline which was called out, which hawkeye confirmed was out.  Four and final deuce after one of Federer's forehand goes out.  Advantage Roddick, followed by a rally which he sends the ball cross court with a forehand - Federer attempts to return the ball but it doesn't go over the net, regardless though Roddick was already at the net ready for the return.  6-5 Federer serves, but at 30-30 he makes two returns that go out giving Roddick the first set!  Roddick broke Federer's serve, amazing!

Another chance almost came for Roddick during the second set when he lead 5-4, Federer serving.  He double faulted to make it 30-30.  But at 40-30 after a 23 stroke rally and both going up to the net, Federer makes a cross court return for a 5-5 tie.  In Federer's next serve, down 5-6, he makes some huge points with three aces and one unreturnable serve, he only let one point go after making a drop shot that didn't go over.  The second set tiebreak started off with an incredible ace by Roddick (143mph or 230km/hr o__O).  (Roddick holds the top 3 fastest serves recorded in pro tennis at 155mph (249.5km) back in 2004, followed by 150mph and 149mph).  Winning each of his service points, as well as a rally on Federer's serve, Roddick was up 5-1 ~ just two points from being two sets to none!  He continues the assult for a 6-2 lead after pushing Federer to a defensive position with a crosscourt backhand and a mid-court volley.  Federer fought back, however, with aces and points of rallies for 6-5.  Roddick missed a crucial overhead volley as well as a flat low ball return from Federer.  This made it a 7-6 set point for Federer on his serve, who eventually won the tiebreak.  That was a bit disappointing of Roddick to let Federer catch up six points. 

Third set, Federer started off strong by winning his first two service games with two aces each, leading 2-1.  Despite Roddick hasn't dropped his service game yet, it's undeniable that Federer was winning on his serve much quicker.  He might let one point go or make an error, but that's about it, where as for Roddick, he's really working hard for the points on his service - plus he tends to lose about two points per.  Not good since Roddick serves heavy and doesn't move around the court as well as Federer (who's more superior in stamina especially on clay and grass surfaces).  What's more, throughout the match Federer had a huge number of aces, which is usually Roddick's main offense.  But then again, ace count only tells half the story since Roddick had far more unreturnable serves.  In the third set tiebreak, Federer served first - in the rallies he really utilized his low backhand returns forcing Roddick to run up to the net.  Roddick was a bit inconsistent at the net, especially at 6-3 in the tiebreak.  Federer made a drop shot cross court, Roddick ran for it but returned it right back at Federer rather than a volley in the other direction.  Eventually, Federer had stronger serves and better net play with his volleys, wining the tiebreak 7-5. 

Down two sets to one, Roddick started the fourt set with his eighteenth ace.  Federer made two returns on the line with a lot of spin making it seem as though it'd go out.  Still though, that wasn't enough to break his serve.  Instead, he breaks Federer's serve during a 2-1 lead at 40-30 after a great rally and a volley return at the net by Federer which doesn't go over.  3-1 Roddick leads and serves and eventually winning the set - each winning on their on serve.  Federer did however made a nice attempt at a comeback with three aces on his serve, down 4-2.  The fourth set really showed the level of tennis Roddick was playing at; Federer was definitely trying to win back the points when it wasn't his serve (he was one set away from winning, he wouldn't have given away free points even if he wanted to), but that never happened.  Roddick did however give up a match when he slipped on Federer's serve at 5-3 ~ he had a bit of a limp for the remainder of the match as well as on his own serve (he was down 0-30 o__O). 

Fifth set, oh my!  Neither player have really great five set records (which is sorta nostalgic for Federer since he lost last years Wimbledon in the fifth set against Rafael Nadal (1) - the key was to break his serve, which hasn't been happening in this and last years finals!).  What's more, there is no final set tiebreak at Wimbledon, so either of them have to win two matches in a row to win.  I noticed a lot more second serves during this set, might have had something to do with fatigue or the placement of the sun since shadows were forming on the court.  The three hour mark came during Roddick's serve, down 3-2.  At 3-3, Federer made his 33rd ace.  on Roddick's serve, dowin 4-3, he shot a variety of serves including two body serves (can you imagine a ball coming 130mph at you? o__O Kinda scary).  At 4-4, Federer made his 36th ace, while at 5-4, Roddick made his 22nd ace.  A break point opportunity for Roddick came during Federer's serve at 8-8, down 15-40 ~ but Federer took it to deuce after some taking the pace off the ball and Roddick's movement.  He won the next two points after Roddick returned just out of the baseline.  At 9-9, Federer made his 41st ace despite a challenge and hawkeye confirming the in.  At 9-10 Roddick made his 25th ace.  Still not having broken Roddick's serve, Federer made three aces in a row for a total of 45 at 11-11 (at this rate it's pretty much play till someone passes out D:).  Roddick, however, puts a good display of his own by winning his serve in straight points.  =.=* Four hour mark came during Roddick's serve, down 12-13.  He pushed Federer around with two huge forehands to lead 40-15, but Federer regained some control at the net, making it deuce.  Roddick closed his serve with a 136mph ace, and an unreturnable.  At 14-14, Federer made his 50th ace, as well as finally breaking Roddick's serve at 15-14.  Roddick made two really bad returns followed by a reassuring ace and two unreturnables, for 40-30.  After a really ugly backhand return that goes way out, the match goes to deuce.  But after two deuces, Federer gained the advantage with a drop shot and a cross court return.  T_T Championship point goes to Federer after Roddick makes a really bad return.  Damn.  Final score, 7-5, 6-7, 7-6, 3-6, 16-14. 

I felt really bad for Roddick, I kinda wanted him to win just so he could stick it to the man (plus, he could really use a grand slam title, since only having the 2006 US Open).  He looked like he was about to cry while accepting his plate, but his interview was really awesome ~ it was well said, humble, and funny.  Pete Sampras was in the crowd, so Roddick said "Sorry Pete, I tried to hold him off" hehe (cause they're both Americans and before the win, Federer and Sampras were tied with 14 grand slam titles).  Oh wells, it's been a good year for Federer (he's expecting a baby sometime around the US Open ^^) and definitely there's no question that he's one of the all time greatest, so kuddos to him. 
🌟 optimistic  |  ♫ The Amsterdams - Suffering & Surfing
Link

Wimbledon Semifinals: Roger Federer (2) vs. Tommy Haas (24)

Jul. 3rd, 2009   10:26 AM
Woke up early to catch another semifinals and it wasn't disappointing ^^.  Haas played exceptionally high level tennis (though I wouldn't say it was up to Nadal's level, so it wasn't that entertaining) for his career.  He served strong and had a lot of well placed, aggressive counter punches on Federer (whom played his usual all-around court display, very rarely showed any signs of pressure).  It was fairly apparent that Haas was playing much harder than Federer ~ Fed made a lot of his shots look easy, as he so often does.  Haas has been pro and on the tournament circuit for years, he can hold his own against top seeded players ~ it's just a shame that's he hasn't been winning the big ones and dropping to 24th.  Federer will regain his number one ranking if he wins this tournament!

I began watching during the first set tiebreak ~ great tennis early on.  However, during some of the replays Haas seemed to be a bit edgy and temperamental during the first set.  He eventually lost the tiebreak.  Throughout the rest of the next sets, Roger really applied the pressure on Haas by exploiting some key weaknesses.  For example, Haas stands fairly far from the baseline when it isn't his serve - this has given Federer a lot of opportunity to advance forward during a rally.  By doing so it keeps Haas at the baseline playing defensively ~ Federer uses this tactic and wins the point with a volley or overhead smash.  The smash is really difficult to return because Federer sends it cross court (the opposite direction) with a really high bounce.  Another fall back with being forced on the defensive is that Federer has sent many forehands cross court causing Haas to chase after them, usually returning them with a backhand lobe that's uncontrolled.  

One of the funnier moments during the second set was when Haas was down 3-4, 30-40.  Federer made a return to the baseline forcing Haas to run back, he makes an under the leg return that hits the net.  The camera zooms in on his girlfriend mouthing "Why would you do that?!"  =P Oh Haas, you've gotta play like ever point matters when facing Federer lol.  Incredibly though, Haas has been able to hold his service game very well mainly because he's putting a lot of effort into his first and second serve ~ consequently it's lowered his second serve winning percentage.  On the other hand, Federer had a ton of break points on Haas's serve which he didn't win.  For example during the second set, set point for Federer (5-4 lead), Haas serves and aces for the deuce.  He wins the next two points with an unreturnable serve down the middle and an error by Federer.  It's obvious though that Federer is having zero difficulty winning on his serve (which he practically always wins in other matches as well).  During the 5-5 tie, Federer gives only one point away winning the rest with a baseline half volley, and going up to the net twice. 

A set and match point that Federer was able to win at, not during his serve, was on the second set up 6-5, and 40-30.  Haas deuced, but Federer advantaged with a running forehand that went cross court.  Haas lost the set after returning to the baseline that landed out.  Another comical moment of the match was during the third set, Federer's serve leading 40-0 and 2-2.  Federer made a drop shot cross court from the baseline forcing Haas to run up to the net, returning with his own drop shot also forcing Federer to run up to the net.  Just as Federer is about to make his return, a high lobe, Haas starts waving his arms in the air trying to be a distraction.  Whether it worked or not is questionable, but Federer's lobe went out =P  I love that they both have a sense of humour since Federer was smiling despite the out.  Man, if it was someone else like Marat, he'd probably smack his racquet on the other player since they were so near each other! 

Third set, another break point for Federer leading 40-15 and 4-3.  Haas makes two excellent serves giving him deuce.  For the advantage, Haas makes a return that lanes on the baseline after a great rally ~ Federer challenges but the call was in.  Goes back to deuce after Haas returns a net ball.  This is followed by another great rally (22 strokes!) where Haas makes another return on the baseline.  Again, Federer challenges the call but this time he's right ~ only 2mm out.  Advantage Federer, but an unreturnable serve makes it deuce again.  This gets repeated for a fourth deuce - Federer is all out of challenges for the set.  Federer wins after a double fault and a bad backhand slice on the net by Haas.  No doubt, this pretty much closed the game since Federer served for a 5-3 lead.  His last shot was the most spectacular!  40-0 for a triple match point to reach the championship finals, finishes with a very sexy Pete Sampras style jump smash!  Beautiful.  Final score 7-6, 7-6, 6-3.  (random stats: Federer winning % on 1st serve = 89%, 2nd serve = 81%.  Haas winning % on 1st serve = 74, 2nd serve = 45%)

Which Andy will Federer face in the finals?!  Right now Andy Roddick (6) is playing Andy Murray (3).  So far Roddick has won the first set 6-4, but he's lost his service game in the second set.  Listening to the Radio Wimbledon, it's so obvious that they're rooting for Murray because he's Scottish (and no English player has won Wimbledon in 73 years!) - not to say the commentators aren't doing a fair job at tracking the game though.  If Roddick wins, Federer will have a way easy time in the finals since he's clobbered Roddick in the past more times than I can count.  If Murray wins, Federer will have a tougher match up in the finals - plus the crowd won't be cheering for him, which rarely ever happens.  If Murray makes it to the finals, the Queen of England will sit in her royal box to watch o__O.  I don't know who I want to win...maybe Roddick since he's only won one grand slam (US Open in 2006).  Murray hasn't won any grand slams (runner-up in the 2008 US Open losing to Federer) but he's only 22 so he still has a lot of time to gain some glory. 

Oh, they also showed the last set of the Women's Doubles Semifinals.  The Williams sisters (4)  demolished doubles first seed Cara Black and Liezel Huber 6-1, 6-2.  Incredible.  The Williams honestly didn't look like they were trying much - as if they were playing a warm-up for their finals tomorrow!  I've never watched a women's doubles match (they rarely even air men's doubles on tv), so it was really interesting to watch how it's done.  I can't imagine how much cash the two are making from this tournament alone o__O
😌 content
Link

Wimbledon Semifinals: Serena Williams (2) vs. Elena Dementieva (4)

Jul. 2nd, 2009   1:14 PM
Oh my gawd...this match was freakin' epic!  Easily one of the greatest women's tennis matches I've ever watched!  I honestly can't stop smiling having watched such brilliant tennis.  These two women were smaking the ball with so much force and control it was insane ~ they were literally playing as though EVERY point mattered.  This match was totally determined by skill, mentality, and millimeteres (challanges and hawkeye seriously raised the level of competition!). 

Deme is a flat ball hitter meaning she returns low and fast.  She also relies on her serve which has vastly improved over the years - she use to serve 7x mph, now she's doing 9x mph, plus she's tossing the ball over her head which makes reading the serve much harder.  Throughout the match she wasn't serving to the forehand side which was great strategy since that's one of Serena's main offense.  What's more, Deme had incredible winning percentages for both first and second serve - most players are less likely to win on their second serve.  Lastly, the matches leading up to this one Deme was double faulting a lot (she had around 30 going into this match) but quite upsetting to Serena that didn't happen much.  She only began double faulting much later into the game. 

On the other hand Serena was whiping out aces while having a bit of difficulty with on some crucial returns.  It was really apparent that Deme's flat returns had an impact since Serena had a lot of net returns and high unintentional lobes.  Her forehand was a bit on-and-off; sometimes they were wide and other times they were beautifully placed right down the line - but when the points really mattered she was phenomenal.  Serena has won 10 major titles while Deme has only made it to two grand slam finals (losing both the French Open and US Open in 2004), however in terms of Olympics she's won a gold medal in Beijing and a silver medal in Sydney.  Finally, Deme has beaten Serena three out of the four times they've played against each other.  So statistically it's a really tight match up, but in terms of playing ability Serena has always been better until now ~ since both players had an extremely easy time leading up to this semi-finals (they never dropped a set). 

The first set seemed seemed to be in Serena's favour when she was up 4-3, 40-0, and serving.  But, moments of possible turning points were plenty throughout the match ~ and calling on these moments would have been unwise since it was such a tight match and points were being traded in both direction.  Deme amazingly caught up and won it for a 4-4 tie.  When it came to the 6-6 tiebreak, the intensity was off the roof; Deme really raised her game to the Williams sister level.  She won the tiebreak 6-4 despite double faulting and relying on her second serve, Serena returned wide and out.

I liked how the rallies were really 50-50, both players were playing so well the point could have gone either way ~ and I think that's why Deme had such great second serve winning percentage even with her variety of serves.  On the other hand I thought that Serena moved a bit more sluggish than usual (like she had weights on or something) but one of her great advantages is her racquet preparation.  Her reaction time and readiness for the next return is amazing - when she's at the ball, her return is so sweet.  The second set was just as tight as the first.  During Serena's serve, down 2-3 and up 30-0, Deme made an incredible recovery after a slip - she twirled around and anticipated Serena's forehand return to the right.  It would have been wicked if Deme got the point, but Serena eventually tied it to 3-3.  Than at 30-30 Deme's service, it was a pretty crucial moment for Serena to win (since the two kept winning on each others serve but not their own - while Serena usually wins her serve with aces and Deme with her second serve rallies).  After trading some points and a forehand error by Serena it's deuce.  Incredibly, Deme gets a second serve ace (wtf!) and eventually wins to lead 4-3.  Another tight moment came after a challenge made by Deme on another Serena serve - which went to break point 40-30 and then deuce.  If Deme won she'd be much more likely to close the set, but instead Serena made a wonderful cross court return and then went up to the net for a volley.  Again, tied at 4-4 in the second set.  After some more great rallies, another huge point came at 5-5 Deme's serve, down 15-40.  Deme returned down the line but Serena challenged that it wasn't called out ~ hawkeye confirmed it was out so she leads 6-5.  Serena closed the second set 7-5 with great difficulty ~ two deuces, three aces, and two challenges.  

A big moment in the third set for Serena during her serve was when she was down 2-1 and deuce.  She double faulted giving break point to Deme, and then after a good rallie Serena lost a very easy forehand down the middle.  Deme served next and lead 3-1 but the game was far from over.  Serena broke Deme's serve and won the next two to tie it 3-3.  Eventually it came down to a 5-4 lead for Deme, and Serena had to win on her service game or else she'd lose!  She made the mistake of going up to the net and volleying it to hard, giving Deme the next possible win of 40-30.  Again, Serena goes up to the net but makes it for a very clean volley followed by two amazing serves for advantage, deuce, advantage and then 5-5 tie.  Deme had a fairly easy time on her serve since Serena made three low returns that didn't go over the net.  6-5 Deme leads - no tiebreaks in the final sets so either of them have to win two matches more than their opponent.  Serena tied it up and went on the offensive during Deme's serve with an incredible baseline return.  Serena serves during her 7-6 lead.  An early double fault build the anticipation, but a nice volley and smash seals a rally to 30-15.  After trading some points it's 40-30 and Deme sends one down the line but lands out closing the match! 

Final score 6(4)-7(7), 7-5, 8-6.  Simply incredible.  Congrats to the both of them!  Looking forward to an outstanding final between the two Williams sisters (since Venus mopped the floors against Dinara 6-1, 6-0). 
🐇 bouncy  |  ♫ Smashing Pumpkins - Rock On
Link

Miscellaneous...

Jul. 1st, 2009   9:43 PM
