The sound of firecrackers reminded me that today is Canada Day (it's been slipping in and out of my mind for most of the day, I blame the fact that I've been having a boring week since it's a study break).  So, Happy Canada Day and hope all my fellow Canadians are making the most out of their day off!

It's unfortunate a lot of events have been canceled due to the garbage strike.  I remember for the last couple of years my friends and I would go watch fireworks and relax at a pub at Mel Lastman Square since it's pretty close to where I live.  It feels a bit awkward this year to have a holiday in the middle of the week since it must be dreadful to have to go back to work for two more days ~ not like it matters to me though.  This year I didn't make much of an effort to make plans with people.  I passed on a bbq invite from an ABDL friend, so I won't whine about being bored =X

===================================================================================

><* Damn I want to travel.  Everytime I finish an episode of departures. I feel the urge to just do something adventurous.  Tonights episode was Thailand ~ Justin got in the ring with a muay thai fighter and lasted three rounds xD impressive!  The one thing I really disliked was when they went north to the refugee camps of the Burmese long neck tribes.  I'm sure they meant no disrespect, but I really don't like seeing people displayed as exoticisms...human museums...'us' and 'them'...it's just the way anthropology has molded me =\  I find going to a country with uber low currency conversion rates really appealing o__O  I want to feel like a million bucks when I step off a plane LOL!

So yeah, Thailand might be a place I'll consider going when I'm done school.  Nebulus64 suggested I check out Contiki since he's traveled with them in Australia and Europe.  It looks really interesting since only 18-35 year olds are allowed, some of the tours hit up on a bunch of places, there's a couple of extreme excursions that look fun, and there's a ton of boozing!  I've been looking into other peoples blogs about their Contiki experience and they all seem to be very positive.  The only thing I'm a bit iffy about is the price - some of the appealing packages look a bit to expensive for how much I want to spend.  Oh well, I haven't fully looked at all the packages.  Just something to consider for now.

Another thing I'm considering is this promotion my parents obtained for an 8 Day tour of the 2010 Shanghai World Expo Pre-Show.  The tour goes to Shanghai, Hang Zhou, Shaoxing, Su Zhou, Nanjing, and Wu Xi (I have no real idea where these places are, but the descriptions make them seem fun).  The package includes hotels and most meals and because of the promo it's absolutely free for two people - the only thing not included is airfare.  The tour company also has six additional extension tours that run between three to six days, but I'm unsure how much they cost.  I wouldn't mind doing the free promo package and either arrive early or stay after - because honestly if you're going to go anywhere in Asia eight days isn't going to cut it.  To make it work my while and go half way around the world,  I'd want to stay at least two and a half to three weeks - four weeks would be most ideal ^^.  Anyways, again, just another something to consider.  I wouldn't mind asking Stephanie to partake since she's shared some interested in traveling with me :D

===================================================================================

Congrats to my old osteology TA...whose name I can't remember >>'' It's on the tip of my tongue!  I know it starts with a D...and it has an A and an N in it o__O  Anyways, she got five of her papers published in a couple of journals.  That's pretty damn good since most people who are working towards their PhD don't get anything published at all D:  (usually because of their supervisors).  I ran into her during my Monday lecture - she sat through it hoping to talk with the prof afterwards since she wanted to TA for the course in the fall semester.  It was really nice chatting with her despite the fact I didn't get the 92% she kept pushing me to work for (=.= I got over 85% so I'm happy enough).  She's heading home to Serbia next month - hope she has a safe trip ^^.

===================================================================================

I updated my Aby and DiaperSpace profile as well as put up some pictures.  You know what happened after that?  A horde of random people just PM'd and friend requested me @_@.  That almost always seems to be the case and it gets a bit annoying sometimes.  I don't mean to single anyone out, but most of the PM's are really cliche and uninteresting.  Also, I don't find random friend requests cool ~ especially when I refuse and message back saying I'd much rather get to know them first...and they don't reply back =\  So it ain't like I'm not trying or being rude or anything.  Another thing, I've repeatedly mentioned that I'm not much of a talker so I do try to work on improving that every so often ~ especially when I get a PM that seems like it'll go no where.  Like, I'll reply back saying "I'll asks a question and answer it, then you answer that question, come up with your own question and answer it, and we'll trade back and forth."  Is the wording to confusing because some people don't seem to get it o__O  Anyways, I'm all for meeting new people, and the few that I was able to exchange contact info from (some local ^^) seemed pretty cool.  Thanks for the effort peeps.

===================================================================================

I downloaded the first season of Storm Hawks ^^  I donno if anyone's heard or seen it, but it's a really awesome cartoon that's got a hint of lameness to it xD  It's about a squadron of young Sky Knights (defenders of the sky, all that is just, and all that other laughable cliche stuff) duking evil on flying motorcycles and funky airships.  Just forget the plot and enjoy the amazing animation (there's lasers!  that's enough of a reason to watch it! ><)  There are two seasons but I haven't been able to find the second yet.  Oh well, the first season should help make my week go by much quicker.

I also watched Fight Club (1999) and Awakenings (1990) a couple of days ago.  I don't know why but I've always felt that Fight Club was overrated, but watching it again I think otherwise.  It's a pretty damn good movie, especially when you already know the ending.  For its time, the way the movie was shot is pretty amazing and the acting is top notch.  If anyone likes Edward Norton definitely check out this movie as well as American History X.  Awakening was recommended to me by my health studies prof, it's based on Oliver Sacks' memoir.  Sacks is a neurologist who worked with a group of survivors of the 1920s encephalitis lethargica (it's a sleeping sickness) ~ he pretty much awakens a bunch of people who have their minds still living in the 1920s despite they're in the 1960s.  The movie stars Robin William and Robert DeNiro and they both do an incredible job at capturing the characters (especially DeNiro).  I definitely recommend a watching if you're into medical miracles and awe inspiring human behaviour.  
🥱 bored  |  ♫ Brother Ali - Freedom Ain't Free
Link

Wimbledon quarter-finals

Jul. 1st, 2009   11:44 AM
As much as I love grand slam tournaments, I haven't really sat down and took the time to watch a lot of great matches played at Wimbledon.  One reason is because they rarely air the matches live so I end up looking up the scores online and then feel a little let down when they finally broadcast it on tv.  One thing I really like about Wimbledon though is their website ~ it's spectacular.  The live scores is incredibly interact; it can cover the scores of twelve courts, it has match statistics, it constantly updates with zero lag, and so much more.  Also, Radio Wimbledon is invaluable if you're unable to watch the matches.  There are three stations; Radio Wimbledon which broadly covers all the matches, and then there's Centre Court and Court 1 which cover said court matches.  The commentators are top knotch (and not just because they're British and got that witty humour) beecause they're really enthusiastic, they capture the intensity of the match, and they talk as fast as the players can swing ~ simply amazing.  So right now the quarter-final matches are taking place (Murray [4] vs. Ferrero and Roddick [6] vs. Hewitt).  I watched half of the first set between Federer (2) and Karlovic (22).  The set looked finished when Federer broke Karlovic's serve at 3-1.  Would have been an interesting match to watch...had I not went back to my room and looked at the scores online >>''  Federer won 6-3, 7-5, 7-6.  Karlovic is a giant (6'10") so his serves are insane because of the better angles he gets.  He's got 160 aces in this tournament alone...that's friggin' ridiculous!  But his height also makes him a slow mover around the court despite really great reach, also if the match gets dragged on he'll lose in terms of stamina.  Oh well, Roger Federer is one step closer to winning 15 grand slam titles ~ a worlds first in men's tennis.  But first he'll have to beat Tommy Haas (24) who defeated Djokovic (4) in the quarter-finals.  I listened to the fourth set live on the radio - Haas was already two sets to one.  It was Haas's serve at 3-1, and it was a pretty crucial point for the both of them.  If Haas won the point, it'd pretty much seal his victory and if Djokovic won it'd give him more confidence to fight on.  It went to deuce and eventually Haas aced it ftw.  Than it went to 4-2...and than 5-2 after an ace and a serve and volley.  And then 5-3...and eventually Haas won on his service game with another serve and volley and an unforced error by Djokovic ~ final scores 7-5, 7-6, 4-6, 6-3.  I have a feeling Federer will beat Haas, while on the other side of the draw I want Roddick to beat Hewitt and Murray to beat Ferrero.  Then...I donno, Roddick vs. Murray would be really interesting to watch.  Everyone is hoping for a Murray vs. Federer finals ~ which there's a good possibility of happening.  I'll probably watch the semi-finals tomorrow morning since it's been a really boring week so far >>'' (Edit: Whoops, completely forgot to mention the women's side...I watched semi-finals should be insane because he top four seeds are facing off against one another!  Dinara Safina (1) vs. Venus Williams (3) and Elena Dementieva (4) vs. Serena Williams (2).  Why can't they all just be winners? =P  I really want to see a Williams sister final just like last years Wimbledon.  Those two are honestly on another level compared to all the other female players ~ they are just that amazing and I totally think they deserve the glory.  =\ I don't want Safina to win (lol).  She's a great player, but I really hate watching her since she's emotionally unbalanced.  I don't mind if Dementieva makes it to the finals since she's a fantastic player, really athletic and good amount of spunk - but I really want one of the Williams to win the title =P)
🥱 bored  |  ♫ Hollywood Undead - Young
Link

Pride Parade 09

Jun. 30th, 2009   11:28 AM
I probably should have made this entry a little earlier since the parade was on Sunday ~ oh wells.

The parade was awesome!  It was alive, colourful, diverse, free, beautiful, proud...indescribable!  Jully and I met up at Wellesley station at around 1:30pm and the crowds were really beginning to pick up.  We were lucky enough to get great spots right up along the fences!  I remember last year I was pretty far back and had to resort to standing on the bike racks; which in Toronto are circular and uber difficult to balance on comfortably >>''.  It rained for a little less than half the parade, but that didn't damper anyones spirit.  There seemed to be less nudity than the last two times I went to watch...I guess that's good if you wanted to take your kids along or something.  Anyway pictures speak louder than words so feel free to check out some of snapshots Jully and I took after the cut.  In total we took over 400 (my camera ran out of juice half way so I resorted to my Blackberry) but I've gone through and picked some of the ones that I feel best captured the moments.

*These pictures are arranged in the order they were taken


Aww, this was pretty cute ^^.  I remember last year there was a dude marching in a diaper.


Hawt!  D:


This guy costume looked really awesome when his arms were spread apart. 


One of my favourite pictures.  It's so charming and his face looks so full of joy ^^.


Pride from all around the world.  I believe this person was holding a flag of Argentina (it was blue and white).  The dude standing next to Jully and I was so awesome, he attracted a lot of people in the parade to come closer for hugs and high fives, as a result I was able to get a lot of amazing pictures!


Woohoo!  It's the mayor of Toronto, David Miller!


This guys costume had to be one of the most amazing! 


I love the colours coming from his costume ^^.  TTC represent in the back :D


Hawt! xD  Also, notice the dude to the right?  'Your best pickup line'  (lol)


Every year VIA Rail participates with this train, totally wicked.


I wish I could have gotten a shot of this from further back since he's actually off the ground.  Another really great moment of expression ^^


=P


I don't know what the pointy thing is, but meh, Asians represent ~ woohoo!


This was a really awesome float and they left a huge trail of foamy bubbles! xD


Another one of my favourite pictures (unfortunately taken with my cellphone, only 2mp).  This guy was doing somersaults, spinny ballet moves, splits, and a bunch of other cool moves. 


Mwah!


XD  I bet Jully would have wanted these angels to take her away!


This was the dude who stood next to Jully and I.  He's so fully of awesome, he certainly made my experience way more fun! ^^

After standing around for a couple of hours and screaming our lungs out, Jully and I decided to walk along Church Street.  o__O The streets were way more packed than the previous years.  Going north from Wellesley wasn't to bad, but going south it was just insane.  Again, surprisingly, there weren't many fully nude people - but I guess that's a good thing since not everyone would want someone's Johnson brushing their leg while getting through the crowds =P.  There didn't seem to be as many stores set up either, rather there were a lot of sponsor tents and food stands (which were WAY over priced, since you could walk a block to Yonge St. for much cheaper food).  Eventually we made our way to the Eaton Center and got a bite to eat.  We loafed around on the sofa for a long while ~ it was somewhat boring, but then again my legs were shot so there wasn't much else to do.  Later we got some java at Starbucks before calling it a day.  (just as an after thought, the Church Street Fetish Fair in August looks pretty cool!  I'll have to consider going :3)

It's interesting because a lot of the time when I'm with Jully I feel kinda bored, while at the same time I'm fine with it.  There's just something about those moments that make things feel 'okay', where as normally I'd probaby whine or be a bugger for excitement.  Like, I know I'm a pretty boring person to hang around with ^^;; so I pretty much dread sharing that boredom or being around other people just as boring.  Because when I'm with another boring person, or listener, I'm usually the one who has to put on the pants to get things going - and I don't necessarily like it.  =\ I guess I'd much rather be bossed around.  The best kinds of people I could ever pair up with are thrill seekers and people who could push me to do more when I least expect it.  Anyways, I feel really fortunate to have a variety of friends even if it's a small handful - it allows me to appreciate certain things.  Like everytime I learn a bit more about Jully, I end up admiring her a bit more - it's almost like a reminder not to underestimate her or take for granted the things I wouldn't normally think about.  Thanks for always keeping me thinking, even if you might not know it ^^.
😌 content  |  ♫ Ben Folds Five - Boxing
Link

WORDS Meme

Jun. 29th, 2009   11:25 PM
Reply to this entry by yelling "Words!" and I will give you five words that remind me of you.  (for the simple reason that I don't know a lot of people on LJ, I'm just going to leave this part out =\)

Then post them in your LJ and explain what they mean to you.

These are the five words I got from
dan_raccoon
 :
- Anime
- Anthropology
- Mouse
- Munches
- Openness

Wicked.  If I were in his shoes I'd probably make the same list, with the exception of 'openness' (which I find to be an interesting observation) heh.  So what do these words mean to me?  Tricky question since I could write pages on each word alone; I'll try to explore the most deep seeded but immediate thoughts that come to mind.

Anime
I love anime, despite not watching a ton of it nowadays, I was pretty hardcore in highschool.  As a kid, I grew up with my eyes glued to the television (which probably explains my horribly vision now) and anime was a healthy part of my diet, even with the very limited number of licensed series in North America.  I also grew up in Scarborough - if you're local you'd probably know there were plenty of Asians populated there in the 90s, nowadays they're moving further and further north.  So Chinese dub/sub anime series where really easy to get.  I was a Dragon Ball nut as a little kid and I still remember parts of DBZ: Return of Cooler and DBZ: Broly - Second Coming.  It is a completely different experience watching the two movies at age seven than it is the age I am now - it's just so much more freakin' awesome back then!  Another childhood favourite is Doraemon ~ time traveling, buns loving, blue robotic cat!  Great comedy with moral lessons and unbelievably imaginative.  I still remember a lot of the crazy stuff he's pulled out of his 4-dimensional pocket as well as his phobia of mice xD  In highschool my family had an unlimited bandwidth plan because we had been with our provider (Bell Sympatico) for a number of years...so I went nuts with the downloads.  I have a CD case the size of a luggage bag full of stuff I've downloaded and burnt onto DVD-R's ~ I haven't updated my list in years, but my last count was 2387.6 gigabytes of movies, music, anime, back-up files, comics, etc.  I can safely say anime takes up an easy two thirds of the things I've burnt ^^  I'll have to update and put up a list of titles someday.

Anthropology
I've been trying to wrap my head around my decisions that led me into studying anthropology for a number of years now.  When I was deciding which university/college I wanted to go with, the thought of attending a 'big name' school for something I was unsure of seemed more appealing than going to a less awesome school for something I wanted to do.  So I chose social sciences at UofT rather than nursing at York/Seneca...one of my biggest regrets in life.  Anthropology, to me, has been more of an interesting topic to learn about than something I want to pursue a career in.  I don't find doing the types of research that I've been reading about very rewarding, plain and simple.  I like some of the practical uses of applying an anthropological perspective on the most mundane things in life, but they've always been from a cultural perspective ~ such as comparative analysis without being bias, thinking with an evolutionary/historical approach, or an ecological approach, or a holistic approach.  I like that the things I've learned aren't just related, they're integrated with one another - anthropology is interdisciplinary.  In the last two years I've been gearing more towards my initial interests by switching to medical anthropology and adding health studies.  I'm really fearful of my future and everytime I'm asked what anthropology will get me into in terms of the job market, I'm almost always without an answer.  Anthropology has become a love-hate for me =\

Mouse
A mouse is the character/identity I assume ~ my fursona.  It's interesting because it has only been very recently that I've found a part of myself really enjoying being a part of the alternative lifestyle/subculture ~ furry fandom.  I've been aware of furries for, easily, five years from websites such as FTT and Karis' Playground.  And I've hung out with many babyfurs under various names since starting Second Life two and a half years ago.  I've had avatars that were bunnies (I <3 bunnies!), dragons, roos', cats, coons, wolves, and even stick figure furries =P  But, I've never felt true to myself.  Maybe it was just the sheer freedom of being and doing whatever you wanted that stopped me from settling with something that required a bit more soul searching.  Oh well, since the start of this year I've identified myself as a mouse because my life really feels like its reflective of some of their characteristics.  I like feeling 'small'.  Big ears makes for good listening when you're not exactly a chatter box.  Mice are pretty curious and I'd like to think I'm rather curious despite sometimes being unmotivated.  I'm not much of a thrill seeker by myself, but I enjoy looking into things that I normally wouldn't, and eventually broadening my interests.  I still feel very new to my fursona despite it being an increasingly important part of my life...in due time I'll flesh out my 'who I am' and the 'why I am' more thoroughly, I suppose.

Munches
What can I say that hasn't already been expressed in one way or another from previous munch entries?  Heh, I love the munches!  It's how I met Dan and many other awesome people ^^.  It's a bummer that I don't make more of an effort to get other people's contact info (or at least add them on Aby or other 'community' sites), since I end up only seeing many of the people I meet just once a month.  I like the fact that there is great diversity among the attendees - babyfurs, AB's, DL's, gays, drag queens, etc.  It really amplifies the fact that 'I am not alone.  This is not completely different.  Always keep an open mind.'  Thank you Orca for organizing the munch, and thank you to everyone who shows up - each month I look forward to going to the munch because of you guys.  (Embarrassingly enough, however, it has taken many munches for me to remember the names of certain faces {o'.'o} damn my horrible memory!)

Openness
I'm going to take a stab at this word and assume Dan is referring to this blog (since he knows me more through this blog than at the munches or the times we've hung out ^^).

I am not an open person in public.  I don't particularly like having oral conversations, but it's unavoidable.  I don't hold much confidence in my speaking, and so I get uncomfortable sometimes.  But if something genuinely interests me I won't shut my mouth (lol).  So I donno, it's reasonably balanced I guess since I take a curious interest in many different things.  On the other hand, I am an open person in cyberspace ~ if you couldn't already tell, this entry alone has things I would be hesitant to talk about in public.  I don't mind being open on the internet and I am aware that there are consequences, some that are unforeseen and some I come to expect someday.  As much as I'm interested in who reads my blog, I don't feel that concerning myself with what they think is important to what I am doing - to some degree I find that rather exciting.  I don't mean to be insensitive in any way for not filtering entries about my alternative lifestyle, I just don't believe it's reflective of what I want to make of this blog.  I want to capture my thoughts, feelings, and experiences before I forget them - by sharing all these things, they become more important for me.  Plus, blogging is just something really enjoy doing.    
🌿 calm  |  ♫ Metric - Live It Out
Link

Fun Friday! Exhausting Saturday!

Jun. 27th, 2009   6:56 PM
Me thinks it'll be another great weekend ^^.  Soaking up the sun, getting some exercise, and sharing good times with friends! 

Kicked off Friday morning with a hair cut.  I could have waited a week or two for it to grow as per usual, but with the kind of weather we're getting in Toronto I think I'm gonna prefer keeping my hair shorter more frequently.  Drove up to Stephanie's place at around 10:45am to play tennis.  Great weather; it was warm with some breeze and minimal clouds.  I regret not using sunscreen lotion cause I totally got baked ~ probably got three or four shades darker!  We played two sets on top of some practice rallies.  I lost the first set only by a margin, and then I got completely owned in the second set T_T  Oh wells, it was fun none the less =P  We finished up at around 12:20pm because the sun was to much - had it not I would have liked to continue playing.  Got back to Steph's place to change and then watched a bit of tv.  She recorded a couple episodes of departures. so we watched parts of the Chile: Ends of the Earth episodes ~ Justin dressed in a pink bunny outfit while they were on Easter Island, it was hilarious and adorable xD.  We also caught a tiny bit of round three Wimbledon.  Djokovic (4) defeated Fish (28) 6-4, 6-4, 6-4.  That didn't really come off as much of a surprise since Fish had a lot of first serve faults and nets - plus he's never gotten further than the quarter-finals of a grand slam.  (random fact: Djokovic is only 22 years old and he's already got his own chocolate bar and bottled water label, plus his family opened a restaurant using his name 'Novak' o__O) 

We left her house for lunch at around 1pm-ish; as we were going we ran into her neighbour and his friend ~ they went to the same high school as us and I knew them on a personal level.  =.=  They both looked like they lost weight and were in good shape...damn, I need to do the same!  The Indian lunch buffet didn't help my glutes much, but it was so good XP  The restaurant is called 'Jaipur Fine Indian Cuisine' and it's sorta tucked in with the strip plazas on East Beaver Creek up in Richmond Hill.  Steph and I went there last year and the food is as good as I remember it to be!  The buffet selection wasn't the most extensive (there was about a dozen and a half ~ that's including salads and desserts), but it was adequate for ten bucks.  My favourite entries were the butter chicken, lamb curry, saag paneer (cheese and spinach), jaipuri pakora (it's sorta like an Indian veggie tempura), and I forget the name but it was like an eggplant potato stew.  They had mango pudding with fruits, but it wasn't very cold so we had it with vanilla ice cream xD  And their gulab jamun is SOOOOO delish ~ it looked like Steph was gonna orgasm! (lol)  It's a golden brown round ball made from milk and semolina that's been soaked in a sugar saffron syrup - the ball tastes pretty similar to an old fashion Timbit =P

After that gut busting meal we went to watch Transformers: Revenge of the Fallen.  Originally we planned to watch the 3pm screening, but settled with the 3:30pm showing since it was getting pretty packed.  The action and CGI is insane!  In the two years since the first flick, Optimus seems to have picked up on some karate moves XD.  In two different scenes he takes on three flanky Decepticons and two major Decepticons ~ pretty epic.  Same with Bumblebee ~ he's goes up against two Decepticons and beat the shit out of them!  I also liked the fact that the humans weren't weak and useless pieces of meat.  The army dudes played a pretty significant role and some of the battle scenes really felt like watching a war movie.  I didn't like the plot and the lame attempts at humour (which very rarely worked for me).  I also didn't like how there were a ton of new robots that didn't serve much of any purpose - primarily on the Autobots side, the Decepticons just had a ton of muscle.  The movie was really long - two and a half hours - which I didn't mind so much, but fifteen to twenty minutes could have been easily cut out of the film.  Over all I'd give this movie a three out of five.  Had I not liked giant robots that blow stuff up, maybe two and a half out of five.

The movie finished by 6:15pm-ish and I drove Steph home soon after (have a safe and fulfilling three week vacation :3 bring me back a souvenir).  Bah, I went to fill up the car on my way home and the prices were a bummer ~ Thursday it was 96, Friday was 98, and Saturday is 97.  More often than not it seems like I pick the worst days to get gas.  I got home by about 7pm and skipped dinner cause I was still full from lunch.  I was pretty tired and wanted to unwind a bit so I began chatting with K-Dawg - calling him out for coffee or something.  I didn't want to take the transit all the way to where he lived just for coffee and suggested we meet somewhere in between >>'' But than he reminded me that it would mean no cuddles afterwards.  So I figure, 'okay might as well make it worth my while' and asked if he wanted to go drinking instead if I were to go down to his area.  ><** He replies back saying he went drinking at three in the afternoon for a work-related function...dammit!  On top of that, he wouldn't be able to give me a ride home since his ZipCar membership expired.  We usually cuddle for a couple of hours so that would mean I'd have to transit home and by the time I step through the door it'd be around 2am.  I got kinda cranky at that point but decided to go and take a chance with how my night would play out.

Took a quick shower and was out the door by about 8:20pm.  Got lucky with the Leslie bus (since it doesn't come often and is never on time) as well as the York Mills bus (since traffic was pretty good).  Got down to his area a little bit before 9pm and gave him a couple of friendly punches to vent my crankiness ~ I'm sure he wasn't aware of my mood, but tenderizing him helped.  We walked to a Starbucks which I thought was hella far so I took the liberty of reminding him with my childish 'Are we there yet?', 'How much further?' and 'I'm tired...' {o^.^o}  I'm sure he knows I'm only teasing =3  Who else could I unleash a bit of my AB-ness if not him?  I got a grande Green Tea Lemonade.  It was okay, not the greatest combination of flavours but rather refreshing at the same time.  I usually nibble on my straws until it's fairly flat and then prolong the beverage my slowly sipping it...apparently that bugs a particular person =P  I forget what time we left but while we were walking back to his place he kept tempting me with pizza cause it's one of my favourite foods (plus there was two Pizza Pizza that we had to walk past).  We eventually decided to split a medium pepperoni which was on promo for $4...I chipped in $1.85 o__O

We made some sorta peach punch when we got back, and ate the pizza while watching an episode of Saved by the Bell.  I'm not really a big fan of 90s comedy-dramas because they're way cheesy (I grew up on cartoons!) so that was a bit blah.  Afterwards I padded him in an Abena X-Plus, and he did the same for me but with a Bambino stuffer and my plastic pants that I brought along.  ><  He was quite worried I'd leak on his bed like the last two times I was over ~ it didn't help when I wet myself not long after...but I was confident I wouldn't leak (and I didn't even after a couple more wettings! XP).  We cuddled on his bed while watching Elf; I've only seen parts of it.  K-Dawg fell asleep half way through the movie (lol!), despite being nice and cozy I was pretty much locked in place xD  It wasn't all bad since I enjoy the feeling of contact...but jeez o__O man snores up a storm heh.  He woke up eventually so we cuddled some more...and then for a second time he fell asleep!  Boourns, that's no real fun heh.  I called it a night not long after he woke up ~ it was about 12:45am.  The train I took to get home stopped at Eglinton station and switched to southbound, so everyone had to take the buses to continue north.  Not the most pleasant ride I've ever had since it was crammed, literally, chest to chest.  Eventually got home before 2am, I should have called my bro for a ride since he had the car and got home not long after me >.o**

Last bit of fun for the night came from Vanyelashke - he messaged for some roleplay.  I was super tired, but he's been pretty stressed out lately and we haven't had much opportunity to rp in the last little while - so I didn't want to disappoint ^^.  (I'll put up with you because you're worth it cutie kit =3) 

Saturday (today) was lethargic.  Got about six hours of sleep before being awoken by my brother.  We was making a lot of ruckus packing for his camping trip.  He left not long after, so I've got the room to myself until Sunday night!  Heh, I'll probably sleep with lots of padding and in my onesie tonight =D  I didn't do much of anything in the morning - my body was pretty worn out from all the stuff I did the day before.  I typed part of this entry, read Naruto chapter 453, downloaded Alexisonfire's latest album (Old Crows/Young Cardinals) which is pretty good, lots of YouTube, blah blah blah.  Nothing exciting really happened until around 2:20pm-ish.  I arranged to play squash with Nebulus64 but he picked me up a little later than we had planned.  He needed to head up to Richmond Hill to pick up his scuba gear before the shop closes at 4pm, so I changed the booking time for the squash courts for 3pm.  Of all the years I've lived in R. Hill I've never browsed through the strip plazas along Leslie (north of Hwy 7).  I had no idea there was a scuba shop in that area; a huge one at that ~ along with the shop there are two classrooms and a swimming pool!  I donno anything about scuba diving but just skimming through some of the items it looks to be a pretty expensive activity D: 

We got our game on a little after 3pm.  I got completely clobbered in the first two matches T_T  My serves were pretty weak sauce and his low shots were really hard to return.  I managed to do a little better in the third game - it was a close one, but I eventually lost that one as well ><**  lol.  I think I'm to use to playing as a tennis player since I pulled a move sorta similar to a drop shot, but was only successful a couple of times.  Unlike tennis balls that spin and bounce with a light whack, squash balls are made of rubber so they absorb a lot of the impact causing little bounce.  So you really have to smack it.  With the drop shot, however, the racquet only taps the ball for a shallow and low return ~ so you need a precise amount of force in our swing (I'm mixing up the two sports and naming moves 'such and such', but in my head it's making sense XP).  I managed to win the next two games, so it was 3-2 with Neb in the lead.  We settled for a best of seven like the last couple of times we played, only this time I lost =P  We won the next game and bragging rights until the next time we meet up!  Good job!  (Damn you XD that one serve that bounced off the left wall for a low bounce was insane!)  So friggin' exhausting but unbelievably fun.  Thanks for the good times, and hope you have a great time at the cottage ^^

Home now and ready to pass out on the floor.  I just feel really gross and sticky so I don't want to climb in bed or on a sofa >>''  No plans for the night and I don't think I want to go out since I really need to recharge myself.  Going to watch the Pride Parade with Jully tomorrow, hopefully the rain doesn't become a kill joy ~ I don't mind getting wet and I really don't want to bother with an umbrella especially if it's going to be way crowded.  Oh well, I'll see how it plays out. 
😴 tired  |  ♫ Alexisonfire - Emerald Street
Link

Dialogic...

Jun. 26th, 2009   7:38 AM
Quite interesting.  This is sort of a follow up to a post a made several months ago.  On March 3rd I opened up my blog to a wider audience ~ people I know on a more personal level.  Really all that meant was putting up the link to my blog on my MSN header (I use two different MSN accounts, the discourse-dumpling@hotmail account is primarily for online/ABDL/Furry friends).  I have 173 contacts on my other account, but quite frankly I only chat with a dozen or so people from that list.  Almost all of the people are those I've met from school, or friends of friends, and even family (my brother whom I blocked, and two cousins).  So it was a bit of a big deal for me to share my personal life - since practically half of my entries have to do with my 'other' lifestyle - with a bunch of people who knew me.  I knew that at best only a handful of those people would read this blog let alone be interested, so it wasn't much of a nerve wrecking decision but rather something that could keep me less bored.  However those who do read I've always been curious as to what they think ~ very few have made comments or told me directly, while others have kept to themselves.  I am not going to directly ask what other people think; I would much rather they ask me when they're comfortable with it or curious enough.  I'm fine allowing my curiosity bubble for now ^^.

However, when someone I know does approach me asking about the contents of my blog I find that quite interesting.  Bunny, whom I mentioned two entries back, read my blog for the first time the other day.  She requested a name change because she felt the pseudonym I gave her sounded like a fatty name xD  I kinda like it and I don't think it's horrible or sounds like a fatty name - so until the day I decide to change it, you'll have to look past it if you get mentioned in my blog anytime in the future =P  Anyways, how does one respond to "Why are you collecting baby stuff?"  I didn't feel embarrassed or anxious or awkward in anyway.  If I did than it would have been self-prescribed and unnecessary.  There is little point in writing a blog that partially involves a paraphilia and subculture, and then lie when asked about it.  I don't mind other peoples curiosity when it pertains to certain aspects of my life because I myself am a very curious person.  I am partial to being judged though, but it's somewhat excusable since any kind of an opinion forms almost immediately.  I felt amused more than anything when answering a lot of Bunny's questions about what is an ABDL/furry and how it has become a part of my life.  To introduce something so irregular to someone is almost enlightening ~ thank you Bunny ^^.  It was certainly different, but definitely something I'd like to engage in more often between mutual friends.  In recent years one of my most favourite quotes has been 'The only normal people are the one's you don't know very well', and I feel that that is so appropriate to how I've been living. 
💤 sleepy  |  ♫ Benjamin E. Morsberger - The Miles And The Hours
Link

Cripes! >.<**

Jun. 25th, 2009   7:23 PM
I got a message on Aby this morning from JockboyBrat (I don't think I've ever met him) with the subject 'AB/DL Gear Clearout in Toronto!'  Pretty certain he just went down the search list for locals and sent his ad out since he's trying to get rid of some stuff prior to moving.  o__O I've gotta say he's got quite an extensive list of stuff.  Two things caught my attention...

Scooby Doo Package - Shortalls Included!
This package contains some really cute and practical items that will be perfect for any good little boy.

Highlight: Sweet shortalls with no print or pattern - impeccably made and durable, spacious in the rump, these fit with or without the thickest of diapers, and the plain look is perfect for those trips outdoors where you want to keep people guessing...

Also included:
- Scooby Doo t-shirt - grey with print
- Scooby Doo little boy cap
- Spongebob Squarepants pyjama set
- Clear blue, buttery soft plastic pants
- See through yellow soft plastic pants
- Overkill t-shirt (big brand in Canada for beach volleyball), Overkill spelled with baby blocks
- Traditional fold cloth diaper with pins
- Tweety bird dinner plate


AB/DL Humiliation Package w/ Cat in the Hat Shortalls!
Want to make your little boy turn beet red with embarassment? Does he love the thrill of public humiliation? This is the package for you.

Highlight 1: Cat in the Hat shortalls - thin, roomy material, plenty of space in the rump for thick diapers - cute Cat in the Hat embroidery on the bib.

Highlight 2: The noisiest plastic pants on the planet! Made with a special type of nylon, these UK-made plastic pants will make sure everyone knows your boy is wearing something naughty... Thin cloth outside cover shows bears with soccer balls on a baby blue background.

Highlight 3: Authentic swim diaper! This is actually the swim diaper from early photo fame - probably the first swim diaper photos to hit the mainstream AB/DL community. I bought it from the guy who took the photos years ago, and am VERY thankful my ex never had the balls to make me wear it to the beach.. PHEW!

Also included:
- One package of Tranquility diaper boosters - by FAR the thickest boosters on the market. SOOOO evil... >:)
- Cute little teddy bear food bowl, and little kid spoon and fork for feeding time.
- Nuk 5 (largest size) White pacifier.
- Flashing kids toothbrush (never used) and musical Barney toothpaste, so you can make sure he brushes his teeth for long enough each night.
- Mickey Mouse diaper tape.


Both packages are $45 and I only like a couple of things from each ><**  From the first package I like the shortalls, two plastic pants, and cloth diaper.  While in the second package I like the shortalls (more than the other cause of the print), really crinkly plastic pants, and the NUK 5.  Everything else doesn't really interest me =\  I exchanged a couple of e-mails with him and decided to go for the second package...but someone else beat me to it! ><***  So in case for whatever reason the first dude doesn't want to buy it anymore, I got dibs on it next ~ gave him my cell number and I'm crossing my fingers xD  Anywhoo, I highly doubt they'll come my way and the more I think about it it's mostly just a 'want' sorta thing rather than a 'need'. 
🥱 bored  |  ♫ Khoma - The Guillotine
Link

Good watching and possible plans...

Jun. 24th, 2009   10:42 PM
Blah, long day at school and quite a boring one at that.  I was really drowsy during my stats tutorial and health studies lecture.  I didn't want to spend any money today, but I ended up breaking a five dollar bill for a coffee ><**  Anyways, no sense getting cranky about it.  My weekend is right around the corner ^^  One hour of stats tomorrow and I'm done for the week.  The following week from Tuesday to Friday is a study break so woohoo! 

When I got home I watched Transformers (cause quite frankly I forgot what it's all about and I'll probably watch the sequel sometime soon) and Forgetting Sarah Marshall.  Transformers was pretty insane, but I really disliked the part where Sam is in his room looking for the glasses - to drawn out and boring.  Other than that, wicked movie; hopefully Revenge of the Fallen will be just as awesome.  Forgetting Sarah Marshall was another really great movie.  The type of humour was really down to earth (not like anything outrageous happened) and the characters were really believable (there wasn't one I disliked!).  Makes me kinda wish for a love life as interesting =\  I also watched departures. ~ the Japan: Future episode.  It made me really crave for sushi o__O  and the Ascension Island episode yesterday made me crave for crabs!  Gawd...the show is really starting to grow on me (and not just my stomach).  I love Scott Wilson cause he's cute and a total big-brother figure, and Justin Lukach cause he's also cute, child-like at times, playful with animals, and a total boozer.  This show is really making me considering going on some sort of life changing (or at least experience exploding) trip when I'm finished school.  Money isn't really much of an issue since my grandparents (father's side) are giving me five grand as a graduation present ~ they did the samething with my older brother.  I was just gonna buy a new computer or put it into savings or something...but now I wanna travel!  My bro spent some of his on a month long trip to Asia with two friends.  No real clue where I want to go yet, though, and planning for something like this should be done four months in advance .__.

Zomg!

I'm a big fan of the cartoon series and I'm a little half-hearted on this movie adaptation.  The casting looks almost as bad as Dragon Ball Evolution.  Practically all the characters in the cartoon are Asian, and yet they've got Dev Patel playing Prince Zuko...wtf!  Patel was awesome in Slumdog Millionaire but how will he do in an action movie?  >>'' Dance his way to butt kicking?  The movie doesn't come out until next year so maybe with a couple more trailers I'll be convinced this movie is something I have to watch. 

In a slightly related topic, I have an idea for a badge I might get commissioned ^^.  Toph is my favourite character in Avatar: The Last Airbender.  I really like the clothes she wear and the Earthbending moves (based on Hung Gar style Kung Fu) are awesome.  I wouldn't mind getting a commission of my fursona in a similar stance as the picture of Toph, in her clothes but with a conical straw hat on, holding a pair of chopsticks holding a dumpling in the right hand.  Lower body maybe in a horse stance with a diaper on of course!  I kinda want to accentuate my Chinese-ness since there really isn't anything about my fursona that hints to the fact that I'm Chinese >>''  Just a thought for now, but definitely something I want to get done in the future!

Looking forward to Friday ^^  Possibly some morning tennis with Stephanie, then Indian buffet, and then possibly mini golf or Transformers 2!  It's a real bummer that Bunny isn't available since she's been craving Indian food, and I haven't seen her in forever.  Would've been nice to catch up with her (she was roommates with Steph in first and second year) since she lives in a really far away hicktown.  Friday will be the last time I see Stephanie for a couple weeks - she's vacationing in and around Russia.  Saturday should be relaxing since my brother will be gone camping for the weekend so I'll have my room to myself.  Possibly get padded, dress in my new onesie and loaf around to some movies :3  Sunday is the pride parade!  I went three years ago with Bunny, and last year with two ABDL friends and their friends.  This year, if my plans fall through, I'll be going with Jully ^^  She's really into the whole guy-on-guy thing so this could be quite interesting an interesting experience xD  Possibly afterwards we can meet with K-Dawg and his friends for some drinks, but we're playing it by ear since anything can happen on the day of. 
😌 content  |  ♫ The Uglysuit - Daniel Peace
Link

(>o.o)> .......................................... [ ( $ ) ]

Jun. 23rd, 2009   11:24 AM
Holy crap >>''  Next month I'm gonna have to cut back on my spending by a lot.  My VISA statement from May 20 to June 19 came to almost $230.  About one third were essentials (gas and drug store items), the other two thirds were leisure (food, drinks, books) and obligatory (presents).  And then there's the $96 for the Metropass which I paid with my debt card, as well as several bank withdrawals for cash on hand.  I'm gonna have to live like a hermit next month or at least play outside more rather than staying in and getting chubby!  (on second thought, tennis with Stephanie last night made me feel really gross cause of the humidity and swarm of insects!  =.= better off inside, perhaps?) I got a letter from G4S - the security company I worked for - at the beginning of May stating they wanted part of my uniform back.  I do vaguely remember that the blazer was a deposit, but I'm more than certain I paid for everything else.  Apparently they've been withholding my final pay cheque and vacation pay since last September.  I'm gonna need to get on that since I have $780.61 waiting for me ><*  If my monies get to low before I'm able to find a part-time during the fall semester, I still have five grand in my Tax Free Savings Account; but I really really want to leave that untouched!  
🌀 crushed  |  ♫ Danger Doom - A.T.H.F.
Link

Happy Father's Day

Jun. 21st, 2009   11:10 PM
Today is Father's Day.  I didn't give my dad anything this year.  I asked him if he wanted anything and he said no.  I've given up on surprising him with a gift because the last couple of times it's been a pretty disappointing reaction. I am grateful for everything my dad has provided me.  I love him very much despite never telling him.  Certainly my actions in the past haven't translated those feelings.  I've done a lot of stupid shit as a little kid and he's beaten my ass more times than I care to remember for it.  He's always had a short temper, but through the years its improved (except when he clashes with the mother-in-law).  A lot of my memories that I've shared with my dad have always been more negative than positive.  I regret creating this gap in my early years, and despite being a little older now it's still very difficult connecting with him.  We have many differences.  For example, he's really intelligent and fairly handy around the house - he knows a vast amount of history, cars, war/military, and the list goes on.  Whatever I know from most of his interests is whatever he tells me about the, and that's if I even remember what he says.  He's really outspoken and always likes being right.  It infuriates me to no end whenever he gets into even the smallest of debates - I'm reserved and soft spoken.  However, the one thing that we're both greatly interested in - food and cooking - we share with a deep passion.  In those not-so-often moments we eat out together, the experience is almost always bonding. Dad, despite you'll probably never read this, I want to thank you for the love, forgiveness, wisdom, protection, and so many other things.  When I was younger I use to tell myself if I ever became a father I'd be nothing like you.  But I don't think that anymore.  If I were ever to be a father, I'd wish I could look out for my kid the same way you have with me. 
🙂 okay
Link

Epic Saturday night \o.o/

Jun. 21st, 2009   3:12 PM
Easily one of the best Saturday nights of my life!  (o__o that's high praise coming from a forgetful person too) 

Kicked it off with the monthly ABDL munch.  I got to the pub a little earlier than expected so I went to the Goodwill Community Store next door and browsed around.  I've only been in three or four thrifty-kinda stores in the past and they've always been pretty interesting to browse through.  They're a bit similar to dollar stores (which I find myself going to more and more frequently) but with a broader spectrum of junk.  At the books section there were some comic books with a bunch of B-list titles (like Darkhawk - cliche backstory and powers...hellz, he was in a team called Loners for gawd sack!).  There were a lot of plushies in the toys section; so if you're ever in a stingy mood and you've got a can of Lysol on hand, there yeah go.  The store was closing just as it was time to head for the pub.  I was third to show up, Dan_Raccoon and Orca were already there.  I'll admit I was a bit sad to see that Shiro couldn't make it because of work.  In my head I was thinking the badge I commissioned probably wasn't finished yet, but I tried not to let that show ><*  A couple more people trickled in not long after (Nebulus64, Diprguy_toronto, BaBy_SB 1, and a lady that looked vaguely familiar).  Seven is a good number I guess, not to big and not to small, just enough distance to hear what the other person is saying from one end of the table to the other.  It was a bit surprising that there weren't more people since pride week started on Friday, but oh well. 

Best part of the munch was during one of the breaks in discussion ~ Dan pulled out my badge from under the table and gave it to me!  It was the most awesome surprise ever!  On the inside I was freakin' jumping out of my socks.  XD  Words can't describe how happy I was!

I'm gonna scan this when my folks are out of the house, but here's a snapshot.  Scanned ^^.  As I've mentioned in previous posts, I always wanted my first badge to have my fursona transforming and/or age regressing.  The descending numbers on each letter of my name is a really nice touch and I'm so glad I went with hand coloured rather than digital - there's something about it that makes it stand out more, me thinks.  I love it!  Thanks a bunch Shiro! *hugs*  Lets hope this'll be the first of many badges (.__. wallet permitting of course).

The munch wrapped up before 8pm.  I had the deluxe pizza, a Rickard's Red, and a 7-Up so my bill came to twenty bucks and some change.  The lady (who's name I can't remember :<), Dan and Orca had their own things to attend to (good luck with your exam Orca!) while the rest of us headed to Kevydl's place for his birthday party!  I've only been to his place once in the past (incidentally, it was last year after my very first munch!) and from the looks of it, its been renovated quite nicely!  It kinda reminds me of a smaller Putnam Place ~ which is an area with first year townhouse-style residence buildings at UofT: Mississauga.  Kevy and his partner Danididit also have this really kick ass wooden patio on the second (or is that considered the third?) floor.  The patio is covered so you can chill outside without getting wet from the rain, and it's spacious enough to fit more than a dozen people (the benches alone could probably sit seven people).  I really like the benches and the big table it surrounds - makes me feel little since the seats are really lengthy allowing my legs dangle :3  The patio also has a clear view to their neighbours bedroom; two cute guys live there and put on a kinky show from time to time (hawt D:)

I didn't want to come empty handed so I brought a bottle of Baileys (a little something to spice up their next bottle of milk ^^) but it looked as though they were pretty well stocked o__O  Practically everyone brought a little booze, so that went a long way.  It was really incredible meeting a lot of new people and some I've only met once but forgotten for the most part.  There was at least twenty people at the party and I got to chat with most of them.  Four were from Seattle, two were from Florida, one was from Calgary, and the rest lived in and around the GTA (hehe, only two straight guys =P)( K-Dawg came to the party but not the munch...that butthead! ><).  I know I've said this a couple times in previous posts but it's so awesomely different for me  to just enter someones house, undress to just my shirt and diaper, and loaf around all night like that.  It's even more wicked when almost everyone does the same ~ it's liberating to say the least, and I love it!  What's more, it's hard not to admire really cute guys in adorable onesies and overall-shorts sucking on pacifiers {o'.'o}.  I had on my usual Attends Slip Regular over an Attends Slip and got a couple of curious awes since the Regulars are hard to get (I'm down to two Regulars!  >< omg I need to find a supplier!)  I later got changed into a Tranquility ATN with an Abena booster pad from one of the fellows from Florida.  I think he's a daddy because he was really pro and a lot of fun ^^.

I kinda regret spending money for food at the munch when I knew a head of time they were doing a bbq at the party >.o  Oh wells, I had a cheese burger and quite a lot of booze.  I'm actually really surprised at myself because I had two cups of really strong vodka punch and four or five beers.  I know I'm a lightweight and a sleepy drunk, but I didn't really feel tipsy or tired until I was about to leave.  I also got to try smoking a bong xD I did while not many people had showed up yet, just to save myself some embarrassment since I've never done it before.  I think I prefer a joint more, but that might just be because I'm inexperienced =X  One of the best parts of the night was the 'chicken feed' (no sexual innuendo there...)  It was pretty much bags of ABDL goodies from Kevy and Dan's closet that they no longer want, dumped in the center of a room, and then a free-for-all grab and keep for those who wanted stuff.  It was awesome! xD  There was plastic-covered baby diapers, boosters, changing pads, bottles, onesies, overalls, bonnets, blankies, plastic pants, and the list goes on!  I remember last years gathering at their places also had something similar, but I was way noob and shy back then ^^;;

The first thing that caught my eye while the bags were being emptied was the Winnie the Pooh blankie.  I've been looking for a big enough baby-ish blanket, but usually they're not long enough to drape over my back.  I had to have it, so that was the first thing I went for! xD  I love it!  It's fleece and it's ample big (covers about 3/4 of my mattress).  Next were the plastic pants, which I came across while digging around (there was a monster size pair that could fit two me's o__O).  I mentioned not long ago that I needed plastic pants since I kept leaking on K-Dawg's bed {o'.'o}  They fit pretty well, but I found them a tiny bit tight around the leg cuffs only because my diaper was really bulky and droopy at the time.  The didn't expect to get the blue onesie, let alone notice them since there was so much stuff, but BaBy_SB passed them to me.  I had told him earlier that I didn't own any baby clothes =P so he passed them my way; thanks a bunch!  Hehe, I wore the plastic pants and onesie while having the blankie caped over my back the rest of the night. 

The only sorta downfall for me about the night was that a lot of the conversations involved multimedia (like celebrities from movies and music I'm not really in to) and Broadway theater.  I don't mean to negatively stereotype, but it seems to me that many gay guys are really into Broadway and other types of performance arts.  Kevy and Dan's walls were covered with autographed Broadway posters ~ it was pretty wicked, in fact.  But, yea, I couldn't really relate a lot of the time so I had to through a fake laugh more so than none .__.''  Anywhoo, my night wrapped up a little after 2am.  Nebulus64 gave me a lift home which was way awesome of him since I live north and he lives west.  (Thanks man!  Try fighting the $30 parking ticket,  your court date will be backed until next year and by then the officer probably won't give a damn to show up =P)  I've gotta find a place to hide my new baby items and wash the weedy/smoky smell out of them heh. 

[Edit: Added the scan of my badge.  Taking it out of the plastic was no easy task!]
🐇 bouncy  |  ♫ Queen - I Was Born to Love You
Link

Wicked start to a relaxing weekend!

Jun. 20th, 2009   1:28 PM
At least, I survived, it's the weekend!  \o.o/

I think I did okay for my stats midterm ~ I'm confident I'll get over 75% xD (just jinxed it? o__O)  There were only 30 multiple choice questions (five choices each), and the midterm is worth 30% of my grade.  We were allowed to bring in one cheat sheet with whatever we thought would help us, but it had to be handwritten.  >>'' I wrote pretty small so I only filled 3/4 of the page.  Some people had an insane amount written (double sided, size 10 font with highlights every other line).  I finished with forty-five minutes to spare so i copied down the questions I had problems with. 

18)  A company produces packets of soap powder labeled 'Giant Size 32oz'.  The actual weight of a soap powder in such a box has a Normal distribution with a mean of 33oz and a Standard deviation of 0.7oz.  To avoid losing money, the top 5% (that means the heaviest 5%) is labeled overweight.  What proportion of boxes is underweight?
A)  0.08
B)  0.24
C)  0.50
D)  0.76
E)  0.92

19)  Using the information given in question 18, how heavy does a box have to be in order for the box to be labeled overweight?
A)  31.60oz
B)  31.85oz
C)  34.15oz
D)  34.40oz
E)  34.95oz

23)  Wood scientists are interested in replacing solid wood building material by less expensive products made from wood flakes.  The following Minitab outputs were obtained from a study of the relationship between the length (in inches) and the strength (in pounds per square inch) of beams made from wood flakes.  The scatterplot of Length versus Strength was approximately linear. 

Descriptive Statistics: Length, Strength

VariableNMeanSE MedianSt DevMinQ1MedianQ3
 Length109.5000.9573.0285.0006.7509.50012.250
 Width10291.322.571.2234.0242.8251.5 343.3


Correlations: Length, Strength
Pearson correlation of Length and Strength = -0.882
P-value = 0.001

What is the value slope of the regression line for predicting strength based on length?
A)  -21
B)  -10
C)  -1
D)  10
E)  21

24)  Using the information from question 23, what is the value of the Y-intercept of the regression line for predicting strength based on length?
A)  292
B)  23
C)  280
D)  488
E)  -280

25)  Based on the information from question 23, which are true?
A)  More than 80% of the variability in the strength measurements is explained by this linear regression of strength in length
B)  The distribution of strength measures in this sample is left skewed
C)  If the length of a beam is less than 9 inches than its predicted strength must be less than 290 pounds per square inch
D)  More than one of the above (A-C) is true
E)  None are true

 

While on the bus home I ran into an old acquaintance.  While working as a security guard at Lawrence Park Condo there was this resident in her early sixties who was covered in awesomeness.  She had a really healthy lifestyle and ate all kinds of natural stuff ~ whenever she passed the front desk after exercising she'd give me a healthy snack xD.  It was also always really interesting to hear about her experiences whenever she kept me company during my graveshift (like how she use to be a trucker or how she's been in dozens of marathons).  Quite an interesting person.  Unfortunately when I recognized her on the bus (which was quite surprising since she has a very common appearance) she didn't look so healthy.  A couple of teeth were missing and her voice was really really soft.  I didn't ask what happened to her, but I really hope she's doing okay now!  It's to bad we couldn't chat longer - she got on the bus just as my stop was near. 

Kicked off my relaxing weekend with a really great night.  Took a nap when I got home, my dad made curry chicken for dinner (mmm, curry ~ my fav), watched departures. (India: Holy episode, really incredible stuff), and had a nice long shower!  Stephanie came over at around 9:30pm, finally got to give her the grad present (a cute picture book called Yay, You! by Sandra Boynton)  I've been holding onto for the last two weeks =P  She gave me a really creepy Dora the Explorer plush, which looked more like Dora's second cousin.  Apparently she picked it up for free at a garage sale o__O guess I'll use it as a duster since its hair is very brushy!  Originally we planned on watching Year One but surprisingly there weren't any theaters near my area that were playing it (Kennedy Commons and Fairview didn't have it, but STC did).  The other place that had it was SilverCity Richmond Hill, but we'd be a bit press for time had we decided to go.  Instead we just bummed around browsing at cellphones online.  She got a 'new' cell last week from Rogers for $15 ~ it's actually a model two years old but the design is really wicked.  -.- I want the new iPhone 3G S, but I have no money and I'm stuck with Telus! XP

We left my house at around 10pm and drove around Richmond Hill's 'night life district' (Hwy 7 and 404).  It seems like everytime I go pass that area there's a new restaurant, or I just forget what's there.  There's a really big Destiny Tea House but it was packed, not an uncommon sight among Asians.  We ended up going to Wild Wings which recently opened on Elgin Mills and Leslie.  Mmmm, the taste of a Heine after a stressful week is very rewarding!  There looks to be just over a hundred different flavours at Wild Wings were as at All Star Wings & Ribs there's, give or take, a hundred and forty-ish.  The good thing, though, is that the menu at Wild Wings lists what's in each flavour unlike at All Star.  Steph got the 'Awesome Honey' (I think) and I got the 'Dirty Bird' (creole mustards & chili and lime).  Really delish!  Mine had a five flame ranking (supposedly one of their hottest) but on my scale it'd be a one out of ten =\  It was more flavourful than spicy ~ which seems to be the case, imo, for white food, where as Asian dishes that are spicy really are tastebud killers.  XD Steph thought the white dipping sauce was ranch till I proved to her it was blue cheese, which she really didn't like! 

We left Wild Wings sometime before midnight and drove over to Richmond Green ~ a gianormous community center that has just about everything!  We checked out the skate park before two pickup trucks came over and told us to leave because they were gating off the area.  I got to drive her Audi TT around the parking hot!  It was friggin' awesome! XD  Afterwards she got back behind the wheel and we took a fun cruise up north Lesile to Stouville than down Bayview.  Hehe, watch out for the deers :3  Filled up the tank with cheap gas (dropped from 101.4 to 98.3 right after midnight!) and then bummed around at Tim Hortons.  Their Long John (strawberry jelly filled, log shapped donut) and the new Stawberry Blossom donut is really tasty!  It's to bad they don't make the Key Lime donut anymore, I haven't gotten a chance to try it but it looks really good.  I'm more than certain I saw some at the Tim Horton's on campus, so hopefully I can get one before they're really all gone! 

Phew, so that was a really over indulgent night.  But really, it seems like everytime I go out with friends it's eat eat eat!  >< omg I'm getting rounder!  Gonna have to look for more cheap, fun things to do ~ more sports preferably :3  Thanks for the really chillaxing night Stephanie, we'll definitely do Indian buffet (o__O more food!), Putting Edge and Laser Quest sometime before you head off to Russia! 

Oh and the new Mars Volta album, Octahedron, is really really incredible!  Possibly their best album to date.  Check it out even if you're not a fan ^^ 
🐇 bouncy  |  ♫ The Mars Volta - Copernicus
Link

Self-induced stress...

Jun. 16th, 2009   6:54 PM
-.- *grumpy growl*  I wish this week would end sooner.  Or...I wish Saturdays switched places with Wednesdays (which would be pretty awesome actually). I had an assignment worth 30% of my grade due on Monday and I submitted something completely below my standard.  Admittedly, I procrastinated but I tend to do that anyways so that's nothing excusable.  Rather, I couldn't wrap my head around staying focused when I actually dedicated the time to getting it done.  It was as though I had ADD or something -.-*  It wasn't as though I didn't know what I wanted to write about, since I already read all the research, I just couldn't connect my thoughts to written words.  My paper was about the Social Determinants of Health framework concerning malnutritious children in India.  The associated risk factors I focused on were caregivers.  The nutritional level of the caregiver (anemia levels, since it's a good indicator of poor nutrition in lactating mothers), maternal intelligence (literacy and primary level education make a huge difference) and depression (psychological stress can affect a woman's ability to cope with motherhood, thus impairing their initiative to take care of a child).     So I ended up finishing the paper this afternoon, it came to eight pages long.  I was prepared to eat the ten percent deduction for late marks, but when I went to drop off box there was a sign that said daily submissions are stamped until 4:30pm.  It was 4:45pm and I could have sworn the cut off time was 5pm!  The friggin' sign had 5pm crossed out with 4:30pm written in sharpie.  Agh...I wanted to beat myself up.  I submitted my crap paper anyways, despite not taking advantage of the extra hours until the next cut off.  I can't convince myself to do better.  I just wanted to get it off my chest and move on.  I'm in too much of a summer mode this year, but hopefully this will be a wake-up call for me.  I'm down to my last six courses until I can graduate so I really need to be on my toes.  It's a good thing I still have time to catch up for this semester.  I'll really be bashing my head against the wall if I'm still dazing off when finals are near.  Anyways, I have a Friday midterm which I WILL certainly work my ass off to prepare for!
🌀 cranky  |  ♫ 3OH!3 - Don't Trust Me
Link

Jun. 15, 2009

Jun. 15th, 2009   8:02 PM
Happy Birthday K-Dawg! ^^ We've only known each other for a couple of months but things have progressed quite quickly ~ for me at least.  Perhaps I'm still breaking out of my shell in terms of the things I never would have imagined I'd experience, say, a year ago.  It has helped me understand myself better and I'm glad the processes have been with you.  I know I've already made a few blunders along the way but hopefully you can look past them as I'll work towards fixing them.  I'm still working on getting you to come to the munches so don't think I'll ever stop pestering you until you decide to go.  Anyways, always leave room for cake and never stop being awesome!  ~.^

Rest in peace...

Jun. 14th, 2009   4:26 PM
Bad news travels fast, unfortunately.  I received a notice in the babyfur group on Second Life from
lil_babytiger
 that one of our own has passed away.  Emberlyn Whimsy (
winged_panda
 ) from Montreal passed away on June 13th; she was 26 years old and an active member of the fandom for nearly a decade.  It's a shame I never had the pleasure of chatting with her, her profile reads like someone anyone could easily get along with (which is a bit 2D, I'll admit, to judge someone by what they've written on their bio).  My deepest condolences to her friends and family.  May she rest in peace. 
Link

I ish a few fries short of a happy meal...

Jun. 14th, 2009   2:40 AM

Pep talks and motivational speeches seem to have lost their value compared to when I was in high school =\  It's as though the things I hear and/or say are just a formality and a soft slap in the face.  It's not like there isn't any meaning attach to the context, but the whole aspect of sympathizing, relating, and being optimistic doesn't quite phase me as much as it use to.  Is that just a part of growing up or getting bored of normality or something else?  For now, and until something changes or new occurs, I'm gonna just say I'm bored. 

I spent a couple of hours with Jully tonight studying at the McD's from which I last mentioned.  I don't think I got a heck of a lot done despite reminding myself how screwed I'm going to be if I don't finish an assignment worth 30% due Monday.  The articles I read just didn't sink into my head and I couldn't narrow my ideas to fit my thesis.  Not blaming either of us in particular, but we took bi-hourly breaks (or close to an hour) which further decreased my productivity.  It wasn't all bad though, I got to know Jully as a person better ~ some moments were priceless.  We talked about school, after graduation, work, love life, aspirations, and a bunch of other depressing/stress inducing topics.  Hearing some of her stories made me recollect on my own experiences, which I kept to myself.  It seems as though many of my more interesting stories are also personal ones ~ the kind where it can only be shared with those who 'understand'.  I don't mean to undermine those who aren't in the circle, but it's readily apparent who is and isn't.  I don't know if it's a good or bad thing living a more interesting life behind closed doors considering it makes me feel and appear like a boring person on the outside.  At one point in our discussion I really wanted to ask Jully if she would go steady with me.  We've been fairly close around one another for a couple of years now and I love her as a friend for so many reasons.  But the passing moments between the opportunity were replaced with awkwardness, so I saved it for another day.  Some relationships are best left as just friendships, having come to that conclusion twice in the past.  But that isn't to say I take rejection like a kick to the crotch ~ it's just something you take a chance with and you'll never know till you do it.  In some sense the aftermath provides a fonder friendship regardless if the scale is heavier on one end.  I'm a patient person so I'm sure there will be another chance. 

💤 sleepy  |  ♫ Tommy February6 - Lonely in Gorgeous
Link

ABDL Short Story Contest 2009

Jun. 13th, 2009   11:48 AM
Wicked.  It's that time of year again at the story forum I use to regularly post in.  About two years ago when the forum was hosted on another site I use to write under the username 'toucher_terre'.  But since moving, the only real thing left behind from my old account is the banner I made for the forum (top left of the page).  Oh well.  They hold annual short and long story contests, which really doesn't reward much besides a bit of status since the community is somewhat small in comparison to other ABDL websites, but still pretty fun to partake in.  I participated in both contests back in 2007; I think I came in second for the short story contest.  If I can find the time and motivation I'd definitely like to write something for this years contest!  In one of my old posts I've jotted down a couple of story ideas so now's a good time for me to use them :3

Here are the rules, which are posted in a members only section:

1. Members only may submit stories, and the story must be your own work.
2. Members may submit up to two stories.
3. The story must be new.
4. The length must be less than 15 pages or 8500 words, single spaced (including lines between paragraphs) in 12 point font.
5. Only submit complete stories.
6. All stories must be submitted by midnight, California time, on July 31, 2009.
7. Stories will be submitted by PMing them to one of the forum admins (here, here, or here) who will then post the story without the author's name attached.

VOTING:

1. DO NOT vote until after we institute a voting thread, which will be after the submission deadline.
2. Authors may not vote for their own stories.
3. You will have up to 5 points with which to vote. You may vote for five stories, one point to each one, or give one story three points and another 2, however you wish, as long as they add up to no more than five points. You may only vote whole points (not anything like 2.5 points or something like that.)
4. This is not so much a rule as a suggestion, since we can't tell the honesty of people who vote. It has been noticed in the past that some people will not read the stories of anyone other than one or two authors, and then they will just vote for those stories, never having even tested the others. This seems a bit unfair. We would like to encourage members to give all the stories a try before voting.
5. As an optional side contest, you may PM the admins (links above) with your guesses as to who wrote each of the stories. The winner will be announced along with the winners of the main contest.
🐇 bouncy  |  ♫ Alien Ant Farm - S.S. Recognize
Link

my bad...

Jun. 13th, 2009   1:02 AM
Blah.  Thursday...what a crummy way that night ended.  I went to K-Dawg's place after school that day for dinner, cake, and cuddles - its been a month since the last time I went over (o__o)  His birthday is next week so I got him a Webkinz Spotted Turtle plushie, red wine (or adult grape juice :3 it was in a juice carton!  so win!), and numbered candles.  I think I'll include the candles for everyones bday gift just to remind them how old they are ~ plus, the dollar store sells them two for a buck!  I also bought a really really wicked cake which was $4 off!  It's got custard sandwiched between two layers of sponge cake, topped with blue berries, strawberries, kiwis, and whip cream.

We went down to No Frills to pick up some groceries and food for his four legged cuddle slut =P  When we got back we cooked some cheap tortellini with some sorta mystery meat filling ~ washed it down with some wine.  The best part was the cake (duh), the custard was thick, but not nauseating if you ate to much.  It kinda reminded me of cakes from Chinese bakeries because they use a really light mousse between layers of sponge cake; unlike most Western cakes where it's loaded with icing. 

Loafing on his bed after eating, we did the usual cuddling while watching whatever flick he had.  He padded me in an Attends (I donno which kind, it didn't have any waist guards or numbers/sizing on the front) and I padded him in a Tranquility ATN with a bit more powder than necessary :3  We played the movies Get Smart and Dr. Horrible, but neither of us were really paying much attention.  I missed having a warm body to snuggle with, so I found it more comforting than fun -  where as the other times it was either more fun or a balance between the two.  All my stress goes away for that brief moment of time and there really isn't any sort of expectations I need to always keep in mind...it's just pleasure without the effort and that's something hard to replicate.  It sorta sucked when I sprung a leak and made a small wet spot on his blanket o.o''  A combination of: too much to drink, plus not-so-super-absorbent-Attends, plus my pecker pointing upwards equaled K-Dawg triple diapering me in two additional Molicare.  I was a bit embarrassed by the whole ordeal but I liked it as well ~ the thicker the better, I say!  It was a bit of a bummer that I couldn't lay on my side since it was so thick, so the rest of the time I was either on my back or chest.  It was kinda cute when he fell asleep with an arm around me, I didn't really have to do much to wake him though.  I told him I thought leaked again after a while, despite having a towel under me and pointing my pecker downwards o__O  It was coming out the sides probably because there weren't any cuts made on the Attends before putting on the two Molicare ~ thus, it was only partially my fault!  But yea...I did quite a bit of damage (luckily it didn't go through to his mattress) and felt way worse than the first time.  I can't apologize enough for my leaky tendencies T_T  I'll have to seriously consider getting plastic pants.  We wrapped up the night with a bit more cake (cheered me up a little :3) and a nice hug.  Took the TTC and was home before 11:15pm.  Hopefully it won't be another month...or two...before I see him again =\ 

😴 tired  |  ♫ Lil' Wayne - Tha Mobb
