Miscellaneous...

Jun. 10th, 2009   10:04 PM
Aww, Better Days has concluded.  If you donno what it is, it's an incredible webcomic that I've been following for several months now.  If you're into slice-of-life and drama types,  I highly encourage this series.  I favourite chapters have to be 7, 10, 14, 16, 19, and 22.  I <3 Tommy, he's so adorable!  (see this, this and this!) 

Luckily Jay Naylor has started another series, Original Life, that's sort of a continuation of Better Days.  It mainly follows adult Fisk Black and his family ~ he's got three kids now o__O.  Three pages have been posted so far and they're looking great!  Feel free to hit up on his LJ for updates and what not ( ). 

===================================================================================

I found an old snapshot from a game I use to play in elementary school.  The game is called Infantry and it was pretty kick ass at the time.  I don't remember if it was in beta testing or f2p, but eventually it became p2p and none of my friends ever played it again - myself included.  Then a couple of us began playing SubSpace, which I thought was okay aside from the really sensative flight controls.  Sometime between the two games Diablo II came out and everyone played it...except me >>''  Back then I didn't have my own computer and the family one didn't have the specs to run the game T_T


I ish Heavyarms.  Mark is Lavadude.  And Sherman is Sherman =\.  This was when the server was having issues because normally it's impossible for a Combat Engineer to set up so many turrets.  My favourite classes were Heavy Weapons and Infiltrator! 

===================================================================================

I thought my HLTB01 (Health, Aging, and the Life Cylce) midterm was pretty straightforward.  I was confident with answering atleast 70% of the questions, but I know I got a really really really simple question wrong because I second guessed myself -.-**  I meet up with two guys from the class a couple hours earlier to study...that was no real help.  But honestly, I didn't expect there to be any discussion aside from things unrelated to the course material ^^;;

I've got an assignment to stress over this weekend that's due Monday 5pm.  And then there's the stats midterm on Friday which I really have to ace.  A couple of my friends who've taken the course say the midterm is easy and the class average is really high, but the final exam is really difficult.  So I need to set myself at a comfortable grade before watching it dip =.=**

===================================================================================

I've been recieving quite a few instant messages from people I haven't talked to in weeks/months (and from one person, at least four years).  it's kinda weird.  I know I don't make much of an effort to keep in contact with a lot of people, but it's fair to say that those people don't keep in contact with me =P  Half the time I don't really have anything interesting to say to them and if I did bother to send them a 'hey, how's it going?', I know the conversation with end after half a dozen replies.  To some extent I feel pretty crummy about being this way.  Like, I've known Wingzor and Tinkabel for more than half a year through Perfect World, and we've shared amazing gameplay and deep conversations...but as soon as i left the game it's like their existence was no longer important to me.  I no longer think about how they're really doing, or care about how they're living their lives.  Moving on shouldn't mean closing curtains on certain friendships for me...but often the case it seems to be that way.  Highschool was sorta the same way - I only really keep in contact with a small handful of friends.  This behaviour seems to be the same way with my personal life ~ I make very little effort to keep in contact with ABDL or furry friends.  They're just there and eventually they'll move on if they haven't already =\  I donno what I'm getting at really...it makes no sense to complain about it since I'm not doing anything to prevent it from happening.  Rather, I'm just watching it happen and...accepting it?  How sad. 

😶 blank  |  ♫ Alexisonfire - Rough Hands

Queenie commencement present

Jun. 10th, 2009   10:10 AM

Yay, You!  Moving out, moving up, moving on
By Sandra Boynton

Dr. Seuss (Oh, the Places You’ll Go!), a plushie, or a picture frame all seemed a little cliché.  This book is way cute so hopefully it was a good choice!  Grats on surviving four years of university and countless stressful moments.  Whatever the future may hold, put it in good hands ~ your own.  Do what you want and experience as much as you can.  I hope life takes you to the corners of your smiles every step of the way!  Dream big, but relish small pleasures. 

~ your friend,
Eric Tam
June 09

Jun. 9, 2009

Jun. 9th, 2009   10:30 PM
Happy birthday mom!  You've lived through half a century and it amazes me.  You've experienced so much but you've only shared so little of it with me ~ I wish you'd tell me more about your life growing up like dad does.  What sort of music were you into?  Any interesting love stories?  What did you do for fun when you weren't working in the family business?  What do you miss the most?  

It's silly that I don't just go up to you and ask these things.  It's even sillier that I don't tell you how many things I regret doing when I was younger, or how much I love you.  Would you feel embarrassed if I were overly effusive in front of you?  But admittedly I'd be more embarrassed.  I feel like one of these pathetic statistics.  I don't believe that actions speak louder than words when it comes to love ~ especially when you tell someone from the bottom of your heart.  Despite all this you'll most likely never read this because I keep this blog private around my family.  For what it's worth, I just wanted to convey how I felt into words.  I love you mom and I wish your years ahead are lived with prosperity and good health. 

Link

Babyfur and Cubphilia...

Jun. 8th, 2009   9:00 PM
(This is a kinda/sorta continuation from an earlier post I made on communities and fandom)

There was a posted made by
swift_fox
  on the babyfur community here in LJ about a babyfur movement on Fur Affinity (which is the equivalent of deviantART ~ one of the largest online communities that focus on art, music, literature, etc).  Very interesting stuff. 

So basically, the art on FA is categorized into a bunch of fetishes/specialties in both a tame and mature section (a very limiting categorization imo, but having an all including/extensive list wouldn't be practical).  One of the categories is 'Baby fur' ~ which also has a tame and mature section.  Erotic cub art makes up the majority of the mature section which has led to a separation of what  the babyfur 'community' means to its supporters.  To some extent, furries dislike babyfurs because lines are blurred between pedophilia (cubphilia) and kinky babyfurs.  An extreme example would be alan_panda_bear.  I really like this reply made by   in the LJ babyfur community discussion, so I'm gonna quote it...

"Child porn involves the physical abuse and probable lifetime mental damage to a human being. The suffering that goes on to make such videos or photos is sickening to even think of, but that suffering is a fact, and it goes on long after the pornography has been made. Child porn is not a victimless crime, its victims are very real, so no, it's not just bits in a computer or lines on paper. A real life human being child had to be molested and abused to make it exist, and it's a crucial factor.

But erotic cub art is a pure fantasy, it's an idea drawn on a page or a screen, coming straight out of someone's head, from pure thought. Nobody got hurt to make it exist, nobody is hurt by it existing, it is a fantasy. It may sicken you to see it, it may injure your morals, but it is a fantasy and all fantasy has the right to exist because it doesn't involve any harm to a real person. There's plenty fantasy out there which sickens me, but I know it's not real and so I exercise my free will not to look at it. I'd defend to the death anyone's right to make it, because ideas and fantasies should always be free."

Combine this with the different perspectives from the FA discussion and you get something quite interesting!  There are people for and against the need for a new category (or separation and distinguishing).  Some think the new category will be a waste of time and ultimately change nothing in terms of public perspective.  Some wish for a complete banning of cub porn.  I'm looking forward to how the potential new category will play out.

That being said...so what is a babyfur to me?  Quite simply a babyfur is a furry who is an adult baby/infantilist.  I've always held the perception that a babyfur is not sexual ~ babies are cute and affectionate dammit!  The aspect of love and nurture are very important, as well as play, wonder, and innocence.  However, asking what is a babyfur community, to me, is a bit more inclusive.  A babyfur community, imo, also consists of diaperfurs (furry diaper lovers), lilfurs/kidfurs (furries ranging from the age of toddlers to teens), sissyfurs (male furs forced to dress and act as a young female), and caretakers (yes, they make up are part of the community).  I believe babyfurs and cubphilia/erotic cub art aren't on the same page.  Cubphilia and erotic cub art are almost always sexual.  The one similarity the two categories share is ageplay ~ however, ageplay is a conduit for living out a fantasy.  You don't need to be sexually attracted to the age of your fursona (or someone else's) to make art out of it.  Some people ageplay because they just wish to relive a particular age ~ which is, again, a fantasy.  But, when there is a sexual desire people choose to interact with individuals older than them, which indicates their attraction for that age bracket, rather than their character's.  Thus the desires are not pedophilic in nature. 

Fascinating! \o.o/

(I recently joined an LJ community called Fanthropology ~ the study of fandom.  I may have to consider contributing my thoughts on this to their discussions D:)
🔍 curious  |  ♫ Finger Eleven - Above
Link

French Open fini!

Jun. 8th, 2009   1:54 PM

(I meant to type this out yesterday but got distracted and forgot all about it ^^;;)

Congrats to Svetiana Kuznetsova (7) and Roger Federer (2) for winning the French Open championship! 

I watched the women's final on Saterday and I honestly didn't know who to root for ~ Kuznetsova or Safina!  Kuzne is a bit of an underdog/underachiever - she last won a grand slam title five years ago at the US Open.  She lost twice to Justine Henin in the 2006 French Open finals and the 2007 US Open finals.  On the other hand, her opponent Dinara Safina is ranked no.1 in women's tennis, but has a lot to prove to both herself and the world of tennis.  She hasn't won any major titles yet despite defeating THREE different world no.1 in 2008 ~ she claimed the no. 1 spot in April from the amount of WTA points she's accumulated from being consecutive runner-ups.  It was a bit sad because Safina has been playing so well up until the French Open finals, where she got clobbered 6-4. 6-2.  Safina is big in size and stature, she's also a heavy hitter but not too nimble on the court.  It's interesting how the weather can have a huge impact on the game ~ the weather was humid with little wind causing the ball to bounce lower and a slipperier playing surface.  This definitely worked to Kuzne's advantage since short people can return low bounces with greater easy, not to meantion she's much more nimble.  While watching the match I thought that Kuzne could have won a lot more points by going up to the net for a volley despite her forehand being her best offense, it probably would have been more helpful since she had a lot of difficulty closing sets.  I liked the fact that she used a variety of techniques rather than staying at the baseline and returning with forehands, since Safina usually returns cross court which gets a bit boring.  Although they both have really wicked kick serves, it was painful to watch Safina double fault seven times!  What's more, on the championship point Safina double faulted!  I think it's an ugly way to lose (but a fair way to win).  All in all, the game was okay, nothing spectacular really happened.  It was difficult to watch Safina gradually break down emotionally, but that's definitely one aspect of the game that's sometimes overlooked.  Hope to see more good stuff  from Kuznetsova since her confidence is probably beaming (it definitely did after beating Serena Williams (2) in the quarter finals).  

I watched the men's finals on Sunday and it was pretty good!  Federer was clearly the favourite, on paper he's beaten Soderling (23) nine times, but it's kinda difficult to really say since Federer also made a lot of comebacks in the matches proceeding the finals (like, on the brink of losing kinda comebacks).  What's more Soderling has been playing exceptional tennis ~ hellz, he defeated Rafael Nadal (1) the four time French Open champion!  It would have been freakin' epic if he also defeated Federer...Soderling the Champion Killer?  Like I said earlier, keeping your nerves in tack is so important - this was pretty apparent in the first set.  As a first timer at a major finals match, Soderling got overwhelmed (Federer was up 3-0 in the first ten minutes) and he didn't play the same way as he did in the matches proceeding ~ his movement was sluggish and he was overall very tense.  In general, he didn't make much of an effort to go after the ball (he probably could have made most of them too since he's a tall and quick guy) throughout the match - which was pretty disappointing since it felt like he wasn't taking the finals seriously.  I really really love Federer's newest shot, a forehand dropshot.  He used to use a backhand dropshot which wasn't bad, since his backhands look really classy, but with a forehand dropshot it's far less predictable.  During the match there was moderate rain and fairly heavy wind, so it was a bit surprising neither of them called for a postponement.  Soderling gained back some confidence in the second set (his massive serves were back and he moved around a bit more), but at the tiebreak he totally got clobbered.  Federer pulled a forehand drop shot for a point, as well as aceing him FOUR times (each over 200km).  Insane.  The third set was pretty good watching as well, especially the part with the crazy dude draped in some sort of flag coming at Federer on the court (was he trying to choke him or something? o__O).  The set and game was pretty much over when Federer served at 5-4 (he rarely ever loses on his serves game).  Final scores are 6-1, 7-6, 6-4.  Congrats Soderling, you've ascended into the top 12 now, hope to see more good stuff from you!  Federer bilingualled his podium speech which I thought was really wicked!  He was really humble about his victory which is definitely why so many people love him ~ he's got so much respect for the game it's incredible.  Winning the French Open title is his 14th grand slam title, he is now tied with Pete Sampras!  I really really hope Federer wins Wimbledon for a 15th grand slam title (he lost to Nadal in the finals last year since winning the title from 2003-2007).  I wouldn't mind if he retired after Wimbledon (if he won) or at least gave other players a chance at glory =P
 

😌 content  |  ♫ The Beatles - Come Together

McStudy...

Jun. 7th, 2009   8:45 AM
I think I can feel less guilty about my self for what little studying I've done lately!  Last night I went to McDonald's with Jully and studied from 9pm till 2am!  Sounds a bit weird doesn't it ~ studying at a McD's?  It was actually a really really nice place xD  We went to the one just south of Steeles on Warden.  In the last one or two years they renovated the interior such that it's way more 'slow-food' style of sitting.  Most of the seats have good cushions; like the box tables have nice back cushions, the bar seats are cushiony, and there are several single seated sofas.  There are also two flat panel tv's (one with sports the other with channel 24), and the kids play area has video games!  The designs on the walls are pretty minimalist; long rectangles of dark red, yellow, and white (as oppose to the traditional bright Ronald McDonald colours).  The atmosphere was pretty chill because the restaurant is reasonably big and people were fairly spread out so that it wasn't really noisy.  D: And it had outlets at each box table!  Wicked!  A lot of coffee shops I've been to only have one or two outlets so if I'm unlucky with where I sit my laptop is as good as drained after two hours.  The only sucky thing I can think of about the place is the delicious smell!  Every now and then I'd feel so tempted to get something to nibble on!  ><**  Well, I got a decent amount done so I just need to find the motivation to keep it up while I'm at home o__O.  Thanks again for the ride home Jully, had I waited for the bus I probably would have gotten home way later!
💤 sleepy  |  ♫ The Beatles - Money

Wonderland...<3 Behemoth!

Jun. 6th, 2009   10:58 AM
I had an incredible time at Wonderland yesterday!  The weather was unbelievably perfect and some of the lines to the best rides went by really quickly.  Thanks Stephanie and Katie, I can't imagine having as much fun as I did without you guys ^^.  The day started with a gawd awful commute up to Richmond Hill.  The York Mills bus from Lesile to Yonge took 45 minutes because of the ridiculous traffic ~ without traffic it normally takes 10 minutes.  When i got up to Finch Statiion, I needed to take the YRT up further...but I just missed the bus so I had to sit around for half an hour (zomg, they raised their fares to $3.25, last year it was $2.75 o__O).  So the trip up to Katie's house took two hours =\, that was a real bummer.  When I arrived we left soon after, picked up Stephanie and than straight to Wonderland.  I was a bit surprised how many cars were already in the lot ~ there was also some sort of band performances going on as well.   Stephanie had discount tickets so it only costed me $33 instead of $50 ^^.  The first roller coaster we went on was Flight Deck, formerly called Top Gun until Cedar Fair bought out Paramount a few years ago.  I wasn't as nervous as I thought i'd be but it might have been because we stood in line for 50 minutes ~ I just wanted to get on with it!  The amount of fun from the ride didn't really justify the long wait.  The safety guards are cushiony but the ride banged up my head pretty good, my ears were pretty red afterwards.  Next we went on Jet Scream (the giant rocket ship ride that does a 360).  Pretty wicked ride and there wasn't any line up; Katie's quite the screamer hehe.  We ate lunch afterwards ~ $6 foot long hotdog, probably one of the cheapest things in the park that can fill you up considering everything else was way overpriced.  Didn't get much digesting done before going on Mighty Canadian Minebuster.  I remember going on this ride in years past and it hasn't changed a single bit ~ really really bumpy and uncomfortably!  The only good thing to come out of it was Stephanie and I sat at the very front cart.  Next up was Behemoth!  Canada's tallest (230ft hill before a 75 degree drop) and fastest (125km/hr) roller coaster!  This ride is freakin' AMAZING!  You're put into individual seats with only a lapbar to hold you in.  It really doesn't look like a safe design, but once you're in motion you're very much restrained in place along the waist.  The cool part is you can bend your legs in a 90 degree angle and when you're dropping from a hill you're butt will come off the seat just a little ~ the freedom and painlessness is an incredible feeling!  Definitely my most favourite ride in the park so far.  It was so much fun and the line up went so quickly (15 minutes) we went a second time right after!  Katie sat out for the second run unfortunately ><  but I guess it's good to know there was someone else more scared of roller coasters than I was ^^;;  On the second run Stephanie and I sat at the very back of the train, and the very last seats in the cart (which had a bit more leg room than the front seats).  The back is suppose to be the best seat in the house because there's greater pull and, I guess, a bit better view than in the middle but not the front.  There certainly was a difference the second time around ~ way more extreme!  After that my throat really began to hurt, and still does! xD Katie joined us for the next couple of rides: Backlot Stunt Coaster (formerly called Italian Job: Stunt Track), Vortex, and Shockwave.  Sadly, nothing really compared in terms of how much fun I had after going on Behemoth =\  Backlot was okay, the only part I really liked was when the train goes into the building where it's pitch dark ~ you have no idea what to expect (wicked).  Vortex was one of the worst rides we went on.  The 25 minutes of waiting didn't justify the very very little amusement from the short and jerky ride.  We went to line up for The Fly afterwards, but after 15 minutes the operators said there would be a delay because of a problem.  A bit bummed, but I guess I could have done without the wipelash (since the ride makes crazy turns at pretty fast speeds).  We went on Shockwave which was pretty fun, I did it without any shoes on (nice airy feeling, especially when you come off your seat =P).  If you're gonna go on this ride I recommend sitting in the seat furthest from the center point, you'll feel a hell of a lot more swing and rotation xD.  After that we all shared a funnel cake topped with icing powder, ice cream, and strawberries!  The cake is a lie D:  because it isn't even a cake o__O  Funnel cakes are the product of deep frying pancake batter dispensed through a funnel (o__O pancakes are a lie too!  T_T why must they have 'cake' in their name?)  The funnel cake wasn't bad, finishing one as oppose to sharing could probably give you a heart attack or at least shorten your life span by a little.  After eating we went on The Bat, Wild Beast, Drop Zone, Ghoster Coaster, and then Behemoth for the third time.  The Bat was a bit similar to Jet Scream such that your head gets pretty beat up.  My ears were really bright red afterwards, but it was a lot of fun!  Wild Beast is kinda similar to Minebuster ~ it's made of wood and it's a pretty uncomfortable experience.  The good thing about it was that there was no line up.  Ditto with Drop Zone, no line up!  Katie sat this one out (boo), but she recorded Stephanie and I with a camera (yay!).  I rode Drop Zone without any shoes on and it is pretty fun (the view was amazing), but it lasted way to short.  I would have been disappointed if there was a line up.  We went to line up for Scooby-Doo's Haunted Mansion (the kid in me screamed to go on it {o'.'o} and why wouldn't it? You get to shoot stuff on the ride!) but the line up went nowhere.  The only time we actually moved was when people left the line =\  So after a while we left as well and went on Ghoster Coaster - which is like a kiddy version of Wild Beast and Minebuster (also made of wood and bumpy).  Katie braved up and went on the Behemoth with us!  Again, the line up was incredibly fast (because the operators wanted to break their personal record, or something like that, I didn't catch what they were saying).  I really love the pictures they take for you while on the ride; when exiting there's a shop where you can view and purchase the prints.  Of course we didn't buy any...we just took pictures of the screens with us in them xD.  Some of our expressions were priceless (Katie looked like she had fun behind all that terror =P). After that Stephanie and I went on the Behemoth a fourth and fifth time all under thirty minutes!  Although it was still a lot of fun, I eventually got use to what to expect and sorta needed a break from it (just to make the experience better when we go on it again!).  After the fifth time we met up with Katie and we went on Sledge Hammer.  Meh, it was an okay ride - I thought it was too safe which dampened the experience.  You're so buckled down you can barely see anything to the side, and only what's in front of you...which is another person faced forward =\.  Behemoth for the sixth time was our final ride of the night!  Katie unwillingly went on it for the fourth time xD I'm proud of her!  Everyone should try the Behemoth with their legs dangling and arms flailing, it's such a rush!  The park began closing up after that so we headed home.  The stupid YRT bus took forever to arrive ~ subsequently I froze my ass off because I was wearing all short sleeves and pants.  {o'.'o} I fell asleep on the bus and missed a pretty intense fight between a couple (yikes).  After saying our goodbyes to Katie, I headed over to Stephanie's house for cup noodles and a bit of tv ~ she gave me a lift home before showering and going straight to bed.  All in all, I got a major tan (I could look Filipino by the end of August!), kinda glad I went padded (not because I'd pee myself on the rides...maybe...but for the line ups; eww, sweaty diaper), and completely beaming with joy from all the good times!  Now I just need to recover my voice and sore body so I can go out and have some more fun! 
😌 content
Link

六四事件

Jun. 4th, 2009   7:08 PM

Today (for North Americans, yesterday for Asians) is the 20th anniversary of the Tiananmen Square massacre.  The government ordered soldiers to turn their tanks and guns on their own people to put down a democracy protest held by students and workers.  It is unclear how many died and wounded there were.  It is disgusting how the Chinese government continues to cover up the incident, censor the information inside the mainland, and deject open talks on the issue. 
 


The infamous 'Tank Man' (or 'Unknown Rebel') scene starts at 2:12.  I hope you get tingles down the back of your neck and goosebumps over your arms.  

 

🌀 morose  |  ♫ Lux Aeterna by Clint Mansell

Epic tennis...and soreness...and excitement ^^

Jun. 4th, 2009   11:17 AM
Despite Wednesdays being the busiest school day of my weeks (one lecture and two tutorials from morning to afternoon), I had a great night of hardcore tennis Its been a while since I've woken up feeling really sore all over ~ it was most definitely worth it for the amount of fun I had the night before!  Last night I went up to Stephanie's neighbourhood to play tennis.  It was a bit of a bummer that we didn't get to start playing until 9:20pm-ish since all the courts were in use.  While waiting we watched a group of, I think, high schoolers playing doubles ~ they were really loud and I swear I never heard more cursing in my life!  But they were really living it up and having a fun time so I suppose it's excusable ~ no doubt I was probably similar when I was that age.  There was also a father coaching his son, they were both really good but it looked as though the kid was getting more and more bored as the night continued.  We didn't play on the court we usually do - which is the ones closest to the side - so I thought it was a bit more challenging while we were warming up.  Something about having to be more considerate, and chasing the ball at any one side can hamper the fun.  Regardless, I think we're getting way better!  We played a game under our own rules while keeping score like normal (we allow double bounce, serving anywhere behind the baseline, and a bounce outside the inner squares from a serve is valid) and I think that's when we really shined.  Stephanie was clobbering me pretty good at 5-2, but I was able to catch up.  We tied at 6-6 so we played one more game (the 'seven point tiebreak') where I lost, I think, 7-2  ><  We stopped after the one set, which surprisingly took a while, so next time I'll have to get back my revenge!  We called it a night at around 11pm-ish.  It was a bummer Katie already retired to bed when we called her - since it was her birthday and she talked a lot of big plans.  Stephanie bought her a shiny balloon so rather than waking her we tied it to the handle of her front door.  Hopefully she found it this morning - either that it blew away during the night (there goes $2.25 heh).  I'm looking forward to tomorrow ~ going to Wonderland with Stephanie and Katie!  I can't remember the last time I went to Wonderland, its been years.  When I was a kid I use to go with my family and family friends and I rollercoasters scared the bejeezus out of me.  Thank gawd for peer pressure as a teen or else I'd have missed out on a lot of fun!  Some of the coasters I remember riding (and unsure if they're still at the park) are Dragon Fyre, Mighty Canadian Minebuster, and the Bat.  Hopefully there won't be crowds of people tomorrow because line-ups are blah.  All that's left is figuring out how to get way the heck up there o__O
🌀 hungry
Link

Jun. 4, 2009

Jun. 4th, 2009   12:19 AM
Happy Birthday Stephanie!  ^^ (this is like the third time I've said this ~ b-day card, msn, and now blog o__O) You're 22 and I think it's incredible that we've known each other for 8 of those years ~ nearly a third of our lives.  I'm really glad we became friends...had you not wheeled over to where I was sitting during computer class in grade 9 (to make fun of my Magic cards) things may have turned out differently.  And I remember back in grade 11 when your MSN stopped working and I went home during lunches, we practically didn't see or talk to one another for months.  We shrug off these memories now that we're older, but I can't imagine ever repeating it...you're important to me and I hope it continues that way forever ^^  May your many years ahead be as fulfilling as you wish it to be!  *shares the bubbly* *cheers!* P.S ~ cake cake cake cake cake cake cake
🐇 bouncy  |  ♫ Kings of Leon - Use Somebody

Birthday card for Queenie

Jun. 4th, 2009   12:01 AM

To: Queenie 

Happy 22nd birthday, I hope the years keep getting better and better for you!   

As you may or may not have noticed over the years I’m not exactly the card-giving type of guy…something about getting embarrassed over people reading my messy writing…anyways, cherish this card and be all sentimental with it!  You might not get another one from me until you’re 30! 

I can’t believe we’ve known each other for 8 years now ~ that’s almost a third of our lives!  I know I’ve got the worst memory ever, but I’m really glad some of my best ones were made with you.  It’s impossible to put into words how fortunate I feel to have such a close friend like you.  Lets keep the good times coming for many years to come! 

 Your friend,
Eric Tam
June 09

Where did half the year go? o__O

Jun. 1st, 2009   9:32 PM
For the longest time I've always felt that the first half of the year goes by way to quickly and I never quite remember what I did with my time.  It's like, all the memories I've made in the first half just escape me when I try to recall them much later into the year.  I wish I weren't so forgetful ~ thankfully I have this blog to keep tab of my experiences.  And it's astonishing because looking over some of my old entries makes me feel as though they were written just the day before.  The impressions they left feel fresh and remembering without having to try is a wonderful feeling.  It's definitely not something everyone would marvel at since I don't live a life of exuberance, but it certainly feels good to do something that I find embarrassingly difficult to do at times.  So anyways, looking a head now, June is going to be a ridiculously busy month!  Currently on my calendar are three birthdays (one of which is my mom's ^^), one family dinner, father's day, HLTB01 midterm on the 10th, HLTB02 assignment due on the 15th, STAB22 midterm on the 19th, and of course, the ABDL munch!  What's more, it'll be another expensive month!  I bought a metropass for the month, which was $96.  The present I bought for my mom arrived in the mail today, splitting the cost with my brother it costed $78.  I already know what I'll be getting for the two other birthday peeps - the total cost could be somewhere along the lines of $80.  Haven't thought of anything to get for father's day.  And the munch could cost me, maybe, $25 depending on what I order and whether I liquor up ~ but like hell I'm not gonna go (lol) I'm quite excited to get my badge from Shiro if he's done with it ^^.  (T_T) Oh how I very much need a job... Anywhoo, peace out. 
😶 blank  |  ♫ Metric - Blindness
Link

Communities and Fandoms...outstanding!

Jun. 1st, 2009   8:43 AM

Fascinating (which is a step up from the usual interesting).  The recent news of a babyfur soliciting an underage teen for sex has shaken up some interesting threads online in the furry fandom.  I really wish I could have written and posted my thoughts on ‘community’ and the book Rejuvenile when I said I would…but I’m still working on it (in other words, I haven’t started it). 

I find it quite fascinating how people have been reacting within the furry fandom – particularly those defending and those against babyfurs.  Here we have a subculture that is negatively perceived by the public (thanks to the misconceptions of CSI, Vanity Fair, MTV, etc) and within it the communities that make up the fandom are arguing/defending the definition of who they are.  I thought that 2_gryphon wrote a very accurate description of what has been going on ~ the range of inclusion far exceeds the exclusion resulting in repercussions which people whine about, despite being at fault from the very beginning.  (Funny, actually, because last week Stephanie and I looked through Furverts at the bookstore.  She had no idea what furries were.  I thought the book was a waste of cardboard)  It’s interesting because I questioned in my head whether a community is static or constantly changing.  Can you contain a diverse group of people within guidelines on what is considered appropriate or not?  Certainly.  There are very obvious things people should and should not do as well as who can and cannot gain acceptance into a community – case and point, pedophiles aren’t welcome in the babyfur community despite the fact that they might already be without anyone’s knowledge.  Will any clear cut guidelines besides the common sense ones ever be in place?  Most likely not.  I think that there is far too much overlap within communities for rules to be solidified – it poses the risk of contradiction or even governing ones interests in general.  Working definitions are, I think, what sustain a community; the fact that it constantly evolves.  But, this isn’t the most ideal thing in terms of a representation for the fandom as a whole.  But strictly speaking, I think things won’t change in terms of how the community defines themselves versus how they are perceived by other communities and the general public on the fandom.  Straight up furs will always have beef with babyfurs and the public will always frown upon the furry fandom.  It’s unfortunate that alan_panda_bear is from Pennsylvania because Anthrocon, held in Pennsylvania, is in June.  Hope the babyfurs are still able to have a good time ^^;;  Also, it's a bummer that I'm unable to read all the discussion about this issue online, there are just way to many people replying to threads.  Some of the respondents have really interesting points of view - maybe I'll trade a few hours of shut eye to catch up.

🔍 curious
Link

Weekend wrap-up..."Just Do It!"

May. 31st, 2009   6:58 PM
Friday was a really chill day, spent most of it with Stephanie. She came over in the morning and we loafed around in front of the computer.  Exchanged a bunch of videos.  I gave her Dr. Horrible, Helvetica, 18 episodes of Hetalia, and Gake no Ue no Ponyo.  She gave me Panda! Go Panda!, Watchmen - Tales of the Black Freighter, Nick and Norah's Infinite Playlist, Over the Hedge, and a few more things I've already seen ~ yay, \o.o/ things to do on Saturday besides 'studying'.  We also watched a couple of YouTube videos (XD had this song stuck in my head for the most of the day!)

Later we went over to her place and caught the third round men's single match of the French Open between Nadal (1) and Hewitt.  Hewitt lost in three straight sets 6-1, 6-3, 6-1 ~ ouch.  After a bit of deciding on what to eat we settle with trying a new place called Panera Bread, the location use to be a Krispy Kreme which failed pretty quickly after like half a year.  Panera Bread is a bakery-cafe that has an overall very relaxing environment.  You order like you would at a fast food restaurant but rather than immediately getting served, they give you a sorta pager device that vibrates when your order is ready to be picked up.  The seating area is really comfortable, so it doesn't feel like you're in a fast food joint.  Aside from baked goods, they mainly serve soups, salads, and sandwiches - which come in combos.  Their combos (pick two out of three: 1/2 sandwich, salad, or soup) left me satisfyingly full and not overwhelmed or still hungry.  Definitely a place worth going back to despite costing roughly eight bucks a head (I can easily get full off $6 on campus). 

After lunch we went to Michael-Angelo's Market Place for cake ^^.  We did the same thing as last time and got some $2 slices.  We had the marble cheese cake again, and the carrot cake - they had some sorta mocha java cake that looked really good, but three slices would have been way to much.  We each got a mocha coffee and dove right in ~ do delish!  I haven't had carrot cake in a very long and theirs really nailed it - it had a bunch of different textures (from the nuts, raisins and slivers of carrot), flavours (the cinnamon definitely stood out, but wasn't over powering), and it wasn't dry!  When we finished, we went to watch Up in 3D at Silver City.  This was my first time watching a movie in 3D, and although it wasn't the type where things were thrown at you, the 3D element was very apparent and made for a way better watching!  This movie is covered in awesome sauce - I don't think there was any single point I didn't enjoy!  For most of the movie my mouth was unhinged...it was just so 'wow' and full of child-like wonder...and omg Charles Muntz (the villain) is such an asshole!  There was even a scene that made my eyes well up!  Definitely a beautiful experience...go watch it!  If the opportunity arises I definitely want to watch it again in theaters.

As we were leaving the theater I thought I saw someone I knew among the staff (the person in my April 11, 09 entry).  I called out his name, but I guess he didn't hear me =\ shame since I would have loved to catch up with him.  Went back to Stephanie's house - since there really isn't that much to do in Richmond Hill besides eat out - and played Wii.  She beat the snot out of me in Wii Tennis xD, while I had a bit more luck in the Wii Play games.  The tank game is SO addictively fun!  She drove me home at around 6:30pm, but we met up again after dinner at around 8:30pm =P.  It began to rain while I was driving up there, which was a bummer because that meant we couldn't play any tennis, but it was sorta weird because going north it was distinctly sunny from a far while to the south it was dreadfully dark.  Regardless, when I got to her house it was still drizzling (boo :<).  Oh well, we stayed in and soaked up some nostalgia.  Any one remember MD players?  Or cassettes?  Or all that corny music from the 90s?  =P Man, those were the days...for better or worse.  Aside from going through some of her obsolete technology (hehe) we browsed through some photo albums.  One should always have a friend who's hobbies include photography ~ especially a forgetful person.  Going through old high school photos of the people we knew and commenting on how much we've changed felt really right.  Like some sorta rites of passage that everyone should experience - taking that moment with someone you're tight with and just being nostalgic.  I donno if I'm just getting ahead my self, but it definitely made me appreciate and feel fortunate for having Stephanie as a friend.  Thank you for the moments, I've always loved you as a friend regardless if they're reciprocated or not :3.

At around 10:15pm-ish we called up Katie (who usually has dinner at that hour...uber late o__O) who was free to come out later ^^.  While waiting for her, we watched a couple of SNL clips ~ Taylor Swift is quite amazing {o'.'o} and Neil Patrick Harris is comedy gold.  Picked up Katie at around 10:45pm-ish and decided to get wings since Steph and I were kinda hungry - plus, the last time the three of us went for wings it was unsuccessful because the place was closing up.  We went to All Star Wings & Ribs (the one on Woodbine) which was packed because of the Lakers vs. Nuggets game 6 ~ Lakers won the Western Conference Championship!  I think we waited like half an hour before getting a table.  Talk about extensive selection!  This place has over fifty flavours for your wings!  To bad it costs a lot more to split up your order to try different flavours =\  I had a basket of Hindi Delight while Stephanie had the Midnight Express.  The Hindi Delight tasted like a stereotypical blend of Indian-ness...whatever that means o__O...it was sorta curry and sorta mango chutney.  The Midnight Express tasted like a blend up of different condiments - dijon mustard, brown sauce, bbq sauce, etc.  Overall it was pretty good, could have been better if I washed it down with some booze but I was driving.  It was nice to catch up with Katie since we don't meet up that often where as Steph and her do more often.  We all made tentative plans for the following month, but knowing our groups track record I hope we can get at least some of the things done!  We called it a night and I didn't get home till about 2:30am-ish ~ went straight to bed.  Phew, quite the day, I must say ^^.

In contrast, I did a lot of nothing on Saterday.  I watched Over the Hedge - the characters were super adorable but the plot was kinda blah.  Hammy the squirrel (voiced by Steve Carell) is my favourite!  I absolutely loved the part where he drinks the energy drink and the whole world slows down.  I also watched Nick and Norah's Infinite Playlist...it was just okay.  Maybe I just wasn't in the mood, but Michael Cera's "no experience with women" characters are getting kinda boring.  And the running gag with the chewing gum was really gross o__O.  I rewatched parts of Lost in Translation ~ one of my favourite movies...probably seen it a dozen times.  I also began reading parts of Soul Eater manga.  Having already finished the anime series, I've got to say the manga is way better.  I'm surprised that they cut out A LOT of really cool things in the anime.  Plus, the manga makes Black Star (my favourite character in the series) ten times more awesome!  For example, the third time Black Star fights with Mifune during the Arachne arc is just amazing!  They didn't even show this ability during the fight in the anime series...are they friggin' nuts?!  Another example is a fight between Death the Kid and Free against Mosquito...it wasn't even shown in the anime series!  How could they remove such awesomeness?!  Bah, if the anime followed the manga even up to chapter 60, it could easily have been 70+ episodes long - especially since the manga is ongoing right now. 

Anyways on Sunday I did some laundry, made blueberry muffins (using the new Dr. Oetker Shaker - so easy to make...plus Dr. Oetker's Ristorante pizza is one of my favs) and watched the fourth round women's single match between Sharapova and Na Li (25).  I don't follow with women's tennis as much as I do with men's, but I am familiar with some of the top seeds.  Stephanie is a really big fan of Sharapova so she's probably beaming over her victory =P  I, on the other hand, wanted Na Li to win because she's representing China xD.  But throughout the match I wasn't very confident in either of them.  I don't watch a lot of women's tennis, but it seems like they use far less technique than the men do.  I didn't see much slices or use of top spin (besides during serves).  I don't think there were any volleys, but it could just be that the two weren't serve and volley style players.  They didn't slide on the clay court surface either - which can really diminish running and aid in balance if your able to do it.  Practically the whole match was played from the baseline and they attacked more than they played defensively.  Attacking from the baseline is a bit risky, especially hitting it cross court, since it has a higher chance of going out...which was so often the case in this match.  Also, the number of double faults was ridiculous!  Sharapova had two double faults in a row...wow...fail.  In essence, it wasn't a pretty game, but it did became more interesting in the third set when it was 4-2 with Na Li in the lead.  If Na Li got the point to 5-2, she probably could have won the game, but instead Sharapova won the next four matches to close the game 6-4, 0-6, 6-4.  It's interesting because Sharapova can really deliver when she needs it the most.  She's a real fighter ('if looks could kill' as the commentators have remarked) if she puts her game face on.  One problem she's having is that every round she's played in so far has taken three sets.  It could really wear her down (since her left thigh was acting up during the match along with her recovering shoulder injury) especially entering the second week of the tournament where the other contenders tend to be more hardcore.  She really needs to play more consistently if she intends to win ~ but if she loses, it'll still be a huge milestone for her since she's been off the court for nine or ten months. 

Oh yea, Nadal (1) and Ivanovic (8) the two reigning French Open Champs were defeated today =\.  Djokovic (4) was defeated the day before.  Week two is gonna be intense for the men's side!  A lot of the top seeded players are facing off against one another.  The matches I'd be most interested in watching are: Murray (3) vs. Gonzalez (12), Del Potro (5) vs. Tsonga (9), Roddick (6) vs. Monfils (11), and Haas vs. Federer (2).  Man, Monfil is so young and he's raising so quickly.  He's French so he's got the home court advantage, plus he's incredible on clay.  Definitely someone to look out for!
✨ chipper  |  ♫ Arctic Monkeys - Space Invaders
Link

Helvetica

May. 28th, 2009   11:32 AM
I was browsing through some of  's old posts - I occasionally skim through other people's blogs that don't require friend-invite or filters - and came across his May 8th, 2009 post on the documentary film Objectified.  He mentions another documentary film by the same director called Helvetica.  They both seemed really interesting.  I downloaded the latter film and it was quite different from other documentaries I've seen.  As stupid as this will sound, I have never heard of the font called helvetica until just now - also, I didn't look it up until after watching the film >>''.  The level of depth and feel some of the interviewees went into describing the font was really astonishing.  I could never have imagined people to be so enthusiastic about typology, not to mention how significant the font is in graphic design and modernism.  It was really great how they interviewed people for and against the use of the font - I've seen a couple of pharma documentaries that are really biased and distasteful.  Definitely a real eye opener if you're going to watch this film - you'll notice how a text looks in its context, like it's size, shape, space, curvature, angle of cut-off, etc...at least that was the case for me ^^;;  I really like some of the soundtrack as well (artists such  Four Tet, The Album Leaf, Kim Hiorthøy, Caribou, Battles, Sam Prekop of The Sea and Cake, and El Ten Eleven) so I'll probably try and look into downloading some of their albums.  My favourite part of the film is the interview with Erik Spiekermann (starting at 35:20) - he's really jokes xD. 

I couldn't find any torrents for Objectified, so hopefully something will come up for it in a few weeks - despite Cargo giving it a 6 out of 10.

🌿 calm  |  ♫ Caribou - Sandy
Link

Mousing around

May. 27th, 2009   10:38 PM



Quite interesting.  I was hopping around WikiFur the other day and came across a Furry Survey by Klisoura.  The creator is a furry anthropologist, sociologist, and writer.  I remember in an entry a while back describing my interest in doing research amongst subcultures - particularly the ABDL community and the furry fandom.  I am not surprised that someone else has had a paw already in the research (since the survey has been going for several years now), but it's a bummer I just discover it now.  The survey is structured along the lines of a census, and the results show some interesting demographics, self identity, sexual activity, and so forth.  Last years results can be found here.  None of the results really differed from my own perceptions prior to viewing them with the exception of 'Roleplay with other furries', where 1722 out of 5680 respondents stated  they never do it.  I was a bit surprised that the majority, 30.8% of respondents, don't roleplay with other furries - perhaps it meant in a real life setting?  If so than that wouldn't be surprising.  But in an online setting, from my point of view, roleplaying is very prevalent - Second Life, Furcadia, MUD and MUCK clients, etc.  Anyways, I still think it would be even more interesting if further research methods were employed - maybe someday I'll get something going.

In comic book news - after an extended period of no talking about it - I want Mouse Guard: Winter 1152 issue #6, which came out this month.  It is the final issue of the Winter series, which is coming along really great!  Unfortunately, no one has scanned it and made a torrent of it yet (does that come off as a little unsupportive to the creator? ^^;;  sorry Mr. Peterson...you're still awesome).  Maybe something will come up in a few weeks, I've got my fingers crossed.  I also downloaded the Mouse Guard Role-Playing Game...even though I've never played a text based RPG (like D&D) before.  I figure it'll provide an interesting read or something, the PDF file is 320 pages with some really incredible illustrations ^^.  Anyone into RPG's?  I'm willing to try my hand at it if enough locals are game!  Oh yeah, I sent David Peterson an email asking if he'd be making an appearance at Fan Expo Canada (his website has an appearances schedule at cons and there's a big gap between August and October) since he lives in Michigan, which isn't really that far from Toronto...I don't think o__O''. 

I also downloaded four issues of The Mice Templar.  I'll probably start it on the weekend.  I'm not overly enthusiastic since new issues come out rather infrequently, but atleast the art looks really nice...no clue what the plot is about though ^^;;
🔍 curious  |  ♫ The Last Emperor - Secret Wars Pt.1
Link

Settling down after much fun...

May. 26th, 2009   7:40 PM
*Sigh of relief*  This is what I needed to keep me from bouncing off the walls with boredom ~ a lot of tennis, mellowing out with Stephanie, and some rain clouds for the following days ahead.  Last night was really great, Stephanie and I played tennis from 8:15pm-ish till 10:30pm.  We were progressively getting better throughout the night (with a pretty weak start, imo), but as it got darker more bugs came out and it became chillier (also because we were getting less active, lowering our body temperatures).  I love how we kept saying we'd call it a night after one more rally...for like thirty minutes!  As exhausting as it was, I had a lot of fun!  And then I woke up the next morning with a sore right shoulder hehe.  She came down to my area at around 9:15am-ish ~ we had considered playing more tennis but the weather was fairly cold and windy.  So instead we went to Shops at Don Mills (see May 22 entry) and walked around.  She was quite impressed with the 'urban village' layout - noting how nice it'd be to live near it since it has almost all your basic needs (upscale stores, Metro, Shoppers Drug Mart, Post Office...LCBO =P).  Interestingly, it also has Canada's first Anthropologie (women's high-end casual clothing store), Ontario's only McNally Robinson Booksellers, and the first North American Salomon store.  Don't I feel lucky to be living near these stores...blah =.=**  We used my two-for-one coupon at Teopia (which turns out you can grab as many as you want from a table) - my tea tasted like watered down Fruitopia o__O.  Bummed around at the book store.  Then played a bit of sketchy tennis ^^;;  The public courts in my area (North York) are really different from the ones up in her area (Richmond Hill).  The courts were right next to Lesile St. (high traffic, noisy, distracting), several restaurants were around the area (delicious smelling air promotes hunger...not good for people trying to burn calories T_T), and it was hella windy!  Oddly enough when we moved to the furthest court from the main road after the previous players left, the smell and noise was less sever ~ only three courts away can make such a difference D:  We didn't play much after moving because the wind was still pretty bad (plus I was freezing my butt off).  We had lunch at Tako Sushi, which I'm sure I've written about before.  I had the curry (...how predictable haha) and it was delish!  Lots of variety and food for only $10 a head, I ain't complaining ^^.  We bummed around at my house for a bit; watched Chowder and YouTube, then had some of my dad's spiffy coffee :3  It sucks that caffeinated drinks don't do anything but make me pee lots =\  I nearly fell asleep in the middle of lecture today -.-**  Anyways, thanks Stephanie for the good times, the mochi (><** you're trying to make me a fatty!), and the sweet lift to campus in your oh so sexy TT *drool*
💤 sleepy  |  ♫ Nine Inch Nails - Right Where it Belongs
Link

Anime North 2009...didn't empty my wallet! D:

May. 24th, 2009   11:28 PM

\o.o/ Woohoo!  Went to Anime North yesterday with

dan_raccoon
 and it was really fun!

 

I got up at around 6am =.=*  definitely the first time of this year I’ve gotten up so early on a weekend.  Left the house by 7am and didn’t get to the Toronto Congress Centre until a little over 8:30am.  There was a dude cosplaying as Reno (from FFVII) and another as Kenpachi (from Bleach) on the bus – I liked how both had the hairstyles really authentic looking.  Although I arrived early there was already a ton of people lined up for registration, which didn’t begin until 9am.  The line up extended around the corner of the building which was something like 200 ft from the entrance >.o  Luckily I brought my DS to pass the time (which hasn’t had any usage in months).  Dan arrived a little while later and we made small talk about the furry cons he’s been to and the cosplayers we saw while waiting.  When registration began the line moved pretty quickly – it only took about twenty minutes for us to get our badges.   

We didn’t do much of anything from 9-10am since a majority of the events on the schedule begin at 10am.  We wandered to the Doubletree Hotel to scope out the layout and to figure out where the panel rooms were.  It seemed as though every time we walked by a particular hall leading to the panels area there was a line up of some sort (concert and masquerade wristbands, autographs, etc o__O).  Also, throughout the day I’ve noticed that there were A LOT of cosplayers – saying something like 35-40% of the people there had some sort of a costume on wouldn’t be an exaggeration.  Most of the costumes were really great, but not surprisingly a lot were from the uber popular series such as Bleach, Naruto, Soul Eater (so many Maka’s…gawd she was an annoying character), D. Grey-man, Inuyasha, Pokemon, Sailor Moon, Death Note, One Piece, and the list goes on.  There were also a couple of video game characters from Final Fantasy, Zelda, Mario, Team Fortress 2, Metal Gear Solid, Kingdom Hearts, Street Fighter, etc.  (T_T) There weren’t many Axis Powers Hetalia cosplayers! (I saw two America, two Canada, one Germany and one Russia)  I was a bit saddened by that.  There was a Hetalia panel on Friday and a Hetalia picnic on Sunday, so I guess I was just unlucky. 


(left) America and Germany   (right) Russia, my fav!

There was a Code Geass panel at 10am that I didn’t mind going or not (cause I’ll be honest, the plot was pretty blah…sorry Jully =P), so instead Dan and I went back to the TCC to check out the Dealer’s Room.  There was a bit of a line up since it didn’t open until 10am, but once it did people were quick to getting in.  The layout was exactly the same as I remember it from two years ago; although there seemed to be a bit more toys/plushies than there were of mangas (everything was tax free and mangas were US cover price).  We got a good look at most of the tables.  Dan had an interest in some furry ears two different tables were selling.  I didn’t see anything that really shouted ‘Buy me!’ but there were a few things I would love to have just because they were cute and/or awesome.  Like, there were these rubber pins (that sorta looked like really thick fridge magnets at first ^^;;) with classic anime expressions on them (like a text bubble with ‘…’ or a sweat drop or the cross section of veins that pop out when you get pissed).  The idea was that you pin them on your hat =P and considering I’m a hat kinda guy I thought they were awesome!  But, not to sound stingy or anything, they cost $6 and I wouldn’t have worn them anywhere else besides the con.  I did, however, try looking for something Totoro related for Stephanie (because that’s her favourite Miyazaki film).  Various tables carried Totoro plushies ranging form $15-35 – as much as you are worth that much (and more) Stephanie I wanted to keep my spending at a minimum ~ sorry ^^;; 

At 11am Dan and I went to the ‘When Bad Things Happen to Good Anime’ panel.  There was a good turnout and people were really active in the discussion (I sat and listened...me no talkie =X).  The main issues brought up were censorship (ie. Jap tv has 5 min of commercials while US tv has 7 min – thus, out take of content and/or intros/outro)(ie. censoring blood for sweat, or foul language, or sexually explicit material), horrible dubbing (ie. high pitched Jap voice acting getting replaced by low pitched US voice acting – think Naruto…the American voice actor makes him sound like he has lung cancer >>’’), and animes turn live-action-movies (*shudders* Goku is WHITE?!...and omg, Zuko from Avatar is being played by Dev Patel?! A brown dude who stared in Slumdog Millionaire is going to play an Asian character o__O wtf).  I think the panel left me shaking my head at the American anime industry; what’s sad is that there will always be a consumer T_T  Hooray for us! 

We had lunch at Harvey’s afterwards – the line up was pretty scary.  I sure hope they aren’t participating in today’s Free Hamburger Day event or else they’ll lose a lot of money =S.  We ate outside under a tree which was a really nice change, sorta reminded me of highschool.  There seemed to always been a ton of people outside the TCC just hanging around and not moving between the hotels.  Also, the Caramelldansen seemed to be the popular dance of choice – which I don't think hasn’t gotten old for a while now.  Every time I listen to the song while watching something cute dance to it I can’t help but smile ^^ (omfg Hetalia and Caramelldensen!  Ten minutes of awesomeness!) 

After lunch it was back to the Dealer’s Room and arts area (comic market, model room, crafters corner, and gallery).  Yay!  Free tissue packs >>’’ lol.  Shonen Jump and Shojo Beat doing a bit of tissue-pack marketing (which is quite common in Japan), I guess I have something to give Jully now =P  I have never seen her without a tissue pack in her purse.  There were a lot of artists selling prints, but the market for commissions didn’t appear to be so hot – which is in stark contrast to furry cons as Dan pointed out.  Man, furry artists at cons must make a fortune *cough**cough**Marci**cough*  I find it a bit surprising since there are so many artists to compete with.  But hey, if you’ve got the talent and a fan base than kudos to you.  A lot of the prints were really beautiful and the prices ranged from $10-20 – some would have made really wicked desktop wallpapers!  After browsing around the Dealer’s Room some more Dan bought a Whitney Wolf plushie (from Animal Crossing), I was still empty handed (i.i) 

At 3pm we went to the ‘Art of VG Cats’ panel – Scott Ramsoomair (the creator) led the discussion which I thought was really surprising since the con program made no mention that he’d be there =D.  I think I stopped checking for updates on the webcomic like half a year ago since its been increasingly infrequent.  It was a bit of a bummer because his tablet PC ran out of batteries right before starting.  Someone in the audience lent him their own tablet, but of course, it was different and some of the things he might have wanted to show us he couldn’t.  So most of the hour was just Q&A, which wasn’t bad had I not been really really drowsy.  All that walking and standing around made me so tired my head was bobbling {o’.’o} so embarrassing!  But for the most part – of what I was able to pay attention to – the discussion was really funny.  Afterwards, Dan went to shake Ramsoomair’s hand ^^ I took a picture of him, but it came out a bit blurry.   


Scott Ramsoomair - Canadian cartoonist and the creator of VG Cat!

From 4-7pm we hung out at the Renaissance Hotel, where all the gaming action (and yaoi panels) were taking place.  The fresh air from the walk between hotels helped wake me up ^^;  There were a bunch of rooms holding casual and tournament card games and tabletop wargames.  There was, I think, two tables doing booster drafts of the latest Magic the Gathering expansion pack.  There was another room with a bunch of consoles hooked up to televisions and projectors – Rock Band on big screen is really cool D:  They even had SNK vs. Capcom: SVC Chaos!  I remember playing that came on arcade back in highschool at the local poolhall!  Kim was my favourite character to use because he had a really broken stomp move that made getting up nearly impossible.  Aside from watching, we tried our hand at Time Crisis 3, Vampire Night, Super Smash bros. Melee, and like 30 seconds of some Japanese fighting game.  >>’’ Some of the people there are really hardcore, even bringing their own arcade joystick game controller (which are enormous…it’d need its own suitcase!).  I got a free AC3 drawstring bag while playing some games, but it’s made of really thin polyester and it’s bright red – which isn’t really my colour.  At 6pm we went to watch Anime Jeopardy.  The set up was pretty cool, but some of the questions could have been worded better.  Two of the contestants were really good (otakus maybe?) – they could get the names of characters from a bunch of different series.  I honestly can’t remember a majority of the character names from the series I’ve watched, not because I’m really forgetful, but because they’re really hard to remember (trying to pronounce Japanese names is hard enough sometimes >>’’).  I was only able to answer maybe a quarter of the questions (the other ¾ of the time I could only recognize the series or not at all).  All in all it was really fun and entertaining! 

We left Anime Jeopardy a little early so that we could make it to some panels at 7pm over at Doubletree Hotel.  Dan went to the Digimon panel while I went to the ‘Sexuality in Anime & Manga’ panel.  I didn’t stay for very long because the panellists kept referencing mangas I’ve never read or even heard about.  I had a feeling a majority of the people at the panel were into female-looking-male characters, don’t ask me why, my senses just tingled.  However, while there I did learn that the Japanese culture is more comfortable with sexuality because they never experienced any repression like the Victorian era in the West – but then a repercussion of that is fanservice in anime/manga, which is quite unnecessary and pointless.  I headed to the Dealer’s Room to pass the time, plus I still didn’t want to leave empty handed! ><**  I find that I’m really picky when it comes to shopping for stuff (which is probably why I buy stuff in malls alone) so it was kinda nice to do it at my own pace.  I rummaged through a bunch of plushies from a table Dan and I had already been to and found something I really liked which I hadn’t seen at other tables.  It’s a 8” plush of Yuki in a rat cosplay outfit (he’s from Fruit Basket).  I thought it was really adorable since my fursona is a mouse!  (mouse…rat…they’re both rodents, no need to get technical for this one >>’’)  I’m usually a bit partial when it comes to buying plushies for myself – I’d much rather someone else get me one so that when I cuddle it I know it’ll have more meaning ^^;;  (yesh, I am the cheesy sentimental type…)  I also bought a Super Mario bookmark and pin from a table in the artist area.  The bookmark has a goomba, sleeping boo, and a piranha plant (on the back it has the four different types of mushrooms), and the pin has a picture of a cheep-cheep (the fish) with a blue background.  Jully calls me fishy because I told her fish have a memory span of three seconds…which is a myth! >< (Mythbusters did a test on goldfish =P) 


I got back to the Doubletree and waited a little bit for the panels to end at 8pm.  I was a bit surprised that there were a lot of people coming out of the Digimon panel o_o I’ve only seen the first couple of seasons bits of Digimon Tamers (oh how I miss the days of Digimon Tamagotchi back in elementary school!)  Dan and I went over to the Tim Hortons across the street for a light dinner – their chicken wraps taste a lot better than the ones at McDonald’s =P  We call it a night after eating.  Dan invited me over to his place to hang out, tempting as it was I decided to hold the offer until next time – I was way tired and I had to walk home in the cool weather wearing only a t-shirt and shorts.  He drove me to Kipling station at around 9:10pm-ish, and I got home in one piece at around 10:30pm-ish.  Thanks for sharing the awesome day, Dan!  It couldn’t have been what it was without you ^^.  Heh, furry con in the future, I will definitely consider.  But till then, interested in FAN Expo in August?  =P 

😊 happy  |  ♫ CAT-A-TAC - All I Knew
Link

Shops at Don Mills......for the bored, rich, and aging.

May. 22nd, 2009   6:18 PM


I have been incredible bored from staying at home lately – aside from going to school.  I was going to call out Jully for coffee last night since I had the car and nothing better to do…but I can’t remember why I didn’t call her >>’’.  And for the last two days I’ve been trying to get a hold of Stephanie to play tennis.  But her response time and mode of reply is irking me the wrong way =\.   

So in the afternoon today I got tired of watching Natsume (finished the first season and now on the second) and bored of waiting for Gurren Lagann to finish downloading, I went out for a walk.  I walked to the newly redeveloped Don Mills Centre - renamed Shops at Don Mills - which took about 15 minutes.  It’s really beautiful unlike the outdated mall before it.  It is now an ‘urban village’ (fancy way of saying enclosed plaza?) with a bunch of stores to attract rich, middle-age hipsters and cougars (no kidding because that’s primarily what I saw while there).  Some stores worth pointing out are Coach, Calvin Klein Underware, Aritzia, Guess, Salomon, Tommy Hillfiger, and a couple of artsy painting/photography shops.  There is a two storey McNally Robinson Booksellers, but it’s kind of a weird place.  It’s like a roomier upscale Chapters that’s way over staff.  In almost ever corner of the store there’s a help desk with a worker behind it >>’’.  Not only that, but if you’re browsing through a book it feels like someone is always watching you.  The graphic novels and mangas are all sealed in plastic! (wtf!)  And the magazine section is on cramp and tiny and the help desk is uncomfortably close to each row.  Oh yea, and chairs are a rarity.  I don’t think they encourage lounging around or even standing for an extended period -.-**  And lastly, on the second floor there’s a place called Prairie Ink Restaurant and Bakery.  Quite an odd place for a bistro-thingie, especially since it’s walled off and you can only enter/wait for a table by its entrance.   

I ended up buying a hardcover copy of ‘No Reservation: Around the world on an empty stomach’ by Anthony Bourdain for ten bucks.  It was in the bargain section with only a small tear on the top part of the jacket (when it came out less than two years ago it sold for $34.95).  I really enjoy watching the tv series No Reservation hosted by Bourdain on the Travel Channel (through YouTube >>’’ I don’t watch tv shows through a tv lol).  He is so down to earth and the places he visits and the local foods being eaten.  The book is mostly full of pictures with and page long descriptions – categorized by country and city.  Ten bucks for 286 pages of beautiful pictures and interesting story-telling, I’m not complaining! ^^ 

Afterwards I walked across an empty area (vaguely reminds me of Dundas Square because it had tables/chairs, and a small water works fountain) to a tea shop called Teaopia.  The interior is really nice – it’s got an Asian-Western fusion going on – and it’s split into two parts, a store area and an ordering area.  The store area carries a ton of different types of tea – some that you can sample – while the ordering area has a menu that isn’t quite as extensive.  The way they make their tea is quite interesting since it’s the reverse of, say, Starbucks.  They mix up your order into an odd looking cup and put it on the counter (with a kitchen timer) to let it steep.  When the time is done (usually two minutes) they filter your drink into regular paper or plastic cups.  I think it’s quite a horrible way to do business >>’’ I can’t imagine going there on the weekend, line-ups must be a nightmare!  I ordered a Rooibos Chocolate Mint Tea Latte on ice, but the dude screwed up and made it hot.  So he redid it and I had to wait double time ><*.  He gave me a two for one coupon afterwards, but I really doubt I’ll be going back because the shopping centre really isn’t a place I’d hang out in to begin with.  My drink wasn’t bad, it tasted like a liquid chocolate mint milkshake, so I don’t really know where the tea part comes it…but I’m sure I got the goodness of tea somehow o__O  It cost $5 for a 16oz cup with no other sizes available to choose from.   

I spent most of the afternoon sitting in the empty area reading and drinking tea.  It was mildly relaxing since it was breeze and kinda chilly on my legs.  There was a street meat smell lingering in the air and quite a lot of white noise.  Ah well, at least it was better than staying home >>’’

🥱 bored  |  ♫ Mother Mother - Try to Change

May. 22nd, 2009   10:51 AM
Happy Birthday
orca_and_mommy
 =3
