Anime feel good...hooray! \o.o/

May. 21st, 2009   11:01 PM

I find it interesting how my opinions change quite quickly.  I began writing an entry last night which I eventually didn't post for a couple of reasons, but rereading it (because it auto-saved) I feel completely different about what I wrote. 

So anyways, yesterday I had a great workout at the gym, but when I got home my tummy began aching (might have been something I ate when I got home).  Thus, I didn't write up my thoughts on Rejuvenile.  From the looks of it I probably won't get to it till Sunday, and that's if my mood is geared towards writing.  Instead, when I got home I watched three episodes of Zoku Natsume Yūjin-Chō - which was a mistake on my behalf.  I had no idea that I was actually watching season two of the series until I asked Jully about it; she told me that Zoku meant 'continue'.  Interestingly, from the three episodes I watched I was still able to follow with what was going on primarily because things were recapped.  So, today I began watching Natsume Yūjin-Chō (the first season), and I am quite impressed.  The entry that I didn't post was regarding my opinions of the first three episodes from season two.  I compared the series to Mushishi because they touch upon a number of similar topics.  Initially I felt that Mushishi was a way better series, but now that I've watched eight episodes of the first season I feel that the two series are quite evenly matched.  I very much agree with Jully  that Mushishi is more creative while Natsume Yūjin-Chō is more heart warming (sorry for prejudging last night ^^;;).  Mushishi is more creative because the 'spirits' are of a new creation and thus need to be defined - as the series unfolds these spirits possess many different attributes.  Mushishi also has an interesting mystery feel to it because the main character, Ginko, is so unparallel.  On the other hand, Natsume Yūjin-Chō is more heart warming because the 'spirits' draw on traditional Japanese culture/tradition.  The main character, Natusme, is very likeable and you can't help but feel good for watching him do a good deed to restless spirits.  So far the episodes I've really enjoyed are 2, 5, 6, 7, and 8.  And seeing as I've only watched eight of the thirteen from season one, this series is definitely something to watch if interested!  (random: i've noticed that there is a lot of running in the series.  nearly every episode Natsume is running or getting chased >>'')

I just hope this isn't the wine talking =S.  I had a couple glasses of white wine, my windows are open allowing the hot air in, and I've been watching the series while loafing in bed.  I feel good so I might be reviewing too good xD  But I'm sure that's not the case...

Anyways, I'm looking forward to Anime North ^^.  I'll be meeting Dan Raccoon at around 9am by the registration area.  Jeez, I hope I'll be able to wake on time...it's going to take me at least an hour and a half to get to the convention by TTC ><**  Like, I don't mind not sleeping throughout the night and getting hopped up on caffeine - I just have epic difficulty waking up so early!  I'm still trying to figure out how Go Transit works because it might shorten the commute by a bit.  I remember my experience at Anime North 2007 - cosplayers stand out so proudly on the bus and subway that it's very admirable.  I remember a hell of a lot of walking ~ wear comfortable shoes!  I went to the Paradise Kiss and Avatar the last Airbender panel, they were quite interesting especially having an opportunity to voice some of my opinions while hearing others.  I remember a lot of bundle deals on manga (something like four volumes for $20 or something) - I bought nine mangas.  But, it's important to browse and come back to the dealers in case you find a better deal!  The artist stands are quite amazing and cluttered with talent, but kinda pricey - I bought Jully an L cut-out from the Death Note series which I think costed $15-20.  This year I want to check out the Code Geass, Art of VG Cats, Sexuality in Anime/Manga, and When bad things Happen panel (time permitting).  I also want to check out the Avatar Special Presentation video - but it's an hour long so it'll depend what they're showing.  I'm going to try to limit my spending to a minimum...I already owe VISA $450 this month (T_T). 
😊 happy
Link

Finished!

May. 20th, 2009   6:00 PM
Wicked!  I have finished watching season one of Chowder.  I like the first couple of episodes the most mainly because I wasn't exposed to the greater extent of Chowder's stupidity.  But in general I really love the series; it's creative, imaginative, and hilarious.  Mung Daal is my favourite character ^^.  I'll have to get around to downloading the second season - I think the most current episode is number eight, so I can catch up and play the waiting game >>'' I have also finished Rejuvenile by Christopher Noxon.  Looking back at my previous entries, cause I wouldn't have remembered anyways, I bought the book on May 6th.  Not bad considering I've only really sat down to read it once and on the other occasions it wasduring the commute to and from school (plus, quite frankly, I'm a slow reader).  I'll try to write my thoughts on it sometime tonight if I'm not to tired, but in general, the book is fairly interesting.  I'm a bit boarderline in terms of how informative it was since I felt the really stretched on a handful of ideas and chalked it up with a lot of examples.  It would have been nicer if the book was condensed into a 50 page essay or something.  I'll get into the gist of it when the time comes ^^. I think I'll look into watching and finishing Natsume Yūjin-Chō (recommended by Jully) before the week is over since my mood has been shifting from comic books to anime/cartoons more and more lately (-.-* oh Sandman, when will I get around to finishing you...).  Plus, there's a discussion panel for it on Saturday at 4pm at Anime North - so if I do go there'll be more to look forward to ^^. 
🌀 lazy  |  ♫ Rammstein - Sehnsucht
Link

Long weekend...

May. 18th, 2009   1:46 AM
In honour of Queen Victoria's birthday here in Canada it is a long weekend.  It's a bit of a bummer because I enjoy going to my Monday lecture - HLTB02, 'Issues of Child Health and Development'.  Oh well.  So far I've been making the most out of my weekend. 

On Friday I got a haircut which was long overdue.  I like wearing a hat when in public but I don't think it looks very good when my hair is long - something about my sideburns and the hair above the back of my neck.  The weird thing about my hair is that it grows outwards like a fro (o__O)  I've never left it long enough to see whether it'll eventually drape or continue poofing, but that's something I'd rather not wait to find out.  When my hair is long I usually gel it, but I'm pretty picky with how I like it done so it's sometimes annoying to spend the time in getting it right =\  The look my friends give me is kinda funny sometimes when they recognize me without my cap on since it's such a rare occurance =P  Anywhoo, I arranged to meet up with Stephanie in the afternoon to play some tennis.  The drive on the 404 going north was a lot busier than usual, but oddly I didn't see a single cop car which usually is the case during long weekends.  We started playing at around 1:45pm-ish and for the most part I think we were pretty inconsistent.  At some points we had really good rallies while at others it was kinda blah.  There was a man at the furthest court from us with a tennis ball machine - it was really cool watching him play like a pro.  The weather was really nice as well.  It was very warm with minimal sun because of the overcast and a really refreshing breeze that didn't hamper the game much. 

We finished at around 3:30pm-ish and went for an afternoon snack at a Chinese restaurant.  At a lot of noodle/congee Chinese restaurants they have discounted menus in the afternoon and after midnight.  I ordered a curry rice and beef dish that came with iced lemon tea - the serving was enormous and I ended up taking home the leftovers which became my lunch for the following day.  It's incredible how two can dine for under $12 including leftovers from two dishes.  It's hard to compete with Chinese food, I say.  I have only Stephanie to thank for this because she is the queen of cheap eats! xD  She was telling me about all these different places that we're definitely going to have to try out ^^ - I'm looking forward to a dessert/drink I use to order frequently as a kid; red bean ice topped with ice cream!  After eating we browsed around a dollar shop next door - it's impressive how many things they carry that you wouldn't immediately think of getting at a dollar store, as oppose to places you'd normally go to for so-and-so item.  We called it a day and I filled my car with $20 worth of gas which was only half a tank, (o__O** so expensive these days) and drove her and myself home.  I didn't do much of anything at night.  I finished watching Skip Beat! and my opinions remain the same from the last time I wrote about it...=.= the cliches are so hard to bare with.  I'll have to debate whether to watch the second season if there is one, since the last episode hinted towards one and the manga is ongoing.  I also played a bit of Second Life and read issues #61 and #62 of Sandman, but both got kinda boring very quickly. 

On Saturday Nebulus64 and I arranged to play some squash and Magic the Gathering before heading out to the ABDL Munch.  I booked the courts for 2pm so he swung by my place at around 1:40pm-ish.  We played some really fun games!  I was down 3-1 but managed to stretch it to game seven for the win (I had nothing to do with his ankle problem mid game...really!  Hope you recover quickly ~.^)  Oh yeah!  Bragging rights remain ^^, better luck next time!  We're both getting a lot better but I need to work on my serves since they sometimes bounce short from the line by a few inches - I think I need to bounce off the wall from a higher angle, although that runs the risk of putting the ball out on the side walls.  I also need to work on returning low shots, which he seems to favour, but that requires a quicker reaction and really reaching for the ball in a tricky way.  Ah well, I definitely shaved a few calories from that fun.  We got back to my place at around 4pm-ish and geeked it out with Magic cards - oh the nostalgia.  I use to go crazy for the game back in highschool, a bit frightening when I think about how much money I spent on it.  I totally got my ass handed to me by his G/W clerics/beast deck and his Red burn deck.  I think it was like 5-1 or something worse!  "Damn that Worship card, I'll just have to mill you for two!"

We wrapped up the game at around 5:40pm-ish, by then my parents and grandparents had come home.  I packed an Abena X-Plus that I've been saving to use for special occasions and a Tranquility ATN just in case I needed something to change into.  The drive to the pub was surprisingly quick, it took 15 minutes; going by TTC takes me at least 40 minutes o__O.  Unsurprisingly however the turn out for this months munch was extremely low - by far the lowest for the ones I've been to - most likely because of the nice weather and long weekend.  It was just Dan Raccoon, Shiro, Kevydl, Diprguy_toronto, Nebulus64, and myself.  I'm not much of a 'big group discussion' kind of person for a number of reasons so I thought it was a really nice change of pace - though it's not to say that I don't enjoy being around big groups!  I've been trying to get a hold of Shiro for a few weeks prior to the munch because I wanted to know if he was open to commission a badge for me (T_T he never replied).  Ah well, I knew I would have eventually meet up with him.  I've known for a while that I wanted my first commission to be of my fursona transforming and age regressing ~ so who better than Shiro since he specializes in that!  After taking down some details of what I wanted he did a sketch in less than 15 minutes o__O pretty easy money for him, no kidding.  I'm really psyched with how the badge will look after he hand colours it! 

The munch wrapped up at around 7:45pm-ish.  My bill came to $23 ~ I had the Americana pizza and two pints of Richard's White (Abenas are a gawd send for lightweights like myself).  Afterwards I think Kevydl and Diprguy went off to do something together and Nebulus went home since he has a long drive ahead of him (have fun at the cottage, hope the weather doesn't make it a bummer).  Dan, Shiro and I were deciding on what to do ~ I wasn't use to going home so early and I probably would have just invited myself over to K-Dawg's place if he was home, had there not been any other options.  They offered me to come hang out at their place which I thought was really awesome!  Oh B-town, why must you be so far away?  I felt so bad for having them drive me back to Kipling station afterwards T_T  While there we played Guitar Hero 2 on the Wii.  Though I've never played the game before, it was really fun on guitar despite sucking big time!  My eye-hand coordination is really crap ^^;;  although I wouldn't mind playing jitz to see just how bad I've really gotten.  Playing the game on drums was way harder, even when on easy mode ><*  I have this urge to dust off my Wii and buy Guitar Hero with the $75 credit I have for EB Games, but I'm a bit worried my excitement will blow over after a few weeks =P.  Afterwards we played Brawl, something I actually know how to play, though that didn't mean I was any good at it.  (>^-^)> Woohoo for Kirby! <(^-^<)  I'm pretty sure I only managed to become first place once out of the many rounds we played heh.  All in all I had a really great night, despite getting home at 1:30am uber tired and uncomfortably squishy.  I can't thank you guys enough for having me over and driving me back to the station!  Hope we can work something out to get together for Anime North next weekend Dan ^^  I think I'll look into a cheap cosplay dress-up this week!  I am also looking into the Islington Furmeet, as recommended by Shiro.  Quite honestly, it looks intimidating for a number of reasons.  But it isn't until June 6, so my hesitancy might be non-existent when the time comes ^^;;

On Sunday I didn't get much sleep because my brother was really noisy doing last minute packing.  He's going to Philadelphia with his prof for five days to attend some science convention.  Whatever, I make it a point not to get interested in whatever he does - it's bad enough we have to share rooms ><**  For most of the morning I was writing parts of this entry and watching Chowder, a Cartoon Network show.  This series is brilliant!  I downloaded the first season which is twenty episodes, the second season is currently running on its eighth episode.  The humour, delivery, and creativity is very similar to Foster's Home For Imaginary Friends - if not even better.  The presentation is really wicked - most of the series is done with animated cartoons, but some scenes are stop motion with clay figures, puppets or paper cut outs.  If you've ever watched the anime series Gankutsuou: The Count of Monte Cristo, you'll recognize the style they used for clothing immediately - they do the same for this series.  Essentially, the character and their clothes are two different visual layers.  When the character moves, the patterns on their clothes do not - so it's sorta like watching a stencil go over wrapping paper.  Also, the series is for children? o__O  I've noticed that there are some very subtle references to pop culture and 'grown-up' play ~ and I've only watched six episodes so far!  Anyways, this series looks to be golden.  If you love cartoons, comedy, cuteness, and cooking than this is a must watch! 

In the afternoon I watched the final match between Roger Federer (2) and Rafael Nadal (1) in the Madrid Open (an ATP Masters 1000 tournament).  They both played great tennis, but Federer played better for the victory - surprise surprise considering he hasn't won anything in quite a while.  When Federer serves he usually gets the point way quicker than when Nadal does it, which is good and bad.  Good for Federer, but bad for the audience...we want to watch some action man!  Bad for Nadal too since his playing kicks up a knotch in very drawn out matches because of his incredible stamina.  Besides his serves, some of Federer's shots were really brilliant as well.  He nailed a couple of forehands right down the line and on the baseline which were so sweet.  Although, I hadn't noticed this as much until the commentators mentioned about the shadow from the stand and how it can be a distraction when it's near the baseline.  This obstacle was quite apparent when Federer was against the shadow attempting to hit on the baseline.  In the second set at 2-2, 40-30 for Federer, and it was Nadal's serve, I was fairly convinced that if Federer won the match point he'd win the game.  Which was the case - he won the match, than the following service match for a 5-3 lead.  Even if Nadal caught up to 5-4 (which he did), Federer would be serving next with Nadal against the shadow- thus strongly putting the win in his favour (which was the case for a 6-4, 6-4 victory).  The match point was pretty intense (you can't count Nadal out till it's over) since it was 40-40 with two advantages for Federer.  I like that Federer had quite a bit of variation in his returns - he's very well rounded!  And it's to bad for Nadal since he had the home court advantage =P.  I'm looking forward to Roland Garros in the coming week!

To top off the day I had a really relaxing night with Jully ~ I miss seeing her since we last met up a week and a half ago =(  She wasn't having a very good week since her relatives from Hong Kong were visiting; the infinite loop of questions and criticisms would surely bring out the bitterness in most people.  I'm not to big on sporadicism but it really seems to be something I'm doing more frequently as of late.  I like making plans several days in advance so that there is some flexibility in scheduling, as well as something to look forward to - a nice way to motivate oneself.  But lately I find that I become intensely bored when I stay home so I'm always making immediate plans and calling people out.  Maybe it's because I've gotten into some sort of regularity for going out these past two months that I can't sit still long enough until the next outing.  Not really a good thing for my wallet, I'll tell you that much >>''  Anyways, we went to Boston Pizza for drinks ~ although, I was driving so I just had coffee. 
(Mmmm, bleeding cheesecake :3)

Their cheesecake is really good!  It's creamy and lite enough so that it isn't nauseating.  Delish ^^.  We chatted about the usual which I thought was kinda boring but comforting at the same time.  It's like, mellowing out every now and then is mind numbingly stupid but in a very feel-good kind of way.  We went to McDonald's for chicken snack wraps afterwards! xD  Omg grilled chicken tastes so healthy it's bad...I should have gotten the crispy chicken o__O 

I don't really know what I'll be doing today.  Although, I really need to open up my stats book and review all the material up unil now.  If I don't I'll probably get screwed when free time will be less abudent later in the semester.  Other than that and watching Chowder, I'll probably try and make some last minute plans to go watch fireworks or have coffee with someone ^^;;
🌿 calm  |  ♫ MGMT - Kids
Link

Cake and cuddles, plans for the weekend, and shopping

May. 13th, 2009   2:48 AM
Oh noes!  D:  Another night of chubbying out!  After school yesterday I went to K-Dawg's place to help tidy up for his messy cat.  Darn thing sheds with every movement and isn't very clean around his litter box >>''  Well, at least his cuteness and friendliness makes up for it.  After a bit of sweeping and vacuuming we ordered white-washed Chinese food for dinner.  While waiting for it to arrive we baked a cake ^-^  I bought a box of cake mix with sprinkles in them to make up for last weeks let down - and to make it doubly good I also bought vanilla icing with sprinkles in them!  But holy cow is that stuff ever fattening ~ 2 tbsp = 170 calories.  We baked to trays and slathered them with icing when they cooled.  It was a lot of fun and way cheaper than buying a premade cake - though it did take us about forty minutes from start to finish. 

Besides the deliciously sweet cake, the Chinese food was unsurprisingly out of my taste.  I criticized everything from the chicken balls to the crappy fortune from the fortune cookie o__O.  Oh wells, it was cheap and filling even though I told him cake alone could count as a meal :3  We watched a bunch of late 80s to early 90s kids cartoon intros off YouTube while eating.  Some of them I vaguely remember, while others were only remotely familiar - these included The Raccoons, My Pet Monster, and The Smoogies.  Afterwards we cuddled up to a few episodes of Freakazoid! while padded in Abena X-Plus.  For the most part it was really relaxing and I probably could have fallen asleep on a number of occasions had I not been so ticklish {o'.'o}.  I did some bit of damage to the diaper but not enough for it to leak ~ ><  dammit that's 0-2, I think I've met a worthy brand!  Blah, also, I was unsuccessful convincing him to go to this weeks munch :< boo.  We called it a night at around 11pm, he ZipCar'd me home in the same red Jetta as last time ^^.  

I'm looking forward to the end of this week!  It's a long weekend for one thing.  Another is that my older brother is going to the states for four days with his prof, starting Sunday, for some conference thingy.  I'm a bit bummed I had to pass on sporadic dinner plans with Stephanie and Katie today ~ Steph called me at 3pm-ish saying Katie had her car for the night (it's usually Steph and I who drives whenever we go out :P) and was thinking of doing dinner at around 7pm.  Oh well, I'm use to the last minute-ness around that particular group of friends - most of the time I jump on the bandwagon since I've got nothing better to do, unfortunately not last night >.o  On Friday I'm planning to go to High Park with Jully to see the cherry blossoms (if they're still around...the hail storm last Saturday did a number on them I'm sure).  She's really psyched about seeing them because she's really in love with anything Japanese.  The weather is suppose to be nice so I've got my fingers crossed that everything falls through okay!  On Saterday I'm going to the ABDL Munch!  But before that, Nebulus64 and I are meeting up for squash and Magic the Gathering.  Oh yea!  We're going to geek it out and stir up some nostalgia!  I haven't played MtG (a trading cards game) since highschool.  I can't wait to see how my burning bridge deck fairs >:3  I gotta remember to ask Shiro if he's open for commissions on Saturday >>'' I've been trying to get in contact with him for a few weeks now.  No clue what I'll be doing for the rest of the weekend, but maybe Chopsticks since it got postponed...then never spoken of again.  I'll have to call up the gang for that one. 

Oh yeahs, I went shopping for clothes the yesterday and today!  (This week is progressing quite expensively o__O)  I went to STC yesterday and bought four pairs of shorts.  Two of them are from Champs, made by Nike, for when I go to the gym or do sports (buy 1 get 1 half price :D).  The other two are from Bluenotes (2 for $20 :D) which are for casual wear.  It'll be a bit hard to adjust to the Bluenotes shorts since they're line/checkered patterned.  I'm more of a sold colours kind of guy, but last summer I brightened up my wardrobe with greens and browns, so this summer it'll probably be stripes/checkers :P.  I also bought a thin white zipper sweater with Canada in red on the front of it, from Old Navy.  I swear to gawd it looks like it's something from Roots which is totally win since I can't afford much from that store xD!  The only bit of letdown is that the back is really plain.  Today I ordered online some t-shirts from Black Market Vintage Clothing.  I went to the store on the weekend and everything was $10 and under - I haven't been to the warehouse section in forever since I normally just go to the upstairs store across the street.  There's a comic book store in the warehouse store o__O.  Anyways, I bought six t-shirts.  The shipping cost $6, which is a little more than going there by TTC ~ commuting and carrying shopping bags, no thanks.  The only thing I'm hoping for is that they don't suck at silk-screening since I don't want the designs to fade after a couple of washes.

Preview: 'Discourse community' and 'Roleplay'

May. 12th, 2009   10:33 AM
Interesting.  A post was made today on the Ageplay community (here on LJ) that may shed some light on something I've been thinking about for a while now.  I've wanted to write about two things for some time now - I've started both but I'm unsatisfied with how they're coming along so I'll probably redo them. 

The first is discourse community.  I'm not a big fan of the word 'community' whenever it is used in the ABDL scene because it encompasses to much diversity under a very broad definition.  I have a suspicion that some may feel the same about using the word 'community' in the furry fandom.  Furthermore, when two interests come together, new distinctions and 'communities' are created - as well as problematized.  For example a furry adult baby, or a furry with infantile behaviours, might call themselves a babyfur.  I've started writing about this but it needs more thought put into it; I think I'll write this when I've finished reading Rejuvenile - I'm on page 160 of 249. 

The second is concerning roleplay in family, sibling, and caretaker relationships.  There are very subtle difference that may not necessarily be important between online and in real life (IRL) roleplaying.  When I was in highschool I knew a group of friends that established family roleplay - referring one another as brother, sister, mother, and father depending on the roles they assigned each other.  In the years I've known Jully, we've established a mother/son roleplay - but for the most part I don't believe it's seriously been established but more of a running joke.  Online roleplay works in very much the same way - a bond between friends that develop into a family/sibling roleplay relationship.  The most obvious example I can think of is Second Life.  I've seen family's on forums as well, such as Fox Tales Times.  Jully has told me on several occasions that she has an online sister in Taiwan who she frequently exchanges gifts with.  To somewhat contrast both online and IRL relationships is this roleplaying of caretakers.  It is not uncommon in the ABDL 'community' for an adult baby to want (or have) a mommy or daddy (or a caretaker).  What I find interesting is that I rarely, if ever, see this roleplay spoken as mommy/daddy taking care of their son or daughter.  Those being taken care of aren't referred to that, but instead they are called: little man, baby girl, princess, scooter, kiddo, any other nickname or simply just their given name.  This perception might change depending on the comments for the post made on the Ageplay community.  I find these roleplay relationships very interesting because they aren't straightforward - and yet, they aren't puzzling either (perhaps I've just grown accustom to them, or they are so pervasively practiced)  I want to know more about the processes and mechanisms at work in these relationships, and I think that is why it is so difficult to write about them.  On one level these relationships are personal and discussing it to people you aren't familiar with would be intrusive. 

Anyways, just strictly speaking, my ideas are based on only my perception and understanding of what is going on.  I am aware that there are a spectrum of opinions other people might hold whether for or against my own.

🤔 contemplative  |  ♫ Portishead - Glory Box
Link

Happy Mommy's Day

May. 10th, 2009   10:21 PM

Today, in North America, it is Mother’s Day – a day to recognize and appreciate your mother for all that she has done and given to you.  When I was younger this day seemed to hold more significance such that more love was given through acts and feelings; making my mother breakfast, washing the dishes, getting her a small present with a card, spending the day at the park, holding her hand, sharing a dessert, taking pictures, and so forth.  As I’ve gotten older these activities have became less and less until recently I would just simply take her out for lunch or dinner and then allow her to have the day to herself, usually with my father.  I think about this not nearly as much as I should – but I’ve become increasingly distant with her; with my parents in general.  In all honesty I don’t remember when was the last time I told her I loved her without having a card do it for me.  I don’t remember the last time I kissed her on the cheek (something I use to do every night before bed when I was little).  I don’t remember the last time I gave her a hug where seconds felt like forever.  Instead, I remember the many sacrifices she’s made for me.  I remember the times when my teenage rebellion made her frown.  I remember the times when I’ve made her cry – one of the most miserable and shameful feelings. 

I love my mom with all my heart, but why is it so hard for me to tell her that without having some cheesy scene of 7th Heaven pop into my head?  It’s pathetic.  She deserves so much more from me that I could provide had I not grown into such a klutz :(

🙏 thankful  |  ♫ Kanye West - Hey Mama
Link

A relaxing moment is...

May. 9th, 2009   12:44 AM

.
A relaxing moment is…

enjoying yesterdays sunny afternoons outside.

sitting in the shade beneath a canopy in front of a coffee shop.

sipping a cup of Earl Grey with one sweetener.

watching the moderately high traffic of a plaza.

the feeling of a constant gentle breeze.

smelling the combined fragrances of coffee beans and fruity hair salon products.

reading forty pages of an interesting book.

watching puffy cumulus-nimbus clouds between pages.

wetting a diaper with little thought or care,

producing a brief warmness and increasing squishiness.

listening to Torley on piano, shuffled on my iPod, through white nose.

feeding small but daring birds pieces of my lemon muffin.

watching the shade shrink,

allowing the sunlight to creep up my legs

is the sunlight that warms my feet through my flip flops.

sharing the moment with a stranger,

separated by only a small round table.


🌿 calm  |  ♫ The Daniel Pemberton Tv Orchestra - My Advice pt2
Link

Some good watching...

May. 8th, 2009   10:56 PM

My router has been acting a little funky lately so I had the horrible pleasure of getting it replaced and setting it up.  It was somewhat painstaking and tedious.  Anyways, lately I've been mooching off my schools internet and an unsecure wireless from my neighbours.  I'm pretty sure I left a big dent in the bandwidth having watched a ton of videos.

Skip Beat!  (As recommended by Jully)

I haven’t really sat down and watched an anime series in a long time; although I enjoy anime (having watched and owning an extensive collection) my main interest has shifted to comic books.  However, that might balance out since this series is pretty good.  The first season is twenty-five episodes long and I’ve already watched sixteen of them – having started the season only three days ago.  I’m pretty open to most genres of anime, so this series being shojo (aimed for school-age girls) I have no problem with.  One thing I dislike about the series is the ‘thinking out loud accompanied by dramatic movements and ridiculous auras’.  It’s very cliché in these types of anime.  The main character, Mogami, goes into a long monologue while the characters around her think she’s mental – resulting in self prescribed embarrassment everytime.  Another cliché thing is this cycle of self doubt Mogami puts on herself, resulting in other characters having to lend her support or reaffirm her confidence.  One final cliché I noticed was the constant questioning by Mogami, asking “Did I do something to make ___ hate me [angry]?”  She asks that in nearly every other bad situation and it drives me crazy.  I really hate clichés so it’s a bit of a miracle I’ve actually managed to watch so many episodes. 

Aside from that, here are somethings I like about the series.  I like the evil aura that’s employed throughout the series – particularly Mogami’s.  Her malicious thoughts are represented by vile spirits that look like her and at times they have the supernatural ability to choke other characters in which she is spiting.   The background music is very good even though certain samples are recycled quite obviously.  I also like Mogami’s history – the ‘meaningless’ things she learns growing up servers a purpose in her present activities.  Certain personality traits of Mogami remind me of Jully and a girl I knew in highschool (she had dyed her hair orange and was fairly eccentric).  Mogami vaguely reminds me of Haruko Haruhara from FLCL =\.  Aside from Mogami, the only other character that’s seen some development is Ren Tsuruga.  I’m not liking all the coincidences between the two characters (another fairly typical cliché), it’s just to easy and uninteresting.  For the most part, the execution of the plot has been fun and witty, while the overall flow is superficial and full of clichés.  And yet, there’s this appeal to the series that makes it standout from other series making me enjoy it and wanting to find out what will happen next.  Perhaps it’s the bubbly drawings or the lame lovey-dovey or the urge to root for the main character knowing full well that a happy ending is ensured regardless.  It’s a mystery.   

Fanboys (2009)

Pretty funny movie.  I’d say, the first 70% of the movie was good, then the proceeding 20% wasn’t good, then the last 10% went good again.  The jokes are a bit cheesy but understandably necessary – so at times I just pretended to laugh.  But for the most part, it was genuinely funny.  The only scenes I didn’t like (that 20%) was the Las Vegas scene and the George Lucas Ranch scene.  I thought the ending was good and appropriate – as in, I felt a sense of completion and satisfaction that it ended the way it did.  There was also a bunch of cameos from Star Wars and sci-fi figureheads throughout the movie (like Carrie Fisher, Kevin Smith, Ray Park, and William Shatner).  There were also some actions I recognized but couldn’t put a name to…but I can only recall one – the guy who plays Roy (Pam’s ex-boyfriend) on The Office – although the black policeman also looked familiar.  Anyways, overall the movie was funny but stupid; only watch it if you have time to kill and no other movies that are available seem appealing. 

Push (2009)

I only watched the first fifty or so minutes of it because the link to the second part on Ninjavideo.net was broken.  I like superheroes and superpowers, so I had the false belief that I should enjoy this movie.  But seeing as I haven’t finished the movie I’ll just say it’s okay for now.  I don’t like the acting – no real surprise since most of the actors are gibberish.  I like the way the movie is filmed.  The angles and panning are sharp and clean (I have no idea what the proper terminology is so whatever), and the colours seem very vivid (or maybe only because most of the filming is done in Hong Kong).  The plot is stupid, but that’s no real shocker.  In general, I’m not going to put any effort into finding a way to watch the second half.  Not that it’ll be worth it or like I’m missing anything – I just don’t have any interest aside from the display of superpowers.   

Star Trek (2009)

Pretty incredible.  No, not the movie.  The fact that someone already has it online for downloading when it just came out yesterday!  I began downloading it, but accidentally closed my web browser – thus I only have the first thirty minutes.  I’m redownloading it now, but it’s only at 35% with over three hours remaining.  Anyways, the first thirty minutes is incredible!  There is no doubt in my mind that I won’t go and watch it in theatres – a movie this epic deservers a second watching of greater quality.  I can’t really go into anything since I’ve only watched a quarter of the flick, so blah.


🥱 bored  |  ♫ Fooldoor - Solitary Walk
Link

Muurr :3

May. 6th, 2009   1:56 AM

Yesterday turned out better than I expected since plans to go see a movie with Monica got cancelled. Instead, I hung out with K-Dawg and got my fix of cuddles :3 We arranged to meet up sometime after he got off work. I have a stats class on Tuesdays and Thursdays for an hour, which is really annoying since it’s the only class I have for those days – the time and cost to commute is no fun. The prof is a short Indian fellow who seems very familiar with what he’s lecturing about, which is great, but I find his tone of voice is very difficult to follow. He speaks with a moderately high pitch that’s fairly mono and quick. So I’m not too happy with that since I need to grasp everything he’s saying because I’ve had trouble with stats in previous courses. What’s more, Suki is in the same class as me – not a particularly bad thing, but it just means I’ll be bugged a lot to clarify what so-and-so means since she’s an international student from Japan with a moderately poor grasp of the English language. 

Anyways, the class ended at 4pm and it took about forty-five minutes to get to Bay and Bloor. I picked up the book I wanted to get - which wasn’t in stock in a lot of Indigo or Chapters stores – called Rejuvenile by Christopher Noxon. The book is about modern culture and the way adults behave in juvenile-like ways (compared to, say, 50 years ago) and how it has affected society. It doesn’t take much brain power to see why I’m interested in reading this, it just sucks that it cost me $18 for a 260ish page book of medium-to-large size font. Maybe if I finish it really quick I can return it (lol). After that I went over to Bay Bloor Radio to look for a new pair of headphones. I dropped my iTouch today causing a piece of my cheap earbuds to break off somewhere – I have a warrantee on it so no worries. I don’t mind spending over a hundred bucks for headphones of good quality that’ll last me a long time – I’m looking into the Sennheiser PX200 which costs $80, so far the reviews look like a favourable item to purchase. 

Not long after I met up with K-Dawg – he looked spiffy in a black blazer and jeans having just got off work. We settled on having pizza and cake…yes, I do not get sick of those two foods *nom nom nom* ^^. I thought it would be a wicked fun idea to bake a cake and top it with frosting. Value-Mart had a cake mix with sprinkles in them, but K-Dawg suggested we go to No Frills because it would be cheaper (and closer to his place), plus it called for eggs which would also be cheaper. =.=** When we got to No Frills the cake mix was $1.30 more expensive and they didn’t have the one with sprinkles in them. After a bit of wandering and decision making, we ditched the cake making idea and got a $3 McCain chocolate cake (gawd, 1/6 of the cake = 280 calories…but there was another cake that was 1/13 = 320 calories o__O) and a frozen no name brand deluxe pizza. 

I helped myself to 1/6 of the cake while waiting for the pizza to bake :3 I’m not a big chocolate fan, but eating it was a bit reminiscent since growing up I use to eat the same McCain cake from time to time. Although, it was frozen solid and I microwaved it a bit to long ~ mmm, gooey-ness. The pizza didn’t taste very good, even with the mountain of extra cheese we put on it, but at least the sangria (which we made from really old wine and triple sec) helped wash it down. Afterwards we cuddled on his bed and watched a few episodes of Parks and Recreation (staring Amy Poehler and Rashida Jones). We both got padded in an Abena X-Plus, which I failed to completely drown and potentially cause it to leak on his bed :P I felt rather unsatisfactory with myself for that (seeing as the last two times I managed to leak in an Attends and a Bambino)…among another thing which shall remain unsaid. Besides that, the cuddles was really enjoyable since we manoeuvred a bit differently than usual. I find that I’m more into lounging and holding and a bit of the foreplay than the stuff that escalates ‘thereafter’. But honestly, I’m not one to disappoint so I’ll go the distance regardless without a peep…though I joke around a lot when in action, which I think is a result of my uncertainty and/or immaturity. I think that’s why I enjoy hanging out with K-Dawg. Even though I know other ABDLs and I meet with some of them on occasions, I don’t really feel comfortable being immature around them than I do with K-Dawg. I can be more of a 4 year old while knowing that the boundaries are more flexible when around him. That feeling is very exalting – like letting out a really good sigh of relief after a really long and hard exam.  I find that my mind and attitude becomes more cheerful and bubbly; and what’s more, I don’t think I could ever get tired of living with that kind of behaviour even though it is impractical.  Anyways, I was off on a tangent just there. I wouldn't mind exploring it further had it not been nearly 2am at this moment. The night concluded with a him ZipCar-ing me home in a red Jetta ^^ (so far I’ve been in a Civic and a Mini).  

💤 sleepy
Link

Unboredening

May. 4th, 2009   1:16 PM

I been a bit bummed out lately because it feels as though I've been cheated of any kind of summer break since my exams ended so late.  Thankfully, it helped motivate me to do something about it, though my attention span is pretty thin so I've left some things on my 'to do list' half finished.

Tennis tonight with Stephanie.  Good chance I can get the car, if not then there goes that activity.  My bro took the car yesterday night when I wanted to use it to go to the gym, he suggested we go today instead.  Well, he can suck it cause I'll be taking the car XP.  Damn, Nadal beat Djokovic on Sunday, winning the Internazionali BNL d'Italia ATP Masters tournament.  Stephanie wagered me a cake if Djokovic won! ><**

Wolverine with Monica tomorrow...hopefully.  She wanted to watch in the afternoon, but I didn't want to cut it so close to my stats class from 4-5pm.  So hopefully she can do 6:30pm - gotta wait till her folks give her the go.  I'm a little hesitant to watch this because I really thought the trailers were really cheesy.  But both my bro and Nebulus64 have watched it and said it was good.  Monica really wants to watch it, she told me Wolvy was her favourite from the three X-Men flicks.  It's funny because I don't think I've ever had one favourite character growing up with X-Men the animated series.  I really like certain powers, but in terms of the character I'm indifferent.  But in terms of Astonishing X-Men, I began reading the comic book series because of my interest in Hisako Ichiki's (aka Armor) power - which incidentally, I think, is one of my favourite characters in the team even though she's fairly undeveloped. 

Squash with Nebulus64 is being tentatively planned for sometime this week.  He's waiting for a call to pick up some scuba diving gear up in Richmond Hill.  So whenever that goes down, hopefully there will be time for squash ^^.

Chopsticks dinner on Sunday night...hopefully.  Back in first year I befriended Jully (sociology course and a bus incident), Hushna (a B level religions course) and Sue (I think friend of a friend...I honestly don't remember), and since then we've been planning to have dinner at Chopsticks.  I think the restaurant is some kind of Thai fusion...I don't remember.  Anyways, so this meet up has been four years in the planning!  Hopefully it falls through because I haven't had a chance to sit and catch up with Hushna and Sue - other than bumping into one another in the halls every now and then. 

Other then those plans, I still need to find a part time and talk with my financial advisor (I keep getting his voice mail ><).  I originally wanted to have dinner with K-Dawg tonight, but I txt'd him last night, which on second thought seemed really last minute.  Oh well, maybe another time.  Now then...what to do with the rest of my day? o_O  I just got off a few hours ago from a health studies lecture on childhood and development.  The course seems really up my ally, so I think I'll enjoy it ^^.  Plus, the prof sorta reminds me of Toby (from The Office) in terms of face, a bit of the hair, and the way he talks (kind of monotoned).  None the less, he comes off as really intelligent, but his sense of humour isn't bad, though he tends to give to many factoids at times.

One day trip to Ottawa

May. 2nd, 2009   10:54 PM
I think I have an idea how mousies feel when they're trapped in cages T_T  I was on the road today for a total of eight and a half hours!  And as spacious as a Toyota Rav4 may seem, it can still get very uncomfortable which can lead to a bad case of flat-ass syndrome.  Every year at around this time my family goes up to Ottawa to visit my grandparents (fathers side) and pay respects to deceased relatives.  We left the house at around 8am-ish, and as per usual my brother and I took turns driving - I drove the four hour trip there and he drove back.  I don't mind sacrificing some shut eye in the morning only because I dislike driving at night, what with my poor eyesight and all @.@  We got there at around noon and had dim sum at one of the few Chinese restaurants in the Ottawa suburbs.  As usual, my grandma was overly hospitable with ordering and showering us with gifts.  I felt bad for recieving a grad card which included a 1937 Canadian one hundred dollar bill since I'm not graduating this year.  I don't exactly know why they choose to give me a bill, but it might have something to do with having a coin collection I use to maintain...like a decade ago.  Oh well, my grandparents are relatively wealthy.  My grandpa immigrated to Canada and opened a Chinese restaurant and then an Asian grocery store - both were really successful.  My dad told me one time had he stayed in Ottawa and worked in the family business for another 15-20 years he'd have been fairly well off.  But, had he done that he wouldn't have met my mom in Toronto and I'd be gawd knows where =\. 

After dim sum we all went to the cemetery and waited around for my uncles family (for as long as I can remember, it's always been this way - lunch, then a big family gathering at the cemetery, and then we get on with our day).  It's a bit shocking because last year there was still some snow on the ground, but this year the weather was around 13 C.  Afterwards, my family and I went to visit my great grandma (again, fathers side).  She's I think 92 and seriously losing her marbles.  Each time we visit her at the retirement building (around twice a year) she doesn't recognize any of us.  What's more, she speaks a Chinese dialect only my dad can make out half the time.  It was a bit of a surprise as well because we ran into my Aunt Liana and Uncle Viktor, whom also made the trip to Ottawa to pay their respects (they left their kid with the in-laws for obvious reasons).  My aunt (my father's younger sister) and grandpa have some bad blood between them, and I don't recall what it's all about, but it's been going on for many years now.  Which is why she didn't go to the cemetery with us nor visit my grandparents.  But, it's interesting because my grandparents really care about her and they ask about her baby, as well as give them gifts (through my parents).  So whatever their feud is about, my aunt is the one not lifting her foot. 

We stayed for about half an hour before departing to my grandparents house for coffee - I loafed around the basement and watched television (my grandparents have five tv sets >>'' my grandpa is a real couch potato).  I catched the third set of the Federer (2) vs. Djokovic (3) semi-final match of the Internazionali BNL d'Italia ATP Masters tournament (4-6, 6-3, 6-3).  Oh Federer, you are sooooo losing it - way to many unforced errors and first serve faults.  Djokovic played really well, from what little I watched; he seemed really calm and made quite a few big hits.  He won the match, as well as last years tournament, so he'll have to defend it against Nadal (1) tomorrow!  I caught the first set of the Nadal (1) vs. Gonzalez (12) semi-final match (which aired right after) before having to leave.  The first set was pretty good, they both played great tennis and their style I would say is somewhat similar.  Gonzalez is really quick, his forhand is massive, and he attempts to hit a lot of the returns.  But sometimes he's to quick causing him to either lose balance when making a return, or he's unable to react to an odd bounce - which happens especially on clay court.  Interestingly, Gonzalez has been pro since 1999, but only reaching the top 15 ATP in the last five years - with a highest ranking of 5.  He's getting old, I doubt he'll be able to climb up the ATP ladder for much longer, although did win a silver medal in Beijing last year.  Oh well, he still lost 6-3, 6-3 to Nadal. 

Anywhoo, we left for the road at around 5:30pm, and stopped for dinner at a newly built Boston Pizza in Kingston at around 7pm-ish.  Of course, I had pizza ^^.  There are three things I can always stomach; pizza, curry, and dumplings :3.  Got home at around 10pm and thus here I am now.  No plans whatsoever for tomorrow, but Monday the summer semester begins...I have a 10am class to wake up for >.<''

😑 blah  |  ♫ Mobb Deep - Quiet Storm
Link

Ben 10 series review

May. 2nd, 2009   12:16 AM
The last couple of days have been really low key and I've been spending a lot of time in front of my computer.  I finished watching season 3 of Ben 10 yesterday, and season 4 a few hours ago.  Overall, it's a very typical kids cartoon show - nothing really wrong with that since I enjoy watching them, especially laughing at the nonsensical aspects of the show.  There are several things I like about the show, but I'll start off with what I disliked the most.  The main character, Ben Tennyson, is so fuking annoying a lot of the time.  For a ten year old he's overly obnoxious, disrespectful, stupid, and a complete glory hog.  Given the chance, I would punch him in the face regardless of young he is.  What's more, after watching season one I figure he'd develop or at least grow on me.  Instead, he got worse and worse in season two and three.  And it doesn't make much sense either especially watching season three, episode one - Ben 10, 000.  In that episode he experience his future self and how much of a dick head he is to everyone; and of course at the end of the episode he learns the moral of the whole ordeal.  You'd expect Ben's personality to change after that, but it doesn't and it irritates me.  This is strongly contrasted by the fact that the two other main characters, Gwen and Grandpa Max, both have great character development throughout the series.  We first learn about Max's Plumber days at the end of season one, and gets its limelight every now and then.  And Gwen, she becomes more of an important character during season three when she learns magic.  I like how the two hold relevance throughout the series - in the early part of the series they were just moral support, and later on they had greater offensive roles.  I also liked how there were two episodes that had no continuity to the over all story plot - Gwen 10 and Ken 10.  They were both a refreshing change of pace; probably cause I watched no stop, I'm sure it would have been a drag if the show was aired weekly or even daily.  I love how there's a complete disregard for property damage.  It's completely ridiculous that half a city block can be blow to pieces without the characters ever bringing it up, let alone the any other characters in the series.  At least in other cartoon superhero series like Justice League and Teen Titans there is a concern for city property just as much as its civilians.  One thing I really like about the series is how imaginative it is.  There's a huge cast of villains, as well as the aliens Ben is able to transform into (besides his initial ten).  What's more, most of the villains make a return in several episodes, and not just the big villains in which entire seasons are plotted around - like Vilgax, Ghostfreak, or Enoch and the Forever Knights.  One of my favourite villians is Charmcaster.  Her character profile is so kickass even though what's known about her can only be gathered in like four episodes.  I really like the look of her magic bag (something I wouldn't mind trying to create in Second Life) and the dog-golem creatures.  The diversity of Ben's aliens is also something that must be applauded.  I wouldn't exactly say some of the powers are very original, but the character modelling is something I always look at to judge how they stand out - and it's amazing.  The appearance of each alien is so different and creative I love it - some of my favourites would have to be Ditto, Fourarms, Diamondhead, Xlr8, Heatblast, Upgrade, Upchuck, and Grey Matter.  Lastly I like how some of the full fight action sequences are replaced by a single frame, sorta like motion picture comicbook-esque.  Rather than going through several scenes, they are replaced by two or three frames with accompanying music and sound effects.  It's different because not many series do it, but not enough to say it's unique and deserves greater praise.  Overall the series has been fun to watch (when I'm not getting frustrated by how idiotic Ben can be), the action has been plentiful, the creative aspect is beaming, and most importantly the show is hilarious to laugh at and not with!  I'll probably download and watch the sequel series Ben 10: Alien Force - which is set a few years into the future when Grandpa Max goes missing and Ben and Gwen team up with Kevin to go look for him. 
🥱 bored  |  ♫ Torley on Piano - Snowflake Child
Link

The Red Handed Revolver (short ABDL story)

May. 1st, 2009   10:11 AM

Since taking down my story archive website and a story forum I use to post at got closed down, I'm unsure where else my stories have been posted.  This is a repost of a short story I wrote on April 19, 2007; after revising it.  I didn't make any major changes, I just proof read, added/removed a few lines, and rewrote a couple of unclear sentences.  When I was writing this I was influenced by Camilla Gibb's anthropological paper 'An Anthropologist Undone'.  Her writing was easy to follow, witty, fun and stirred up some interesting emotions of how humans behave - I wanted to try my hand at something similar.  To date, this is one of my favourite short stories I've written.  I hope you enjoy.
 

The Red Handed Revolver

I’m squatting in a white hallway looking at my hands while I turn them.  The Scandinavian woman with the greasy hair ahead of me calls this nightly ritual “Smartie time” – we wait in line for our medication.  As I turn my palms to stare at the red, the man behind me interrupts my private agonizing.  He asks how I burned my hands and I’m about to object.  I’m working up the words:

They’re not burnt.  It’s red paint.  Paint my friend Johnny and I played with while visiting our mommy Miss Sanderbrook.  We were babies last week, dressed in bonnets and diapers in the eastern boroughs of Mandinare. 

I don’t speak it – the story just sounds too implausible, like the stories of all the other people here.  Last week I was a baby.  This week I am crazy.
_________________________________________________________________________________

In the brief journey between the walls of a stranger’s home and those of an English psychiatric hospital, I am alone.  I am thirty-one years old going on three next month.  I take plane rides for a living and drink much wine to compensate a year of abstinence.  The sobering helps blur the passage between the blue and pink walls of a peaceful nursery and the cloudy, familiar grey that is Mandinare; that is home. 

Home is a swamp waiting to swallow.  I’ve been neck deep in its mud before – depressed enough to have been hospitalized and wild enough to be diagnosed maniac.  Both depressed and wild enough that when I left for Miss Sanderbrook’s nursery to unwind, I took two duffle bags: one full of play things, the other full of pills.  Maybe I’m exaggerating, but that’s hardly important, because my three day stay went by all too quickly.  With a swat to my bottom and a wave of goodbye I was thrown back into my miserable life with only the remnants of paint stains to serve as a reminder of all the fun I experienced. 

I’ve been back for a day now.  I walk the grocery aisles aimlessly thinking about my next flight out and when I’ll be back again, hoping in time to catch the Saturday morning cartoons.  I cry at the sight of farm animals – poultry suffocating under plastic – broccoli, green beans, foulmouthed toddlers, and florescent lights.  I think of my childhood while gazing at Trix; how I’d long for the treasure buried in every box.  I still do, admittedly, but I know the sugars are bad for my health.  I carry my bag full of disgusting vegetables down an unfriendly street passing door after closed door.  Life here is the weak light behind shutters, the faint smell of cooking oil, graffiti and broken car windows, and the footsteps of men making their way to the damp pub at the bottom of a hole.   

I make my way into a park, one of few that are presumably safe to stroll around after dark.  There is a sandbox, a set of swings, and a lopsided slide.  I stick with what looks safest and hesitantly approach the sandbox with a superficial subconscious.  Sitting on the inside of the wooden border I begin shovelling with my hands.  In this act I am happy and calm, my depression subsides.  An autumn gust reveals a glimmer at the center of the sandbox and for a second it catches my eye.  Knowing this neighbourhood, the only buried treasure is a syringe or used condom.  Never the less my curiosity pulls me closer and I begin to uncover something far stranger.   

It my hand is a revolver.  It immediately reminds me of an old nursery rhyme.  Both short and stout, I hold it by its handle and tilt it, a trickle of sand pours out its spout.  It makes me giggle.  It’s just a toy, I tell myself, sounding more like a question than a statement.  Well, there’s only one way to find out.  I point at the sky and pull the trigger.   

BANG! 

“HOLY SHIT!” 

I frantically pick myself up as I toss the gun aside.  I no longer feel like an absent minded infantile.  Dogs are barking, my feet are shaking, and a build-up of tears send mixed emotions throughout my mind.  Breathing heavily now, I let the adrenaline take over.  Nearly forgetting my groceries, I grab the gun and stumble away as quickly as I can.  By the time I reach my suite I am completely exhausted.  Each breath escapes me, but a smile is slathered across my face.  I am laughing.  It was fun, in a non-maniac sort of way.   

I allow my mind to ease in the shower and by the time I’m done my fingers resemble raisins.  Climbing in bed with a towel around my neck and a thick disposable diaper between my thighs, I turn on the television and pretend to watch.  It has begun to rain outside, not uncommon in Mandinare, it rains its way into the next day.  I look to my left; the revolver resting atop of my nightstand asks to be played with.  I look to my right; an empty bed mocks me of my solitude.  I pick up the gun several seconds later and carefully examine it until my back and neck ache.  As I finally figure out how to rotate the cylinder out, my bed sheets are sprinkled with sand.  Two bullets remain unused.  I rub the handle where grains of sand crust to the grip.  It reminds me of Aladdin.  He must’ve been pissed with all that sand scratching his lamp – to bad his genie can’t kill, unlike my own.  I fall asleep shortly and have a dream that I don’t ever remember having.   

It’s eleven o’clock in the morning and I’m filing some paper work, making a few calls, and packing for a flight the following day.  I drink milk from a bottle, puree a blend of carrots and lithium pills, and sit at the dining table alone with the Wednesday funnies.  Ziggy’s complaining to his psychiatrist about not really existing again; I feel for him.  When I’m done, I get dressed for the day and prepare to go out.  I have to deliver a package, restock my diapers, and go see a doctor regarding some chest pain.  While waiting for the elevator I decide not to go back for the revolver.  I didn’t need one yesterday so I won’t need one today.  Maybe by the time I get back I’ll have decided what to do with it. 

The streets are damp and the city is humid, but cool winds breeze by occasionally.  It’s neither ideal to wear heavy or light so I’m walking around town with a windbreaker on, sweating profusely half the time.  The conditions at the post office aren’t as flattering.  The long line-ups seem slow and the air is as thick as clay.  I’ll pass and come back later, although chances are it’ll remain the same.  I am hesitant as I make my way to the drug store.  It’s almost ten past twelve and the lunch crowd is already loose.  I convince myself it’s just an in and out errand and go about my business.  The lady at the cash register looks bored but I don’t bother making small talk.  No later, I’m walking out holding a bag in each hand and the package between my right underarm.  A gust sends chills down my back and for once it feels good.    

The post office is several blocks away and as I’m walking there a ‘small world’ occurrence happens.  I run into an old pastor who was in charge of the church my family use to go to before moving cities.  I’m startled at first when he points me out upon crossing paths; his memory has yet to catch up with his old age.  I greet him with the usual compliment and handshake while trying my best to liven up my dull and expressionless face.  He speaks of the times when I’d seek his guidance; I was so full of questions.  It reminds me of how young and confused I was and how the circumstances have hardly changed.  It also reminded me of how I lost my faith.  Unbeknownst to him I had seen the filthy acts he’d done nearly fifteen years ago.  She was thirteen, but to him she was just another young angle with clipped wings.   

My grip tightens as he continues to speak.  My mind rages with merciless thoughts.  I want to shoot him.  He breaks my concentration when he questions the belongings I carry.  Blushing before swaying my body to block his line of sight, I don’t respond.  Instead, I abruptly walk around him and try my best to politely take my leave.  I tell him I have to return to work.  Having taken only a few steps he startles me once more.  My shoulder is anchored and I hear him whisper in my ear, “Still having trouble keeping the sheets dry, my boy?” 

I try to suppress my feelings of frustration, embarrassment, and disdain while the chemicals in my brain fight their own battle.  I can imagine how my face must have looked while walking down the street.  In some instances I’m grunting and gnawing my teeth, in others my head spasms and shakes violently.  A young lady avoids eye contact when we pass each other.  I try to keep calm and save myself from further embarrassment.  The post office is just meters away – I can make it.  I hear a group of teenagers laughing, in an instant I’m looking in their direction and then back again.  My sweaty palms force my grip to tighten while my pace becomes quicker.   

Entering the post office a turmoil of activities makes a reflection of my thoughts seem real.  A child is screaming uncontrollably, a man slams his fist in frustration, and a woman sighs in exhaustion upon leaving.  I join the circus and play my part for nearly thirty minutes; each minute tests my composure.  It is finally my turn.  I put the two bags of diapers beneath the counter and remove the bulky packaged from my armpit.  After greeting the clerk, I tell him what I want.  He tells me what I need in order to fulfill my request.  I am confused because this isn’t the first time I’ve done this.  A laughter coming from behind distracts me again, I look over my shoulders.  When I look back, the clerk is pointing to his left.  He is telling me something but all I can hear is laughter.  I’m sweating heavily again and my legs begin to shake.  I nod my head pretending to understand and then shuffle to one side as the next person in line is called forward.  I look at the man behind me, he looks down at me.  I’m worried I’ll embarrass myself again.  One last time I look at the clerk who continues pointing to his left.  I hesitate to ask for more assistance as I’ll most likely fail to comprehend.  Finally I give up and simply stand against the wall to the far left, a laughter resumes somewhere amongst the crowd.  Nearly reaching a sobbing state, I think of nothing more than to be at home with my teddy bear.   

“Excuse me?  I believe this belongs to you.” 

My neck kinks from the amount of time I’ve spent staring at my shoes.  I’m not crying, but I know I’m about it.  A blond haired lady in her early twenties stands in front of me.  Her voice sounded sweet and sincere.  She holds two plastic bags identical to the ones I brought along.  I can see the content in each one and assume she can as well.  I’m embarrassed beyond belief and I want to deny her claim, but who am I kidding.  I begin to stutter – a mere ‘thank you’ is unbelievably hard to utter.  She looks down at me, her eyes look as though they are judging me, as if expecting more but all I offer are my open palms waiting for the hand-off.  I am caught red handed.  I leave soon after without looking back.  The sound of laughter continues to choke my dignity.   

Finally I am home.  I throw the two bags against the floor as hard as I can, only to watch them cushion right back up.  I kick one bag and send it flying across the hall.  My mind races with the thought of defeat, insecurity, and malice.  I cannot think of anything more than how much my beloved diapers have betrayed me.  I run to my bedroom and retrieve the revolver.  I scream, kick, and cry at the package that sits helplessly in the corner.  I can hear my neighbours banging on their walls.  I cock the hammer and pant heavily.  The submissive is the one who’s always in control. 

BANG! 

It is completely silent now.  I feel good.  I feel in control.  I feel like adding insults over injuries.  I laugh at how easy it is to end all the miseries I’ve undoubtedly inflicted upon myself.  I don’t deny it because there’s nothing to hide – I am just going slightly crazy.  As I laugh and cry simultaneously, I wonder why I just killed my beloved diapers.  I drop the revolver; it lands equally as hard as I do.  I’m brawling like a baby.  I just wanted a stupid package of diapers, but never at the cost of what I’d been through.  I clench the remains of my fluff spewing purchase.  While looking at it I feel comforted, depressed, and confused all at the same time.  I don’t know whether to cuddle it or choke it.  Minutes later I throw it to one side.  I’m tired and worn out, but I feel as if something is missing; repentance. 

I reclaim the gun and stare at it as if my answers resided within.  Perhaps it did.  I slide the cylinder out and take a peak at how many wishes I have left, just one.  Perfect.  I spin the cylinder and let fate decide what my sanity is worth.  With a flick of the wrist my fingers work their way into place.  Click.  I cock the hammer back.  I yawn before pulling the trigger with a grin smirked across my face. 

BANG! 

Click. 

BANG! 

Click. 

BANG! 

I let out a relief stricken sigh like none other.  Looks like I need help, I declare to myself.  I chuckle half heartedly as the adrenaline flushes my system.  Last week I was a baby, this week I am crazy.

🥱 bored  |  ♫ Philip Glass - Metamorphosis Two
Link

My baby

Apr. 30th, 2009   3:18 PM
Is it odd to give a name to an inanimate object?  My brother snickered at me the other night after restarting the router when he found out I named my desktop computer Lucile >>''  Oh well.  I think it's a pretty smexy name for my baby.  *kiss*  It's probably one of the most expensive things I've ever bought for myself using my own money!  I got it nearly two years ago when Future Shop had a promo deal on Acer Aspire ASE700-EQ662A, which included a Samsung 21.6" LCD monitor and an HP All-in-One Printer, for $1100.  The specs are okay ~
- Intel Core 2 Q6600 Quad
- 2.4GHz
- 2048Mb DDR2 RAM
- 500GB 7200 RPM
- DVD+/-RW Dual Layer
- ATI Radeon X1650 256MB (512MB Hypermemory) PCI-E 16X Video Card
- PSU 300W Lite-On

From what I've read on forums, the video card and power supply are the two main things people recommend replacing.  But when it comes to buying the parts and installing it, I'm pretty dense on the 'how to'.  So when I took the casing off to clean it today, I thought that was a big step up (lol).  My gawd was it disgustingly dirty.  No wonder whenever I run Second Life for a little while the fans go nuts - I must've taken out several tablespoons of dust wads from just the video card fan!  I think I was a little to trigger happy with the can of compressed air, though, so that was a bad use of $15 >>''.  Here's a before and after picture :3  

The video card fan is the blue slotted thingie beneath the big black thingie at the middle.  I have no idea what the green things to the right are, but for the most part they're empty spaces - so I'm guessing there's room to upgrade? o.O  Oh well, my baby has to last until quad-core processors become obsolete, then I'll be willing to get a new desktop ^^;;  I also cleaned my room - particularly my book shelf and school notes.  For the most part after every semester I keep my books and don't resell them.  I always imagined having a book shelf in the future where I can be like "Yup, I read all of these".  Well, I've got the book shelf all tidy, but I don't exactly think I can brag much since I hardly remember most of the things I've read -.-**  Of the approximately fifty books/course readers I have, only maybe a dozen I found memorable or had a lasting impact.  I think it's really sad that I don't feel A LOT smarter from the time I started university till present.  I just feel somewhat smarter, but a lot broker =\ 
🌀 dirty  |  ♫ Ludacris ft. Jamie Foxx - Contagious

Squash and process...

Apr. 27th, 2009   7:33 PM
Oooooh Yeaaa!  I get total bragging rights on Nebulus64 when it comes to squash!  We played at my aunts condo this afternoon, and I'm more than certain he won most of the six games we played.  But on the last game he said winner gets bragging rights XP.  Okay okay, won't rub it in too much, but it was definitely a lot of fun considering I haven't played in half a year and he hasn't played in a year.  We're both at around the same level which is great since we can each make one another play better!  Hopefully we can make this a weekly thing depending on our schedules.  Excellent, I've got a tennis partner and a squash partner, now just to complete the last of my racquet sports!  Jully, we need to get back into playing badminton because I need to lose an epic amount of weight o.O  The weight lose pack me and Stephanie made is almost underway.  We haven't set up a final date yet. but we've each set our goals high - 20lbs.  I weighed myself this morning and was horrified to see what the scale read (especially since I feel the lightest during the day).  I always thought I weighed around 150lbs, but apparently I weigh 172lbs T_T  I'm gonna try to go to the gym more often and play more sports, as well as watch what I eat :<  It's going to be really tough. Anyways, I've downloaded some more comic books ^^.  I downloaded the complete series of Starman (the one with Jack Knight) which is 80 issues long.  I think I'll start it when I'm done reading Sandman.  I also downloaded the DC 1985 crossover Crisis on Infinite Earth ~ it's a classic.  I want to read it mainly because The Flash (Barry Allen) dies in it.  I really love The Flash, he's probably one of my favourite superheroes, but I have a hard time picking which version of him I like the most (I'm gonna say Wally West for now).  I also downloaded and read Flash Rebirth (issue 1 of 5).  It came out at the beginning of April, so i'm going to try and stay current with it ~ so far the plot is really good.  Barry Allen and Bart Allen (Silver Age Flash and Kid Flash) are back on the same Earth as Wally West and Jay Garrick, and without a doubt evil shit happens at the reunion party.  Can't wait to see what happens next!  Oh, and Ex Machina #41 is still stuck at 29.6%, I have a feeling it'll never finish downloading.  Speaking of progress, I'm 20min into My Neighbours the Yamadas ~ as recommended by Stephanie.  Really awesome movie, with a dead simple style of animation, story telling, and voice acting - outstanding, can't wait to finish the remaining hour and forty minutes!  Also, I'm in the process of revising The Red Handed Revolver, a story I wrote on April 19, 2007.  I'm only on the second page, of five, but I'll probably have it done by tonight.  Lastly, my studying for ANTC48 has been going slow (as expected).  I'm at about 30% through all the material, but hopefully tomorrow I'll get more done when I study with Monica at the library.

Piggy flu...on my! D:

Apr. 27th, 2009   12:14 PM
How exciting!  Possibly the next big one ~ swine flu!  Having just finished two epidemiology courses, I feel somewhat enlightened that I can apply it to current events.  Swine influenza A virus (H1N1) primarily resides in wild birds and some mammals - unlikely for us, subtypes A are the most virulent and variable.  So in the right conditions a strain of influenza A could jump species such as wild aquatic birds transmitting to domestic poultry.  What's more, piggies are delicious and they can carry both human as well as their own varient of influenza A (because disease transmission is bidirectional, ex. zoonotic diseases).  Thus, pigs make excellent vessels for recombination - meaning they have the potential to create new, more virulent strains.  Luckily, the swine virus is susceptible to prescription antiviral drugs like oseltamivir and zanamivir.  But, unluckily, the current outbreak originates from Mexico (I think Mexico City, but the stupid newspapers keep generalizing the entire country) which is a pretty poverish place.  Diseases inplaces with high levels of poverty and structural violence have a greater chance of mutating and gaining resistence to drug treatment - this ties in to economic, social, political reasons.  For example, if the drugs aren't available, the virus stays in a community longer, increasing the risk of being transmitted to other communities.  If the drug is available, but people don't follow the proper regimens (ie. stop taking the drugs as soon as you feel better because they're expensive) than the few remaining viruses still in the body could become resistent the next time around.  Luckily, this can be prevented with proper medical and social interventions, and it seems like this is taking place.  More people are wearing face masks while in public and self-isolation recommendations are being taken seriously.  As of yesterday, the WHO reported 20 laboratory confirmed human cases of the swine influenza A (H1N1) in the United States - 8 in New York, 7 in California, 2 in Texas, 2 in Kansas, and 1 in Ohio.  In Canada, 6 cases have been confirmed (4 in Nova Scotia and 2 in BC) but all symptoms were reported as mild and don't require hospitalization - which is good since hospitals are cesspools.  In Mexico, 26 laboratory confirmed human cases and 103 deaths from the virus have been reported.  More than 1614 are sick, bit two-thirds are recovering.  On the bright side other possible cases in other countries, but so far it seems to be contained in North America.  The good news is, if people limit their travelling and seek medical attention or isolation, this could blow over in a few weeks.  The bad news, this could just be the beginning to something scarier D:  The WHO have declared the flu a 'public health emergency of international concern' but they haven't slapped any travel advisory yet (like they did to Toronto during the SARS outbreak, causing a decline in our economy).  It'll be interesting to see how this progresses - i'm thinking how many of Fox's (1989) generalizations on the historical responses to epidemic disease we'll encounter.  Or if there'll be massive cullings like the Avian Flu in Indonesia, since Mexico has a large pig farm industry.  I'd be interested to read about the local responses and how they understand the situation - stigmatize anyone lately?  Xenophobia?  It's also a bit important to point out that swine influenza isn't brand new, it's been around for decades; this strain is just new.  This begs the question, when this blows over, how quickly will it become forgotten? 
🐇 bouncy  |  ♫ Battles - Atlas
