Cuddle discourse

Apr. 24th, 2009   10:52 PM

Interesting.  A recent conversation I had with an online friend sparked a query in my head concerning the meaning and usage of the word ‘cuddle’.  A discourse, according to Michel Foucault (a French sociologist known for his critical studies of social institutions) is an entity of sequences of signs, in that they are enouncements (Foucault, 1969).  An enouncement (often translated as ‘statement’) is not a unity of signs, but an abstract matter that enables signs to assign specific repeating relations to objects, subjects, and other enouncements.  Thus, a discourse constitutes sequences of such relations to objects, subjects, and other enouncements.  To take a line from a heritage paper I wrote recently, ‘the authentic experience is subjective, but the subjective experience is not necessarily authentic’ is a kind of discourse.  Foucault gives an excellent example (he is quite critical of power and state – for example his theory of governmentality) in how discourses affect perspectives; a guerrilla movement can be described as ‘freedom fighters’ or ‘terrorists’.  In other words, discourses in the delivery of vocabulary can mean person A states X, but person B understands it as Y. 

So how does such an innocent word such as ‘cuddle’ fall into this?  In the conversation with my friend, and in my own personal experience, there has always been a distinction of the term when used online and in real life.  In online usage, there is no sexual innuendo (this is not universal, just my own discourse at work ~.^) such that a straight male can cuddle with a bisexual male with no awkwardness.  Cuddling online is a form of roleplay that can involve two imagined bodies snuggling, nuzzling, pouncing, scratching, rubbing, squeezing, and making sounds of arousal or affirmation such as murring, purring, and giggling.  It can be practiced to signify a deep relationship between two friends.  Furthermore, since it is practiced in roleplay, a more relaxing atmosphere and mutual trust is established – which can occasionally correspond to very private discussions.  On the other hand, defining cuddling in real life usage is somewhat different.  A straight male and a bisexual male will more than likely not cuddle (again this is not universal, just my opinion).  Though it may start similar to the online practice, real life cuddling is not imagined – it is both mentally and physically demanding.  Ones attention online can be elsewhere while engaged in cuddling (the internet = anonymity in nearly all forms), however the same cannot be said about cuddling in real life.  What’s more, a sexual implication can escalate, such as kissing, necking, masturbation, dry humping, and fingering.  Why is there, or rather than generalizing, why do some people make this distinction?  Is there an even deeper meaning implied when used online (one that connotes the same level of affection offline?), that's shy of being explored?  Is the distinction problematic when online relationships are unified in real life?  Or does it transgress?  And if so, why have such a distinction to begin with? 

Bah, more questions than answers…gawd I hate analyzing like an anthropologist!

♫ Method Man - Meth vs. Chef

So full...feeling chubby! {o'.'o}

Apr. 24th, 2009   7:54 PM
Phew, its been a really satisfying day so far ~ thanks for the good times Stephanie! ^^  Started my day at 8:30am cause I intended to drive my mom to work, since she talked my dad into letting me use the car today.  Instead, my dad drove her - probably took care of some quick errands.  So this was probably one of the only days in a while that I've woken up early and ate breakfast (Cinnamon Toast Crunch with soy milk - breakfast for chaps o.O).  Chapter 24 of Better Days concluded - it seemed pretty long while following it these last couple of weeks, but it was really sweet rereading it.  Tommy is such a sweetheart, I love it.  And hopefully we'll get to see a bit more Fisk :3  I downloaded issue #29 of Astonishing X-Men; -.- issue #28 came out on Jan 21, 09 so its been a hell of a wait.  Overall, kinda meh, but not a disappointing meh.  The plot didn't move forward by a heck of a lot - the issue ends with the team confronting Forge.  I <3 Hisako, she's so silly sometimes.  Cyclops and Emma Frost make a pretty nutty couple, their little bickerings are amusing.  And Storm, well, one can never have enough of her hotness.  I await the next issue!  (June 17, 09...dammit ><)  I'm waiting on issue #41 of Ex Machina, I started downloading this morning and the progress is stuck at 29.6% :<  Issue #40 came out on Dec 17, 08, but luckily it was a single issue story so I didn't have anything concrete to look forward to - which is also a bit sad because I'm beginning to forget some of the events that are taking place!  I also read chapter 445 of Naruto, which was pretty boring.  It just gave the readers half the backstory of Nagato, Konan, and Yahiko =\ Chapter 445 will probably be just as unentertaining.  I find that getting back into comic books after neglecting it for a little while is pretty comforting.  Right now I'm reading issue #58 of Sandman.  I'll probably finish the 75 issue series by the end of the weekend. 

The weatherman is a lie.  It said thunderstorms, but it was sunny practically all day.  I got up to Stephanie's place at around noon.  We chatted for a bit before deciding on what to do.  We played some sucky tennis (we've played suckier :P first game of the season is just warm up!) until 1:30pm-ish.  It was really windy, and it came from cross-court so it wasn't like either of us had any advantages heh.  My accuracy is really crap cause I'm having difficulty gauging how far the ball will go with the strength I'm putting into the swing @_@.  I think I'll look into getting a tennis game for the Wii (if one exists, and not the crappy sports pack that comes in the box) when I trade in my DS and PSP.  We went for Korean BBQ afterwards.  The place we normally go to is in Commerce Gate on Hwy 7 and compared to the other places I've been to the quality and price are very good ($8.99 a head).  We gorged ourselves in the all-you-can eat appetizers, beef, pork, and chicken - was so painfully delicious!  The damn waiter gave us two of everything when we asked for seconds >< he made us chubby! (lawsuit!)  Ah well, open flame cooking makes me feel like a caveman :3  Afterwards Stephanie was like "I need a nap" which was too jokes!  What's more, we went to Michael-Angelo's Market Place for cake!  I've heard of the place and my parents have shopped there, but this was my first time going.  It's sort of an upper-middle class grocery store with a place where you can sit and eat their ready-made stuff.  o.O  They have a clearance section for produce which I thought was sorta icky - they looked good, priced low, and questionably safe to eat.  I need to go to this place more often, the cake section was amazing!  They had clearance cake that I didn't bother questioning cause they looked so friggin' delish.  We got a slice of mocha cheesecake, lemon tart, and black forest cake.  The other clearance single sliced cakes were a regular cheesecake, fudge cake, and a lemon cake.  Each slice was only $2!!  And they were a pretty fair size!  If you go to Caffe Demetre, a slice of cake maybe a bit bigger would cost $8-10!!  That's bananas! D:  What's really jokes is that the grocery store sold a really really similar looking cake to the one I bought at Raba - see April 6th post - but cheaper!  It was a rectangular mocha cake with the same kind of chocolate shaving decorations on the top...I wonder if it also smells like soy sauce o.O?  Anyways, we ate at the grocery store and washed it down with coffee - the cheesecake was the best!  To bad we over indulged haha, but once in a while is okay in my books :3  It was really great catching up with Stephanie and thankfully there weren't any awkward post-graduation discussions which I sorta feared.  (You're graduating!  I'm really happy for you! :D)  It's good to know I'm not the only one she knows who's staying back a year ^^;;  (Thanks for giving me one of your grad photos Stephanie, it was really sweet of you :3)  Oh, and we're starting a weight-loss pack with potentially one other person!  I told her to ask the third comrade since she's got a heavy build and I'm bad with talking about these sorts of things to girls =x  So if this thing falls through, we're each going to set a goal and tell each other our weight  Then we're going to give ourselves a due date and get on it!  The reward is cake...which is a lie! D: 
♫ The Dying Breed - My World Down

Kiss and blog

Apr. 23rd, 2009   3:20 PM
Last night with K-Dawg was fun ^^.  I brought a strawberry shortcake; it was delish and sorta spongee heh.  We made pizza, my fav <3.  We were chatting earlier about having salmon, then it became shepherds pie, then I bossed up and said I wanted pizza (even though I had it on Tue with Jully.  I ish trying to make it a part of my weekly diet?).  I was kinda hoping he'd get the doughy crust because it seemed really fun to play with (reminds me of Play-Doh) but he got the pre-made crust instead ^^;; boo.  Oh wells, he did most of the work since he's pro at making pizza, having worked at a pizza joint - I just grated the cheese.  Besides the mushrooms, olives, and pepperonis, we put a lot of cheese.  It was so tasty! :3 Later he padded me in a Bambino Bianco, something he picked up when he was in New York.  I've only worn them one other time, so this was a nice treat ^^.  I really like the feel of the plastic and the waist bands, to bad the leg guards aren't as good as everything else ._.  After a little while of cuddling while watching the movie Click, I leaked a bit on his bed...for the second time! >< I'm sorry!  But at least this time it was a much smaller patch ~ even Bambinos can't stop me! *evil laughter*  Hrmm I've never been much of a kisser - or maybe I'm just inexperienced - but when we kissed I thought it was kinda strange (and it's not because I've never kissed a guy before).  Like, I'd help set the mood , but if I had to I wouldn't be the first to do it.  I think one of the reasons is because of this girl I knew in first year.  I still have her on MSN, but I'm more than certain she blocked me since things got really rocky (o.o'').  If I had to describe her in one word, I'd say I remember her best for being very 'kinky'.  She was a bisexual with a same sex partner and she was really blunt about inviting me over for some 'fun' all the time.  I think one of the reasons she was being persuasive was cause I'd encourage it whenever we chatted online, but when it came face-to-face it was really uncomforting for me.  So quite frankly it was partly my fault, I really shouldn't have put forward something I couldn't (or wanted to) deliver.  Like, there was this one time we made out in a piano room (AA building if anyone wants a private place to have fun, heh) and she was totally in the driver seat ~ and believe me, you do not want to stand in the way of an aggressive person.  The way things were going, it really wasn't something I enjoyed much.  I'm pretty sure not long after that we stopped seeing/talking to one another - I had to play the douchebag card, which was really stupid of me.  It would have been nice if I could have continued being her friend she seemed really charming, I was just insecure, insensative, and absent minded.  I wish I could appologize to her :<

Course selection...

Apr. 22nd, 2009   3:25 PM
I just finished enrolling in some summer courses.  I've decided to do a Major program in Health Studies (B.Sc) rather then the minor I originally mentioned.  So, if I pass all my courses this semester, and there's only one I'm a little worried about, I will need... - ANTC35 or ANTC60 - ANTC60 or SOCB05 - 1.5 science credits - STAB22 - HLTB01 - HLTB02 - and 0.5 credits of anything The summer courses I'm taking are STAB22, HLTB01, and HLTB02.  It kinda sucks because the statistics course is on three days of the week (lec and tut) where as the two health studies courses I've managed to cram into one day each.  Also, the 1.5 science credits won't be very difficult to get since health studies is considered science.  Next fall hopefully I can get into ANTC35 or ANTC60, and SOCB05 - since they have limited enrollment.  And if all goes well, I'll only need to take three courses in the fall and I'll be finished with two science degrees under my belt!  I better start praying I pass all my courses! T_T

Gayness :3

Apr. 22nd, 2009   2:05 AM
I had a really relaxing night ~ thanks Jully ^^.  We went to watch 17 Again, a movie that's not really up my ally, but she wanted to watch it.  We each had our complimentary tickets from the time we watched Watchmen and the projector f'd up, but luckily today is half price Tuesdays so we're still holding onto our freebies.  The movie was okay - predictable and a bit cheesy, but very sweet.  Thomas Lennon's role was to jokes, I couldn't stop laughing whenever he was on screen!  And Zac Efron was a real cutie :3  His acting wasn't bad either.  Michelle Trachtenberg's acting (especially the sceen where she's crying and Efron comforts her) was below par =\  She should stick with tv shows. Now, here's what I find interesting.  For the past while Jully has been more and more open about her fanaticism for yaoi (homoerotic male relationships primarily in Japanese anime/manga).  We talk about sexuality and stuff like that pretty liberally so her interests don't really come off as a big surprise.  However, from what I can make of it, she doesn't seem to be aware of my'interests (kink-wise...ABDL stuff).  Although I don't talk at all about it in front of her, I write about it on this blog.  What's gotten me curious is that I know for a fact that she reads this blog (*waves at Jully* HI!! You made it this far!!) but she doesn't read everything...because I write to much and it gets boring (so she tells me, heh).  And I find this odd because it contrasts me.  I normally read blogs of people who fascinate me, and I feed my curiosity by reading the very first entry to the present ones.  It's like, how can you settle with knowing only the tip of the iceberg when the stuff below the surface of the water IS litereally just a mouse scroll down?  (awkward metaphor?)  I can understand it if people don't want to know about certain details of a bloggers life - which is why some people use filters for their blog - but I personally don't see the need to use them just yet.  I've only got two subscribed friends on LJ and I only know of a handful of online and in-real-life friends who read this blog (whom I assume don't care much about what's being written).  Besides, I use tags to categorize my entries as best as I can =\.  Anyways, I doubt I'll break the ice about my kinks to Jully.  As much as her secrets have been revealed to me, I unfortunately I will not return the favour unless she makes it her business ^^;;  So...tomorrow should be fun.  I'm meeting up with K-Dawg after he gets off work.  He's gonna pamper me and make me dinner :3  And I'm bringing cake and the cuddles <3  I probably won't stay out to late since it's a weekday. Tennis and cake plans with Stephanie are tentatively being made for Friday.  I hope that falls through since I haven't played tennis since last summer, and you can never have enough cake in your system! Lastly, good luck with your job interview Di5Red!  You seemed really excited about going to the UK, and you've been working so hard lately - I hope you get what you deserve!

- a couple I've never seen came as well, fun and interesting pair they are.  What's more, they live in the same complex as Ricky and K-Dawg...ABDL central? o.O

- picked up a new webcomic to follow!  Little Tales!  Doesn't seem to follow any sort of story arc, but updates every Mon, Wedn, and Fri!
- interesting, Kyandeisu created a new MSN and added me ~ always though our friendship withered because our obsessive gaming, I guess things patched up when I quit? (lol)

- the series is a very typical kids show (duh), so I wasn't expecting anything grand.  Supposedly it gets better, and I've already dowloaded all four season.

- good recommendation by Jully!  H:AP = worlds' countries turn bishonen in a WW1 and 2 setting glossed with stereotypes!  Outstanding!

- i will not be surprised if I fail the exam...but I'm more than certain I'll pass the course ^^;;
- enjoyed the company of Monica and Jully while studying
- witness some public display of extreme affection between two gays just before the exam ~ Jully's wet dream, no doubt :P
- had dinner with Jully at Boston Pizza afterwards ~ thanks for the ride home!  Catch a movie tomorrow?

Random:
- when the weather gets nicer I must seek out Stephanie!  Tennis time!  And I owe her cake...
- need to change LJ profile bio, seems rather misanthropistic
- look into getting some commisions done for me by ozzyfox, Shiro, and Marci
- work on Second Life builds
- work on stories
- write article on 'community' ~ reminded by Di5Red, thanks for the great chat!
- job hunting!
- new issues of Astonishing X-Men and Ex Machina out on April 22...after months upon months of waiting!

Two exams finished, two more to go (+ an essay)

Apr. 17th, 2009   12:38 PM

At last, it is the weekend! \o.o/  I've already got most of it planned out, so really, I just need to make sure I don't procrastinate and follow on schedule.  Today I need to take care of some errands and hopefully finish my D15 research paper.  I need to call my financial advisor and find out what my options are for the five grand I put into my Tax Free Savings Account.  I'm learning just now how RRSP's work and how you can invest them in GIC's or Mutual Funds - but I'm still a bit confusing and I don't exactly want to screw this up since it's a long-term planning sorta thing.  Since transferring the five grand, my chequing account has becoming horrifyingly low.  I’m down to about twenty-five hundred dollars, which has to last me until I can find a job.  It's also a bit of a bummer because I called G4S (the security company I worked for) this morning and they said I abandoned them, so I’m no longer working for them @_@...so much for that leave of absence I asked for several months ago – which supposedly got approved.  Oh well, I can reapply and skip their training session, but they can’t guarantee that I’ll get back the site I use to work at.  I don’t know how long that process will take if I do decide to go back to them.  What’s more, my security guard license needs to be renewed by the government (which costs $80 T_T) since it expires on May 22.  I have no idea how long that process will take either.  If I do decide to go back working as a security guard, I want to try and get a job with Garda instead since they built a new head office two minutes walk from where I live.  Again, I have no idea how long that would take before actually working for them (resume, interview, and possibly training – it could take way longer than I can afford).   

Anyways, on to Saturday.  I will be a bit disappointed in myself if I don’t get my D15 paper finished on Friday.  I’ll be beating myself up if I don’t get it done by Saturday afternoon!  If I don’t get it done, I won’t go to the ABDL munch because I’d like to go with nothing to have to stress about.  I’d feel too guilty for relaxing because I couldn’t finish my homework when more than enough time was allocated to getting it done.  If I do finish and go to the munch, then happy times! ^^  I’ll probably wrap up the night with a squishy diaper, a little tipsy, and a nice nights sleep.  And then that just leaves Sunday.  I’ll be studying like crazy for my ANTC67 exam on Monday night.  After Monday I’ll be free (…to job hunt -.-) until the 29th where I have one last exam. 

So that’s the plan for my weekend.  The days leading up to it have been pretty intense and I’m glad I survived that hurdle.  On Wednesday I had my ANTC20 exam at night.  I really need to do well on the exam since I bombed the midterm and final paper.  I met up with Monica earlier in the day to study and although we didn’t chat much it was nice to have company.  I didn’t think the exam was too difficult, and I know I didn’t study enough – but I think I got away with studying most of the things that were on the exam.  So I felt pretty confident after writing it, lets just hope I don’t jinx it >.o  I didn’t see Emy at the exam and found out later when I called her that she wrote it in Accessibilities.  :P I teased her about not seeing my stereotypical Chinese-look (cause I was wearing my glasses and didn’t have my hat on to hide my spiky hair).  I got home at around 10pm-ish and didn’t really start studying for my next days exam until after midnight.  Even then, it was kinda hard to study since I was chatting with Jully (who should have been studying too! >< so bad heh).  It was a good conversation at least; to summarize…
1)      ._.* She reinforced my forgetfulness, as usual.
2)      Geeky girls and gamer girls are hawt D: 
3)      Mundane memories are subjectively important…recalling them can make you look silly >.o
4)      I miss the good ol’ school days of cooties, jinx, and typing 55378008 on a calculator
5)      *adds to the top of the ‘stories to write’ list* I owe her a story about gay highschoolers

 I think I went to bed at 4am having only covered two weeks of lecture material >>’’  Oh well, I wasn’t to stressed about my Thursday exam cause I could afford to fail it and still pass the course.  I woke up at around 9am to study some more and got through another two weeks worth of notes before my mind wandered off to teh internet ._.  I left the house at 3pm cause I arranged to meet with Jully, Monica, and Merry to study.  But, Monica called and said she’d be late and wouldn’t be joining us…and I called Merry and she said she also wouldn’t be joining us – found out as well that she broke up with her boyfriend of three years.  I hope she manages, it’s a bummer to see optimistic people get frizzled.  I got to school late cause all the highschoolers were off packing the buses and slowing down traffic =\  Studying with Jully wasn’t bad – we didn’t get distracted to much and it’s nice to work on questions where at least one of us has an answer to!  When it came game time, I thought the exam was to demanding @_@  The prof expects us to write so much, for questions worth so many marks, in a time span that’s hardly enough.  I came out with mad pain in my hand ._.*  I know I did poorly on at least three questions, but I’m sure I did okay for at least half of the essay – so I’ll pass :P  Afterwards Jully and I went for cheap Chinese food.  Here noodles were oily and my fish was sorta bleh.  And the stupid restaurant didn’t take credit so I ended up eating ‘soft rice’ (a Chinese expression when a woman feeds/pays the bill for a man) T_T.  I walked her home, and didn’t get home myself until midnight.  Took a shower and watched episodes 7-10 of season one Fringe.  Oh how the plot thickens :P  The story is getting good, but the acting is still nothing special.  I <3 Walter Bishop. 

Last thoughts.  I wore a Tranquility ATN for about seven hours yesterday and surprisingly they didn’t leak (o.o)  Throughout the day I had two medium Tim Horton’s ice caps, and a glass of lemon tea.  Also, I was sitting and walking around quite a bit.  Even though they were pretty clammy and uncomfortable during the final stretch of the night, I’m pretty impressed ^^.  The one thing I noticed about them that I don’t really like is that the width of the padding is very narrow.  As in, you can use them as stuffers, but it’s difficult to stuff something under them.  An Attends 10 can go over a Tranquility, but a Tranquility cannot go over an Attends – which kinda sucks since the Attends I have are jockstrap-ish style so doubling it over a Tranquility looks and feels really weird.  I’m a bit suspicious whether a baby diaper could even fit inside a Tranquility =\. 

🌀 busy  |  ♫ Bloc Party - Sunday

The Social Life of Human Organs

Apr. 16th, 2009   1:28 AM

I meant to write this in late March, since learning about it, but I haven’t gotten around to it until now - which is kinda funny since I wrote an exam tonight with two questions concerning this topic. The issue of human organ transplantation was discussed in the ‘Gifts, Money and Morality’ course (ANTC20) I took. One of the required readings covering the topic comes from Margaret Lock’s novel, Twice Dead: Organ Transplantation and the Reinvention of Death (2002), chapter thirteen ‘The Social Life of Human Organs’. In the chapter she does a comparative analysis between North America and Japan on the idea of donating organs. 

 However, before she gets into the distinctions, she raises the issue of whether donating is an act of gift giving or commodity giving (exchange). Two theorists are presented. Karl Marx critiques the idea of commodity exchange – he states that capitalists fetishize objects. In 1887 ‘fetish’ referred to magical objects in so-called ‘primitive’ societies. In other words, commodities have an inherent power over people such that commodity exchanges gain a degree of control over people. So, when commodity exchanges dominate an economy (as it so often does in capitalism), commodities appear to obtain properties of their own. In effect, consumers see values inherent in the object, but we do not recognize the value of the labour put in to it by the producers – thus labour is devalued (ie. think sweatshops and brand name). Producers benefit less from their labour than the capitalists they work for. Marx calls this separation of labour from workers ‘alienation’. 

 Anyways, the second theorist is Marcel Mauss – interestingly he’s Emile Durkheim’s nephew. Mauss makes a distinction between Marx’s idea of alienation/commodities and gift giving. When you receive a gift a moral obligation to reciprocate is created. Although he did his studies among non-capitalist tribes, the idea is still applicable to us as I’ll discuss in a bit. He distinguishes that commodities are alienable, and commodity exchanges are of alienable things between aliens. Essentially, the exchange is not defined based on the identity of the exchanger and no obligations are created in that process. For example, when I buy a cheeseburger my identity does not matter nor will there be any enduring relationships after the exchange between me and the cashier. In contrast, gift exchanges are an obligatory exchange of inalienable things (or services) between non-aliens. For example, the non-capitalist tribes Mauss researchers practice ‘potlatch’ – a continuous “I give you, you give me more, I give even more, you give me a lot more, etc etc” between tribes. Furthermore, there is a notion that the ‘spirit of the gift’ exists. Mauss states that whether the gift is an object or a service, it has an emotional meaning to the giver and the receiver, thus creating an obligation (or a ‘hold’) over the receiver until they reciprocate back.  

 So, returning to Margaret Lock. Can organs be alienable? One way of thinking about this is to look at what circumstances the organ enters an exchange – as in, look at the social life of the organ as it moves through space and time, rather than the function it served. By doing this, it tells us about the objects value and the ideologies surrounding the objects exchange. For example (and this I find interesting) in North America sperm donors are routinely paid, but women selling their eggs can cause a public offence. The idea that a woman’s body part should not enter commodity exchange, but for a man this is okay; why is that? (probably has something to do with Western gender ideologies) Here’s another example. After surgery from organ transplants, the recipients have been known to say that their ‘mode of being in the world’ has changed. As in, if the donor was a female and the recipient was male, he might say he feels feminised. Sometimes a recipient will give names and stories for their organ (because the identity of donors remain anonymous, no matter what, doctors are not allowed to give out the donors information – other than gender), one might imagine the donor was Italian so they would eat more garlic to be kind to the organ. In these examples Marx’s idea of commodity fetishism and Mauss’s idea of moral obligation to reciprocate the ‘spirit of the gift’ can be seen. (I especially like Marx’s concept – like, people get really uncomfortable not knowing who the donor is. What if you receive a heart from a convict? How would that make you feel? Would you think that the organ has some degree of control over you – that you’d feel like a bad person?)

 I kinda notice I keep steering away from the question, and instead of answering it I come up with more questions (which I really hate, but it’s a reoccurring theme in a lot of cultural anthropological research – it frustrates me to no end a lot of the time). Can organs be alienable? In North America the answer is yes and no.  The idea of altruism is a modern form of fellowship building that strengthens the society; that by signing your organ card you become a better person (which is a thoroughly secularized idea). As in, knowing that an unknown individual in society will benefit and that you (or your family) can expect no direct return for your organs/donation. What’s more, anonymous donations are supposed to create objectivity; it rests on this notion that organs are an alienable thing. In contrast, the ‘gift of life’ (a commonly coined term in North America) does not work in Japan. The idea of an anonymous donation as a charity is seen as a foreign concept. For starters, in Japan charity is strongly associated with pity, inequalities, anonymity, distance, and cold interpersonal relations. People tend to struggle with the idea of donating or receiving organs because gift giving is key to social solidarity. Social ties are cultivated out of obligations because the Japanese are acutely aware of their obligations and the values embodied in gifts. So, it becomes troubling to receive an organ from an anonymous donor because they are unable to reciprocate appropriately. Here’s something to think about, if doctors were allowed to give the identity of the donor (or their family), would it be so hard to imagine the donors demanding reciprocity from the recipients? (No offence to Asians, considering this is coming from a Chinese guy, but I’ve seen our type do some pretty shameless stuff.  Sure, we speak a lot about ‘saving face’ but I have to admit I get pretty embarrassed by the way some of us behave, in contrast to the way I was brought up.) Lastly, living donations (usually from relatives) are also a troubling concept. Japanese people (and I’m sure this also applies to North Americans) don’t want a family member to cut themselves open for their sake – this is usually resolved when family members come forward without being asked to. 

 Anyways, I’m glad I finally got a chance to type this. I hope it’s as interesting to whoever is reading this as it was for me. Never stop thinking or being curious :) Peace out.

💤 sleepy  |  ♫ Styles P - Switch My Style

Sara the third trumpet...

Apr. 14th, 2009   11:01 PM
I've been thinking about my past school experiences lately.  Just the other day I was browsing my old year books from grade 3, 4, 7, 8, 11, and 12.  And today I was looking through a back-up CD I burnt in 2005 containing miscellaneous files (like MSN logs, old school reports, and a bunch of random stuff I've written).  I think I'm a sentimental person, but sometimes I choose the worst possibly time to reminisce.  I've been pulling out my hair over my ANTD15 essay (which was due today, but managed to ask for an extension until Monday), as well as finding time to study for my ANTC20 exam tomorrow night, and my ANTC68 exam the day after.  Anyways, I guess it just 'feels' appropriate that I'm being nostalgic now since this is a time when some of my friends are graduating from university.  One of the shortest MSN logs I read was from a conversation I had with someone I now wish I had gotten to know better.  It is dated June 27, 2005.  The conversation only lasted six minutes, and is as follows: Sara: hey Sara: did you ever get around to do tape test? Me: HA Me: i have plenty of time to do it...but will i?!?! Me: no :P Sara: same here...i was gonna do it this morning but then i just gave up Sara: no point in doing it now anywaz Me: :P Me: horray! the trumpet elders fail repitor! Sara: whoohoo! <:o) bring out the champange! Me: :P haha Sara: u going to school twm? Me: yup Me: agh...me havta go Me: din time Sara: k ttyl Me: later :P It's interesting that something so mundane can flood my memory of things past.  I knew her from grade nine until grade twelve through band.  We both played the trumpet.  Outside of rehearsals and classes that we shared, we would hardly talk with one another - partially due to being in different groups of friends.  One of the reasons I wish I had gotten to know her better is because we were the only two trumpeters that stayed in band for all four years.  Pretty much every year we'd watch one of our friends from the same grade quit band while students from lower grades would fill the void.  What's more, as we ascended in grade, I ascended in seating (for an instrument, sheet music is divided into first, second, and third - where first usually plays the main melody which is more difficult than the third, which usually plays offbeats.  Hardly anyone likes playing third).  When I was in grade eleven and twelve I was first seat and section leader - essentially being in charge of all the other trumpeters and making sure they didn't suck.  Sara however remained in third seat.  I always felt bad (and now that I think about it, I still do) because she had seniority, but wasn't a very good player.  I don't remember if she minded so much, but I wish I had known better so that things could have at least been more favourable for her.  Sometimes when I reminisce I wonder if I'm being bias - by romanticizing my past.  It's frightening how forgetful I am, so having logs and documents to jog my memory is enlightening as well as regretful.  I have done a lot of stupid things in my past, to say the least.  It is a shame I am so sentimental.
🌀 restless  |  ♫ DJ Krush - Song 2

An idea for research on furcons

Apr. 13th, 2009   2:18 PM
So I've been reading a doctoral dissertation by Hyounggon Kim titled 'Serious leisure, participation and experience in tourism: Authenticity and ritual in a Renaissance festival' (2005).  It's freakin' 230 PDF pages so its taken me a while to mull over it, but it's also given me a lot to think about for my ANTD15 research paper.  It has also given me a lot to think about in terms of my interest in anthropology and possibly furthering my studies in the future.  One reason is because the article seemed to have quite a few possible similarities between Renaissance fairs and furry conventions - and this is purely speculative since I've never experienced a furcon, but I have heard and read recaps, seen pictures, and been presuaded to go to one.  I consider myself very fur-curious, as in, I take on a furry persona and hang around furries in Second Life, and I've met and spoken with furries in person - but I've never fully engaged in it or considered it part of who am in contrast to my ABDL side (since the two 'communities' can become blurred sometimes).  Anyways, some of the themes covered in the article include liminality/liminoid, communitas, symbolic materials (ie. costumes and masks), a blurring between fantasy and reality, objective/constructive/existential authenticity, 'being in the nature', rituals of self-transformation, constructing self-identity, intra- and inter-personal authenticity, alienation, 'us' vs. 'them', role-playing, spatial and temporal change, social norms, and so forth.  You cannot tell me there aren't some similarities just reading that list =\.  I've searched my schools online scholars portal (an academic journal database) for articles on the furry fandom and I haven't found anything remotely concrete, though I haven't tried other key words that could provide some hits.  So I thought it would be really interesting if someone (or possibly myself in the future...though I'm doubtful) did an anthropological/sociological study on furcons (for ex. a study to understand the social world of highly commited furries at a particular furcon in regards to the nature of their involvement and phenomenological expereinces during the con).  For me, the most obvious research methods would include participant observation, indepth interviews, quantitative demographic analysis, comprehensive literature review and reflexive journaling for supplementary interpretations, and theoretical sampling.  One of the most stark differences is that Kim did his study at the Texas Renaissance Festival - which is a 14 day event held only on the weekends for 7 weeks.  Furcons, I think, only run for a weekend - possibly as much as four days.  That isn't really much time, unless you are following a sample that participates in a number of furcons - whereby forcing the researcher to also have to go to a number of furcons.  Furthermore, there isn't enough time to establish any sort of trust (which is important when doing ethnographies since it is the threshold to how open participants will be).  Also, certain issues of ethics might hinder the study - which I haven't thought thoroughly about, but is something to consider.  It might simply be easier to do a study on broader aspects of the subculture instead, or even something on a smaller scale such as monthly furmeets. A random thought just crossed my mind.  Research would be so kick ass if it was done today as it were in the 70s, when ethics was much lax.  I am reminded of sociologist Robert Humphrey's 1970 study on the 'Tearoom Trade' - a study of anonymous homosexual encounters in public washrooms.  Humphrey is really bad ass because his study involved observing acts of homosexuality by masquerading as a voyeur, he didn't get consent from his 'participants', he tracked down the names and addresses of his 'participants' through their license plate and interviewed the men in their homes in disguise and under false pretenses.  Research just seems more Jonesy back then, but it totally contaminated the integrity of the academia.  A few years ago I considered writing a story based on Humphrey's way of research, but concerning the ABDL 'community'.  I haven't given up on that idea yet, it's just somewhere near the bottom of my massive list of ideas for stories I'd like to write about :< I wouldn't be surprised if someone else had thought of doing a similar study, but any sort of feedback would be interesting to read.  Anyways, back to writing my paper -.-** progress is such a slow process.
🌀 hungry  |  ♫ Philip Glass - Tiquie River

Random happenstance...

Apr. 11th, 2009   2:16 AM

Some random happenings over the last few days...in point form cause it's getting late...

Wedn:
- arranged to meet up with Jully at school to study
- not surprisingly she was late, but i've come to expect it so oh well
- had interesting discussion about how proud I can be over certain things - and how not good it can be
- she spilt some coffee on the keyboard of her laptop, now the buttons on the fritz - probably unrepairable :<
- later we went to some Thai/Viet restaurant near Silverstar Blvd - the Thai green curry is spicey and coconutty, and the pad thai is delish

Thur:
- stayed home and procastinated
- went to Olde Yorke Fish & Chips for lunch with my dad
- food was tasty but I didn't think it was tasty enough to have such long line ups
- downloaded 6 episodes of Fringe, watched 4 of them
- i find the plot good, but not great
- the acting is nothing special, with the exception of John Nobel (dude who plays Walter Bishop)
- the characters are interesting - especially Walter Bishop, I love his random food obsessions
- i'm disliking how each episode so far has a chase scene
- scare of my life at around midnight, several of my hotmail accounts had empty inboxes.  several hours later things were back to normal.  my guess is it's their fault. 
- my Windows Messenger hasn't been signing me in properly either.  however i can sign in just fine on Pidgin (a multi-proxy IM program)

Fri:
- mildly productive day
- having a hard time getting into the ANTD15 essay because the articles are somewhat dry
- starting to regret doing my topic on medieval fairs
- found out that Jully got into a car accident the day before...how unlucky :<
- thankfully no one was hurt, just a bump to someone's rear
- read Shiro's DA post, found interest in looking into SnowFox - a local furry who commited suicide several days ago
- knew of him through Fox Tales Times and other ABDL/Furry 'community' sites/forums
- seems he owed a lot of people a lot of money
- I'm a bit uncomfortable by how many people follow the Christian belief that suicide is bad
- went to Second Cup to study, only to be kicked out at 9pm since it's holiday hours
- went to Country Style and studied ANTC20 till 11pm
- add new 'things to build in SL' list: stair maze illusion thingy

I've been padding myself for the last three or four days and I don't know what it is about it, but I'm not feeling much of anything in terms of 'interest'.  Normally I would feel kind of relief, or pleasure, or some sort of sensation.  But I'm not feeling much of any of it, it's as if it's becoming routine.  I'll admit it is comfortable and convenient, but that's all there is.  I miss the giddiness, so I think I need to wheen off before it just becomes a habit and not an enjoyment =\.  Can stress or intense boredom make you lose lust in a fetish item? 

Anyways, I'm off to bed.  Peace out.

Uncertain, unaccomplished, and unsatisfied...

Apr. 11th, 2009   12:10 AM

There was this person when I was in grade six who I was pretty close with, or at least I'd like to think we were good friends.  We shared the same hobbies, got roughly the same grades and hung out within and outside of school quite often.  I think our friendship grew apart sometime near the end of grade nine when we began making new friends and picking up different interests.  In grade twelve we were still friends but not close ones.  We knew one another, but mostly through nostalgia and memory - we wouldn't have been very much aware of each others recent activities.  When it came time to graduate from highshool he stayed back a year (or maybe half a year, I'm unsure).  Between then and now I haven't spoken with him or many of the friends we shared.  To my understanding, he has gone for training with the Toronto Emergency Medical Services (EMS), and I presume he's working with them now.  I am unsure if he's taken any collage or university courses.  I don't know how much someone in whatever position he is in would make, but to me it seems he's in a steady stage in life.  I mean, heck, the work he does is certainly important - enough to have some measure of accomplishment to it, I'd imagine.  I've been thinking on and off lately about my own position in whatever stage of life I may be in.  I've been thinking this for a lot of reasons.  One of them has to do with watching some of my friends begin to transition from one stage in life to another as they graduate from university and into, supposedly, the real world.  I'm proud for several of my close friends who are graduating - even envious - but I am also unsatisfied with myself.  One of the ways I cope with problems is to try and bridge them by association or relation.  So I've been wondering, how did my friend feel when he watched his friends graduate from highschool while he remained behind?  Since I don't know him as well as I use to, I also wonder how much uncertainty he may have felt with his future.  Did he have a plan as to what he wanted to do?  Do I have a plan?  He certainly seems to be doing fine; will I manage as well?  Right now I don't have an answer for myself and it feels as though I've wasted four (possibly five) years of my life.  I keep thinking I could have done better in the grand scheme of things had I gone into nursing when deciding what university to go into.  It feels as though this regret will burden me until I do something with my life that is even remotely related to nursing.  And yet, I've already spent four years of my life studying something that I have difficulty proving useful or relevant to anything I could ever possibly want to do.  I'd like to think I'm just channeling how much I am sick of school up till this point, but then what?  Even when I get out of school, what then?  I am so unsure of myself that I sometimes lose hope in believing that 'things could get better'. 
😵 stressed  |  ♫ Carl Orff - Carmina Burana
Link

Torley amplifies my awesome!

Apr. 8th, 2009   3:12 AM
I've known about Torley Linden since my first account on Second Life, about more than two years ago, mainly through his video tutorials.  Recently I looked into him a little more just out of curiosity, and he is really fascinating!  From his biography, he is Asian, he's got hyperacusis and Asperger's Syndrome, he works for Linden Labs (creator of SL), he's wicked with the piano and editting programs, he's really into the social media, and he love watermelons! :D  No doubt I've only scratched the surface, but that's not why I'm writing this.  He recently made a post on his page concerning the social media and the initiatives that aren't really being done to track creative practices (since it's a perpetuated cycle of 'I help you, you help me').  What he's saying, I thought, is really interesting.  Especially coming from an anthropology background, I can't help but think about the ways I've been taught to think in terms of qualitative and quantitative methods for research.  I think that in the academic circle, research and its affects on future research does not require certain forms of metric that the social media should call for.  I think this because references and citations are vital and impossible to get away with when doing research.  Bibliographies and in-text citations are excellent ways of tracking who's influence played a part in a paper.  This is somewhat in contrast to social media (media/content/info that nearly anyone can be engage in because of its high accessibility - ie. the Internet) where anonymity can be an issue.  Someone can take/use someone else's ideas or content and claim it is their own - even with minor tweaking the credits are rarely mentioned.  So how do you measure something such as creative influence?  You know it's out there, and people do it - but how do you measure it (besides asking each and every person who engages - in a long term study - how such and such as affected you to do x, y, z)?  That's extrodinarily difficult to answer, and it might require breaking down the question to more manageable pieces - which I hardly have the capacity to accomplish...which is another way of saying I don't want to think more thoroughly of it right now D:  Back on anthropology now, long term studies are very common.  It's not uncommon I hear my profs talking about returning to the field to follow up on whatever research they've been doing.  It's great because progress from your findings only helps to continue progress in your research.  It's difficult, if not impossible, to ever finish a research (particularly in anthropology where you are studying people...people don't ever just 'finish'...unless they die off, people will continue to change) so I guess that might be one leg up for the discipline - it has no end! >>''

Anyways, that was just a random blip what I was thinking when I read Torley's post.  I guess, to indirectly respond to how his tutorials have influenced my creative actions is to look at some of the things I've built.  I can't recall exactly which tutorials I've watched since some of his early videos were on very basic things which I've watched and become naturalized to doing.  Just thinking off the top of my head, his tutorial on tiny prims has helped me for a number of things I've made.  The belt I've been working on has tiny prims in it - the Pocky sticks!  I have to watch that tutorial every time I want to make tiny prims cause I never remember the path cut numbers or dimples {o','o}.  Oh!  And his tutorial on how to run multiple SL viewers at the same time helped me me a lot when I worked on multiple accounts - for modeling and building purposes.  He's got a 24min tutorial on particle editing, which I'll need to watch when I make my fist gloves xD.  So,and I'm sure I speak similarly to what others have said, Torley has been super helpful and I hope he continues amplifying my awesome :3

Btw, check out his piano music, they're fantastic arrangements.  I haven't read many commentaries for songs and music scores which made his interesting to read.  It kinda reminded me of some of the story commentaries I've read, and written for my own - but of course they're harder to follow without having read the actual story as oppose to listening and reading at the same time :P  I think I'm going to watch Fringe this summer only because he plays the opening theme and it sounds wicked (and nuuuu not also because my brother and Stephanie also watch it and say it's a really good series >:D). 
🌀 satisfied  |  ♫ Jake Shimabukuro - While My Guitar Gently Weeps
Link

Movie, comics, tv, music, and games...oh my! D:

Apr. 8th, 2009   12:13 AM
Finally watched the ending of Watchmen because someone finally uploaded a camcorder version, the last couple since its release were blank video files that were around 700Mb.  No idea how that's even done >>''.  Anyways, the file I downloaded is 1.37Gb, which seems more appropriate considering how long the movie is.  For a camcorder version the quality isn't that bad!  Only beef that there's a spot on the middle of the screen probably due to dirt.  Anyways, the ending...didn't really like it that much =\.  I've read and heard people say it's slightly different than the comic, but the effect you get remains the same.  I didn't really think so.  I don't understand why the movie would take out the exploding aliens...and replace it with explosions partially caused by Doctor Manhattan.  He leaves the Earth in the comic regardless, so it's not like they needed a reason for him to leave in the movie.  The part Silk Spectre II shoots Ozymandias was kinda lame - because in the comic there's dialogue between Ozy and Night Owl II where they joke about Ozy being able to catch bullets.  So the little blip of humour was replaced with thrill (for people who haven't read the comic).  I didn't really feel as emotional as I did when reading the comics during the part where Doctor M. kills Rorschach, but it might just be because the actor wasn't crying when he says 'Do it!'  The part where Night Owl II lashes out at Ozy was a nice add in, I thought that it was one thing better than in the comics - cause in the comic N.O. is way more accepting what Ozy has done...and he goes and sleeps with SS2 naked (>>'' which I still think is lile wtf...millions of people just died and you're gonna go bang our girlfriend?)  Whatever, I'm glad I finally got to finish the movie even though I'm not to happy with how it was done. 

And another thing, WTF!  They killed off Kutner (actor = Kal Penn, one half of Harold & Kumar) in newest episode of House MD!  It pisses me off for two reasons.  The first is because he is one of the least developed characters in the series, but also one of the most colourful.  He can be silly, but wittiful - as demonstrated in the episode prior to his demise.  Though he wasn't particularily an important character (because we don't know much about him) he was certainly one of my favourites.  The second reason is because it happened unexpectedly.  There's nothing that builds up to why he kills himself, and worse there will probably never be an explaination.  This is in contrast to Amber...at least she died with a wicked story plot, not to mention a great backstory to the entire series.  Bah, they should've killed off Wilson intead =\.

Yay! More bad news!  I finally found the opportunity to download and listen to Chris Cornell's newest solo album 'Scream' (released March 10, 2009).  I don't like it.  Great artist (was singer/drummer in Soundgarden and singer in Audioslave), but awkward collaboration with Timbaland (hip hop producer).  Cornell's last two solo albums were really 'what all he's about' - maybe to apoint where some of the tracks were kinda boring.  This third solo album, I donno, just doesn't really sit right for me.  I think the tracks were just vocal with acoustics, I'd be happy.  But all the funky heavy beats with his wide vocal range is just kinda weird and hard to listen to.  In one of the tracks the sound of a door opening/closing and squeaking is sampled and looped >>'' can anyone say wtf? 

In other news, the DSi is out in North America...which makes my DS Lite pretty worthless.  I got it for RayRi last summer, but kept it since I hadn't given it to her before we broke up.  I played it for maybe two months, and then on and off, and then never at all.  I should have gotten rid of it way sooner! ><  Now I'm thinking I should either try to eBay/Kijiji/Craiglist it or trade it in for store credits at an EB Games.  I also have a PSP which is in the exact same category as my DS Lite - since PSP Slim has been out for over a year.  Agh!  Must get rid of both!  But then the issue becomes, what the hell am I going to do with store credits if I trade it in?  I don't place console games...not even the Wii my brother bought a couple years ago.  I think I'm going to have a hard time selling it on online listings since it's sorta obsolete =\.  Maybe I can give someone the store credits - summer is sorta here and there are a lot of birthdays coming up. 

Oh, I added some new stuff to the fun belt I've been working on (and mentioned about on March 31).  I added a rolled up comic book and a NES Zapper!  Here are some pics...

So far the belt is 83 prims.  The comic book cover is from Incontinent Student Bodies #3, an ABDL furry comic written by Karis and drawn by Marci.  I couldn't find anything good to put on the back (inner part of the roll), so I used my old store sign texture xD  That's my old av from my old account getting killed by a giant mouse trap!  Happyfeet helped me photoshop the image I took ^^.  I'm not really liking the white hocks/holders I'm using, but I can't find a good texture or colour that works well.  I want to add a box of Vitasoy cause I grew up drinking that stuff, but I can't find a good scan/picture of it - and the new box design is kinda lame (cause it's now enriched with calcium).  I could bug Jully to help me scan it, since I've seen her drinking ones with the old design, but I doubt she has a scanner.  =\ I could crop the image from various sources...but I sorta stink at that.  Other than that I haven't thought of what else to add to the belt.  But, I do want to make some new shoes (even though the sculpt shoes I'm wearing now only costed L$1, they're also scripted to change colours!).  I want to make velcro shoes, something a little more kid-ish ^^.  I also want to make a sculptie puzzle piece...but the process of learning how to sculp has been going very slowly >>''  If I am able to make it I wonder if it'll be a hot sell since i have yet to see anyone else sell them - let alone make/use them.  I haven't really thought of what I'll do with the puzzle pieces, but I have considered making wings based off of them or some sorta steampunk/cyberpunk backback thingie!  
😑 blah  |  ♫ Beat Crusaders - Moon on the Water
Link

I <3 Cake

Apr. 6th, 2009   3:32 AM
Hoi, just got home not to long ago.  >>'' Though I showered before leaving, it doesn't feel right to sleep on my bed since I still feel sorta dirty/sticky/unclean.  I doubt I'll get much sleep since the sofa isn't long enough to stretch out and the clothes I'm wearing is uncomfortable to lay in.  Anyways, met up with K-Dawg at around 9:30pm to watch I Love You, Man.  Total bromance flick, but really hilarious!  Most of the humour comes from Paul Rudd and his brutal word play - plenty of *facepalm* moments.  The plot is really predictable, but it's the kind that finishes appropriately.  Had it finished differently I'd have been surprised.  Happy endings are always ftw. By the time we left the theater it was quarter till twelve.  I reminded him I wanted cake :3  I had a craving for cake after chatting with Stephanie earlier today.  We've been chatting more often lately and I can't complain since my initiative to start conversations is shit.  I'm really glad we don't really take things seriously when we chat - I don't have to worry much about the things I say since I'm usually just teasing, where as for other people I have to consciously be the person they know me as.  I have a feeling my desire to be younger has made my chatting more playful in undertone.  So back to cake...there weren't many places open that sold any - Raba, Metro, and 7 West.  We went to Raba since Metro was like 10min walk away, and 7 West is expensive.  I can't really look at food from Raba with a straight face - something about the whole 'convenient store feeling'.  They had around five little rectangular cakes to choose from; I picked out the mocha flavoured one since chocolate usually messes up my stomach.  We then walked back to his place ^^; I only whined once about how cold it was.  Mmmmm, the cake wasn't as horrible as I imagined it would have been.  It was a standard layer cake with mocha icing between and around the visible surfaces.  K-Dawg pointed out that it smelt like soy sauce, which I couldn't disagree with o,O  But it still tasted like normaly tasty cake.  We cuddled on his bed and watched a couple of episodes of The Office, season four.  I didn't whore up his kitty like last time because it wasn't allowed into the room :(  I got to try on a yellow sleeper with truck prints on them that K-Dawg was growing out of, that was really sweet.  I've never worn a sleeper before and it was really comfortable and fuzy and snug ^^.  I might have to consider getting one for my own sometime!  LOL, oh and I didn't leak on his sheets unlike last time.  I had on a Tranquility ATN for most of the night and got changed into an Attends at some point.  Mmmm cocoa butter smells really good.  I stayed till around 2:45am-ish.  He Zipcar'd me home in a spiffy looking blue Mini Cooper :D.  Anywhoo, I'm gonna finish it here cause I can barely keep my eyes open =.=  Have a fun and safe trip in NYC+Boston K-Dawg, I'll bug you about coming to the munch when you get back >:3
😌 content  |  ♫ Murs and 9th Wonder - Yesterday & Today
Link

14 pieces of sushi later...

Apr. 5th, 2009   6:49 PM
My dad and I went to Mikado Japanese Restaurant (114 Laird Dr, Toronto) for dinner the other night cause my mom and brother were doing there own thing.  When it comes to sushi restaurants in Toronto, 600 of them are run by Chinese people - only 30 are run by Japanese people (I read that from somewhere, so I'm not pulling numbers out of my butt >>'').  So naturally you'd expect a difference in quality between the two types of owners, right?  Mikado is the third Jap run sushi restaurant I've been to.  There's this one place up in Richmond Hill in a plaza by East Beaver Creek and Hwy 7 that I have zero recollection of eating at, but my parents say I've been.  And there's this really expensive and sorta minimalistic place in downtown called Hiro - when my family left the restaurant we were still hungry but wallets empty!  Mikado is definitely cheaper than Hiro, but in terms of good eats, they're about the same - though Hiro is a bit more exotic in terms of variety.

So anyways, Mikado has been around for since 1978 and its chef and owner is Keiichi Masuda - he's the VP of the Japanese Restaurant Association of Canada...must hold some credential to dining tastiness right?  I couldn't tell how small the restaurant is, but when you step through the sliding door there are 2 tables that seat 2 people, 1 table that seats 5, and a sushi bar that sits 6.  There's also room in the back which I have no clue how large or small since it's completely out of view - but my guess is it probably holds a little over a dozen, based on the in and out traffic while I was there.  Also, they do a lot of take-out.  The interior is very authentic Japanese looking, kinda cozy too.  My dad read from somewhere that owner use to be a bit of a Nazi on etiquettes and customs.  Like, he'd bark at people for not dipping a sushi fish side down into soy sauce.  Or like get pissed off when people ask for wasabi, cause you're suppose to trust the chef to put the right amount into the sushi.  And apparently you're not suppose to rub splinters off the wooden chopsticks >>''.  Inside the menu there are pictures and pullout brochures of what you should and should not do - no lies.  There are more pages on the menu of that kinda stuff then there is of food!  Oh yea, and you're not allowed to use cellphones in the restaurant; there are literally a bunch of stickers put up everywhere.

Here's what we ordered...
Agedashi Dofu - $4.90
- 3 pieces of deep fried tofu that sits in a broth.  Came with 3 pieces of veggies also deep fried.  Tiny proportion.  Not as good as other places =\

Nasumiso - $3.90
- A bowl of cubed eggplant that's been broiled with soybean sauce then topped with sesame seeds.  The glaze is sweet and tasty, but it masks the eggplants nutty flavour.  Proportion is much - maybe like a dozen pieces. 

Kani Croquette - $4.90
- 2 pieces of breaded and creamed crab that's fried - essentially a crab cake.  This one was interesting since it used cream cheese as its binding ingredient.  Pretty tasty, but over priced. 

Green Salad - $3.50
- This is a complete rip off, don't ever get it.  We only got it because we ordered a combo that came with a salad, so we figured each of us should have a salad.  Plus, the server asked if we wanted a salad and we didn't know the price.  It's essentially the same salad you get when you order set meals that include soup, salad, and ice cream. 

Take - $24.90
- 14 pieces of sushi that include tuna, shrimp, yellowtail, salmon, fatty tuna, oilfish, octopus, and white fish).  Six of those pieces are spicy-tuna and vege rolls (3 each) that are the rolled up sushi.  The rest are the ones where the rice is a ball with fish sitting on top if it.  The fish tasted pretty fresh and they were sliced thicker than most places I've been to. 

Ebi Fry Complete Dinner - $23.80
- Breaded and deep fried jumbo shrimp, not to be confused with tempura.  I don't recall how many pieces there were, but I think there were ten and it's served with something that tastes like HP sauce.  It came with a bit of potato salad (Japanese people are crazy for that stuff, no lie), and a potato tater.  Since it was a combo it also came with soup, salad, rice, and ice cream.  I thought they were deep fried pretty good, might also be because I haven't had stuff fried with panco very often. 

2 pcs of Uni Sushi
- I can't find it on the a-la-carte menu so I donno how much it costs, but they're generally pretty expensive (maybe like $6-8).  Uni is sea urchin and my dad always orders it at sushi restaurants.  I'm not really a big fan, but I had a piece heh.  The texture is hard to get use to since it's slimy - sorta like a melting soft ice cream - but it tastes delish, sweet but oceany.

In the end we were about 85% full, which isn't exactly reasonable for the amount of money spent.  Sure it looks like we ordered a lot, but the proportions were really small.  We joked at each other about going next door to Olde Yorke Fish & Chips to get ourselves full.  The place is REALLY popular.  Before and after leaving Mikado there was a long line up at the sit down entrance and the take-out entrance.  Guess I'll have to try it out sometime ^^.  Anyways, I give Mikado a 3/5.  The sushi was great but the cooked stuff was just okay, and the service was really slow - from start to finish we were there for an hour and a half.


Anyways...ew, Andy Murray (#4) beat Novak Djokovic (#3) 6-2, 7-5 in the Miami Masters (an ATP World Tour Masters 1000 event).  Murray plays really great tennis (baseliner), but I really can't stand the way he looks @_@.  I've never been a big fan of dudes with curly hair, so that's one reason.  The other is his smile.  When he smiles with teeth there's just something really off >>''.  I only caught the last set this afternoon, and Djokovic seemed kinda sluggish.  He was serving really well against Roger Federer (#2) in the semi-final.  And omg, Federer made 47 unforced errors!  He even smashed his racquet (3:50 into the video), which he hasn't done since...I think since when he was a junior.  He's losing it xD  I don't feel much confidence in him reclaiming the number one spot.  Plus, I sorta don't want him to be number one - he's was number one for 237 consecutive weeks, which ended last year against Rafael Nadal.  Nadal is so young...and hot {o'.'o}.  Can't get enough of that ass LOL.

Last thoughts...I'm gonna watch 'I Love You, Man' at Cineplex Varsity tonight with K-Dawg.  Just found out what the movie is about not long ago.  Heh, looks funny and something gay people would watch, which is pretty jokes cause he's gay and I'm bi xD.  Gonna use my 2 for 1 ticket to save some bucks...then spend it on cake afterwards!  If you're reading this Stephanie I hope this makes you very jealous ^-^  *nom nom nom*  Only downer is it's suppose to be cold and snowy/rainy tonight.
🥱 bored  |  ♫ Styles P - Switch My Style

Blah...extensions are no fun

Apr. 4th, 2009   12:32 AM
I survived the week...sorta >.o  The assignment for ANTD15 that was suppose to be due on Thur got extended to Saterday.  Only two poeple, from a class of twelve, got it done (good job Jully!  >>'' sucks balls though, since you pulled an all nighter to get it done...).  I had half of it done =\ so technically I don't really get to celebrate until the end of Saterday.  I really want to try and get it done by afternoon so I can go out at night.  Hopefully I can hook up with K-Dawg this weekend before he goes to NYC + Boston until the 10th.  I sorta felt bad for Larry, my ANTC67 prof.  I missed Mondays class so I went to this mornings (-.- it was brutal getting out of bed to make the 9am lecture) and there was, like, less than twenty people.  T_T and it's the last week.  And the course isn't being offered next year.  >>'' only twenty people showed up...damn.  I guess the crazy downpour,  assignment due dates, and early morning lec contributed to such low attendance.  Oh well, I thought it was a great course (Foundations of Epidemiology), and Larry Sawchuk is one of the best anthro profs in my opinion.  I think a lot of people underestimate him cause he's so flexable with just about everything.  But once he's going with something he really knows, he's really enthusiastic about it.  Then when you do one of his exams or assignments o.O it's like wow overwhelming...but fair.  He makes you think critically - but not in a sense that he challenges things, but putting things into application.  Which is probably why I like medical anthropology more than cultural anthropology/archaeology.  Med anthro is more applied, methodological, and more often than not there is a rationale.  In cultural anthro,/archy the perspective of almost everything is challenged (ie. roles of power, authenticity and identity, burial contexts analysis).  It's interesting in that it makes you think about stuff that's naturalized and mundane in society...but it's almost half pointless to me.  Questions more often than not are answered with more questions - which fustrate me to no end.  Concepts aren't so much applied, but instead categorized - what's more, cultural anthro. research is relevent almost exclusively to the academic circle.  You can take it out of the circle and try to apply it to real world situations (aka Applied Anthro), but more often than not this route isn't persude. As you can probably tell, school hasn't rubbed well on me lately.  D:< Grrrr!

Being to relaxed...Oh noes! D:

Mar. 31st, 2009   9:42 PM
Wow...the last episode of Soul Eater (ep. 51) was pretty disappointing.  But then again, you could have easily guessed the ending several episodes prior...but I just sorta expected a little more action.  The final fight scene between Maka and Ashura was pretty cliche in terms of the genre.  Maka gets her ass whooped.  Ashura monologues.  Maka powers up and performs a new technique (goes into an unconscious state and develops 'weapon' abilities).  Maka starts owning.  Ashura makes a comeback.  Maka weakens and regains consciousness - but gets suddenly stronger.  Maka shatters Ashura's rationale and perception of the world by telling him how wrong he is - big monologue, real snoozer.  Maka punches Ashura.  Ashura shatters and explodes.  I dislike Maka even more after watching this episode.  What's worse is she can't redeem herself since the series is over.  Maybe if I read the manga, since it's different, it'll change my opinions =\  But overall, quite disappointing. 

Only part of the episode that made me smirk...


I also caught up with The Office, season five.  I watched episode 19 (which I think is the season finale, but I'm not to sure) and the ending was also pretty 'wtf o.O'  In the episode prior, 'New Boss', I thought it was a really interesting twist - a new character that's the complete opposite to Michael.  And the ending of that episode, Michael quitting the company, was really surprising.  The cliffhanger sorta died though in episode 19.  The plausibility behind the two weeks notice was fine and all, but the ending of the episode, when Pam quits with Michael to start his own company, I thought was lame.  Sure it'll open up new plots, but I sorta like keeping the setting in 'the office'.  I'm probably just to familiar with the setting and attached to the characters, but I hope I don't dislike season six based on how I am now =\

I've been working on a new belt in Second Life!  I'm so excited even though I should be stressing out my mind :P  I don't have classes tomorrow since my osteology course ended last week - until the exam in April.  So I'll worry about homework tomorrow.  Here are some pics...

Yea, the front buckle part is sorta similar to the previous belt I made (there should be a pic posted of it somewhere).  But rather than the evolution face, I'm using a Fisher-Price See n' Say picture that I edited ^^.  I've seen plenty of toy belts with fun stuff attach to them, so I want to make my own to reflect my character (that's why the arrow is pointing to a mouse, duh).  It's more or less going to be a toddlers belt (note the animal faces :P).  So far I've only had time to make a box of chocolate Pocky, but I'll be adding more fun stuff when I get the time to make them - like a NES Zapper gun, a plushie, maybe a rolled up comic book, and other childish toys ^^).  I just hope I can fit the things I like around my waist D:  So far it's 42 prims.  (Oops, I captured Cameron Kuhn in the last pic.  Hope he doesn't mind.)  After this belt project I want to make my own fists gloves (attempt number 2!).  I like the ones I have right now just fine, but I want something more personal - something I can call my own.  I'm thinking of having it emit particles (sorta like the gloves in Perfect World), rather than moving textures.  Also, I want to try out SLoft to make sculpties for the gloves! :D  Should be fun and interesting!

Besides the relaxing that I shouldn't be doing (since I still have three assignments to do), I've been bouncing emails with my program supervisor.  I am quite content with the responses to the questions I've been asking.  I know I won't be graduating this year since I've haven't been taking full course-loads since first year; thus making me behind everyone in my year.  I don't mind it so much.  To graduate, one of the requirements is to have 20 full credits.  If I pass all my classes this semester I'll have 17 full credits in total.  Also, I'll only be short one stats course and one science credit course in order to fulfill my program requirement (I'm doing a Specialist program in Medical Anthropology).  The good news is next fall semester the stats course I need (ANTC35) which wasn't offered this year, will be available.  The even better news is it's also a science credit course - so two requirements for the price of one.  Even if I pass the stats course in the fall I'll only have 17.5 credits.  So I've decided to pick up another program - either a major or a minor in Health Studies.  I only need to take two health studies courses (both of which are offered this summer) to meet all the requirements for the minor.  I haven't looked into the major, but I may have to since I'll still need some credits to reach 20 - and a major is better than a minor (duh).  I probably should have planned my academic life a little better (even though UTSC sucks balls in terms of the courses that are required vs. those courses actually being offered).  The only reason I chose to do a 'Specialist' program (which honestly I still question wtf it means since most universities in Canada just have major/minor - which everyone is familiar with) is because I was a lazy butt head and didn't want to complicate my life with a double major or a major and a minor.  The specialist program was the most straightforward in terms of its requirements, and honestly didn't take much planning.  But doing a double major, you have to work out which courses will fit into your schedule and it doesn't leave much room for electives.  So it has its ups and downs cause I've actually found some of the electives I've taken quite interesting.  But anyways, I'll need to mull over my academic future after this week ends.  I really need to find work this summer cause I'm getting really broke, but I also want to take courses cause I don't want to stay in school forever.

Stress makes bitter dumpling...

Mar. 30th, 2009   11:00 PM
I've been talking to a couple of school friends lately cause we're all in the same stress boat.  Everyone's pretty similar in that they've left their term papers to do at the last minute.  Some part of me finds comforting in knowing that others are suffering in the same way as I am.  But then another part of me gets really jealous at those who are able to finish assignments in a day or two even under this pressure - when it takes me probably twice as long to finish the same assignment.  I tend to bottle up my envies, but today while having coffee with Jully I nearly cried cause I was astonished and jealous at how quickly she finished an assignment that took me, literally, the whole weekend.  I'm happy for her, but it just annoys the crap out of me that I can't do better.  Maybe I'm competitive, or maybe I have issues with inferiority (or possibly both), but I'm having a lot of trouble finding my ground.  It's like I said, I find comfort in asking people where they stand in an assignment.  It's especially comforting when I've accomplished more than said person - yes, that comes off wrong in many ways, but it makes me feel better regardless >>.  But asking people is a double-edged sword.  If I find out the person has done more in a smaller amount of time than me then I just end up kicking myself.  I could just stop asking, but the feeling of stalemate is so unsettling.  Anyways. I just had to clear my conscious a little.  I don't think I ever remember feeling this stressed about school before (or so much so that its made me cry about it in private).  I really can't wait until school ends (and damn you Merry for finishing all your classes and only having exams to look forward to!  >_<***).
