Dentist and restocking...

Mar. 27th, 2009   10:28 PM
Bleh, I hate going to the dentist; always have since I was a kid.  One reason I dislike going is because I have a heart malformation called Ventricular Septal Defect - it's basically a hole in the wall (septum) between its two lower chambers (the ventricles).  People with VSD have to take antibiotics before surgical procedures on the mouth or throat to help prevent endocarditis.  When I was little the antibiotics were these gianormous Dr. Mario pills and I had to take around a dozen of them throughout a certain number of  hours.  It was pretty awful since they were really difficult to swallow.  Nowadays I only need to take four capsules an hour before the appointment - might have something to do with my age or the dosage, I donno.  They're not as hard to take now that I'm older, but it's still a really strange feeling.  I don't think I'll ever get use to the feeling of popping pills - >>'' even the little, round, sugar-coated Tylenols I get squirmish when taking. 

But besides that, a trip to the dentist is rarely a pleasant one.  I've been going to the same dentist for as long as I remember - he's my uncle's (who's also a dentist) friend.  Great guy, but I can't help but feel sad for him cause he makes people feel uncomfortable...but helping them at the same time.  I think the monies might be the only reward to his occupation.  My bill was suppose to come to $100 since I also got some x-rays done (because my jaw is a little sore) but he only charged me $70 ^^.  Sucks that I can't claim it on my dads, now, non-existent benefits plan, so it came out of my wallet >.o  I'll have to look into my schools dental plan!

So the whole ordeal took about 25 min, but I could swear his assistant was chatting with me for a good five minutes of it.  Afterwards I took the opportunity to restock my diapers since I had the car :D  I went to my usual place, Diapers N Kids Wear Factory Outlet.  The same little lady was behind the cash register like last time.  A bit of a bummer since she slaughters the English language.  I haven't seen Ali Khan since last last time I went, probably minding the other location.  Another bummer was that they no longer carry Attends Slip Regular - 10 Super Plus.  They didn't have it the last time I went (see 01/23/09 post).  So the last time I bought it was all they had left of them.  They had an Attends 9, but it was high up on the shelf at the back so I couldn't really make out the description - but I'm pretty certain it was a large.  Instead, they had Tranquility ATN, which were new to the store.  I've heard really great reviews of them and the price tag I could afford ($10 for a package of 12 diapers), so I got three of those and one package of Attends Slip - 10 Super Plus (which were $28 for a package of 28 diapers).  I think the Attends Slip are growing on me.  It irks me that they aren't fitted briefs but they hold a fair amount and are convenient to get on and off when changing in public.  Oh!  And I also got two samplers each of a Tena Tena Absorbent Pads - Night Super (which look ridiculously enormous) and Tena Ultra Brief (based on the last samples I got of these, I didn't like them.  But who's to complain if they're free :3).

When I got home I tested out the Tranquility ATN and they live up to the hype that I've read from forums.  They hold more than Attends,  I've tested this quite thoroughly but I'll spare the details - 'insert imagination here' (lol).  Plus they're plastic back!  It's getting harder and harder to find plastic covered diapers without having to buy from online :(  The only things that irk me is 1) the colour of the inner padding.  It's really cushiony and comfortable even sitting in it when wet for an extended period, but the material is this funky salmon colour @_@.  It sounds like no big deal, and probably isn't, but the colour just doesn't sit right with me - especially when 2) the spongy/cushiony stuff chaffs after being thoroughly soaked.  The weight of the absorbent materials gets to heavy and it begins to bulk up at one area of the diaper - a definite sign for a change, but still, not a feeling I really enjoy.  You can sorta see little pockets of missing padding from the front because they're orangey.  It kinda looks like poop stains, I kid you not lol!  So, those aren't the worse things to complain about - not to say I've found anything else that I dislike about them - so I'm quite pleased with these.  I just hope the store doesn't run out of them because the shelf only had a couple more packages and I didn't really notice any boxes of them along the walls. 

Hoi, back to my homework ^^; Peace out.

😌 content  |  ♫ Mother Mother - Ball Cap
Link

Due date overload...

Mar. 25th, 2009   9:48 PM
I need to survive until the end of next Friday but the more I think about it the more increasingly difficult it seems.  Tomorrow I have an appointment with my program supervisor - she couldn't answer my questions through email since she needed to look at my transcript...which I have a feeling will be embarrassing since I bombed one of her classes.  Afterwards I have a seminar class (D15), for which I've only read one of four articles...doubt I'll contribute much to the discussion.  Then after that my weekend begins, but I have two final papers due Monday and another final paper due Tuesday!  So my extended weekend will be occupied with nothing but research and writing...I have a feeling I might not make the deadlines without finishing half-ass'd.  Next Thursday I have to hand in my final bundle of journal responses for the seminar class - which means I have to respond to three weeks of readings (approx. four articles a week - majority of which I haven't read).  And finally, next Friday I have an assignment to hand in involving statistics...which I suck at >.o  Man, I'll be happy if I make it past Tuesday, I'm so worried :*(

On a slightly brighter note, the marks are available online for the osteology presentation I did today - with Lindsay and Jen.  We got perfect and the presentation was worth 25% of our overall grade.  So all that's left is a final exam that's worth 35% of the overall grade.  I can't say I really enjoyed working with the two of them at all.  They're really nice people with charming personalities, but working with them I am not happy with.  We divided up the work (unlike our last assignment as a group, the osteobiographic report) but a majority of my contributions weren't incorporated into the presentation...I took four articles from a pile of print outs Lindsay had, and took the time to summarize them, but none of them were used.  My only contribution was finding pictures and speaking a section of the presentation.  Plus, we stayed in school yesterday until 8pm...most of the time I was either kept out of the loop or was watching them waste time talking or goofing off.  So, I feel cheated of my time and unaccomplished with the great mark =\  I kind of wish I partnered with Monica now.  I probably wouldn't have gotten perfect, but at least I would have had some say in the work, plus it would have been nice to have gotten an opportunity to get to know Monica better. 

🌀 scared  |  ♫ Eksi Ekso - Russian Excuse

Happy Birthday Happyfeet Hax!

Mar. 24th, 2009   12:18 AM
D: Noes!  You're becoming an old bunny!  Soon your teeth will all fall out and you'll be wearing pop bottle glasses o_O!!  Well, till that day comes, its been really something since I first met you - even the times when I was being a douch >.o  Happy birthday Happyfeet and I hope you like you're present (even though I donno where you'll put it...or when you'll ever need'em ^^;;)  But I know you like them enormous so enjoy :3


It's sculpted boobies!!!  Attach to a torso.  I donno what you'd call it...a statue?  Some sorta giant cusion thingie?  Ah well.  They're five prims.  Each boob is actually a tear drop sculptie I've had for a long time.  I bought the sculpt map for the torso recently, and it came with the skin/nipple texture.  The remaining two prims are invisible pose balls and they're positioned infront of each tit.  I made a simple static animation using Avimator.  *grope*

Last last Christmas (2007, cause I was grinding on Perfect World in 2008) I gave Happyfeet, while I was still using my old account, a miniature version of himself!  I pretty much modified and shrunk the Kani av and made it into a huggable plushie - complete with Happyfeet spectacles, hair, and a plainer version of a blue shirt he use to wear!


And here I am just being a snapshot whore :P

~ awww cute xD

Second munch! *nom nom nom*

Mar. 22nd, 2009   4:58 PM
Last night was really fun!  In fact this whole weekend has been good to me, though I probably should buckle down and get a tiny bit of school work done :P  I have a feeling that won't happen till late tonight - if I still have energy cause I want to go to the gym after dinner D:

Anyways, I took a nap at 3pm yesterday cause I didn't get much sleep the night before.  I had set my alarm to wake me up at 5pm but I slept right threw it.  Probably should have known better since I need three alarm clocks to wake me on school days {o'.'o}.  I woke up at 6:20pm and was like "oh shit!  i'm gonna be totally late!"  Luckily my mom was able to drive me to the subway station, which saved me thirty minutes of walking and busing.  I quickly got ready (got padded in an Attends Slip Regular over an Attends Slip like last time) and was out the door in like three minutes @.@  Taking the subway and walking to the pub took about twenty minutes, so I arrived a little shy of 7pm.  I normally like coming on time so that I can sit in the middle where it's normally more chatty, so it was a bit of a bummer to have to sit at the end where it was less so.  Gradually as some people left I moved closer and closer to the middle >:D  A good number of people showed up ^^ - somewhere between 15-17.  I recognized about a dozen of them (now if I could only put names to those faces rather than "I'm pretty sure your name starts with..." xD I'll get better at it eventually!).  I think three of the people there were from the states.  I know for certain that one lady is from Rochester, she was sweet but a little shy heh.  There were three Asians not including me!  Two I recognized from last munch and the other I'm pretty certain is a furry ^^.  My bill came to $20, which wasn't bad for a burger, a sprite, and a Moosehead - yesh, made good use of the padding hehe.  At 9pm-ish Ricky invited everyone over to his place which was literally a two minute walk away.  I thought it was really cozy to fit everyone into a two bedroom apartment since not everyone was packed in a single room ^^.  It was really nostalgic too!  Cause the very first munch I went to was last years March and afterwards a bunch of people went to Kevydl's place (which was right next to the pub).  It was pretty much the same kind of atmosphere and vibe, but if you could put yourself in my shoes it was the very first time I had ever been around other ABDLs.  I was both excited and nervous, and pretty much clammed up for most of that night.  What's even nuttier was that as soon as we walked through his front door a couple of us whipped off our pants and just got comfy in their diapers! xD  Oh yeah, you know it...I totally did the same thing this time around - stripped off my pants after entering Ricky's place.  I had a really great time and got to chat with mostly everyone.  I watched Shiro sketch two of his characters - some species of bird and a chimera - in only a couple of minutes which I thought was amazing (I really like watching time-lapse videos of artists at work, so watching him in action was wicked).  xD Ricky is a really lucky boy!  I remember he talked about making his room cars themed last munch so I got to see it yesterday.  His room's got a car bedframe, the walls are painted blue and red with racetrack stripes, and he's got Diego (Dora the Explorer's pimp) bedsheets.  And he's got a loving daddy which is totally priceless ~.^  I'm completely jealous haha, much like other ABDLs I'm sure xD.  Ricky gave out packets of Spider-Man and Winnie the Pooh stickers as party favours which were cuite.  I left at midnight and hitched a ride from my brother cause he was leaving from the UTSG lab - made sure to change out of my droopy diaper, of course :P.  Good times, good times. 
😌 content  |  ♫ Chris Garneau - So Far
Link

Eep, morning soon D:

Mar. 21st, 2009   5:18 AM
*stealthy mode off*  o.o  Sooo, I just got home from a really wicked night out.  Don't really see much point in sleeping since sunrise is in two hours.  Plus I haven't showered yet and my hair is gelled so I can't sleep comfortably - and showering would wake everyone in the house.  The big 'one-month after-birth' dinner my aunt Liana and uncle Viktor threw wasn't bad.  Held at the Ruby Chinese Restaurant in Scarborough with ten tables (ten people sitting at each) and loads and loads of formality and hospitality.  The bill came to around three grand, which is like three hundred bucks a table for ten courses.  I thought the food wasn't bad, but my dad disagrees - he criticized pretty much everything >>.  Whatever.  The whole ordeal was from 7pm to 10:30pm, although technically we didn't start eating until 8pm.  Their baby is looking really healthy and a little more lively than the last time I saw him.  He was very good and slept through most of the dinner.  I bared witness to a burping after his liquid diet...it was pretty gross.

Anyways, my night really began afterwards!  My brother had some birthday party to go to at some club or whatever, so I hitched a ride off of him to downtown.  I got dropped off and legged it to K-Dawg's ghetto apartment complex.  Shady neighbourhood I must say.  The first thing I saw when I got dropped off were two parked police cars with their siren lights on, by the intersection.  We walked to West 7 after I buzzed him down - it was friggin cold and I whined most of the time :P.  Hellz, his jacket wasn't even zipped up and he didn't think it was chilly (and I thought I had a reasonable tolerance to the weather i.i).  He had the worst cough going on all night (the kind were it sounds like a lung might fly out the mouth).  Everytime he coughed I would say 'ew, contagious' and literally kept it up the whole night :3.  Anyways, I can't say I remember how long we stayed at West 7, maybe an hour and a half?  I had hot chocolate cause all forms of liquor was uber expensive ._.''  We walked around afterwards then went back to his place.  I sorta lost track of time thereafter...I blame his damn kitten Dx it's so friggin' adorable!  I was being a total cuddle whore with him for atleast an hour.  We chatted, listened to some tunes (thanks for burning the two Mother Mother albums btw!), and watched some random videos.  At some point we padded up.  Haha, in my head I was thinking "smoooooth, breaking the ice by subtly going to your stash in the closet ~.^"  We cuddled up on his bed and watched Family Guy Presents Stewie Griffin: The Untold Story - cause I had no real recollection of watching it before.  I'll leave out the details, but my hair was pretty ruffled after a few hours xD.  And that little leak I made on his sheets grew to a relatively large patch o.O  I felt pretty bad about that...but he knows it was worth it (lol).  Anyways, he Zipcar'd me home in an ugly 2008 Honda Civic Si.  Here's some pictures of his kitty!  ^^

🐇 bouncy  |  ♫ Mother Mother - Burning Pile

SL-ness :3

Mar. 20th, 2009   2:49 AM
:D spent the last hour watching clips of Handyman's Corner from The Red Green Show with a couple of people at Happyfeet's sim.  It's pretty cool how they did it cause there's this tv you can get that can stream YouTube videos by telling it the ULR.  So it's pretty cozy watching hilarious shenanigans and such with a bunch of people ^^.  Then they began logging off one by one heh and I'm not sleepy yet >>''.  So I went to Cub Conclave, a babyfur sim.  Woohoo for free swag!  Corri Dawodu is opening a new store and he's got two posters out as a sneak peak.  He's got a little contest going on stating if you can find the typos in either you can get a free Cub Harness and Snugggies Diapers when he releases them.  From the looks of it they're both loaded with some fun scripts and it didn't really take much effort to find the mistakes - so WIN! :3  The diaper market in SL is kinda competative in my perspective.  Cause once you've got a good one chances are you'll stick with it till something better comes along; which isn't often since most people just recycle old functions and sell for cheaper.  The first diaper I bought on my old account was one of Bob Gupte's Coolie Diapers - which back then was one of the best so everyone had them.  A few months before switching games (to Perfect World) I bought one of Musuko Massiel's CKC Diapers.  Personally, I totally love them cause everything is customizable and there are a bunch of interactive things you can rez.  Really talented scripter from when I use to know him ;).  No real clue as to what I'll do with Corri's Snugggies since I doubt I'll wear them unless they have a hide function ^^;;.  I lost all my babyfur contacts on SL so I don't hang around them anymore @_@.  Heh, anyways, now I'm just loafing around Furgate with Wolf Brennen playing cat and mouse (even though she's a wolf/dog) and getting nibbled at xD.

Ohz!  I've finished my Mini-Ragnarok!  Dead simple, nothing fancy to it.  It's 70 prims, I think like five of them are sculpties.  Lookie...


The belt I'm wearing I made a couple of days ago. 

I took the evolution texture from an actual watch that I'm kinda interested in getting cause it's so jokes.  Yes, those are veins coming out of the belt and attaching to my belly :P  I later wanted to shrink the belt and make it an upper arm band attachment instead, but a lot of the prims were as small as they could go without major modifications.  Cause I wanted to make a new belt with a bunch of things I like hanging off of it (mostly things I have in my Roo avatars' pouch - like a box of Pooky, an NES Zapper gun, a companion cube, and a few other things I'd have liked to add).  But from the looks of it, it's not going to happen until I can think of a way around the dilemma D:

Oh!  I added buttons to the strap of my veinolin case :D


And here's some snapshots I took to show off the cute poses my Animation Override does ^^  (do those gloves contrast to much? lol)


The next thing I want to build, which I haven't though entirely through of, is something to do with Wonderflonium.  It's straight out of Dr. Horrible's Sing-Along Blog (yes, I watched the movie again...probably making it the sixth time).

Aside from that, I'd also like to atleast start the final assignment for ANTC67.  If I'm gonna get buggered with stats sometime, it might as well be this weekend since I can counter it with the bit of fun I'll be having at night ^^.  Hooray for optimism! 


😄 cheerful  |  ♫ Damien Rice - Amie

What's Your Personality Type?

Mar. 18th, 2009   11:48 PM
You Are An ISFJ
The Nurturer

You have a strong need to belong, and you very loyal.
A good listener, you excel at helping others in practical ways.
In your spare time, you enjoy engaging your senses through art, cooking, and music.
You find it easy to be devoted to one person... a partner who you do special things for.

In love, you express your emotions through actions.
Taking care of someone is how you love them. And you do it well!

At work, you do well in a structured environment. You complete tasks well and on time.
You would make a good interior designer, chef, or child psychologist.

How you see yourself: Competent, dependable, and detail oriented

When other people don't get you, they see you as: Boring, dominant, and stuck in a rut
What's Your Personality Type?

Interesting.  I had a little difficulty deciding on a few questions since some only felt half applicable - and there were only two answers to choose from.  But, for the most part I agree with this is telling me with the exception of the 'At work...' and 'How you see yourself...'.  I can think of some examples to back the other points, but I'll keep them to myself for now. 
🌿 calm  |  ♫ The Bravery - Believe

\o.o/ Weekend! Already? o.O

Mar. 18th, 2009   10:30 PM
Blah, feels like I'm living for the weekends and then when it is the weekends I don't really do much but waste time >>''.  Like, this week has gone by incredibly fast - all I have left of school is two hours tomorrow and then it's my weekend!  Maybe it's just cause my Mondays and Tuesdays are really stressful and my Wednesdays and Thursdays are pretty much a joke...Anyways, Friday I have a family dinner to go to and afterwards I'm meeting up with K-Dawg.  My aunt Liana and Uncle Viktor are having a big dinner for their one moth son.  It's sort of a Chinese tradition for the family to through a dinner a month after birth - although my mom read in the paper recently that some people are doing it a hundred days after birth nowadays.  Saturday night I'm going to the ABDL munch.  And Sunday I'll probably loaf around and finish off last minute assignments. 

The crappy Skullcandy Lowriders headphones I got replaced a few weeks ago broke again (in a similar way as last time might I add!).  I can understand that you shouldn't bend headphones laterally, but it's just completely stupid if they can't even withstand it a little.  Worst of all, the Lowriders are foldable so they're designed to be bent medially.  What a flimsy design.  My warrenty only allows me to replace it once more.  I'm going to see if I can replace it without them taking it out of its package at the store - they did that the last time.  So hopefully I can kijiji or craigslist or eBay the friggin thing.  I started researching for another pair and came across this site.  I'm debating whether to get the Sennheiser PX100 or the AKG K26P.  Based on the reviews I've read they're both considerably good in sound and quality, and the price tag isn't too too bad.  I also need to buy a pair of cheap earbuds - I think I saw a pair of Skullcandy earbuds for $8.99 >>'' dare I?  I need'em for when I go to the gym or jog.  I don't like wearing headphones while jogging cause they tend to come off easily and I'm not fond of the sweaty headband. 

I watched episde 47 and 48 of Soul Eater not long ago; boy was it ever disapointing.  The fight between Black Star and Mifune wasn't bad, but what led up to it was pretty terrible - which sorta brings back my point a few entries back about characters becoming supercharged on the brink of defeat.  The whole 'mobilizing Death City' was really retarded, but it just had to happen in order for the plot to proceed.  But honestly, I could have thought of much better ways to progress the action >>''.  The fight between Death and Asura was completely stupid.  All the talking they did completely ruined the crappy action that was going on.  Plus it was so inconsistent.  So, at the beginning of the fight Death can absorb a blast from Asura with one hand.  But at the end of the fight, in order to save Death the Kid and that four-eyed chick from a blast Death doesn't do the same...he stands in the line of fire and gets himself wasted?  Considering there are only 51 episodes it's pretty obvious what'll happen - Maka and the gang will clobber Asura some how and ladeda happy ending.  I read somewhere that the manga is different and much better than the anime.  I may consider giving it a read.  On a related note, I've begun working on a Mini-Ragnarok (Crona's demond blade) in Second Life!  When I get it done i'll post some pics.  I tried making a Death avatar a while back, but his mask (easy as it may look) didn't come out as good as I wanted it to.  I'm considering sculpting the mask when I have time to learn how to use SLoft (which Happyfeet Hax gave me a copy of!  Thanks a bunch ^^).

Sad face.

Mar. 16th, 2009   12:53 AM

Two people made me feel bad this weekend. Just now almost a third >>''. How unlucky. 

 To K-Dawg. I really wanted to hang out with you this weekend and I felt really bad when I kept refusing your suggestions.  Well, I felt a bit annoyed too, but more sad that I couldn’t fuel your needs. But seriously! I kept telling you I had homework and wanted a little personal time. Then you suggested movies, pizza, diapers, cuddling (T_T) you’re such a tease it pains me to refuse =P. I know I could’ve had an awesome weekend but hopefully we can meet up the following weekend (*cough**cough* ABDL Munch *cough*) or even during the week ~.^ Hope your head cold gets better :3.

 To Monica. Man do I feel really bad for passing your offer on partnering up for the C48 presentation assignment. It really surprised me that Merry and … (oh gawd this is gonna look horrible but I honestly never remember her name…it starts with an ‘N’) wanted to go off and do the assignment solo. The only reason I’m staying in my current group with Lindsay and Jen is because we’ve already divided up the work and I know it’ll take very little effort to get a good mark. I know it sounds really selfish but I sorta like stability – I don’t want to pile myself with work when I already know I have a ton of stuff due throughout the week. Again, I am really sorry and it honestly feels like I got gutted because I really like you as a friend. Best of luck, I know you’ll do great! :D

 To Tempest Fromund – who almost made me feel bad. Thanks for the friends add on Second Life and MSN ^^ though we’ve only really chatted with one another for the first time today, you’ve been a fun person to be around the last couple of times I've seen yea. I felt a little bad I couldn’t stay on SL to keep you company and to offer some cuddles, but I’m on my laptop with no headphones – and using the speakers in a house full of sleepyheads is unwise. I just popped on to update my profile and to checkout something a friend wanted me to see. I didn’t feel as bad when we chatted on MSN – I got to see you through webcam and your kitty is really adorable!  Hope we can keep in contact! 

 Oddly enough, Tempest sorta reminds me of the person I was last in a relationship with. Both I met online. Both bubbly/cute character. Both younger than me. I’m not hinting anything because I really don’t want to suggest anything. Keeping it just friends for me is cool for now.

Veinolin!

Mar. 14th, 2009   2:16 AM
Hoi, what a day.  I'm sorta glad it's over.  Started my morning off at 8am; I usually don't wake that early on days I don't have class, but I must've had a good sleep cause I was pretty awake.  Bummed around in front of the computer for two hours.  Started working on the veinolin I mentioned in my last blog.  Went down to the kitchen for breakfast at 10am.  My grandma gave me quite the scare.  She passed out while at the table - would have slid off her chair had my grandpa not caught her.  She came in and out of consciousness about two more times in the ten minutes after.  We didn't have much of a clue as to what was wrong - she hadn't even taken her Tylenol 3 for her leg pains yet.  She kept insisting to go back up to her room to lie down so my dad and I carried her up.  Although I don't particularly like my grandma because she doesn't know when to stop talking, I was pretty worried for her and my grandpa as well.  They stayed in their room till around 2pm.  I kept having this tragic thought in my head that my gradma died and my grandpa offs himself to be with her >>''.  Don't ask me where the idea came from cause I don't have a clue.  But every now and then I kept knocking on their door to see if they were still alive.  Oddly enough my grandma livened up in the afternoon - even walked around some.  Didn't do a hell of a lot in the afternoon.  My dad went to some office or what not to set up his first coffee machine.  He seemed to be in pretty good spirit when he got home. 

Check out my finished veinolin!  I'm quite happy with it ^^. 

And here it is in the case I made for it.

The veinolin is 195 prims - all are of which are torus prims except for one, which is a sculptie.  The sculpted prim is a heart, it's where the shoulder rest would be on an actual violin.  I'm a bit buggered that I bought the sculpt map (it was a brain and heart set) for L$175.  The brain looks fantastic but the heart wasn't as detailed as I imagined it to be.  Oh well.  The case is made with a rounded cube (inside-out) sculptie map.  It's a really interesting sculptie because all external faces are alpha (see through).  So no matter what angle you look at the case you'll be able to see its contents :D.  The sculpted rose I put in just for show.  It's sort of my 'thing' in SL.  I usually give a rose to people I find beautiful or hospitable ^^.


🌿 calm  |  ♫ MF Doom - Kookies
Link

Lesson of the day...and fun stuff :3

Mar. 13th, 2009   12:12 AM
I won't go into the nitty gritty, but todays I learned that you shouldn't underestimate the people you think you know fairly well.  Those people are quite capable of surprising you even in the most subtle and interesting of ways. 

Okay, onto the less serious stuff. 

I recently read issue 46 of Sandman and I'd have to say it ranks somewhere in the top three of my favourite issues so far in the series.  It's funny, touching, clever, creative, and all around wicked!  So the issue is about Dream calling it quits on his little journey to find Destruction with Delerium because of the repercussions - pretty much because every significant person they run into dies.  This leaves Delerium depressed.  Dream goes back to his realm to ponder.  He conjures up a dream to communicate with Lady Bast, an anthropomorphic cat goddess (she's made an appearance in earlier issues), because he wants to know where his brother Destruction is.  There's a bit of oral foreplay and eventually Dream ends the dream since he doesn't get the answer he's looking for.  Mervyn and Lucien (Dream's servents) have a pretty comical discussion - what's funnier is that Mervyn is putting glue on a brick wall and posting a wallpaper that resembles a bookcase.  After he finishes the bookcase becomes three dimensionalized!  Holy crap I wish the real world was like that, I'd so deck out my room!  Anyways, Lucien leads Dream into his gallery where he discovers the portrait to communicate/enter Delirium's realm has gone black.  Dream calls upon his older sister, Death where they have a really cute discussion.  Two panels in particular really stood out in this section because I haven't noticed this cartoony style in issues past... 

The issue ends with Dream going into Delirium's realm (which is crazy psychodelic) to make peace with her - and to refresh where they left off in their journey.  Totally looking forward to the next couple of issues, only three more till the story arc finishes!

On to Second Life news.  I've been relatively bored on SL the last few days mainly cause I haven't found anything interesting I want to build - or found something but gave up on it prematurally.  The last thing I built was Metron's Mobius Chair (from DC comics, he's a New God).  I finished building the chair but when it came time to making it fly with a script I already had, it didn't quite work the way I wanted it to.  The script already has a sitting position written in it (it's cross legged), but I didn't like it and I wanted to replace it with an poseI already had (a 'thinker' pose).  I have no clue how to edit scripts, even the dead simple ones, so not surprisingly I 'epic' failed.  Gave up with the chair and just stuck it in a folder with all my other half complete projects.  But anyways, today I was flipping through some comics to see if anything interesting my spark an idea - like a crazy looking weapon or funky armour.  The cover of The Umbrella Academy: Apocalypse Suite #4 sparked my lightbulb.  I want to build the violin that number seven (yes, that is the name of the character >>) is holding.  If you look closely it looks like either tree vines/branches or human veins (much like the red stuff by her feet).  Building this would be relatively easy with sculpted tree branch prims, but that particular collection of sculptie maps cost L$400 on Xstreet and I have less then L$150.  So I'm going to go old fashion - before sculpted prims existed people used the torus prim to build curvey stuff like trees and hair.  The only issue I have is that I suck when it comes to using the torus prim.  It is one of the trickest prims to use since the path cutting, twisting, tapering, etc take a strong understanding of how they work.  However, torus is also one of the most versatile prims in SL.  It is used in virtually everything in the most nuttiest of shapes possible.  If I do manage to finish the build I think I'll call it a veinolin xD.  

And lastly, random news - the recession has hit Sesame Street!  Oh noes! D:  The company has eliminated 67 of 355 staff positions!  I wonder if any muppets got axed :(  Thank gawd I lived through their Golden Age (lol).  Anyways, peace out. 
🌿 calm  |  ♫ Mother Mother - O My Heart
Link

Authentici-wah?

Mar. 11th, 2009   8:36 PM
Wicked, I think I've got an idea of what I'll write my paper proposal on for ANTD15.  Originally I was thinking of doing it on something about Iraq's threatened heritage.  Surprisingly there is only one acknowledge site in Iraq by UNESCO's World Heritage List, which is Hatra.  This is surprising considering Iraq is often described as the cradle of civilization (so technically the whole country should be designated on the List - thousands of historic vilalges, towns and cities are dotted around the country).  There are two other sites (Ashur and Samarra Archaeological City) in Iraq that are on the List of World Heritage in Danger, and a bunch of others on the Tentative List.  There are a lot of really interesting things I could research about since some of the issues quite fresh considering the war that's going on right now.  But I think I've found something a little more fun to write about all thanks to Prof. Alan A. Lew's podcast/lecture, and slides.  I want to research on the authenticity of Renaissance Fairs (as 'living history').  The prof only very very briefly brings it up so honest to gawd I'm not taking any of his ideas!  He just sparked the idea into my head so I looked around for some articles on the schools scholars portal.  I found an article called 'Touristic Quest for Existential Authenticity' by Kim, H. and Jamal, T.  It's like one of the only papers I found that really goes into my topic - so I'll have to use some of the references they've cited or change the direction of my proposal. 

Anywhoo, todays ANTC48 lecture was really short - consisted of a one hour lecture and the rest of the time was left for us to discuss our group presentations amongst ourselves.  So a three hour lecture was reduced to one.  Quite the rip off, but I'm not complaining since I really wanted to go home.  I ended up taking five articles on osteosarcoma that Lindsay had printed, and I'm in charge of looking for pictures to put on our slide presentation which is due in two weeks.  If all goes well this should be a very easy 'A'.  (Just a random thought, Merry was wearing a 'I <3 ?Love' t-shirt today which I thought was really really cool!  Practically no one got it cause they don't listen to The Roots - oh yea!  her and I are so cool, lol.)  Aside from that, I'm not really looking forward to the weekend.  I've got to do the assignment for ANTC67, which involves stats but I still haven't learned it thoroughly.  Plus I'll probably find something to distract myself which will end up making me do the assignment last minute!  >< Omg I want to read Sandman.  I am literally in the middle of the seventh story arc (Brief Lives issue #45) and it's getting good.  Oi, and the ABDL Munch is next Saturday, definitely something to look forward to especially since I'll be really stressed out by at the point in the semester!

🐇 bouncy  |  ♫ Lauryn Hill - Freedom Time

Blah day...

Mar. 10th, 2009   8:58 PM

Phew, what a heavy day…

-          the weather was wet and shady all day

-          I got back my ANTC20 midterm and barely passed (multiple-choice 7/20, short answer 18/25)

-          The tutorial for ANTC67 was really confusing (because I’m having trouble with the statistics portion of epidemiology)

-          I stayed in school until 7:30pm to finish a 20 page osteobiographic report for ANTC48 (with Lindsay and Jen, but for the most part Lindsay did a majority of the work which I feel so guilty about…but she barely lets me do anything ><)

-          And my day isn’t over just yet.  I have to start a paper proposal with an annotated bibliography due Thursday (which honestly wouldn’t take very long if I had a clue to what I want to write my paper on – on top of the research I have to do).  I also have to read four articles (I’ve only stated one) by Thursday. 

 

Well, a few pleasant things to point out…

-          I got an 81% on my site analysis paper for ANTD15 (mark posted online so I won’t get to read the feedback until Thursday).

-          I got to look around the new science wing on campus (I had no idea it was even open.  Personally I don’t think it’s any sort of big deal, I didn’t see anything flashy.  It’s three floors, but only the first floor is open to students since the others require an access card.  There’s a lecture hall, some offices, and some psych labs (or something labs). 

-          I downloaded the LittleBigPlanet soundtrack.  Never played the game before but I’ve watched gameplay and review videos, looks fun and creative.  The songs by Daniel Pemberton are really chill, some goofy even – sorta reminds me of spring.  Give it a listen if you want something refreshing.


And that’s pretty much all >>”.  Here’s a picture of my mouse av in Second Life ^^.

🌀 gloomy  |  ♫ The Go! Team - Get it Together

Watchmen Fail!

Mar. 8th, 2009   10:26 AM

Don’t let the subject header fool you.  I’m just really disappointed because Ciniplex ruined my Watchmen experience! Last night I went with Jully to watch the 10:30pm screening at STC – the drive wasn’t the greatest because of the rain and fog on the highways and roads. We got there early, at around 10:10pm because I wanted good seats and I wasn’t worried about lining up for tickets since I already bought them online. The theatre was about 75-80% full, but we managed to find descent seats along the side.  I always knew the concession stand stuff were really expensive but it completely blew my mind that a 20 oz bottle of Dasani water cost $3.  Also bought a small bag of popcorn, which was $5, that I didn’t finish.  But anyways, those things can’t really be helped.  Two hours into the movie (the part when Rorschach and Nite Owl II confront Ozymandias in the arctic) the screen goes black, like something was blocking the lens, but the audio was still playing.  It was like that for about three minutes and people were talking amongst themselves and some shouting refund.  I’d say about 40% of the people began walking out after that, Jully and I followed.  So I don’t really have any idea when they got the thing working again, but I didn’t really care.  I was really disappointed and I wasn’t going to sit through the rest of it even if I missed a couple minutes – the experience was already ruined, yea know?  Anyways a majority of the people waited by the concession stands and ticket booths (it was 1am at that point and everything was closed and staff were doing gawd knows what).  About five minutes later a woman comes out explaining that the projector lost power and that it’s back up again and that they’d give us a courtesy tickets in exchange for the ones we paid for.  It took Jully and I a couple of minutes to get our tickets; so by now we’d already missed about fifteen minutes (and there was maybe like twenty minutes left of the movie).  I didn’t go in and watch the rest because the continuity was already ruined.  It’s not like I wouldn’t have understood what would have been going on since I read the graphic novel, but I didn’t want to go in also because I’ve heard the ending is filmed slightly different from how it was published.  So yeah, I was pretty bitter.  Jully went in to watch the rest while I bummed around outside.  I don’t think it really mattered to her since she didn’t know what was going on for most of the movie anyways (o.o)  Oh well, I think the movie would be a little difficult to understand if you don’t listen and watch closely to the politics (and the era in which the film is set in).  Plus there are a fair number of characters that could get confusing. 

Okay!  On to the criticisms and compliments of the movie!  I don’t claim to be any sort of movie/comic book guru so what I say is entirely based on my opinions whether accurate, right or wrong, or whatever.  I’ve never given any formal spoiler warnings when I’ve talked about comic books or other stuff in my previous entries so don’t think I won’t go soft on this one.  And lastly, whatever I say here is only based on what I’ve seen – had the theatre not ruined the ending for me maybe I would have been blown away by it other wise and followed this part of the blog with high praise.  But here we go:

First off, good job Zack Snyder for taking a stab at the ‘unfilmable’.  I thought it was very visually pleasing and more parts than not did justice to the comic.  I think it’s really difficult to capture all the elements when making an adaptation since each provide a different kind of visual stimulus.  In a comic book you have all the time in the world to absorb all the details and the writers can go at lengths with character development.  You can go at your own pace as well as flip backwards to see what you’ve missed.  But in a movie three seconds of frame are as good as twenty panels in a comic.  Things are full blown and they have to be precise since you can’t immediately go back.  Essentially you’re just pulled through from beginning to end.  I thought Snyder balanced the two fairly well.  The movie was aesthetically pleasing – almost to a point where it was Disneyfied (completely my opinion).  It’s like, everything is so vibrant in the film where the 2D was more grainy (in old Disney flicks it’s grainy, but when you go to Disneyland it’s colourful).  Comprehending that adjustment (the ‘brining out of the page’) took a little bit of time for me.  One part in particular that embodied this feeling was the costumes for the Minutemen.  Mothman, Hooded Justice, Captain Metropolis and Dollar Bill looked so friggin cheesy!  Honestly it looked like they were wearing freaking costume party suits or something because the colours were so bright.  If I were robbing a bank and they heroically came to stop me I’d surrender due to laughter.  (Just a random thought, Madam Rouge from the Teen Titans animated series looks like The Silhouette from the Minutemen).  And while I’m on the topic of costumes, what’s up with Rorschach and the neatness?  In the movie his clothes (mostly the trench coat, really) were spotless, if not relatively new looking.  In the comic he lived in the freaking ghettos!  He’s got shit smears all over jacket (probably because he hides it in the trash and under floor boards).  Yea it’s being picky, but it wouldn’t have been difficult to throw a little dirt on it while designing.  I liked how the floppy thing on the left shoulder of his jacket was always unbutton – just like in the comic ^^.

I really liked most of the choreography for the fight scenes (sorta the one thing they can’t screw up in adaptations since you only see so much in comics so you have to interpret, while in movies they just fill the gap with wickedness).  The first scene where the Comedian gets murder really kicked off the movie on an exciting note – and usually you’d expect the movie to get better and better as it progresses.  Some of the fight scenes were really brutal and bloody.  The one where the Comedian beats the crap out of the first Silk Spectre was really violent and hard to watch mainly cause of the gender thing.  I’m a little ‘meh’ on Snyder’s trademark speed-ramping (where he slows down/speeds up parts of a fight to accentuate the force of a blow or to show a punch connect).  It’s cool to watch, but to often is to much for my taste.  It’s a little hard to judge since I don’t remember how often it was used – but in the trailers they slowed down a lot of parts that didn’t really seem that slow.  The opening credits (which was after the Comedian’s murder scene) crammed A LOT of information about the alternate realities history.  Like, if you don’t pay much attention you’ll miss out on a lot of details – particularly the politics and evolution of costumed heroes.  I realize that the two unfold throughout the movie, however if you start off watching not knowing the setting it just makes it harder to get into later.  But then again, the whole opening credits was filmed in really slow motion so you deserve a bop in the face if you can’t even pick out some details.  Lastly, the film wasn’t as engaging as the comic book in terms of the emotions/feelings they work with.  The comic blended humour, horror, action and suspense very very well.  But in the movie it was obvious to see each scene focused on one or two elements.  Basically, it was more than obvious to tell when one scene progressed to the next rather than a continuous flow.  I think they could have done that better by chopping up the scenes (the one transitions into the other) and mixing them together.

In terms of the acting, I really thought Patrick Wilson (Daniel Dreiberg/Nite Owl II), Jackie Earle Haley (Walter Kovacs/Rorschach), Billy Crudup (Dr. Jon Osterman/Doctor Manhattan), Jeffrey Dean Morgan (Edward Blake/The Comedian), and Laura Mennell (Janey Slater) did a freaking spectacular job at capturing the essence of each character.  I can’t say the same for Malin Ackerman (Laurie Juspeczyk/Silk Spectre II) though cause I felt her acting was far weaker than everyone else’s.  Her acting came off as though the role wasn’t natural to her.  Oh well, SS2 wasn’t really a memorable character in the comics, for me.  Sure she was really important in the story, but she’s sort of a whore (having a relationship with Doctor Manhattan and then Nite Owl II).  Patrick Wilson played the sensitive (‘only manly when wearing a costume’) Daniel Dreiberg very well.  Only little thing I didn’t quite like was his costume.  A huge part of the comic was that retired superheroes were getting back into the game and it was pretty apparent since Daniel was out of his prime and a bit tubbier.  In the movie his age apparently hasn’t caught up with him.  His costume makes him look ripped, plus it looks a lot more modern.  In the comic I was just this ghetto mono-coloured suit, but in the movie it’s all scaly and shiny.  I was a little worried with how Doctor Manhattan’s voice would sound like since I don’t recall hearing it in the trailers.  Whenever I read comic books I don’t consciously give each character their own tone or dynamic (unless they already have one based on an adaptation or anime or cartoon or whatever).  So I usually just read in my own voice for all characters.  I thought the thing they did with Billy Crudup’s voice was wicked (it was completely monotone with a tiny bit of fuzziness (but of course, the monotone came from his acting, so good job to him!)).  Jeffrey Morgan and Jackie Haley, I donno what to compliment them specifically.  They were just brilliant in capturing the emotions and overall all feel of each character.  

Last thing I want to get into are the things taken out or changed from the comic to the movie.  There are very obvious things taken out (like the ‘comic-book-within-a-comic-book’, Walter Kovac’s extended therapy session, and Hollis Mason’s (the first Nite Owl) death).  And there are some subtle changes (like Daniel Dreiberg warning Adrian Veidt, rather than Rorschach, and obviously the ending).  I noticed more things taken out than changed (or I wasn’t paying as much attention).  I’d go through each issue of the comic book and point them out had I not been such a lazy person.  I don’t really know what to think about the take outs and changes.  The movie worked without them, but then that particular dimension is missed – mainly in terms of character development.  So it either questions the importance of the content to begin with, or reinforces its significance.  Some things would be really really hard to incorporate (like the brown dude who reads comics) and they wouldn’t hold much importance in the movie.  While others are questionable – I’m thinking the last few pages after the end of every issue there are ‘documents’ of what’s happening ‘at the time’, they really give a deeper perspective of what we’re reading in terms of the story as a whole).  I was a little surprised they took out some of the thought process that went into Doctor Manhattan’s spinny ship thingy on Mars (when he’s talking with Laurie Juspeczyk) – it really added more depth to understanding who the doc is.  I can’t really think of any other examples, and I’m sure there are a few more, but one thing I did enjoy was seeing actual shots from the movie that are taken directly from the original panels.  There are quite a few that immediate jog my memory of ‘hey this looks exactly like…’ which excited me.  And the soundtrack was more often than not pretty fitting – since some tracks used were popular at the time (late 70s-80s). 

Overall, even though I haven’t finished the movie, I think the movie is solid…if you haven’t read the comic book.  For those who have read it I think it really depends on how badly you want to dissect the details.  In either case, I think Snyder really nailed the core features that made Watchmen such an epic graphic novel, and it’d be really difficult to say if anyone could have done it better.  I’m really glad I watched it, but I’m really pissed I didn’t get to finish it (I feel quite cheated having to find time to watch it again while considering the first sitting to be less authentic – guilt free time wasting is hard to come by!).  I give this movie a 9/10.

🌀 busy  |  ♫ Classified ft. Chad Hatcher - All About U
Link

Monies and Mocha

Mar. 7th, 2009   3:14 AM
This week has gone by awfully quick.  Not particularly good >>'' four week left of school and a hell of a lot of stress to look forward to.  Anywhoo, loafed at home for most of Friday.  Was on Second Life for most of the day.  Shopped around for an Animation Override and some badass power fists/gloves/gauntlets/whatever doohicky.  The AO I've been using for my other av's don't work to well with the mouse av I'm using as a main.  The poses are sorta serious-ish and mature looking - which is the opposite to what I'm going after for my mouse av (trying to rekindle to youthful and playful).  So I started looking for a children's AO which was harder than I first imagined since I only had L$900.  I had a children's AO on the first account I ever made, Nodame Hyun, but I gave it away to someone.  Kinda regret that now since I have no recollection who I gave it to and because it costed L$700.  But I was a pretty reckless spender and bought things on impulse back then cause I still worked a job and SL was fun when I just started.  I don't think I want to through money at SL nowadays since nothing really interests me in terms of wants or needs.  Well, actually, there is this one thing that seems interesting (lol) but costs L$2995 or $13.05 USD.  A fellow named Moy Loon created an in-game sculptie developing system called SLoft last year.  I've seen other in-game sculptie tools but nothing as simplified as his - I've only seen the ones before him, haven't looked into the recent stuff.  I've always wanted to learn how to make sculpted prims but never got around to doing it.  I still have Blender and Wings 3D installed on my computer, but those are more complex since they aren't in-game programs >>''.  Oh well, maybe I'll learn it during the summer.  But back to the AO bizz.  I ended up finding a L$200 Chibi AO on Xstreet.  I was debating whether or not to get a L$300 Kids AO that I found from an actual Kids mall, but the description and images weren't to convincing - it also had fewer animations and poses.  I think the AO I bought is for female av's cause some of the standing animations are a little to pizzazz for my taste (but cavity inducing cute!).  So I started tinkering with the notecard, which the script inside the HUD reads.  I pulled out four animations and ended up breaking the script somehow.  Unfortunately the script is no modify so I can't tinker further with it even if I wanted to ><.  The AO still works fine but since it's on a random cycle everytime it shuffles to a standing pose that doesn't have the animation within the HUD, a broken script icon appears.  It's mildly annoying, but I'm not going to complain since it was so cheap.  I was debating whether to build the power fists since I had nothing better to do, but ended up buying a pair from Fail Hard Armoury for L$250.  They're made entirely out of sculpted prims, the right glove is scripted for SL combat, and they each have a really wicked wavey blue energy texture that moves along the finger tips ^^.  So basically they look awesome, and stuff.  >> don't ask me why I wanted power fists because I just did (impulse!).  It's like, they're a nice add on to an av - not overly flashy but not something uberly common.  I blame Chase Steins' fistigons (from Runaways) for drilling the coolness into my head. 

O.o  I just noticed how much I typed for the mundane things I did in SL.  Argh enthusiasm surging all over! D:  Noes, >< addiction could come next!  Okay, lets talk about comics/manga!  I read Naruto chapter 438 and the pace is picking up :D  The fighting wasn't bad.  Naruto in demon fox form with six tails pretty much vaporized part of his already destroyed village, which I thought was hilarious.  Part of me keeps thinking 'and you want to be Hokage someday?!  You just laid waste to part of your village (though somehow I doubt he managed to kill any of the many many unconscious bodies lining the streets - what a shame).'  I'm a bit annoyed that they showed three under-developed characters near the end of the chapter (Yamato, Sai, and Anko) rather than showing Team Gai (whom made an appearance last chapter).  Also the last frame totally perpetuates ninja cliches (or dare I say Japanese fighting anime cliches).  Pein has a secret technique!  oOoOoOo cheesy!  Honestly it's like that every other time.  In a fight some dude totally gets his ass handed to them, they make a monologue, then BAM!  They reveal a super secret forbidden stunt that juices them up ten fold!  Some examples I can think of would be the fight between Gaara and Rock Lee - which was awesome I'll have to admit {o'.'o}.  The whole celestrial gates thing Lee does totally reminded me of Dragon Ball....which is another example.  'Oh I'm getting my butt whooped, maybe if I get constipated enough I can go SSJ!'  Bah, lame...and yet I still follow with these animes o.O

Anyway, at night I met up with K-Dawg from Aby/DiaperSpace for the first time.  We've chatted maybe two or three times online - I can't honestly say I remember most of the things we chat about, what with my horrible memory and all.  But that sorta seems like my case for a lot of the initial chats I have with online people.  I think it's because I've become so use to the things some of these people say and ask.  Almost like I'm having a repeated conversation but with a different person - so I sorta pay less attention >>.  Not really the best way to make a first impression, but it's subconscious (I think...) so it can't be helped...I think.  So yea, I end up forgetting certain details (like occupation or location) which I feel awkward for having to ask about again.  We chatted online in the afternoon and settle on meet up for coffee at around 9:30pm.  I got the car because my parents had a change of plans at the last minute, so the TTC can go suck it xP.  Pretty cool dude (cuddle worthy!), but our conversations weren't all that intense - forced maybe?  It's like putting two listeners into a room, eventually one is forced to be a talker or else shit gets really awkward.  We ended up talking a bit about everything (>< I was the one to break ice and ask about his ABDL side - man I hate being the dude who starts it, lol, but it's just a personal thing), walked around some, drove around some, and loitered around some parking lots xD.  We met up at Lesile/York Mills and ended up departing at Yonge St (between Lawrence and York Mills) o.O  I ended up getting a $30 parking ticket cause my car was in a permits only zone - stupid small neighbourhood street laws!  Oh well, the next time we meet up we'll probably bum around his neighborhood (some ghetto part of downtown from what he describes xD) cause gawd knows there's absolutely nothing to do in North York.  Which is friggin' hilarious cause when I lived up in Richmod Hill that's EXACTLY what I said.  Whenever I hung out with my friends we'd always bitch that Richmond Hill had little to no entertainment centers.  There was a pool hall across from the highschool but it closed down :<  Man, I thought there'd be more things to do when I moved to North York - a bit of a let down, but I am a hell of a lot closer to downtown now so finding stuff to do isn't nearly as impossible as before.  But yeah, I'm going to pressure him to goto this months ABDL munch cause he's never been (but he's met up with several other ABDL's in the past).  I got home at around 2:15am-ish and slept on the couch in the basement.  I sorta regret changing out of my diaper before coming home since it still had a lot of life left in it, but I didn't know if my brother would leaving the house early in the morning (which he did ><).  T_T I'm down to less than ten Attends so I gotta be conservative till I get the opportunity to restock - which seems more and more limited since my dad has been using the car during the weekday to meet up with potential clients.  Anyways, I'm bushed.  Goodnight :3
💤 sleepy  |  ♫ Muse - Showbiz
