Mm...Food

Mar. 5th, 2009   6:41 PM
(MF Doom has a CD titled 'Mm...Food'.  It's quite amazing because he raps about food in each track, while sampling Adult Swim sound bits.  Great rapper, but disrespectful to his fans.  This has nothing to do with my post, just some random facts :3.) Here's a delicious combination!  Toasted English muffin with a light spread of Greek flavoured hummus sandwiched between thin slices of smoked gouda cheese.  Yeah, these items just so happened to be in my fridge the other day and I was feeling pretty daring.  Funny thing is, I've never had smoked gouda before and that stuff is so tastey!  Other than the textural difference, it could probably substitute smoked samon in recipies like salads.  To bad I'm partially lactose intolerant or I'd eat the whole wheel xD.  Usually havarti cheese is in my fridge and it tastes completely different, but also very yummy.  I don't usually eat English muffins but my parents bought a bag of dozen and hummus doesn't go to well on plain sliced bread.  The thing I don't like about English muffins is that they aren't filling and they're so tiny.  They're like the rejected cross breed between a bagel and sliced bread.  Slice bread isn't filling but at least it's big enough to sandwich stuff and bagels are filling but not tiny.  Plus, I usually have a hard time deciding what goes well with English muffins.  They're so porose that butter just seeps through all the holes giving the perception that you need more butter!  And I'm not to big on jams since my family keeps them in the fridge so biting into a chilly filling isn't to tastey.  But whatever, if you ever have the three ingredients on hand (hellz, you could even put your own variation on it) definitely give it a try ^^. And another combination!  I've never had this before until today, my friend and I were at Tim Hortons and she ordered a half hot chocolate and a half coffee.  Sounded interesting so I gave it a try (I'm not to big on chocolate cause it messes with my stomach), and it's really good.  Crazy part was I actually felt awake afterwards!  In my lifetime I've consumed so much coffee that more often than not the caffeine doesn't do squat unless I have multiple cups - but even then it only gets my heart racing for a very short while.  Coffee for me is just a social thing now...a very expensive one unfortunately since I've been going out for coffee ALOT these past couple of months.  But yea, the hot chocolate/coffee mixture really got me jittering, probably because of the ungawdly amount of sugar they put in it.  Oh yea, my dad's got one of his coffee machines fully operation now (it's sitting in our family room oddly enough), but he's using really cheap beans because he had to tune it to the desired settings (like water temperature, how fine to grind the beans, etc).  So the coffee it's spitting out is pretty gross.  I'm just waiting till he loads it up with some Starbucks beans lol. Other than those tidbits, my weekend has started!  I honestly have no idea what I have due next week - quite possibly nothing, but I'm not certain.  I haven't found the motivation to bother checking just yet and I probably won't until tomorrow.  I dug through my inventory for Watchmen so I can skim through it before I (hopefully) watch it this weekend - probably gonna do that later tonight.  Possibly gonna go see it with Jully, but she was like 'if I can't get my car then we'd go at night when she has hers'.  I always feel really awkward having a female friend drive me.  Like I'm fine with it if it's reciprical (which is usually the case with Stephanie and I), but the track record between Jully and I has always been her driving me around.  It makes me feel like I owe a debt or obligated to return the favour which I have yet to - and if there is one thing I dislike the most in life it's having a debt.  Worst part is, there's a good chance I won't get the car this weekend cause my brother probably has plans to use it - and he plans things ahead of time where as I'm more sporadic, so it's unfair of me to ask for something when I already know a head of time that he needs it (even if he's a jerk face ><).  Anyways, I started reading 'Brief Lives' (issues #41-49), the seventh story arc in the Sandman collection.  I think this is gonna be a really good story arc since the Dreamlord's family seems to have a prominant role (so far Delarium, Desire, and Despair have made an appearance in #41.  Also, Destruction made his debut).  I also started working on the appearance of my AnthroXtacy mouse avatar in Second Life.  Originally I wanted to make my own mouse av with my pre-existing av's as a template, but blaming my laziness is more than enough of an excuse for to give up on that.  I want to use this mouse av as my main since the one I'm using now is a red rabbit by Exterminatus Labs, which looks really badass, but not cute.  I started dressing up my mouse av, and omg was it a pain.  I hate looking for hair since demos don't have modification permissions and paying for expensive ones than fumbling around with fitting is a bitch.  After a while of browsing Xstreet I eventually found a really cheap, mid-length, black hair.  Personally I think it looks really typical and common, but I'm not gonna exhaust myself further looking for anything else.  Clothing wise I have stuff that's 'ok', but not spectacular.  My pants are cartoony which I'm fine with :P  but I really want to get a t-shirt from Boing Formage (!BF!) because they're graphics are really nice and simple.  The only problem is a lot of their tees are for females because there's a cleavage shadowing drawn into the graphics...real bummer ><.  I bought a L$1 pair of skater shoes that have a colour changing script in them xD.  I don't think I'll stick with them in the long run, but till I find something better they'll have to do.  I'm sorta tempted to get sculptie clothes since it literally seems like every other person has some on >>.  XD Oh gawd I'm getting excited over SL again!  This can be fantastic good or terrible bad ~ lets hope I can stay focused on school! 
🐇 bouncy  |  ♫ Nas ft. Busta Rhymes - Fried Chicken
Link

Concerning this blog...

Mar. 3rd, 2009   2:32 AM
I want to open up this blog to a wider audience. For the longest time I’ve been feeling this intense boredom with my life – as if nothing interesting happens and everything I do is routine and naturalized. I want to do something deviant that will provide some long term excitement but I don’t want to end up hurting myself in the process. So far only my online and ABDL friends know about this blog; none of the people I come face-to-face with regularly know. It’s interesting how I can be fine with letting people I’ve never met (with the exception of a small handful) read about my thoughts and feelings. And yet I feel anxiety if people I have a more personal relationship with read the same. Not many people like reading about themselves (even if most names mentioned in this blog are pseudonyms) especially if it’s something personal or bitter. Thus, I don’t want to feel a sense of malice from people I know who might read this blog if an entry pertains to them. But, I don’t want to have that limitation (constantly being sensitive to what I write) because sometimes I simply just need to write about something that one might not agree with. The purpose of this blog has always been to be as real to myself as possible. I don’t want that to ever change, but the guilt is frightening.  I’m uncertain if the excitement from ‘simply doing it’ will be worth it in the end.

I can imagine a handful of people will read this. Maybe a smaller handful with questions about the personal things I’ve been open about and another small handful quietly constructing an opinion I’ll never likely hear. I think I want this, but I don’t know, so I’m going to give it a try. I realize that it is practically impossible to take back the things I’ve written after they’ve been read. In whatever way it will effect my life, there after, I will accept it as a form of excitement because I don’t think there are many things worse than the boredom I’ve been feeling as of late. 
Link

Weekend highlights

Mar. 2nd, 2009   10:46 PM

So, on Saturday Stephanie txt’d me at around 5pm asking if I was free for coffee at night. I didn’t have the car so she came down and picked me up at around 9:30pm-ish. The last time we met up was on Dec 22nd (I wouldn’t have remembered had I not blogged about it – I are soooz forgetful). After months of waiting she finally received her Christmas present! We went to the Tim Hortons just outside my house and as usual we talked a bit about everything – catching up mostly. Kind of a bummer when she reminded me of how quickly the school year has been going. There’s only five more weeks of school until she graduates. I’ll be staying back a semester, but same difference, time really is progressing quickly. Whenever we meet up we usual bring up ideas for our grad trip. The last couple of times it’s been ideas of an overseas trip, but this time she suggested Disneyworld in Florida. I was kinda iffy about it cause it seems rather corny and ‘unexotic’ since if I were to travel alone for the first time I want to make it a big one. But I guess it sorta starting to grew on me since I’m feeling more incline to experiencing anything now. Interestingly, my AB side didn’t jump at the idea of Disneyworld. I’ve been there once as a kid and it was fun (from what I remember), but I guess the whole idea of feeling little didn’t seem fitting in something like this – celebrating a milestone. Plus, it would seem a little awkward to get super excited about it since I don’t particularly like expressing that side of me around people who don’t know about it. I donno, I think I’m making myself seem to mature now >>. Anyways, it was really nice catching up – comforting really – but a part of me is a bit depressed that we even need to ‘catch up’.  I hardly make an effort to keep in contact with her (let alone several other of my friends) online when I have no reason not to. It’s like, I don’t make an effort to strengthen or support certain bridges in my friendships and it gets me pretty down. I don’t want to say it’s because I’m a lazy butt head, but that’s the only answer I can think of unfortunately. I’ve always been a better listener than a talker, but that’s hardly an excuse not to make an effort from time to time. I fear that someday it’ll come back in haunt me – and I can’t really imagine why it wouldn’t, let alone having to blame myself if nothing else. 

To cheer myself up a little, onto comic book news! I skipped issues #29-31 – Distant Mirrors – of Sandman because they’re just one-take stories that don’t contribute to the bigger story. I’ll probably read them when I’m finished the series just to get a greater depth of Neil Gaiman’s writing. I read the fifth story arc, A Game of You, issues 32-37 and it was wicked. It had a bit of everything, literally; action, drama, fantasy, lesbians, a transgender, violence, blood, witchcraft, etc. The story arc doesn’t really involve the Dreamlord much – and doesn’t contribute all that much to his storyline – but it develops some of the minor characters we’ve met so far. I was really surprised by the issues that were brought up – particularly identity (sex vs. gender). It’s like, I had no idea these things were being discussed at the time (Nov 1991 – May 1992), It’s really ‘wow’ to read about homosexuality when now a days it’s common place in nearly all aspects of society; I wonder if it was controversial to write about gender identity at the time. Coming out of the closet is a really big deal, right? And yet Giaman is writing about it so ‘openly’ at a time when being different would get you ‘outed’. (Re-reading this paragraph I’m beginning to question how mainstream homosexuality really was ‘back then’ since this issue is making me think of the movie Milk – which is a friggin awesome movie. I’m not trying to say that there weren’t homosexuals in the 90s because that’d be ridiculous, but I question how openly discussed was it really?) Anyways, I’ll probably skip issues #38-40 since they’re also one-take stories that don’t contribute much to the bigger plot. I’m about halfway through the entire series.  I kinda like the pace I’m going at – no real point in rushing since I haven’t downloaded anything new and the current titles I’m reading won’t have new issues till April. 

In music news, I have added a new artist to my playlist. As usual, I heard about the artist from my brother; Classified, a Nova Scotia rapper and producer! I was pretty blown away to even hear about a Maritimes rapper since the east coast doesn’t seem very…er…modern? I hardly remember anything I’ve ever learned about Nova Scotia, so I blame my reasoning on that. But seriously, what the hell comes to mind when you think Nova Scotia in terms of fun and entertaining things? I haven’t given him my full attention yet so I can’t pick out any tracks I really really like (though I’m sure there are a couple). Lyrically he reminds me of Slug (another white rapper, but from Minneapolis and makes up one half of the hip hop group Atmosphere). Heck, his voice even sorta sounds like Slug’s. His beats are…okay. I don’t really find his beats consistent from song to song – some are really amazing and fit perfectly with his lyrics while others sound to processed and overly done. Of Atmosphere’s entire discography, I only like about three-fifths of their songs. I think I’m leaning to the same number in terms of Classified’s stuff. One difference between Atmosphere and Classified is that I find the former has more darker and emotional songs than the latter. Like, if you listen to ‘Guarantees’, ‘In her Music Box’, and ‘The Waitress’ you will feel something deeply provoking (at least I get a tingly feeling when I listen to them). I can’t say I’ve found a Classified track that has done the same yet. I’m gonna have to look into the Canadian underground scene - kinda strange how I don’t think of local hip hop artists when I’m so enthusiastic about indie bands. Before this, it was the NYC underground scene thanks to Print’s mixtape (mentioned in my Feb 10 blog – solid album!). 

I watched episodes 10-15 from season five of The Office, so now I’m caught up!

Oh, and I thought of a new story idea! Kinda pisses me off though since I haven’t gotten around to starting ‘Be Normal’ or revising ‘Misplaced Justice’ – not to mention all the other story ideas I’ve compiled. Someday…someday. So I haven’t done any research yet, but the premise is relatively simple. Affairs in an AB/mommy context! Well, not affairs per say, but lemme explain. I want to capture the dynamics of how it feels to be going through an affair. I always say I want to write emotional stuff, so I’ll say it again! I want this story to be emotional. I want the reader to cheer for a side and feel something when a character is afflicted. So here’s the broad situation. There is a married couple – a male AB and a female mommy. The female works a job that requires her to be on the road (or perhaps she gets a promotion so suddenly her occupation requires her to travel a lot). The male is thus left at home for days on end at times. His AB side yearns for a mommy so he calls up a mommy hotline (I keep seeing a mommy hotline add on DiaperSpace, I’m gonna have to look into it to see how it works). Eventually he hires a mommy while his wife is away. She finds out a bit it and they get into an argument which (I hope) escalates to something that ‘feels’ like an affair. It’s like, their relationship was never a sexual thing, but their special roles as mommy and baby. So for the man to go off and find another mommy is totally like cheating on his wife! I have no idea how I’ll end the story let alone when I’ll get the chance to write it. It’s crazy too because I haven’t come up with any new story ideas for a while now, so coming up with this one felt really satisfying. Gawd, school is such a stressor, it’s totally holding me back from thinking up ideas and writing!  (T_T)

😌 content  |  ♫ The Tea Party - Psychopomp
Link

I Like/Dislike #2

Mar. 2nd, 2009   2:08 PM
I like...the smell of a girls hair when they walk by. Does that come off a little creepy?  Because I really don't mean it like that - I don't purposely take a huge whiff or even get closer to sniff.  Cause that's just sorta weird.  But I've always liked the fruity shampooy smell of a girls hair when they pass by.  I've never really paid attention, but I wonder if this only occurs in girls with long hair =\.  I've tried Herbal Essence once and that stuff is amazingly delish to the nose.  I dislike...gum that liquifies the more you chew, as oppose to getting tougher. I bought a pack of Wigley's 5 (Lush flavoured) last Monday cause I needed to break a twenty.  Really wicked flavour and it made my jacket smell tropical cause I kept it in the inner pocket.  But after chewing it for an hour it began liquifying.  Really gross texture - sorta like having a cheese curd in your mouth.  Trident, Dentyne Ice, Extra, and the lot of them don't liquify so what did Wigley's do wrong? o.O  Anyways, I've nearly finished the pack I bought and not to long ago I got two more packages (Colbalt and Elixir) cause they're doing a promo on campus and handing them out for free.  I'll spread the wealth and offer everyone I see some gum just to get rid of them.  The paper they used to wrap the sticks of gum is really nice and shiney though - I've been giving them to my dad because he folds paper crans (he's got over ten thousand, no lies).
🙂 okay  |  ♫ The Mars Volta - Meccamputechture
Link

TGIF

Feb. 27th, 2009   8:45 PM

I <3 Fridays.  So much slacking off to do, so little time.  Started my day off at around 9:30am cause my dad need help unloading his new coffee machines.  He got into coffee machine business around a month ago and his machines from Italy finally arrived.  So I guess this makes him half jobless since getting canned about half a year ago.  These coffee machines are pretty up tight and sophisticated looking, not the shitty ones you see in community centers or wherever.  His are about five foot tall and about three foot long and wide.  You have to load up roasted beans and they grind them on the spot when you buy a coffee (so you can use any type of coffee mean like Starbucks or Tim Horton’s and sell them for way cheaper).  The wicked thing about it is it uses powdered milk, and it can foam this stuff!  So you can make a latte that literally tastes exactly that looks and tastes like one you’d buy from an upscale coffee shop, but for a fraction of the price.  The last few months he’s been browsing through the Toronto business directories for places he can put his machine (he’s got four of them).  Some spiffy places to consider are auto repair shops, funeral homes, and business offices.  But before all that, he’s got to tune each machine, which means a lot of coffee is gonna be made!  This reminds me of the times he’s tried to make new foods he’s never done.  Just off the top of my head, a few years ago he tried to make mango meringue cake – it took him I think eight or nine tries.   

Other than the early morning workout (there were eight boxes and four of them weighed ninety-ish pounds), I watched episodes 14-16 from season five of House MD.  So now I’m caught up!  Sometimes I feel they pack to much plot into each episode.  And it’s a little tricky sometimes determining how much time has passed – especially when they run all these tests in, supposedly, one day.  I had the opposite reaction when I watched CSI (Vegas and Miami, I’m not a fan of NY).  They ran a whole bunch of tests in one day and sure it’s okay in tv land, but in reality some of these tests take hours!  So I’m just sitting there thinking, no way can this be possible – solving a case in a couple of days (if even that).  Oh well, I don’t watch the two series any more, but I’ve downloaded and burnt most of the seasons for each.  I heard Grissom is gone, and though I haven’t watched that far, I think that’s really depressing.  His character is comparable to Dr. House in my opinion – so with him gone the whole dynamic of the show might be different.  But that’s entirely based on speculation.

 I read chapter 437 of Naruto.  Wow!  This issue was a bomb, so far, for the current story arc!  I’ve always loved the Hinata/Naruto thing (even though I don’t read fanfictions, but I’ve seen some drawings) so this chapter was definitely a wet dream to those fans.  I really really hope they don’t kill off Hinata though since cause she gets stabbed, but we don’t see where.  Really cool to see Naruto’s sixth tail come out, but I didn’t quite get why his transformation gave off a skeleton thingy over his fox body – kinda weird looking.  Look forward to the next chapter!  Anyways, I’m really hyped to watch the movie Watchmen!  I read the graphic novel sometime early last year and really loved it.  When I head it was being made into a movie I was a bit worried it wouldn’t do it justice.  But I watched Blunty’s review of it (cause the press screening was a few days ago) and my worries were relieved.  Blunty does really good reviews of movies, plus he’s a hardcore fan of the graphic novel – so he really knows his stuff and what to watch for (plus he makes pretty good videos of other things).  I was a little dishearten when he said the ending is different, but he also said that would have the same effect you’d get from reading the graphic novel.  In the book Ozymandias teleports fake squid aliens to Earth, that blow up upon materializing.  He does this because he’s calculated that humanity would unite itself from this scare/attack (plus, a whole bunch of people get killed when the aliens blow up).  But in the movie, the whole alien dillio doesn’t happen and is replaced with something else.  But Blunty says the ending is consistent with the grand scheme of the movie, so I trust that.  OMG every time I hear about it I get so hyped up and want to watch it even more!  I’d better wear a diaper when the time comes cause I might just wet myself! XD 

The curry at Tako Sushi Japanese Restaurant is really good.  I just have to bring it up cause I recently came back from a very satisfying dinner.  I ordered their Curry Matsu Complete Dinner ($16) and it came with the usual soup and salad, along with 6 pieces of sushi (the really cheap ones), seaweed salad (really small portion, which is good cause I don’t like that stuff), a chicken cutlet (really nice and crispy batter, but in general the chicken was rather bland), shrimp and vegetable tempura (two shrimp, some sweet potato, and other stuff), a bowl of rice, and a bowl of curry.  If you ask me, that’s a hell of a lot of food for only $16, especially at a Japanese restaurant (they’re generally pretty pricey if you don’t order box meals).  I’ve mentioned it before that I really really love curry (Dec 16th – wow it’s been that long?!), and this restaurant does a bang up job!  It’s really interesting the way they do theirs; it’s a darker brown than most places, and it has cubes of apples in it (along with carrots and onion).  I think this is possibly the first time I’ve had a curry with apples in them, and they go together really really well.  They used a firm apple so that it still had some crunch when you bite into it, and it gave a nice contrasting flavour since it’s a bit sweet and sour.  Because the chicken was bland I mixed it in with the curry (donno if you’re suppose to do that) and the two together tasted amazing.  I first thought there wouldn’t be enough curry since there was a lot of chicken and little rice, but proportion wise it balanced out pretty good when I finished.  Definitely a must try if you ever go there (as I’ve mentioned before, there are three sushi restaurants near my house – this is one of them). 

Debating whether to read Sandman or start research for an assignment due Monday...hrmm, decisions decisions.  Oh and random opinion before I end this.  I read in the newspaper that cities mayor Twitters (they listed a bunch of other politicians who do as well).  I thought that that was really cool - since I'm so use to thinking of politicians as, um, not human?  LOL, but you get the idea right?  Anyways, the paper put out a sample of his entries and they were pretty interesting - like seeing him in a new light.  He writes about his family, working out, watching our shitty hockey team play, etc.  Like normal stuff!  And I'm just like, wow, he's a human regardless of how the limelight portray him as. 

🐇 bouncy  |  ♫ Classified - Find Out
Link

filler

Feb. 27th, 2009   2:06 PM

Music and my life...

Good gawd! I have yet to buy a new pair of headphones since breaking mine on Jan12th. It was rather frustrating for the first week since I was so use to listening to music on the bus and while studying outside of home, but I’m getting use to it. I don’t like that I’m getting use to it – sometimes the white noise is just plain annoying. A few days ago I was on the bus and a kid, no more than two years old, wouldn’t shut up! And the mother was doing diddly-squat! The kid was doing one of those fake crying/screaming just to get some attention, and it was obvious everyone else on the bus was going nutty cause of it. So could’ve used some music then…

 I looked into getting some Skullcandy headphones, since they seem to be the tread lately with their super cool designs. I’m seeing a lot more people with headphones than I use to, but I’ve been debating whether or not to go with earbuds instead. I want better sound quality, comfort, and something that’d look like a fashion accessory. I’m not to concern with noise cancelling ‘thingies’ (whatever they’re called), since I can just turn up volume. Headphones fit the description but I don’t really like the fact that I have to tuck the cord inside my jacket because it could snap in cold weather (from what I’ve been told). I find it uncomfortable since I wear a scarf and the cord pops out kinda awkwardly. Also, I don’t like using headphones for when I’m working out at the gym. They tend to fall off when I’m jogging, or getting really sweaty (which his gross). So earbuds would be ideal to work around those cons. Or, get both! But what am I, made out of money or something? So decisions decisions – in the mean time it’ll just mean more days on end without music. I think it sorta has an affect on me, because lately when I’m at home I don’t listen to music anymore. Strange. 

 Its also got me thinking about how much of an importance music is and has been to my life. Like, I don’t mean to sound similar to one of those ‘music saved my life’ bullshit or people who claim to ‘listen to everything but country’ – but music REALLY has been an important part of my life.

 I began learning violin when, I think, I was five. My brother picked up the piano at around the same ago. My parents wanted us to learn how to play an instrument since they didn’t have the luxury growing up. Plus, they wanted it to be something we could fall back on in the future (like teach lessons) if we were jobless – and I don’t say that in a funny way, they literally spoke of it like an option. But come to think of it the whole ‘parents are usually right’ is starting to kick my conscious since I have no clue what I’ll do for a living with a degree in Medical Anthropology (big whoopy…). Also, and most obvious I think, they wanted it to be a fun learning process – it didn’t turn out so much this way since I didn’t take it very seriously. Which is a real shame cause I know I probably could have accomplished a lot more than what I have today…if I’d only taken it more serious. There was something about being forced to practice everyday at the crack of dawn (and later changed to evening) for an hour that made the learning process not fun. It might have also had to do with the fact that I knew I didn’t produce good music – it was like nail on chalkboard when I first began learning. 

 I’ve only had two instructors in the, I think, ten years I studied violin. My first was a complete pussy who showed me zero discipline (and being the bad kid I was I totally abused his weakness). The second was a man I’ve come to hold in high regards for his dedication and handwork to his students and the music community. I’ve studied under him for about seven years and played under his orchestra (as a second violin, later as a trumpet) for about just as long. Eventually in my last couple of years with him his age began to catch up with him – he’d fall asleep sometimes during lessons…it was really awkward. For violin I have a grade nine and for theory I have a grade two rudiments – based on RCM examinations. It’s a bit disappointing, if I had reached grade ten I could have furthered my education to becoming an instructor. You can only take teaching classes with a grade ten certificate – which my brother is eligible for. So, technically, I can’t teach, if I were ever jobless, since my theories certificate has probably expired and I need to compete grade ten (which apparently is suppose to be really difficult). What’s more, when I think about it, if I were to try and get my grade ten now I probably have a good chance because I actually ‘get’ music. It’s like, back then I was really immature about learning how to play violin – I did it mainly because my parents told me to. But now, I actually understand the value of having such an education. Plus, I have a better appreciation for music, thus allowing me to enjoy it better if I were to play it. 

 But who am I kidding, I stopped taking violin lessons during highschool and stopped practicing (or playing leisurely) since the start of university. If I were to attempt to play my violin now it would make grown men cry. I think I can safely say the same for my trumpet. I picked up the trumpet when I was, I think, in grade six. The elementary school I attended taught music and had a band as well (I live in Canada so don’t go thinking marching bands like in the US, we were a concert band). There were weekly practices and we performed during seasonal concerts or against other bands at regional competitions. But then again, it was grade school so this shit was pretty jokes. I remember for a year I switched to flute because it was lighter – I had to walk home that year since I didn’t live far enough for the bus to give me a lift (something about a ‘< 1.2km walks’ rule or what not). Flute wasn’t so bad – strange how a lot of girls play flute. I don’t really understand the gender divisions when it comes to instruments – I mean, anyone can play them so why’s there always this division? From my observation, girls tend to play flute and clarinet while guys usually played brass. There was a pretty good 50-50 for saxophones and percussions. 

 You know how there are cliques in highschool, like drama club students, sports team students, etc? I was a music nerd. After grade nine, music courses are an elective. Every year after grade nine I’d take a music course. I was the section leader for the concert band, the lead trumpet player for the jazz band, and a teaching assistant for the enrichment band (it was a band where seniors taught grade 8 and 9 students – then perform at the end of each semester). I remember the head of the music department, Mr. Horner. He was by far one of the most awesome teachers I’ve ever had, as well as the most dedicated. He was like a fuking machine. He ran the Jr/Sr Jazz band, the Jr/Inter/Sr Concert band, and a couple other bands. You have no idea how much work was involved for him – having to conduct during practices, teach during classes, select scores, sort administrative stuff, etc. I swear to gawd he was at school everyday from 7am to 7pm (if not later). His eyes were always bloodshot. It was kinda creepy since he had really nice blue eyes. It was pretty apparent he was burning out when I was in grade 12 cause the other music teachers in the department began taking some of his duties. Man, I’m getting all reminiscent, but he totally deserves being remembered – there were very few teachers so down-to-earth as he was at the highschool I attended. 

 While I was in my second violin instructors orchestra playing second violin, I can recall how little fun I had. The scores for second violin are bullshit compared to first violins. I mean, who actually enjoys playing the background noise? I mean, sure second violin is kinda important, but we always get drowned by the first – plus they always got to play the catchy tunes. I think it must suck to be a first chair second violin, it’s like you’re good enough to play first violin but instead you have to front for the whimpy seconds. Anyways, that wasn’t really why I disliked going to orchestra practice. I disliked it because they were held on Friday nights at a community center a good 25min drive away. What’s more, Stephanie and our group of friends would go out every week on Fridays tow watch am movie. I had to sit through three hours of bullshit when I could have been enjoying my Fridays with my friends. I couldn’t skip orchestra practice if I wanted to because the conductor was my violin instructor – I see him once a week for my lessons anyways! I was pretty much obligated to go as well since he’d give me a guilt trip for all the years I’ve been with him and what not. The only real, memorable thing I enjoyed was driving back home. I would go 140km/hr on this one lane street that was poorly lit and it was fuking awesome! It was also kinda terrifying too since I drove an 89’ Toyota Corolla. I remember a couple of times when it’d rain…man was it ever relaxing, driving in the rain at night with a narrowmindedness of a teenager. But anyways, the orchestra bullshit continued when I was transferred to trumpet since they were in dyer need for some. I eventually quit sometime during grade eleven. 

i learned how to play piano since they say if you learn piano after you'll have better finger coordination or something - plus it just benefits you musically over all
- wast of money?


trumpet, school, friends, about me, family, violin, music
Link

Anthropology and an early weekend

Feb. 26th, 2009   9:52 PM
Aside from not having more than three hours of shut eye the night before, my day went really well.  I stayed up until six in the morning working on my heritage site analysis assignment - which is the reason why I went to the museum on Feb 21.  I focused my paper on the 'Mysteries of Ancient Ukraine' exhibit and criticized it using Laurajane Smith's concept of the 'authorized heritage discourse'.  I could have easily used a lot of other concepts and readings from the course (such as the World Heritage List's concept of 'tangible heritage') but it would have required more time and work.  Putting the exhibit in conversation with the author was easiest and the prof is really fair in that she doesn't favour a paper with more references to a paper with less - instead the quality outways the quantity.  So I'm somewhat pleased with the six pages I churned out; less so that it took me a while resulting in a lack of sleep.

I don't have a working printer at home so I emailed a copy to Jully and asked her to do it for me.  Normally I would have been able to done it at the school computers, but our class was meeting up at the Royal Ontario Museum for a field trip (must be a record for me!  going to the ROM twice in one week!).  We were all suppose to meet at the staff entrance, but not surprisingly two people from the class (and there are only a dozen people taking the course) were late - namely Jully.  It sorta etches at my brain from time to time whenever she's late to whatever things we do together.  But it might just be because I like to be punctual - honestly I arrive early for practically everything and it makes me feel dorky sometimes for doing so.  But oh well, I pass that bit of time with music or pacing or looking at my watch every ten seconds or ... (lol). 

So part of the field trip was spent on discussing this weeks reading (since it is a seminar class after all), 'Contested Representations: Revisiting Into the Heart of Africa' by Shelley Ruth Butler.  The prof was able to get one of her curator buddies, an Africanist, to guide our discussions.  She talked non-stop, and unfortunately for me I was sitting in an uncomfortable squeaky wooden chair.  She didn't gear the discussions towards the book as much as I'd have liked her to and instead focused more on what her occupation involves, the economics of a museum, and the contrasting public/private views the museum attracts.  But that may have been because I only finished a quarter of the book and hoped the discussions would supplement the rest.  After an hour and a half of discussion in, what I think was, the staff room for the anthropology department (it was on the fourth floor), the curator gave a brief tour of the African section in the museum.  Funny enough, of the six hours I spent at the ROM on the 21st, I missed a fairly huge portion of the museum!  I didn't visit a lot of the natural history exhibits, but I also completely missed out on the dinosaurs!  (and everything around the dinosaurs section - more world culture stuff like Egypt, Africa, Middle East, etc). 

Luckily after her brief tour our class ended and we were left to our own leisure.  Jully, Emy, Cheri and I (literally, the only Asians in the class) managed to branch off from everyone else (the Caucasians, har har) and pretty much fooled around like ten year olds.  I cannot ever recall not having a fun time when I'm with Emy.  She is so down to earth and can connect with probably anyone - those are some of the best kinds of people to chill with.  We looked at pretty much everything I missed from my previous visit.  We also took some pretty fobby pictures (as that seemed to be our theme and topic of interest for the most part).  I was feeling pretty wasted by about 3pm because I hadn't eaten anything at all (got out of bed at 10, skipped breakfast, got to the ROM by 12, skipped lunch, and endured a lecture and an extensive tour).  Unfortunately there aren't many restaurants within eyeshot of the ROM.  The museum has a restaurant (or food thingy, I've never actually seen the inside of it) but it's a pretty safe bet that everything is expensive on the menu.  Fuk, even the gift shop was selling the most useless things for well ever $20 (a shiny paper-weight rock for $24?  LOL, kiss my ass!).  There are two coffee shops, a hot dog stand, a shady looking Chinese and Vietnamese restaurant, and a McDonald's just outside the museum.  Neither of us were to enthusiastic with the latter, but we settled with it since we were starving.  The late lunch was pretty jokes - hell of a lot of good laughs going on amongst us (more than likely enough to draw attention).  We eventually called it a day and went our separate ways at around 4pm-ish (oddly enough none of the public transports we took were the same). 

I didn't do much of anything when I got home.  It felt kind of weird since I actually got a chance to catch my breath after a stressful week (one midterm, one assignment, and one postponed assignment!).  I was really surprised when I looked at the page counter on my DiaperSpace account.  The night before it was at 850ish and now it is over 1000!  I think that's the highest number of profile views I've seen in such a short amount of time - must be because of my charming good looks ~.^ (i kid i kid).  I watched House MD, season five, episode 12 and 13.  I use to follow with the show on tv - it was quite literally the only hour I'd ever spend a week infront of a tv.  Since moving to a Monday time slot, however, I haven't been following with it.  No particular reason why I hadn't just changed my schedule around.  I use to use watchtvsitcoms.com to watch tv shows online, but the site got deleted.  I hadn't made much of an effort to look for a new video site, since I usually just download the torrents, but my hard drive is getting pretty full so I began looking.  tv.blinkx.com seems okay for now - it's a portal site.  megavideo.com has this 72min limit per sitting (then you have to wait an hour before watching again) which I find really annoying. 

Here's a really interesting tidbit I learned recently!  Hugh Laurie (aka Dr. House) is cousins with prof. Hugh McKenzie of the anthropology department at UTSC!  I took an osteology course with prof. McKenzie last year and he's a pretty cool guy.  He offered to take on two students over the summer to help with his research in Siberia.  I was somewhat interested at the time, but didn't apply for it.  A friend of mine, Derik, got that opportunity to go with him.  Derik is really lucky because he also got chosen by our prof. for the current osteology class I'm taking.  I don't remember where she's doing her research in, but it's abroad and she offered two spots at the start of the course.  Anyways, I also watched a bit of 'Dr. Horrible's Sing-Along-Blog' because I was in the mood for some musical (don't ask >>).  I really love this flick because it's hilarious, tragic, catchy, and so easy to jump into (since I skimmed through most part).  I've probably watched it at least five times.  Neil Patrick Harris plays the main character really well.  And it's so low production so you'd expect it to be cheesy but it isn't!  Mad props to the writer and director Joss Whedon - I still follow his comic book series (Runaways and Astonishing X-Men) even though the next issues are months away ><!  I got one assignment to do by Monday and a few articles to read, but other than that my weekend starts now ^^.
😎 relaxed  |  ♫ Radiohead - Hunting Bears
Link

I Like/Dislike #1

Feb. 24th, 2009   11:03 PM
I'm gonna start a new subject of continuous discussion.  The title and tag will be 'like/dislike' (I don't really like starting new tags - it gets confusing when I have to many of them).  I find that for some websites whenever I fill out my profile it seems really dorky to say more than necessary, but at the same time this results in cliche things to write about.  Plus, I don't like thinking on the spot for these things (sorta like when someone asks what my favourite movie is - there are quite a few I can't recall but hate to leave out).  Also, what better way to get to know someone than to know their likes and dislikes.  I'll try my best to avoid being broad (sometimes it's unavoidable especially if it's particular things), and making sure to explain why I like or dislike something.  Here we go!

I like...swaying my legs when I sit in a chair that's higher than my feet can touch the ground.
I've always felt a little closer to being a little kid when I sway my legs in a tall seat.  I get this sorta feeling whenever I ride the TTC bus - the 95 line still uses the really old buses which I frequently ride, the newer buses have seats where I can touch the ground.  In the old buses there are seats at the back (behind the second exists) where they're just a little to high for my legs to fully planter on to the floor.  I'd say only 50% of my feet can actually touch the floor, but I'm not entirely certain.  I blame the uber contour butt and back rests.  Oh well, I don't really like sitting at the back since you're facing the side of the bus and the jerking motion is kinda uncomfortable.

I dislike...sitting behind someone on the bus or subway when they sneeze or cough. 
It doesn't even have to be directly behind them, it could be a couple seats behind even!  I blame sneeze pictures - first saw one in an anthro class in second year - they totally scarred me for life.  I don't have a phobia of germs but can you imagine getting a whiff of a sneeze?!  Research says one third of the world has tuberculosis - but it's just dormant and inactive due to our strong immune system.  Makes you wonder what other respiratory infectious diseases you can catch.  I hope that picture has scarred others xD  beware of the cooties! 
🥱 bored  |  ♫ Queen - Innuendo
Link

God Squad! Kapow!

Feb. 24th, 2009   12:06 AM
Hooza!  I just finished reading the four story arc of Sandman, 'Season of Mists' (issue #21-28).  Really wicked plot.  It's about Lucifer (ruler of hell) closing down Hell because he's tired of his duties and all that jazz.  He kicks out all the souls and demons - which raises the dead in the living world and displaces some demons.  Previous issues do help build up what's going on in this story arc but it isn't completely necessary to have read them to know what's going on.  The prologue issue (#21) is really cool.  As I've mentioned in previous posts, I find Dream's family really fascinating (before this story are we've only met Death, Desire, and Despair).  In the prologue Destiny (the oldest of the Endless family) assembles a meeting with his siblings - which includes Delirium (she's not ok in the head, to tripped off on life).  In terms of personality, my favourites are still Dream and Death.  Desire is really interesting - I can't imagine  how Neil Gaiman modelled his characters.  Desire is pretty much everything and anything a person could want (because of this Desires appearance is female and male - never sticking with one).  Desire is sort of the bad apple in the family, always stirring up trouble.  In the meeting Desire starts an argument with Dream which prompts the backbone of the story arc - and rightfully everyone stand behind Desire (possibly beecause Desire is making them but more likely because Desire is correct). Issues #22-23 is about Dream going to Hell and confronting Lucifier, and finishing with Lucifier giving Dream the key to Hell.  So now Dream owns some monstrous real estate and doesn't know what to do with it.  So he bids it off to people of authoritative power - each offering him something he might want (like information or really rare items).  I totally loved this part of the story (issue #24-27).  Dream invites Thor, Odin and Loki from Norse mythology; Anubis and Bast from Egyptian mythology; Susanoo-no-mikoto from Japanese mythology; Angels Duma and Remiel from Christianity; Shivering Jemmy, a Lord of Chaos with the body of a child and the mind of a monster; Order a Dijinn-like entity; Azazel a biblical demon; and the fairies Cluracan and Nuala.  They all party it up and get wasted before talking business - comedy gold, especially Thor!  Each have their own motives for wanting to own Hell and each offer up something interesting.  This part of the story reminds me of the 'God Squad'; created during Marvel's Secret Invasion in 'The Incredible Hercules' comic book.  The 'God Squad', named by Amadeus Cho, consists of: Snowbird, Hercules, Ajak, Amatsu-Mikaboshi, Demogorge, and Cho.  Short lived but super funny.  There seriously needs to be more of these - God gatherings (lol) - because with what goes down it's so friggin jokes.  So anyways, Dream decides to give the key to Hell to the two angels because their Creator said so - they think it's unjust and unfair since they're essentially being kicked out of Heaven (which sucks).  They rule Hell with a soft heart though, I don't really know how they're fair or if they'll pop up again in later issues.  The rest of the God Squadlers are either accepting of Dreams decision or really bitter (Azazel starts beef and eventually gets trapped in a jar by Dream).  Best part, I think, was when Chaos threatened Dream during their business talk, and than offered him a ballon with Chaos transformed back into the form of a little girl.  I LOL'd when I saw Dream holding on to a red ballon xD.  Overall I really enjoyed this story arc - probably my favourite so far! Blah, so reading that story arc went against my better judgement - told myself i'd commit my night to studying for my midterm tomorrow.  =.= plus I'm getting sleepy now and if I climb into bed for a quick nap there's practically no garentee I'll get out of bed until morning.  But if I don't rest my head and attempt to study for a few hours nothing will really get retained.  Stupid drowsiness.  I could make myself some coffee but than it's such a hassle to bring down everything I need for studying from my room to the kitchen... So anyways, I got back my deconstructing epidemiology (ANTC68) and not surprisingly I got a 70% (I completely bombed definitions, but did well with short answers and essay questions).  I've always been a 70s student so I'm more happy that I didn't fail than being disappointed that I didn't get higher.  I was studying for my osteology bellringer, which was the day before my epidemiology midterm - so I feel that that can serve as a good platform for excuses.  
💤 sleepy
Link

Best Invented Words

Feb. 23rd, 2009   8:18 AM
Meant to post this yesterday.  Taken from The Toronto Star (local newspaper) on Feb 22, 09.  Compiled by John Sakamoto.  The Week's Best Invented Words!

JUNIOR MOMENT, n.:
a brief period during which an adult is unable to remember how to act maturely. (trendwatching.com)

PHOTOGAFFE, n.:
a picture taken in a moment of poor judgment that ends up on the Internet. (Cindy McMillan, Brooklin, Ont.)

AUTOLANCHE, n.:
what cascades onto the front seat when you open your car door before you brush the snow off your car roof. (Mike Allum, Bowmanville, Ont.)

CARCOLEPSY, n.:
"a condition affecting buddies on a trip, who fall asleep as soon as the car starts moving, providing no company or driving help." (urbandictionary.com)

DOOM LOOP, n.:
what ensues when a company's cost-cutting isn't enough to offset low profits – which leads to another round of cost-cutting. (businessweek.com)

HYPERVENTIDATE, v.:
to suffer the unfortunate side effect of too much speed dating. (Jen Kuta and Nigel Russell, Toronto)

ROMANTHICK, n.:
"someone who habitually looks for love in all the wrong places and wonders why it turns out badly." (verbotomy.com)

First four are pretty jokes ^^, though the last three I'd probably have difficulty squeezing in to normal conversation. 
Lastly before I start my day, I checked the updates on my marks and I got a 83% on my first assignment for foundations of epidemiology (ANTC67).  Wicked :D.  Considering I only use two scholarly references (when five were required), but I made up the losses with a lot of non-academic news source based references (the topic I did the paper on is the cholera epidemic in Zimbabwe today - so non-academic is the most current, but not reliable-ish source to use).  Hopefully I'll do just as well for the second assignment, which is due this Friday :3.

✨ chipper
Link

filler (anthro)

Feb. 22nd, 2009   10:39 PM

Someone: So what are you studying?

Me: I’m doing a specialist program in Medical Anthropology at UTSC.

Someone: That sounds pretty cool, what exactly is that?

Me: …

 

Since choosing my subject post in second year, I’ve never been to enthusiastic when someone asks about my field of study.  I have a hard time convincing others I’m not a complete moron probably because I can’t convince myself that what I’m studying is what I’m enthusiastic about.  I decided to go in to anthropology primarily because it was my best subject in first year – I took two intro classes as electives.  I thought the subject was interesting but never really looked ahead to find out it might lead in to occupational-wise.  In second year I took a lot of cultural development classes. 

 

I’m never to enthusiastic when someone asks about my education primarily because I’m uncertain with what I’m really getting myself into.  One the one hand I should be somewhat proud to proclaim how fortunate I am to be attending a post-secondary school.  But on the other hand, what good is that education if it doesn’t propel you closer to some sort of future – whether it be occupational-wise or self fulfilling-wise? 

If I were to define anthropology very broadly based on everything I’ve come to learn about it, I would say that it is ‘the study of the seemingly mundane aspects in everyday life, often done by examining its discourses.’  Dictionary.com

 

I’m ever to enthusiastic when people ask me what it is I’m studying and what exactly it’ll get me into occupational-wise.

 

Link

Munch! *nom nom nom*

Feb. 22nd, 2009   2:28 AM

Tonight was good :3  Went to my first ABDL munch of the year after several months of not going.  Met some people I recognized but couldn't put a name to, met some people I have no recollection of ever meeting, and I met some old faces who I knew but they may not have known me :P.  The last time I went, I think five months ago, it was held at Fran's Restaurant - but due to the poor service it is now held at Groundhog Pub.  Cozy place and the pizza is delish ^^.  Normally I don't like thin crust cause some places make it taste like I'm eating a cracker, but their pizza wasn't bad; it had a bit of a doughy texture which I like.  Went pretty heavy on the fluids (had a pint of Moosehead and two coke's) but I came prepared heh.  I had on an Attends Slip Regular over an Attends Slip.  It was nice and cushiony when I went but when I left it had a bit of a waddle going on {o'.'o}.  I snapped a few shots so I’ll upload them on DiaperSpace and DLTown.  The turn out was really good as well considering the heavy snow fall.  I think I counted around fifteen to eighteen people; some people left early while others came later.  Omg, and there were two other Asians!  Both of them were first timers and unfortunately I didn’t get much opportunity to know them better since they sat at each end and I was sorta in the middle.  Of the four or five times I went last year there wasn’t a single Asian person (well, technically there was this one other guy but he wasn’t into the ABDL scene, he just accompanied his partner to the munches.  He doesn’t go to them anymore though).  There were about five first timers that showed up and it was cute cause some of them were really shy and didn’t speak up much but I could totally relate.  My first munch I was exactly the same!  It just seemed as though everyone knew everyone and just jumping in to a conversation seemed so intrusive (feel free to read more about my first time in my Dec 31, 08 post ~.^)  I hope the experience didn’t make them feel uncomfortable enough not to come back – that would be a real bummer.  But anyways, I’m looking forward to next month (even though my memory won’t hold up to matching names to faces accurately) since there’s a little incentive – Boy Ricky and his daddy are holding a party at their house afterwards ^^.  

In comic book news, I have finished the third story arc in my Sandman collection - issue #17-20.  I don't really known if it's accurate to call it a story arc since each issue has nothing to do with each other, but it's certainly the third paperback of the ten available.  I didn't like issue #19 as much as I liked the other three - it was about the Dreamlord and Shakespeare - mainly because it just seemed so ordinary - maybe I just expected to much from it to begin with.  Issue #18 was awesome - it's about a cat cult! It did a really good job showing that comic books can be more than just stories of adventure or action or superheros.  Granted, there are many titles around that aren't geared in those directions and still have a really good storyline, but this issue captured 'it' in just twenty-five pages.  The way in which the story is narrated reminded me of the short anime OVA 'She and Her Cat' by the amazingly talented Makoto Shinkai.  In issue #19 Death makes an appearance to assist Element Girl (the female Metamorpho - a DC superhero).  She's so indifferent with what she does (appear when people are about to die and essentially tell them 'you're dead') it makes her appear so cool.  Issue #17 is my favourite in the collection.  It's about a chick whose held captive, and by doing so plot ideas are generated in your mind (though you may need to bang her in order for it to work - it wasn't to clear on that).  This came in handy for a writer down on his luck.  Wish I had the opposite though - someone to help me write my stories while I just churn out ideas.  For me, it's challenging to refine the ideas and put it into writing.  It's not like I can't do it, it's just I don't like doing it since it often takes up a lot of time.  But then again I guess I also consider that the fun part in writing stories.  (Did I just contradict myself?  If I did, whatever.  It made sense when I thought of it!  See?!  Damn you writing!)

💤 sleepy  |  ♫ Metric - The Twist
Link

Welcome to the world baby cousin!

Feb. 21st, 2009   2:39 PM
Just got home not to long ago from the hospital.  Went with my parents to visit my aunt Liana and uncle Viktor.  My aunt gave birth to her son yesterday morning!  Congratulations!  His name is Mitchell Timothy Chao and he's 6.3 pounds.  Personally, I think his name is really white for a Chinese person, but whatever I'll get use to it.  His middle name like most Chinese people, is the equivocal to his Chinese name - in this cause it'd obviously be pronounced in Chinese, but that Chinese name would sound similar to Timothy.  I'm probably making it sound more complicated than it really is, but take for example my middle name: Wykin.  If you said it in Chinese it'd make complete sense, but writting it down in English is just by how the word sounds and makes up to be.  Anyways, I don't recall ever having gone to visit someone so soon after a birth and seeing what a baby so young would look like (maybe I have since I have four cousins a lot younger than me, but I don't remember) so being there felt pretty special.  He didn't open his eyes unfortunately but his parents say they look like my aunts.  Right now it's a little to early to say who he resembles in general.  It was a bit different seeing my mom hold the baby and nurturing it - I guess I feel a mixture of nostelgia and desire.  She would make these cute little noises and be really gentle and motherly (duh), and I guess I just wasn't use to seeing that side of her.  It was really beautiful.  The whole being there was beautiful.  We stayed for about an hour and my parents exchanged a lot of memories and 'how to's from experience'.  Some embarrasing stories shared to -.-  Really crazy thing happened too, we were talking about pediatricians cause they hadn't been assigned to one.  So my parents were talking about my pediatrician (and I was born in the same hospital as Mitchell) cause his office is literally a couple of blocks away from the hospital.  Not soon after I noticed someone walk past the room enterence who looked like my pediatrician so I called out to him and it really was him!  My parents and I haven't seen him in like seven or eight years so it nuts running in to him.  We introduced him to my aunt and uncle, so there's a potential he might look after Mitchell ^^.  I thought it was pretty amazing that he looks almost exactly the same as I remebered him, maybe with just a little more strands of grey hair and a little skinnier.  You'd think people would look much more different after twenty years. 
😊 happy  |  ♫ Joshua Radin - The Fear You Won't Fall
Link

Test your ABDL nerdiness!

Feb. 21st, 2009   1:32 AM
Went to the Royal Ontario Museum today with Joyee to do research for our D15 seminar class.  Lots of walking and note taking.  Tired-ish.  Don't want to talk about it, maybe another day.

Anyways, I just made an ABDL quiz and I'm quite proud of it (lol).  I intended to post it on DiaperSpace because 90% of the quizzes posted there are lame and stupid.  My quiz didn't pass the approval the first time around - no idea why - so now I'm just waiting to see if they'll reject it a second time.  I sent a copy to DLTown (while discovering I made an account there a while ago and only recently rediscovering it) and it's waiting for approval - even though there isn't a single quiz there!  So whatever, at least I know I don't have to wait for approvals on the ABDL Story Forum and of course my own blog.  Enjoy. 

Question 1
Which diaper holds the least amount of fluids?
a) Mega Care Line Premium
b) Tranquility ATN
c) Attends 10
d) Abena Abri-Form X-Plus
e) Molicare Super

Question 2
Which of these is an ABDL story?
a) The Double Life of Ariel Clownford
b) Ashley’s Diaper Adventures
c) The Funtime Diapered Friends Hour
d) A Merry Sakura Christmas
e) Tiffany’s Tales in Diapers

Question 3
Which is the newest website listed?
a) DPF.com
b) Aby.com
c) WetSet.net
d) deeker.com

Question 4
Which is the largest sized pacifier on the market?
a) NUK Medic Pro Size L
b) NUK 6
c) NUK 3
d) NUK Maxi Trainer

Question 5
Is it possible to be LG/Daddy/AB/Furry?
a) Yes
b) No

Question 6
How many diapers make up an ‘Orca Stack’?
a) 4
b) 5
c) 6
d) 7

Question 7
Which issue of Incontinent Student Bodies does Mike break his arm in?
a) 2
b) 3
c) 4
d) 5

Question 8
By paying, the service of an ABDL nursery is available everywhere but…
a) Montreal
b) San Francisco
c) Greater Manchester
d) Vancouver
e) Phoenix

Question 9
‘Diaper stuffing’ means…
a) Getting high while diapered
b) Simulating a messy diaper using household stuff
c) Obtaining diapers through theft
d) Layering multiple diapers for greater absorbency

Question 10
Diapers haven’t made an appearance on…
a) Nip/Tuck
b) Weeds
c) That 70s Show
d) Scrubs
e) Lost







Answers:

1 – c    2 – b    3 – b    4 – a    5 – a    6 – a    7 – b    8 – d    9 – b    10 – c 

🌿 calm  |  ♫ Jadakiss - Feel Me (skit)
Link

Ah damnit...

Feb. 18th, 2009   10:31 PM
This is a record, let it be marked in the passages of this blog.  My 15 day old Skullcandy Lowrider headphones have, literally, snapped in half.  I normally go through a pair of earbuds every four or five months so I decided to buy headphones thinking they'd be more durable.  Thank gawd I bought a warranty or else I'd lose my mind!  I wasn't even using them rougly or anything.  I totally blame the way they're designed.  You can fold them up (starting from the center then tucking each ear piece in), and they do sorta feel fragile because of that.  The crappy thing is when mine snapped, the plastic hinges broke off to so they can't be fixed (except with tape...tape fixes everything!).  I'll probably go get a new pair tomorrow, if those break in less than a month I'll get another pair!  Then i'll put the new pair of shitty headphones on eBay and get my moneys worth!  Besides that, I feel somewhat proud of myself today.  I've been putting off a short paper that's due Monday worth 5%.  Relatively worthless compared to everything else thats required in that course, but finishing it and not having to think about it feels pretty good.  If only I could do that with all my assignments...if only.  Speaking of which, I'll be going to the Royal Ontario Museum on Friday with Jully for our seminar class - we have to do a site analysis of a 'heritage' site.  I haven't been to the ROM in a couple of years - last time I went was with Bunny, one of Stephanie's roommates.  Admission is half price on Fridays starting from 4:30pm ^^.  I'd like to get through three articles for that course (which was actually last weeks readings) before going to the museum.  Hopefully I'll be able to pull it off.  Than I still need to study for my midterm on Tuesday, then I need to hand in two assignments the following days.  The coming week is going to be hell.  But I'll try to enjoy myself as much as can before than - I'll be going to the ABDL Munch on Saterday! I read the first two story arcs of Sandman (#1-8 and 9-16).  Really enjoying it so far.  The second story arc, particularly issue #14, was really creepy - it's about a 'collectors' convention.  Collectors are people who kill people for whatever reason, some people take souveniers (like lips, ears, eyes, etc).  I'm really liking the Dreamlord's family too; so far I've met Death and Desire.  Can't wait to read more.  I'm also in the process of finishing WingZ's Muskahovie Summer.  I'm on chapter eight of ten.  I really like how chapter four sorta sets up the following chapters - the three other categories Zac puts girls in.  Chapter five, super cute!  Lizzy's little crush on Zac kinda reminds me of something from my childhood (I'd write about it, but i barely remember all the details so no sense making a puzzle out of it).  And chapter six, Gary had it coming he totally deserved an ass whooping.  And lastly, while talking about leisurely things, I have caught up to the fansubbers for Soul Eater.  I watched episode 35-43 in two days.  The plot is meh.  I'd like to see more action, but right now it's heavy in character development.  I seriously want to punch Maka in the face in episode 42 because she's being such a friggin' emo!  Normally I don't care much about emo's but she's constantly like this in the serie!  She feels sorry about something for the longest time, than after a lecture or quick realization she goes back to being all peachy.  Then after all the excitment she finds something else to dread over!  Fuk it's annoying.  And she's suppose to be team leader?  Death the Kid has way better leadership skills than her skinny ass.  Plus she's weak as shit!  AGH! She fustrates me to no end!!  Anywhoo, I downloaded 'Hounddog' and 'Last Life in the Universe', haven't watched either.  I really want to watch the latter, but the .srt file (subtitles) isn't sychronized properly and the other srt files i'm trying to download have no seeds/leechers.  Kinda bites, but whatever.  Peace out.
😑 blah  |  ♫ Last Emperor - Secret Wars Pt 1
Link

When I was three years old...

Feb. 18th, 2009   12:11 AM

While trying to fall asleep last night I read three chapters of WingZ’s Muskahovie Summer.  I don’t recall if I’ve read it before (something that happens to often – forgetting things I’ve read) but whatever, I had time to kill.  He doesn’t write much about childhood and it’s kind of apparent – like, just the way the character thinks and the way its written (ie. word complexity).  It makes sense though since it’s coming from a ‘back when I was younger’ narrative…I think.  Okay enough of that, what I wanted to get at was that the story made me remember something from my own childhood.  I use to go to a lot of day camps when I was a kid – like pre-kindergarten, March break, summer, and so forth.  I also use to go to a babysitter everyday after school with my brother and two other kids while in elementary school, but that’s for another time.  I went to this pre-kindergarten learning center/daycare when I was three called Happy Time Child Care Center.  But considering I’m the most forgetful person I know, there could be no way of me ever remembering anything from that place let alone the name of it!  They had this pretty cool system where they’d slap some line paper in a duotang and the teachers would write comments about the kids they’d look after.  Then at the end of the week they’d give the duotang to the parents for them to read and write their own thoughts.  It was a way of supplementing the verbal feedback when the parents pick up their kids – ‘certainly not a substitute’ as it says on the first page of the ‘communication report’.  I don’t think my parents know or perhaps they’ve forgotten about it, but this report has been in my possession for at least a decade.  (May be they do know I have it and it’s just a ploy to make me think about things I’ll be writing about shortly!) 

From what I recall, I probably read this report once a year – each time it makes me think, then I put it aside and forget about it until the next time something makes me remember of it.  I can’t say what part of WingZ’s story made me want to dig it up, but that’s what I did a few hours ago.  It actually took me a while to find it; it was buried beneath my bed.  It’s kind of funny because one of my earliest memories of camp/daycare comes from the person who writes in the report (the teacher/caretaker).  Her name is Cory and although my memory may be incorrect, I’ve put one particular face to that name.  Caucasian female, mid-twenties, very short curly hair, fairly big eyes, and a nice smile.  That image might be for some other camp councillor, but in my mind I’ve always associated it to the name Cory.  So anyways, the very first entry is dated to March 19, 1991 and the last is May 22, 1991.  From what’s written midway into the report the center was closing.  The place is no stranger to my parents, my older brother attended the same daycare. 

I don’t have much recollection of most of my childhood.  I remember getting spanked a lot for doing dumb shit – which is funny cause a lot of what I remember was more bad than good and nothing really in between.  So reading this report each time (and forgetting what I’ve read since the last time) shines a new light on what my childhood was like, if even a very very small portion of it.  As I’ve mentioned in previous posts, the ABDL side of me has always yearned to relive the times when I was four or five.  I was three when I was at this daycare.  I don’t know shit about children – as in size-to-age measurements, progression in cognitive development, ability to read/write, etc – so it was somewhat of a surprise to read that I could have mediocre conversation with people (heck, if I didn’t know better I’d have thought three year olds would’ve been potty trained yet).  So I’m just going to summarize and make point-forms of the entries from the report, followed by commentaries in brackets.

-          Teenage Mutant Ninja Turtles were the fad.  My favourite was Michael Angelo

-          During the lunches they provided I usually passed on dessert, which was fruit cocktail (strange since kids like sweet things)

-          On the first day during naptime I cried because I didn’t want to sleep – my parents wrote it was because I missed my teddy bear (I don’t have any recollection of sleeping with a teddy bear when I was little.  But strangely, I do remember having a stuffed animal collection growing up.  One of my most cherished stuffed animals was a blue rabbit my babysitter gave me).

-          On the second day, Cory writes I’m ‘quick as a whip and very straightforward’, that I’m ‘personable and gets along with everyone’, and that I’m more outgoing than my brother – since she’s taken care of my brother.

-          My dad wrote back saying that I was more of an extrovert than my brother, and that I’m much quicker to tears and pouty faces (and falling asleep on long car rides).

-          Also on the second day, I cried when we were told to come inside from the playground (I was a cry baby, as apparent in pages to follow – told by my parents).

-          I really loved Duplo/Lego, Fisher Price people, cars, arts and craft, roleplaying, and making props for roleplaying

-          My dad wrote that on the weekend we, my family, went to the museum to see the dinosaurs – and that I was disappointed since I was expecting live dinosaurs (thought that that was cute!).  Afterwards we went to Movenpick’s for Shirley Temple, ice cream and fries (I actually remember going to Movenpick’s quite as a kid whenever we went downtown).

-          In another of my dad’s weekend entry, it says that I got envious of my brother since it was receiving more attention for learning how to ride the bike

-          He also writes that I hold grudges – ‘If he feels that he wasn’t being treated fairly, he’ll get mad and put on a grudge pout face for hours’…or throw tantrums (yeah, it’s being to sound like I was a ‘problem’ child).

-          On the third weekend of April my family did two things.  1) my brother and I got suits for an upcoming wedding (my mom’s brother’s wedding – I can clearly recall the picture of us in the suits) 2) my parents got us pet turtles (I remember having turtles for pets up until nine or ten years old – than we freed them in a stream.  We named the turtles Leonardo and Rapheal (TMNT *hint**hint*)).

-          Apparently the room I was in is called the Funshine room (I googled Happy Time Child Care Center and came up with nothing, but found something for Funshine Child Care – their website says they’ve been operating since 1985, but their location is really far from where I use to live so I’m not sure if they’re related (since, as I’ve mentioned, Happy Time was closing).  I’m considering calling them and asking whoever picks up a bunch of odd history questions @_@)

-          In one of Cory’s entries, she says that I mentioned to her that my favourite ice cream is strawberry (It is…well, it was.  I donno how things happen, but I’m lactose intolerant now (and for the last six or seven years).  I have no clue how things turn around just like that since, as a kid, I use to guzzle milk like it was nothing!).

-          In the second last entry my dad writes, it states that my parents haven’t found a proper daycare for me, and they’re seriously considering Montessori.  They feel that their type of instruction will be good for my personality and character (as in, I need more structure and discipline.  Everytime I read that passage it gives me the jitters.  I think about how different of a person I might have been had my parents sent me to a Montessori, or how different my family would be in general because they cost a lot of money.  I’ve always been really money conscious when it comes to spending it on me – I really don’t like being a financial burden on my parents.  I’ve felt that way for as long as I can remember and each time I get in to trouble that feeling usually accentuates).

-          In Cory’s entry that follows, she states that Montessori is a very structured way of learning – emphasis on self-directed activities teaching life skills – and that there isn’t a wide array of ‘free play’.  Of course she adds that I have ‘the smarts and would catch on’ but she’s also concern about the restriction of creativity – since that’s the kind of kid she sees me as.  (I retain a lot more information through hands on than listening or reading, I’ve always been that way)  Her last paragraph is gold ‘If I had to be say honest – I’m not for a very ‘high structured’ environment.  I think it’s important to be flexible, both with the children and myself.  And I also feel that a large part of learning is done spontaneously.  But by all means, check it out and let him[in lieu of my name] be the judge!’ (I sorta feel I owe Cory for her comments, it may have been the tipping point for my parents’ decision.  I don’t ever remember visiting a Montessori – let alone my parents bringing up their initial decision to me.  So as you can imagine, reading this issue always makes me think of how much of something else I could have been).

-          In my dad’s last entry, it comments on the previous point.  Verbatim: ‘Hmmm, too structured…We found him[in lieu of my name] to be clever…a little too clever at times.  He knows how to play situational games with adults.  He will observe a situation and attempts to manipulate it to suit his wishes.  If he doesn’t gets his own way, tears and pouts and/or grudges and tantrums are a split second away.  However if you defeat his situational play, he will shape up very very quickly.  At home he is quite willfull and will put up a scene on most times that does not please him.  Does he exhibit this tendency at school/daycare?  Primarily for these reasons, we feel he[in lieu of my name] needs the discipline and structure to ground his behaviour into a solid track.’  (This exactly what’s written, there isn’t any bullshit to this!  And it friggin scares the crap out of me each time I read it.  I really don’t know what to think at times.  I mean, I was three years old, I probably knew what I was doing only half the time.  I donno since I’m not a parent, but can one really make a judgement of character at such an early age?  I mean, this is really life changing stuff – had they gone through with it!)(Sometimes I really feel I have no write reading this, but than my cynical thoughts tell me my parents planted this for me to read and constantly think and question what could have been.  It’s terrifying.)

-          In the last entry of the report, Cory recalls experiencing my ‘pouty’ side – I don’t acknowledge her after walking past her, arms crossed, following a playground dispute.  She continues to stand by her principles, stating that I really enjoy my ‘freeplay’ time and that I have a wonderful imagination.  As well that a daycare setting helps to promote good social skills and creativeness – mostly done through play.  She feels that a Montessori-method (one way of doing a task and that is by following steps) stifles creativity and the chance for a child to experience what’s write and wrong and how to solve problems on their own (I thought that that line was gold!).  She finishes with a very direct ‘He[in lieu of my name] has a lot of creativity and I feel it would be best put to use in a setting that encourages it à daycare.

And that sums up three months of daycare – I thought it was quite intense reading about it.  How lucky that such events were in writing or I’d never had known about them, but then again that might have been a really good thing.  I don’t really know what to think of it all.  Sometimes I want to ask my mom about it, but I’m afraid it’d stir up a conversation I’d regret starting.  I would never ask my dad about it since we have bad history.  Some part of me really wants to seek out Cory, perhaps email her and tell her how thankful I am – but then that’d seem like I was a nutcase or something.  Another thing I tend to think about was how retarded I was as a kid.  Like, I was so hyped up and untamed that my parents wanted to send me to Montessori – that’s like boarding school for preschoolers/grades-schoolers.  But in all honesty, I understand where my parents are coming from.  My brother certainly turned out ‘normal’, so why was I so different?  Something definitely needed fixing.  Like I’ve mentioned before, things would have probably been a lot different had I gone to Montessori – probably would have gotten much better grades than the 60-70’s I was so use to seeing in elementary school.  If I raised this issue to my parents, I wonder, were they proud of me than or while growing up?  Unlike 7th Heaven, I cannot remember when my parents have ever said they were proud of me, or that they loved me.  It’s a bit of a heartbreak, really, but I guess I’ve become so conditioned that it doesn’t phase me anymore.

🌀 morose  |  ♫ Queens of the Stone Age - Six Shooter
Link

Lesson learned from a restless night

Feb. 17th, 2009   3:31 AM
Real time news coming at'cha! (I'm typing this on my iTouch while laying in bed) I CANNOT SLEEP!!!!! I rarely, if ever, wear diapers to bed because a) I don't need to, b) there is the possibility my brother might catch me (he wakes up at gawd awful early hours to get to school and he doesn't respect my privacy - plus we share the same room, c) wet diapers smell bad after wearing them for several hours (plus the possibility of diaper rash which I've luckily yet to experience). And now to add to the list d) I'm not use to the feeling of layng on a padded bottom (let along a squishy one) which is thus keeping me awake. I could of course change out, but the noise level will be unsettling. I mean, done get me wrong, I love the damp feeling between my legs as much as the next ABDL, but I'm tired and would really like to get some sleep T_T. I guess I shouldn't have let curiousity gotten the best of me...or had those two glasses of water before bed. But man, I haven't worn since Thursday, so hopefully this reading week I'll have plenty more opportunity,
