Movies to be watched...

Feb. 15th, 2009   11:51 PM
So I was flipping through a Famous magazine (a monthly movie guide magazine you can pick up at theaters) and noticed a lot of movies coming out that I've never heard of.  This is largely due to my lack of television watching and I don't keep current with films besides through tv.  I rarely read the entertainments section in the local papers and most of the trailers I've seen are through YouTube (when they're featured or in five pages of the most viewed).  I don't make it a point to be in the know.  But then again, pop culture is something that's hardly even been something I could keep up with.  I have a hard time remembering all the names and faces and who was in what, and so forth.  But anyways, here's a list of movies I would like to see (in no particular order) based on reading the short description in the magazine than searching up the trailer on YouTube. Coraline - I first heard about this film from Stephanie (she's really into pop culture), and when I watched the trailer I told her it really reminded me of Spirited Away.  The whole 'going off into another world that's sorta-but-not-really like our own because the main characters life is boring and dull' storyline.  She didn't have much recollection of Spirited Away, but it did spring up a long conversation about Totoro.  I think it would be interesting to watch because the stop-motion animation is really wicked.  Plus, it looks like a really trippy movie to get off on if blazing. He's Just Not That Into You - Definitely a chick-flick, but hey, guys can learn a lot from those kinds of movies.  The trailer made it seem like it had a decent amount of humour - not the stupid kind, but the ones that are silly and make you blush.  Its got a lot of A list actors, and to my surprise even has Ben Affleck (I don't know what to think of him.  He's not really good looking nor ugly (both with and without the facial hair).  But his acting has definitely stepped up after some disappointment a few years back). Fanboys - I wouldn't pay to watch this movie.  The only real reason I want to watch this movie is because it looks hilarious and it's got cameos from the most randomest aray of actors (omglolwtf William Shatner).  Plus, who wouldn't want to watch a film parodies parts of Star Wars? It's Not Me, I Swear - The trailer and the tiny blurp in the magazine didn't seem to tell the same story.  But it's a film that won't get much commercial hype because it's one of 'those' films - foreign, underground, etc.  Yea know, those films that are only out in select theaters?  Those are almost always the golden.  It's a shame, really, that not enough people watch them.  But this is all just speculation - the film is in French and it's set in 1968 Montreal. Nurse. Fighter. Boy - After watching the trailer I could already guess how the film would end.  The plot seems to have been done before - although I can't put a finger on which I'm thinking of.  It goes something like, 'Good woman meets bad guy and makes him good.  Good woman has son who gets bullied.  Bad guy turn good helps good woman's son.  Son gets revenge.'  Atleast that's how the trailer went, they're never really clear on endings anyways so I'm just doing a bunch of guess work.  I'm more interested in the acting.  This film looks like the type where the acting really touches your heart and makes you feel something.  Powerful stuff like that are hard to come by. Two Lovers - A sorta love triangle with lots of conflicting interests, kinda movie.  I like Joaquin Phoenix - he's got this serious kinda look to him that I find smexy - and Gwyneth Paltrow is pretty nice on the eyes as well.  I have a feeling Phoenix will lose both girls in the end and I don't think I'd feel bad. The Pool - Another Indian movie, awesome!  I don't compare this to Slumdog Millionaire because they're completely different - but it's the expectations I don't want to set myself up for.  The plot seems pretty simple, a poor Indian boy wants to swim in a wealthy dudes pool, so to get closer to that dream he works for the dude.  I have a feeling that this won't get much commercial hype, but I could be dead wrong since Slumdog took home a couple dozen prizes.  I think the acting will really communicate something beautiful - or I think I want it to (damn, setting expectations). The Necessities of Life - Looks like a really beautiful film and I'm afraid it'll have a heart wrenching ending.  It's about an Inuit hunter with tuberculosis sent to a sanatorium in Quebec City.  He's separated from his family and is placed in unfamiliar land where his is the minority (and when I say minority, it's like you can count how many other Inuits there are in the city).  I don't think this'll get much commercial hype - again, another gem people will brush aside from flicks with hot and flashy celebs/directing (think Twilight vs. Let the Right One In.  Man, fuk Twilight, LtROi is a way better flick that very little people have even heard of.  Both films are about vampires, but LtROi is about forbidden love and it's beautiful - it doesn't catter to teenieboppers. Know1ing - I really liked the trailer, but I'm praying that it isn't one that's misleading (ie. The Happening - complete bullcrap).  I'm a bit borderline when it comes to Nicolas Cage.  One the one hand, I really love him in Family Man, Matchstick Men, The Weather Man, Adaptation, Gone in Sixty Seconds, Leaving Las Vegas, and maybe one or two others I'm forgetting.  On the other hand, I really hate him (and this might have to do with the plot/directing as well) in Ghost Rider, Next, National Treasure 2, The Wicker Man, Bangkok Dangerous, and probably a couple others.  Like, the flims I hate him in are SOOOO cheesy compared to the ones I like him in, which I think are SOOO awesome and/or funny.  Leaving Las Vegas is definitely in my top 10 (but I'm just saying that now, not like I can actually name ten movies I really love without having to think about it for a week).  I donno, I'm a little bit weery about watching this one, but it looks interesting.  Guess I'll wait for more trailers and decide then.
😄 amused  |  ♫ Ghostface Killah - Wu Banga 101
Link

Friday wrap-up

Feb. 14th, 2009   1:42 AM
Well, Friday was a fairly good day to finish off the week.  I pretty much did nothing, almost to a point of pure bordem.  I actually slept most of the afternoon away.   - I read issues one and two of Sandman (issue one is 40 pages, so that's pretty sizable).  Very interesting premise so far.  I love how there is tie-in's with JLA and Hellblazer (I'm interested in reading the latter - might have to get to it after Sandman). - I hopped on Second Life a few times, haven't been on for maybe two weeks.  I don't normally go on unless I'm really bored because, well, I don't know many people on SL or have many things I particularily like to do.  I'm hinged about building, I like it but I can't keep up with the whole sculpt primes stuff (or more accurately I'm to lazy to learn how to use Blender).  But luckily today I had the opportunity to hang out with Happyfeet Hax then Di5Red Moulliez, than Happy again.  It was interesting because I really miss the random nuggets of fun I had with Happy.  And talking with Di5Red was really great since I don't often talk to other ABDL's on the same level and openness as I do with him.  Though, our little chess game was one to be fumbling, heh. - I watched Pulse (2006).  I thought the acting was OK and the way it was filmed was OK.  But the overall story telling was boring.  It lacked horror and this is coming from a person who use to hate watching horror flicks.  They use to scare the crap out of me, but I grew out of that mentality.  Now, most horror films are pretty predictable, or I psych myself up to expecting the moment of shock to a point where when it does happen I just laugh at the predictability.  - I watched Soul Eater, episode 26-30.  I'm not to fond of anime series (or tv shows in general) where it's like one big bad guy followed by another.  This series sorta seems to be like this, but not really (?).  I'm at the episode where questions are left unanswered so I don't quite know where it's heading.  But overall I like the series.  I really love Black Star, and not just cause he's a ninja.  His character is so jokes and his abilities are covered in awesome sauce. - I downloaded a couple of albums - trying to stay current since I'm way behind!  Busta Rhymes, Back on my BS (it isn't out yet, but most of the songs have leaked).  Gnarls Barkley, Odd Couple (haven't listened to the whole album yet but the tracks got the same vibe as their first album).  Dr. Dre, Detoxification (haven't listened to any of this and I'm not expecting anything spectacular tbh).  Common, Universal Mind Control (I really like Common, but this album is mostly party tracks - it was suppose to be released in the summer but got uber delayed).  Metallica, Death Magnetic (I've listened to the who album, pretty awesome, very 'Metallica', and much better than St. Anger).  Flobots, Flight With Tools (although this album came out more than a year ago, I really love it.  It's a hip hop band!  And they combine the nutties instruments!  Like guitars and violins and horns.  Plus, they're really political, definitely worth a listening to).  - Perhaps a record, but this week: I got padded for four consecutive days, I had a nose bleed everyday (kinda bizzare considering I haven't had a nose bleed in the longest time), I practically lived off of pizza or pizza pockets (last week it was hotdogs o.O) - and I burned the roof of my mouth, hurts like a mofo. 
😶 blank  |  ♫ Flobots - Mayday!!!

Let the reading week begin! *cheers*

Feb. 12th, 2009   11:27 PM

I think I should be frightened.  The marks are posted from the bell-ringer I did yesterday.  The class average is 45.43%.  Two of my friends got 52%.  I got 65.33%.  What the hellz man?!  I mean, I know it was difficult, but these numbers are way low!  I know that biology and calculus courses usually ball park around these numbers, but honestly...an anthropology class?!  Shit, I'm gonna have to work my butt off for this one.  And speaking of horrible grades, I don't think I did so hot for my epidemiology midterm today.  Practically all the questions were worth 1-3 marks.  Only three questions were worth 5 marks.  So basically, if you screw up three or four questions you can kiss 10% goodbye just like that.  >.o It's a conspiracy dammit!  All the profs are teaming up and making life difficult for all the students! Noes! D:

Keeping with the sour remarks, I finished the Secret Invasion crossover (last night, against my better judgement telling me to study instead) and the ending was utter bullshit.  But to be fair, I honestly wasn't expecting an epic ending or anything remotely close.  There are two types of readers for this crossover (I'm just biting off of some dudes review article on IGN.com).  People who enjoyed it beginning to end, or those who just follow along for the sake of seeing how it flows into Dark Reign.  I have fallen in to the latter.  But, due to my remarkable laziness to sort out each individual Dark Reign comic that's available for download (they're usually mixed in with monthly batches that are a few gigabytes) - I'm gonna wait it out until someone else does the dirty work for me!  SOMEONE PLEASE COMPILE ALL EXISTING DARK REIGN COMICS SO I CAN DOWNLOAD THEM!  THANK YOU!  I think I'll pass on the other Marvel crossovers I've downloaded for now.  I've been reading a lot from that company and I think I want a taste of something different.  Hello Sandman! :D  The only real bummer is I'm starting from the very beginning - issue 1 was printed in 1989 by Vertigo comics (which is a branch of DC). 

So I'll probably be spending half my reading week with that comic book series, the other half I have my work cut out for me.  I've got two assignment, a research proposal, two journal responses, and one midterm to write and study for.  I also really, really want to look into occupations that remotely tie into my feild of study.  It takes more than eight months to land a job and I'm already starting that search a little late.  And of course, I'll have to throw around time to write 'Be Normal' - just waiting for that right moment.
💤 sleepy
Link

Air drums while studying! xD

Feb. 10th, 2009   11:04 PM
I'm really digging the Print of Good Day Good Night mixtape 'Comic Book, Book 1'. The entire album is solid! I've been listening to it once a day (if not more) since discovering it a couple days ago. Not only because the whole premise is about comic books and superheroes, but also because the lyrics are so tight. They flow so well with the rhythem. And some of the samples used are so fresh! I dislike repedative music, but I'm totally loving these samples and even without the lyrics I wouldn't mind just listening to the beats (particularily 'A Day with Clark Kent', 'Super Friends', and 'I am Legend').

This isn't the first time I've fallen in love with an album.  The album before Print's was Ludacris's Theater of the Mind.  I've always loved Luda, his lyrics are fun and they flow really well (he sorta reminds me of Busta Rhymes for these qualities).  I loved his Theater of the Mind album cause it featured a lot of amazing artists (Jay-Z, Nas, Lil Wayne, The Game, Common, Jamie Foxx, etc) and two producers I really like (9th Wonder and DJ Premier).  Before that, I fell in love with Lil Wayne's Tha Carter 3 album.  Some of my favourite tracks are Dr. Carter, Shoot Me Down, Mr. Carter, and Tie My Hands.  Again, a lot of great producers on that album - Kanye West, Wyclef Jean, Dr. Dre, David Banner, etc.  And before that, I fell in love with Common's Electric Circus album.  It wasn't a commercially successful album, but critics gave it good reviews - though most Common fans like Resurrection and Like Water for Chocolate the most.  I really liked Electric Circus cause it had this really wicked experimental/electronic melody to all its tracks.  It made me feel as though I was listening to something 'retro' (like psychedelic hippy stuff).  Tracks I recommend are Soul Power, Aquarius, Electric Wire Hustler Flower, and Jimi was a Rock Star.  I don't recall which other albums I really loved in order prior to that, but The Mars Volta's De-Loused in the Comatorium and Nine Inch Nails' The Fragile would be included (I really love albums that have one track flowing straight into the next). 

So besides the wicked vibes I'm feeding my ears, my brain is starting to leak knowledge from all the anthropology I've been cramming with it.  I've got a bell ringer for my osteology class tomorrow afternoon, which I'm not too too nervous for (which I think is a bad sign), and a midterm for an epidemiology class on thursday which I am nervous for (bad sign!  so screwed!).  I really hate how my Thursday exam is gonna be a brain-dump kinda exam.  Everything I need to know for that class is gonna be pulled straight from the profs lecture slides, meaning all I need to do is memorize everything!  And I really hate that since I can't memorize for shit and ultimantly, I don't learn shit.  Like, I remember things better when I need to think about and processes to understand stuff.  I think I'll do better on my Wedn exam though since it's a hands on course and I did really well in last semesters prerequisite course.  I doubt I'll be getting much shut eye these next two days, but luckily after that it's reading week!  Unluckily, I have three assignments and one midterm to do the week following (LOL). 

And in comic book news, I am almost finished with the Secret Invasion crossover.  I am at 86/97 (though 86-87 is Captain Britain and I've only been skimming his issues, not really enjoying them).  Technically, I already know how the crossover will end but I'll hide my excitement for the post when I do actually finish it.  Which reminds me, I really should start downloading the Dark Reign crossover! >< But shit, I have 19 other crossovers I want to read (particularly World War Hulk).  I'm a little pissed that I have to wait till April for Astonishing X-Men vol3 #29, ditto for Ex Machina #41.  To friggin long and the series is to friggin good!  Peace.
✨ chipper  |  ♫ Fugees - Ready or Not
Link

Opinion...

Feb. 9th, 2009   11:32 PM
'One death is a tragedy. A million is a statistic.' I'm fairly certain most people have heard of this saying and probably take it as fact. Perhaps so, but I think it's completely illogical. One death is a tragedy, so a million deaths is a million tragedies. To feel more for the loss of one than a million is a real tragedy.

Super Friends! (Wonder Twin powers activate! LOL)

Feb. 7th, 2009   10:55 PM

Fuking a!  Got home not to long ago from playing jitz/foosball/table soccer (whatever you refer it as) with my brother.  We go to Team Play Internet Cafe (it's under Club 300).  Only drained about a dollar fifty each and killed about an hour and a half.  But anyways, my bro usually drives when we go and we listen to his iPod.  He's a big hip hop buff (he got me into it) so a lot of what we listen to I've never even heard.  On the way home he played this one song that's over ten minutes long and it is so kick ass!  It's called 'Super Friends' featuring some of New York City's rising underground talents (Print, Mickey Factz, Homeboy Sandman, Outasight, 6th Sense, Oddisee, Von Pea, and Fresh Daily).  Part of the track is on Print's mixtape called Comic Books Vol 1.  The entire song featuring all the artists can be downloaded on the same link.  What's awesome about it is that each rapper takes on a persona of an existing superheros.  It's sorta like Wu-Tang Clan since they do the same, but this one track is just insane.  Listening to the song the first time was really fun since I could try to guess which hero they were rapping about - some easier than others.  I haven't listened to the mixtape yet, but from the looks of it I'm sure I won't be disappointed.  Enjoy.
🎉 excited
Link

Foods, thoughts, and renovations

Feb. 7th, 2009   12:50 AM

I managed to get to class on time – I slept in a bit and the buses were quite slow. I got to class before Jully but not before Monica. It was rather alarming when Jully told me this was her first time coming to class, considering it’s been five weeks. I’ve known for a while that she has a tendency to skip classes for whatever reason, but on Fridays she has two tutorials and one lecture – all of which were her first times attending, today. Tutorial participation is normally worth 10% of your overall grade, so she’s digging her own ditch. Though, I can understand why she would want to skip the ANTC68 lecture, the prof was boring as fuk. I donno why people were telling me the Monday lecture was important cause all she did was read verbatim off her lecture notes. Oh well, I played Solitaire and Hearts for the most part. Plus, I was padded so the comfort factor served as a mental block to the boringness the prof projected. 

Jully and Monica had tutorial afterwards so I didn’t loaf around school. I went to my usual Second Cup around where I live to ‘study’. It’s really jokes cause the major intersection where I live is cluttered with coffee shops. There’s a Starbucks, Tim Hortons, Country Style, and two Second Cups – they aren’t all on the corners of the intersection, but they’re close enough together for anyone to find it ridiculous. And yet, they are all managing to survive with whatever business they’re getting. The Country Style is 24/7 is closest to the intersection so a lot of poor people tend to go there (and by poor I mean homeless since they’re notorious with the buses). The Tim Hortons is also 24/7 but it’s further down the road and the line ups tend to be a b!tch. The Starbucks gets a lot of business, mainly from the business offices surrounding it. I use to go there because it had free wi-fi and an electrical plug near 2-3 seats. I don’t write my notes by hand, nor do I print out my typed up notes so I really rely on outlets. If the seats are taken then I’m usually screwed and am forced to hound the seats before my battery dies. One of the Second Cup’s has a drive through and is in the heart of a plaza so they have a fairly heavy traffic. I use to go there since it is the closest coffee shop to where I live and cause it had one outlet (but only one seat could use it due to how the layout is arrangement). My usual Second Cup is a bit further down the road. It’s a lot more spacious and the seats are really comfortable, plus they’re smart enough to put a powerbar where the outlet is. I’ve gone there so often that barista knows what I’ll order – large Earl Grey tea (I take my teas with two sweeteners). Today instead I got a medium Green Tea Latte. I probably shouldn’t have since I’m partially lactose intolerant (and yet my favourite ‘coffee-shop-made’ drink is a Chai Latte), but it was damn tasty.

My aunt Liana txt’d me about twenty minutes into my ‘studying’ (I was reading comic books >>) asking if I wanted to help renovate – of course I said yet. She picked me up not long after. The labour wasn’t to bad this time. They ordered 75 boxes of hardwood floor panels (3x3/8) and weighed about 30-40 pounds. I didn’t think they were heavy, but they were bulky to pick up and move around. I moved two skids full from the garage to the family room since they need to climatize before being installed. It took less than an hour and a half. Afterwards I helped my aunt sweep and remove the remaining nails (surprisingly there was a lot meaning we missed a lot yesterday). When we were done it was about 6:15pm. We waited for my uncle Viktor to get here so we could go out for dinner for about twenty minutes. 

I forgot to mention in yesterdays post that we went to Kelsey’s for dinner. There are quite a number of restaurants around the major intersection where I live (Swiss Chalet, Shoeless Joe’s, Kelsey’s, three pizza stores, a McD, and a few other non-chain restaurants. Oddly enough, after living where I am today for three years I have yet to eaten at about 80% of these restaurants. I’ve been to a Kelsey’s, but it was my first time at that particular one. I had bbq ribs & hot wings with mash potatoes, and we shared a four cheese and spinach dip. No real complaints, I don’t go to ‘white’ restaurants very often cause they’re pretty pricey and I still stand by that. It’s like, I’ll go for social things, but if I had a preference where to eat by myself, I’d choose a fast food or Chinese restaurant (doesn’t mean I don’t have sophisticated taste though, cause I really love food). So anyways, when Vik arrived we all went to a place called Crazy Sushi – there are four sushi restaurants in the area but that one’s her favourite, it’s a White/Jap fusion type. We ordered glass noodles, curry rice chicken, a sashimi box, two specialty sushi rolls, and a seafood pancake – quite a lot between three people. It was pretty good overall, and we shared a lot of silly/gross/embarrassing things we did as kids – the conversation began, not surprisingly, with them asking me about school. My aunt resurfaced a lot of memories I had forgotten – with good reason! Like this one time when I was four I slept walked (woke up in the middle of the night groggy) to the play room and peed in the bucket of Duplo (they’re like Lego but the pieces are 10x larger). Or this other time when I was a Beaver Scout (it’s sorta like Boys Scouts but in Toronto it’s called Beaver (5-7 years old), and Wolf Cub (+7 years old)) and we had to sell apples at a mall. I would run away and hide behind my parents cause a bunch of people wanted to buy from me and I was pretty shy – my parents say it’s cause I was cute {o’.’o}. 

So after the sushi dinner my aunt went home (she’s almost nine months preggy and worked half the day, and it wasn’t like she could do much renovating anyways), while my uncle and I went to Rona. He picked up a $330 staple gun – professional grade costs twice as much – and looked into tiles. I picked up quite a bit of tidbits that’ll come in handy if I ever decide to do construction, or if I’m even able to retain that info (so forgetful). When we got back to the house we didn’t do anything intensive. We took out part of the stair railing and the nose board along the second floor walk. I got home before midnight, my uncle wanted to finish up earlier cause he’s got a lot to do the day after. His friends are helping him out over the weekend so hopefully they can get a lot done (cause they have a lot to do before moving in – which they planned for the 15th). 

I think I’ll take it easy this weekend and not just cause my body is really sore. I don’t have anything due the week coming, just a bellringer and a midterm to study for. The week after is reading week (16th-20th) so I’ll definitely have time to catch up on whatever I’m behind in. I’ll consider attempting to tackling the ‘Be Normal’ story, cause I really don’t want to lose to writing mojo by pulling it off to long. Maybe I’ll reread Incontinent Student Bodies #3 just to pump me up since it deals with bullying. Peace out!

🌿 calm  |  ♫ Jay-Z - I just wanna love U (Give it 2 me) [MTV Unplugged version]
Link

Feebleness from the night before...

Feb. 6th, 2009   9:21 AM
Got home at around 12am last night, back and joints were feeling a bit feeble.  My uncle and I stripped the hardwood floor out of two rooms, then denailed a, quite literally, thousands of nails that held them in place.  So just imagine being in a bent position with a hammer and crowbar yaunking out one inch nails for four hours.  Woke up not to long ago feeling excruciating pain everywhere.  I'll survive, but uncomfortably.  A little worried about my typing (i'm making quite a few errors as I type this, but letting spell check cover my arse) since I have class from 12-2pm.  In terms of comic book status, i'm still slowly getting through the Secret Invasion crossover.  Couldn't read much this week cause i had a lot of things due.  I'll probably read a bit more on the weekend, though I'll need to make time to study since I have a midterm and a bellringer next week.  I'm at 49/97 - and that's according to the ordering of whoever made this bundle.  I still haven't gone over the official checklist and probably never will since I'm lazy.  Like I mentioned in my last post, I'm only skimming through the titles I'm unfamiliar with, but I'm finding some of the unfamiliar ones can be quite interesting.  Like Thor (which is 41-43/97) was pretty interesting.  I had no idea Thor had a vessal body (Donald Blake) let alone oporated in Oklahoma (he built a midevil village thingy called Asgard with a bunch of trippy warrior/viking people - i donno the specifics, just telling it how i'm seeing it! ><).  But yea, I thought the three issues were pretty interesting - and felt really epic!  The way Thor speaks (viking english? lol) gets a bit irky sometimes, but the illustrations are really beautiful.  The three issues are written by Matt Fraction and illustrated by Doug Braithwaite.  I'm really disliking the Runaways/Young Avengers titles in this crossover - while I really liked them in their own series, so it's a bit of a let down.  I really really don't like Takeshi Miyazawa's art style.  Normally I'm fine with anime/cutsey/bubbly cartoony/manga influenced stuff, but the thing is, Runaways started off like regular looking Wester characters (I'm I have no idea what the proper terms are, but there's is a clear distinction between how Western comic book characters look to Easter manga characters look).  So you can understand the sudden change is unsettling, but thank gawd he's only had a helping hand in a few issues of Runaways (I think 5 or 6?).  He's from Canada, and went to Queen's University - so that's a pretty cool fact.  Lastly in comic book news, the 4GB bundle of crossovers I was downloading is complete!  I'll try to get over the pixely/dotty/grimmy way comics were illustrated/printed way back when and read the Kree-Skrull War's when i'm bored with the Secret Invasion crossover.  Just to wrap it up, I think I'm gonna go to school diapered today.  I could use the relaxing comfort against my acking body and I'll probably be downing a lot of coffee while I study after class.  I've worn about three since the weekend, which is a little more than usual since I only wear one or two a week on average.  But again, I've been feeling a yearning to be littler.  Peace out.
😫 exhausted  |  ♫ Gorillaz - Hong Kong
Link

The Furry Fandom post

Feb. 5th, 2009   5:10 PM

I’m feeling pretty good that the week is coming to an end; would’ve felt great had I not had to go to class tomorrow since I skipped it on Monday. I skipped ANTC68 cause I was rushing to finish my ANTC67 assignment. I sorta bugged me that people told me the lecture was important (not surprising since next week Wedn is the midterm), but she’s the type of prof that reads verbatim off her lecture slides. So I don’t know if people are just bullshitting me or if she actually deviated from what she usually does – guess I’ll find out tomorrow. I handed in a bunch of crap for ANTD15 today (9 pages of weekly reading reports and 1 paper proposal), including a lecture facilitation I did with Jully for half the class. I didn’t expect much since there are three people who talk a lot, so once initiating our questions they pretty much took the show – the class has less than a dozen people (not a lot of people take D level classes since they’re usually seminar type and heavy on the workload). Spent another $50 on two course readers today…savings are slowly dwindling. 

In a couple of hours I’ll be going out for dinner with my aunt Liana and uncle Viktor, than afterwards we’ll be renovating their new house. I’m sorta hoping it won’t stretch until midnight like last time since I’ll probably be exhausted after and I need to get to school by 11am tomorrow. But I’m still looking forward to spending some time with them.

So anyways, last night between writing weekly reports I downloaded issues #1-3 Incontinent Student Bodies by Marci McAdam. Currently there are four issues, but I don’t think they come out bimonthly or anything – possibly just whenever they get done, but I’m not sure. I’ve been feeling this yearning to be treated like a toddler since last week, it might possibly have something to do with stress. So reading this comic made me feel pretty fuzzy inside (mind the furry pun) – though the plot is a bit cheesy, I thought the colourful cast of characters was cute. In terms of my AB/DL ratio, I’ve always felt I was 20% AB and 80% DL. But my AB side has never really been geared towards wanting to be or treated like a baby. To have a bit of control over my own actions and to project a sense of wonder, innocence, and freedom is really appealing to me – including the love and support given from adult figures. I don’t particularly remember much of my childhood, but around 4-5 years of age seems to have the most appeal. I think it might have something thing to do with a) I went to Disneyland when I was five, and b) I didn’t wear glasses at that age. B) might sound a bit weird, but I began wearing glasses when I was five – because I watched a lot of tv and played a lot of Nintendo. I really, really hate wearing glasses. They’re just annoying and I don’t think they look good on me. I first started wearing contact lenses at, I think, grade eight. Thank gawd. 

So back to Incontinent Student Bodies – it’s about ABDL furries in a wacky school.  It's written and illustrated by Marci McAdam (her husband is Karis, also a furry artists). Marci calls herself the ‘Badge Queen at furry conventions’, and with good reason. Practically every other furry I’ve met, or seen online, has a Marci badge. It’s not hard to notice what a Marci badge looks like (besides looking at the signature) since the layout and illustration style is distinctly consistent. Karis has a website called Karis’ Playground and it achieves his stories and art (as well as sells them). A long time ago, like when I was first discovering my ABDL side, I frequently went to Karis’ website, but had no clue his wife was Marci. Plus, I think it’s really wicked that they’re Canadian – pride up!

I’ve always been interested in the furry fandom, but I’ve constantly had difficulty placing my involvement in the ‘community’. I have an issue with calling certain things a ‘communities’ since it homogenizes and encapsulates something far greater than what it really is. I’ll go into that later. I like the social aspects and overall appearances of furries, but the furry lifestyle is something I don’t know if I would want to be a part of. It’s like, I like being an observer, but sometimes I really want to jump into it and get involved yet at the same time it kinda scares the crap out of me. I don’t know why it scares me, let alone what exactly is it that scares me, but I guess I just fear uncertainty. I really like the feeling of fur, especially fur on a warm body – it’s very snuggable. I like how the ‘community’ seems to be so tight – think six degrees of separation tight. Like, if you know Bob and he knows Joe, and Joe knows Stan, you and Stan will probably have sharing friends – thus making it easier to be friends with one another. I think that’s one possibility why I like furries, their social networks seem so wide and diverse and you can make so much friends just from branching out off one or two people. This might have something to do with the networks they use, like Fur Affinity and Second Life. Which leads in to another thing I like about furries, a ton of them are really amazing artists. It’s kinda insane how many of them can draw really wicked stuff. One of the first furry websites I ever visited (like when I discovering my AB/DL side) was CS_Fox’s Fox Tales Times – it’s a babyfur website with a story and arts section (both on forum and website). 

The only actual furry ‘experience’ (for lack of a better word) I’ve ever had was through Second Life. But even then, I played under different names and gender – similar reasoning as writing under the alias ‘toucher_terre’. It was because of these pseudonyms and made up identities that made my ‘experience’ not really count as ‘experiences’ in my opinion – since I don’t openly talk about who I pretended to be (for obvious reasons, but also because I’ve hurt people emotionally). The furry persona of my old pseudonyms, Nodame Hyun, Baylyn Fayray, and Isobel Piek, were cat, bunny, and mouse. My current furry persona (character-wise, it resembles who I am the most), TiAmo Dumpling, is a shape shifter. I have a lot of avatars and I like them all, these include:

 All of Wingless Emoto’s free avatars (they’re very high quality)

BlackFire Kitten by Skeet Freaks

White Mouse by AnthroXtacy

Red and Black Rabbit by Exterminatus Labs

Mocha Kani by Curious Inc

Grey Wolf by Lil’Punks

Blue Kittay by Lil’Punks

Grey Bunny by Lost Creatures

Wallaby by Luskwood Bunkies

Blue Tiger by Luskwood

Xmas Bunny by Luskwood

Raccoon by MiniFeru

Moonba by M3

White Mouse by Luskwood Bunkies

White Mouse by MiniFeru

Black Chibifox by Ninja Weasel Studio

Stick Furry Avs by Ephyu Reino

Star Sloth by Syn Squee

Tiny Cell-Shaded Fox by tikyuchan Fride

Gear Soldier by TiAmo Dumpling

 I don’t exactly know how one chooses a furry. Like do some people go by their zodiac? Or an animal that matches their character (cat = sly?)? Or an animal that they just like? I donno, but that’s probably why I can’t settle with just one. I would say that one animal I can always fall back on is the rabbit partly because it’s my Chinese zodiac sign, and partly because I just find them so damn cute. (OMG! Lucky feet! *chop**chop*) I also sorta like being a mouse – something about being quiet and ‘small’ (not size wise, but metaphorically (?)). Ah well, I don’t play much Second Life anymore, though I really wouldn’t mind getting back into it. I think the only real turn off is I don’t have a regular hangout place where I can chill comfortably. I use to hangout at Cub Conclave (a babyfur sim), but after I got hooked on Perfect World and coming back after half a year things changed pretty drastically. Every time I log on now I just bum around at Furgate – a German furry sim, but even then I only actually talk with one person there (Wolf Brennen). Any other rare occasion I’d be at Happyfeet Hax’s sim or a DL pirate airship with Di5Red Moulliez. The only, sorta, memorable things I’ve done on SL with my current character is build the current version of Club Rainbow Tiger for Spike Nico (I’m only somewhat proud of it – it looks pretty crap tbh) and model for UFonly owned by Eternalynn Calliop (she was a pretty spiffy chap). I mean, sure I’ve done a lot of other things (like the time I offered free vending and hurt BT’s little sales area) but most of the time it wasn’t really me – it was me pretending to be someone else thus offsetting the experience =\.


🌟 optimistic  |  ♫ The Fratellis - Chelsea Dagger
Link

Rest in Peace William Windor (aka 'Baby Man')

Feb. 4th, 2009   11:02 AM

I've heard of William Windor through forum discussions and I've seen her from a documentary posted on YouTube.  Generally, she's gotten mixed responses from fellow AB/DL's - some respect how he lives the lifestyle publically, some find it gives other AB/DL's a bad rep.  I am indifferent because it has never had much of an impact on me.  I did't know her, but she will be missed and mourned for by many.  My she rest in peace. 
🌀 numb

Update: ABDL pictures

Feb. 3rd, 2009   10:12 PM

I have added some new pictures on my diaperspace account.

Link

School experiences and beyond

Feb. 3rd, 2009   7:15 PM

It’s February! Crazy how quickly a month has already gone by – I’m still putting 2008 when I write my notes in class. Time is passing way to quickly for me and it’ll probably only get worse especially since my workload is gradually increasing. I managed to finish my epidemiology assignment, sorta, on time – only 2 hours late! My gawd, I pulled an all nighter and it was brutal. It makes me wonder how I managed to work the grave shift during the summer as a condo security guard. I certainly didn’t drink much water, but I don’t remember ever feeling so out of it the next morning. But it’s pretty stupid of me for pushing the assignment aside until the very last minute. I honestly say this every time, ‘next assignment I will start early’, and I have yet to prove that statement. It’s really really pathetic and I beat myself up every single time. I don’t have any idea how I’ve managed to last four plus years (yes, I was a procrastinator since highschool) writing papers worth a quarter of my grade the night before the due date. I know I’m not the only student who’s as bad as I am, but that’s no excuse for doing it. I really need to manage my time better…or just struggle through my last few semesters of school and never have to work about assignments or exams EVER AGAIN! Man would that ever be awesome.

 But then it makes me think about what I’ll even actually do after I graduate. I seriously have no friggin’ clue what I’ll do. I’m doing a specialist program, which in my opinion only holds merit if you’re going in to post-graduate school. I regret not doing a double major instead, but at the time (second year) I was really really lazy. Two majors, in my opinion, has a pretty heavy workload. I had considered doing a major in anthropology and a minor in sociology, but after taking four sociology courses I wasn’t to interested in the field. True, anthropology is really interdisciplinary, but the specialist program seemed to have more appeal to me – at the time. There are a lot of different branches of anthropology, so I chose the one that sounded most useful (or smart, for lack of a better word). Normally any program in anthropology will get you a bachelors of arts degree, instead I chose take a bachelors of science root – which requires at least seven science credits based on the courses I take. So, when I graduate I’ll end up with a B.Sc in Medical Anthropology…no clue what I’ll do with it, but sure sounds spiffy.

 One of my biggest regrets in life was during the selection processes of choosing what university/college to go to. My marks in highschool (and for the most part in university as well) have always been in the 70s. I don’t know what you’d call that…average? Anyways, in my opinion it’s not good – but only because I get compared to my brother who gets much higher marks. If I had better memory, because I am hopelessly forgetful, I can almost guarantee I’ll do better in, well, everything. I don’t know if that’s just an excuse I tell myself or if it’s the real deal, but I really feel that my crappy memory is holding me back. But anyways, that’s not what I regret – back to the story! I applied to a lot of schools, most of which were ones considered ‘just okay’. Like, if you were to ask someone on my campus to name ten universities, a majority of the ones I applied for wouldn’t be mentioned. Something about ‘okay’ universities and lower grade requirements and what not. Most schools I applied to were for social work, or less specific things in the field of arts, I think two were for music, and two for nursing. When it all came down to choosing, after getting confirmation, I narrowed my selection to two choices. Astonishingly, I got in to University of Toronto: Scarborough Campus for social science, the second choice was a joint program between York University and Seneca College (Kings campus) for nursing – two years at each school. My biggest regret is choosing UofT over York/Seneca. One of the main reasons I chose UofT was simply because of its reputation. I know it sounds like a stupid way of deciding, but that was one of the big ‘pros’ on my list. The name of the university carries a really heavy weight (or so we’re led to believe – in some sense). Like, if two people of equal GPA apply for a position, one from UofT and the other from Wilfrid Laurier University, the UofT person would most likely get the position. I’m sure there’s a similar unspoken system in the US and elsewhere. The other thing that led to my decision was Seneca college. In Canada, there is a difference between a university and a college – colleges are far more hands on and practical while universities are fundamental and (to put it bluntly) more scholarly and academic. Again, it’s pretty superficial making a decision based on names. Furthermore, Kings campus is quite literally in the middle of nowhere. You practically had to live on campus. I regret not living in residence (here at UofT) because I feel that I totally missed out on probably some of the best experiences I could ever have had. You can make a shit load of friends, meet girls, go to socials, the whole nine yards. Instead, I took the less costly route of staying at home. I regret choosing UofT over York/Seneca because after my four years and graduating at York/Seneca, I would be a Registered Nurse. Do you have any idea how high the demand for nurses are in Toronto? Let alone a male nurse?!? It’s friggin’ bananas! Instead, I’m left with wondering what the fuk I’m going to do after I graduate at UofT. 

 My parents have put, on numerous occasions, provided occupations to look into. The one that sounds the most appealing right now is teaching ESL people. One of my moms co-workers has two jobs, besides working as an office assistant she also teaches ESL. She gets paid $40 an hour teaching ESL…to me, that’s completely unheard of and I want a piece of it (if I fail to find a job when I graduate). The only real draw back is she only gets 5 hour days with that job. But when you add it up, the reality of it is you’re getting paid a lot for not a lot of man hours. If I get a certificate for teaching ESL, I could be getting paid $60 an hour (according to my moms co-worker, which I have no reason to no believe). The daughter of my parents’ friend has a certificate for teaching ESL. She said the course costs about a thousand bucks and is only a couple of weeks – mostly teaching stuff that’s common sense. So with those two pieces of information, it is definitely something I should look into. But I will graduate before all that – I’ve come to far to just quit regardless of not knowing what to do with my diploma.

 On a less serious note, I’ve been reading Secret Invasion (the Marvel crossover I talked about last post). I’m not too too sure if whoever sorted the files for the batch I downloaded has them in order, but I’m at issue 41 of 97. I’ll have to double check the official checklist. I haven’t been really reading titles I’m not familiar with (such as ‘Guardians of the Galaxy’, ‘Nova’, ‘New Warriors’, ‘Ms. Marvel’, ‘Thor’, etc), only skimming them. I mean, they aren’t essential to read and it doesn’t impact the overall story by not reading them. The two titles that I’m unfamiliar with (familiar with the lead character, just not their storylines) but really enjoying are ‘Deadpool’ and ‘Incredible Hercules’. I’ve always known Deadpool was a serious jokester, but vol3 #2 was fuking comedy gold! I may have to consider getting into his series! I haven’t made much progress in Astonishing X-Men, since picking up Secret Invasion I think I’ve only read two issues of X-Men. There’s this 13.66GB torrent file that I would download had I not a bandwidth limit. It chronicles the major events/crossovers in Marvel (I donno about the accuracy, but come on, 13GB is a fuk load and surely covers at least 80% of all major events, right?) After picking and choosing, I am currently downloading 4GB of the batch (this includes ‘Kree-Skrull War’, ‘Mutant Massacre’, ‘Infinity Gauntlet trilogy’, ‘Planet Hulk’, ‘Winter Soldier’, ‘World War Hulk’, ‘Messiah Complex’, etc). Lets just say I’m looking forward, it’s at 67.2% right now :D.

 Last couple of things. I bought new headphones! I got really (irritatingly) tired of listening this little kid saying ‘mommy _____?’ on the bus ride home today, so I bought a black and white Skullcandy Lowrider headphone at the Source. To be honest, I had no clue what the reviews said so I went home and did some searching before opening them. A lot of mixed reviews apparently, so I guess it just boils down to how I’ll like’em. So I opened them, and they look, feel, and sound pretty good. I’ll need to use them a little more before passing any sort of real judgement though, so those were just my first impressions. Of course, I can’t return them now since I’ve already opened them, but here’s what I’m thinking. I bought a three year warranty (costed $10), so if I really don’t’ like them I’ll just destroy them and get them replaced. With the new, unopened pair I’ll just eBay, Kijiji, or Craigslist them. I’ll lose a bit of money, but better than keeping something I don’t want. Anywhoo, OMG I feel so bad for Roger Federer when Rafael Nadal beat him (7-5, 3-6, 7-6, 3-6, 6-2) at the Australian Open finals. Federer was crying worse than the time at Wimbledon 2008 (lost to Nadal in the finals). On a contrasting opinion though, I do feel that Federer’s emotional display has made it awkward for Nadal to express his joy during the closing ceremony. Nadal has done really really well in 2008 (French Open, Olympic Gold, Wimbledon, World no.1) since he’s only 22 years old. Kuddos to him. Don’t worry Federer, you’ll win your 14th Grand Slam title (tying Pete Sampras) sometime this year, I can feel it. Also, no progress on the ‘Be Normal’ short story yet, but it is definitely on the top of my list (next to two assignments and two midterms). I’ll make an effort to write it during reading week (16th-20th), but it’ll depend on my availability too since I got an email from my aunt Liana asking for my help again – told her my available hours, including reading week, that I’d gladly help. 

😑 blah  |  ♫ City and Colour - Waiting...

Rawr! Muscle exertion!

Jan. 31st, 2009   12:46 AM

I am so exhausted! I just got home recently from helping my aunt Liana and uncle Viktor renovate their new house. She called me earlier to ask if I was free tonight, which I wasn’t. I managed to get my brother to share the labour. We got there at around 8:30pm and didn’t leave till about 12:15am. The most time consuming thing was pulling out the carpet – the previous owners placed carpet over hardwood, which some foamy stuff sandwiched between. It wouldn’t have taken long had it not been stapled and nailed like a muthafuker. I swear to gawd there was like four staples and one nail for every inch of parameter (the carpet itself was only maybe eight by eight feet). Stupid carpet people…

 Other than the mad labour I thought it was really cool hanging out with them – as mentioned before I don’t know much about them, and want to know more. My uncle Viktor does construction stuff as a hobby (to my understanding) so he was really fluent with how to everything (like doing dry wall, sanding, carpeting, etc). It’s vaguely similar to my dad, in terms of scope of knowledge, but I can connect a lot better with Viktor than with my dad. I learned a lot of practical stuff to, but will I remember any of it…probably not. My aunt Liana order pizza for us, so that was a bonus! But it really pissed me off when my brother and I got home. Cause it was late my dad assumed we were playing foosball (which my brother and I do rarely). I don’t want to come home after breaking my back and getting my hands dirty to hear is mistrust. He knew full well ahead of time that we were going to his sister’s place…he can shove a boot up his ass.

 Anyways, prior to the sweat and hard work, I didn’t do a hell of a lot today. I did a tiny bit of research for my ‘foundations of epistemology’ assignment that’s due Monday. I read ‘Pride of Baghdad’ – which I blogged about earlier. I skimmed through ‘Secret War’ (a five issue limited series by Marvel) cause it’s the crossover event that happens before the start of ‘Secret Invasion’ crossover (which I’m reading now). ‘Secret War’ isn’t bad, it’s basically about Nick Fury rounding up some heroes (Wolverine, Daredevil, Spider-Man, Captain America, and Luke Cage – which honestly just sound like the roster for New Avengers) to overthrow Dr. Doom’s reign on Latveria. This of course, all happens under the table (thus making it a ‘secret war’), and when they succeed Fury mind wipes the heroes. A year later they begin to remember, and shit hits the fan. But naturally, Fury is the ace of strategy and he say he did it for humankind, blah blah blah, ‘Secret Invasion’ blah blah blah. Of course the plot is more wicked than I describe it to be. The illustrator, Gabriele Dell’Otto, did a really awesome job – the comic has this sorta pastel look to it (I’m not art major so I have no clue what it’s called). 

 I gotta point out though, issue #2 of ‘Secret War’ is hilarious! The part where the heroes are on a plane as citizens, before take off, Wolverine is drunk. Man, the way his facial expressions are illustrated with the smack he’s talking, it’s comedy gold! On the flip side though, S.H.I.E.L.D database says that Wolverine is immune to poisons and alcohol…so what the hell?  Cause in Astonishing X-Men vol3 #25, Wolverine has a hangover from drinking four bottles of hemp wine – so I’m not too too sure about the accuracy of whichever. Btw, I don’t even think I know what hemp wine is o.o’’ does it have any alcohol in it? Anyways, what’s more, how does a mutant with a metal skeleton get through security at an airport? xD Other notable funnies was Luke Cage, while they’re walking the streets of Latveria he states ‘I think I just raised the black population in this country by a hundred and fifty percent.’ LOL

😫 exhausted  |  ♫ The Hush Sound - White Red
Link

Pride of Baghdad

Jan. 30th, 2009   1:53 PM

I just finished reading ‘Pride of Baghdad’, it’s a graphic novel released on September 2006 by Vertigo (which is part of DC Comics). It’s written by Brian Vaughan (dude who did Y: The last Man, Runaways, and Ex Machina), and illustrated by Niko Henrichon (a Canadian artist, this is his second major graphic novel). Really really good, both in visual and story. It’s an interpretation piece inspired by real events, about four lions escaping Baghdad Zoo after a US air strike/invasion in 2003. The ending is really sad, but sends a powerful message regarding how some people feel about the Iraq war and the US involvement. I thought it was really interesting how the lions refer to themselves and their surroundings – in one panel Safa calls her species ‘people’. They do make a very strong distinction between humans/man/zoo keepers from other animals though. I totally recommend anyone to pick this up and give it a read, it’s about 120 pages. 


🐇 bouncy  |  ♫ British Sea Power - No Lucifer
Link

Comic book crossovers...

Jan. 30th, 2009   2:12 AM

I don’t recall if I mentioned it in a pervious post, but I love comic book crossovers. Crossovers are pretty much events that affect a wide cast of characters from a comic book company. For example the Onslaught saga in 1996. It originated from the X-Men series, but eventually tied in with the Fantastic Four and the Avengers. Point to note, crossovers usually occur when shit’s about to go down on Earth, potentially destroying it – so I suppose it’s only appropriate to get everyone involved. But the one thing I don’t really like about crossovers is that they rarely ‘just happen’. As in, the story doesn’t just fall from the sky and land on your plate. Crossovers happen as a result of an accumulation of stories from the initial title series. Going back to the Onslaught saga, for example, it began after the conclusion of the X-Men story arc ‘Fatal Attractions’. It’s difficult to jump straight into a crossover without knowing the events that led up to them.

 That being said, I read ‘House of M’ today. It’s an eight issue limited crossover series by Marvel published in 2005. I don’t have any particular reason for reading it, other than the fact I just have it from downloading bundles of comic books. I already knew what the crossover was about, since I read the plot online years ago. Also, it’s kinda difficult to not know about it since (like most crossovers) it was a major event that shaped future comic books there after. It was only brought up, I believe, once in Astonishing X-Men (in issue #25 when it is mentioned that there are only 198 mutants left). Interestingly, crossovers were largely ignored in Astonishing X-Men (or so far they’ve been ignored), because Joss Whedon, the writer, dislikes them. But, that could change since Warren Ellis is the current writer, incidentally he began writing from issue #25 to present. 

 Interestingly, ‘House of M’ is a follow-up to the crossover event ‘Avengers Disassemble’. Essentially the crossovers are about Wanda Maximoff (aka Scarlet Witch) losing her sanity and killing half the Avengers (in Avengers Disassemble), then erasing 98% of the mutant population (in House of M). Even though the ‘House of M’ crossover is only eight issues long, between each issue are expansions of the story – found in stand alone comic book series that are involved/affected by the crossover (this is most often the case in crossovers). So although the crossover is eight issues long, the story is elaborated in other comic book titles (in this case there are 42 other issues). I debated whether to read the crossover in chronological order (as in #1-50), but I got bored of that pretty quickly – New Thunderbolts #11 was a total turn off (to much happening in one issue for my taste). However, I did enjoy Black Panther v14 #7. Basically Magneto begins a war against Storm and Black Panther – Namor, Dr. Doom, and Apocalyps ally with each because all of them are rulers of continents. A couple of other random supers make an appearance – pretty jokes. Overall though a definite must read issue, it’s wicked. 

 I was looking forward to Layla Miller while reading the first few issues of ‘House of M’. I first read about her in an issue during the Civil War crossover (which occurs after House of M). I didn’t really understand her character since she was in an issue of X-Factor, which I don’t follow. She made her first appearance in issue #4 and throughout the rest of ‘House of M’ – pretty important character since she’s the fail safe to Wanda’s madness. There is a one-shot issue of her, X-Factor: Layla Miller published in August 2008, that I may consider reading if I can find it. I’ve begun downloading the Secret Invasion crossover (began April 2008 and ended Dec 2008), partially because all these Marvel crossovers have gotten me hooked. There is a comic book storyline called ‘Dark Reign’ which began in Dec 2008 and is currently running – it isn’t a crossover even though it’s read from various titles. ‘Dark Reign’ occurs after the major events of the previous five years – ‘Avengers Disassembled’, ‘House of M’, ‘Decimation’, ‘Silent War’, ‘Civil War’, ‘World War Hulk’, and ‘Secret Invasion’. So, I sorta want to get caught up before starting the ‘Dark Reign’ storyline. My only real concern is that ‘Secret Invasion’ concerns the aftermath of the Kree-Skrull War, which dates back really far in the Marvel Universe. I’m not very familiar with parts of the war and the impacts its had, but I’ll take it as a learning process while reading ‘Secret Invasion’. 

😌 content  |  ♫ Stars - Personal
Link

Astonishing

Jan. 28th, 2009   12:17 AM

Finished reading Astonishing X-Men issues 13-24 and the Giant-Size Astonishing X-Men issue. Really good. Though technically 13-18 is one story arc and 19-24 + Giant-Size is another. The transition between the two arcs is to easy in my opinion – the cast get teleported, how unoriginal. But whatever, the continuous adventuring was pretty sweet. Issues 13-18 is about Cassandra Nova’s revenge, pretty badass villain. Issues 19-24 + Giant Size is about saving Breakworld and Earth. I loved issue 23 – master strategist plans are such a huge turn on. Plus, Cyclopes is so fuking badass. In a lot of the other X-Men series he’s plays the pussy team leader (in my opinion), but this time around he really nails the awesomeness and gets the job done. I don’t really know what Giant-Size comic books are all about (maybe has something to do with being twice as many pages as regular comic books?), but it featured other Marvel heroes like Spider-Man, The Fantastic Four, Iron Man, Dr. Strange, Sentry, and other X-Men characters. They didn’t really serve a huge role, if anything they were pretty hilarious (oh Spider-Man…). Err, last thing, Agent Brand, come on, even I figured she wasn’t a human early on…did they really have to make it dramatic-ish? On the flip side, why does Agent Brand remind me of Fire (Beatriz Bonilla da Costa), a DC superhero. I mean, they’re both chicks, they both have green hair, and they both have hot hands. Anyways, I may have to consider looking in to reading The Fantastic Four – something about master strategists and geniuses I find interesting to read. But till then, I think I’ve had my comic book fix for a few days, or at least until Friday (>< OMFG the neglected hwk!)

 So anyways regarding my story writing, as I mentioned earlier I’ll be focusing on ‘Be Normal’ before revising ‘Misplaced Justice’. Recently Adrian from I Heart Diapers blogged about ‘Diapers in professional sports’ which got me thinking – since the bully in ‘Be Normal’ supposedly an incontinent high school football quarterback star (if you didn’t know now you know). So after reading some of the comments her post received I may need to rethink about my character. I mean, statistically speaking, one diaper wearing quarterback out of the thousands of non-diaper wearing football players is plausible – but will people buy it? Must think what else could be a suitable sport…baseball, basketball, hockey? Bleh. 

 It was like 50 Celsius the other day in Melbourne, Australia. Of course, it wouldn’t serve much relevance to me had it not affected some of the wickedest tennis players in the Australian Open. Former winner, and number three in the world, Novak Djokovic retired after four sets against seventh seed Andy Roddick. They both have monster serves, but Roddick definitely has the upper hand in terms of speed and aces. Djokovic was playing really rusty in the third set (6-2) so the match itself didn’t look in his favour anyways. Roddick is set to face second seed Roger Federer (who demolished, eighth seed, Del Potro) in the semifinals. Kuddos to Jelena Dokic for making it to the quarterfinals only to lose to Dinara Safina (3). Safina is Safina is the younger sister of Marat Safin (26). While Dokic is a wildcard in the tournament (wildcards are often given to players of the tournaments location so they can represent), it isn’t often you see people ranked below a hundred get so far in to the tourney. Nadal (1), having creamed Gonzalez (13), faces Simon (6) and Tsonga (5), who put up a good show against Blake (9), faces Verdasco (14). Ideally, I would like to see a Nadal vs. Tsonga. And then after that I’m really up for anything since who ever wins will face either Roddick or Federer (depending on who wins). Roddick is really good on hard court and he’s shed fifteen pounds which has improved his game and speed. Federer, well, he’s versatile in all courts, and pretty brilliant in both offence and defence – so his chances of winning are way high. For Roddick to win he’ll need to rely on his serves and forehands. He usually gets a high percentage of points when his first serve is successful, but then again, he’s never won a Grand Slam final against Federer (losing twice at Wimbledon and once at the US Open). 

 

🐇 bouncy  |  ♫ Kanye West (ft. Consequence & Cam'Ron) - Gone

Countdown to screwedness...

Jan. 27th, 2009   3:12 AM
Just read Astonishing X-Men issues 7-12.  The story arc was a bit to imaginative in some regards, but the writing and thought put in was good.  Just a few more issues until Hisako Ichiki (aka Armor) joins the team.  I've downloaded up to issue 28 which is the latest.  At the rate I'm going at I'll probably be caught up by the weekend.  That being said, I am really screwing myself in the long run.  I have two short papers to write, a crap load of twenty plus page articles to read, and an assignment to complete by next Monday.  I did nothing since coming home from school and my sleeping pattern is a wreck (its 3:20am and I'm only a little bit tired).  Furthermore, I began downloading more episodes of Soul Eater.  I stopped watching at episode 23 for reasons I don't remember, but this afternoon I had a sudden urge to watch Black Star kick butt.  Skimmed through some old episodes, than downloaded seven more episodes (meaning I have episodes 24-33 unwatched).  I hope I don't get addicted cause episode 41 is the most recent fansub available for download.  Yea, I'm sorta fuk'd. I had a couple other things picking at my brain, but I don't think they're really worth ranting about - I'll save'em for another post.  I'll try to find a way to pass the nine hours I have until I got classes, hopefully I can get some shut eye.
