Weekend wrap up...

Jan. 26th, 2009   1:44 AM

Happy Chinese New Years! (to the few, and probably zero, Chinese people who read my this).

 I’ve been far to relaxed this and last weekend. I’ve barely scratched my pile of homework – it’s frighteningly huge. Sure, finishing a story deserves some merit, but it’s only a leisurely activity. I was so dedicated the first week of school, the enthusiasm sure died down quickly. I’ve gotta get back in to the groove of things (so hard to do!), especially since my first assignment is due at the end of this week.  

 Well, maybe I’ll start doing homework after this entry.

 The 2009 Australian Open (tennis tournament) is in its eighth day, fourth round matches. I managed to catch the third and final set on Friday between Roger Federer (2) and Marat Safin (26). It was really good, though it almost always seems to be that way (final sets to conclude the match usually due to a tie in the progressing score). Federer won it in three straight sets (6-3, 6-2, 7-6). It was really hilarious when Safin got foot faulted. It rarely happens in tennis, especially on the professional level. If you YouTube him, you’ll find that he’s been foot faulted before – he goes ballistic on the ref. Safin has anger problems, when he’s frustrated he pounds his racket against the ground. I read somewhere that as of 2005 he’s trashed over 300 rackets.

 On Saturday I watched the fifth and final set between Fernando Gonzalez (13) and Richard Gasquet (24). I don’t really know much about these two, but the match was set was really really good.  You can tell how bad they’re struggling to fight on, since they’ve been playing for four hours. It was also awesome how they played with the crowed, sometimes even getting annoyed by their own fans. Gonzalez ended up winning (3-6, 3-6, 7-6, 6-2, 12-10). I had no idea earlier, but in the Australian Open they don’t play by 7 matches win for fifth sets. Instead, they keep playing until someone wins two consecutive matches. Surprisingly, this isn’t the highest in this year’s tournament. In the earlier rounds there was a fifth set that went to 14-12 (I can’t imagine how taxing it must’ve been on the players, it wasn’t even for a major match). 

 Besides those two matches I haven’t gotten around to watching any others – saddens me a bit. I watch the daily highlights online but sometimes it doesn’t cut it. I listened to the live radio broadcast online last night during the Roger Federer (2) and Tomas Berdych (20) match. I listened for part of the third set (it was like 2am), and Federer was doing horrible. The score was 4-6, 6-7, 2-2. The commentators were saying how out of his element he was, but if he managed to stretch it to the fifth set he’d win it (because Federer is a powerhouse when it comes to stamina). I was a bit worried since Jelena Jankovic, the number one seed on the woman’s circuit, had lost earlier in the day to Marion Bartoli (16). Btw, number beside each players name indicates their seed/rank. Federer pulled through to win the match (4-6, 6-7, 6-4, 6-4, 6-2). I’m a bit sad that Gael Monfils (12) retired in his match today against Gilles Simon (6) due to a wrist injury (4-6, 6-2, 6-1), since he’s a player I really like (he’s young, and he’s got a lot of enthusiasm). 

 If I have the time or opportunity, I would really want to watch James Blake (9), Andy Roddick (7), Andy Murray (4), Jo-Wilfried Tsonga (5), Rafael Nadal (1), and Novak Djokovic (3) play. For obvious reasons they’re all in the top ten because they’re really amazing players and definitely have entertaining matches. James Blake is one of my most favourite players. He’s kinda old (29), but his game has progressively gotten better and better. I see him as a bit of an underdog since he doesn’t win major titles often, usually winning the smaller ATP tournaments. He broke his neck in 2004, due to his absence his rank dropped to 210 in late 2005. So it’s really something to watch him climb up to 9th in the world (his highest being 4th in Nov. 2006). In the round of 16, he players Jo-Wilfried Tsonga (5) later today.

I spent a bit of time with family, more so than usual, this weekend. Saturday night I had dinner with my mom, dad, dad’s younger sister, and her husband. I really like my aunt Liana even though I don’t know her as much as I would like to. I think I like her partially because she’s sort of the ‘odd-one-out’ in her family, kinda like me. She and my dad had a really big argument one time and they held a grudge for, I think, a ten years. I don’t remember when it started, or the exact reason, but it had something to do with a death in the family and my aunt not being able to make it to the funeral. My dad got really upset about it (probably cause he found better reasons for her to attend than whatever else she was doing). Which is one of the reasons I really dislike my dad. He likes to argue (not even so much debate, since he has the shortest temper and raises his voice over the smallest things). The worst part is, he always likes to be right, often extending an argument just so he can prolong his side of the fight. I will admit he is really intelligent (he’s a fanatic with Asian history and military history), which adds to the fact that he doesn’t like it when his intelligence is insulted. You know how husbands dislike their mother-in-laws? Well I’ve seen first hand battles backing that statement. My mother’s parents live with us, and my grandmother is, in some regards, quite the opposite to my dad. She talks A LOT. Like, non-stop sometimes. If she’s around people, she won’t be able to shut up for more than 20 seconds. It drives me fuking insane. Furthermore, she is reminiscent of the past. She relates every other thing with the way things were ‘back then’. This can get annoying. If you put my grandma in the same room with my dad, an argument will almost always break out. Short tempered people cannot co-exist with chatterboxes. 

But anyways, one reason I like my aunt is because she pissed off my dad and held her ground for so long. They made peace about two years ago, though, which was sorta nice to see since between that time I haven’t had much opportunity to see my aunt. Another reason I like my aunt Liana is because of the way she met her husband. She met Viktor through Lava Life – very much against the norm especially when they’re both Chinese. Chinese people, as far as I know, don’t practice arranged marriages (now a days), but Chinese tradition is also slow on the technological side. It is rare (from what I’ve seen), to find two Asians hooking up and getting married via an online dating website. I really like Viktor, he’s a cool guy, a bit short though, but he plays a lot of sports and seems to be doing okay financially. Mind you, my aunt is twelve years younger than my dad (who turned fifty last Nov), so they’re a bit of an old couple – since most people get married in their late 20s, early 30s. They got married last year at a couples resort in, if I recall correctly, the Mediterranean’s, and held their big family gathering dinner when they got back – which I went. The gathering was a bit different since my dad was really the only one representing my aunts side of the family, while the other 30ish, ten seated dining tables at the banquet were all of Viktor’s family. A few other tables were of their friends, but still, must’ve been weird for my dad >>. So the dinner on Saturday went pretty awesome, my aunt is about eight months pregnant (her boy is due sometime early March). They recently bought a house about five minutes drive from where I live, so it looks like a pretty wicked future for them. 

Sunday night my family and uncles family (mother’s younger brother), went for a Chinese New Years Eve dinner. My grandparents are pretty traditional so she likes to have family gatherings and eat certain foods for special Chinese occasions – plus they usually shell out the bucks. Our dinner was an eight course meal that cost, if I heard correctly, $120. Chinese people have these set dinners that you need to order in advance, they usually feed 10-12 people and you order than for special occasions (like birthdays, weddings, anniversaries, etc). The meal usually consists of 8 or 10 or 12 courses, depending on how much you’re willing to pay (usually 8 though since it’s a lucky number in Chinese). More often than not they consist of a cold meat platter, steamed, two veggie dishes, whole fish, roast chicken, lobster, shark fin soup, fried rice, fried noodles, and desserts (plus or minus a few dishes, again, depending on how much you’re willing to pay). It’s a bit of a shame since my brother and I don’t really get alone with my two younger cousins – most probably because of the age gap. One is in, I think, grade eight or nine, and the other is in grade ten or eleven (that’s how well I don’t know them lol). I’m a bit jealous cause I watched them grow taller than me. When they were younger they were short little pipsqueaks – quite literally – now they’re skinny trees a few inches taller than me. Like my dad, I’m quite confused sometimes by my uncle. My uncle is a pharmacist (therefore he makes quite a lot of mulla) and he’s able to afford, minimum, two ‘out of the country’ vacations for his family every year. But what confuses me is why he doesn’t take my grandparents. He’s financially set, so why doesn’t he get my grandparents to live with them for a change? I don’t mean any disrespect for my grandparents, but they’ve lived under the same roof as I have since I was like twelve. My grandma usually defends my uncle in saying that “he’s got kids who are younger and need to be looked after” which is pretty bullshit considering they’ve lived with me when I was their age. Whatever, not something worth boiling over.

 In other news, I got my replies back from the PM’s I sent to WingZ, Elizabeth, and Victoriah Nichole Little yesterday regarding my latest short story. I didn’t bother sending one to Teekabell since he hasn’t been online for a while (my last PM is marked as ‘sent’ rather than ‘delivered’ meaning he hasn’t read it yet). Didn’t get much out of Victoriah’s PM – in terms of how I could improve. Elizabeth’s PM, meh. Maybe I was just expecting a little more (which is a bit inappropriate considering she’s doing me the favour), but she didn’t really tell me anything I didn’t already know. Yea, diapers and superhero stories are a difficult combination to pull of, and yeah, what little I mentioned about diapers in the story were really pulling it – in terms of fitting it in for the sake of calling it what it is, an ABDL story. A bit stupid of me, though, seeing as she’s reinforcing what I already know, should be a kick in the butt telling me to go back and fix the things that could be presented better. I donno, not to exciting though, so I wasn’t to motivated. WingZ’s PM hit the spot though, that’s why I like the feedback I get from him (maybe I should send one out to Teekabel – might give me a contrasting opinion like last time). He points out that I gloss over the things that interest the reader (Kara’s past) which I don’t fully agree. I do bring up some things of her past, but probably not enough of in his opinion. Also, like Elizabeth, he notes that the ABDL elements are abrupt and undeveloped – which I agree. Essentially, the story begs for expansion – which I’m beginning to agree. Maybe I’m just lazy and hold a “what’s done is done” sorta attitude, but going back and putting in the pounds for this story could be a lot of work. I could go back to my original plan, however, where it could be a two part story – that could help settle the issue for expansion. All in all though, I don’t think I’ll post this story anywhere besides this blog. It’s unfinished and I’m not ready to beef it up just yet. I’m in no rush to get it posted either (because I got what I really wanted, which was feedback to improve the story). In the mean time, I think I’ll focus on ‘Be Normal’, and when that’s done I’ll come back to this story and put some more meat into it. 

 In comic book news (it’s hard to go one entry without mentioning my addiction lol)! I have begun reading, when I probably shouldn’t since I still have other things to read, Astonishing X-Men. It is an on going series that began in 2004. I’m having a bit of difficulty reminding myself while reading the first couple of issues that certain things haven’t happened yet! Going back four years is a long time! Like, House of M hasn’t happened yet, or Civil War. But then, I have to remind myself that certain things have happened and the series takes place afterwards – like the Legacy Virus and E is for Extinction. One of the reasons I want to read Astonishing X-Men, a little embarrassing to say, is because of Hisako Ichiki (or the mutant known as Armor). I think she’s got a really wicked ability (can generate psionic body armour that grants super strength and durability), plus in Runaways she’s, supposedly, going to become the leader of X-Men…but knowing comic books it could be in another universe or version or whatever. Of the first six issues I’ve read (still downloading the next bundle) she’s only made one appearance using her powers. From Wiki, I know that she makes a couple more and I can’t wait ^^. Currently the series has published 24 (or 25?) issues so I’ve got a lot of catching up to do – but downloading is making that difficult for me. 

 Think I’ve covered everything I had in mind. Time to burn the midnight oil and catch up on homework. Gotta remember to wear something red and be vegan today! (it’s traditional on Chinese New Years). Peace.

🌀 awake
Link

Misplaced Justice (short ABDL story)

Jan. 25th, 2009   12:05 AM
Meh, it took a little longer than usual (due to procrastination and being sick) but I'm finished.  I don't feel as good about this story as I did with Love Thy Neighbour, but I think it turned out relatively good.  It lacks the obvious 'omg ABDL content' that smacks your face silly which turns some people off, but oh well, their loss.  It's only 5 MS Word pages.  I've sent a copy to WingZ, Elizabeth, and Victoriah Nichole Little in hopes to get it proof read before I post it on any other websites.  Hope you enjoy the sneak peak, and again there is a possibility I might edit this a bit.

Misplaced Justice

 According to the kitchen clock it’s a quarter to seven. Unconvinced though, my father looked up from his pacing. Only seconds have passed. I can hear his heart pounding between the seconds and the steps. If he continues he’ll probably pass out. 

 “Let’s make a good impression and show up a little early.” I finally suggested. As if on cue he retrieved his jacket and headed for the door, I grabbed my book bag and followed.

 He’s out of practice, as far as I could tell, but it was expected from a man so devoted to his job. Reassuring him that his date would turn out a success, I rang our neighbour’s doorbell and waited with my own uncertainties. This would be my first time babysitting. I had to make an equally as strong of an impression as my father would. 

 “This is my daughter Kara. Kara, I’d like you to meet Mrs. Hayes.” The woman who answered the door extended her hand at me. I smiled and gave a firm handshake to reflect me personality. 

 “Your father tells me a lot of good things about you. I hope Deana won’t be a handful for you.” When we entered I began to feel nervous as I got a glance of their family room. My father only mentioned that his date had a little girl, not specifying what age. And although they lived across the street form us I had never paid much attention. 

 Deana looked up as we entered her room. It looked exactly as I’d imagined a six year olds room would look; Disney bed sheets, a shelf of picture books, and a dresser decorated with stickers, among other things. “Sweetie, this is Kara and her daddy from across the street. While we’ll be away for the evening, Kara will be looking after you.”

 I began to warm up around her as she smiled. I sensed a comforting acceptance as she acknowledged her mother and greeted my father and I. The innocent tone she used suggested our night would be not the least bit challenging. As the four of us proceeded to the door Mrs. Hayes went over the ground rules and what I might expect. As we waved goodbye to our parents, I encouraged them to have a fun night as we’d await the same. 

 “What do you want to do?” Deana asked as I closed the door. I had hoped to ask her the same since I was unfamiliar with what she liked to do. 

 “Um, what book were you reading in your room? Would you like me to read it with you?”

 “No, I don’t want to do hat.” Instead Deana led me to the family room where I got a better look at the array of toys scattered across the floor. “Do you want to play dolls?”

 “Sure.” A familiar figure caught my attention so I picked it up for a closer look. Just as I though, “Are you a fan of Buckshot?”

 Deana shook her head. “It’s my brothers, he forgot it when he visited mommy.”

 I tossed the action figure aside. I wasn’t a fan either. As I continued scanning the clutter I noticed that Deana had settled with something. “What’cha playing with?”

 “Miracle Girl. She’s my favourite superhero.” I took a seat next to her and watched a brawl between dolls accompanied by sound effects.

 “How come?”

 “Cause she can fly and she’s super fast. And she never gets hurt cause she’s super strong. And she always helps people in trouble and beats up all the bad guys…”

 I pondered over her fanaticism. I missed living a youthful life of narrow understandings. I could close my eyes and pretend to see the world as she sees, but only to be brought out of it by the very things that made me the way I am. “Yeah,” I finally replied. “She’s pretty cool, even if she was before your time.”

 “What do you mean?” Deana asked with curiosity and wonder written all over her face. 

 “It’s a bit complicated…” It took a second to come up with the right words. “You know how Buckshot is real? And how he fights crime and saves people all the time?”

 Deana nodded with a continued look of interest. 

 “Miracle Girl is real too. She was the cities hero before Buckshot. Even before you were born.”

 “But why did she stop?”

 “Well, it’s because she got replaced by Buckshot. Everyone agreed that it wasn’t right to let a child fight bad guys. It didn’t matter who was stronger. That’s just how adults are; because they’re older Miracle Girl always had to listen to them.”

 My reply didn’t sit well for her, as if it was unsatisfactory. In the most straightforward of answers it was the truth. The world’s first superhero was a child, who was replaced by the world’s first adult superhero. I could sense her mood was changing and I didn’t want to end my explanation with a bitter aftertaste. 

 “Do you want to know how I know?” She didn’t show much anticipation until after I had finished. “But you have to promise me to keep it a secret.”

 Sticking out her pinky while nodding with enthusiasm, I did the same. “Then we’ll pinky swear on it.”

 It only took a second to undo years of subconsciously suppressing my powers so that I wouldn’t break anything or hurt anyone by accident. Lifting the sofa with one hand, I brought it over our heads. 

 “Wow…”

 Deana’s breathless whisper increased, as well as her excitement, as I placed the sofa back in its place. Suspecting a storm of questions, instead, I began my story. 

 “I started using my powers for good when I was about your age. My father was a military engineer, and he could make almost anything. He…“

 “But my mommy told me that your daddy’s a lawyer.” 

 “Oh, he is, but he adopted me when I was much older. The daddy that raised me as a child was another man.” He put the man in manipulate, I once told my psychiatrist. He didn’t love me. He loved what he controlled and the things we did together – saving people, preventing disasters, and living a double life. We did a lot of good together. But it doesn’t matter who my father was, it matters who I remember he was; a bastard.

 “But what about school? Miracle Girl’s always wearing a school uniform, how’d you saved people even during school time.”

 “I never went to school, Deana. I was home schooled by my daddy until I was twelve. He believed the uniform would throw off my identity to the reporters. So whenever the city needed me all I’d have to put on was the domino mask.” Unlike other students my age, however, combat training was in my curriculum. Learning how to assess situations and thinking strategically were just some of the things my father taught me. In my own naïve frame of mind I treated a lot of our adventures like games. I was so ignorant to the realities of failure. 

 “Wow! That must have been the bestest years of your life!” I lowered my head and allowed my hair to hide the smile I wasn’t making. Deana continued playing out a pretend battle while, probably, conceiving another question to ask. I envied her. There were so many undesirable consequences to being a superhero that my father never had the luxury of experiencing. 

 “Did you know when my x-ray vision powers manifested I threw up all over my dad?”

 “Ewww, why did you do that?”

 “I couldn’t control it! All of the sudden I was looking at my dad’s insiders. It was really gross.”

 I continued finding ways to make myself less dispirited, as well as further Deana’s uncontrollable giggling. “And the first couple of times I went to fight bad guys I was so scared I had accidents! My daddy made me wear diapers for a couple of months, can you believe that?”

 Hiding the embarrassment from laughing at myself I buried my head into my hands. Deana continued giggling, this time a little more apprehensive. 

 “My…my mommy makes me wear diapers to bed. If superheroes wear them than I think it’s super cool too!”

 “Yeah, adults make us do pretty silly things some times, don’t they.”   I sighed, exhausted from the laughter. I consider telling her the time I nearly killed a cyborg – who turned out to be an amputee with prosthetics and a pacemaker, as I later learned – but the humour would have been mine alone.

 “How come you called yourself Miracle Girl?”

 “Oh, I didn’t choose it. The news reporters began calling me Miracle Girl after I stopped the second plane, so everyone else started calling me that.”

 “What plane?”

 “A year after I started using my powers there was a terrible attack on our city. You weren’t born at the time, so you might not know about 9/11. I don’t think I should be talking about this, Deana. It’s something you’ll learn when you’re older.” It was something I had wished a grown up would have said to me – to be able to learn when I was older. But instead I had to learn everything first hand. I couldn’t save all the jumpers that day. Just like how I couldn’t save the kids I had influenced – the one who thought he could fly leaped off a building only to die.

 No one ever thinks about how bad other people might have it even when they look as though they’re in full control. Having great power doesn’t always translate to great responsibility, as my psychiatrist explained. Most people in life don’t have great power, and the few who do are almost never responsible with it. The people who have the greatest responsibility are the kids with no power because they are the ones who have to keep everybody else in check. So then why was I the exception? Just as I always wonder, why was I robbed of a normal childhood? 

 “Okay. Do you miss being Miracle Girl?”

 “No, not really. I don’t think a lot of people missed her either.” 

 It’s difficult not to feel guilt for wanting to blame a child who couldn’t do what was always asked of her. Similarly you can’t ask a child to take sides when they don’t understand what’s at hand, as I was forced to during a protest that got out of hand in front of the United Nations. You cannot tell a child to go fight a war overseas. It’s all unethical. That is why no one would miss me when Buckshot took my position. Everyone believed he had done the right thing by allowing me to live a normal life. Little did anyone know I had no real knowledge what a normal life meant, I was only eleven. 

 When the city no longer needed me I wasn’t the only one thrown back in to civilization. My father was no longer needed as well. He was the one pulling all the strings in seclusion. And that was where he killed himself. 

 “Buckshot is a good guy. He’s done a lot more good than I have, so we just have to keep believing in him.” I could have continued rambling but I could tell Deana was getting sleepy. “I think it’s time for someone’s bed time.”

 Not putting up a fight, Deana nodded and got ready for bed. She didn’t need me to make sure her Pull-Ups were on properly. After tucking her in and reading a short story, I spent the rest of the night in the family room. It felt different being alone while surrounded by things I had missed out on. The desire to take a tiny piece of someone else’s childhood in hopes to replenish my own was uncomforting. Consequently, I ended up putting a few alphabet blocks into my book bag. When Mrs. Haynes and my father came back from their date I was happy, both for my father’s apparent success as well as my relief from duty. It was also nice to be around adults again. Not long after we went home and I retired to my room. 

 I felt a strong sense of pleasure as I retrieved a box from beneath my bed. The envy from earlier subsided. Less of who I was became burdened by my actions in the past when I draped a purple baby blanket over my shoulders like a cape. I no longer spoke or thought of all the things I had seen when I placed a pacifier into my mouth. While I curled up in bed to have a dream I wouldn’t remember having, I recalled the words of my psychiatrist. “There are two ways to deal with envy. One is to admit it, and therefore become free of the guilt of desire and learn how to live without. The other option is to merely take what you want.”

 

 Commentary:

As I mentioned in an earlier post, I got the idea for this story from Runaways Vol2, issue #1, page 16/25. Basically there’s a help group for teenage superheroes and Julie Power makes a comment about being robbed of a normal childhood. So from that I just threw in a little closet TB tendencies and simple enough a story came through. But not so simple are the things Kara recalls having to do – some of which I got ideas from Ex Machina. For example, in Ex Machina the protagonist stops the second plane, has some conflicts between protesters marching to the United Nations, and so forth. The idea of having only one superhero in the world also came from Ex Machina – I had considered whether making Kara’s identity known or not, but chose the latter. 

 The name Kara came from Kara Zor-El (Supergirl), and the last name Haynes came from a family name in Runaways. In earlier drafts Miracle Girl was called Kid Incredible while Buckshot was called Ani-Man (for Animal Man since I wanted him to have animal related powers). 

 Also, I considered writing this story in two parts. One about babysitting, which would focus less serious issues, and the other a session with her psychiatrist, which would focus on more serious/darker things. I wanted to write something about revenge. Kara felt that her first dad committed suicide because of Buckshot. And the person adopting Kara would be Buckshot (he wouldn’t know Kara is Miracle Girl, but Kara would know he is Buckshot because of her x-ray vision). That idea didn’t really fall through with how my story was progressing so I didn’t include it. 

 The line about ‘great power and responsibility’ comes from Runaways Vol2, issue 11, page 18/27 when Gertrude and Victor are having sushi with Spider-Man. The last two paragraphs, I felt, were rushed compared to how the rest of the story was paced. I could go back and try to fix it but I’m going to see what feedback I get from the PM’s I sent to WingZ, Elizabeth, and Victoriah Nichole Little. The last three sentences from the last paragraph I took from Justice League: Justice issue #3, page 2/29 when Brainiac is giving a speech to Aquaman (before cutting his head open). 


🌀 creative

Attends 10 Super Plus

Jan. 23rd, 2009   7:57 PM
I went and replenished my diaper supply today, wasn't to happy with the selection at the store I normally go to.  I go to Diaper N Kidswere Factory Outlet.  I've been going since early last year, and it looks like they're business is doing pretty well.  The first time I went the store owner, Ali Khan, was like,
"how'd you find out about this store?" 
"found it on the internet"
"are you good with computers?"
"sorta, it depends."
"do you know how to design websites?"
And so forth.  There website hasn't changed since the first time I went on it, but the reason I say their business looks like its improving is because they have a new female store clerk and their business cards actually look like business cards.  The card I got the first time I went was printed on cheap paper and wasn't laminated/glossy (I've worked at a printing company so when I see paper I sometimes get a little inspector gadget).  Anyways, I went to pick up my usual Attends - they carry a variey of product lines from Attends, Tena, Depends, and a couple of no name brands (like Tranquility, At Ease, and an asian brand).  Actually, I don't know of many stores that sell Attends in Toronto, and that are accessable under my circumstances.  I usually get two packs of medium Attends Slip Regular - 10 Super Plus.  They're the kind that have two tapes on each side, no waist band, and pastic outter cover.  I have a suspicious feeling they're discontinued or something since Attends has been coming out with cloth-like/breathable cover diapers which everyone hates.  They didn't have the Attends Slip Regulars I wanted.  Made me quite unhappy, and I wasn't really willing to sell out the bucks for Tena - they're one of the more expensive brands; granted they absorb well, but they're cloth-like covers.  Instead, they had Attends Slip - 10 Super Plus.  On the package it says 'The new improved Attends Slip with the S'.  The who 'S' marketing thing was to drive at the fact that they were 'S-Form for comfort', 'Skin friendly', 'Super skin dryness', and 'Easy 2-Step fixation'.  I have never seen this product in my life, ever.  Not on photo websites, reviews, forums, etc.  That didn't mean I was hyped to try them though.  They look like a cross between jockstrap style diapers and fitted brief style diapers.  Also, the diaper is made with both plastic and cloth-like material.  The waist strap area is cloth-like while the rest of the diaper is plastic cover.  Smart design in my opinion since it's semi-breathable, while keeping the pastic to prevent leaks.  The thickness and absorbency is exactly the same as a Attends Slip Regular.  It has only one tape on each side (seems like quite a few ABDLs wanted one tape before Bambinos came out).  The tape is plastic, but on the front of the diaper there are velcro tabs.  So the velcro tabs stick to the cloth-like straps while the tapes attach to the plastic front.  Pretty clever design.  After trying one, I've come to conclude that I don't like them.  Or maybe, I haven't gotten use to them yet.  It doesn't have the snug feeling you get from wearing a fitted brief diaper, but it doesn't have that naked/hanging feeling of a jockstrap diaper.  It's somewhere in the middle.  It the same sorta feeling I get from wearing a babys diaper with tapes holding up each side (I've done this when I was younger).  With the whole design the way it is, the diaper is fairly quiet since the only real part that crinkles is the backside.  Until I get use to them, and if I ever, I think I'll use them as doublers since it's no different to wearing a suped-up booster pad (with more abosrbency). 

I also bought a pack of medium At East fitted brief diapers.  I don't expect a hell of a lot out of them considering they're a no name brand and relatively thin.  But it'll simmer my fix for 'real' diapers when I need them.  Another great thing about Diaper N Kidswear is that they have a lot of samples.  The clerk gave me three different Tena's and a no name brand to try.  I probably could have gotten more since they have a package open of every product. 
☮️ peaceful  |  ♫ Joe Hisaishi - Sunday
Link

First cold of the year. Outstanding.

Jan. 21st, 2009   10:53 PM

Haven't posted in a couple of days cause I caught the cold on Saturday.  Felt really out of it till now.  Haven't done a hell of a lot since my last entry. 
- read Justice League - Justice 12 issue miniseries (2005-2007).  It was part of the Alex Ross comic bundle i downloaded a while back.  Not bad, as expected.  No doubt I downloaded Alex Ross's stuff because his photorealism illustrations are amazing, but plot wise it was fairly strong.  Aquaman wasn't the usually pussy character, he actually had a fairly strong role in the series so it was quite interesting to follow.
- read and caught up in Ex Machina.  I'm at issue 39, which was the end of a story arc.  The most corrent issue is #40 which I've downloaded, but I don't want to read yet until I have a few more issues of that story arc.  I don't like waiting in anticipation - so I've opt not to read it at all :P
- read and finished Runaways vol 2.  I have yet to download anything from vol 3, but I know that four issues are out already.
- read two issues of White Tiger, a limited series released in Nov. 2006.  It's the Angela Del Toro version of White Tiger.  The first issue didn't really suck me in so I don't think I'll finish it even if it's only six issues long.  Daredevil (Danny Rand version) and Spider-Man make an appearance in the later issues, but that isn't enough to get me to continue reading.
- I forgot who (sorry), but someone from DiaperSpace recommended I read Stray Bullets.  I don't really know what the series is about, but it's only 32 issues.  He's given me the first issue to sample which I'm still getting around to reading.  Hopefully I'll like it.
- zzz Naruto chapter 431 came out a few days ago.  Some lame action scenes and always the cliffhanger which leaves only the slightest buzz.  I think the who 'natural energy' session with the sage frog progressed way to quickly.  And the destruction of Konoha was also really quick.  The series over all has always been 'meh' so its not like I'll miss it if I stop reading for a while, but to stop all together just seems like a waste (considering I've been following with the series for years).  >> stupid naruto.  Wouldn't mind picking up One Piece though, was it not such a ridiculously long series. 
- My superhero abdl story is coming along extremely slow.  I think my problem is I have a lot to say (or I want to write a lot), but I also want to keep it slim in description and easy to follow.  Also, the longer I sit on a story the more and more ideas I come up for it.  Not necessarily a bad thing, but I really want to keep it a short story (like under 15 pages).  For this superhero story I've been debating whether to make it a two part story - each part doesn't flow into the next, but follows the same theme.  As in, I wanted the first part to be about a babysitting job, and the second part to be about a shrink session.  But then I decided to mesh the two together - which is becoming quite difficult without having repetitive sentence starters.  For this story, I really want the characters 'robbed of childhood' complex to shine.  I'm not as confident with how I'm getting it across so far, compared to how I've gotten the message across with my other stories.

🌀 ditzy  |  ♫ Ravens & Chimes - This Is Where We Are

Detached...and then some...

Jan. 17th, 2009   11:25 PM

Waddup? 

 (don’t ask, I just felt like opening with something slang-full of broken English).

 I feel oddly detached from myself. As I mentioned in my earlier post my weekend wasn’t going to be as ideal as I had hoped it would be. And strangely the circumstances changed but my feels remained gloomy. My parents left Friday night to a ski resort for the weekend and, unknown to me earlier, my brother went to a party and stayed over night – he didn’t come home until 7pm. I should be pretty peachy considering I wanted the house to myself before said circumstances, no? Well, I don’t really know what ate me up inside but I just felt detached. On the one hand, a week prior I wanted to car for the weekend too (family car…gotta share…it’s a real bummer). I wanted to use it to go replenish my diaper stash. My dad was kinda iffy about letting me use it – don’t really blame him considering I rarely use it – plus he wanted to drive his own car rather than using their friends (family friend who went with my parents). So I sorta feel cheated from a perfectly good opportunity. Plus, it’s started snowing up a shit storm this evening – had I gotten the car and gone to the store in the afternoon I would have had even less to sulk about. 

So Fridays are my day off – no classes – and I had planned to write up (or at start) the story I mentioned in my earlier post; ‘Be Normal’. Not surprisingly I got nothing written down. Though, I did do a tiny bit of research. I looked in to thumb sucking, and bullying, and ended up watching ‘Thumbsucker’ (2005) again. I also ended up watching like 30min of ‘The Curious Case of Benjamin Button’ (2008) – it seems like a really good flick but the bootleg was glitchy so I stopped watching it – and I also watched about 20min of ‘Bully’ (2001). The film ‘Bully’ is based on the novel ‘Bully: A True Story of High School Revenge’ which is based on the true events of ‘The Murder of Bobby Kent’ in 1993. After watching 20min of the movie I thought it was pretty stupid, not in terms of how the plot was developing, but because of the sucky acting and directing. The only actual reason I watched the movie was because I didn’t have enough knowledge concerning stereotypical jocks. Oh well, I tweaked my story plot a little bit. I want Henry to be a bit of a comic book fanatic (don’t get any ideas, Henry isn’t me…seriously!), so that I can add something like this: ‘Everyone knows the kingpin is the one who devises all the master plans while the cronies execute them. And everyone known’s once the kingpin goes, everything beneath follows.’ Well something along those lines. I was thinking Daredevil: Reborn Again at the time (since Kingpin uses his connections and cronies to play out his plans on ruining Matt Mardock, but when he gets greedy and takes matters into his own hands the Kingpin essentially gives himself away). So where am I going with this? Well, I want Jason to follow that Kingpin role – he isn’t the bully, his friends are, he just sits back and watches. So when Henry exposes Jason, the whole confession process will have a bit more depth – or I’m hoping to capture. 

But none of that really matters since I haven’t written diddly-squat yet. 

Oh I forgot to mention, I also wasted my Friday reading Ex Machina. I thought I had the complete series since I read up to issue 33, but it didn’t feel like I was reading an ending. Stupid me, of course, I completely read wrong when I Wikipedia’d it before downloading the torrent file. At the time of whoever wrote the Wiki, there were only 33 issues out – and the torrent file I downloaded just so happen to have 33 issues. I read wrong, the series is still in continuation. Now, I could be wrong, but I think there are 40 issues out right now – or that is the latest issue available for downloading at the moment. But yea, I have seven more issues and then I’ll be caught up. Than I’ll have to play the waiting game which is hate – its bad enough I have to wait till Mondays and Fridays for a new page of ‘Better Days’, on top of the unscheduled releases of the latest chapter from Naruto mangas. Yea, not a particularly big fan of waiting. When I use to be hooked on anime, I would watch only the ones that had completed series – or finished seasons. Same with Asian dramas (for a brief period) – I really didn’t like having to download one episode at a time, much rather prefer big bundles. 

Anyways, Friday didn’t turn out as good as it could have. Mind you, I did make an effort – padded myself up for the night and had two screwdrivers. It was kinda weird though since I’m a lightweight when it comes to drinking, and I’m a sleepy drunk. But I had so much difficulty falling asleep last night. I went to bed at 1am, which I consider early, and I don’t recall when I actually fell asleep. I couldn’t have gotten more than three or four hours of shut eye though. I eventually got up at 8am, which I consider a sin especially since it was a Saturday (its 00:14, Sunday, as I am writing this – ignore the blog time stamp).

Saturday was productive. I intended to write my story on Friday, than spend Saturday polishing it and getting some school work done. Well guess what? The procrastinator did school work. Yea, I’m a bit surprised myself – since I tend to push things back and do more leisurely things. I also washed two baskets of laundry, read four issues of the second volume of Runaways (which sparked another story idea), and I played one straight hour of the arcade on DiaperSpace.com. The game ‘X-Training’ is dead simple, but so much fun! (either that or I’m half brainless and easily entertained…yay! o.O). But yea, I played that game for a solid during my ‘study break’ – don’t be hating, I read four epidemiology articles…yesh, sounds smart lol). I really wanted to get another ‘Arcade Champs’ title. I have two right now, but I only recall receiving one from the game ‘Battleball’ (I know I’m super forgetful, but I honestly have no recollection ever playing and winning any other games besides Battleball). Oh Battleball…I honest to gawd ruined this game for any/everyone who considers or has played this game. Why you might ask? Well, the previous champ scored 7180 which is pretty damn good. The best I ever got was 7020…until I discovered a game flaw. Don’t hate the player, hate the game! Because of the flaw I have first (20100), second (11050), and third (9050) for overall rankings. I think I ruined the game, no one’s going to try and take my title by playing legit, unless of course they discover the game flaw. Anyways, X-Training! My newest obsession. The first place score is 46 right now and my highest is fifth place with 37. I haven’t found any flaws yet, but found a much easier way of playing the game (it took 45min till the light bulb flickered). I can safely say I wouldn’t have gotten 37 had it continued playing the way I did in those previous 45min…the trick, however, I’ll keep as a secret. 

Okay, last thing I wanted to write about. The new story idea – influenced by Runaways Vol2, Issue 1. Actually, to be honest, I donno if this story has been done yet, but I don’t think it has. I know Elizabeth has done a couple of superhero stories (either I’ve forgotten the plots or haven’t read yet), and so has Dementia’s Knight – but I’ve read his superhero story and it’s different, however, I think he has new stories I haven’t read yet. But anyways! The story idea is pretty simple (and though I’ve already said it before with another of my story ideas…’I can’t believe someone hasn’t done it yet – or that I’m aware of’). The idea is almost straight out the text of the comic! In page 16 of 25, Julie Power (aka. Lightspeed) of Power Pack (a sibling-based superhero team), explains how she felt as though she’d been robbed of a normal childhood due to fighting crime while growing up. She says something about trying to get some of her innocence back, etc etc. ABDL readers of my blog, please tell me you’re catching on cause if you haven’t I feel ‘fail’. Now, I’ve never read Power Pack (I think I’ve heard of them…I forget), nor have I done any research yet, but this is how I want my story to go down. Think of it a child actor losing his audience while growing up (like that comedian who made that really shitting movie…I don’t recall who, but I can put a face to it). Well, picture this. A child superhero with phenomenal powers – fights crime but interprets it as a game due to ‘just following what his/her parents say’. Other than the kid being robbed of his childhood, I personally think it’d fuk the poor kid up. I mean, kids and violence? Bound to have some repercussions in the future. Okay, so there’s the ethical and moral issue – excellent, makes good story filler. What else do I want? Reporters! Paparazzi’s! The media! Commercialism! Everything and anything a society can throw at a child superhero that’d fuk him/her over in the future. I’m still debating whether the kids identity should be public or not, I don’t know if it’d matter as much or not. So how does his fame end? Adult superheros! Duh, adults always take away all the fun from kids – everyone agrees that the adult should take his place since crime fighting is no place for a kid. Again, still debating whether the kid is like the only superhero alive (up until the adult takes spotlight) or if superheros are pretty mainstream. I’m going with the former right now. So the kid is essentially thrown back into living a normal life, even though he/she isn’t normal – has a pretty messed up conscious due to all the violence and blood and the swearing.  The kid essentially wants his childhood/innocence back and that should make for a wicked story. I may need to re-examine The Umbrella Academy since its about sibling superheros growing up with difficulties. Heck, I might even finally get to reuse my made-up city…Mandinare! Even though this story popped in to my head a few hours ago, and what I’ve written here is really incoherent, I’ve got a pretty solid idea of how the story will flow (yes, it will make sense with explanations – if I ever get to it of course). All I really need to do is research a little and find some time! 

 Debating what to do today, Sunday. I still have some articles to read, but I’m feeling pretty pumped about my latest story idea. Decisions, decisions.


🌀 awake  |  ♫ The Fratellis - Flathead
Link

GRRrrr! LJ ate my entry!

Jan. 14th, 2009   10:44 PM

Fuck this!  I wrote up two paragraphs describing the shitty week I've been having, then accidentally hit the 'Enter' button cause I was using my Googles search toolbar.  And you know what happened when I pressed back?  My fucking entry got deleted.  I swear to gawd this happens every time I forget to use MS Word to type out my entries before pasting and posting.  >< Not happy. 

So rather than living through, and having to rethink of what I wrote in the two paragraphs I’ll just point form it. Shitting week because…

-          friggin -20 C weather

-          don’t get the house to myself = bro changed his mind, he’s not going with my parents who will be out of town for the weekend

-          behind in reading (crammed 60 pages yesterday and another 60 today/tonight)

-          authors of academic journals write to motherfuking complex for their own good

=== Frustration mode off. ===

 So the 2009 Australian Open Tennis Championships starts on the 19th, it should be something worth watching. Right now they’re in day two of the qualifying matches. I’m hoping James Blake will reach some good rankings. He doesn’t win Grand Slam tournaments all that often, but he holds a couple of Masters and International Series titles. He’s my favourite player cause he’s a bit of an underdog – he’s currently ranked 10th in the world. There’s a lot of new blood in the circuit lately, so there’s definitely going to be a high level of competition. Nadal, Federer, and Safin are probably the ones who’ll reach the finals in the men’s division. I’m not as familiar with the females division though. 

 FusionFall officially launches today. I stopped playing it a couple weeks ago – mainly because I could complete a quest due the network constantly closing. The official launch is a real joke. You can play free but can only get as far as level four. Or, you can get unlimited/full access starting at $6 a month. It’s a joke because my character (I logged on just to check out my account, not to play – seriously!) is level 14 and I can’t enter the game because I need to be a subscriber. Before the release they were saying accounts would get carried over after open beta testings were complete. Well, a lot of good that did! They didn’t even mention anything about having to pay for a subscription before hand. It’s a fun game, but I’m not shelling out $6 to play it – it isn’t even worth $6 of fun…for a month. Plus, it’s a game designed for ten year olds – I wonder if those kids will be able to convince their parents to pay up. 

 Last thing I wanted to write about is about my story ideas. In my Jan 5th entry, my second story idea has been given some thought – and I likie. I want the story to be titled ‘Be Normal’. It should spark some interest (to people who judge a book by its cover) since ABDL stuff is anything but normal. The title and plot are influenced by a quote I’ve been using for my MSN header; ‘The only normal people are the one’s you don’t know very well.’ I don’t know who said it, and I don’t remember where I first read it from, but I really love it. So this story is about an anti-social, nerdy-sorta, shy male high school student who has a thumb sucking habit (we’ll call him Henry for now). And yes, I am totally biting the main character from the 2005 move ‘Thumbsucker’. Sucking his thumb sooths him of his worries, and although he does it unconsciously, he retreats to washroom stalls to suck his thumb while in school (again, just like in the movie – the similarities end here though). His main worries come from the school bully, who is also the school jock (whom we’ll call Jason for now). I based this character mainly off of Flash Thompson (from Spider-Man). This character is the star quarterback for his school, he receivers undeserving grades because teachers know he’s a moron who needs passing grades in order to continue playing football – which, when he plays, makes the school reputation better. It’s a fairly common cycle I’m sure in the states, but I’ll need to do more research since I’m not 100% sure. So these two characters are whom the story revolves around; the bully and the victim. So one day Henry is doing his thing in the washroom (although on this occasion he’s in the change room washroom connected to the gym) when Jason walks in. Henry drops is uber old cellphone from fright. Unaware to Jason that it belongs to Henry, but being the bully that he is, he throws it in to the trash bin. When Jason leaves, Henry goes to retrieve it only to discover a used diaper. So guestimating the time of day and activities surrounding said time, he figures it belongs to someone on the football team. In the days follow, he hides in the washroom to find out who’s wearing and disposing diapers (I may need to give a reason for his curiosity [OCD perhaps?]). He catches Jason red handed eventually. What follows is what I hope to be (write) a realistic, but mature conversation (or series of conversations) describing each of their flaws (incontinent and thumbsucking) and their position in the school hierarchy (jock and nerd), and how the two may or may not influence either. Basically, I want to show a more human side of Jason. The whole school is counting on him, there’s a lot of pressure and expectations that he has to meet, but in a sense it is entirely superficial since no body really cares about what’s going on with him from inside. No one understands the position he’s in or how he feels about being titled a moron and having to ride on the gravy train. To add to that, he’s incontinent – a loss of control contradicts the way he behaves around others. He always puts on an image of ‘being in control’ of his life since it seems so grand to be a jock. So one cause of his bullying (when Henry asks why he does it) is because he feels that it is one of the only things in his life that he actually has control over (as in he can control how much hurt he puts on individuals where as he doesn’t have control over many aspects of his life [education, recreation, stereotypical things]). If that isn’t human(izing?) enough for you than I don’t know what will tip the scale – cause I think this could be some really powerful stuff if I can write it as good as I’m thinking it. I haven’t thought of an ending but a truce would be most ideal. I’m going to give this story some more thought and attempt to write it over the weekend. 

🌀 frustrated  |  ♫ Final Fantasy - Hey, Dad!
Link

A day without music...

Jan. 12th, 2009   8:57 PM

I go through ear buds something like one every four months. It gets pretty frustrating. My last couple weren’t even the standard cheapest on the shelf ear buds – they were iPod ear buds and one that came with my Blackberry (it had a receiver attach to it too). So I went without music today; I almost always listen to music while on the bus to and from school. The trip to school was ok, since I normally fall asleep. But the trip going back home was a bit frustrating. Something about wanting to shut out the world around me and just drowning in loud music always brings a comfort to my mind. Instead, I had to listen to the noisy as hell bus engine, loud conversations from people on cell phones, and numerous coughing sounds. Made me feel a bit grouchy by the time I got home. It’ll probably be like this tomorrow as well. I’m considering getting headphones, but they usually run for $50 and up. Might be a worthwhile investment if I can take care of them properly. Seeing as I’ll be wearing them outside they’d have to withstand the winter weather. I’m not to concern about my hair (since wearing headphones leaves a line of flattened hair) since I usually wear my beanie or a cap when I go outside. I’m just a little worried about carrying it around when I’m not using them. Leaving them draped over my neck seems like I’m trying to make it a fashion statement or something (plus, I doubt the headphones would always match the clothes I’m wearing, and I don’t want to look ridiculous). Shoving them into my backpack is a sure fire way of shortening its lifespan. I donno, I’ll shop around for now and find some salvation with some old/broken earbuds that play out of only one ear.

 Other than the music issue, today was pretty exhausting. I dropped the 9am class I had originally, but substituted it with a 10am class. The class is a third year Deconstructing Epidemics course taught by an Asian anthro prof. I think she’s Korean, but her name sounds a little Chinese as well. I don’t think I’m going to like her, to say it bluntly. She is extraordinarily boring. She reads off the lecture notes that she provides to her students before lecture verbatim. I really dislike those kinds of profs. I would be more optimistic had she looked more attractive – she’s short and skinning, which are good features, but not particular profile that I’m attracted to. I can’t really tell if she really knows her stuff either. When she reads off the lecture notes she tends to ramble and go in to more detail than necessary – sometimes even confusing herself. Anyways, the class wasn’t as horrible as I so strongly speak of it. My friend Merry is in it; we met last semester in Osteology. We share three courses this semester, to my knowledge. My friend Jully is also taking this course, but she’s in the Friday lecture. Concerning the tutorial for this course, I spoke with the prof to see if she could get my into the Monday tutorial rather than the Friday one I’m assigned to – she couldn’t confirm anything for me. I donno what she’ll see she can do, but hopefully I can get in to the Monday tutorial – I want my Fridays to be school-free. 

 After tutorial I had a three hour break until my next class, which I shared with Merry, Jully, and several others (Emy, Monica, and Lindsay). So Merry and I had lunch at the student center. Surprisingly she told me she was in to hip hop. It blew my mind since a lot of my friends aren’t in to it (or I’m unaware if they are). She went to the Rock the Bells concert, and the Mary J.B/Jay-Z concert. She showed me some photos and videos on her MacBook (which she’s obsessed with) – they were wicked. Merry is head over heels with Mos Def and Common (she and her boyfriend are more in to East Coast rappers). I’d like to think I’m well rounded since I like to listen to all regions of rappers (East, West, and South). We went to class 20min early and just killed time – she forced me to sit at the front, which I’m not to keen about. Foundations of Epidemiology wasn’t bad. I nodded of a couple times, than eventually fell asleep for I think ten minutes (again, sleeping while sitting in the front row >>’’ not my thing). The prof teaching the course is Larry Sawchuk, I’ve mentioned him in an earlier post. He was freakin’ hilarious today – everything from love life jokes to violent threats (in a not serious way). He even gave out five bucks to someone who participated. I didn’t know the answer though! He asked who and when isolated and described tuberculosis first – answer being Robert Koch in 1882. And in 1883 he praised John Snows early epidemiological work on cholera by drinking cholera infected water – to prove that Snow was right and not a nut job. Well, Koch was the nutty one. He ended up committing suicide several years later. Just some random facts. Afterwards I went to have coffee with Jully at Tim Hortons. Caught up a little, nothing all that interesting in either of our lives.  Ended up leaving campus at around 5:40pm while she stayed to do some reading, we normally ride the same bus.

 I’m getting concerned with my workload – mainly stressing because a lot of people I know are ahead of me already (it’s only the second week of school!). Some people have already caught up in all/most of their readings, while Merry and another person I know has already started assignments. Assignments that we haven’t even been given a thorough outline of, just rough descriptions of what we are required to do. I’m beginning to feel kinda screwed. My head really isn’t in the zone right now and its really nerve wrecking. I think it has to do with getting my priorities straightened – although I have been making attempts at keeping organized. The comic books and story writing aren’t helping me much either. I started Ex Machina and I’m on issue #5 now – the plot is good so far, but I don’t like the nonlinear narrating. Like, it’s fine the way it is, but I’m just having a difficult time remembering the dates. I’m probably just not putting any effort in to remembering cause I’m sure I could keep tab of what’s going on when if I tried. Also, story writing…not helping! Without music today, all I was thinking about were story ideas while walking to the bus stop (takes me 15 minutes) this morning. It doesn’t seem like a very long time, but it’s enough to have preliminary plots processed. I thought about the story idea ‘Still Human’ and ‘What’s Wrong with You’ (I wrote about both of them on my story archive blog). They could be potential stories to be written, but I’d need to do research on both of them. I also did more thinking about the Show & Tell story. Something to consider would be doing it in first person narrative through the classmate of the individual he shows off his older siblings ABDL stuff in class. Could add another dimension by making the narrator have an older sibling who’s friends with the classmates sibling. Something sorta like a gossip could get passed around or what not. 

 Speaking of stories, I got back a reply from the private message I sent to Teekabell concerning help with editing my story. I wasn’t pleased when I read his reply at first since it seemed as though he completely missed the point of my story. But after thinking about it, I was satisfied. I liked how there was contrast – WingZ thought my story was good, but Teekabell didn’t. It was interesting to read about why he didn’t like it (said it contained to much details about irrelevant information). And I guess that was the real objective of sending them out to people I knew would give me good feedback (not positive, but constructive). It allowed me to step back and re-observe my writing – to see through his perspective of what went wrong. Even though I don’t agree with his perspective, it was eventually refreshing (rather than displeasing). I replied back to him explaining my interpretation and what I felt he missed from it and how it may have been uninteresting to him. I encouraged him to send me another PM – I’m genuinely interested in what he has to say whether it’s good or bad. 

🙂 okay  |  ♫ Weezer - Burndt Jamb

It has been one month...

Jan. 11th, 2009   3:22 PM
I just noticed that I've been using this blog for a month now.  I remember one of my earlier posts I mentioned being enthusiastic about blogging for a couple of months (based on previous blog sites I've used over the years), but then my interest dies down about three months later.  Lets hope it isn't the same case with this current blog. I haven't done much of anything today.  There's something about the sunny weather that makes me just want to stare out the window all day...or loaf around the net watching hours of YouTube.  I feel guilty though since I know I have a couple of articles to read (I'm a slow reader so I really reallly should get on it...).  Plus my brother is working really hard on whatever Masters Degree sorta stuff he needs to get done...makes me feel like a deadbeat for wasting my time so effortlessly.  I'm considering doing article reviews on this blog, but I doubt it'd go far since 20 page journals are rarely interesting - i wouldn't want to churn out a dry review of something that'd make me (let alone ppl who read this) asleep.  But something to consider I guess. I dropped ANTC14 (Feminism and Anthropology) and ANTC31 (Ritual and Religious Action).  Than I enrolled in ANTC68 (Deconstructing Epidemics).  The class had a waiting list backed by 10 people before the first lecture, a few days after the lecture I checked and the waiting list was gone.  Wonder if it's a good class >>''.  Now I have classes everyday, before I had Fridays off.  My Fridays now consist of 2 tutorials - I can probably try to talk my way into another tutorial session with a different day.  I don't want purposely go to school for a two 1hr sessions of bullshit since I pay for my own transportation to school.  Having classes on Fridays would mean I'd need to by a $90 bus pass, where as without that one extra day of the week it'd be cheaper to buy tokens seperately.  I'm gonna try to go to the gym every Tuesdays and Thursdays!  I just hope my student card swipes properly cause the barcode area got scratched ._. I think getting replacement cards cost $25 :<

Love Thy Neighbour (short ABDL story)

Jan. 10th, 2009   11:01 PM

I feel good.  I finished the short story I mentioned earlier (the fourth idea from the Productive...maybe post) a couple of hours ago.  It's by far the shortest story (even for a short story) I've ever written.  It's only 2 and 4/5 MS Word pages.  All my other short stories have been five pages or more.  But oh well, I feel good about this one and I don't want to drown it with descriptions (I know I'm capable of it).  I've sent it to Elizabeth, WingZ, and Teekabell for some help with editing and constructive criticisms.  WingZ and Elizabeth are my two favourite ABDL authors from what I've read, and Teekabell gives really good feedback when replying to other peoples stories (he's written a few stories as well).  I don't have anything to lose, really.  I'm in no real rush to post my story and I'd rather have it refined prior to posting (time and interest permitting to those I've sent it out to, of course).  Here's a sneak peak, but there's a good chance some details might get edited. 

 

Love Thy Neighbour

 You’re crying. You always do whenever you want attention and you’ve certainly got mine. Laying down my book I sit up in bed and concentrate on your voice, the soft whimpering builds to a loud bawl. I close my eyes and imagine how you must be feeling; cranky, uncomfortable, and maybe a bit winded since no one has attended to you yet. Your door latch makes a clicking sound followed by the release of the door handle. Daddy’s here now, be a good girl, no more tears. You immediately simmer down, he and I know those tears were just for show. You’re probably smiling too. The giggling is convincing, after all who can resist a tickling after a nap. I can hear a bit of movement from one side of the room to the other, followed by some mumbling and more giggling. I bet your diaper must be feeling pretty icky by now, good thing Daddy is already on it. He’s quick because not long after the sound of movement becomes distant. The absence of crinkling as you exit your room suggests you’re back in Pull-Ups. I smirk to myself while prying away from the wall; one ear is red and the back of it is slightly numb. My eyes remain closed while I replay what I’ve imagined. It isn’t long before I’m squeezing the groin area of my jeans. All too quickly though my excitement goes away. 

 I hear a crashing noise outside my bedroom door. I run to help my Dad to his feet. He’s heavy but I’m taller than him now, so it’s not hard. Dad groans and rubs his elbow. He manages to look at me. “Benjamin,” he says.

 “What?”

 “The store…Bud. I need a case of Bud.”

 “What happened to the case you bought this morning?”

 “Just go dammit. You’ve been in your room all day. You could use some exercise.”

 “Ok. Ok.”

 “Chips. Get chips too. And milk. Hurry up, the game starts soon.”

 I move towards my room, away from his foul stench. I feel blood rushing to my head. My eyes swell up a bit. I’m holding my breath. I told him to let me go instead, but I never thought it would save him.  He told me I wasn’t old enough. I wished I could have done more for him as he had for me. A year later his pictures are kept face down. Right next to the face down pictures of mom. The one on Dad’s bedside table too, surrounded by empty beer cans.

 “Ben.” He’s walking away as I gasp and turn around; thankful he isn’t looking at me. “Use your brother’s ID, he won’t be needing it.”

 I step outside the townhouse and look towards my neighbours. I wonder what kind of mischief you’re up to or how you’re enjoying the afternoon with your Daddy. Imagining the things you could be doing brings a smile to my face as I walk the two blocks to the store. The liquor store is quiet. The cashier’s name is Jully; she eyes me as I near.

 “Another case of Budweiser, Kyle?” I nod.

 “I bet you’re partying everyday before summer is over.” I smile. She scans my beer as she talks, no longer asking for ID. But I bring it just in case. I load the Bud into my backpack as I leave the store. “See you tomorrow,” she tells me.

 I make a quick stop at the convenience store to pick up the chips and milk before hurrying home. 

 “It’s about time. A second more and I would’ve come looking for you.” Dad helps me out of the backpack. No you wouldn’t. “Ah, good man,” he says as he unpacks the beer and chips. He lumbers off to the living room, leaving me alone with the milk. The sound of a whistle blowing and fans shouting fills the empty space. 

 I put the milk in the fridge before sitting at the table with a glass of water. A breeze enters through the screen door coming from the backyard. The clinking and pinging from utensils arouses my interest. As I approach the screen door I notice the noise is coming from next door; made possible because your door is open as well. Is Daddy feeding you a snack or are you helping him prepare dinner? My imagination feeds on the ruckus you two are making. Your highchair scraps along the tile floor. Daddy brings you closer, he and I both know the level of messiness you’re capable of making. He feeds you spoonfuls of rice pudding. Trying his best to imitate trains and airplanes, you giggle at the silly noises he makes. Soon it’s all gone. I smile at how content you must be feeling, there is nothing more precious than moments like these; if only it were me.

 “For Christ sake!” 

 I turn to my Dad. He is still curved into his armchair bathed in the pale light from the television. The square lines of his face are smoothed by a frame of shadow. One hand is in the mouth of the chip bag while the other holds the can of beer to his mouth. He keeps squirming restlessly. He looks at me, than back at the game, crushing the now empty can and reaching for another. 

 It seemed like it was always this way. Dad was a wreck even before Mom died. When he got laid off we all mourned. It was hard on him no longer being the provider for the family. He found his support in aluminum cans and unemployment compensations. Than Mom caught pneumonia from the hospital she worked at, we grieved when she didn’t get better. My brother and I were most affected while Dad grew more and more distant. Although we were only three years apart, Kyle filled the misplaced gaps – he was more of a father to me than our Dad. He had so much to live for.  And then he died in a car collision en route to the liquor store. Dad and I were all that was left. The house I grew up in became colder, quieter, and eventually someone else’s. We downsized and moved out of the suburbs and into a townhouse at the heart of the city. We live off TV dinners and cry over spilt beer. 

 I retreat to my room. Escape seems like a paradox sometimes when I’m disconnected from the internet, my friends, and having little to no money in an everlasting summer. But escape is also only as far as the distance between my room and the neighbours. I wonder what you’re up to tonight. I rest my head on the wall while sitting in bed. It sounds as though you’re having a one sided conversation. You’re on the telephone perhaps. No, I can do better than that. 

 I reposition myself this time with my eyes shut. You’re having a tea party. Elmo, Barney, and that red teddy bear you received on Valentines surround the makeshift table. Daddy has allowed you to have a bottle and some cookies before bed, you serve them around generously. You giggle as you talk for your guests, giving each their own accent. As the night carries on you become quieter and quieter. Daddy and I both know you’re getting tired. He checks in on you and makes sure your Pull-Up is holding up okay. It’s dry but you fuss, not because he doesn’t believe you but because you don’t want to go to sleep. There is a bit of commotion, I hear you stomping out your room. The pipes between our walls rattle and then they calm to a constant hum. I know when Daddy gives you a bath since the humming continues until your tub is full. Instead you’re brushing your teeth this time. As you finish I can hear Daddy taking you back into your room. While he changes you into your night diapers you make one final effort to having your bedtime extended. Your whining tells me those puppy dog eyes have lost their charm. He tucks you in and puts a pacifier in your mouth. Reading a bed time story then planting a kiss on your forehead, he turns on your nightlight and flickers off the lights. Leaving you undisturbed, we wish you sweet dreams.

 It is my turn to get ready for bed. While doing so I try not to think about how little I know you and how possible I could be wrong about who you are. I tell myself that you aren’t mentally challenged or being abused, nor am I being paranoid. I allow myself to believe that you are living out my fantasies. Even though I’ve only caught glimpses of you, it’s enough to let my imagination become my escape.  And then I dream in to the next day.



[Edit: I've gotten a reply back from WingZ, he's pointed something I'm looking in to now.][Edited Jan 11, 2009.  13:15]
[Edit #2: change two words, added two sentence, took out a few sentences from the fourth body paragraph, and voila!  final product.  I've posted it on the ABDL Story Forum, DiaperSpace, and Aby.com.][Edited Jan 11, 2009.  10:53]

Comic book and movie status...

Jan. 10th, 2009   12:08 AM
I read Craig Thompson's Blankets for a second time today.  The first time I read it was about a year or two ago.  Being so forgetful and all I picked up on some things I missed, mainly how religious base the graphic novel is.  I love how the whole questioning processes which leads to a gradual distancing of faith.  I got sad when religion got in the way of his love life - must've been tough to be so dedicated to religion than end up kicking yourself upon seeing it from another perpsective.  I really want to read Alison Bechdel's Fun Home graphic novel (she calls it a graphic memoir).  It's sorta similar to Craig's book - the whole coming of age theme - but I think the content is a bit heavier (like sexuality and orientation, suicide, gender role, etc).  It would be nice to compare the two since the two authors are of differing gender which surely affected their works/lives.  I can't find her book to download online so I'm considering buying it.  Speaking of buying, Flight Vol. 2, the collection of comics I bought myself before the new year, I am half way through it.  So far my favourite comics are 'Solomon Fix' by Doug TenNapel and 'The Orange Grove' by Kazu Kibuishi.  Solomon Fix is just jokes - the plot is straightforward but the drawings are silly and the dialogue is tries to sound sophisticated (all in all a wacky combination).  The Orange Grove is fun and imaginative, but also sad. I finished Daredevil: Born Again by Frank Miller not to long ago.  Even though I read the reprinted version (it was originally published in 1986 spanning from issues #227-233), I really liked how they always introduce the characters (with one or two text boxes) in every issue.  It's like, even if you don't follow the series atleast you know who is who and what they do.  I don't really know if that's something they do in comic books since I haven't read enough series, but it's a nice set up - especially for forgetful people like me who might forget certain characters if there is a huge time gap between issues.  The story is really well written.  It describes the shit The Kingpin throws at Matt Murdock, causing him to go crazy.  Made me feel pretty sorry for the dude - who picks on blind poeple, man?  Seriously!  Anyways, definetly worth checking out, it's timeless. I don't know what to read next.  I know I said I wanted to read Sandman, but it's 75 issues long.  I haven't picked up Runaways from where I left off a year ago (somewhere in the middle of Vol 2).  I downloaded Batman - The Dark Knight Stricks Again (the follow up of Batman - The Dark Knight).  Loved the first one, probably gonna like the second.  It's about 200 pages long.  I also downloaded Ex Machina, written by Brian K. Vaughan (same dude who did Y: The Last Man and Runaways [Vol 1 and 2]).  He also did the first 20 issues of Swamp Thing Vol 3 which I'd also like to eventually read.  Ex Machina is only 33 issues so I can wizz through that one pretty quickly.  Probably not going to get around to my Alex Ross collection, and Green Lantern collection.  Lastly, I'll sneak some issues of Franklin Richards between series, I'm about half way though the 14 issues I have from his title.  In terms of movies, I watched 'The Year of Getting to Know Us' (2008), 'How to Lose Friends & Alienate People' (2008), and 'Bolt' (2008).  I don't have classes on Fridays so i was pretty bored, as you might imagine.  The Year of Getting to Know Us is pretty much a movie about a dysfunctional family told in flashbacks.  It was 'meh' in my books.  Nothing to special nor memorable, didn't get all that many chuckles out of it either.  Lucy Liu is in it, haven't seen a movie with her in it for a while (although I did fast forward to the fighting scenes in Kill Bill 1+2 since I burned it on the same disk as Blankets).  How to Lose Friends & Alienate People was funny but cliche so it's another 'meh' in my books.  It's one of those flicks where the main character goes from rags to riches, than goes back to rags at the end for love.  Worst part was the speed.  They put alot of time and effort in to showing how hard his life was (due to his own stupidity), but while climbing the corporate ladder he just wizzes through it.  I realize the audience is suppose to see some sort of time progression, but it wasn't done well.  Simon Pigg did a pretty good action job though. Bolt...oh Bolt.  So heart warming and fuzzy, yet remains something I would never have gone to the theaters to pay and watch.  I don't normally watch animated films, but the line between kids movie and 'mature' movie are getting pretty blurry.  Like Wall-E or Finding Nemo...those are considered kids movies, but the content requires a bit more maturity to fully appreciate.  But I don't think Bolt had any deep content in it, just some good humour.  It was like watching The Truman Show, but animated and with talking animals.  The difference being the deep content since one could really feel for a human being, being subjected to an artificial reality.  Whatever, getting to complicated for my own good.  Bottomline, cute movie, great cast of voice actors, predicatable (duh), but worth a watching if your bored and need a last resort movie.  (I probably enjoyed it more than I'm letting on since I have a desire to relive a small part of my childhood, it's just tucked somewhere far at the back of my mind.)
😊 pleased  |  ♫ Biirdie - Petals

The end of 'Y: The Last Man'

Jan. 7th, 2009   4:38 AM
Damn, the mixed reviews were certainly that (see my previous entry, second paragraph).  It is 4:38am right now and I've just finished issue #60, the last issue, of Y: The Last Man.  Overall the series is really good - the plot is amazingly well thought out.  But, issue #58 is what really turned me off.  At the end of #58 you know the series is gonna do a full 360 - everything changes...abruptly!  It was pretty disappointing, in my opinion, but maybe only because I really liked the character that dies.  It's like, I'm disappointed but I don't want to feel ripped off for reading 58 issues and not continue to the end.  Pretty much the remaining issues weren't exciting anymore and I was just reading them to get it over with.  Last issue wrapped up the series okay-ly (very fitting with how the series was progressing).  If I wasn't affected by issue 58 I'd say the ending in 60 was great. Anywhoo, I think I'm gonna read some Franklin Richards before starting either Sandman or Daredevil.  I downloaded two more bundles of comics.  One is a 7.91 Gb collection of Green Lantern comics.  The other is a 1.30 Gb collection of artist Alex Ross's stuff (he's the artist who did Kingdom Come).  I don't know when I'll start either of those but I definetly want some funnies in me before starting another series.
💤 sleepy  |  ♫ Talib Kweli (ft. Common, Consequence, Kanye West) - Wack Niggas
Link

Productive...maybe.

Jan. 5th, 2009   11:27 PM
