The first day of my winter semester began today – it was uneventful. It’s common knowledge that the first week is the least important week. The professors usually just go over the syllabus in detail, introduce the TAs, and answer any preliminary questions students might have. Well, the first class I went to was a 9am class – meaning I had to be out the door and at the bus stop by around 8am, meaning I woke up at around 7am. Not a happy camper at that time of day, plus it was pretty chilly outside – I’m not a big fan of winter and waking up early. So my 9am class, Feminism and Anthropology, was cancelled just as everyone was settling in. Some faculty member came in to the lecture hall ten after nine and said something about the prof being in the hospital or what not. I don’t have much sympathy for her, and I don’t mean to be a prick but a little heads up earlier would’ve been nice – could have even slept in till eleven. 

 So, I had four hours to kill until my next class. I went to the library, since it’s the only place on campus with a sufficient number of electrical outlets making them less difficult to fight over, and read Y: The Last Man on my laptop.  I got through issues 1 to 30, that’s half the entire series. The plot is really good – it kind of reminds me of a tv show (one I can’t recall), cause each issue ends with a little hook or ends with a short story arc leading to the next. The characters are likeable since they develop at a good pace. The dialogue makes a lot of tv/movie references for some easy laughs – some that I’m not entirely familiar with. I’ve read mix reactions with the ending, something about an abrupt climax or ending or something. I don’t want to think about it just yet since I’m in the middle of a good story arc. I think I’ll finish this comic book before moving on to either Sandman or Daredevil. 

 I bought a sub at subway, the line up was ridiculous. It seemed like a ton of people showed up on the first day of classes – which seems odd to me since the crowds spread or even out later into the semester, based on previous years. It might have something to do with people dropping out of classes before the last drop date – I’ll have to find out when it is since I only intend to take five courses instead of the six I’m enrolled in. I had the same sub I always get, a cold cut on wheat with everything and ranch dressing. I should really try something else besides the three specials that cost $5 for a foot long. But then again, I’m always tight on cash and I don’t complain all that much about what I put in to my mouth. It’s a bit strange though since as a kid, maybe in grade 5 till the middle of highschool I always at a homemade sandwich for lunch. I usually got sick of eating sandwiches and took breaks by buying cafeteria food – but now I prefer eating sandwiches. I don’t know if there are any technical differences between a sub and a sandwich besides the bread since the toppings are the same, but I can’t understand what I’m not sick of them. 

 Well anyways, my 1pm class was pretty straightforward. Prof talked about the same old stuff and dismissed us an hour early. Funny enough there was this girl in my class that I swear to gawd, I’ve shared at least one class with EVERY semester, in the class I was in. I’ll call her Emy. I guess I shouldn’t be too too surprised since we’re both in anthropology, but I’m just amazed that every single semester since first year, I’ve had at least one class with her – if not more. I never talked to her until last summer. I took two courses last summer – both of which weren’t even anthropology courses – and she was in one of them. In that class we were also in the same tutorial, which had only a dozen people in it. So I got to know her a little bit during that time. I looked through her timetable and she’s in another of my classes, ‘Gifts, Money, and Mortality’, which begins tomorrow.

 I had one last class after my 1-3pm class, its ‘Foundations of Epidemiology’. The prof is a real nut job, I been in at least three or four of his other classes. The thing with him is that he’s really really nice and flexible. I remember in first year he would encourage people to participate with money – those who raised their hand and spoke he’d give them his pocket change (till he ran out of course and not for every week). Another time he did the same sort of encouragement, instead the first person to talk would receive a copy of a required textbook (textbooks that students had to buy at the bookstore – usually runs for $30 and up). I like him to an extent though. He tries to hard with jokes, sometimes they’re funny and sometimes they’re pitiful and recycled. Another thing I don’t quite like about him are the things he teaches. Every single course I’ve been with him he always discusses about malaria, yellow fever, cholera, and influenza. He talks about them constantly like it’s the most important things in the world – probably has something to do with his research. He’s a workaholic who’s dedicated to his students though. I remember in first or second year one of the intro courses he taught was in really really high demand. The class was overwhelmingly full, even with 2 lectures and an online section. I mean, I’ve seen this guy at his best, looking healthy and pacing around the lecture hall, and I’ve seen him at his lowest where you can tell he’s exhausted and sluggish. Prof Larry Sawchuk. If you ever get the chance, look him up, enrol in one of his classes, and you won’t be disappointed. 

 I ran in to a couple of friends today. The Sawchuk class I’m taking Jully is in as well – though I don’t think our tutorials are on the same days, I’ll try to switch mine to hers. Some dude I met in ANTC47 (Osteology) last semester, Arvin, I ran into again. He’s a part-time cleaner for the campus – I might look into the job since it has extremely flexible hours. I’ll also be seeing him on Wednesday since we’re both taking ANTC48 (Advance Osteology). While in the library reading comic books I ran into Darcy. I met her through my friend Lucy, who I met in first year through Jully – its all about connections and networking. Darcy use to be VP of a students association – she convinced me to be the Anthropology Representative, an executive position, last year. The students association (more of a club really), was ok, but I was the type who wanted to be as far away from responsibilities as possible, so I quit after she left. The only good thing about the club that I miss is that we had our own office. Goodbye quiet studying area with electrical plugs and comfortable sofa.

 So I’ve been thinking about story ideas and I’m seriously considering taking a stab at one of my ideas. I’ve cooked up a couple (ones not mentioned in the blog section of my old story archive site) but the one I thought of today seems most promising and doable. I’ll go over my other story ideas before getting to the big one.

 1)  This one will be a chapter based story. I liked certain elements of the movie A Scanner Darkly. The whole undercover drug investigators really interested me. I also liked the drug (I forget what it’s called) that makes the user remember old/good memories from the anime series Code Geasse. So I wanted to mingle the two together. Like, a drug that makes people regress in the mind back to the good’ol days. Why do people do it? Because they live in Mandinare (my fictitious city from other stories I’ve written), a morally bankrupt metropolis. Mandinare is sorta my version of Gotham, it’s dark, crime ridden, and rains its way into the next day. So the premise would involve an undercover investigator working on a case that involves his friends (or something) who use the mind regressing drug. If you OD on it, than you get stuck in the regressive state. And OD people get sent to a special facility that’s all Area 51 – but in actuality the facility is like a nursery (or something?). I don’t know how to further the plot, or what the plot even is beyond what I have. But I’d want the undercover investigator to be a male – so I can throw in a girlfriend user if needed heh. A detective story sounds tempting – but then that’d require a lot of preplanning, something I have trouble doing. One quote I’d really like to use for this story would be “I’m asking Johnny Walker for advise…” (or Remy Martin or Jack Daniels). It just seems to set a certain mood and describes an alcoholic pretty well

 2)  Second story idea would either be a really short chapter based story, or a short story. More keen on the later since I think I enjoy writing short stories better – more immediate gratification and less planning for the future. So, this story would be about a scrawny kid who gets bullied. One time he’s getting his head flushed in the toilet bowl (do they even do that now a days? Kids would probably just steal your iPod or PSP or something…so I could make this story based on the 80s or something). While the bullies are leaving they throw his glasses into the trash can. He goes to retrieve it and finds a used diaper. Now, this is where I donno where to take it. The diaper can either belong to a) a jock, b) a bully, or 3) a nerd. Donno which to pick, don’t want to think about it just yet. 

 3)  I cooked up this idea yesterday, it is way too easy. One of the comic strips from Franklin Richards inspired this…and I can’t believe no one has done it yet, or I haven’t read of it yet. Show and Tell! Like come on! Who’s childhood isn’t complete without show and tell. So, the main character (male or female) would bring in an onsie, or a pacifier, or a diaper, or a bonnet or something that screams baby. And the main character would describe it as if it belonged to his/her sibling. Maybe through in a little sidestory, or reactions from the students and teacher. But what I really wanted to do was represent the ABDL scene through an outsiders view – or someone indirectly affected. The thing with most stories is that it is the main character that gets screwed – put back in to diapers, treated like a baby, etc. Rarely is the main character a SIDE character from the actual story. Elizabeth wrote a story loosely based on my idea called The Death and Rebirth of Aeris Delaroca – where the main character helps everyone but herself get put into diapers when she wanted to be in them from the very beginning. With leads me to my last story idea, the big one…

 4)  Again, I want to work with the idea that the main character is a side character from the bigger picture – the one not being involved with ABDL stories. I got this idea somewhat from the movie Let the Right One In, and from the house I live in. I live in a townhouse and the walls aren’t exactly soundproof. I can hear my neighbours from either side if I put my ear to the wall and really listened. So I wanted the perspective to be from a teenage boy who lives with his alcoholic father. Their mother and his older brother both died. The father makes his son buy his beer for him (quote “I’m underage dad”/”Go use your brothers ID, its not like he’ll be needing it.”). The son looks after the dad and does everything he’s told to do – maybe he looks up to him? Maybe papa use to be a boxer or something. The neighbours are what’s interesting. They are the main characters ‘escape’ (as in escapism). He can hear through the walls and the things he hears he reinterprets and re-imagines them in an ABDL context. Whether what’s going on next door is really ABDL stuff is completely a mystery to him (and to the readers?). I really want to focus on HIS imagination, HIS interpretations. The neighbours consist of one father and one teenage daughter (maybe 15 years old). The things the main character hear would be like the daughter crying. Or footsteps indicating running and crinkling. Or maybe he sees parts of the daughter and father outside in their backyard – but not fully cause of trees or something. Essentially I want his idealized fantasies of what he hears on paper, as the story. Maybe his neighbours consist of an abusive father or a crybaby daughter. I think this short story has a lot of potential if I put my mind to it (like with everything I could have potentially ‘put my mind to’). I want this story to be deep – like exploring a humans psyche deep. I also want it to be a little bit dark (like the main character has ‘sickening’ interpretations of hardcore ABDL stuff). But I also want it to be plausible and ‘it could be true’ sorta story telling. I’m gonna sleep on this story and hopefully cook up some more ideas – I’m really looking forward.

🌀 giddy  |  ♫ Lykke Li - I'm Good, I'm Gone
Link

Superheroes and comic books...

Jan. 4th, 2009   11:55 PM

As a kid I’ve always loved superheroes – or action type cartoons. My childhood memories are often paired with The Ghostbusters, The Teenage Mutant Ninja Turtles, Samurai Pizza Cats, Biker Mice from Mars, Power Rangers, Dragon Ball and various other 90s children cartoon shows. It’s a bit amazing when I step back and look at how much violence I watched as entertainment – or how much I would play out violence with action figures and other sorts of franchise merchandise. But I guess there were lessons learned and morals taught along the way even if they may seem cheesy now. 

 One fond memory I have was getting off from school and going to my babysitters house (along with my brother and another pair of siblings that lived in the neighbourhood). We would spend a majority of the time sitting in front of the tube eating popsicles or grilled cheese. Batman: The Animated Series was one show we’d always watch – the series ran from 1992 to 1995. I think a majority of my Batman knowledge came from that show, whether it was accurate or not. When I compare the current Batman series to the one I watched I don’t feel any nostalgia as the current version seems to lack authenticity to me. Another fond memory I have was waking up early on Saturday mornings with my brother. We would always crawl down the stairs to the living room to minimize the squeaking from the floorboards. I remember we’d always watch X-Men (the 1992 series) and Spider-Man (the 1994 series). My brother loved Spider-Man, I didn’t as much but some of enthusiasm rubbed off on me. I vaguely remember watching Super Mario and Link during the Saturday morning line up. There were other superhero type series that I watched as a kid, but none as memorable as the two I mentioned; these include The Tick, Gargoyles, Mummies Alive!, He-Man, Captain Planet, and Transformers (I’m probably forgetting a few). Other cartoon shows I’d watch were Duck Tales, Animaniacs, Inspector Gadget, Rugrats, Doug, Reboot, Dextar’s Laboratory, Simpsons and a whole bunch of other Cartoon Network and Nickelodeon shows.

 This rambling of superheroes and cartoons kinda came back to me when I dug up some old comic books I have. I don’t really know how I would classify my childhood and comic books. I wasn’t a serious reader or collector but I was fascinated by them. And yet, even though I wasn’t serious about Western comic books I was really into Lao Fu Zi (aka Old Master Q) a Chinese comic. Lao Fu Zi was first published in newspapers and magazines in 1962 and was serialized in 1964. Every now and then my mom would buy me a volume when we went to a Chinese mall. The series is comedy in nature, follow a 3 to 6 panel strip, and hardly use words to – which was probably why I loved it since I can’t read Chinese. I really wish I kept the volumes I had, I can’t remember when I through them away but I had stacks of them. I remember reading Dragon Ball and DBZ Chinese mangas as well. When I lived in Richmond Hill there was a plaza with a Chinese bookstore and several restaurants on Hwy 7. Whenever we ate at that plaza I would borrow a book from there – the store did book rentals. I remember borrowing a couple volumes of IQ 博士 from a family friend, but never really got into the series that much. 

 The Western comic books I have are mostly from Marvel – things like X-Men, Spider-Man and Iron Man. It seems a bit odd when I look at them now, it makes me wonder why I have random issues when I never even followed the series. I wouldn’t have understood what was going on in the series, so why had I bothered buying them? Must have been something of a childhood fascination – the impulsive wants that disregard the needs in life. I remember as a teen I loved Green Lantern, I feel in love after buying and reading Green Lantern Secret Files & Origins #1 (1998). It pretty much summarized how each of Earths Green Lanterns got their rings and the crossovers between the characters. Speaking of crossovers, I remember when the DC versus Marvel (1996) 4-issue limited series was published. Even though I didn’t know the history of the many characters, I recognized most – and it was really cool watching them clash. I own the second issue while my brother had the third – it wasn’t to hard guessing what happened in one and four. My brother got in to Spawn around that time but I never did. I don’t recall buying any comics from Planet X, mostly just cards. But I had this one friend, John, who I knew, and was friends with Stephanie, that bought a lot of comic books during highschool. I remember one time me and Steph were hanging around his locker – while he was opening it I caught a glimpse of his combination. Later Steph and I went into his locker and borrowed a few of his comic books. He had quite the collection in his locker, and I’m sure a lot more at home, but the ones I remember borrowing were The Fantastic Four, X-Men, and Exiles. I don’t know if John ever found out, then again he never talked about his comic books – it was a bit of an eye opener and my fascination began to spark again. 

 Between high school and university, I remember watching the animated series Justice League (2001 to 2004) and Justice League Unlimited (2004 to 2006). Those two shows really opened up the DC universe to me. I may be incorrect (as my superhero knowledge isn’t that extensive and I’m to lazy to do research) but as a kid Marvel always appeared to be more mainstream. Maybe I didn’t pay enough attention to DC back then. Marvel also seemed to have more team orientated superheroes, most notably X-Men, Avengers, Fantastic Four. Yes, I know there are a ton of non-team heroes like Daredevil, Hulk, Spider-Man, etc – but it just seems like I grew up with Marvel and its teams. DC always seemed to have more individual heroes like Wonder Woman, Superman, Batman, Flash, etc. So watching them all jumbled into a team was simply awesome. What’s more, the two series really dives in to the histories of individual heroes – most episodes are based on story arcs from actual comics. My favorite DC heroes, based on the shows, would be Green Lantern, Batman, and the Flash. Teen Titans, another DC superteam, was another animated series I watched; it ran from 2003 to 2006. It followed a less serious tone than Justice League, but was just as entertaining to watch. Other tv series that I followed that aren’t superhero per say are: Avatar: the Last Airbender, Gundam Wing and Gundam SEED, Dragon Ball/Z/GT, Naruto, and a bit of Bleach. To be honest, I don’t remember the last time I watched any of these shows on tv, I streamed and downloaded all these series.

 In university I didn’t follow any comic book series, even though I had a passion for superheroes – I guess I didn’t really know where to begin or was too lazy to play catch-up. I remember one series that was recently published at the time, that I followed. I found out about it from Elizabeth the ABDL author (she’s a big comic book buff). Runaways, debuted in April 2003 and still running, is a monthly series published by Marvel. It may be because it was the first comic book series I followed and had nothing to really compare it to, but I really liked it. It’s in its third volume right now, but sadly I stopped reading it midway into the second volume for whatever reason. I’ve started reading it again and now I’m catching up. It’s pretty funny cause Runaways made me interested in another series that I followed to the end. It was the Marvel crossover Civil War which ran from 2006 to 2007. The crossover wasn’t exclusively a limited series – as in having its own title – the Civil War storyline mingled (mingle = the point of crossovers) with other long standing series continuum (like Spider-Man, Iron Man, Captain America, X-Men, Fantastic Four, Avengers, etc). Every so often I would download a torrent that sorted out issues from series that followed the Civil War storyline. Runaways was actually a series that got involved with the Civil War storyline – it was sort of a joint storyline between them and Young Avengers. It was issue number 26 out of the 111 from the Civil War storyline that featured Runaways. So I caught up and continued to the end of the crossover – it was wicked even though some of the characters individual background stories leading up to the crossover I was unfamiliar with. 

 It wasn’t until maybe a week or two ago I began to really get into comic books and graphic novels. I donno if it’s just the flavor of the month for me (something that will come and go), or something that I’ll become addicted to (What’s next after MMOs, comics?) – but I’m thoroughly enjoying what I’ve read so far. I downloaded The Watchmen sometime ago when I first heard about it coming to the big screens in March. I didn’t start reading it until recently; it’s got an incredible plot and I’m really looking forward to watching the movie. I totally think it is worth all the hype and I recommend the graphic novel to anyone. I read The Umbrella Academy – The Apocalypse Suite. It’s a six issue series written by Gerard Way (lead singer of My Chemical Romance – I had no idea till after reading) and published in Sept 2007 and still running. The current series is called The Umbrella Academy – Dallas, and I’ve only read the first out of the two issues that are out right now. The cover art is a bit abstract at times, but the storyline is really easy to follow – its got a good blend of comedy, drama, and action. Again, I recommend this comic book series to anyone. I picked up Mouse Guard while looking for pictures of anthropomorphic mice cause I wanted to make a mouse avatar in Second Life. Mouse Guard is an Eisner Award winner so you don’t need me to tell you it’s good. I’ve read Mouse Guard: Fall 1152, which is the first series, and the art is very beautiful. The story is like something out of Lord of the Rings. I read three issues of Mouse Guard: Winter 1152 though I believe there are suppose to be six issues in total – the series is still running. I recommend reading it and not just for the characters cause they’re cute and fuzzy to look at.

 I read Kingdom Come yesterday – a four-issue mini-series by Mark Waid and Alex Ross published in 1996. They art is really smooth, if I had to describe it somehow – not the traditional comic book style. The storyline is okay – its essentially about the old DC heroes becoming replaced with new heroes (ones that are more violent), and the reactions it stirs. I didn’t really like the ending, it wasn’t as epic as it lead up to be in my opinion. Today I finished Batman – The Dark Knight Returns by Frank Miller and published in 1986. I gotta say, it is damn good and worth all the praise its received. It’s dark and deeply human, like you can feel some of the struggles going on. The thing I like about Batman comics is that it isn’t always about the action, it’s the story that’s golden – and this comic book really makes it shine through. My head sorta ached though, took me a majority of the day to finish the 200 page comic (it is packed with writing I kid you not). I also read the first volume of Franklin Richards (it’s more of a one-shot series since each strip doesn’t continue into the next). Franklin Richards is the son of Mr. Fantastic and the Invisible Woman from the Fantastic Four. The series essentially parodies him in, literally, a Calvin and Hobbes sorta way. Franklin is portrayed as Calvin and H.E.R.B.I.E (his cute tiny nanny robot) resembles Hobbes. Each strip pretty much explores what happens when Franklin plays with one of his dad’s inventions (Mr. Fantastic is a genius and brilliant inventor) – often resulting in trouble. The volume I read is the first one, Franklin Richards – Son of a Genius published in 2005, and I have 11 other volumes I intend to finish. Definitely worth reading for the LOL’s.

 I also intend to read Sandman (the entire 75 issue series), Y – The Last Man (the entire 60 issue series), and Daredevil: Born Again (a story arc [issues 227-233] by Frank Miller that got collected in a trade paperback). I’ve downloaded all three titles – I just donno how I’ll get to them all with school starting tomorrow. So far I’m enrolled into six courses, but I haven’t paid for one and I’m thinking of dropping one. I don’t know which I’ll drop, so depending on how my first or second week goes I’ll decide after – but I will definitely drop a course. I have three classes tomorrow, one of which is a 9am class – I hate waking up early and it usually takes an hour to commute to school. Tuesday through Thursday I only have one class each, all are after 12 noon. My courses are (all of which are in the anthropology discipline): ‘Feminism and Anthropology’, ‘Gifts, Money and Mortality’, Ritual and Religious Action’, ‘Advance Topics in Human Osteology’, Foundations in Epidemiology’, and ‘Frontiers of Anthropology’. Hopefully I’ll wake up on time – or not fall asleep in class since the first week is the least informative week – and hopefully I’ll see some old faces, and make some new ones. 

[Edit - I completely left out something (me and my forgetfulness again).  I read Craig Tompson's Blankets (2003) some time ago.  From what I remember it's got a really good storyline (coming of age), and its deep but beautiful.  It sparked an interest for graphic novels in me - whenever I'd go into a Chapters or Indigo I'd look for the graphic novels cause of it.  I often read graphic novels like Flight, published by Villard.  Flight is in its fifth volume (which came out Sept 2008), and just last week I bought the second volume.  Costed me almost $35 (damn graphic novels are pricey), but I couldn't find any downloads for it so its worth it.  There are three other graphic novels I want to read.  American Born Chinese by Gene Yang (2006), To the Heart of the Storm by Will Eisner (2000), and One Hundred Demons by Lynda Barry (2002).  All of which, to my knowledge, are about coming of age and cultural stereotypes and prejudice.  Aside from those, I also want to read the DC crossover 'Identity Crisis' - I downloaded it a long time ago, so I'll have to dig around for it.  There are a couple of mangas I'd like to read that I've downloaded some time ago too; like Bitter Virgin, Cherry Juice, Telepathic Wanderers, and Domu: a child's dream.][Edited Jan 5, 2009. 00:18]
☮️ peaceful  |  ♫ Rihanna - Unfaithful
Link

It was written

Jan. 2nd, 2009   8:18 PM

Did you know I use to write stories on an ABDL story forum under the name ‘toucher_terre’? Probably not. I’ve been blogging about my experiences in the ABDL scene a little more than I imagined I would – since the intended purpose of this blog was to record things that have happened throughout my days. But, this blog is also meant to share some of my thoughts in ways that help cope with these day to day happenings and the my experiences among the ABDL community have had profound – probably an understatement – impact on the way I think about things. And its mostly an on and off thinking but lately I’ve really been thinking about these sorts of things. It isn’t the most attractive thing to talk about, since it’s a rather personal and touchy subject – and often I can imagine it being a turn off for some people – but I feel good about writing about it; like my conscious becomes a little more organized and clean. 

I want to write about my experiences during the time I was an author. Reminisces came from my website where I archived my stories. I check that website quite infrequently, but several days ago I checked it and was surprised to find some posts in the little chat box to the left of the home page. Three spammers (I can tell from the URL they linked their names with) posted well wishes for the Christmas and New Year’s holidays. I just thought it was bizarre for them to do that since I can’t imagine anyone even ever visiting my website to begin with – let alone clicking on their URL. I would also like to take the time to thank Orms Einbani, the host of thirtyone-ten.net and an ABDL/Furry, for lending me a site to call my own.

ABDL stories, I think, are something that link many ABDLs together in that stories are among one of the first things we discover on the internet (in my case DPF.com and deeker.com). It is one of the things on the internet that makes us say to ourselves. “Wow, I’m not alone. There are other people just like me.” At least for me that is true, and based on various forum threads I’ve read it is also true for others. One of the most classic and most read stories, I think, is Ashley’s Diaper Adventures by RK Sanford, Tiffany’s Diaper Tales by BabyVern, and The Double Life of Ariel Crawford by Megan. I’ve only read the first two, but these three stories – more like novels since they’re extremely long – are among the first stories most ABDLs have ever read. But, that wasn’t what sparked my interest in writing. There is a community of people on a forum called the ‘ABDL Story Forum’ (pretty generic I know), that use to follow. They forum has gone through three websites to my knowledge. The first was on the web host bravenet.com; because of server issues or something the community moved to the web host xsorbit26 (http://xsorbit26.com/users5/abdlstoryforum/index.php). For the most part I was a lurker during the bravenet days, and posted stories during the xsorbit26 period. The current forum is hosted by myfastforum.org (http://abdlstoryforum.myfastforum.org) – for the most part I’ve gone back to lurking. Throughout the three web hosts, and from the top of my head, Victoriah Nichole Little, Elizabeth, WingZ, RSI, PlasmaIncinerator, seventyeyes, Spokane Girl, Lin, Nemo, and many others are regulars. 

I began writing because I wanted recognition and acknowledgment; pretty egotistical I know but it’s the truth. I admit I’m not the greatest author – far from great – but I do have the balls to say that I am better than some people who’ve posted cliché and poorly structured stories. I enjoyed writing but I wasn’t proud of my stories. The thing is, for the longest time ever I’ve always held the belief that my goals were to be remembered. This even goes beyond the story forum but more on that another time. I always created this other person (of the opposite sex because I always believed females garnered more support and fan base) that I pretended to be – and it always worked. But, in time I began to come sick of it. I was lying not only to myself but to other people and to this day there is a deep pit of regret for those I’ve hurt emotionally. It was pathetic of me and I am deeply sorry Nighthawk, Happyfeet Hax, and many others who I cannot remember off the top of my head. 

Throughout my experiences on the internet I have pretended to be many different people (baby_mika, toucher_terre, Isobel Piek, Nodame Hyun, Baylyn Fayray, Kuronekodou, lain, and some others), at times I have forgotten who’s screen name I’ve coupled with the pretend real name, or which screen name is for which email address I’ve created. I rarely pretended to be more than one person at a time – often killing off or abandoning one for another when times got sticky. I chose the screen name ‘toucher_terre’ because in French it meant ‘touch’ and ‘earth’ – and the character I created would have a down to earth personality. Her name was Melissa Ito, 20 years of age, and she’s from Vancouver. I have written the story De Facto on between March 25-28, 2006 confessing the false identity I had created, among others, and it can be found on my archive site or the xsorbit26 forum. But to begin, under the screen name toucher_terre my first story I had ever written was called The Unexpected. On my story website it is under Long Stories titled ‘Terre: a three volume story’, to the left of the page. Looking back and skimming through The Unexpected I can say I’m quite amazed at myself. It was 99 pages on Microsoft Word and contained 10 chapters; I began writing it on January 8, 2006 and finished it on the 20th of the same month. In my entire life I have never written so much in such short amount of time, ever. I was fuelled by people who followed my story and the comments they left me with every chapter – it was sort of an addiction. When I think about it, the craving for views and replies was really disturbing and that is why I enjoyed writing the story, but grew to be unproud of it. 

 When I look back at The Unexpected, there are a lot of grammar and sentence structure mistakes. From one chapter to the next it jumped from first to third person. And some parts of the story were somewhat irrelevant and could have been left out. But for the post part I was really really descriptive – even to a point where readers sometimes got turned off. By the end of the story the fan base siphoned – somewhat common based on other stories I’ve seen. In affect I began writing another story to refuel my addiction for praise, recognition, and page hits. Originally I didn’t intend on making Terre a three volume series – though I did end The Unexpected rather abrupt and now that I think about it pretty stupidly. My second story was titled Let Bygones be Bygones. It is 39 pages, 8 chapters, started on January 23, 2006 and finished on February 6th. I was influenced by a tv documentary on the 2008 Beijing Olympics and the children the Chinese government put into training. Again, I really enjoyed writing this story and the reception I received rekindled my fire, but I do feel disgusted when I think about my motives and actions while writing it at the time. I coupled it with my first story near the ending – thus renaming the two stories into one big one. I sometimes reread random chapters from this story because it’s a fun read – its about rediscovery of two sisters in a country away from home.

 I began writing the third volume of the Terre series (The Ties The Bind) on February 11, 2006 but only got so far as one chapter. I had rewritten the first chapter twice but didn’t like either – probably had something to do with mental fatigue from toying with the same characters to long. When I start a story I usually begin with a title rather than a plot. Then I shape the plot to fit the title – which is pretty stupid when I think about it since it becomes limiting to what I can write about, to an extent. Sometimes I pick up quotes from lyrics or tv shows that I find really useful in a story that I haven’t even come up with and I end up jotting it down. I have a document full of quotes waiting to be used – but it seems pretty silly to write a story based off a title, let alone just to be able to use a quote. I’ve never really examined how my stories come to be, but I know that I’m influenced by animes, mangas, songs, television shows, and my own lived experiences. I have this belief that there aren’t many original ideas left to be used, but the things people recycle, mingle together, and write up in their own unique styles for a rehashed version of something that can be called ‘original’. I never liked being cliché, so I suppose my motivations also derived from that aspiration. 

I gave up on The Ties That Bind and haven’t touched it or thought of any ideas for it even up until now. Instead I began write more thought out stories, ones that don’t jump straight to the diapering or babying aspects. Stories that were meant to have a plot that would develop over time. I began writing Quid Pro Quo on February 19, 2006 and it eventually became one of many incomplete stories. Currently the story is 39 pages and four chapters long. The main theme was foresight and Tarot cards (I had a fascination with than, even bought a deck, but pretty quickly lost interest). I think the story has a lot of potential since I left many things open ended when I stopped. I can’t remember why I stopped writing, especially since I received quite a few compliments. I think I would have a hard time picking up where I left off if I were ever to return to writing – something about having to decide which story to continue bothers me since it’s so hard to choose; I like them all. Chapter four was posted on March 10, 2006 and not long after I started writing another story called Six-Seventeen – started on March 15th. I’m really disappointed at myself for writing this story since I completely ripped off the anime series Nanaka 6/17 – some embarrassed and fearful of being exposed of such I posted it under the screen name ‘lain’ instead. I wouldn’t feel nearly as bad if I had written it as a fanfiction, since the anime series can easily be ABDL-ified. 

When I look back I’m a bit surprised as to how far I had come in only three to four months. It wasn’t the writing aspect per say (but I do admit my writing developed exponentially since I was never really in to writing before my first story), but the humanity of the forum. The people I associated with from forum threads to MSN chats really impacted me in a profound way. Things that real. These were real people with real feelings, with real problems, with real concerns, with real identities. But I on the other hand was a person pretending to be a person. In a sense, maybe half of everything I posted or chatted was completely made-up. Sure, opinions may be genuine, but I sculpted them to fit the character that was Melissa Ito. I began to feel something. I cannot remember which I felt first exactly, but both lead me to write my confession story De Facto. One feeling was that I was being cheated upon – I would be remembered not for whom I was, but for who I had created. There was a rush to it when I began pretending, but the excitement only lasts so long until my own ego began to cave. The other feeling I felt was guilt – an enormous weight of guilt for realizing the bad that I was doing. So I’ll say it again – it surprised me. I had created and hated something that felt so real, but so dangerous, in three to four months. 

De Facto was meant to be my last story because I assumed people would dislike me for lying to them and that I’d be to embarrassed to stick around. I intended to post each chapter a few weeks apart so that I could finish my two other stories (Quid Pro Quo and The Ties That Bind), but the reception I received and my own ego made that difficult. The story is 17 pages, three chapters, written between March 25-28, 2006, and largely drew from an old blog story I had posted before I had joined the story forum. Before the story forum I used the screen name ‘baby_mika’ for the DPF forum (another ABDL website) and Xanga (a blogging website). Like toucher_terre, I created an identity (in this case Mika Tsukino) and toyed with people for a year or two. I ended up killing baby_mika instead of confessing what I had done, like toucher_terre. 

The above probably seems either confusing or irrelevant to those who are reading this entry but this story and the reception it received means a lot to me. You are free to read what other people had to say about it (http://xsorbit26.com/users5/abdlstoryforum/index.php?topic=4014.0) and I can only agree for the most part. I was a thoughtless, dishonest, cold hearted person – even if those feelings weren’t being felt during the time I was acting as something I wasn’t. The point is I was inconsiderate. Even though I didn’t get booed out of the forum like I thought I would, things changed. I changed. I don’t know why I stuck with the screen name but I did. I lost friends and hurt some people and I am truly sorry. 

Moving on. I didn’t write or continue any stories for a long period of time. I don’t remember why. I did everything but write stories – racketed my post count to over a thousand. On June 16, 2006 the forum had a short story contest. I wanted to win, badly. I submitted two stories. The first is Eye love You, posted on July 14, 2006 at 25 pages. I got the idea from watching a Korean (or was it Japanese? I don’t remember) music video on YouTube. Its been so long I don’t even remember the title or artist. The story tells the tale of a photographer and a hairdresser – they fall in love, they post ABDL pictures online, the hairdresser becomes blind, the photographer gives her his eyes. It’s romantic and dramatic, doesn’t really move away from my first story in terms of genre. The story bounces between characters in first person – some people found it unnecessary and I later agree. I thought it was a strong story at the time, but looking at it now, I feel sorta ‘meh’ about it. Nothing all that special about it, and only mildly entertaining to read – probably cause it was my first attempt at writing a short story after several months without writing.

My second entry, Looking Back at Tomorrow was a lot more fun to write, but took a lot of thinking and organizing. Its 10 pages and I posted it on August 31, 2006. The story is about a girl writing a picture book – one that concerns her long time friend and their past experiences. She ends up hand delivering the finished book to her friend only to be stopped by, what she believes, his partner. In actuality her friend is an AB and his partner is actually his daddy. Fun to write, but sorta had some holes in its plot – I probably could have fixed it had I spent more time on it…but my ego just screamed for me to post it so I could get some feedback asap. Stupid ego. I feel the same way as I did with my first short story when I look at it now…meh. My first entry ended up coming in second. I took a shorter break from writing stories after that.

Atelier Lucile, posted on October 21, 2006, would be my second unfinished story. I only wrote the intro and first chapter before giving up – for some reason I can’t remember. My intentions were to make the story really dark and mysterious. I first introduced the made up city of Mandinare which I would later use for other stories. I envisioned Mandinare to be something similar to Gotham. The name come to me while listening to Kenji by Fort Minor. I kept hearing Mandinare when Shinoda was actually saying Manzanar. Like I mentioned earlier I tend to come up with the titles first. Atelier means studio – but in the anime series Paradise Kiss the atelier was a bar where the characters hung out and made fashion. In the anime series Beck, Lucille is a guitar that had a wicked tone despite having seven bullet holes in it (I like the spelling Lucile better). So I coupled those two ideas into a plot that I have sorta forgotten. Something having to do with the main character (Melba) getting attacked in the alleyway after dark, but getting rescued by a cat. After the struggle Melba discovers an audio disk among the garbage in the alleyway where the cat shelters. She brings it home – the engraving on the collar is scratched out. The whole mystery aspect comes in to play here. She tries to discover its owner which gets her into some trouble…or something. I think I intended to have some sort of relapses in the cats history or something – its been to long since I touched this story idea. Since it only wrote up to the first chapter, no real ABDL stuff was introduced – only foreshadows. When I look back at the story, my ideas for the plot are a bit incoherent with how any ABDL stuff could actually be applied. It’s a shame really since I thought this story would be a keeper. I never really remember why I give up on my stories when the ideas are really sound (in my head at least). I probably blamed it on poor time management or something stupid. 

Or maybe chapter based stories weren’t really my thing anymore. My next three stories were short stories. The first is ‘Desperation’, posted on November 12, 2006, and only eleven pages long (though probably much shorter since the story is full of dialogue separated by spaces). I think the story is bland, but executed fairly well. It’s relatable, I think, and even possible to re-enact in real life. But it doesn’t change the fact that the story is bland and only the ending contains the tiniest remnants of ABDL stuff. The second short story I wrote is ‘A Different Story’, only five pages, and posted on December 6, 2006. This story could give you a bit of a chill – especially since, again, it could happen in real life. I explore the darker side of the fetish. If your mother were to discover your AB side, and be disgusted of it, would you kill yourself? The main character does just that. The reactions I received were quite alarming – some thought it was based on real events, others thought I had issues and were concern for my well being. I really enjoyed writing about the real and the emotions being portrayed. I tried to duplicate this style in my third short story. 

‘Covenant’ (six pages long and posted on December 17, 2006) is one of my most favourite stories I’ve written to date. It is a short story largely concerning my goals as a writer and how unhealthy they are. I really wanted to move away from writing for the attention. I enjoyed writing and I wanted to be proud of the things I wrote by getting over intentions. I wanted to write for the sack of writing better. I didn’t want to thrive off of feedback and page hits – cause those things don’t necessarily measure ones talent. I just needed to remind myself to man up and move pass my old ambitions cause they were stupid. Aside from the general message of the story, I thought the plot was decent and the characters were fun to play with. Though I got pretty meagre comments on it, I’m satisfied with how it turned out regardless.

My fifth chapter based story, and third incomplete story, is Blind Introversion – 16 pages, two chapters, and first posted on January 21, 2007. I main character, Lin, is strongly based on Toph from Avatar: the Last Airbender, and Daredevil. Although it was written in third person, I thought the idea of exploring a blind main character who sees by listening and feeling, was really exciting. It goes back to my whole obsession with describing things – doing the short stories really helped detach myself from the sometimes drawn-out descriptions I’d write. I hadn’t introduced any ABDL stuff yet, don’t really remember how I was going to either, but I had some of the plot worked out. Chapter two ends with Lin getting ready to departure her home with her butler Sebastian. I wanted to recapture the ‘Let Bygones be Bygones’ feel, where the main character is being introduced to new and foreign things. She would travel into town and befriend a son of a café owner. I wanted the son to be stereotypical African-American since it’d make an interesting contrast between the two characters (since Lin was rich). I’m sure morals and ABDL stuff would’ve been thrown in somehow had I finished the story (something I probably tell myself with every unfinished story). 

The most recent story I’ve written – far from recent since I posted it on April 19, 2007 – is the short story called ‘The Red Handed Revolver’ (5 pages). This is also one of my favourites. It is short, to the point, and stirs up some interesting emotions of how humans behave. I was influenced by Camilla Gibb, an anthropologist who’s article I had to read. Her writing was really easy to follow, witty with word play, and fun. I wanted to write something similar in style. The story is about a sometimes maniac, sometimes depressed AB who finds a revolver with three bullets and explores what he does with them. If anyone were ever to read one of my stories, this would be the first I’d recommend. Then I’d say, don’t bother reading my first couple of stories since the writing style would appear amateurish to it. But I guess it’s a good thing since I can see a progression in my abilities. It’s just a bit depressing that I haven’t written anything in over a year. What makes it more depressing is that I have many, many story ideas. You can read eight of them on the blog of my story website (http://toucher-terre.thirtyone-ten.net/blog.html).  I have several other ideas tucked away somewhere on my computer, and some more in the back of my head waiting to be forgotten.

Probably already the longest entry I’ve written so far, but I wanted to touch upon one more thing. I’ve been following the ABDL Story Forum for quite some time now and I gotta say there are some really talented writers. I don’t read as many stories on the forum as I use to, but my favourite authors have always been WingZ and Elizabeth. WingZ is a journalist (or reporter? Or is there a difference? I donno), so his writing is really good. His ideas are so fresh. From the ones I’ve read, they’re mostly drama, slice-of-life, mystery, action and romance. Often his plots are really well thought out and flow very smoothly. When I’m not looking to read fiction, I go to Elizabeth’s stories. She has more fantasy and sci-fi types of stories, the ones that aren’t tend to be really cute and fun to read. She incorporates a lot of ‘dream-like’ twists and descriptions. I’ve always been impressed by her since when I first read her early stories she was something like 15 or 16 years old. Remmy, an author and furry artist, use to write really hilarious parodies – wonder where is she now. There are some more authors I use to follow like Nighthawk, Eyebrows Mulligan, Orms Einbani, and Teekabel – they all sorta went their own ways after the third forum.

You know what I just noticed, the third forum is still using the banner I made for the second forum (the one with green, yellow, red, and blue – write, read, discuses, ABDL Story Forum). I think this year I will seriously consider writing again – I’ve given up on MMOs so I do have a lot of spare time that should be used for doing homework. Anyways, this ends the entry – my mind feels a little more sound now.

😫 exhausted  |  ♫ MF Doom - Fillet-O-Rapper
Link

Looking back at '08...

Dec. 31st, 2008   7:50 PM

[I wrote most of the things below yesterday cause I knew it required a bit of thinking.  Sleeping on it didn't really help jog up any old memories so I didn't add much.  I spelt in until 12:30pm which is considerably later than usual, but i went to bed at around 4am last night.  I didn't do much but clean my room and do some laundry - something about starting the new year clean and organized seems appropriate.  I also watched The Fall (2006), it was very beautiful and the acting was superb.]

It is New Year’s Eve and though I remember saying this last year, but this year has really gone by fast. I don’t know if that’s a good thing or bad thing, but for now I think it is kinda bad. Bad in that I have no real future planned out after (and if) I graduate sometime soon. I have a pretty uncertain future and it scares the crap out of me, and I’m saying that will all honesty. It is bad in that when I reflect on this year, I can’t say I remember doing anything that really stands out – so have I wasted a year of my life doing meaningless things? I’m sure there are a few more bad things associated with the speed in which the year has passed, but I cannot recall any more as of now. So what about the good things associate with said topic? Well, it’s good that I’ve survived half of my fourth year in university in such a haste – since I am losing interest in school, best to get it behind me as quickly as possible. It is good in that…um…well that was a short list. I guess I lack the optimistic gene (damn that pessimistic gene – I bet its from my fathers side of the family).

 Last year my New Year’s resolution was that I wouldn’t eat any snacks after 11pm in hopes that it would help me lose a little weight (burning the midnight oil playing computer games on an empty stomach isn’t easy). I think I managed to keep that resolution for ¾ of the year. It was a sort of an on-and-off thing; strong in the beginning of the year, than it got flimsy throughout. This year I don’t know what my resolution will be. Committing to not playing MMOs – besides Second Life – seems to easy for some reason, even though I know it has its difficulties. Perhaps being more organized? Committing a certain number of hours to doing school work a day? Or maybe finding more time to be with friends, or even a girl/boy friend (i are bi)? Bah I don’t know, I might even be better without one – that way I’ll feel less guilt to not fulfilling the goal.

 Reflecting on my year, although it was fast I thought it was a pretty interesting one. Here’s why (and these subheadings are made to fit the description and not the other way around – as in no templates were used, I’m just making it up as I go along)…

 Best piece of technology I got this year…

A Nintendo DS Lite, an iTouch, or a BlackBerry Pearl; of those I’d have to go with my BlackBerry. I would have loved my BlackBerry even more had I gotten the full data plan, but that would cost an extra $15 a month – for unlimited internet use though. I would have placed my iTouch number one had I not continuously been too lazy to upload it with applications. Also although I love the fact that my iTouch is pure eye candy – people look when you pull it out xD – I do miss buttons. I miss the simplicity of pressing a button to turn up the volume or to change songs immediately. It gets fairly annoying to have to whip out the iTouch just to do those simple things – especially if I’m freezing outside waiting for the bus, I want to be able to change songs with my hand in my pocket. Also, greasy fingers and touch screens don’t mix. My screen is very smudgy making it difficult for the screen to sense my touches. The Nintendo DS, well, I blame RayRi for that. She wanted it so I got it for her, then I broke up with her so I kept it reached her. But RayRi related stuff is for another post – one that requires a bit more thought and confession. The DS is fun when I’m bored, but only when I’m bored with all the other fun things I could be doing thus playing the DS as a last resort thing. 

 Something new and memorable…

Meeting a fellow ABDL (Adult Baby Diaper Lover – a paraphilia) in real life. It won’t forget I meet Dannyboy103 and what followed. We exchanged PM’s on ABy.com in early March. At the time I really wanted to get more involved with the community – like be more active on forums, possibly get back to writing stories, stuff like that. So we chatted for a bit, and I don’t recall who broke ice, but either of us suggested meeting up. We set up a date and a public place near a subway station. I was pretty nervous since it was the first time I meet up with someone I met online (and of all things someone I just recently met), but I wasn’t that petrified since he seemed like a cool guy and we were meeting in an open environment. So on the day we meet, my gawd, the dude was like a walking tree. He was SO tall! Not to hard to miss. My anxiety slowly diminished as I got to know him. Dan is, for the most part an AB, while I on the other hand is mostly DL (although to get technical I think I’d be 80% DL and 20% AB). We had sushi for dinner – was the first time he ever had sushi. Surprisingly he suggested we go to the Toronto Area AB/DL/AgePlay Monthly Munch, since I did want to be more active and open in the community and cause luckily the munch was on the day after. I have known about the munches for years, but never really got the balls to go to them. So I agreed to go with Dan and that’s what we did the following day. 

 I don’t remember which bar it was exactly, but it was somewhere downtown (which doesn’t say much). Ben’s Pub? Something pub. I can’t recall. The owner of the bar is ABDL aware, which I learned on the day of, making it even more awesome. What’s more, it was St. Patrick’s Day! The munch had, literally, all time record number of people showing up. Something like +40 people. It was something I’d never experienced in my life! So many different kinds of people with the same interest in mind. But no doubt it doesn’t take a moron to know how to be discrete – we were in public after all. You don’t go fronting around your fetish in public cause that’s just stupid (unless you want people looking at you strange). One of the reasons it was really packed was because ten or so people came down from Ottawa – one of which was boyricky. I’ve seen some of his videos and read some of his blogs, so it was pretty awesome seeing him in person. Equally as awesome was meeting three babyfurs I’d come to nearly every other time I went to future munches. The organizer of the Toronto munch is Orca, a babyfur. Shiro and Dan the Raccoon are his friends, and fellow babyfurs. Wow! Just Wow. That’s what was going through my head. Shiro! Omg! Shiro is an artist – he specializes in transformation art (human to furry), and I’ve admired his works for a long time. He use to post his art (I don’t know if he still does) on a babyfur website/forum called Fox Tales Times. I had no freakin’ idea he was Canadian, let alone living in a neighbouring city. Wow!

 So my first munch was really really amazing, but nerve wrecking since I had no idea who was who or how to behave since almost everyone was older than me. The alcohol mellow things out for me, so that was helpful. The night didn’t end there either! After the munch another gathering was being arranged at Kevydl’s pad. Dan knew Kev from before so we went. Wow…Yes, that went through my head again this time with more distress. I walked through the entrance and there were like a dozen other guys. The first thing a couple of them did was strip off their pants. You can imagine I had no idea what I had gotten myself in to. But luckily that was all they took off – to get more comfortable in just their diapers ^-^. We sat around, had some beers, I met a lot of new people that I sadly don’t remember. I remember Kev pulling out a huge bag full of AB clothes that he was giving away for some reason I don’t remember. The pile of clothes didn’t sit around to long – but I don’t blame them, fitted AB clothing is hard, and sometimes expensive to get. I should probably mention that a large majority of ABDL males are homosexual.  I’m bisexual so best of both worlds :P. 

 I went to the following months munch with Dan as well – although it was at a different location, Fran’s Restaurant. Definitely a big difference in atmosphere – there was maybe twenty people. The three babyfurs were there. I befriended Jerald (an AB) and his partner David (neither AB or Dl), Incomplete Dude, Nebulus64, and a few others I can put faces on but not names to. Usually after the munches the babyfurs carpool back to their city, and the rest of us locals go to the Pegasus (a gay bar) to shoot some pool. I’ve gone to, I think, five or six munches. I sorta stopped for no real reason during summer. Sometimes I’d forget what day it’d be on (which sucks cause I’d have to wait an entire month), and sometimes Perfect World was to addictive (my guild had weekly events on Saturdays, munches were every third Saturday of the month). I haven’t chatted with many of the ABDL people I’ve met, though I have met up with Jerald a couple of times. I hope to go to more munches next year – definitely something I enjoy every time. 

 Worst memory…

Bah, RayRi. You cold hearted devil. I don’t want to remember you anymore, but it’s hard not to for what you’ve done to me. I believe in karma (like what goes around comes around), but not in a strict sense (reincarnation based on past lives). So you’ll get yours as I have gotten mine. Again, I may elaborate in future posts, but not this one.

 Best job…

I have worked some pretty different jobs – mostly those completely unrelated to my field of study. My very first job was in a retirement home as a Dietary Aide – essentially slaving in the kitchen washing dishes, serving and preparing food. I thought it had a pretty decent mix of backbreaking-ness and easiness to it. Working in the kitchen was hard, but receiving free meals and being able to snack whenever was pretty awesome. My second job was all muscle. I worked for a printing company that did CD booklets, ice cream boxes, business cards, etc. I didn’t like it very much, not only because it was exhausting. The hours sucked balls. There was the 7am-3pm shift, or the 3pm-11pm shift. Choices were either wake up early (which I hate) so that I could enjoy my summer afternoon with friends, or sleep in and eat lunch but work at night while everyone else parties. The labour involved moving things from the warehouse that needed to be pressed or cut, feeding machines, catching, boxing, etc. My latest job is with G4S doing condo security. It was the best job last year and it’s still the best job this year! I do a floater schedule – meaning on the weekends I go and do my regular site while weekdays I take shifts from people who are on holiday. Taking shifts from other people is awesome. Most of the time the site they work at is ten times classier than my regular – and ten times easier. Lets face reality, where I’m from, patrol officers for condo security don’t do a hell of a lot. At my regular site I could patrol the building in twenty minutes, than I’d sleep or play on my computer for a few hours – repeat until shift ends. This is twice as easy for me since I worked the grave shift at my regular site (12 lunch to 12 midnight). Now put this in to perspective with other peoples shifts at classier condos. When I wasn’t patrolling I had access to practically everything – fitness room, billiards room, tv lounge, kitchen, party rooms, game rooms, the roof, etc. Simply awesome.

Music appreciation…

My taste in music changed. It happened not to long ago, actually. Maybe I think sometime during August or September – fairly late in the year. I use to groove to rock, alternative, indie, etc. Yea know, stuff with a band and a vocalist head banging and dancing to the beat. The Mars Volta is what comes to mind for me – they can get pretty psychedelic and jiggy at lives. I’m also thinking Bloc Party – before the switch I was getting into British artists. I began moving towards hip hop and rap, probably due to my brother’s influence. I’ve always sorta listened to rap, the mainstream stuff, but not really paying attention to hip hop music and its culture. There is a difference between rap and hip hop – the latter is so much bigger in importance. I began to not just listen, but to appreciate the music, its relevancy, and its history. Artists like KRS-One, Big Pun, Big L, The Roots, Common, Jay Dilla, and so much more build the foundation of hip hop. Kingpins and mongrels like Diddy, Jay-Z, Dr. Dre, etc are important but they’re mainstream and sometimes people look past the importance they have for being the supports in hip hop. At least, that’s my take on the scene. I don’t know nearly as much since I’m pretty new to the culture, but I’ve been listening to a lot of great beats and lyrics. It is so much more than guns, bitches, and bling – don’t let commercial artists pave your perception of true talent in the industry. 

 There are probably many more noteworthy things to mention, but I don’t remember than right now. This year has been good to me and it sucks that time goes by so quickly, and it sucks doubly that I can’t remember some of the things I’ve done. Maybe a resolution to help sharpen my memory – or something to do with this blog and jotting the things I do down more frequently. Well, I hope anyone who reads this has a good New Years and forever after, peace.

😶 blank  |  ♫ Fort Minor - Kenji
Link

What's next?

Dec. 30th, 2008   11:30 PM

I uninstalled Perfect World a few hours ago, not before saying some final goodbyes, giving away gold, dolls, heiros, pets, and gear. I’m quitting a little earlier than I stated I would, but I’m not phased. I don’t feel as though I’m missing out on all that much. It’s just a game that I’ve gotten tired of playing. I have made good and bad memories although a great number of which I don’t quite remember in detail. I would like to thank the friends I’ve made for the great gameplay – and my apologize if you are reading this and I’ve forgotten to mention your name. Guildies from when I had a guild (Discourse) include: Epicness, dArKeR, Avdotya, Valorman5, and Hade27. Friends from the first guild (Euphoria) I was in; Echoica, Kyandeisu, and FulMetal. Friends from one of the last guilds (Imortaz) I was in; Wingzor, Tinkabel, Reiato, NightDove, Etc, Viktorrr, and Quimeras. And friends I’ve made from simply playing and getting to know; TOKER, SayangWu, BONEHEAD!, Tigrisor, S0uls2, DEATHCAST, and Necoco. Lastly, I would like to thank RayRi – that crafty little cold hearted bitch – who taught me something and hopefully the same can be said from her. But, I’ll save RayRi for another post.

Of all those players I just mentioned, I don’t think I’ll ever keep in a steady contact with no more than eight of them. More than half of them have my email address, five of which are on my MSN list, but I just have a feeling it’ll remain just that – people on my list that I’ll rarely if ever chat with. It’s fairly similar to my MSN list on my main account (the one I use for real life friends I’ve made from school or work or family), it’s a long list but I only chat with, maybe, seven of those people regularly. It makes me question the value of having people on my MSN list. I don’t mean to speak in a tone of negativity or disrespect, though. It’s just, I have a lot of people on my contact list that ‘I just know’. Some of them I was friends with while in school but grew distant with, and some I just know because of a friend-of-a-friend association or something similar. I don’t even know some of these people all that well as I use to. And it’s really sad. It’s like, it’s lonely to have a short contacts list, but it’s sad to have an extensive list with people you don’t know very well and don’t chat with at all. Could I ever muster the effort to chat with someone I use to be friends with in junior high? I think I could but the awkwardness would make me regret doing so. This ‘MSN contact list’ blues feeling I get can also apply to my FaceBook. I rarely use it but I have an account. And yet, all these people I just know cause we went to the same school are adding me as friends. I think it demeanings the word ‘friend’. 

Well that was rather reminiscent. So, my heading of this thread is ‘What’s next?’ and I’m beginning to ask myself just that. I have known for a while that I can get really addicted to specific leisures – usually ones that cost money or are time consuming. My earliest memory of this, I’d have to say, was when I went over Marvel OverPower cards. I remember one of my friends got me a Onslaught IQ card (it’s fairly rare and powerful) for my birthday. Now, I don’t even recall how to play the game. My next most distinct memory I have goes back to sometime in grade six or whenever Pokemon cards came out and were the ‘it’ thing. My gawd, I was hooked. I lived in the suburbs then and there was this comic book shop called Planet X. I would literally go there once a week to buy some booster packs with the allowance I was given. My parents always taught me to save at least half of my allowance so that I’d have something to sit on a few years down the line, but man I remember spending like it was nothing. I remember one time I went to the shop and bought something like fifteen Team Rocket booster packs – I remember being pretty disappointed only getting one foil card. I stopped playing sometime after the Neo Destiny set came out (which was about four years since I first started) because most of the people in school stopped playing. I don’t even remember what sort of deck I used.

 The next obsession I had was Magic: the Gathering cards. It was a step up, literally. Magic was like the big kids version of Pokemon – the game mechanics are similar but required with more sophisticated thinking. I started playing when Seventh Edition was in and quit sometime after Eighth Edition came out (core set editions come out once every four years). So during that four years, and because people in my school played Standard, I used a lot of cards from Odyssey Block, Onslaught Block, and Mirrodin Block (each block consists of 3 expansion sets – every four months a new expansion set comes out). I was addicted. I collected cards from sets prior, even though most of my friends didn’t play Extended. My most favourite card is Sol Ring – I loved it so much I had something like twelve copies (they’re from the very early sets so they aren’t easy to get). My most favourite deck to play is Burning Bridge. I had a modified Red/White red that was a lot of fun – it pissed off a lot of my friends since they ran creature cards and I nulled them with my Ensnaring Bridge card. Man, I kinda miss those days. Especially pre-release events! Whenever a new set would come out, a pre-release even would be held at a community center. A bunch of my friends went to a couple events and got to play in tournaments. Those events are deadly as well, cause they have card shops set up with things I’d never have been able to get from my local comic book shop. In the future I wouldn’t mind jumping into MtG again – when I have a job and able to afford $20 cards. But I think I’d have a lot of difficulty relearning old mechanics and the ton of new things they keep adding with each set. 

 During highschool MtG and Star Craft were the things to play. I never got in to computer games until my senior year. I played CounterStrike at net cafes (since I didn’t get a laptop until entering university, and the family desktop was crap. I wasn’t hooked since there was a pool hall next door to the internet café. I played a lot of jitz (or foosball) during my last two years of high school. The tables were FABI, and I mostly played ‘everything’ while at the front. I still play jitz, just not as frequently. I play ‘slow ball’ now, and I’m not bad with forward or defence. I’m horrible with passing moves, but I’m pretty good with snake shots. My special move is the turtle shot (or so I like to call it, but different places might call it something else). 

 I don’t remember how I got in to Second Life but I started playing as soon as I got my laptop – a Gateway 6021 GZ. I’m pretty certain Second Life and my obsession for the game shaved years off my laptop. It use to run really quickly and the fan use to be silent; everything is reverse now. Back then I had my own room so I usually SL running while I was asleep. Back then they had camping chairs – chairs where you literally just sit and earn money. Granted the minutes to dollars where minimal, but I didn’t complain…FREE MONEY! Well, it was pretty stupid of me, since doing that has no doubt killed my laptop. Now it overheats and its slow and etc etc. It’s also pretty dumb since now I just buy all my moneys with my credit card when I probably could have easily done that in years past. Oh well, I only use my laptop for taking notes in class now. My quad core desktop is my number one baby. So Second Life, that took up a good chunk of my first through third year of university. I mostly hung around the babyfur community and built a bunch of wacky stuff. I still play, but just less involved as I use to be. I keep telling myself I’ll get back into it, since it’s a MMO that’s timeless in my opinion. I’ve been meaning to learn how to make sculpted prims, hopefully now with PW out of the way I can.

 Which brings me to Perfect World. I began playing, I think, sometime in March or April of this year. Fuck Cubizone and their cubi’s. I don’t want to go into exact amounts, but I spent triple digits (that’s real money digits [$xxx]) on this game. I hated the obsession, but I loved playing the game – something of a bittersweet relationship. But I’ll just detach myself from the pissy feeling I’m getting from realizing how much I’ve spent on PW. Next addiction! >.> FusionFall. Let me just clarify – this is a game for little kids and I will not get crazy obsessed with it. It is entirely PvE, so it’s fun, but not that fun. The chatting function sucks. And the cannons disconnect me from the network whenever I use them – extra sucky. I will not get addicted. I will not get addicted. I will not get addicted. Ah crap I’m already level 13. I have seen, but not many, players at my level and higher so I can feel the addiction creaking within. The games full release isn’t until the middle of January – so I’m hoping I’ll lose interest by then, or get piled with school work before I get hooked like a real fiend. 

 (T_T) Why can’t I be addicted to text books and academic articles! Why! Why! Why!

 Okay well that aside, my final week of holidays has been somewhat productive…in terms of sitting in front of my monitor. I have watched Changeling (2008), The Day the Earth Stood Still (2008), Milk (2008), Four Christmases (2008), Yes Man (2008), Gran Torino (2008), Unbreakable (2000), Boogieman 3 (2008), and Real Time (2008). The only movie I’ve watched and paid for in theatres this holiday is Slumdog Millionaire (2008). Stephanie gave me a fake copy of The Fall (2006), among other things, and I think I’ll watch it tomorrow – its by the same director who did The Cell (2000), extraordinarily arts and visually appealing. 

🌀 hungry  |  ♫ Atmosphere - Garentees
Link

What I missed...

Dec. 28th, 2008   4:08 AM

I came back home from a three day trip in Ottawa at around 6:30pm, and I'll admit I haven't moved from my computer since - besides showering and brushing my teeth.  I have been playing FusionFall (had to recreate my character), and a bit of Perfect World (selling off junk before I quit after New Years).  I'd like to think I don't have an online gaming addiction even though I'm still at it...at 4am.  Oh well, I feel pretty damn good right now.

The trip with my family was ok - nothing particularly exciting or horrible happened.  I tryped up brief notes summarizing what happened each day, since I was fairly bored a majority of the time, but I've decided not to post them.  Nothing really worth remembering in the future, nor sharing.  But just briefly, on the 24th my family and I visited my senile, ninty something year old, wheelchair-bound great grandmother (fathers side).  On the 25th we went skiing at Edelweiss Valley; conditions were icy and gawd awful, but the slopes were pretty much empty.  On the 26th we went skiing at Mont Ste-Marie; conditions were much better but I feel 3 times bruising my arm - the right side of my neck kinda hurts as well.  At night we had dinner at my dads childhood friends house and I played Halo 3 for the first time in an amazingly decked out basement.  On the 27th we left after having lunch - the drive was a nightmare since it rained heavily half way and was extremely foggy the other half.  All three days we stayed at my grandparents (fathers side) tiny townhouse. 

I've been thinking, I'm going to be more open with where I am on the map - since it gets annoying to have to describe rather than just explicitly state.  Like, it's just easier to say I went to Ottawa from Toronto than saying some place four hours drive from where I live - and where my grandparents live.  I think I wanted to write about something else as well, but unsurprisingly I completely forgot.  Maybe it'll come back to me another day. 

🙂 good  |  ♫ Gorillaz - M1 A1
Link

Out of town...

Dec. 24th, 2008   12:34 AM
I'll be out of town from 24-27th to visit my fathers father, with my family.  It's an anual thing we do and I'm not exactly looking forward to it.  My grandpa and grandma are really sweet and I like the very much - at times I wish I knew more about them.  But the fact that I'll be spending time with my family tells me it won't be a very pleasent.  I'm sure I've already written about it but i don't enjoy being around my family - they each have a little something that kinda irks me though, least of which from my mom.  My dad's short temperedness is pathetic and often unnecessary/uncalled for.  I have to many differences with my older brother that get compared unspokenly making our relationship uncomfortable - or at least enough for me not to like him. Since my brother and I have gotten our licences, we've taken shifts driving the four hour trip to get to where my grandparents live.  There isn't anything to do where they live, or that I'm familiar with.  There are a couple of malls that aren't all that spectacular, and there is a canal that my family use to skate on during the winter - but each year winter has been coming later and later resulting in the absence of skating when we visit.  My dad grew up in that city, so sometimes we visit his friends and their family.  My parents want to go skiing on the 25 and 26 - having already had an unpleasent skiing experience earlier this season, I'm not looking forward.  I use to enjoy skiing when I was younger, but in the last couple of years I've been bitter with winter and the cold weather.  I have a feeling I'll be the first to give in to exhaustion or feet/leg pain - that should be embarrassing.  I think another reason why I'm not looking forward to going on this trip is because the sneak peek for FusionFall is this Saturday.  I won't be home until night time, on the 27th, and I'll probably tired from the drive and too flat-assed to sit in front of my computer screen to play.  The closed beta testing are done and they've ereased all the accounts, so I really wanted to play earlier or to at least claim my name.  Cause based on the preliminary registrations there's another 'dumpling' out there.  But, this could serve as a test for how commited I can be from not getting hooked on MMORPGs.  I'm slowly using up my dolls in PW, and I've already sorta decided which of my friends will get whatever I have in my inventory before I go (mostly rare pets, but maybe one or two eq). For this trip I have packed a lot of electronics to keep myself occupied - since I don't watch TV, and my grandparents don't have internet.  I'll be bringing my laptop, I recently transfered ep. 18-26 of Soul Eater and the jdrama Zettai Kareshi.  I'll be bringing my 8Gb iTouch loaded with tons of songs.  I'll be bringing my Nintendo DS Lite with thirty something games thanks to my R4 card.  And I'll be bringing my Blackberry Pearl, just cause I never leave the house without my cell phone.  I've also packed a book which I doubt I'll touch -
