it's a required text from one of the anthropology courses I've taken, but neglected to read a majority of the articles.  It would suck if I forget to bring my earphones or the chargers for my gizmos.  Today I went downtown to do some last minute Xmas shopping.  I bought a red pillow with a pretty artsy flower embroidered on it, and a mimobot designer USB 2Gb flash drive for Stephanie.  I also bought a shoulder and neck warmer for my mom (it's a microwaveable minicap filled with beads that retain heat, and you can tie it around your neck).  I ordered a USB plug-in heated slippers for Jully, which hasn't arrived yet.  And I ordered my brother a beaker mug, because you said he wanted it.  Aside from scrambling from one mall to the next, I saw a lot of cheap clothes that I could really use.  But I didn't buy anything for myself, plus I didn't want to have to carry extra stuff since I was taking public trasportation.  There were some graphic tees at Old Navy for $6, and more graphic tees at Bluenotes that were 2 for $20.  There was a nice sweater at Roots that I wanted to try on, but thought it was a little thin.  On Monday Stephanie came over for lunch - my dad made pasta with a meat sauce (usingleft over meat loaf).  Afterwards we went to a theater she'd never been to and watched Slumdog Millionaire.  It's a limited screening film and it is really amazing.  The plot centers around a contestant, who's raised in the slums, on 'Who wants to be a millionair?' Indian version.  And pretty much for each question asked the contestant has a flashback showing how he came to know the answer - as well, each flashback contributes to a larger story which at the end all gets pieced together (and it is really well done).  I highly recommend it.  I'm going to cut myself off and end this post.  It's getting late and I'm probably driving tomorrow morning.  To anyone who reads this, I wish you a Merry Christmas or whichever religious/non-religious holiday you celebrate.
💤 sleepy  |  ♫ Jay-Z - Brooklyn We Go Hard
Link

Uncomforting feeling and friends

Dec. 21st, 2008   4:03 AM

I get this feeling in the pit of my stomach every time my parents or grandparents start up a conversation about my friends. A part of me wants to believe that they’re being condescending, but I know that they probably only mean well. I got this kind of feeling today when I invited some old highschool friends to come over, but I guess it would be worthwhile to mention a bit about me to begin. I consider myself the black sheep of the family – it’s heavily punctuated when I’m compared to my older brother. A majority of the things he is I am not, each having their own particular reasons which I won’t dive into. My brother is studying hardcore sciences (biochem), he gets good grades, he’s doing his masters, he has lots of friends, he’s had a serious relationship with a girl, he’s organized, and his communication skills are fairly strong – to state some of his qualities. For this particular post I’ll just concentrate on the aspect of friends. He has many, some ranging from elementary school, to highschool, to jobs he’s had, to friends of a friend. He maintains these networks, as he’s very social – he hangs out with them, does sports with them, goes on trips, etc. He talks about them around our family, my parents are familiar with a majority of them. And in effect, I often get compared – not verbally per say, but something you can feel that they’re just hinting. I will admit I do not have many friends – particularly ones past highschool – nor am I a very outgoing or social person. I rarely talk about my friends around my family, and when I do its usually bombarded with questions. This position of me knowing that I’ll never be like my brother – the feeling I get from my family – is uncomfortable. It isn’t as though I’m trying to be opposite, it’s just the way I am – though I question myself whether it is a reason or an excuse. Why don’t I try to get more friends, or at least build stronger relations with my existing friends? 

Anyways, back to that feeling. It felt as though my family were making a big deal out of something that I didn’t want it to seem. They did things that seemed a bit unnecessary. My parents bought buns so we could eat them for snakes, they reminded me to offer them drinks, or that they could drape their jackets on the sofa. My grandma commented on how happy she was for me seeing me happy (though I wasn’t necessarily looking happy, it was the idea that I would be happy from having friends over that made her happy), and how long it had been seen the last time she saw what’s his name. A small part of be wants to say that my parents just want to reinforce good manners as a host – as face is important to Chinese people seeing as it can reflect the entire family – but the point is they don’t have to treat me like I’m a moron. I know how to be polite, and of all things around my friends – people I know better than my parents know. I wish they would just stop making such a big deal out of something that isn’t. Perhaps I avoid this uncomforting feeling by not inviting friends over, or talking about them around my family – but maybe it just perpetuates itself in a cycle (what I do makes me feel uncomfortable and what I don’t do makes me feel like a black sheep). 

Well to make light of my ranting, I had a fairly good time. We all went to the same grade school (gr5 or 6) – Andy, Mark, and Kelvin – but I’ve also known Mark since elementary school (gr2 or 3). I live in a metropolitan area that’s comprised of many cities. The city I lived and went to elementary school in was pretty underclass and though I don’t recall being friends with Mark, we were in the same class. He moved to another city up north in grade 4. Unknowingly I went to the same school as he did when my family moved up north as well when I was in grade 5. Again we were in the same class and I recognized him. Our friendship started after that, but hasn’t exactly been a very tight one. Although I have my friends, I really want to get to know him better, and perhaps have a stronger relation with him. But we’re pretty different people. Out of the three of them – Andy, Mark, and Kelvin – Mark, I would say, is the most school smart (and in their regards they are all really smart – like in highschool they were all +80s). I know as much as they probably know that I’m the dumbest amongst them. Now, they would sound horrible had if it weren’t for the fact that Mark has the weakest communication skills amongst the four of us. He is indecisive and soft spoken. Andy, I would say is the strongest, second to me, followed by Kelvin, and then Mark. 

I keep going off track from how my day went. I apologize, seems like I just seems like my thoughts are jumbled. They all live in the same area (where I use to, and where we all went to highschool), so they carpooled down. Usually during major holidays or breaks in the semester (like spring break or Christmas holiday) we get together to play Risk, the time consuming board game. I think this began during first year – four years ago. But this and the last gathering we didn’t play Risk and instead played Wii. They arrived at around 2pm and we played Super Smash Bro. Brawl and Rayman Raving Rabbids 2. I haven’t touched my Wii (or more accurately a Wii) in months, so though I’ve played Brawl, it was really rusty. Kirby and R.O.B. were my favourite characters. Rabbids 2 is a lot more fun than Mario Party because the minigames are longer and has less NPCs talking – but it is a hell of a lot more tiring, the games are all about arm stamina pretty much. We went to a sushi place nearby at around 7pm – ran into my parents (uncomforting feeling ver. 2). Mix BBQ appetizer wasn’t worth the six bucks but the sushi and sashimi had thick cuts of fish. Afterwards we went back to my place and Wii’d till 11pm, playing Brawl, Mario Party 8, and Rabbids 2. Played a bit of Perfect World after they left, I decided to sell off some stuff I don’t use for gold, than buy cubi-gold so I can buy replacement dolls and heiros. Than I’ll go on a PK streak until I’m all out of dolls – than I’ll quit the game hopefully by New Years. I killed 3 people already, so I have 6 hours till my name becomes white again. Also played a bit of Second Life afterwards – the new AnthroXtacy Mouse avatar by Tinintri Mistral came out today and I bought a white/grey one for 700L. I haven’t really gotten a full tinkering with it, but here’s my opinion. The head and skin look amazing, something I wouldn’t mind using as my main av. The head and hair are no copy/no modify – I donno what’s up with that. I can understand the head aspect since it mostly consists of sculpties, but the hair? Maybe it has scripts in it I haven’t looked into. I don’t like the paws – Tinintri used an invisible prim to hide the hand, than made the paws out of sculpties putting that on top. You can still see the invisible prim, and though I haven’t tried I’m sure it’ll mess up the way items will look when held. It is similar to using digilegs, where a prim makes the leg invisible to show the sculptie leg in its place – which usually messes up pants since they don’t show on the prim leg. I’ll look into it tomorrow. I have begun making my own mouse av though it’s mostly just a recombination of other avatars. 

Since my last post I haven’t done anything all that noteworthy. I watched Seven Pounds (2008) and Let the Right One In (2008) online – watched the later because of The Amazing Atheist from YouTube, he critiqued it saying it is way better than Twilight (they are both romance and vampire movies), but will receive will less views since it isn’t mainstream (it’s a Swedish movie). It’s really disappointing, but I completely agree – although I haven’t watched Twilight my friends who have said it was terrible even with such low expectations walking in. If anything, watch Let the Right One In, it has minimal horror, but a lot of heart and humanity. It’s about forbidden love – I don’t want to ruin it, but the main characters are 12. The human boy is 12, and the vampire girl is +200, but looks 12….and looks like a girl!!!! (spoiled, sorry). The movie only shows a 2 second glimpse of the girls crotch, but in the novel it states that the girl is actually a boy who got castrated. The novel, from the reviews I read, is a lot deeper (talks about pedophilia) and more descriptive with plot and background story. I’ll admit I’m quite interested in forbidden love – as portrayed in tv/movies/anime/manga and not in real life. It just seems so much more romantic and perhaps meaningful. One of my favourite Webcomics, Better Days by Jay Naylor, depicts its two main sibling characters Lucy and Fist as being in love with one another, but know that they cannot be together. It gets touched upon in a couple of the early chapters which I thought was really sweet (in terms of romantic feeling). One of my favourite animes, Koi Kaze, depicts two siblings with an age gap of I think 12 years. The series progresses with them gradually expressing their love for each other and the difficulties it causes. At the end of the series they sleep together (spoiler #2 – so sorry!!). But other than watching those two movies and playing PW or SL, I wasted a majority of my Friday playing FusionFall. Strangely enough, it was open as a sneak peek, had Happyfeet not told me I’d have missed out. It’s kind of weird since right now for this weekend I am unable to play – it is only open for beta enabled testers, which I am not. I levelled my character from 7 to 11 on Friday, I completed all the quests for each level with the exception of two since I couldn’t figure them out – possibly cause they’re buggy. 

Damn, I’m typing this entry onto MS Word (for the spell check and cause my browser has crashed in the middle of typing before), and it’s almost 3 pages. I wish writing essays were this easy, which brings up a strange reminder of when I use to write stories for a forum and I posted about the same subject (writing stories with easy while bullshitting through essays to meet page minimums). But, I’ll leave sweet and savoury confession for another entry cause I know it’ll take up just as many Word pages and it is 4am. Till next time, have a safe holiday to whoever reads this.

💤 sleepy  |  ♫ Finger Eleven - Temporary Arms
Link

Examless...

Dec. 16th, 2008   6:20 PM
My three week holiday started yesterday after i finished my night exam.  It was for a political ecology course and I already have an idea what grade I'll come out with.  I'll admit I'm not very school smart - or hard working for that matter - and I usually only put in an effort on things I enjoy doing, but I this semester I think has been one of my better ones.  I only know two of my final grades so far, I got an 88 in an osteology class and a 90 in a seminar class on zoonotic diseases.  They were both taught by the same prof which most of my friends don't quite like, but I'm finding him a bit interesting.  I've been meaning to write him an email or speak with him directly about some input to where anthropology can take me in the near future.  I did poorly in an anthro of development course - I discovered that the social aspect of anthropology answers questions with more questions and rarely leads to a straightforward answer.  I really hated it, so I'm now gearing towards science base with a bit more concrete answers - though I know that the social aspect still plays an important role in what the sciences cannot fulfill. Its been a pretty curry week... Last Thursday I went to have lunch with a highschool friend.  I'll call her Stephanie - for the entirety of this blog I will not use the real names of my real life friends, online friends I will use their screennames.  We usually get together during breaks in the semester or when we're both free and she's in town - she lives on res in a neighbouring cities university.  When we do these things we usually meet up with our group of friends - usually two or three others.  But it's difficult to set up since their universities are also out of town, and one works full time.  So this time it was just the two of us.  As usual with that particular group of friends we plan things last minute.  I picked her up, and quicker than usual we settled on an Indian buffet restaurant that I'd never been.  Small place tucked into a rather businessy area.  The selection of food was pretty good for a lunch menu.  They had one of my favorites, mata paneer.  Its green peas with cheese curds (a firm cheese called paneer that doesn't melt) in a curry base.  Delish ^^.  They also had this really awsome dessert.  At first I thought it was a fruit just by looking at it, but when I dug in, it was actually a ball of dough - similar to a timbit - and it was drenched in a honey syrup.  Stephanie and I caught up and such, but whenever I hang out with her I kinda feel distanced.  She's really into pop media (like celebs, movies, artists, etc) and I'm not so much - so it sometimes leads to her doing most of the talking or semi-awkward silences.  Afterwards we went to a mall that I haven't gone to in probably a year and a half.  It's one of my favorite malls since it has a lot of great shops that I like.  It was pretty nostelgic since I've gone to that mall with Stephanie and our group of friends a couple of times.  There were a lot of sales but we walked out empty handed - I gathered some gift ideas however.  Later I drove her home and then went home as well. On Friday I went skiing with my dad and older brother (by two years).  I didn't really want to go, but it wasn't like I planned on doing much studying, it just seemed kinda compelling since the men in the household rarely get together - plus the lift tickets were only $20, reg. price is $55.  Another reason I didn't really want to go was cause I don't enjoy being around my family.  For the most part I find them really annoying.  My dad is a pretty angry guy, sometimes I don't understand why he can be so aggravated over miniscule things.  He also eats like a fuking pig.  I've noticed that his table manners have been going down hill since roughly March.  He's makes a lot of noise when he eats and puts things down with a lot of force - just a bunch of unnecessary things that grind my gears.  I dislike my brother because I have to live in the same room as him.  My family downsized houses three years ago and since back then my brother lived on res, I had the room to my self.  But now that he's back, it drives me insain.  I value my privacy more than most people and he just gets in the way of that.  But anyways, more on my family another post, back to skiing.  We went to Blue Mountain and I drove - it was a bitch, not only because we left early in the morning.  No matter the route you take to get there, you have to cut through 2 small towns.  It took us two hours to get there, one of which was spent sitting in slow traffic getting through the one lane towns.  The weather didn't help much either, there was a snow storm.  We got to the moutain by about 10:30am.  I think last year I only went skiing three or four times, probably the same as the year before that.  I've gotten rusty to say the least, but I've been skiing since I was six so I'm not terribly bad.  I went to the gym on Wedn and did a bunch of cardio; having not gone to the gym in months my legs were pretty shot.  Skiing didn't exactly help either.  My feet were in so much pain i had to test for an hour after only doing four runs.  After doing a couple more runs in the afternoon we left at around 3:30pm.  All in all, I didn't have much fun.  I hate winters and my muscles were aching and chances are I'm going skiing with my family again when we go and meet relatives in Ottawa next week.  Nothing noteworthy happened on Saterday.  I think I wasted a couple of hours playing Cartoon Network Universe: FusionFall - damn Happyfeet got me hooked on it even though its a mmorpg for little kids XD.  I reached lvl 7, but they were just beta testing this weekend.  They're opening up the game again on the 27th. On Sunday I went to school to study with a friend, Jully, after talking on the phone with her the night before debating where and when we'd meet up.  She was being finicky saying that the public libraries open at 1:30pm and close at 5:30pm, and that coffee shops were too noisy, and that going to school on a weekend was really uncool.  I told her I'd buy her dinner if we settled with going to school.  I meet Jully in second year, I think, but I'll never forget how I met her.  We were on the bus heading to school, she sat behind me.  I was reading my sociology textbook since we had a test that day.  This is where her accounts come in - she noticed the textbook since she was taking the same course so she tapped me on the shoulder and I completely ignored her.  I probably did, but unintentionally.  I usually have my headphones blasting and it was winter time so I might not have felt it through my thick jacket.  She caught up with me while walking down the hall and our friendship started no sooner.  She's the type of person who will say she's shy, but actually isn't.  She picks up quite alot of friends just simply by starting up random conversations.  Getting to know her has been interesting.  She's sort of a white washed fob (fresh off the boat [people who are still culturated by their country of origin - in this case Hong Kong]).  She use to like asian idols, but gradually shifted to animes and manga.  She dresses sorta fobby punk-ish, normally black coloured clothes.  I enjoy hanging around her since we can connect with a lot of things.  Anyways, we decided to meet up at 2:30pm, but as always I don't expect her to be on time.  I had lunch with my family since they were eating someplace near campus, thus I could skip the bus, get a ride, and a free meal.  I had seafood and squash in curry with rice at a Chinese restaurant.  It was predominantly coconut milk based so I didn't really like it as much.  I like curries with a strong flavouring of spices.  Afterwards they drove me to school and I was fairly early.  I got alot of good studying done, even while with Jully.  She's not a distracting person, but she can get distracted by others.  We left campus at around 7:45pm, it was pretty cold outside and the buses on weekend nights don't run very frequently.  We settled on white washed fobby food (lol)(Chinese transliteration we call these kinds of restaurants Hong Kong style) cause its really cheap - plus Jully liked these kinds of restaurants cause the meals come with soup and a hot drink.  I had braised ox tongue with tomato sauce on fried rice, all of which gets baked.  Jully had curry lamb with rice, I ended up having a bit of hers and their curry was pretty tasty.  The restaurant was in her nieghborhood so I walked her home, afterwards it took me an hour to get home.  I tried to pull an all nighter since my exam was the day after, but ended up climbing in bed at abour 3am. Monday I didn't do much besides study and do my exam.  Didn't get home until 11pm cause the exam was from 7-10pm.  I kicked off my holiday by plaing Perfect World until 5am.  I did a couple of dungeon raids with some guildies - the last boss in Frozen Realm dropped a molder so we were pretty lucky, and we only get as far as the first boss in Valley of Disaster.  At around 4ish there was some pking (player kill) going on at Temple of Orchis between Warlords and a couple of popular guilds.  I quit the guild I was in temporarily to join Warlords since I plan on quitting the game after New Years and have't really done much pking.  Man, the Warlords roster is insain.  Their recruitment requirement is lvl 90 and up so I'm one of the lower lvl members.  They have alot of 100 and 101 players - its no wonder they're the most powerful guild with the most territory.  Its scary too since alot of their regular members have amazing gear, most of which are +10, 11, 12 enchanted, which can cost hundreds of dollars in real life.  Anyways, I'll probably be in Warlords till next Monday, I want to experience som Territory Wars and go kill other players - I've already stocked up on dolls. Today I slept in until 12:30pm.  My grandparents, who live with my family, went to visit my greatgrandma (she's 103).  Had lunch with my dad and did the dishes.  Did my daily quests on PW.  Our housecleaning maids came at 2pm, they come biweekly, and left at 3pm.  I watched episode 13 and 14 of Soul Eater during that time.  Afterwards I streamed Thumbsucker (2005) and Wall-E (2008) and watched them both.  They were amazing.  I'm not much at critiquing or reviewing films or animes so I won't even bother trying, nor will I give a plot over view since you can google that.  Thumbsucker had a sort of Juno feel to it when watching - just the way the characters talked and how the plot was narrated (the plots are completely different though).  I really like movies that are down-to-earth, relatable, and express genuine human emotion.  I find it really beautiful, almost like it touches a bit of my soul.  Wall-E was worth all its great reviews and then some.  I can't believe I waited so long to watch it even though it only came out in the summer.  Parts of the movie made me want to shed some tears - its brilliant how body language can substitute talking, while being easily understood.  The moral was straightforward and definetly something to keep in mind especially how with our our retarded and fat society is taking the environment to its grave - taking ANTB01 and C10 helped fuel my hatred for how we can fuk with the environment.  I'm a bit bummed that House MD is a rerun - its the only television show I watch a week. Anywhoo, off to enjoy more of my holiday now.
🙂 good  |  ♫ Ludacris ft. Jay-Z & Nas - I Do It for Hip Hop
Link

A bit about me...

Dec. 11th, 2008   10:38 PM

I have questioned myself concerning how open I should be with this blog.  On one hand if I choose to be very open I will have a variety of things to write about while worrying about whether or not I've said to much.  Being open means a larger audience will be able to read this blog, but the possibility of having conflicting opinions may not be in my favour.  For example, A) I write about my computer gaming experiences, and friends I've made through these games read this blog, then B) I write about the fetish I have and other uncommon topics (ex. furry fandom), and friends I've made from those interests read this blog.  My friends in category (A) would think quite differently of me upon reading things I post concerning category (B) - while the awkwardness probably wouldn't happen vice versa.

I will admit I do not have many people I can call my friends in real life, probably only a handful that I'm close with.  But online I've made quite a few that, sometimes, I wish not to exclude from my real life. 

There is always the prospect that absolutely no one will read this blog and being as open as I want will have no real impact...

So a little bit about me - or things that immediately come to mind that I think best describe what I am willing to share about me.  I will more than likely blog about these particular things in future posts.

1.  I am a bisexual Canadian born Chinese.

2.  I am studying at UofT hoping to get a BSc. in Medical Anthropology.  Although it sounds half spectacular I am in fact extremely lost with my future in terms of what career to pursue in.  I am at a point in my academic life where school no longer interests me.  I'm tired of it and I want to work.  But for the most part, anthropology usually leads to a path in research which, besides making me sick to my stomach, isn't something I want to do.

3.  I spend a majority of my time playing computer games, although I am trying to slowly ween off.  Currently I am playing (followed by usernames)...
Second Life = TiAmo Dumpling (my old usernames include Isobel Piek, Baylyn Fayray, and Nodame Hyun)
Perfect World MY-EN (Oracle server) = dumplings (previous username: magic_BBQ)

4.  I enjoy watching anime but I am not an otaku.  I do however have a suitcase full of burnt cd's - mostly anime, but also movies, music, and mangas/comics.  Currently that suitcase holds 2387.4 GB.  I am currently following a series called Soul Eaters.

5.  I enjoy listening to a broad genre of music.  Quite recently I've really been digging hip hop - to name only a few among many artists = Common, Jay-Z, Nas, Lil Wayne, KRS One, Kanye West, Ludacris, Murs, Lupe Fiasco, Wu-Tang Clan, The Roots, Mos Def, Big Pun, etc. 
Before getting into hip hop I was really into indie music like Bloc Party, Metric, Alexisonfire, Arcade Fire, Deerhoof, Arctic Monkeys, Electric Six, Sons & Daughters, etc.

6.  I'm not much of a fitness buff but I do go to the gym irregularily - though I am trying to fit it in to my lifestyle.  I like playing and watching tennis and quite recently I've picked up squash but find it difficult to play since it is hard to find friends who also play.  As a kid I use to swim, skate, and ski a lot, but I don't do those any more. 

7.  I have a diaper fetish.  In the 'community' I would categorize myself as a diaper lover (DL).  Other related categories (that I am not) include; adult baby (AB), teen baby (TB), little girl (LG), sissy's, babyfurs, etc.  It is important to note that this fetish has absolutely nothing to do with children.  I simply have a sexual attraction to diapers. 

8.  On a somewhat related topic, I am not a full blow furry.  I am interested in the furry fandom, I know of a few furries in real life, and I do every now and then look at furry art on sites such as Fur Affinity, but I do not consider myself to be a furry.  On Second Life my avatar is a furry shapeshifter.  I find it difficult to settle with a particular species but if I had to narrow it down it'd probably be a bunny, a mouse, and/or a wolf.

9.  Just off the top of my head, I dislike; being late, being in debt, being lied to, being used, uninteresting conversations, awkward silences, being woken up, bitter foods, cold weather, poor table manners, unnecessary aggression, and cliches.  My greatest fear is growing old without friends. 

10.  I enjoy eating but I do not do it excessively.  I will admit that I have a small tire around my waist - something I'm honestly working to get rid of for the sake of my future health.  I may review certain foods or restaurants with this blog.

11.  I love technology but I have little to no knowledge concerning computer parts or programming.  For example, if I'm told you posses a GeForce 9800 GX2, all that'll translate to is that you have a graphics card - I have no idea how great it is or not.  I doubt I'll seriously get into it as most people do.  I love using and buying technology whenever my wallet will let me.  Currently I have a quad core desktop, a piece of shit laptop, a DS Lite, a PSP, a 5 megapixel camera, a 8GB iTouch, a Blackberry Pearl, a no longer used Nokia 6265i and a no longer used HP iPAQ - no idea what model.

12.  I am more of a listener than a talker in real life but I find that when I chat online I'm more balanced and sometimes even more outspoken.  I've helped friends cope with loss, smoking addiction, and depression through online chatting which I'd probably have a harder time doing if it were a real life conversation - sometimes I find this pathetic, on my part.  I wish I could be a better speaker in real life but I have trouble wrapping my tongue around the thoughts that float around in my head.  It's difficult to explain, but I guess I feel more comfortable chatting online than opening my mouth to talk - this isn't to say that I can't have a meaningful conversation in real life with friends, but if it were based on preference I'd choose not to talk.  It's just the way I am, whether I can change or want to change is another matter.

I'll admit I pushed myself to have a list of 12 even though it wasn't necessary.  None of this is made up nor am I bullshitting, so in some sense I feel satisfied with what I've written.  In this blog I wish to elaborate on some of these topics although future topics will not be limited to just these.  These just seem to take a bit more precedence in how I go about in my life (not to suggest that this list was compiled in any particular order).

♫ Arctic Monkeys - 505
Link

A brief introduction...

Dec. 11th, 2008   10:13 PM

1.  The username 'dumpling', 'dumplings', and 'discourse' were taken - a little expected but whatever - so I'll settle with what I have now.  On other websites, communities, forums, etc I usually go by the first two usernames or a combination (discourse-dumpling).

2.  I do not write with perfect grammer, sentence structure, etc.  If you have a problem with misspelling don't feel the need to tell me, just turn your attention to something else or go away.  I have a habit of using '...' excessively.

3.  This is not my first blog.  I have used other blog sites before, but never LJ.  I have found that I am usually very enthusiastic when starting a blog.  I post regularly for a couple of months and then I slowly post less and less until it becomes non-existent.  Then maybe after a few months I'll come back to it and comment on how inactive it has been.  And then after that I usually just say fuck it and abandon blogging.  With this blog I would like to try to make a post atleast twice a week with no particular set dates.  I do not, however, want this to be a chore. 

4.  The purpose of this blog is to help me blow off some steam (rant), comment on my lifes absurdities, and perhaps record some fond memories - I kid you not I am extremely forgetful.

5.  A majority of the names used in this blog are pseudonymous. 

Nothing else comes to mind that is worth mentioning about concerning this blog.  If you are curious to know more about me perhaps my entries will fill that void.  If it does not, email me/leave a comment and I may reply back.

♫ Common - Soul Power
